boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Plane Token Pack
description:
The Starter Box for Leviathans only has a few plane tokens -- two each of two different types in two different colors -- and the four card to use them.

This pack contains another two fighters and two bomber tokens which will bring your total up to four of each if you have the starter box as well.  It also include four each of five different planes in two different colors and the cards needed to use them.

The rules for these planes can be found in the Starter Rulebook and Core Rulebook.

Tokens included (in each color) with "green" and veteran sides:

2 x Fighter 
2 x Bomber
4 x Spotter
4 x Heavy Bomber
4 x Turret Fighter
4 x Interceptor
4 x Torpedo Bomber

14 Cards - Green & Veteran of each plane type

Bonbon is a short and light push-your-luck card game for 2-6 players about eating as many chocolates as you can. Players take turns to draw a card (chocolate) from a common deck and add it to their set either face up or face down. Cards have a printed value on them representing the points earned by the player.

Face-up cards will count towards the personal and collective limit players must respect. If you pass your personal limit you immediately lose that round, and if the collective limit is passed during your turn, the round ends immediately and you also lose that round. There are some trick cards that will force you to play face up, or to reveal your face-down cards, so at the start of your turn you can choose to pass for the rest of the round.

Once all players have passed or been eliminated, or if the collective limit has been passed, the round ends and the player with the highest score wins. The first player to win three rounds wins the game!

Tactics and luck combine to make this a fun filler-style or family game. Simply delicious!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Kickstarter Ship Cards
description:
This is a set of five additional ship cards that was part of the rewards for the Kickstarter campaign.

The cards included:
Borgnet -- Faucon class (cruiser) -- French Republic
HML Tiger -- Defender class (battle cruiser) -- British Empire
Romanov -- Cathrine class (battle cruiser) -- Russian Empire
SML Von Zieten -- Saxony class (battle cruiser) -- German Empire
Sabatini -- Vulcano class (battleship) -- Kingdom of Italy

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Kickstarter Admiral Cards
description:
This is a set of five additional admiral cards included as part of the Leviathans: The Great War kickstarter.

The cards with point values are:
Airman -- 10 points
Lieutenant -- 20 points
Commander -- 30 points
Rear Admiral -- 40 points
Sky Admiral -- 50 points

boardgame: Holiday Hills: Let it Snow Promo
description:
It’s a chilly winter this year and there’s extra snow falling in Holiday Hills! Maybe you can put all the extra white fluff to good use while you Decorate the town...Do you wanna build a snowman?

The Let it Snow Promo adds a new way to reserve Trees so only you can Decorate them for a few turns. As you Push your Sleigh around the Town Board you will collect snowflakes to build your own Snowman!

—description from the publisher

The Polar Promo includes snow cubes and wooden snowflakes that you'll use to help you gain Cheer Points with Full and Festive Trees. You'll randomly place the Polar Action Tokens in empty spaces along both the top and bottom paths, then flip them over. As you Push Your Sleigh, you'll be able to take the action you pass over.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Jingle Bell Roll: Naughty or Dice Promo
description:
This mini-expansion for Jingle Bell Roll introduces a bit more mischief into gameplay. Steal cards from other players and freeze their turns to stop them from scoring. Roll the Coal Die with every dice roll and potentially score Krampus enough points to ruin Christmas!

—description from the publisher

Period Party is a chaotic trick-taking game for 2–6 players where hosts compete to throw their very own party to celebrate their period. Shifting goals, emotional reversals, and surprise events keep everyone on their toes until the final card. The game is played in Menstru-Nation, a chaotic, colorful world, where mythical creatures all get periods just like we do.

During play you'll collect party materials for your unique period party by satisfying goals that all players compete for over the course of 7 tricks per round. Earning these materials will require awareness, strategy, and a little bit of luck. The first player to collect all tokens for their party wins.

Players play and compare period cards from one of four suits: blood, pad, pain, and care. Each period card features a unique period related object, feeling, or citizen of Menstru-Nation on their period.  Within these suits is a split trump hierarchy, where only pad trumps blood, and only care trumps pain. Some period cards also feature special period powers depending on their value. Whether reversing the number hierarchy, resetting the starting suit, or something else entirely, there are plenty of ways to collect tricks towards the goals that you are targeting.

Additionally, each round, players reveal a new event card, inspired by personal experiences or social scenarios, which modifies the rules and adds variety and unpredictability. By cycling through period cards, goal cards, and event cards, no two cycles play the same.

More than just wild fun, Period Party is on a mission to break the period taboo. Periods are woven into every card name, illustration, and flavor text, making the theme impossible to ignore while staying playful and lighthearted. You don’t need to talk about periods to play, but don’t be surprised if laughter sparks unexpected, stigma-busting conversations along the way.

—description from the publisher

In this exciting collecting game, players help Maya the Bee and Willy carry all the nectar drops from the poppy meadow to the beehive before the nasty hornet snatches them away. With a bit of luck on the spinner and a good memory, everyone together can save the precious honey!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Players role the die and move the correct number of spaces which will either have funny part of a teachers day-to-day, or one of three cards to draw. 
Implement Cards: These are actions cards that the player must complete
Hard Truth Cards: The player must answer honestly
"Not" a Quiz Cards: Questions about K-12 education and subjects 
The first player to make through the workday win!

—description from the publisher

One, two - mine! In this frantic dice game, players try to collect SpongeBob and his friends in matching pairs. Turn by turn, they snatch figures from one another until all the pairs are taken. Whoever has the most pairs wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Lucky Bears: A National Parks Board Game
description:
Lucky Bears is a game where you collect, steal, and sneak your way through the U.S. national parks! Collect tokens along the way.

The game is a mix between Sudoku and Sequence. You’re dealing with numbers, and you want those numbers to be in order. In the game you want to be the first player to fill your board with national parks tokens. The numbers must in numerical order, both horizontally and vertically. The game drew inspiration from the game, Lucky Numbers, designed by Michael Schacht.

Note: An additional copy of game allows up to 4 to play, but only 2 with 1 copy.

A shedding game fusing Draft & Climb!

Draft to build your hand, then strike in climbing duels. Play smart with same-number sets and suited runs to outplay opponents and shed out first.
Master both phases to dominate: draft to build, climb to win.

The iconic board game returns in a new, strategic version. Roll the dice, collect eggs, chickens, and hens. Take care of your henhouse and upgrade it, because you never know when the Fox will return under the cover of darkness. Plan, plan, and be the first to get all the hens on their roost. More excitement, more decisions, and even more Fox! Fill the henhouse and outsmart the Fox!

The goal of the game is to acquire enough chickens to fill your henhouse. Two sides of the board allow for a shorter (7 chickens) and a longer game (9 chickens).

—description from the publisher (translated)

Drôles de Bibittes is a strategy and tactics game with a light element of bluffing for 1 to 4 players (8 players with 2 boxes). It plays quickly, has high replayability, and features a distinctive cartoon atmosphere. Each player takes on the role of the leader of a small alien tribe — the red Bibittes or the green Bibittes — who compete to rule the universe and, incidentally, your living room table.
Players aim to gain the upper hand through good planning and tactical use of their pieces.

The game experience is unique because the game changes depending on the number of players. 
Playing solo with the game board, it's a puzzle game. 
Playing with two players on the table, it's a real game of strategy and tactics: preparing your piles of token for the entire game forces the player to take a side. Will I be defensive? Aggressive? Balanced? When will I be able to play my trap? As this phase is hidden, there is a high risk of having stacks with different numbers of token, creating an imbalance that you will have to manage during the tactical phases. 
In the two-player game with the game board, the game is much more aggressive and you will have to change the way you build your piles of token at the start to counter your opponent.

Finally, playing in teams is the most fun, strategic, and festive: with 4 or more players (up to 8 players with 2 boxes), the atmosphere is very exciting and lively because the game is aggressive and tense. Players on the same team must communicate about their initial strategy and then coordinate their tactics without their opponents figuring them out... especially when it comes to the trap token! which is the key token in this game. So there's a lot of banter, laughter, and thrills!

Objective : 
As the Supreme Leader of the Bibittes, your goal is to lead your Bibittes to victory. Control the board by placing your Bibittes strategically over 3 rounds: create a colony, flip as many opposing Bibittes as possible, or maintain the largest visible presence on the board.

Game Flow : 
Each game is played in two phases.

     Strategy: Each player prepares three piles of Bibittes behind a screen. Each pile must contain between 4 and 7 Bibittes.
     Tactics: Players take turns placing their pieces according to placement and confrontation rules.


Main Placement Rules : 

     A piece may be placed next to an opposing Bibitte of equal or lower strength, or next to an allied Bibitte.
     A piece may cover an opposing Bibitte of lower strength.
     Bibittes that are completely surrounded are captured and change color.
     The trap piece can eliminate an opposing Bibitte when triggered.


End of Round and Special Conditions : 

     When a player has placed all the Bibittes in their pile for the round, the other player finishes their turn.
     If a player can no longer place any Bibittes legally, their remaining Bibittes for that round are eliminated.


Victory Conditions : 

     Immediate victory: Create a closed 3×3 colony.
     Otherwise: The player with the most captures wins.
     In the event of a tie, the number of visible Bibittes determines the winner, followed by controlled territory.


Game Modes : 

     Classic: Standard two-player match on a table.
     Solo: Play against the red Bibittes using a dedicated board. This is a puzzle game.
     Duo on board: Same rules with a fixed initial placement.
     Team: Two teams compete, with coordination allowed.


“Tooth Hunt” variant: 
Played over three consecutive games. Victories grant tooth tokens. The player or team with the most teeth at the end wins.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Welcome to Claustrophobia 1692, a two-player combat game set in a fictional maze of caverns where human warriors battle infernal forces rising from the depths of Hell. One player leads a desperate band of condemned warriors, led by their Redeemer, who are seeking to fulfill a divine mission, while the other commands numberless demonic hordes seeking to destroy them. Every game is a story of survival or damnation.

Using modular dungeon tiles and scenario-based objectives, Claustrophobia 1692 is played over a series of rounds, each divided into a human turn and an infernal turn. Rounds continue in this way until the endgame conditions described for the scenario have been met, at which point the victor is determined.

—description from the publisher

An abstract, cooperative game for 2 or more players on an hexagonal board.

Overview:
The board starts with 3 colors of tiles spaced evenly across all the spaces of the board such that each tile is isolated from the tiles of its own color. The goal is to join all the isolated tiles to form communities of their own color so that no tile is left isolated. This is accomplished by players swapping nearby tiles while moving their pawns.

The goal must be achieved without talking or indicating to another player what you intend to do, nor what you wish them to do on their turn. The nature of the game is to correctly judge the skill, motives and actions of your collaborators so that the group reaches the goal as quickly as possible, and does not get stuck.

Play
Each player starts the game with one piece, placed one tile in, from opposite corners of the board.

A turn consists of swapping two empty, adjacent tiles and then moving your piece onto any of the tiles in the larger of the communities associated with those 2 tiles.
If the two modified communities are the same size,

Restrictions:

     The color of the swapped tiles must be different from each other.
     The swap must decrease the number of isolated tiles in the 6 spaces adjacent to the piece.
     The piece must move to a different color than it started on (Note: this means if one of the swapped tiles matches the color of the tile where the piece starts its move, then the other tile must become part of an equal or larger group.)
     A piece may not land on another piece.


Players need to be careful where they place their piece, because some locations might not allow for legal moves, and your piece will become stuck there, forced to pass until its surroundings are changed by another piece.

Special moves:
If there is another piece on a tile in the same community as the moving piece, the player swaps as normal, but does not need to decrease the number of isolated tiles, and/or may move to the smaller group, if desired. The piece must still end on a different color. This rule provides another way in which trapped pieces can be freed to move again.

Voluntary passing is not allowed.

Ending the game:
The players win when there are no isolated tiles left. Otherwise, the game ends in a loss when no piece can move.
If the players knowingly repeat a position, they also lose.

—description from the designer

Törööö! The four animal children - Eric the polar bear, Hipp the lion cub, the panda baby, and Polly the penguin - are hiding in the zoo. Can you help Benjamin Blümchen find them?

On a turn, players roll the colour die and move their figure to the next space of that colour, choosing the direction. Spaces may be blocked, as only one figure can occupy a space. Landing on a plain colour space ends the turn. Landing on an animal space allows the player to flip one of the facedown animal tiles. If it matches the animal on their space, they earn a flower; otherwise, the tile is turned facedown again.

The game ends when a player has collected five flowers. That player wins.

This game was included with McDonald's Happy Meals in 2020.

Across the universe, fighters are drawn to the Nexus, an interdimensional hub run by the shadowy intergalactic mafia. As battles unfold, fans throw objects, the mafia interferes, the arena twists, powerful implants are installed and influential sponsors acquired.

Echoes of the Nexus is a fast-paced deck-building miniature fighting game for 2 to 4 players, with asymmetrical characters and multiple arenas.
Players simultaneously and secretly select action cards, trying to sequence and combine combos thanks to the innovative Echoes system. Tactical movement and no-dice combat resolution work together with the simple but deeply strategical initiative system.

Your hero evolves during the game and acquires stronger cards and implants, while gaining the favor of the crowd. Take advantage of your fighter's unique abilities and playstyles, from portal manipulation to turret deployment, stealth assassinations, or grotesque horror.

Will the Nexus crown you, or crush you?

—description from the publisher

A two-phase blocking-annihilation game, played on a hexhex grid with Go stones. Edge size 5 or more is recommended.

Goal: Annihilation.

Definitions:

     An 'egg' is a stone isolated from its own color.
     A 'snake' is a group of connected stones of one color. An egg is a minimal snake.
     A 'mutant' snake is a snake that contains a stone adjacent to more than 2 friendly stones.
     The 'smallest of (a player's) snakes' is one that is as small as that player's smallest snake.


Play
Creation Phase
Players alternately place eggs on isolated empty cells.

The creation phase ends independently for each player, as soon as that player places a stone next to a friendly stone; after which that player may no longer use a turn to increase the number of their snakes.

Wriggling Phase
This phase always applies to only the smallest of a player's snakes.

A turn for a player in this phase consists of either:
A.) The growth AND movement of a snake, if possible; - or else
B.) The death and removal of one or more friendly snakes because they are incapable of growth and movement.

Growth and Movement

     The moving player must first place a stone next to the smallest of her snakes. This new stone may extend a snake, merge two snakes, or temporarily create a mutant snake.
     Then she must move a different stone, which also belongs to the resultant snake, a single step to an empty space in any direction. If the placement had merged two or more snakes, the movement can redivide them. If the placement had caused a mutation, the movement must remedy it.


The result of this growth and movement must not leave any snake in a mutant form, nor may it increase the number of snakes in play.

Death and Removal
If growth and movement is not possible for the smallest of snakes, then the player must remove any dead snakes that cannot 'Wriggle' one at a time, until the smallest of her remaining snakes can grow by 'Wriggling'.

You lose when you remove your last snake.

—description from the designer

The nation’s wounds have yet to heal. What was once disarray has now deepened into unrest. While the conflict between the classes continues, new threats loom over the nation. As crime increases, discontent spreads, and environmental crises intensify, the State pushes for increasingly repressive laws, tightening surveillance and censoring free speech. With democracy in danger and hard-earned liberties stripped away in the name of public safety, where will you stand?

Shadows of Authority is an expansion to the award-winning Hegemony: Lead Your Class to Victory. It introduces a new ideological axis, Authoritarianism vs Liberty, over which players will clash, featuring new policies to vote on and new mechanisms to make use of. At the same time, environmental concerns and pollution are introduced, challenging players to confront ecological issues and shape the future quality of life for their class and the nation.

—description from the designer

The '15th Stamp' takes place in the great Halls of Bureaucracy in Odrixia, continuing the story of Factory 42 and Mine 77. Many documents flood across the desks of overworked government officials, and they all have to be stamped and authorised according to due process. In this fast-paced trick-taking game, mistakes happen and tables turn - just make sure you’re one of the officials who get to make their mark.

15th Stamp supports 3-7 players and plays in about 15-20 minutes.

Components:
80 suit cards in 8 suits
scoring pad

Play
For each Trick, beginning with the Lead player, you play 1 card at a time in clockwise order around the table until the combined Rank value of played cards hits the Target Value of 15.

You do not have to follow suit and can play any Suit with any Rank into the Trick. As soon as the total Rank value of the cards reaches or exceeds 15, the Trick ends immediately. This may mean that players may have played different numbers of cards into the Trick. Some may not have played a card at all.

When playing cards to the Trick be sure to play them separate from the other player’s cards so it it easy to remember and retrieve your own.

What's Next?! is a game of relative history, that is, which man-made objects appeared around the same time as one another.

Each round, five objects lie in a row and a sixth object stands apart. In player order, each player marks which of the five objects appeared in time closest to the sixth object. Once all players have chosen, turn over the sixth object and compare the values for all the objects players chose. Whoever first chose the object with the lowest value scores 2 points, and everyone else who chose this object scores 1 point.

If everyone chooses the same object, the first player to choose it guesses the number of years that separate the two objects, then everyone else guesses higher or lower to determine who will score 1 point.

To end the round, discard the chosen object that earned players points, then add the former sixth object to the row of objects, thus revealing a new sixth object to start another round. Whoever scores 10 points first wins.

boardgame: The Fantasy Nations Table Game: The Chaos Cult Strikes!
description:
Fantasy Nations is a game played on a tabletop where each player controls his own nation in a fantasy world. Each game is a new experience since players create the game “board” anew each game by placing large cardstock terrain tiles on the table. Players conquer territory, recruit new units, draw magic items, gold, healers, heroes, sorcerer’s apprentices and additions to their royal family from the Cup of Destiny to increase their power, making and breaking alliances with other players, slaying monsters and fighting desperate battles. It is possible for more than one player to win. Any game set can be played by itself or added to any of the other Fantasy Nations game sets to increase the size of the playing area and add new races, allowing larger games with more players. Each nation comes with its own special rules, terrain tiles and die cut pieces. Explore all the possibilities!

Each set is compatible with any of the others or can be played by itself.

—description from the publisher

This stand alone game adds the Chaos Cult, Knights Templar and Celts to the Fantasy Nations game system and can be played by itself or paired with any other game of the system.

—user summary

STEM Family Battle is a trivia game where players travel along a board, answering trivia questions about Science, Technology, Engineering, and Math to be the first to unlock the chest.

The aesthetic is a cartoony, family-friendly vibe, akin to a theme park. The board is full of traps, sneaky shortcuts, and secret details.

Each turn, players must answer questions related to STEM. The questions are divided into two different ages and include references to pop culture and video games so no one feels left out.

A family trivia game is a fun and educational choice that keeps all ages engaged. Whether you're testing your knowledge of world facts, geography or crazy world records, this family board game transforms family game night into a lively learning experience.

Packed with traps and shortcuts, you will always find something new on the game board every time you play. The game questions have two levels of difficulty. Ideal to keep younger players engaged while they absorb trivia and factoids.

Become a legendary fossil hunter!

Travel to the fossil-rich sites of North America and uncover the iconic dinosaurs that roamed there millions of years ago. Excavate their remains, assemble full skeletons, and build your legacy as a palaeontologist and adventurer.

Successful expeditions need skills, funds, equipment, and... luck! Recruit top palaeontologists, geologists, and academics to boost your expertise. Secure the funds and tools at the Funding Bureau and the Workshop to prepare for the challenges ahead. Complete dinosaur skeletons to earn rewards from the Museum – or, for the more cunning, visit the Black Market for alternative ways of procuring valuable parts and funds...

Dino Creek is a worker-placement, set-collection game for 2 to 4 players – perfect for friends, families, and dinosaur lovers aged 7 and up.

—description from the publisher

In the dark, grim future, in the Underdeeps of the great Spire Cities, vicious gangs battle for survival against each other and the horrors that lurk in the dark.

For solo or competive play, this game uses the Verrotwood game system as its basis, and is the first in the Rooted in Verrotwood game system.

Written by Straw (of the Battlehammer fame)

With blessings and help from Mike Crutchett, author of Verrotwood.

—description from the designer

Terra Mystica Special Edition adds brand new artwork and deluxe components. It integrates the Automa expansion into the core game, allowing solo play with only the core box. It also pairs with the Special Edition Expansion Vault, which combines all released Terra Mystica expansions  (except the Automa expansion), including Fan Factions.

Terra Mystica is a full information game, without any luck, that rewards strategic planning. Each player governs one of the 14 groups. With subtlety and craft, the player must attempt to rule as great an area as possible and to develop that group's skills. There are also four religious cults in which you can progress. To do all that, each group has special skills and abilities.

Taking turns, the players execute their actions on the resources they have at their disposal.  Different buildings allow players to develop different resources. Dwellings allow for more workers. Trading houses allow players to make money. Strongholds unlock a group's special ability, and temples allow you to develop religion and your terraforming and seafaring skills. Buildings can be upgraded: Dwellings can be developed into trading houses; trading houses can be developed into strongholds or temples; one temple can be upgraded to become a sanctuary. Each group must also develop its terraforming skill and its skill with boats to use the rivers. Proximity to other groups is a double-edged sword in Terra Mystica. Being close to other groups gives you extra power, but it also means that expanding is more difficult.
This promo was part of the Spielbox 5/2025. It contains 4 extra tiles for this game, each 7 spaces big and with 3 kois and 3 water lilies.

boardgame: Catan: Ein Herz für den Räuber!
description:
This expansion was part of Spielbox issue 5/2025. It adds the wish tableau of the robber. You can fulfil his wishes (spend resources) to gain VP and profit from resources production he blocks. 
The expansion is one double-sided sheet of paper containing, rules the wish tableau and tokens to cut out.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Scattered Nexus: End of the World
description:
Scattered Nexus is a battle card game about indie comics. Designed to give the feeling of Trading card games, with set up in 5 minutes at a fraction of the price. Although we love Trading card games here, we started feeling that their practices where turning predatory, and became a barrier to enjoying the game. So we went with a product that 2 players can play with a single box containing everything you need to have a good game, with the option of getting more cards and boxes to add to your options if you want.

To play the game each player selects 3 comics (which contain 10 cards each) to shuffle together to make their 30 card deck, so with a single box with 6 comics, there are 20 possible deck combinations. As of 2024 we have 3 boxes (18 comics) available making 816 possible combinations, and when we meet our goal to partner with 90 indie comics we will have 117,480 combinations possible.

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Summerset
description:
Summerset, one of the most beautiful places in Tamriel, is under the control of the Aldmeri Dominion. A land of magic and mystery, there is no end to what adventurers will discover. Mages wielding Aedric spears roam the province, and their insight may prove invaluable. Assassins on the hunt for their marks have also been seen at night; maybe their skills will prove useful in these dark times.

In Summerset, magical anomalies abound. Adventurers must be wary, as unexpected things may occur before they’ve had time to prepare. As you delve below ground, the land remains unstable!

Mages thrive in this land, and these spell-casting connoisseurs can manipulate rolls, moves, and even enemy actions. Assassins lurk in the shadows, waiting to strike at powerful quest units who prove too tempting a target. The Assassin upgrades once they’ve eliminated the threat.

Aedric Spear encourages clever character builds between battle forms with heavy weapons that rely on the range of your magicka stat. Assassination renders enemies vulnerable to critical strikes and aids players with damage augmentation. Keep an eye out for Grim Focus!

The possibilities in front of you multiply, and there is more than one path to greatness in the Second Era.

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Elsweyr
description:
Home to the Khajiit, Elsweyr is a land of deserts and canyons. Political intrigue abounds in this domain, and dragons prowl the skies, devouring the luckless and unaware. As the fate of Tamriel hangs in the balance, new classes of adventurers make their mark in The Elder Scrolls; thieves and berserkers put their thumbs on the scale and curry favor with the guilds. And unique skills are commonplace in Elsweyr—medium armor and soul magic are ready for the brave and the curious.

In Elsweyr, navigating the map is a dangerous affair. A powerful dragon rules the sky, and it will pursue you throughout the campaign, making overland encounters much more trepidatious!

Thieves prioritize positioning and covet new items, but can surprise unsuspecting enemies with their fatigue-reducing, damage-dealing Dark Vigor ability. Berserkers who fall below full health will enter a rage, triggering tenacity-filled attacks and endangering anyone close to them.

Medium Armor focuses on clearing your cooldown track and crafting agility-based builds instead of pure damage mitigation. Soul Magic ensnares the souls of your enemies and then unleashes them from your active slots for powerful bonus effects.

In a land full of complexity, what will you seek first?

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Heroes of Tamriel
description:
More answer the adventuring call as there are unfinished quests and unfulfilled destinies in the provinces of Tamriel. It will take all of your skill and tenacity to overcome Deslandra and other looming threats in the Second Era. Discover the strengths of dual wielding and storm calling. Learn the techniques of warriors and witchhunters. And lean on the natural abilities of the many races who travel the continent.

Witchhunters are doggedly focused on certain types of enemies. This magic and range-focused class gains bonuses when engaging with those unique targets, and don’t forget to use potions with abandon. Warriors are the jack-of-all-trades that will carry you through countless battles. They possess a lot of powerful abilities, and it's encouraged to switch between battle forms to gain an advantage!

The second light weapon battle form skill, Dual Wield rewards fast, reckless damage-dealing. With the proper skills trained, adventurers can even attack with multiple weapons in a single engage. Storm Calling, on the other hand, magically improves your stamina-based damage through control of the weather. It’s another skill line that encourages mixed battle forms!

Become a hero of Tamriel and wage an epic campaign against the growing darkness!

In Conquer the Castle, you are the ruler of a powerful kingdom that unfortunately experienced an act of treason by 10 noble persons and their guards who have all locked themselves within the walls of a castle which you must besiege. Over 11 turns, you must fight multiple guards and try to take out as many nobles as you can using a regular 54-card deck (including the jokers and minus 10 for the nobles) by combining your soldiers' ranks in order to create the strongest warriors possible! Your goal is to take out all the nobles and regain your authority as the supreme ruler of the land.

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Fur and Fang
The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Shadows of Oblivion
description:
The Daedric princes rule their respective planes of Oblivion, and their influence over mortals cannot be understated. Whether summoned to Tamriel for worship or study, these entities of great power will frequently strike deals and make pacts with those who dare. Such a bargain is laced with poison and sure to bring misery, but a serious adventurer doesn’t dismiss any potential advantage, and the prospect of wielding Daedric power is intoxicating!

Each of the five Daedric princes comes with something akin to a side quest, offering a blessing and a curse. It’s recommended that adventurers play with both, but those looking to further manipulate the game’s difficulty could choose one or the other. With unique componentry and so many ways to adjust the gameplay experience, we’re very excited about what Shadows of Oblivion has to offer.

Shadows of Oblivion introduces Hermaeus Mora, Sheogorath, Mehrunes Dagon, Azura, and Sanguine to Betrayal of the Second Era… for now!

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Adventurer's Cache
description:
Items and relics from across Tamriel! Adorn yourself in the finest gear! Eat, drink, and cast like a boss!...er hero!
The Adventurer's Cache is a special set of items and relics for veteran adventurers. This proved so popular in our first campaign that it only made sense to do it again! Whether scrounging for those useful common items or holding out for the perfect legendary find, your adventure will be much more robust with the addition of the Adventurer's Cache.

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era – Luxury Wear
description:
Wouldn’t it be nice if, beneath all of that hulking armor and worn adventurer gear, you could luxuriate in some truly divine negligé? Followers of the campaign will receive the most resplendent underwear, courtesy of Chip Theory Games.

Do you prefer the Skywatch Swan-Satin Bathing Thong or the Silverweave Small Clothes? What about the Sustaining Corset, to keep everything tight and trim while you’re out campaigning?

These foil-treated undergarments will sparkle on the table (and shimmer elsewhere)!

Yargh! is a fast-paced bluffing and set-collection card game where pirates battle to plunder the most treasure before the final broadside.

Players draw, steal, and defend booty while navigating traps, fake treasure, and misfortune — all while trying to read their rivals’ next move.

Easy to learn and endlessly replayable, Yargh! rewards clever timing, bold bluffs, and a bit of pirate luck. Designed for 3–6 players, it delivers quick, high-energy rounds filled with laughter, risk, and a healthy dose of betrayal.

Published by High Tide Studios, Yargh! brings classic pirate chaos to the tabletop with modern mechanics and a playful twist.

—description from the publisher

A board game released in the late 80s in Greece my ΜΙΚΑ toy company. It is played with 2, 3, or 4 players and includes a board and 16 pieces in 4 colours.

The pieces are moved by each player in turn, horizontally or vertically, provided that there is no other piece in the square they are going to. The aim of the game is for a player to place all 4 of their pieces on 4 squares that are exactly the same colour.

A magical accident has transformed what was once a peaceful farm into a dreadful dungeon. The animals are mutating into all sorts of creatures — goblin mice, dragon chickens, witch goats... Luckily, the pigs have turned into powerful adventurers, and they’re heading into the dungeon to see who among them is the most powerful.

In Porcus: A Dungeon Adventure, each player takes on the role of a pig adventurer class, fighting alongside the others to defeat powerful monsters but only one of you will win, and the secret lies in taking advantage of your companions.

It includes a family mode suitable for all ages or you can dare to try the expert mode, featuring unique classes and powerful monsters.

Choose a secret attack for each battle, capture monsters to earn gold and treasures, sneak around to grab even more loot, and sabotage the others to come out on top.

Because in Porcus, only the piggiest player will win the game!

Awards
1st Place for Best Prototype in Meeple Factory 2025
2nd Place for Best Prototype in Concurs Ciutat de Granollers 2025
Top 4 Finalist for Best Prototype in Verkami Dau Barcelona 2025

boardgame: Notorious: Tales of Hardscrabble Bounty Hunting
description:
Notorious: Tales of Hardscrabble Bounty Hunting is a game for one player.  Play to tell stories of the Nomads; notorious bounty hunters who strike fear among the scum and villainy of the universe and follow the dubious code of the Nomad's Guild.

You'll choose bounties from one of three factions and hunt your targets across planets, interacting with locals as you search for leads. The job is simple: bring the target back, dead or alive. No disintegrations.

But your presence also attracts hostiles. Suspicious locals who simply don’t like you (and their friends might not either), rival Nomads or faction agents working against you. Survive these encounters to face off against your elusive target. Finish the job and receive credits and infamy, or fail to live up to the code and face the consequences.

Notorious has its roots in roleplaying and players will craft their own narrative through cards and dice rolling. But mechanically it's more comparable to an open-world adventure card game like Earthborne Rangers.  A single session sees you completing one bounty by choosing a faction to work for and hunting your target across one of three planets. A full game combines three bounties into your own personal trilogy.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ironclads of Ferrosia: The Sky Offensive
description:
Introducing The Sky Offensive: A New Chapter in Ironclads of Ferrosia

The Sky Offensive, is the first supplement for the tabletop game, Ironclads of Ferrosia. This expansion elevates your gaming experience by incorporating aerial combat into the rich world of Ferrosia.

A New Era of Warfare
Imagine formidable ironclads clashing on the seas, now envision that conflict taking to the skies! The Sky Offensive introduces flying ships, enabling players to engage in strategic aerial dogfights. These vessels offer unique advantages and challenges, fundamentally altering the dynamics of warfare.

What’s Included in The Sky Offensive?
1. New Flying Ships
Command new flying ships, each possessing distinct characteristics. From the heavily armoured sky-ships of the Valtoris Imperium to the nimble raiders of the Zephyros Coalition, each vessel provides a unique playstyle, complemented by an assortment of new ocean-going ships.
2. Aerial Manoeuvre Rules
Navigate the skies with new rules for aerial combat, including altitude advantages and innovative bombardment mechanics that enhance the gameplay experience.
3. Custom Scenarios
Experience three specially designed scenarios that seamlessly integrate airborne and naval engagements, encouraging players to think creatively and immerse themselves in exhilarating aerial battles.

Prepare for Battle!

With The Sky Offensive, the struggle for Ferrosia has ascended into the skies! 
This supplement offers a new gameplay experience while expanding upon the core rules of Ironclads of Ferrosia.

Are you ready to command your airborne armada and achieve supremacy in the skies?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Operation Kotluban: Piercing the Stalingrad Corridor, Sept. 1942
description:
On August 23rd, 1942, the German 14th Pz Corps broke through with amazing speed to the Volga, north of Stalingrad. The stunning euphoric victory quickly turned into a desperate battle of survival for the Germans as they try to hold their positions that cut-off the Russian 62nd Army. Within days, the Germans were in dire situations by Aug.28th. The Russians quickly massed three tank corps opposite their flank that stretched for 25 miles or more and moved newly formed armies also. The first Kotluban Offensive began on Sept.1 and slowly began to cut off the 16th Panzer Division again. The next few days were critical for either side.

Game includes one 11x17" map, 280 counters, rules.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
PANZERSCHLACHT: Operation Gemse 1945 covers the very last German offensive in the Berlin area in mid February-March 1945. 
While the 6SS PzA had been deployed to Hungary in their ill-fated attack there, GEMSE would be the last of the German offensive capability in the Lauban area after the Russians had crossed the Vistula River. The Lauban area contained a vital rail hub that the Germans wanted to keep and although the Russians had severe losses by the time they reached the river, so had the German panzer units. Thus, the Germans launched this offensive to push back the advancing Russian units despite the front crumbling elsewhere. It would include several panzer divisions that did succeed initially forcing the Russians to divert forces.

Game scale is one mile hexes, battalion level. Includes 11x17 map, 280 counters, rules.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Dive further than you’ve gone before to discover what lies in the murkiest depths of the ocean. Be warned: not everything down here is friendly!

New movement cards allow specific modules to move to highlighted areas. Arrows extend the range of possible spaces even further.

The life of a retired god of passionate desire isn’t exactly filled with thrills. What is one supposed to do, when one isn’t supposed to intervene in human affairs anymore? A job writing Christmas movies sort of fills time, but it’s not as exciting as the good old days.

But still, one last twang of the old bow and arrow won’t hurt? And those two people, sat opposite each other on the train, they look compatible. Surely, a little helping hand, or arrow, could help things along. There’s only sevem more stops to go for romance to bloom.

This 9 card solitaire print and play game challenges you to roll dice to push forward a romantic conversation between two passengers. Choose instructions to program your trainline, letting you re-roll, nudge, copy and lock dice.

—description from the designer

Outlandish is a an absurd conversation game of silly question and useless awards.

150 hilarious, quirky, and debate-worthy questions guaranteed to get your friends talking. What superpower would you want, if it only worked in the dark? Would you turn into a human lie detector, if it meant you could never lie again?

Also included are 75 awards to hand out for the weirdest, wisest, and boldest answers. Decide who deserves the “Lucky Cat Award,” or the “Dumpster Fire Award.” Collect as many awards as possible to see who is the most Outlandish!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to the Garden Club, where fruits, veggies, and flowers flourish into clever combinations. Match, grow, and out-bloom your friends in this lighthearted game of strategy and charm!

In Garden Club, players compete to grow the most vibrant and well-balanced garden in the neighborhood. Each tile you place will plant fruits, veggies, and flowers — all carefully arranged to create stunning patterns and score points.

On your turn, you’ll select and place a tile into the collective garden, connecting matching icons to form combos and fulfill unique objectives. Then, you'll select one tile as a point multiplier for what's in your garden. The challenge lies in planning your layout — every placement can unlock new scoring opportunities or block a path to a perfect harvest.

As your garden grows, you’ll earn points for completing arrangements, surrounding trellises, and meeting your character's preferences.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Daddy Bought the Farm... Now What?!?
description:
“A farming game by people who don’t know how to farm—because neither do you.”

Daddy Bought the Farm… Now What?!? is a worker placement game, featuring 6 siblings who have just inherited a plot of land from their father. He had always meant to start a family farm, but never got around to it. Then, he died. Now, it’s up to the siblings to make his dream a reality. However, there are plenty of obstacles to overcome – One, there’s still a pretty hefty loan from the bank to pay back, and only 5 years to do it. Two, none of the siblings have ever farmed before. And three, the siblings don’t necessarily like each other.

Up to 6 players can play as one of the siblings, using what skills they DO have (cause don’t a single one of them actually know how to farm!) to get the family farm up and running. They can also have the siblings reach their own selfish objectives, and keep the other siblings from reaching theirs.

Players are able to increase their skills in Animal Husbandry, Building, and Crop growing to build and repair farm buildings, raise cows and sell their milk, and grow and sell crops. They’ll have to contend with locusts, animal rights activists, and even taxes if they want to be successful. They’ll also have to watch out for the other players, as the siblings can steal from each other.

The game can be played solo or by up to 6 players, and either competitively or cooperatively. The siblings have their own skills, which become supercharged when they have maxed out their Karma (by helping each other in a cooperative game), or Schadenfreude (by hurting each other in a competitive game), which increases it’s replay value, as each sibling’s gameplay will look different.

—description from the designer

How well can you symbolize your thoughts? And how quickly can you read your friends’ imagination?

In Synta, one player describes a random object using only a synthesis of rolled symbols. The other players race to interpret these clues and guess the intended object.
Players move based on their success, racing each other to the finish line!

Synta delivers a light, clever, and hilarious association experience that challenges creativity and intuition.

—description from the designer

Players take turns rolling their own six dice, one roll per turn, and adding some of their newly rolled dice into their own hand.  Players sometimes kill their opponent's hand.

Straight - Collection of one or more dice, numbered sequentially, such as (2, 3, 4), (3, 4, 5, 6), and (1).  [A straight is always as long as it can be.  It’s never a sub-straight of a longer straight.]

Full straight - (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6)

Complement (or Run Buster) - The set of dice which, when combined with a non-full straight, completes a full straight.  For example, the complement of (2, 3, 4) is (1, 5, 6).
 To win, your hand must be a full straight.  [Hands are always comprised of exactly one straight.]

Begin your turn by rolling the dice in your cup.  If your newly rolled dice contain the complement of your opponent's straight, you must remove his hand from the table.

Then (whether or not you've killed your opponent's hand) you must extend your straight with at least one of your newly rolled dice, if you can.  For example, say your hand is (4, 5) and you've rolled (3, 4, 4, 6).  Your new, extended straight options are (3, 4, 5), (3, 4, 5, 6), and (4, 5, 6).  But say that, instead, you've rolled (1, 2, 4, 5).  In this case, there's no way to extend your (4, 5) straight.  As always, put your remaining dice back in your cup, concluding your turn.

Loony Clues is a lively party word game where players hide behind secret identities and try to guess who’s who. Use clever hints, uncover the masks, and score points by figuring out the identities of your fellow players.

At the start of the game, players are randomly assigned one of the 12 secret identities on the game board. Each round, players ask one another a simple question using cards. "Are you colorful?" "Are you flat?" Yes or no answers help players find the right identity! Will you be courageous and guess quickly, or would you rather wait for more clues? And is sushi washable, really? The interpretation is entirely up to the players, but don't try to be sneaky, as you also score points when someone guesses you correctly!

With simple rules and a variety of word cards, every round is fresh, fun, and endlessly replayable.

—description from the publisher
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
A fast and furious game of skill, luck and action for two players. A 3 Dimensional plastic game board complete with spacecraft targets and spring loaded triggers.

A game where you shoot marbles up and around the track to hit different value targets, to gain the biggest score.

AIM OF THE GAME: Win three pairs!

1. Split into two teams. Shuffle one deck of the cards and deal five to each team, question side up. Each card has 5 questions.
2. Teams take it in turns to read Question 1 to their opponents.

If one team is right and one is wrong the correct team win the pair. Carry on to Question 1 of the next card.

If both are right it's a tie and you move on to the next question on the same card.

If both are wrong (or both teams got all 5 correct) nobody wins this pair yet. Set it (the pair) aside on the table and move on to the next card beginning with Question 1 again.

When you win a pair you also win any set aside pairs on the table.

3. If you use all five cards and it's still a tie, then pick a new card each and carry on until there is a winner.

4. First team to 3 pairs wins the game.

In this version of Top Trumps Quiz all the questions are about the Harry Potter movies.

boardgame: The Last Panzer Victory: Bautzen, April 20-26, 1945
description:
The Last Panzer Victory was a shock to the Russians. 
At this very late state of WW2, the assault on Berlin was the main objective and within the grasp of the Red tide. 
As this drama played out, the German 4th Pz Army with over 200 tanks (many Pz V and Nashorn) which was largely intact south of the main Red offensive struck from the south into the flank of Polish 1st Tank Corps (over 200 AFV) and elements of the 2nd Polish Army, whose advance was ordered to take Dresden to meet the Americans.  
The Russians had thought the Polish troops would have no problems with a defeated German army. 
The opposite was true, so much so, the 20th Pz and Herman Goring Pz Divisions demolished the Poles and the Russian 4th Gd Tank Corps had to make a U-turn from their planned advance to prevent the Germans reaching Berlin!

Scale is 1.5 miles/hex, units are company/battalion/regiment.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Pyrrhic Victory: The German Blucher Offensive, May 1918
description:
In 1918, the Germans would have several offensives to end the war. Only Blucher, which aimed for Paris once again, was nearly a success. Once again Stormtroopers infiltrated the enemy lines with devastating effect, gas broke open the static lines and the limited use of tanks gave the offensive real impact and speed. All this created a huge bulge that the Germans had not expected nor prepared for and came within 35 miles of Paris! 
It seemed as if nothing had changed since the last time it happened in 1914. The British seemed to just melt away in this quiet sector and had it not been for American divisions, the outcome might had been different.

Game scale is 3 miles\hex with daily turns. Units are tank battalions, regiments and brigades. One 11x17" map and 280 counters plus "what if" scenarios had the Germans used more tanks in their possession.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Space Privateer Command: 10 Cards Mini Add-On
description:
The SPC Add-on of 10 cards is meant to increase the playability of the original game by adding new missions and event. You will need the original Space privateer command game to be able to use these cards.

The Add-On cards consist of Story Mission: Prison Break card, Privateer Job: Escort Mission card and Freighter Merchant card. An official Errata card for the core rule book for the main game, planet tracker cards and Ship event cards are also part of this release. These new cards are meant to expand the world of Space Privateer Command and are OPTIONAL. Players do not need to play these cards to enjoy the main game of Space Privateer Command.

Trivia should be fun–not feel like a homework assignment. That's why this Junk Food (& Food Food) Trivia set by Brass Monkey is filled with some of the most pointless food knowledge we could find…and even better, you'll choose your questions from a bunch of misleading categories. For example: 'Hunger Games.' Those are questions about eating competitions, by the way.

Inspired by classic two-player card games like Gin Rummy and Koi-Koi, in Heavenly Bodies players meld three or more cards in sets and runs, aiming to pass thresholds for each suit in order to score points. Each turn, players may either draw two cards from a shared deck, then discard a card to either of two discard piles OR draw all the cards of either discard pile, melding any that they can into sets, then taking the remaining as penalty cards.

Once players have melded cards, with the number of cards depending on the suit, they score points. Each suit in the game — planet, moon, comet, and star — has a different composition of ranks based on a pyramid, and card thresholds worth different amounts of points.

Each hand is a race to 5 points. Once a player reaches 5 points, they can stop the current hand and collect one star fragment OR "Nova" and push their luck to reach 10 points before their opponent reaches 5. If they Nova successfully, they collect two star fragments; if not, their opponent collect one star fragment instead.

Whoever collects four star fragments first wins.

—description from the publisher
artists: United States National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA)

description:
Play Hard and Fast! Based on popular Australian TV Quiz featured on the Australian Broadcasting Corporation (ABC). Features card version over earlier fold out board version. Play the Expert Round, Tom’s (Host: Tom Gleeson) Round and the People’s Round like the TV show. And you try and dispense with the "Tom" tokens as you answer correctly. Win Hard Quiz - at home.

—description from the publisher

It’s the 1990s: Reality runs on magnetic tape, and ... someone’s been recording over it. Officially, you work for a small, chronically underfunded government office, just another lost spoon in the alphabet soup of agencies.
Unofficially, you chase strange signals: corrupted broadcasts, strange transmissions, flickers of something hiding in the static.

CASE 1: THE TAPE
A VHS tape is making the rounds at a local high school. Those who watch it come back… different. They speak of hours that vanish, of voices twisting through the static, of frozen frames that twitch when no one is looking. The longer they try to ignore it, the closer it seems to get
You’ve got a copy sitting on your desk right now.
Do you destroy it ... or do you press play?

Infrared is a hyper-experimental mystery game that blends analogue horror with digital interaction. Presented on a single page, it’s a choose-your-own-adventure navigated entirely through QR codes—no app required, just a mobile camera and an internet connection.

Players explore branching storylines, make skill checks with dice, collect items on a player sheet, and piece together the truth behind the mystery. Combining physical and digital play, Infrared offers a unique storytelling experience.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 2 Dice City: An English conversation game
description:
This is an Eikawa (English conversation) activity for 2 or more students in Japanese junior high school and elementary school. However, this can easily be modified for other classroom applications. Students roll dice to create a city and help the people there. If they can help all the people, their city will grow big and rich, but if they cannot help the people their city will stay small and poor.

This is inspired by  Brent P. Newhall's: 3 Dice Dungeon: A Solitaire Dungeon Crawl Game, Daniel Dias Rodrigues's: 3 Dice Dungeon and Greywulf's: RPG Solitaire Challenge: 3 Dice

—description from the designer

The Abbot is a mini-expansion for Carcassonne introduced with the 2014 new-art edition. Each player gains a special abbot follower that can be placed on a monastery or a garden. Instead of placing a follower on a later turn, a player may retrieve their abbot and immediately score points equal to the monastery/garden tile plus each orthogonally adjacent tile at that moment. If the abbot remains on the board at game end, it scores like an incomplete monastery/garden.

KNOCK is a fast-paced, mind-bending card game of memory, risk, and deception. Beneath its simple setup lies a battle of wits where players must bluff, guess, and outsmart their opponents without ever truly knowing what’s in their own hand. Every move is a gamble — trust your instincts, read your rivals, and time your final “Knock” just right.

Gameplay:
Each player begins with four facedown cards they can’t see. On every turn, players draw and discard cards to lower the total value of their hand which could go as low as minus 6 — but the twist is that most actions rely on limited information and sharp memory. Special power cards let you peek, penalty, swap, or reveal cards, creating chaos and strategy as players try to piece together what’s hidden. Timing and observation are key — one wrong discard or forgotten card can turn the game around.

Goal:
The round ends when someone boldly decides to “Knock,” freezing their hand and challenging everyone else for the lowest score. When the final turn comes back around, all hands are revealed — and the player with the lowest total value wins.

Essence:
KNOCK blends memory, strategy, and daring into one quick, competitive experience. It’s easy to learn, unpredictable to master, and guaranteed to keep players guessing until the very last reveal.

—description from the designer

Summer is coming to an end, and the forest dwellers are getting ready for the long-awaited Fall Fest!
Fall Fest is a strategy game where players must wisely choose which cards to play, gathering them into matching sets; all from the same Guild of Trades or the same Animal House.
But beware: time is running out! Each time a player runs out of cards, a leaf falls from the mighty red oak, marking the approach of autumn and the end of the game.

The first half of the 20th century was a period of extraordinary discoveries in nuclear physics.
During those years, scientific competition and international collaboration led to the rise of world-class research laboratories across Europe and beyond.

In this game, each player leads one of these physics laboratories, inspired by the genius and experimental approach of the renowned Italian scientist. Through new theoretical and experimental research, you will develop cutting-edge instruments, train brilliant assistants, and present your discoveries at international conferences, with the goal of making your laboratory the most prestigious and influential of its time.
Each turn, the player chooses one of the available actions and resolves it. The main actions are: building machines in your laboratory, producing new ideas, publishing theories, and attending a conference.

The game is based on mechanics where you chain effects for continuous growth and stronger actions.
The main tension is that the costs and end-game value of the four machines are common and  increase quickly. You need to finish everything before the costs become too high!

—description from the publisher

5Pairs is a quick, clever, and colorful card game that anyone can learn in a minute. Players race to collect matching cards, form sets, and outsmart each other in a fast-paced battle of wits and timing. With simple rules and snappy turns, it’s designed to be the perfect “just one more round” game for families, casual players, and game-night warmups alike.

Each turn, you’ll draw cards or try to build valuable pairs. Make a pair of the same color or number to score points — but the right timing can earn you double! Every decision matters, and every move can swing the momentum in your favor.

As players race toward their fifth pair, things heat up — bonuses appear, points can be stolen, and clever combos turn simple moves into big swings. The first to collect five pairs ends the round, but getting there takes equal parts luck, observation, and timing.

Bright, fast, and endlessly replayable, 5Pairs fits in your pocket but brings big-table energy. It’s a filler game that rewards quick thinking, light strategy, and friendly competition — a simple game for simply everyone (except Steve).

—description from the publishe

boardgame: Rayman: The Board Game – 5-6 Players Expansion
description:
The Adventure Just Got Bigger!

With the 5-6 Players Expansion, even more heroes can join the action, elevating the chaos, strategy, and fun of Rayman The Board Game to new heights.

This thrilling expansion lets you:

     Add Two New Heroes: Join forces with the radiant Teensy Queen and the fearless Murfy!
     Take On Fresh Challenges: Explore two stunning new maps — 20,000 Lums Under the Sea + Olympus — and battle an all-new Boss!
     Level Up the Chaos: Now playable with up to 6 players. More heroes mean more slaps, surprises, and strategies to outsmart your opponents.


Are you ready to take the adventure to the next level?

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
As winter draws near, the Arctic Ground Squirrels prepare for hibernation. As one of those critters, can you store enough food to survive the upcoming season, while also avoiding the many predators that threaten you?

Gameplay Overview
In just 8 weeks, you'll have to gather at least 16 food. Weather conditions and predators will inevitably get in your way; smart planning becomes a necessity when trying to survive winter.

Weeks going by

Each of the 8 weeks is divided into 2 phases: the Action Phase, where you MUST take exactly 2 actions, and the Environment Phase, where you'll resolve events like weather and predator movement.

There are 3 types of action: digging, where you spend stamina to unlock a new chamber of your Burrow card each providing a benefit, foraging, where you gather food from a selected spot on the map card, and resting, where you spend food to restore health and stamina.

The Burrow

Movement on the map will always start from the burrow tile, but most importantly, the tile the burrows in will determine its soil type.
Sandy soil is easier to dig, but will make your food stash more prone to spoiling.
Rich soil instead is harder to carve, but provides more capacity to save food from weather.
Grassy soil allows you to regenerate better in stamina, but it makes it so you won't draw Stat Cards when resting.

Predator Encounters

ely want to taunt them, as they may guard a particularly rich foraging spot.
Each predator has 2 stats: Danger, which you must meet or exceed to escape, and Strength, which indicated the damage done on a failure.
When in an encounter, you play stat cards for their value; if the sum of cards played is enough, you successfully escape, else you take the indicated damage. Each predator also has an effect which may interfere with your stats.
After an encounter, whether successful or not, you'll be able to rotate one card in your hand, making it more powerful and unlocking the effect  on it.

Shifting Weather

During each Environment Phase, you must draw a Weather card; there are 2 types of cards: Serene Weather, which not only doesn't cause you to lose food, but creates an Abundant foraging spot which yields double food once, and Adverse Conditions, which will make you lose all unprotected food. Protected capacity is gained by digging more food storage chambers, but those are limited.

Squirrel's Long Rest is a quick game of strategy, with many variables in play. The Map and Burrow cards are doubled sided, with a different configuration on each side. You are sure not to encounter all predator types in a single game, and you can challenge yourself by trying out all soil types.

—description from the publisher

Fiesta is a competitive memory game for 2 to 4 players, ages 5 and up, with quick rounds of about 10 minutes.

Inspired by the Andean celebration of Pachamama —the “Mother Earth”—, the game blends the ritual spirit of music and dance with the fun of uncovering matching pairs and trios of cards. Each player seeks to connect with nature through play, evoking traditional instruments like the zampoña, charango, or rain stick, in an atmosphere that celebrates harmony and balance with the Earth.

The goal is to reach 10 points by finding matching cards on a 3x6 grid. On their turn, a player flips a card and tries to find its match; if successful, they can either stop (earning 1 point) or take a risk and try to find a third identical card (worth 5 points). If they miss, the turn passes to the next player. Whenever matching cards appear in the same row, those rows are shuffled to keep everyone guessing.
The game ends when someone reaches 10 points, crowning the Fiesta in honor of Pachamama with a joyful mix of memory, intuition, and collective celebration.


Spanish:

Fiesta es un juego competitivo de memoria para 2 a 4 jugadores a partir de los 5 años, con partidas de unos 10 minutos. Inspirado en la celebración andina de la Pachamama —la “Madre Tierra”—, el juego combina el espíritu ritual de la música y la danza con la dinámica de descubrir pares y tríos de cartas. Cada participante busca conectar con la naturaleza a través del juego, evocando instrumentos tradicionales como la zampoña, el charango o el palo de lluvia, en una atmósfera que celebra la armonía y el equilibrio con la Tierra.

El objetivo es sumar 10 puntos encontrando cartas iguales en una grilla de 3x6. En su turno, la jugadora o jugador voltea una carta y busca su par; si acierta, puede decidir detenerse (ganando 1 punto) o arriesgarse a encontrar una tercera igual (ganando 5 puntos). Si falla, el turno pasa al siguiente. Cada vez que se revelan cartas iguales en una misma fila, las filas se mezclan para mantener la incertidumbre. El juego termina cuando alguien alcanza los 10 puntos, coronando la fiesta en honor a la Pachamama con un clima de memoria, intuición y celebración colectiva

As a ranger, feed wild birds efficiently and attract them to your avian sanctuary. But be careful; sheltering more than three bird species from the same habitat will harm the ecosystem. The player who collects the most birds from a variety of habitats wins!

Hungry Birds is a strategy game where you feed adorable birds with colorful food tokens and compete for points. Blending Mancala mechanics with clever set collection, it offers the perfect mix of cuteness and strategy for players of all ages.

Players choose food tokens, play Mancala, and get cards that have been given the number of pieces of food that the bird is requesting. There's no problem using your own food to get cards, but if you use another player's food tokens to take a card, that player will receive points equal to the number of pieces of food used.

The player who collects the most valuable cards and achieves the highest total score, calculated by adding the points from their cards and the food, will be the winner.

—description from the publisher

Vier is a strategic card game in which players compete to collect four cards of the same number. Each player begins with four cards, and four cards are placed face-up in the center. On their turn, players may swap a card with the center, burn a card that matches a symbol, or pass. Rounds continue until a player achieves a matching set of four. The game features special elements such as Jokers, which act as wild cards, and the “FOUR-TUNE” event, which occurs when all center cards share the same symbol, prompting players to pass a card to the left.

Boss Hunters invites you to join up with up to four hunters in a cooperative adventure of strategy and courage.

Plan every move, combine unique abilities, and coordinate impossible attacks as you outmaneuver your enemies to strike at their weak points. Each battle is a titanic clash where cunning is worth as much as the sword and a single mistake can seal your fate.

Challenge the impossible and claim the glory that only the boldest will ever know. The realm needs heroes… will you be the one to etch your name into legend?

The hunt has begun. And there’s no turning back.

Arkham Duels is a competitive game for 2 to 4 players in which each participant battles for control of the portals. These portals are the gateway through which fantastical creatures, inspired by the works of H. P. Lovecraft and his collaborators, seek to enter our world. The game uses 54 Basic Cards divided into 6 different Factions.

The game is played in rounds, and each round is divided into three phases: Preparation Phase, Game Phase, and Scoring Phase.
1. Setup Phase: Players draw cards to form their hand (usually 7 cards for 2 players, or 6 cards for 3/4 players). If the Beyond the Portal expansion is used, a Portal Modifier is applied to the round.
2. Play Phase: On alternate turns, players perform available Actions:
    ◦ Play a card: Players place a card from their hand on one of their three Portal Columns. The goal is to dominate the Column (indicated by the Dominance Marker). Dominance is achieved if the card played does not have the same value as the opponent's card. There are specific requirements for playing cards in Columns where there are already cards. In Advanced Mode, the unique effects of the Factions of the cards played apply.
    ◦ Pass: A player may pass up to two times. The second time they pass, they are considered to have passed the round and may no longer play cards until the round ends. The Play Phase ends when all players have passed.
3. Scoring Phase: Players accumulate points based on the Columns in which they achieve Victory (dominance). Points are equal to the difference in value between the two cards in the Column. If there is only one card, the victorious player earns 5 points.

The game ends immediately when a player is declared the winner.

     The first player to accumulate 10 or more victory points is the winner in 2 player games.
     In 3 or 4 player games, the point requirement for victory is 15 or more points.


—description from the designer

This game is the second installment in a trilogy supplement to be played using the counters from La Bataille d’Eggmühl 1809 as published by Marshal Enterprises in 2024.

boardgame: Turnabout: A Game of Fair Play
description:
A race around the board to collect five posts for victory! Each different coloured player piece has different abilities or advantages...

Green (AKA The Banker) starts the game with three times the coins as all their opponents. They also collect three coins instead of one each time it lands on the green square.

Yellow (AKA The Shopkeeper) can hop over traps and barricades as if they weren’t there. Each time they land on the yellow square, they get a free trap from the shop.

Blue (AKA The Speedster) starts with one less post to have to grab. It also can teleport from one blue square to another whenever it lands on one.

Purple (AKA The Protected) is immune to all negative Chance cards. Furthermore, every time it lands on the purple square, it can choose what colour it wants that square to be.

Red (AKA The Warden) can always just walk right out of the Pokey anytime they are sent there.  No need to stay and try to roll red to get out.  Also, immune to punishment when it lands on a Red square.

Orange (AKA The Card Reader) is able to pick up two chance cards each time it lands on the orange square. It can also put cards they don’t want back on the top of the pile for the next player to pick up.

Coloured squares on the board and die match the colours of the player pieces.  Players may choose their piece voluntarily but if there is conflict, a roll of the die determines the owner of the piece for the game.

There are two turning board platform that give or take away shortcuts as the game proceeds.  Cards of chance, traps and old-fashioned luck add more elements of unpredictability.

Every turn starts with movement based on a die roll and movement to a next square of the colour shown on the die - this can provide strategic options.  Other actions that can be performed are Shopping for Items, Playing Chance Cards or Bargaining with other Players.

Described as "nostalgic schoolyard play" by several players and reviewers.

—description from the publisher

Braggarts is a simple trick-taking game for two to four players.

Each round begins with a bidding phase in which the knights brag about how many beasts they will slay (and thus how many tricks the player will have to win).

Then the trick-taking begins, with players laying successively higher head and tail cards to construct a huge beast. Players must play cards higher in value than those already played, but can do so at either end of the trick.

The player who plays the final card takes the trick.

Max and Goofy face off against Peter Pete and P.J. Pete in classic Goof Troop backyard competition!

REWIND is played in a series of Rounds until a Player has claimed Victory. During each Round, there are three Phases: Inspiration, Action, and Scoring.

Inspiration Phase: Each Player plays one Inspiration Card from their hand and gathers the indicated Inspiration Tokens, placing them on the Inspiration Card. Some Inspiration Cards have effects that happen immediately when played during this Phase, as indicated on the card.

Action Phase: Starting with the Player who has Initiative, each Player takes a turn performing up to two actions with one Character. Turns continue to alternate until each Player has passed.

Scoring Phase: Score points based on the requirements of the Episode Card. If a player has reached the Victory Condition of the Episode, they are the winner! If the Victory Condition is not met, begin a new Round starting with the Inspiration Phase.

—description from the publisher

description:
Energize your game nights with a shocking twist on the classic game Jenga!
In ShiZap!, players take turns using electrified tweezers to carefully extract blocks from a teetering tower and place them on top, all while trying to avoid a random zap of electricity that could jolt your hand at any moment. The game's illuminated LED base adds a thrilling glow, especially in low light, heightening the tension as the stack grows taller and more precarious. Race against the building buzz of anxiety-inducing sounds, but move too hastily and risk toppling the entire structure—or worse, steadying your shaking hand just enough to knock it over yourself.
Choose your shock level to match the mood: mild "Zip" for beginners, medium "Zap" for a tingle, or full-throttle "ShiZap!" for heart-pounding intensity. The shocks deliver a harmless but startling buzz, adjustable to your comfort, ensuring everyone feels the electrifying fun without real harm.
Whether playing solo to beat your personal best or in a group shouting encouragement (or laughter) as friends flinch, ShiZap! transforms the familiar dexterity test into a hilariously nerve-wracking party game.

Operation Brunete was an epic battle of the Spanish Civil War in July, 1937.

It would be the only large scale use of Russian tanks during the war. 
Over 100 T-26B tanks and armor cars would be used to break the stalemate setting in around Madrid. 
The goal of the offensive was to cutoff and isolate the city with two prongs: One from the west from the Brunete area and the other from forces south of Madrid. 
The plan for these two pincer movements would converge cutting off the Nationalist Franco forces that occupied areas near Madrid. 
The first 10 days would make or break this plan.

Game scale is one mile hexes. Turns are several hours. This game (unlike all others on the topic) covers the both attack pincers, one from the Brunete area, the other from Madrid.

Game comes with 280 counters and one 11x17" map, rules.

—description from the publisher

description:
In this "What if" WW2 game, a realistic simulation on the combined Japanese and German attack on the Panama Canal in May, 1941. 
Using the available and possible air units at the time,it is presumed that such an attack to disable the canal happened. 
It presumes that the u-boats sent to the US East Coast in December historically, were sent first to the Canal zone. 
Further, small amounts of Japanese aircraft were given to Peru at their request to be used in the attack and a small group of bombers were given to Ecuador, which historically had been requested. 
America was not at war with either country in May. 
The Canal zone had yet been reinforced as it was in July and afterwards. 
America defended with P26,P36, P40 and used B18 bombers to hunt u-boats. The radar stations were of little use. German air units include Ju87, He177, FW200, Ju52. Japanese units are Seiran, Zero, Flying boats, torpedo bombers.

Players use the Operational map and then a Tactical map for enemy air units to make there canal attacks ONE time and\or try to damage Gatun Dam, the key to the canal!  
U-boats try to sink merchant ships and perhaps, CV-Yorktown which was in the canal area moving from the Pacific to Atlantic.

Scale is single aircraft and units of five aircraft, single naval units, and AA batteries. 
Tactical map is 400 yds\hex, Operational map is 30 miles\hex with 30 min turns. 
One 11x17 map, 280 counters, Gatun Dam map.
