This is the most detailed game covering the epic northern Kursk prong to seize Ponyri during the Kursk offensive.

The game covers the carnage that began for both sides on July 7-9 after the Germans had broken through the 2nd defensive belt.

The battle saw both sides their heavy armor: for the Germans, they were the Ferdinand, Tiger, and Brummbar. 
For the Russians, their new Beast killer, the SU-152, and KV1, T-34. 
While the 3rd defensive belt was thinner, it still was armed to the teeth with antitank weapons. 
Both sides fought the other to death and after the 7th, the town of Ponyri was still contested. On the 8th, the Russians sent in their 3rd Tank Corps putting the Germans on the defensive. When the Germans resumed on the 9th, it was like hitting a wall of steel.

Each hex is 300 yds. Each turn 3-4 hrs. Units are company/battalion.

Included are air units like the Stuka. 
Random events may change everything in the battle while artillery, both off and on board remains the Queen of the Battlefield! 
Movement is slow going capturing the grind that this battle was at high loss. 
The Germans have the 18 Pz, 292, 86 to advance a mile or two, while the Russian seem to have endless units to toss in.

Upon the arrival of the 5th SS Wiking Division, a decision had to be made where and how to use it. 
Initially, it was used in the Russian 37th Army area but once that stablized, several options were looked at. 
Deploy some or all north of the Terek River where the Germans made their deepest penetration to Grozny against the 9th Army, or, south of the Terek in order to breakthrough near Malobek.

In this Alt-History game, some or all of the SS may be used north of the Terek since elements had already penetrated deep. Success depends on just how much fuel and SS have been committed.

Could the Germans have reached Grozny? Find out!

The game has one 11x17 inch map, 280 counters, rules. 
Fuel consumption is a key factor in this game and do you stay north of the Terek, or go south of it? or both?

With the SS arrival, German confidence rose and fuel and ammo were given to the SS for this purpose. The 13th and 23rd Panzer Divsions had already tried with little success from across the Terek River. 
The battle would involve over 100 tanks on a narrow front defended by T-34, M3 Lee, M3 Stuart and Valentine tanks from the Allies. 
Grozny was just 20 miles away and the SS with AFVs from 13th Panzer totalled over 100 tanks, which equalled the Russians waiting along with 50 antitank guns.

It was a face off battle!

Game scale is 1 km a hex, AFV units are 5-6 tanks, other units are battalions. Each turn is one day.

boardgame: Panzerwaffe: Death Rattle at Debrecen – Oct.1944, Hungary
description:
Panzerwaffe: Death Rattle at Debrecen, 1944 covers one of most forgotten major battles in October,1944, in Hungary.

The designer, Perry Moore, was the first to do the battle in 1984 for West End Games. Since then, very few other publishers have done it.

Using a totally new, innovative, game system not seen before, the battle captures the see-saw nature of this month long battle on the Hungarian plains! 
The Russian offensive attempted to two objectives: clear the largely flat plains to demolish the German 6th with its elite 3rd Panzer Corps of three panzer divisions defending the area south of the major city, Debrecen.  
Once the city was taken, Russian forces would strike even farther northwards to trap the retreating German 8th Army! 
It was a bold and daring plan. 
Over 300 tanks would fight over the area all month. The German 6th Army was weak, yet, a very deadly rattlesnake that managed to pocket the lead Russian forces until they broke out! 
After Debrecen fell, the already chaotic battle turned into a melee of armor with the German 6th Army trying to stall a Russian juggnernaut as the 8th Army entered the battle area from the east trying to escape annilation. They were in no condition to fight long and they crossed as the 6th Army tried to provide safe passage, which proved to be difficult. 
The game literally comes down to the wire hinging on how well or bad the escape is!

The Game: Three 11x17" maps, 280 counters, rules. 
Scale is company, battalion, brigade, 1.5 mile\hex. game is 10 turns.

Unique rules use a system that truly reflects the see-saw battle with Action phases and unit activations. 
Rules cover air use, armor ratings, HQ communication that impacts unit movement, troop quality, and more.

—description from the publisher

It was during this time the Russians north of Vyamza (this battle is depicted in Red Descent 1942) were in dire position (just like at Vyamza) as Russian forces busted through defenses west of Rzhev with the 29th and 39th Armies. 
The 29th was ordered to take the city, the 39th moved much further south to cut the rail road, so critical to German supply (it ran through Vyamza). The legacy of Avalon is written in stone and steel, with ink of blood. In this land of rich history and forgotten magic, you are both an architect of a rising empire and a ruthless warlord. Take command of one of four unique factions—from the disciplined knights of the Order of the Eagle's Claw to the fanatical Cult of the Forgotten. Your goal is absolute: to breach their walls, shatter their defenses, and bring their castle crashing down, ensuring that only your kingdom's legacy endures.
Will you forge an unbreachable fortress, unleash a tide of wild beasts, perfect the art of the siege, or pay a dark price for ultimate power? Your kingdom awaits.

GAMEPLAY OVERVIEW

Karciane Królestwa: Dziedzictwo Avalonu ("Card Kingdoms: Legacy of Avalon") is a card-driven engine-building and combat game. The game is played entirely with cards, with no dice or pawns.

Each turn, players perform a sequence of phases: they generate resources (Food, Wood, Stone, Gold) from the building cards they have in play, draw a new card, and then use their resources to perform actions. Actions include playing new cards from their hand, such as:

    Building Cards:These are placed in a player's kingdom (their tableau) and provide ongoing benefits, like generating more resources each turn.
    Unit Cards:These represent the player's army. They are played into an "army zone" and have Attack and Health stats.
    Technology Cards:These provide powerful, permanent passive bonuses.
    Action Cards:These are powerful one-time effects.


Players build and lead their armies by playing these unit cards. During the Combat Phase, a player can declare an attack against the player to their left. Combat is resolved by comparing the Attack stats of attacking units against the Health stats of defending units. Any unblocked damage is dealt directly to the opponent's Castle card.

A player wins the game immediately when they reduce their opponent's Castle's Health to zero.

boardgame: Wargames Rules of the American Civil War
description: This rule set provides miniature wargaming rules for the American Civil War. It includes mechanics suitable for both large-scale battles and smaller engagements, without specifying a particular miniature scale. The rules cover infantry, cavalry, artillery, and various auxiliary aspects of gameplay.

A mini-expansion pack containing 5 new patrons for Philharmonix, with rules in English, French and German.

It was given for free to backers of the Kickstarter-campaign.

A box containing all stretch goals from the Kickstarter-campaign, given for free to all backers and sold separately.

It contains:

     16 piano track bonus tokens
     8 screen-printed plaque tokens
     6 patron cards
     5 demand tiles
     8 musician reward tiles
     4 conductor standees
     1 region abilities mini-board
     1 screen printed round marker
     1 turn order tile
     3 rulebooks

You're a made cat in the Mischief Meowfia, vying for control of the family. The goal? Earn more Respect than your rivals by Pulling Jobs, Setting up a Fall Guy, and avoiding The Law.

Each turn, you’ll move through the house, interact with face-down Job Markers, and play action cards from your Mischief Deck. Successfully completing a job earns you Respect—but also adds Heat to the room, drawing the attention of The Law. If you’re the loudest cat in the wrong place at the wrong time, you’ll get Busted and lose everything you worked for.

But this isn’t just about pulling off crimes—it’s about pinning them on your rivals. Play cards like Set Up a Fall Guy to transfer Heat onto unsuspecting cats. Plant the Evidence, Lay Low, or even Stir Up Trouble to trigger a Law turn on your own terms. Timing is everything.

The Law is a neutral force that patrols the board, moving toward the noisiest rooms and busting the Cat with the most suspicious Job. Each decision you make—whether to act, hide, or frame—ripples across the board and shapes the flow of the game.

How to Win

The game ends when The Law’s deck runs out or all Jobs have been activated.

The Cat with the most Respect at the end becomes the new boss of the Meowfia.

—description from the designer

Legends of the Seas is a family-friendly, narrative-driven, cooperative game that takes you aboard a ship to experience adventures and battle pirates. You play as the shipmates of the Arrow! Amid the crash of the waves and the thunder of cannons, you are the last stand against the ocean and its armies. Take the helm, man the cannons, patch the hull, for only your bravery can save the ship and forge its legend!

At your side stand Ely the lookout, Oldor the boatswain, and Captain Elara. They are brought to life through the companion app. It is through them that events unfold: enemy attacks, shifting weather, and the consequences of your choices on the ship and on your story.

Players move around the ship, fixing and upgrading it, fending off attacks (or attacking others) and rescuing treasure, and try to complete story driven quests.  But be warned! If the hold or the helm of the Arrow is destroyed, you lose the game.

An AI app is required to play this game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Panzer Corridor: Rommel's Nightmare May 22, 1940
description:
The Allies had planned it yet it was never executed.

To cut off the panzer corridor and the German race to sea after its breakthrough by May 20th. 
Cutting off Rommel and others was inviting as their flanks were fragile until May 26 or so. 
If it failed, it would delay what historically happened. 
If a success, perhaps an entirely different outcome!

The Panzer Corridor explores this never been done before "what if". 
No fantasy here like Case Geld silliness. The game presumes that the Arras attack on the 21st was postponed until the 22nd when French forces scheduled to attack Cambrai were diverted to Arras. The Cambrai attack never happened. With plenty of tanks with heavy armor, the Germans would be at risk. From the Somme River, French forces with two reserve armor units would strike from Amiens area along with part of the British 1st Armor Division, that had just arrived. This was Rommel's nightmare even in the historical sense.

280 counters, one 11x17 inch map, rules, charts. Units are battalions/regiments, with 6 hr. turns.

French and British armor were more than a match for the panzers.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Get lost...

Paper Apps LABYRINTH is a first-person-perspective dungeon crawler that fits in your pocket. Each notebook is procedurally generated so there's a different adventure in every copy!

Solve the maze, battle enemies, get treasure, and then ... get out!

Quick features:

Jaw-dropping 3D graphics!
    Solo gameplay for ages 8+
    60 pages with 50 rooms over 2 levels
    Every copy contains a unique maze layout
    Randomized battles & encounters in each copy
    3x5" coil bound notebook (Pencil Dice fit perfectly)
    Printed in the USA

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Dawn of Pangaea: Wild Woods
description: The Wild Woods expansion perfectly complements the gameplay of The Dawn of Pangaea by adding an additional layer of — height! The 24 wooden treetops, 4 player boards and new rules increase the strategic possibilities and depth.

Arctic Wolves covers the German air and naval Operation Wunderland in the summer months in the arctic to intercept and destroy Allied supplies going to Russia from the Barents Straits and convoy PQ-18 to Murmansk. 
German intel had received information that 70,000 tons would be arriving on Russian transports via the Vilkitsky Straits. 
The Germans had available the Hipper, Scheer, Koln and six submarines to conduct the mission in the Arctic. The CV Zeppelin was nearly completed as well. 
The Allied lend lease program to Murmansk was in full swing as Operation Wunderland began and PQ-18 with British and American carriers were escorts. 
Optional rules include the use of the German CV Zeppelin.

Game scale is turns are two days, 45 mile\hex, naval units are a single ship, aircraft are squadrons. 
One 11x17 map, 280 counters.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In this quick game, you are attempting to brew more potions than any of your friends. You have the help of your charming cat companions, but they aren't exactly under your control – they follow the whims of the moon.

On your turn, you'll roll the cat dice and then the moon die to see which potions the cats will help with. Then you'll roll the ingredient dice and try to complete as many potions as possible. When all the Major and Minor potions have been completed, the game ends. The player with the most points wins.

With whimsical illustrations by Naomi Lord (and questionable yet intriguing potion ingredients), Purrfect Potions is a superb family game that you'll want to play again and again.

—description from the publisher

When humans aren’t looking, bathtubs become battlegrounds. You are the Captain of a rubber ducky fleet, sailing across the treacherous Tub waters. Collect soap bubbles to power up your duckies while hunting rivals. Up to six players battle in the Tub to become Top Ducky. May the best Captain win!

Each player gets three duckies that swim around the Tub, collect bubbles to build stats, and squirt water at other duckies to sink them.

The goal is to become the last player with duckies swimming around the Tub.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blitzkrieg: The Destruction of the Russian 12th Army at Riga, Sept.1917
description:
As the Germans approached Riga in Russia in late August, 1917, the lines stagnated with the Russian 12th Army. The Dvina River was 300m wide and presented a problem for the Germans. A plan was devised to concentrate breakthrough divisions and Stosstruppen, highly trained special forces. As forces gathered for the breakthrough operation, Russian units reinforced suspected river crossing sites. To enhance the chance for breakthrough, the Germans would use mustard gas and y and diversionary attacks. The Russian 12th Army was confident their line would hold behind the river and in the city of Riga.

What happened beginning on Sept. 1 was the start of the destruction of the 12th Army under the massive weight of the attack that was a precursor to the WW2 blitzkrieg!

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: Denied Access: Battle for the South China Sea
description:
Denied Access covers a probable future conflict for control of the South China Sea in a few years. 
Since 2014, China has been creating, expanding, its military bases in an area that is part of the Philippine's territorial waters. Most of the world's shipping lanes move through this key area and often is patrolled by the USN. China continues to warn ships entering it and transit at their own risk, as if they own it. By 2021, the Chinese atolls were armed with deadly ASMs and airbases for fighter aircraft. The Chinese warned the USN that their "carrier killer" missiles would sink a flattop. As the game shows via scenarios, the war could ignite quite suddenly because one of these days, the Chinese will fire on a ship ignoring its warnings.

The game is tactical with individual carriers and ships, submarines, 30 miles a hex, 4 hr. turns, aircraft units (each represent 4 AC). 
Aircraft include F-35, F-22, F-18, ECM, Bombers. 
Detection is key in the game.

One 11x17 inch map, charts and tables.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

boardgame: The Naval SITREP: The Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming #69
description:
The Naval SITREP is published twice a year by the Admiralty Trilogy Group. It supports the Admiralty Trilogy game system with scenarios, articles on history and technology, and rules expansions & corrections.

The Naval SITREP Issue #69 was published in October of 2025.

Table of Contents

Features

CaS Scenario: Convoy HG 76

DoB Scenario: Chile Crisis

Shuiqiao-Class Landing Barges

Trilogy Q & A: How We Came Up with Size Classes

The ALQ-248 Has Been Cancelled

The South China Sea Collision

Admiral Nakhimov Leaves Drydock

Matinee Turkey Shoot Follow-Up

China’s 2025 Victory Day Parade Showcase

The Philippine Navy, Air Force & Coast Guard, Annexes A - L

Product Updates

Rule Changes and Corrections

Trilogy Rules Change: Altitude Bands and Flying Real Low

Rule Change: Battery Mishap – New Battery Discharge Rates for

Harpoon V and CaS 5th ed

CaS 5th Rule Change: Aircraft Ready Times

Book Reviews

Devil Dogs, by Saul David

So Sad to Fall in Battle, by Kumiko Kakehashi

Book Non-Review

Cold War Antisubmarine Warfare, by Dr. Norman Friedman
family: Magazine: The Naval SITREP – Journal of Naval Miniatures Wargaming

description:
Comix Trip takes you on a cooperative game of hide-and-seek, right inside the pages of your favorite comics!

A game of limited communication, visual deduction, cooperation, and creativity... Play it in a graphical novel, manga, or comic book from your own library!

Choose a comic book and open it to a page with at least as many panels as there are players. Each player receives a card indicating which panel is theirs. Using the glyphs randomly assigned to them, players must recreate their panel as best they can so the other participants can identify it. If the editor-in-chief (who changes each round) succeeds, the round continues. If they fail, all players discard one of their glyphs and the round continues.

If no player runs out of glyphs before the end of the 5 rounds, you win the game.

In the card game Oh Ship!, play as a last-minute replacement merchant ship captain trying to quickly inventory, remember, and fill your new ship with cargo so you can be the first to set sail for distant shores. But beware, fill the wrong holds or set sail too early and your trip may be filled with expensive stowaways, junk, or even lazy crew instead of valuable merchandise!

Oh Ship! is a card game where players are dealt 4 cards to represent their ship, and will take turns drawing and playing cards to learn about and make changes to their ship's contents with the goal of setting sail before their opponents are ready. Rounds continue until one player plays an action to set sail where each player gets a final turn and scores are calculated. THe highest score wins the round.  The first player to win 5 rounds is the game winner.

—description from the designer

Long before the age of harvests and hunger, before the salt winds shaped the shores and the soil birthed its first sprout, there was Tuberia —
a realm of starch and sorcery.

In this land, every hill breathes with life, every root carries power. But at the heart of it all stands the mountain Tort Sierra, its peak shrouded in eternal steam and storm.
There, within sacred stone, lies the Spud of Power — a divine relic said to hold the very essence of creation.

The Potato Zeus, ancient god of balance and thunder, guards it with his legion of titans: beings forged from fire, earth, and starch itself. Under his gaze, the nations of Tuberia once lived in uneasy peace.

But peace cannot last forever.

When the first cracks appeared in the crust of the world, whispers spread like wildfire through the fields — “The Spud stirs again.”
And so, the nations rose.

From the smoldering furnaces of Forge Citielle marched the Iron-Skinned Spuds — warriors of flame and steel.
Across the golden dunes, the Desert Knolls gathered under the burning sun, swift and cunning as the wind.
Deep within the Forest of Sprouts, druids awoke ancient roots that could crush mountains.
Along the misty shores, the Herb Isles prayed to the sea and shaped storms in their cauldrons of salt.
And in Spud Valley, the humble farmers took up their tools, swearing to reclaim the world their ancestors built.

Each nation follows its Hero Potato — champions blessed (or cursed) by a power of their own:

     Strength, for those who stand unbroken.
     Magic, for those who bend nature’s laws.
     Corruption, for those who draw might from decay.
     Agility, for those who strike unseen.
     Speed, for those who move faster than fate itself.
     Mana, for those who command the flow of all starch-born energy.


Now, the battle for the mountain has begun.

—description from the publisher

Some people are only in it for the money, fame, or bragging rights. Some are after all three and they are normally very good at what they do. Enter: the veterans. This Death Derby expansion adds a new dimension to the game by upgrading your vehicle's driver and gunner.

—description from the designer

boardgame: North of Kursk: The Russian Kutuzov Offensive – July 12, 1943
description:
North of Kursk covers the dramatic Russian offensive just after the German Kursk offensive had failed on the 10th.
 Beginning on the 12th, the Russian offensive included five armies and six tank corps against a German force that had yet to see any Kursk action north of Orel. 
As the Germans were focused on the Kursk offensive and the German advance towards Ponyri, the Russians quietly prepared. When launched, the Kutuzov Offensive attacked from three different areas and by the 16th, the 11th Gds Army had made a serious hole in the far north while the 3rd Army had caused concern towards Orel from the east. The Germans would eventually shift five panzer divisions from the Kursk salient north to hold off the Russian breakthroughs! Even their heavy AFV units moved north to hold Orel at all costs!

Each turn is 1 day, 4 mile hexes, units are regiment\divisions. A unique combat system using unit proficiency and morale that determines its combat strength.

280 counters, 11x17 map.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Boutique Hotel Manager is an economic strategy game where players compete as owners of independent boutique hotels. Each player manages pricing, staffing, and guest satisfaction over a simulated two-week operating period. Decisions made in early rounds influence later demand, reputation, and profitability.

Each round represents a day of hotel operations, starting with players secretly setting daily room rates and allocating staff to key departments such as check-in, housekeeping, and breakfast service. Guests arrive with specific requirements, budgets, and flexibility levels. They book rooms with the hotels that best meet their expectations at the lowest qualifying price, introducing an element of simultaneous bidding and market competition.

During the rating phase, hotels are evaluated based on how well they fulfill service commitments. Overbooking or understaffing reduces reputation, while consistent performance earns rating stars and repeat business. Players can also invest in room expansions to accommodate higher demand in later rounds.

After two weeks of simulated operations, the player with the highest cash balance wins. In the event of a tie, overall hotel rating determines the winner. The game emphasizes economic optimization, market timing, and resource allocation under uncertainty—rewarding players who can anticipate demand while maintaining service excellence.

Resort Hotel Manager is a competitive bidding / auction game with a hotel theme. Players act as resort hotel operators who bid to win guest booking requests, balancing budget, room class, and flexibility demands.

Each round, a request card is revealed showing a guest’s budget, minimum hotel rating, and optionally flexibility requirements. Players may (a) make an offer matching or under the budget, (b) outbid an open offer by another player, (c) peek at future request cards, (d) shuffle their offer cards, or (e) close a deal if they currently hold the winning bid.  ￼ Offers consist of an accommodation (Villa, Suite, or Room), a price, and optional flexibility.

If a deal is closed, the offering player places the cards used for that deal onto the request and keeps them face up. If all a player’s accommodation cards are already engaged but they hold a winning open offer, they must close one deal. When a “cancellation” request is flipped, all open deals requiring flexibility are cancelled and cards returned; any closed flexible requests are also voided.

Goal: Over the course of the game, players attempt to win the most money by making optimal offers—winning requests at profitable prices while avoiding overbidding or being outbid. The player with the highest total earnings wins.

boardgame: Red Descent: The Air and Ground Assault for Vyamza 1942
description:
Red Descent covers the epic large paratroop drop and ground offensive by the Russians in Feb. 1942.

It would be one of the largest use of paratroops to drop far behind front lines and cutoff the key German held city of Vyamza, a hub for German supply. 
Once dropped, the Russian offensive began against an already weakened German force. Within days, the Russians managed to breakthrough to within a few miles of Vyamza only to be pushed back. The chaotic and seesaw battles were difficult for both sides in winter. As time wore on, the stalled Russian offensive struck again from a different direction cutting the main German supply route. For either side, victory was always a step away.

This game has two 11x17" maps and 280 counters, rulebook.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Flames of Orion is a dark future miniatures war game where players pilot giant mechanized combat machines, set in the Orion system. Renegades, outlaws, and mercenaries litter the sector. Years have passed, and the planets in this sector sit heavy with shadows and foreboding.

Players will control a Combat Unit consisting of 4 mechs and battle over the remnants of fading planets. Systems overheating, missiles flying and engines going critical. Fast paced, deadly and a core system designed for campaign play. Grab your set of favorite battle mechs, or make your own in this model agnostic game.

Flames of Orion is a turn-based skirmish game with alternating activation. The games main focus is the customization of your units, deadly combat and the tension building HEAT system. 
The HEAT system is designed to put you on the edge of your seat as you make your way across the battlefield. As you perform actions, you gain HEAT. Every action pushing you closer to oblivion. If you aren't careful you can overheat, causing complete mayhem across the board. When you reach your models HEAT threshold, you have a chance of exploding, leaving a cascade of destruction in your wake.

At the end of every round, you have a chance to gain additional HEAT. Due to the state of your ramshackle machines & the stress of the situation. You must balance your turn between action and inaction, safety or harms way. Everything dancing around the edge of overheating and leaving a smoking crater.

WHAT YOU NEED
All you need to play a game of Flames of Orion are the following;

     4 models per player.
     A handful of 6-sided dice.
     A measuring device.
     A 2x2’ or bigger play area.
     The rules.


—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Chuồn Chuồn (pronounced “choon choon”, meaning dragonfly) draws inspiration from Vietnam’s traditional balancing dragonfly toys, transforming them from decorative pieces into a playful light strategy game. Suitable for all ages, it offers endless replayability, magical twists, and a charming reflection of dragonflies in nature.

Handcrafted Components With Vietnamese Cultural Charm.
Our dragonflies are made of bamboo, an eco-friendly and durable material that’s a well-known symbol for the Vietnamese spirit.

Easy to Learn, Challenging to Master!

Your goal is to catch and arrange dragonflies on your bamboo trees to match the patterns on your objective cards. Once completed, you earn flower points, that will be counted at the end of the game. On your turn, you have the choice to pick one big dragonfly or 2 small dragonflies, or new objective cards, or new magic cards. Magic cards allow you to remove or rearrange your dragonflies like sunshine forcing all dragonflies move up 1 level, or thunderstorm knock them off the table. You win by earning as many points as possibles!

The game has Three Play Modes

     Competitive: 2-4 players
     Cooperative: 2-8 players
     Solo mode for 1 player


—description from the publisher

Everyone has 7 cards in their hands. 
Draw and discard a card to match the king's whims.
Each turn, a new whim is added to the objectives!
Use the effects of the 9 castes to optimize your hand…
Meet the right objectives to score the most points!
Games ends after 6 turns. To win, you'll have to adapt!

In The Dunwich Horror, you relive H. P. Lovecraft’s classic tale through the fast-paced and tense Dice Quest system. This dice management game uses just 55 cards and 6 dice to plunge you into a story of cosmic horror where every roll shapes your fate. Can you stop the madness spreading through Dunwich before it’s too late?

In Dice Quest: The Dunwich Horror, players venture into the cursed town of Dunwich, exploring mysterious locations through a dice management and resource optimization system. Each turn, you’ll roll and assign dice to investigate, gather clues, and prepare for the final confrontation. As the ritual progresses, tension rises and time grows short — you must uncover the exact site of the ceremony and stop the cult before the ancient horror is unleashed.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dice Quest: The Tower of the Elephant
description:
In Dice Quest: The Tower of the Elephant, you dive into a legendary sword and sorcery tale inspired by Robert E. Howard’s classic story. With just 55 cards and a few dice, you’ll climb the tower, face deadly traps, and battle supernatural creatures while managing your dice to survive each challenge.

In Dice Quest: The Tower of the Elephant, you embark on a daring adventure of exploration and dice management inspired by classic sword and sorcery tales. You’ll travel through perilous locations, facing guards, traps, and strange creatures as you make your way toward the mysterious tower. Along the journey, you’ll recruit allies, gather powerful items, and train your abilities to prepare for the final challenge. Your ultimate goal: reach the top and steal the wizard’s gem before he vanishes with it forever.

—description from the publisher

In Toy Store 51, you lead a squad of plush toys defending the shop where alien experiments once took place. Now the creatures are trying to escape, and your toys are the last line of defense. A fast deck-building game filled with action and a touch of sci-fi.

In Toy Store 51, waves of aliens break loose each round, threatening to escape into the outside world. Using a deck-building mechanic, your squad of plush defenders must fight them off, upgrade their gear, and strengthen their deck by replacing cards strategically. Each turn, the alien horde grows stronger — you’ll need to act fast and coordinate your toys to stop the invasion before they tear down the doors of the toy store.

—description from the publisher

Be the fastest, the cleverest and collect all the dice in record time in a crazy atmosphere...
Throw, Catch, Win!
Who will be the DÉS DINGUES QUEEN or KING?
Roll the dice to determine your "game number". Altogether, throw your dice in the middle of the table and quickly grab as many dice as you can, whatever colour they are! The Crazy Dice King will be the one with the most dice in his or her kitty.

Khamzat is a war of attrition board game for 2 players using various sided dice to represent the players units on a chess board. It places emphasis on tactical positioning & flanking as well as risk taking and smart resource management.

—description from the designer

In Stamp Showdown, you’re battling for the win of a high-profile stamp competition. Each round, all players secretly choose a stamp to trade with the market, then reveal together. In ranking order, each trade reshapes the market by pulling in all matching suits and ranks before joining it. Will you strengthen your own collection, or block your opponents at the perfect moment? After seven tense rounds, the final showdown begins, and only the most dazzling poker-style stamp collection will win!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flying Fortresses: B-17 Missions Over Europe
description:
Flying Fortresses: B-17 Missions Over Europe puts you and your friends in the cockpit of legendary B-17 Flying Fortress bombers during World War II.

Gameplay Highlights
1–3 Players – Fly solo or as part of a squadron. Work together to survive or see who can score the most medals and mission success.

Dynamic AI BOT – Axis defenses are automated, keeping you on your toes with unpredictable flak, fighters, weather, and hazards.

Fighters are placed face down on the board according to the scenario setup. As your Flying Fortress (referred to as “your Fort”) enters an Air Zone, defined as any space within the white grid, those fighters are flipped face up. Based on a die roll, some may become activated and are then able to move according to their rules.

Tactical Choices – Plan your route, manage altitude, and position your gunners to survive relentless opposition.
Historical Immersion – Each mission echoes the real challenges faced by Allied crews in the 1943–45 bombing campaigns over Europe.

Plus, play in "Character Mode" and increase the proficiency of your crew as they fly multiple missions. Each successful mission you complete your gunners will increase their aim and the flyboys will become even better at avoiding the hazards of the air.

—description from the designer

"Al Final" Card: At the start of the round set aside a card from your hand, face down. Solo players set aside two. These cards MUST be played in the final trick.

The card was obtainable through the "Tantrum House Board Game Media: Season 11" Kickstarter campaign.

Dusk is gathering, and the air is trembling with a hidden threat. The world around you is teeming with mysterious creatures, and only the sharpest and fastest hunter will be able to distinguish the real undead from a harmless doppelganger! Together with the fearless Sergius, you go on a dangerous hunt. Your eyes are vigilantly searching for true monsters among the ghostly faces, and your hand is reaching for the right weapon. Are you ready to grab the cross and fight back against evil spirits, or would you prefer to wait out the attack in an old hut? Be quick and decisive, otherwise the dark forces will take away the last drops of living water from you...

Сергий против нечисти ("Sergius versus Evil Spirits") is a fast-paced game of dexterity and speed matching. Your task is to be the first to recognize the type of evil spirits that have appeared and react with lightning speed: grab the right weapon, take shelter in a hut or protect precious living water. The game will not let you relax for a second, testing your attractiveness and reaction!

In the box you will find everything you need to fight the insidious evil spirits. And fast games, tension and fun excitement make this game the perfect choice for a noisy group of players eager for thrills and unforgettable emotions!

How to play? The excitement of hunting begins with three cards open in the center of the table (if there are two Baba Yagi among them at once, the hunt is considered thwarted, and the cards are changed), and one secret on each player's hand. At a signal, everyone reveals their cards at the same time, and your eyes must assess the overall picture in a split second: find combinations of two or more identical monsters or five different ones among the many images, which marks the beginning of the sabbath! Not only speed is important here, but also the right decision: each evil spirit has its own weapon. Vampires are driven away with a stake, ghosts with a cross, a kikimore with a sword, and a woodman with a staff. If a sabbath is gathered on the table, the only salvation is to be the first to hide in the hut.

Your goal is to do the right thing faster than others.: grab the right weapon or put your hand on the hut. But remember: grabbing the wrong weapon or hiding in the hut with the second one, you risk losing precious living water...

Who won? The game ends when there are no cards left in the deck to continue or someone runs out of water. You count your trophies: each card of the defeated evil spirits earns you one victory point. The blue tokens of living water that you managed to save add two more points each, and the red ones add one more.

The one whose stack of trophies and saved living water turns out to be the most impressive is declared the main defender from the forces of darkness!

Choosing one of fifteen difficulty levels, players help each other meet the requirements to prevent each of the six labs from melting down by playing cards to gain beaker tokens with positive and negative values to balance volatile chemicals, while placing their meeples in the different sections of the central hub to activate bonus actions.

Each player is assigned a planet that has a list of eight things it wants (moons, aliens, etc.) and take turns drawing a card from the draw deck, playing a card they need in a grid around their planet, or striking an opponent's universe with asteroids to remove their cards. The first person to finish their universe wins.

Judgy Fish by Brass monkey is the ultimate party game for adults who prefer their fish served with a side of sass! Say goodbye to boring old "Go Fish" and hello to a hilariously competitive game where players compete to craft the best (aka, funniest) insults imaginable for hundreds of overly-critical fish. These aquatic critics are hell-bent on mercilessly judging everything in sight, and it's up to you to bring the laughter! From cheeky quips to downright savage burns, it's a battle of wits where no one is safe from the sharp tongues of the Judgy Fish.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: World of Tanks Miniatures Game: Soviet – T62A
description:
Capable of running rings around slower tanks, the Soviet T-62A is the top-of-the-line configuration of all things Medium Tank – strong Survivability and Mobility combined with top-tier Firepower and Initiative. Maximise the high Initiavtive of the T-62A by taking Maria Boyko and Lend-lease Oil, and boost the rest of your platoon with Signal Boosting.

Inside the T-54 Expansion Pack, you will find a highly detailed, assembled, pre-primed plastic T-54 tank miniature and tank card. Your tank can be further personalised to match your requirements using the five crew, module, and upgrade cards included.

—description from the publisher

In this do-not-follow battle between superhero teams, players must each play a different group of heroes from one of eight suits, with the largest team winning the trick, until someone has won three battles.

boardgame: Texas: Card Games Inspired by the Lone Star State
description:
Texas: Card Games - Inspired by the Lone Star State is an illustrated book of six modern games for fans of card games, board games, and the Lone Star State.

All you need is a deck of cards to scurry for grub as an armadillo, pick out constellations in the sky, race across Texas as a roadrunner, and more. Created by San Antonio high school sweethearts as an ode to the natural beauty and warmth of the Lone Star State, these six games are a great way to dive into a world of fast and fun games with a familiar set of components:


    Roadrunner — Card shedding, 3-5 players
    Armadillo — Set collection, 2-8 players
    Prickly Pear — Drafting, 1-3 players
    Bluebonnets — Tile laying, 1-2 players
    Echolocation — Trick taking, 4 players
    The Stars at Night — Area control, 3-4 players


—description from the publisher

Players decide who will be Owls and who'll be Kittens, taking that respective deck. The Owl player goes first, placing a card in the center of an imaginary grid. The goal is to make three-in-a-row of one's chosen animal, allowing the person to collect every card on the table, ending the round. When both players run out of cards, the person who has collected the most wins.

Players get a hand of nine cards and take turns drawing from the deck in an attempt to gain an animal that matches one in their hand, which allows them to play the pair into their area. The goal is to be the first to empty one's hand, granting one 4 points, with other players getting one less point in the order they run out of cards. Several rounds are played until someone gets 10 points.

This promo card was available at different events to promote the game launch.

Place adjacent to any card with petting zoo icon. Scores two points per directly adjacent petting zoo icon at the end of the game.

Each player is dealt a hand of three cards with a Permit card being placed face-up, which puts a limit on how many people can be invited to the party. Players take turns playing a character card (each with a numerical value) to the same row on the table. When someone has no option but to play a card that causes the sum total of the row to exceed the permit, they're removed from the game, and a new round begins, with a new permit being drawn. The last person still in the game wins.

Each player is given a row of five icebergs, each with a numerical value that determines how many penguins it can hold. Players take turns drawing three cards and placing a blue penguin onto their icebergs, or a black penguin onto an opponent's iceberg. If the number of penguins exceeds the iceberg's limit, the iceberg flips over and all the penguins are discarded. The last player with an inhabited iceberg wins.

Each player begins with a hand of $250 of -opoly money and take turns drawing from the deck. If they draw a Property card, they place it face-up to show ownership, then on a subsequent turn gives that card to another player in order to charge them rent; if they are unable to pay rent, they must hand over a property they own or are out of the game. The goal is to make all one's opponents go bankrupt or be the person with the most property and money when the deck runs out.

Players decide who will take the blue deck and who'll take the pink, then arrange their respective decks in a 7x3 grid face-up, and each draws one card from the Secret Friend deck. Play then consists of each person drawing a card from the Question deck, which determines what kind of question each can ask the other, such as, "does your friend wear a hat?" until players are confident they can identify the Secret Friend. The first to do so correctly gets 5 points, with multiple rounds being played until someone gets 15.

The deck of cards is arranged in a grid of 6x11 face-up and players take turns flipping a coin to determine if they move one or two moves that turn, a move consisting of swapping one card with another in order to create a chain of cards of the same type (lanterns, skeletons, etc.) thus collecting those cards. When all cards have been collected or no more legal moves are possible, the person with the highest score wins.

Dindex is our card game for 2 to 7 players, designed above all to be fun. You slip into the role of political parties and play as democrats or oligarchs. Your role remains secret.

As democrats, you try to get into government in order to earn government points and win the game. Without a lot of votes, there's nothing you can do. The temptation to become populist in order to manipulate voters is great.

The oligarchs also want to come to power. But they are not interested in government points, but in the abolition of democracy. Therefore, they must be careful not to be found out too quickly.

If you play anti-democratically, the democracy index (or “Dindex” for short) drops and increasingly harsh action cards can be played. If the democrats are not careful, the game will be over quickly.

We'll leave the moralizing aside, by the way. You'll see for yourselves what populism does to democracy. Slip into your roles and enjoy the game.

Our board games are made of wood. This is rather impractical for playing cards, so we opted for cardboard. But proverbially speaking, Dindex is really “harder than wood”!

—description from the publisher (Translated)

Dindex ist unser Kartenspiel für 2 bis 7 Spieler, das vor allem Spaß machen soll. Ihr schlüpft in die Rolle von Parteien und spielt Demokraten oder Oligarchen. Eure Rolle bleibt geheim.

Als Demokraten versucht Ihr, an die Regierung zu kommen, um Regierungspunkte zu erhalten und so das Spiel zu gewinnen. Ohne viele Wahlstimmen ist da nichts zu machen. Die Verlockung ist groß, populistisch zu werden, um die Wähler zu manipulieren.

Die Oligarchen wollen auch an die Regierung kommen. Aber ihnen geht es nicht um Regierungspunkte, sondern um die Abschaffung der Demokratie. Daher müssen sie achtgeben, dass man ihnen nicht zu schnell auf die Schliche kommt.

Wenn Ihr antidemokratisch spielt, sinkt der Demokratie-Index (kurz "Dindex") und immer härtere Aktionskarten können gespielt werden. Wenn die Demokraten nicht aufpassen, ist das Spiel rasch vorbei.

Die moralische Keule lassen wir im Übrigen beiseite. Ihr werdet schon von selbst merken, was der Populismus mit der Demokratie anrichtet. Schlüpft in Eure Rolle und genießt das Spiel.

Unsere Brettspiele sind aus Holz. Für Spielkarten ist das eher unpraktisch, sodass wir uns für Karton entschieden haben. Sprichwörtlich aber ist Dindex wirklich „nicht von Pappe“!

—description from the publisher

description:
Set in the intricate World of Erra, where ancient civilisations, diesel-punk machinery, and cybernetic evolution coexist, five ancient races once lived in harmony — until their pursuit of technological perfection led to the creation of the Arch-Intelligence, a self-aware entity that turned against its makers. From deep within its network core, it commands ground armies of reanimated hybrids known as AI Zombies — living beings whose minds have been overwritten by neuro-links. The AI also commands aerial units of Drones and Nanoclouds.

Players take on the roles of elite Agents of the Resistance, each belonging to a different race and class with unique abilities. Together, they must infiltrate enemy-controlled zones, complete missions, and collect four fragments of the Key needed to breach the Ultimate Intellect’s bunker. Inside lies the final objective — to plant and detonate the K-800M tactical explosive, ending the Arch-Intelligence’s reign before all life on Erra is consumed.

The game blends cooperative strategy, tactical planning, and resource management. Players explore randomly arranged map locations, dig underground tunnels, upgrade their stats, and face randomised Events — from AI Drone assaults to devastating twists of fate. Every turn tests teamwork and coordination, for without unity, the Resistance cannot survive.

Victory comes only when the Resistance successfully reaches the Ultimate Intellect’s Bunker and detonates the K-800M inside before the Event Deck is depleted — but the deeper they go, the more relentless the enemy becomes. The fate of the World of Erra lies in your hands.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Forge Knights: A Mecha Skirmish Wargame
description:
Forge Knights is a mecha themed skirmish game, it is a redesign of my previous title: In Shadow of Titans.

Forge Knights has a streamlined reaction system, more engaging opposed rolls, more tactical options and an easier to use customization system.

Features:

     Highly reactive gameplay: you are always in the game
     Tactical command and control: every action is a resource, each decision matters
     Fast Outcomes: no extensive flowcharts to resolve actions
     Small Footprint: Low model count and terrain requirements make getting to the table easy


Force size is ~8x 40mm models with each model needing a small profile page that can easily be printed.

—description from the publisher

Puzzometry Hex comes with a frame and 12 pieces.

2-Player Puzzometry Keepout:

     Spread out all the pieces on the table.
     Each player takes turns selecting one piece at a time.
     When all pieces have been picked the game begins.
     The player who selected the second piece goes first. He takes one of his pieces and places it anywhere inside the frame such that at least two edges of his piece mate with the frame.
     The other player then places any of her pieces anywhere inside the frame such that her piece also has at least 2 edges mating with edges of the frame OR a previous placed piece.
     The players alternate moves until a player can no longer fit one of their pieces into the frame.
     The winner is the last player who is able to successfully fit a piece inside the frame.


The game can also be used as a puzzle with the goal to fit all the pieces in the frame.

Puzzometry Steps comes with a frame and 13 pieces.

2-Player Puzzometry Keepout:

     Spread out all the pieces on the table.
     Each player takes turns selecting one piece at a time.
     When all pieces have been picked the game begins.
     The player who selected the second piece goes first. He takes one of his pieces and places it anywhere inside the frame such that at least two edges of his piece mate with the frame.
     The other player then places any of her pieces anywhere inside the frame such that her piece also has at least 2 edges mating with edges of the frame OR a previous placed piece.
     The players alternate moves until a player can no longer fit one of their pieces into the frame.
     The winner is the last player who is able to successfully fit a piece  inside the frame.


The game can also be used as a puzzle with the goal to fit all the pieces in the frame.

Countless colorful gemstones glitter on the seabed. Help Shelly 
the mermaid and her friends make their treasures sparkle in their favorite color. However, the rainbow creatures guard the precious gemstones, and they will only give you one if the color of the character you visit them with matches the color of the gemstone they are guarding. Find the right creature, then visit it in the sea. Who will be the first mermaid to decorate their treasure with five gemstones in their chosen color?

—description from the publisher
family: Components: Game Box Used In Play

description:
Move fast, make matches, and get ready to scream!

Row Your Goat is a rapid-fire picture-matching, fast slapping game. Get rid of your cards by placing them in a grid. The second you see a matching set of 3 in a row, slap the nearest Lifesaver. Hurry, there's only a few of them. And if you don't grab one fast enough, you're left collecting cards. When you're down to one card, scream “ROW MY GOAT”, play your card, and slap a Lifesaver to win.

It's fast, frantic, and fantastic as a party game, kid game, or a showstopper for family game night.

—description from the publisher

Peter Piper is a quick and clever 4-player card game about color and number balance.
Players play cards to their tableau, triggering swaps with cards of the same color but exactly 1 higher in value. By timing these exchanges carefully, players can disrupt others while trying to reach a total value of exactly 11 in front of themselves.
With all 24 cards (0–5 in four colors) dealt at the start, every move reveals information and affects everyone else. The challenge is to anticipate what your rivals are aiming for and prepare your hand accordingly — the winner is the one who ends their turn perfectly at 11!
Designed for quick yet thoughtful play, Peter Piper offers a satisfying mix of timing, observation, and subtle interference.

—description from the publisher

A hot summer day and the ice cream truck has arrived! All the friends gather, eager for a tasty cone. Which compartments hide their favorite flavors? And will there even be strawberries or rainbow cream on top? Who will lick first?

In the ice cream truck, the ice cream and toppings are hidden in three compartments. Each child gets a cone to fill with scoops and toppings. A color die determines which flavor to look for. The child then opens a lid where they think the flavor is hidden. If it’s there, they may place the scoop or topping on their cone. Pay close attention! Whoever fills their cone first gets to lick it and wins.

Two game variations provide playful fun for little sweet tooths.

—description from the publisher

Here's Śpiewać każdy może ("Everyone Can Sing") – a game that will get any party started! Full of unforgettable hits, singing, and dancing, it'll keep even the most reluctant listeners on their toes. Guess the titles, finish the songs, move your hips… It's not just a game, it's a musical show in your living room!

Ready? Let's go!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Schlau & Fix: Auf die Wörter, fertig, los
description: The players use dice in different colours, each colour represents a group of letters, the eyes a letter within the group. They collect letters and try to form as many words with them as possible. The player who found the most words wins.

Glückswurf is a party dice game with two different variants. The players roll the dice and collect their points. They receive lucky tokens in exchange for either noting the points or winning the most games. They can exchange the tokens for little prizes (not included in the game).

The central element of the game is a wheel with sections in different colours. At their turn, the players roll the colour die and proceed to the respective section. They can either continue rolling the die or stop and collect their win (depending on how far they've come).

If they throw the black colour, their turn ends immediately and they didn't win any money.

The Forests of Death shroud the mountains above Thornglove, casting ominous shadows over the village. Few dare enter, fearing certain death at the hands of its ruling Deities. Yet, legend tells that those who brave their torture earn glory and immortality. Will you impress the Deities of Death enough to claim your place among them, or will your soul be trapped forever?

Gameplay
In this strategic card game for 1–3 players, you’ll grow and explore your own deadly forest while competing to gain divine favour. On your turn, place a card to expand your forest and move your pawn through discovered terrain. Forest cards are layered on top of one another in a puzzle-like fashion. As you travel through your forest, you may gain favour from the forest deities – every forest you enter has the potential to score the points of a deity of that forest type. Gather enough favour from the forest deities, and you may face the final boss: the Deity of Death herself.

Cards can be used to expand your forest, score deity points, or sacrificed to fuel additional movement (potentially giving other players their desired cards) - every choice matters. Both points and timing are crucial: you are attempting to earn deity points before the deck runs out for the second time, and within two rounds of your rivals’ success.

Two players try to colonize planets far away from their respective home planet. To achieve this, they use energy and so-called "Knullis" (figures). The players choose a flight route, each of the routes require a different consumption of energy. They can restock on energy on the space bases - but they can only restock if their tank is completely empty.

It is important that the players focus on clever energy management.

The player who colonizes 2 of the 3 colonialization planets wins.

A New Star Is Born

The Drop Drive universe is expanding. Second Sun brings bold new challenges, deeper strategy, and thrilling ways to play—solo, cooperatively, or in classic competitive mode.

Whether you're defending against planetary invasions, hiring a crew to power your ship, or taking on high-stakes missions, Second Sun transforms Drop Drive into an even more dynamic and replayable experience.

—description from the publisher

The players start their adventure at the Pacific coast. At the turn, the player rolls the die and travels accordingly, then takes the respective travel card and takes action.

The player who arrives at Lima airport first wins.

Walter Ulbricht and Erich Honecker were involved in a quiet but decisive power struggle within the Socialist Unity Party of Germany (SED) that defined East German leadership in the late 1960s and early 1970s. It wasn’t a “public” fight in the typical Western political sense; rather, it was very much a controlled internal succession conflict, centered on generational change, ideological tensions and Soviet favor.

The card game Erich & Walter (2025) explores this muted political rivalry between Erich Honecker and Walter Ulbricht, as they struggle for control and leadership of the SED and the East Democratic Republic (GDR). Players navigate the shifting domains of the GDR’s public, the Party’s Politburo, and Soviet favor, balancing ideology, influence, and intrigue to outmaneuver each other and seize leadership of East Germany.

The game supports two primary modes of play, each offering a distinct approach to strategy and tension. Before starting, players must agree on which mode to use. The two game modes are fundamentally similar, with the only difference being how players acquire cards—either by purchasing from the Intershop market in Normal Mode, or by drawing directly from the deck in the Stasi-Variant.

Normal mode offers more open strategic planning and transparency, while the Stasi-Variant introduces greater secrecy and unpredictability, favoring hidden tactics and bluffing.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Fritz Flunker: oder – ich glaub, mich knutscht ein Elch!
description:
At their turn, the player rolls the black die and moves their pawn. According to the colour of the field they reach, the mini challenge begins: drawing/mimic/true or untrue/party game.

The players win "fibbing tokens" for their mini challenges. For each completed round (i.e. crossing the start/finish line) they receive tokens as well.

The player with the most fibbing tokens after 3 rounds wins.

A collection of games for children to learn about traffic safety. 
The box includes a number of different games, eg.
- Color cube game for 2 players: One player takes the parts of the roadway with the red point, the other one the ones with the green point. The players take turn rolling the dice. Whenever a player roles his colour, he is allowed to take a part of the roadway. Who by accident put a raodway part on the sidewalk, has to remove it. The player who is the first one in putting all his parts on the table, wins. 
- Hidden pulling: All parts are put face down on the table. Every player decides for a color and when he manages to get a part with his colour, he puts it on the table. The player who is the first one in putting all his parts on the table, wins.

In each round, one player is the game master. The game master rolls the die, moves the pawn accordingly, and draws a card of the respective category. The other players try to solve the challenge of the card. If they are successful, they move their pawn towards 0.

The six categories are: opposite-drawing, reverse-writing, treasure hunter, miscalculation, mime artist, colours.

In the next round, the next player is the game master.

The first player to reach 0 with their meeple wins.

The board game can be combined with the GegenteilSpiel card game.

Also known as Kvitlakh, Kwittlech, Quittlach, Quitklock and Quitli, Kvitlach is a variant of vingt-et-un associated with the European Ashkenazi Jewish Orthodox community, but also known to the Jewish community more widely but less so following the Holocaust.

It has its own distinctive deck of 24 cards, comprising two runs of 1-12 with the number 2s and 11s marked by frames. Frequently two decks are combined, allowing for a common configuration of 5-6 players.

The most long-running of these printed decks was Piatnik's Quitli Karten 17, but the deck has been published as recently as 2025 by Counter Clockwise.

Traditionally the game is played on Chanukah alongside other festival favourites including Hammer and Bell, and Dreidel.

One player is selected to be the banker, by single cards being dealt to the players and the highest becoming banker. A minimum bet is agreed. The banker deals single cards to each player, who then consult this card. The first player to the left of the banker begins and may decide to put in a bet up to the agreed maximum. The bank matches the bet, and the player's card is kept face down. There are three options for the player: (1) 'blot', receive an extra card face up; (2) 'bet', place a further matched bet for a face down card and; (3) 'stay', play with the dealt cards and play passes to the left. The dealer is the last to flip their cards.

The aim is to hit 21 precisely, going bust if you overstep.

The game distinguishes itself from vingt-et-un in the following ways:


     The 12 card: A player or banker can at any time determine the value of the 12 card as a 12, 10, or 9.
     The 2 Frame Cards. If the framed cards are dealt (that is any combination of 2 and 11s as marked by the special deck), this is considered an automatic 21.
     If a player's (not the bankers') combined total is 11, they may declare 'aluvun'. If the next dealt card would have caused them to have gone bust, then this card may be binned, but 'aluvun' may not be declared a second time.


Once all players have busted or stayed, the banker than deals their own cards face up. If they bust all players have their bets matched, otherwise only those beating the banker are paid out. A banker remains in place for three hands which proceed clockwise, anti-clockwise, clockwise.
Players must get rid of all their cards, and have three flop cards down. The player with the lowest total of trump and flop cards wins the game!

Play as people in a real-life neighborhood, working together to survive crisis. A printable, cooperative, real-time dice placement game designed to introduce players to basic community disaster preparedness concepts.

In the real-time phase, players roll a dice pool and place dice on slots (with options to re-roll). Then, in the resolution phase, players take turns resolving those slots, triggering helpful effects for themselves and/or others. Finally, the dice that remained in the pool trigger negative effects (the Crisis), causing players to spend resources and take damage and/or harm.

Players lose if their town takes too much damage (the Aftermath track), or if all players are eliminated due to taking too much harm. Players win by surviving eight rounds.

—description from the publisher

Free expansion offered at Essen 2025 with the purchase of Winning Ticket!
This version is played with the usual game components, but this time you’re not a bettor — you’re a jockey!
Choose your horse before each race and try to win the Golden Whip!

—description from the publisher

Stella Polaris is a cooperative game where the players aim to solve a case that is part of the late detective Yngve Ek's legacy. The detective agency was based in Uppsala and active from the late 1920s up until the detectives passing in 1977. The game's design aimes to mirror its location and setting in time.

”Stella Polaris” is the first case out of several that are being written and developed by Vintage Mysteries. The game includes interview protocols, newspaper cuttings and other evidence material to help the players follow in the tracks of the detective and discover the truth about the murder of singer Sonja Adler in 1944. The case has a connection with a dramatic episode of swedish history.

—description from the publisher

Peter Pig games always cover a narrow period in order to capture that period. Thus the most important aspects of that war can be modelled and the less important ones piled into the general mechanisms. The Mexican revolution 1910 -1920

Game setting
Army versus army defence/attack games.

Typical army composition
Most PP games use about 100 figures a side. enough for PP to make some big sales and not too many to paint.
Typical per army= 4 Regiments  each of 11 bases. 3 MMGs. Pair of field guns.  General.

Unit motivation mechanism
3 motivations per unit commander.
He chooses 3 squares containing parts of his unit.
Rolls a D6 and that is the squares action allowance.
With officer= owner re-rolls
In proximity= as it falls
More than proximity = roll, but opponent can demand a re-roll.

Action/moving mechanism
Something simple that can be memorised. PP rules give a move distance worth having.
Bases in a square can move together. Action point cost depends on scenery type.

Shooting mechanism
Shooting takes into account amount , skill and modifiers but ends up with a bunch of D6.  Saving roll gets both players engaged. 1D6 per base. Shooting can result in hammering target. Return shooting and opportunity shooting too.

Close assault mechanism
PP fight mechanism only consider about 7 modifiers. But these are the important ones for that period.
Another reason for keeping the period span small.
Then a pile of D6. 6 = kill. No saves. More decisive than shooting.

 Morale mechanism
A pair of D6.   
9 or more=all Ok.
Less than 9 the unit risks loses bases.
Raw units degenerate quicker.

—description from the publisher

Expansion for Sidereal Confluence, just requires the base game but works with Bifurcation.

Adds 42 advanced colonies, played during rounds 2-5, that make buying colonies valuable on every round.

Adds cards and rules for real-time bidding.

—description from the publisher

In this game, you will learn how to gain or retain power in politics as a party leader. You have four years to negotiate with business leaders, participate in secret consultations behind closed doors, possibly buy off key figures of the rival party, or distribute votes in exchange for their support. Step up and win the next elections. PolitX is made by Politcal experts, based on real ideologies and inspired on real poltical events.

In this game, you will play a 4 year election cycle. In each quarter (turn) you will bid for cards what represents ideological based promieses, media influences, smear campaigns and "independent"  office holders - or if you dont like the "offer market" you can  take the state treasury instead. In each year there will be a dominant headline and each party get state grant too, but the parties could collect money from promises too. The final goal is to earn the trust of the voters - party based and idea based voters in two different way - and in the end of a 4 year, the party who has the most voters win the election (and the game).

To Kyiv! is a card-driven wargame that simulates this modern conflict at the brigade level, capturing both the chaos of the early invasion and the methodical, grinding war that followed. The game uses a shared deck of event cards to guide the unfolding narrative, while each player also wields a hand of faction-specific cards representing operational choices, tactical initiatives, and political pressures.

Time advances in variable intervals, reflecting the changing tempo of operations, from fast-moving breakthroughs to long periods of stalemate. Players must balance bold offensives with the realities of logistics, morale, and international support.

This is not just a test of military skill, it is a struggle for initiative, adaptation, and endurance in one of the most consequential conflicts of the 21st century.

To Kyiv! is a historical simulation designed to model the strategic and operational dimensions of the Russian invasion of Ukraine.

—description from the designer

Step into the darkly satirical battlefield of Culture War, where ideology is your weapon and cultural capital is the currency of conquest.

Choose your faction - whether it’s the rebellious Anarchists, idealistic Communalists, steadfast Conservatives, vicious Libertarians, or elitist Technocrats - and fight to outmaneuver your opponents in a strategy game where no belief is sacred, and every move is a gamble.

It’s not just about winning; it’s about making sure everyone else loses first! Rally your forces, sharpen your arguments, and prepare to emerge victorious in this satirical contest of ideas.

—description from the publisher

The D6 Derby: A Dice-Based Racing Game

The D6 Derby is a fast-paced, tactical racing game for 1 to 6 players where you must master the balance between raw speed and precision driving. 
Can you push your engine to the limit without losing control on the next turn?

Recommended for ages 10 and up, each race provides a thrilling 30–60 minutes of play (per race, dependent on the track you made).

Core Gameplay:
Every turn, you allocate your 6 dice between Accelerate (for speed) and Manoeuvre (for turning).

High scoring Accelerate dice, collisions and crossing the track edge with one or more wheels adds Strain dice to your pool for the next turn, possibly causing the loss of Accelerate or Manoeuvre dice.

Features and Modes

Campaign Mode: 

     Experience & Upgrades: Earn XP based on finishing position to unlock persistent Driver Skills (like Drift Master or Quick Reflexes) and powerful Car Upgrades (like Turbo Boost or Nitrous System).
     Championship: Run a season of 5 to 8 races using a standard points system, with XP and upgrades carrying over between seasons.
     Solo Play and AI Opponents: Race against a simple AI Opponent system, with difficulty settings from Novice to Champion that determine their dice allocation and movement strategy. Add Personalities (like the Aggressive Charger or Blocker) for extra flavor, and play a Solo Campaign with static or progressive AI rivals.
     Variant Rules: Introduce realism and complexity with optional rules for Weather (affecting maneuver and strain), Fuel Management, Team Racing, and Qualifying.


What You Need To Play
To play the D6 Derby, you will need this rulebook, a track (hand-drawn or printed), measurement tools (ruler/tape), car miniatures/tokens (with a clear front orientation), and six-sided dice (D6) in two or three different colors for your Accelerate, Manoeuvre, and Strain pools.

Get ready to hit the track—race hard, race smart, and may your dice roll true!

—description from the publisher

Space Battles: 2218 is a 1-2 player tactical skirmish wargame depicting futuristic spaceship warfare.

The game features 6 spaceship classes, 4 types of spaceship damage, and 5 types of battles. Campaigns consist of either 5 or 10 random linked skirmish battles. For solitaire players, the game offers an advanced enemy bot opponent which makes calculated decisions based on its current situation. Features such as line of sight, crew skill points, missile and laser cannon weapons, asteroid fields, planets, jump gates, and space stations are also included.

Features include:

     3 Spaceship Fleets: Blue, Yellow, and Alien.
     6 Spaceship Classes: Cosmos, Explorer, Galaxy, Nebula, Pulsar, and Scout.
     4 types of Spaceship Damage: Engines, Hull Armor, Shields, and Systems.
     5 Types of Battles: Meeting Engagement, Planet Attack, Planet Defense, Space Station Attack, and Space Station Defense.
     10 different enemy bot actions will keep players on their toes.
     Adjustable difficulty based on player's skill level.
     Solitaire or 2 players.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     Medium size table recommended.
     Total campaign play time is approximately 2.5-5+ hours (depending on campaign length chosen). Individual battles can last between 15-45 minutes.
     One D6, two D10, one D12, and one D20 dice are required to play. A dry erase marker is also required.
     For game support, please be sure to visit the Black Cat Wargames Facebook group.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with 6 spaceship classes, 5 types of battles, randomized enemy units setup (single-player), an advanced enemy bot opponent (single-player), randomized enemy unit skill levels (single-player), and quick setup/breakdown times, Space Battles: 2218 offers an entertaining game for players of all skill levels, plus high replay value.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Golden Age of Piracy: 1718 – Tactical Ship Boarding Combat Expansion
description:
Tactical Ship Boarding Combat is an expansion for both the PnP and Compass Games version of Golden Age of Piracy: 1718 tabletop wargame.

This expansion was created in order to increase player choices during ship boarding combat, plus to provide a visual reference of ship boarding battles compared to the more abstracted base game rules. Also included are a set of standalone randomized mini-game setup rules. These rules were created for players who may sometimes want to play single battles instead of an entire Golden Age of Piracy: 1718 campaign game.

This expansion may also be played as a standalone mini-game by those of you who do not own Golden Age of Piracy: 1718 by using the included standalone randomized mini-game setup rules.

—description from the publisher
