description:
Merchant Trader: 1718 is an old-school style tactical solitaire board game of action and adventure, with a bit of wargame and RPG elements mixed in. An open-ended sandbox style game where you, as a merchant trader, may choose your own path.

As the owner of a merchant shipping trade business in the Caribbean during the year 1718, you will hire a merchant captain and crew and have them sail your ship around the Caribbean, buying and selling goods for profit. But with dastardly pirates roaming the seas, plus town plagues and fires, you will certainly have your work cut out for you. And with a large amount of game content and many player choices, no two games will ever play the same.


     10 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A large size table is required for full game setup.
     Total play time to the end is approximately 5-10 hours (depending on game length chosen).
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with 6 types of sailing ships, ship damage modeling, weather conditions, special items, merchant captain skills, specialist crewmen, random events, a multitude of table modifiers, and an almost unlimited number of player choices, Merchant Trader: 1718 offers high replay value.

Will you retire in luxury, or spend the remainder of your life in extreme poverty?

—description from the publisher
Try to be the first player to spot which letters match the first letter of one or more of the images and collect the most cards to win!

Rules of Sacred Realm is a high-intensity multiplayer fantasy card game set in the magical academies of Elysia. The core idea of the game is shared fate: your team shares one pool of Vitality, so every decision matters — every strike, spell, and sacrifice changes the battle for everyone.
Players draw from a shared 150-card deck to battle each other and drain opponents’ Vitality to zero. The combat system is built around two main card types — Combat and Spell — each one giving players meaningful ways to react, counter, and shift momentum. The game is easy to learn, but allows deep skill expression and replay.
Blending strategic depth with fast-paced dueling, Rules of Sacred Realm challenges players to balance power, intuition, and risk in every turn.
—description from the designer

The cheeky bugs are frantically munching their way through the cockroach’s dangerous territory. It doesn’t like that one bit: suddenly it darts out of its vent. The bugs freeze in horror, hoping it won’t catch them this time - or it will toss them out of the back alley. Who, with courage and luck, will be the first to reach the safety of the grate?

—description from the publisher (translated)

The beetles play against the cockroach and try to reach the grid on the other side of the backyard as quickly as possible.

You start the race at the arrow and choose which path to take. If you pass the looping space, you may switch from one path to the other or continue along the same color. On the pink or yellow spaces, you can be caught by the cockroach. There are also four safe spaces in blue with a glowing outline. The pink-yellow looping space in the middle is especially dangerous: any beetle there will definitely be caught if the cockroach appears.

There are 3 ways to play the game:

Intro Rules (2–4 players):
Each player gets three beetles of one color. Goal: be the first to get all beetles to the safe grid.

Gameplay:
Players take turns rolling two dice. Move any one beetle the total rolled. Occupied spaces count, but if a beetle lands on an occupied space, it moves one extra space. On looping spaces, players choose the direction.
Rolling a cockroach symbol flips a cockroach tile. After the fourth tile is revealed, the active player flips the cockroach coin (pink/yellow). Beetles on spaces matching the coin’s color or on looping spaces return to the start. Tiles are then reset face down.

End of Game:
The first player to get all their beetles to the goal wins.

Action Rules (3–4 players):
Each player gets three beetles. One player is the cockroach, who keeps their beetles aside and takes the cockroach die, five chips, and the coin. The beetle players place their beetles at the start and use the points die. Paper and pencil are needed for scoring.

Goal:
Beetles: be the first to get all beetles to the goal and score points for each arrived beetle.
Cockroach: send as many beetles back to start as possible and score points accordingly.

Gameplay:
Beetle players roll and move in turn. Movement rules are the same as the beginner game: occupied spaces count; if landing on an occupied space, move one extra space; looping spaces allow directional choice. Pass the die quickly to the next player.

The cockroach player rolls as fast as possible, aiming to roll five cockroach symbols. Each symbol uncovered flips a chip. After the fifth chip, shout “Stop!” and immediately interrupt beetle movement. Flip the coin: all beetles on spaces matching the coin’s color or looping spaces return to the start. Turn the chips face-down and continue. You score one point per beetle sent back.

End of Round:
The round ends immediately when a player gets all their beetles to the goal. Beetle players record points for each beetle in the goal.

Role Rotation & End of Game:
The player to the left of the cockroach player becomes the new cockroach player; the previous cockroach player becomes a beetle player. The game ends after everyone has played the cockroach. The player with the most points wins.

Duel (2 players):
The beetle player places seven beetles of any color at the start and takes the points die.
The cockroach player sets out four chips and the coin and rolls the cockroach die.

Goal:
Beetle player: get four beetles to the goal.
Cockroach player: capture four beetles.

Gameplay:
Both players act simultaneously on the start signal.
After flipping the coin, any beetles caught are immediately claimed by the cockroach player.

End of Round:
The round ends when either the beetle player reaches four beetles in the goal or the cockroach player captures four beetles. The winner scores one point.

Role Rotation & Scoring:
Players switch roles and record points over a predetermined number of rounds. The player who scored the highest total wins.
family: Versions & Editions: Travel Versions of Non-Travel Games

description:
Cut the Crap is an adaptation of the internationally famous card game "Hearts." Each suit has a color, and one of the suits is... crap. A strategy game in which the winner is the player with the most crap cards at the end of each round.

—description from the publisher

The game is played in a similar manner to Hearts, with a slightly different scoring.

A memogame, in which you may turn round a third card sometimes.

Preparation for the game: The memo discs are spread out face down. Each player gets a joker stone.

Procedure: The game is played in turns. The player whose turn it is reveals 2 discs. If he has a joker stone, he can return it to the supply and reveal a third disc. This makes sense if, after revealing the second disc, you know where the twin of one of the two revealed discs is.
If you have uncovered two identical emojis, you take them. Emojis that don't fit together are turned around again.
If you don't have a joker stone, then after revealing the first disc you can cancel your move, turn the disc over again and take a joker stone.
In any case, it is always the next player's turn.

End of the game: As soon as there are only 2 discs left face down (no one gets those), the game ends immediately. The player who has collected the most pairs is the winner.

—description from the publisher

You start at tee box number 1 and play to hole number 1, and so on until the eighteen holes are completed. The player with the fewest strokes wins. To play an eighteen-hole match on the nine-hole course on the game board, you play the nine holes twice.
The seven discs on the smaller board represent seven of the major clubs used in golf. The clubs are designed so that a different distance is achieved by using each club. You must use your judgment in selecting the correct clubs for your different shots to achieve the lowest possible score. The clubs are designed so that a different distance is achieved by using each club. You must use your judgment in selecting the correct clubs for your different shots to achieve the lowest possible score.

—user summary

War in the Newland between 2 armies (Red Bear and Blue Eagle), with the ultimate mission to dominate resources in Newland and convert to victory points. There are 5 resources Rice, Wood, Metal, Fuel and Missile. They are randomly position in each region of Newland.

Player will strategically put soldier in the vacant route, trying to control specific area for built up specific resource values (resource tableau building). And at the same time to influence and seize resource chip which can be converted to victory point depend on resource tableau built up of each army.

Simple and Deep game style which has interesting strategic depth and challenging tactic angles in playing.

—description from the designer
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
In Grifoni: Heroes, two players compete to assemble a perfect group of five heroes (either all different or all the same) by drawing and playing cards from a shared deck.
Each hero belongs to a unique Guild with special powers that shape the course of the game. Easy to learn yet rich in tactical depth, Heroes rewards clever plays, strategic discards, and smart responses.

This special edition features characters from the Grifoni - Blades of Hire boardgame.

—description from the publisher

In Good Doctor, players become skilled physicians dedicated to restoring health and order in the city. Each patient they successfully cure brings them closer to earning the title of a true lifesaver and proving their professional mastery.

Players take turns managing their own small medical practice. On a turn, a player may draw and play cards from their hand to admit new patients, use treatments, or combine action symbols to heal those already under their care. The cards feature colors and icons representing different types of medical procedures, and matching these correctly is key to success. A shared market of five face-up cards provides new opportunities every round. Strategic hand management is vital — choosing when to treat, draw, or exchange cards can determine the outcome. The game balances planning with adaptability, as the availability of cards and the actions of other doctors constantly shift the situation.

The game continues until one player successfully cures their fifth patient. When that happens, all players tally their victory points, and the one with the highest total is declared the best doctor.

In Barzone, players take on the role of bartenders competing to complete cocktail orders using ingredients, co-workers and shift cards.

Each turn, you’ll need to complete a cocktail order by using your ingredients wisely, activating your co-workers special skills, and playing shift cards from your hand.

The game ends once a player reaches 26 Mastery Points or after the eighth round.

Strategy, timing, and creativity will decide who becomes the ultimate bartender behind the bar.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The Glorious Guilds of Buttonville: Promo Pack
description:
The Glorious Guilds of Buttonville Promo Pack contains "Leon the Copycat" and was part of the Spielbox Magazine 05/2025. It is in English, French and German.

The Copycat is an additional villager you can add to The Glorious Guilds of Buttonville. To play with the Copycat, select their deck during setup. They belong to the Guild of Citizens.

Copycat effect: apply the effect from one of the sets of the player to your right. The effect can’t have a keyword in it (such as Action or Ongoing).


     If the copied card has an effect with an X in it, X refers to the set size of the Copycat set.



     A Copycat can copy the effect of another Copycat. When this happens, apply the effect from one of the sets of the player one spot further away.

In Antscape, players represent competing ant colonies trying to expand their territory in a shared landscape. The goal is to claim as many valuable areas as possible, recruit new ants, and use the queen wisely to score points!

How does it work?
Each turn follows three simple steps:

     Take an action (optional)
     Choose to either claim a region or recruit an ant
     Refill your hand to four cards


Areas are claimed by playing the right combination of cards, while recruiting ants grants access to stronger cards and more options in later turns. As the game progresses, the conquered areas together form a colony, contributing to the final score. Bonus cards add unique twists and strategies, while each player’s special terrain-shift card lets you reshape the landscape to your advantage — creating rich tactical choices every game

The game ends once all areas have been claimed or when the queen areas remain unused. When the dust settles, the colony with the highest total score wins!

—description from the publisher

Doge (short for "the Dumbest cOnnection Game Ever") is a drawless connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, perform exactly one of these actions:


     Place a stone of your color on an empty point. After your placement, for any two diagonally adjacent stones of your color there must be another stone of your color adjacent to both.
     Fill a region with enemy stones. A region is an empty point along with all empty points one can reach from it through a series of steps onto orthogonally adjacent empty points.


You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

—description from the designer

Silent Service: Dutch Harbor Expansion is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the US Navy submarine war against Japanese shipping in the Pacific during World War II.


    Silent Service base game is required to play this expansion.


As the captain of an antiquated and unreliable S-class submarine based in Dutch Harbor, USA during 1942-1944, your ultimate goal is to locate and sink as many enemy Japanese ships as possible, while at the same time bringing your sub and crew safely back to base after each patrol. It won’t be easy due to persistent enemy aircraft and escorts continuously hunting your sub, but the American people are depending on you. Don’t let them down!


     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a full campaign takes approximately 2-4 hours, with a typical patrol averaging 20 minutes.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.
     For game support, please be sure to visit the Black Cat Wargames Facebook group


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with water depth rules, compressed air rules, oxygen rules, flood damage control rules, silent running rules, numerous crew skills, weather effects, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Silent Sercive: Dutch Harbor offers memorable experiences and high replay value. Are you ready to help defend the Pacific against the onslaught of Japanese shipping?

—description from the publisher

Silent Service: Pearl Harbor Expansion is a tactical-level, narrative-style solitaire wargame using a tables-based game engine simulating the US Navy submarine war against Japanese shipping in the Pacific during World War II.


    Silent Service base game is required to play this expansion.


As the captain of a Salmon, Gato, or Balao class submarine based in Pearl Harbor, USA during 1942-1945, your ultimate goal is to locate and sink as many enemy Japanese ships as possible, while at the same time bringing your sub and crew safely back to base after each patrol. It won’t be easy due to persistent enemy aircraft and escorts continuously hunting your sub, but the American people are depending on you. Don’t let them down!


     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a full campaign takes approximately 2-4 hours, with a typical patrol averaging 20 minutes.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.
     For game support, please be sure to visit the Black Cat Wargames Facebook group


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with water depth rules, compressed air rules, oxygen rules, flood damage control rules, silent running rules, numerous crew skills, weather effects, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Silent Service: Pearl Harbor offers memorable experiences and high replay value. Are you ready to help defend the Pacific against the onslaught of Japanese shipping?

—description from the publisher

Tank Commander: North Africa is a tactical-level, narrative-style 1-2 player wargame using a tables-based game engine where you command a British Army Matilda II tank fighting against enemy German forces in Libya, North Africa during May-June 1942.


     Abstracted British Army Forces. You are never alone on the battlefield.
     One D6, two D10, one D12, and one D20 dice are required to play.
     5 minute game setup and breakdown times.
     A medium size table is recommended for game setup.
     Total play time to complete a Short campaign game takes approximately 2.5 hours, with a typical battle averaging 30 minutes.
     Total play time to complete a Long campaign game takes approximately 5 hours, with a typical battle averaging 30 minutes.
     Adjustable starting difficulty based on player's skill level.
     PC module is available on the Vassal website. Search for module:Tank Commander: North Africa


With most of the rules integrated into the tables, there is practically no learning curve or rules memorization. And with detailed damage modeling, numerous crewmen skills, 12 types of enemy German Army units, battle weather effects, terrain types, random events, and a multitude of table modifiers, Tank Commander: North Africa offers memorable experiences and high replay value. 
Are you up to the challenge of surviving the Battle of Gazala?

—description from the publisher

A board game for two players themed after Disney movie Oliver & Company manufactured and released in Greece my ΜΙΚΑ toy company in the late 80s - early 90s.

To start the game, one player takes Oliver's pawn and the other takes the remaining pawns (Rita, Dodger, Einstein, and Georgette). Players place their pieces on the corresponding squares of the board that have their faces on them. The game is played on the green, yellow, and orange squares, and moves can only be made diagonally. Oliver can move forward and backward diagonally, while the player moving the other pieces can only move one piece at a time diagonally and only forward.

The goal of the player with the four pawns is to surround Oliver so that he cannot move, while the player with Oliver aims to reach the opposite side of the board in the squares with the faces of his friends.

In honor of the 150th birthday of the great master Piet Mondrian, artists from around the world are transforming Dam Square into Mondrian Square. They fit and measured colorful tiles into a patterned square according to the principles of de Stijl. The result of the stepped and stacked structures is stunning. Soon the people of Amsterdam renamed the Mondrian Square as Piets Piazza!

Piets Piazza is a smooth game where everything revolves around speed and insight! Each round, players must get rid of their tiles as quickly as possible by placing them on a starting layer of tiles according to the building rules. During placement, Mondrian tokens can be earned that earn extra points. The game consists of four rounds of play with a new building and stacking method in each round. If the building is complete or one player has got rid of all their tiles, the round ends and a scoring takes place. In addition to the Mondrian tokens obtained, players earn points based on the number of tiles they managed to place during the round. The fewer tiles a player has left, the more points a player gets. After four rounds, the player with the most points wins the game. Piets Piazza can also be played in the Junior version or in the Adversary version.

—description from the publisher

Battle Game Heroes is a series of tabletop games with collectible anime character pieces.  Not just a game, but you can also collect the series and reproduce the world of the anime!

A 2-player board game using character pieces.  Shake the scroll to move your piece ahead on the rocks, choosing either the 1 or 2 step side, or the 0 or 3 step side.  Battle happens when you encounter an opponent.

The first player who reaches the opponent's starting point first wins.

Tidepools The Card Game was carefully crafted to be both accurate to the ecology of tide pools, and a fun, engaging game that has room for people who delve into strategy or those that just like the idea of collecting critters.

A key mechanism is that the cards have two values, depending on whether they are in your hand or in your 'pool'. This causes the player to constantly choose which elements are alive in their pool at any given time.

New cards are added to the deck as the game progresses through three 'tide' levels. Obstacles, in the form of event cards, occur during each of those tide levels. To win the players must maximize their tidepool biodiversity, by collecting the most different types of critters.

'Tidepools is a 2 to 6 player game that has a playing time of about 20 min per player.

Another roll 'n' write in Zerua's "horde" series, in this version one is trying to ensnare evil pumpkins trying to ruin Halloween. As with the other games, each turn one flips over a card from a ten-card deck to determine which "treat" on the board will provide a bonus, then roll the dice and assign one to movement and one to a list of polyominoes one will try to map out on the board. When no more moves are available, the person who has acquired the most points wins.

boardgame: Dungeon Universalis: Uncanny Palaces Campaign Book
description:
The Uncanny palaces Quest Book is a new concept of quest book. It will no longer be necessary to use the World of Arasca web APP to explore the maps without spoilers. It will be a spiral bound book (A5 size) with many pages (more than 100 color pages). It will be quests that will use the Tile Set I, II and III but where the elements to be registered and the encounters will be more personalized and immersive.

—description from the publisher

El libro de misiones Uncanny palaces o "Palacios extraños" es un nuevo concepto de libro de misiones. Ya no será necesario utilizar la APP web de World of Arasca para explorar los mapas sin spoilers. Será un libro encuadernado en espiral (tamaño A5) con más de 100 páginas a color. Serán misiones que utilizarán el Tile Set I, II y III pero donde los elementos a registrar y los encuentros serán más personalizados e inmersivos.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

A5, 112 pages, two-sided full-colour glossy spiral-bound

200 cards, few rules, huge possibilities.
With this random map generator you will be able to play 8 different types of missions, with 10 possible secondary objectives, in the scenarios you want. You can explore a castle, a sewer or a crypt complex, and search for a huge beast in a forest, volcano or jungle. The icon system is easy to visualize and with it you can configure your decks, and you can use it to play independent quests or random quests in any territory of the Arasca world during a campaign, because you only have to choose a number of cards whose icon coincides with the type of territory where you are (swampy, wooded, mountains, city, etc.).

—description from the publisher

200 cartas, pocas reglas, enormes posibilidades.
Con este generador de mapas aleatorios podrás jugar 10 tipos diferentes de misiones, con 10 posibles objetivos secundarios, en los escenarios que quieras. Podrás explorar un castillo, unas alcantarillas o un complejo de catacumbas. Buscar una enorme bestia en un bosque, un volcán o una selva. El sistema de iconos es fácil de visualizar y con él podrás configurar tus mazos, y podrás utilizarlo para jugar misiones independientes o misiones aleatorias en cualquier territorio del mundo de Arasca durante una campaña, ya que sólo tendrás que elegir un número de cartas cuyo icono coincida con el tipo de territorio en el que te encuentres (pantanoso, boscoso, montañas, ciudad, etc.).

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Contains the factions whose standees are already included in the Standees Bestiary II box: First Elves, Celestials, Kurgan Tribes, Witch Hunters, Siarons and several independent creatures (giant rat, fairy, gorilla...).
 48 Bestiary cards (120x80mm.).

—description from the publisher

Contiene las facciones cuyos standees ya están incluidos en la caja Standees Bestiary II: Primeros Elfos, Celestiales, Tribus Kurgan, Cazadores de Brujas, Siarones y varias criaturas independientes (rata gigante, hada, gorila..)..
 48 cartas de Bestiario (120x80mm.).

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Dungeon Poker is a unique board game that combines Texas Holdém poker with the most intriguing fantasy elements and role playing games. The game has been created by passionate team of Finnish poker and fantasy roleplaying fans in a ten year long project.

In Dungeon Poker players will compete in a poker tournament with unique fantasy characters. Each character has unique special abilities they can use to interact with the game. During Dungeon Poker tournament, players will face monsters, find treasures, and use magical artifacts. Players also need to adapt to changing rules & conditions, when the blind levels get higher. Who will claim the ultimate title of the Dungeon King?

—description from the designer

Your Hired, Job description: Save the city by whacking the invading mobster zombie boss Tony 2 worms, and his gang.  The only way to save the city, is to keep whacking zombies, until you run them all out of your town.

Whack’em Zombies is a fast pace set collection game for 2-4 players. During the game you will collect and play whacker cards to make money whacking mobster zombies. Earn the most money and you win the game. Keep an eye out for bonus brains to earn extra money.

—description from the publisher

A fast and explosive card game for families and friends. Players take turns discarding a card with a number close to the one in the center of the table. If a player cannot play an appropriate card, they "explode the bomb" and must take all the cards from the center as a penalty. At the end of the game, the player with the fewest penalty cards wins.

—description from the publisher

The Branchbourne Dynasty is Crumbling ?

In the tiny bug kingdom of Branchbourne, a political dynasty is beginning to crumble, and the future of the entire tree is at stake!

Pact is an intensely asymmetric game about big politics in a little world. As one of four unique future rulers, your goal is to secure your ascent to power by gaining support from the influential people under you.

You must make pacts with powerful neutral Factions and use the resulting political capital to manipulate the kingdom's courtiers and undermine your rivals. The alliances you forge will determine the fate of Branchbourne.

—description from the publisher

Flamenco is a passionate dance performed in theaters in the Seville region of Spain.

This game is a “climbing game” in which players must play higher cards while maintaining the state set by the previous player. As in traditional climbing games, a player must play the same number of cards with a higher value in order to beat the previous play.

However, a stylish step allows a single card to be split into two. An even-numbered card can be beaten by two cards whose combined value equals half of that card. If two even-numbered cards are played, they may be beaten with four cards; if three even-numbered cards are played, they may be beaten with six cards.

The highest number is 7, and under normal circumstances it cannot be beaten. However, the dazzling step of playing 1 and 2 together can defeat it. This combination, called the “Ultimate Praise,” cannot be beaten by any other play.

Players score points according to the order in which they empty their hands. After the designated number of rounds has been completed, the player with the most points wins.

The Games We Played is a solo campaign game about the mechanisms of a toxic relationship.


     Walk a mile in the shoes of a survivor.
     The mechanisms themselves are the story. 
     A truly unique and emotional experience.


Toxic relationships are widely discussed nowadays, but explaining and understanding them is not easy. That’s partly because they are hard to grasp through one coherent narrative. Being inside is more like trying to find your way out of a maze with constantly moving walls, or trying to win a game that keeps changing its own rules to play against you. The Games We Played aims to recreate this experience through simple but meaningful game mechanisms. This gives the player a unique experience that sheds light on how these kinds of relationships work, and why it is so hard to break free from them.

The game itself takes the form of a ring bound notebook, in which a person who lived through a relationship with a toxic partner has recorded their experience. Not as a diary, but as a series of solo card games. Each of them will present you with a different situation, highlighting various aspects and mechanisms of psychological, mental and emotional abuse, and the struggles of trying to deal with them.

Each turn you play a card from your hand to one of the available slots in the book, activate effects you triggered, and then draw a card. Each slot can hold several cards, but they must always be ascending in value. Once you have a sufficient amount of cards on specific objective slots, you turn the page and check the consequences of your actions. You will often permanently mark cards with keywords such as “Anxious”, “Cautious” or “In Love”. These keywords change how you can play those cards in the future. This very simple base structure is broken up by all kinds of mechanical variations. Each chapter will play a little differently.

But these variations aren’t just there to make play more interesting. Each mechanism is closely related to the subject matter, each action means something within the story. Maybe you are presented with two possible objectives. One that is very easily achieved and represents what your partner wants to do. And one that is very hard to fulfill and represents what you want to do. Which objective will you pursue? And what will the consequences be? Will you be rewarded for giving in? Are you doing the right thing or are you running into a trap? Can the game be trusted?

—description from the designer

Welcome to Felandia – the land where bubbles shimmer with magical power and every princess strives to fill her kingdom with light and life!
In Bubblemagi i Felandia ("Bubble Magic in Felandia"), each player takes on the role of a Pixie Princess who collects colored bubbles to complete the parts of her kingdom. But beware – mischievous sprites lurk in the shadows, ready to steal bubbles and create chaos.

The game combines simple tactics and luck with fun, colorful actions that make every round exciting and unpredictable. Choose which cards to draw, build combinations of bubbles, and try to complete all three kingdom cards before your opponents.

—description from the publisher

A solo PnP puzzle game blending chess tactics & dungeon grids! Primarily played by printing & cutting unique Grandmaster cards for a true deckbuilding feel. Alternatively, jump straight into the action using the innovative "Flowing Arsenal" sheet system! Acquire chess piece movements to solve intricate labyrinth challenges. Think like a Grandmaster!

—description from the designer

In the game, players need to get their pawn out of a maze by using movement cards that are obtained through a deckbuilding mechanism.

boardgame: Cat Packs: Party Animal Promo Card
description:
Cat Packs: Promo Card Party Animal adds one additional cat to your cat pack, the Party Animal.

Party Animal
Cost to Play: 2 Catnip
Pawer Value: -
Ability: Counts as each Cat Type

The card is part of the Cat Packs: Purrstock Kickstarter.

boardgame: Chronicles of Crime: Beyond Doubt – Cold Cases

3 Scenarios! One for each Chronicles of Crime: Beyond Doubt game.

Includes alternate, custom-made character and location cards designed to test even the sharpest detectives. There is no description on the boards or cards - just an icon indication which game it goes to.

What’s Inside:

     3 Alternate Character Cards

—description from the publisher

description:
This expansion pack for Diktat featuring all the game's miniatures, plus enhanced faction-specific equipment.

The Artifacts box contains :

     6 bunker figurines for the "Black Guard" faction
     6 revolt miniatures for the "Resistance" faction
     6 "cladogram" cards with foil effect for the Secret Police 
     6 membership banners for the "Syndicate" faction
     10 Corruption miniatures for the optional faction, opposed to all players, "Other"
     3 monstrous miniatures representing the "Others" opposed to all players

Harmonium allows to play Diktat solo or cooperatively. Players form a Dissidence group opposed to the totalitarian Administration of the NOC dystopian world, and will be confronted with the machinations of the mighty Ministry of Effort.

boardgame: Wonderland's War: Caterpillar & White Queen Expansion
description:
The Caterpillar and the White Queen join in the War for Wonderland, introducing all-new content and systems to enhance the Wonderland's War base game.

Two New Playable Factions - The Caterpillar and White Queen factions are now playable, each with their own special quirks. The Caterpillar uses his hookah to introduces a tricky new smoke mechanic where you can bewilder your opponents with new tactics in battle. Meanwhile the White Queen brings her special chess piece units into battle for a strong opening.

Modular Forge Tracks - Draft a unique forge track during setup and build a strategy around a whole new spread of effects that can impact the things you care about in final scoring and the goodies you can collect along the way.

New Content - The expansion introduces 12 new Wonderlandians and a new set of Ally cards.

Solo Mode - Wonderland's War can finally be played as a solo game! Choose your Rival during setup, which features a set of special abilities and a difficulty level, then use a deck of Tactics cards to automate an opponent's decisions. Every Rival is different and presents you with unique challenges for extra replayability.

Crows versus Cats is a game of collecting shiny things while avoiding thieving magpies, troublesome cats, and the winds of change. A cozy chaos strategy game with some take-that elements and some random events that affect all players. 3-6 players and suitable for ages 8+. 

Select one of six crows and attempt to collect five of their favorite shiny things. First to collect all five wins. But chaos cards can appear and affect everyone at the table or just one crow.

To collect shiny things, players draw cards during their turn and place drawn shiny things next to their crow in the play area. Chaos cards can also be drawn and are either "Play immediately" or "Play during turn" and must be played before a player's turn can end and cannot be discarded.

All art is hand-painted watercolors, no AI was used to create any part of this game.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to your very own restaurant, where only the sharpest chefs survive the dinner rush!
Your kitchen team may look cute, but don't be fooled: this is a high-stakes culinary battle. Every decision counts as players compete to craft the most exquisite menu, manage limited ingredients, and adapt to unpredictable challenges in their quest to become the ultimate Master Chef.

Each player has their own restaurant, which is located in front of them as a player board. On the left is the queuing area for the guests. The aim is to provide as many guests as possible with food and thus collect the most points. There are four different types of guests. You receive new random guests at the start of the game and of each round. When your guests received the right meal, place the tokens from your player board in your bag and the guests in the “Finished guests” area. The different queuing areas require different actions:
- To-Go: require a Service Worker, all guests from a field receive their food and are put away
- Sit-In: require a Service Worker, all guests from a field are placed at a table on the game board and get their food by a Delivery Worker
- Online: require a Delivery Worker, all guests from a field receive their food and are put away

On your turn, pick one of three actions:
    1. Send a Worker Into Action!
Each worker can only perform one task per round — they have one main skill (Cooking, Service or Delivery) on level of 1–3 and can be upgraded up to level 3 and one second skill (Cooking, Service, Delivery and Trading).
       
       - Service: Place your worker on the Service field of your player board. Depending on the level, you can cash in the corresponding number of fields with guests – either on To-Go and Sit-In.
       
       - Delivery: Your worker’s level determines, how many tables on the game board or Online fields you can serve. To do this, place your worker on the waiter field of your player board.
       
       - Cooking: Place your worker on the field of your player board. Use a kitchen station for each cooking action and place a dirt marker on it. These are the cooking actions:
        ◦ Cooking the Signature Dish: To cook the signature dish, you need a free kitchen station, the right ingredients and a cooking action. The main courses are available from level 1–5. On each card you find details how many courses you get at which level. If your worker/boss has cooking at level 2 or 3, you can cook your signature dish multiple times. 
        ◦ Upgrading the Signature Dish: Fulfill the requirements at the top left of the next level card. You need an own cooking action and must use the special station shown. If you don’t have it or it has a dirt marker, you can’t upgrade. If your worker has cooking at level 2 or 3, you can upgrade your signature dish several times if you fulfill every requirement and there is no dirt marker on the corresponding station. Once you have upgraded your signature dish to level 5, the game ends after this round. You can take further actions after this, but you can no longer cook main courses. So think carefully about when it is wisest to upgrade to make as many points as possible in the last round.
        ◦ Cooking Starters: The starters are independent of your signature dish and every player can cook them. You get 2 points for each starter you deliver from your own player board regardless of your signature dish. Starters from the buffet scores no points. Five starter cards are face up next to the game board. You decide how many ingredients (always from top to bottom) you want/can use. Return the ingredients from your player board to the display and take the bonuses shown on the card.

- Trading: the marketplace with various bonuses is at the bottom left of the game board. It is a secondary skill and brings additional bonuses for one worker per round.

2. Activate a Robot!
These specialized machines focus on specific tasks: cooking, service, or delivery. Use each robot once per round to enhance your kitchen's efficiency. Don't hold back — expand your robotic team as much as your kitchen space allows!

3. Use Your Ingredient Cards Wisely!
Choose bonuses from one side of the ingredient cards to cook up dishes. Reveal your chosen card face up to immediately collect all the tasty rewards. But be careful: once you select a side, you must take everything — even if it’s not exactly what you need!

End of Game and final scoring

- the first player upgrades their signature dish to level 5 gets five points for their bag
- the round is then played to the end until no player can perform any more actions

- the other players can also upgrade their signature dish to level 5, but each receives one point less (2nd player 4 points, 3rd player 3 points, 4th player 2 points)

- after the round: all players count the points in their bag

- check the three individual end conditions of your signature dish on card 5 (regardless of whether you upgraded to level 5)
- each end condition earns 3 additional points

Solo Mode
Ready to face the ultimate challenge on your own and elevate your restaurant to legendary status? In solo mode, you’ll experience the core multiplayer rules with just a few tweaks — test your skills, strategy, and determination like never before. This is your chance to prove you've got what it takes!

Long before kingdoms rose and crumbled, before men spoke words of power they did not understand, there was the Veyra of Vefariheim. It bound all things together — the threads of fire and stone, root and shadow, blood and moonlight. And so long as the Veyra endured, so too did the world.

For an age, men were charged as stewards of the Veyra. They were gifted with the power to nurture balance or to grasp for dominion. But man is seldom content. They carved the earth, chained the strong, and bled the Threads for their own desires. They called their greed progress, their arrogance destiny. And so they unraveled what they were sworn to protect. The Veyra faltered. Magic curdled, the skies tore, and whispers of doom spread.

Salvation, if it comes, will be woven by those once cursed as monsters: vampires seeking eternity, werewolves yearning for the hunt, dragons craving flame, Leshens desperate for renewal, and trolls reforging their oaths. Together, the Questweavers will face trials that test strength, cunning, loyalty, and sacrifice. Each Thread pulled risks the Veyra further — or mends it anew. Outcasts all, will those once reviled rise up and repair what men have sundered?

To win Questweaver, complete quests to score the most Victory Points in the game.  Recruit your characters to form a team of ten.  Take turns committing your characters to the questing party.  Gain the most Victory Points by completing quests using your recruited characters, spells, and strategy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: March of Empires: Time of War
description:
March of Empires: Time of War is a fast-paced strategy game that pits 2 to 4 players against each other in a high-stakes battle for supremacy. Command your Faction—be it a noble King, a proud Tsar, an ambitious Sultan, or a disciplined Shogun—and lead your people to glory. With a unique hourglass-based system representing each player’s orders, the game demands both quick thinking and bold strategy.

Throughout the game, you will place Hourglasses to issue commands: construct buildings, gather resources, train units, or move armies across the map. The timing of your actions is crucial, as your opponents can exploit every hesitation or strike before your plans unfold.

Once battle ensues, tactics take center stage. Players select Combat cards and resolve clashes based on initiative and dice rolls, with unit composition and chosen strategies determining the outcome. Each victory brings you closer to dominance—whether by achieving scenario objectives or seizing control of your rival’s Castle.

Outmaneuver your enemies, expand your empire, and carve your legacy into history—there can be only one king.

—description from the publisher

The MasterPieces Smokey Bear Happy Camper Coop Game is designed for children and adults aged 4 and up. This cooperative game encourages teamwork and cooperation. Help Smokey Bear and the cubs pack their van for an epic adventure! With 5 activity cards to choose from, collect the necessary equipment before your forest friends arrive. This engaging game includes a game board with a dial, camper van, spinner, 3 playing pieces, 25 tokens, 5 cards, and rules perfect for 2-4 players.

—description from the publisher

In Vector Tactics, players command a squad of directional units on an 8×8 battlefield. Each piece is a puck-shaped token marked with one or more arrows. These arrows define exactly how the piece can move—no more, no less.

After moving your piece, you have the option of rotating the piece, thus changing which directions it can go in subsequent turns. This upends the usual thought process around Chess, and introduces additional complexities and the ability to throw off your competition. Once you've placed the King into a checkmate scenario, you win.

Each turn is a puzzle. Each rotation is a decision. Victory comes not from brute force, but from mastering directional control and positional pressure.

It looks like chess, but it feels like war!

—description from the publisher

Obex is a head-to-head tactical board game where one player builds to ascend, and the other schemes to block the path forward. With just 20 turns to complete a towering monument, the pressure is on—and only one can win.

In Obex, two players take on opposing roles:

The Architect: Control the mighty Obelisk. There are five ancient segments of the Obelisk scattered across the board. On their turn, the Architect moves their pieces to capture their other tower pieces, thus building the Obelisk as the game progresses. The Architect's goal is to complete the Obelisk before time runs out.

The Maze Master: Shape the labyrinth. With only 10 walls and limited time, your mission is to stall, trap, and delay your opponent—deny them their glory! This is done by strategically placing your angled wall pieces to slow down the Architect - but you have to be selective, since you have only 10 walls to place within the game's 20-turn limit.

—description from the publisher

Phantoms & Intruders is a miniatures based version of the Avalon Hill board game Mustangs, with a low to medium complexity rating.  
Aircraft maneuver on a hex grid using stands with altitude sticks (easy to make) with markers denoting their maneuvers for the turn.  
Intruders is a complete air to ground module that adds SAMs, AAA, and various missions for strike aircraft.  
This deluxe version also includes the 2008 and 2009 updates with additional markers.  
There are dozens and dozens of pre-made aircraft charts covering most jets from the 1960s to the 1980s, allowing you to play out almost any historical or hypothetical combat during that time frame.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Banner Blitz is a dynamic, head-to-head board game where tactical movement, sharp decisions, and bold captures rule the battlefield. Two players go toe-to-toe in a high-stakes capture-the-flag scenario—except this time, you need both enemy flags (or total domination) to claim victory.

Each turn, both players roll a die - where one player gets to choose whether they'd like to go first. Players take turns, moving about the board, picking up the flags and returning them to their base - while trying to block the opponent's movements.

Plan your moves, spread out your forces, and strike when the moment is right. Every round starts with a dice roll to determine who seizes the initiative—and every turn is a chance to outmaneuver your opponent. Capture flags, recover your own, eliminate enemy tokens, and bring glory to your home base!

—description from the publisher

Blorp Quest is a fast-paced, asymmetric board game where a party of heroic Blorps faces off against Gildorp the Dread—an ancient golden dragon who's gone completely mad.

One player controls Gildorp, wielding terrifying attacks like fire breath, tail whips, and claws. Up to four others play as a team of adorable yet mighty Blorp heroes: the Paladin, the Rogue, the Wizard, and the Cleric. Each hero brings unique abilities to the battlefield—from divine healing and magical teleportation to devastating sword strikes and sneaky flanking maneuvers. The heroes and Gildorp all have their own health counters, movements, and attack abilities - with the team players working together to fight against the dragon.

With modular board pieces, custom dice, and clever turn mechanics, Blorp Quest challenges players to think strategically, cooperate effectively, and roll their way to victory. Will teamwork triumph over terror—or will the dragon reign supreme?

—description from the publisher

In Blorps, two players go head-to-head in a fast, tactical puzzle to outwit their opponent and line up their Blorps. With only four pieces each and a 6x6 board, the goal is deceptively simple: be the first to align all 4 of your Blorps in a row—vertically, horizontally, or diagonally.

But here’s the twist: Blorps must move as far as they can in a straight line. There’s no jumping, no capturing—just pure positioning strategy. Every move matters, and every round is a mind game.

—description from the publisher

The Blorps have mastered the ancient and honorable art of Blorp Sumo! In Sumo Blorps, players take control of an elite group of Blorps trained to shove, slide, and strategically dominate the arena.

The goal? Maneuver your Sumo Blorps into the exact patterns shown on your secret Position Tiles—before your opponent does. Use classic Sumo Blorp movement, tactical pushes, and even wrap-around board tricks to secure your placements. But beware—your opponent is just as pushy as you are.

—description from the publisher

In the bogs and swamps of planet Blorp, a species of Blorps evolved powerful hind legs and fierce tactical instincts. These are the Leaplorps, and they're ready to bounce into battle. Fast, nimble, and strategic, Leaplorps is a tactical elimination game about positioning, chaining leaps, and surprise captures.

In this Checkers-type game, each player can choose to hop by one space at a time, or leap across other Leaplorps. Opponent Leaplorps are eliminated by leaping on top of them, and the first to eliminate all the opposing Leaplorps win.

—description from the publisher

Waterfall Chess is an evolved form of traditional chess, played across five 4×4 boards. While each piece moves according to classic chess rules, the battlefield is far more complex—and volatile.

Every move can cause a cascade of warping pieces across the layered boards, creating unexpected threats, sudden openings, and chain reactions. Mastering Waterfall Chess requires not just strategic foresight, but multilevel awareness.

—description from the publisher

Flip and find!  Flip the card to explore the Universe and collect the elements…… Be careful! Don’t fall into the Singularity.

Cook the Star!  Mix the elements and cook a delicious star dish~ Attention! Match all balanced.

Flip the matrix and collect the elements, and use the elements collected to cook the dish! 
Rigil Kent becomes a cake, Neutron star has a part of noodles. 
This is the first step to become the Chef of Universe!

This 54-card expansion pack includes three 18-card expansions for the DOOM PILGRIM Pocket board game:

FUTILE HAVENS make the game easier by bringing more relief, helping, or healing points, and more easily achievable loot. However, nothing is too bright in this corrupt world – even these havens are hostile under specific circumstances.

FATAL ENDS make the game harder, with cards that are mostly fatal, bringing doom on their own. Using these cards adds to the “need-to-remember-the-danger” aspect of the game. However, you can also remember under which circumstance the particular card is beneficial – and indeed, there can be some valuable gains here too, balancing the mortal risk.

PAGAN ECHOES is a thematic expansion, adding the flavor of heathenry and paganism into the mix. Concerning difficulty, beneficial cards are mixed with threatening ones.

Available in the Pocket form now, faster and more actionable.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rising Dragon: Platoon Level Combat in the Invasion of Taiwan
description:
October 1, 2034 marks the beginning of the conflict that unfolds in Rising Dragon, a standalone game and campaign setting for the Armageddon War system. With the United States turning inward in the wake of the catastrophic ‘Armageddon War’, China seizes the moment to assert its territorial claims, letting the world know that it is now the unchallenged power in the region. This sets the stage for a military showdown that spans East Asia, with Taiwan at the epicenter.

Platoon Level
Scale of 150 meters per hex
15 minutes per turn
Continuous Chit-pull Activation
18 stamped dice for combat resolution
Naval, amphibious, and urban conflicts
Age: 14+
Players: 2
Playing Time: 1-3 Hours
Scenario based

The game introduces cutting-edge technologies, including hypersonic missiles, advanced amphibious operations, and features detailed maps that bring the battlegrounds of Taiwan and the wider region to life.Scenarios include the Chinese invasion of Taiwan, where hypersonic missile systems like the PRC Heaven Sword devastate Taiwanese defenses.

—description from the publisher

In FlipDraw: Mondrian, players will be creating beautiful abstract art in the style of Piet Mondrian. Each turn you will have a difficult choice containing both a color and a shape to add to your canvas. You only have 16 turns to complete
your masterpiece and are aiming to fill as much of the canvas as possible but also balancing your colors for scoring while creating something amazing.
On a turn, players will simultaniously select from three color/shape options. Once you select the shape/color combo, you will add it onto your canvas. Each shape will be a specific size rectangle, and they will be paired with a specific color (red, blue, yellow, white). 
After 16 rounds of adding colored rectangle to you canvas, players will score based on specific scoring requirements.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Head to Head: Elf V Santa
description:
Who can destroy the presents quickest? Elf or Santa?

One player dresses up in a Santa hat, the other in an Elf hat.
Players knock the present targets over using their hats!
The fastest player wins the round!

boardgame: Black Friday: Blood, Sweat and Deals
description:
At the break of Black Friday dawn, a restless horde of consumers pours inside the super store like an unstoppable force of nature!

Will you be the fastest shopper, get the best products and emerge victorious?

Or will somebody record your shameless behavior and put it online for all to see?

---

Party / Set collection / Dexterity / Bluffing
3-6 players / 10 Min / Age: 8+

---
RULES:


    Shop!*


All players simultaneously reach either for an available product or their smartphone card.

Collecting 3 products earns you 1 point. 
Collecting 3 matching products earns you 3 points.


    Fight!*


If multiple players reach for the same product, the fastest player gets the product.


    Record fights!*


Picking your smartphone card and recording a fight earns you 1 point and each fighter gets a penalty Shame token instead.

First shopper to reach 10 points wins.

"Whether you are a seasoned historian or just making your way through past events, Historiae gives any player a chance to shine!

Place historical events in the right order to get rid of your cards, but watch out, Paradox and Political Turmoil cards can shake things up in your favor, or not. Play events in a random order or swap hands with your opponents. In this game, strategy and a bit of luck can rewrite the course of events! First one to get rid of all their cards wins.

Ready to make history?

—description from the publisher

Components:
3 Starting Building tiles

Changes in setup:
Before resolving Player Setup, add the new Starting Buildings to the respective stack

PR5
At the end of each Income phase:
Place a Neutral Worker on any Barge, and gain 2 Resources provided by this Barge.

PR6
During each Income phase:
Draw 2 Regular Bonus tiles from general supply, keep one of them, and discard the other.

PR7
During each Income phase:
Place a Delivery marker on any available Delivery space of any ship in the Yan region gaining the appropriate reward (but not from the Demand tile). Do not place the Shipping marker.

boardgame: Classic of Mountains and Seas: Nine Ding
description:
In Classic of Mountains and Seas - Nine Ding (山海经-九鼎), players step into the myths of ancient China, drawn from the Classic of Mountains and Seas (山海经). The sacred Nine Ding (九鼎) —symbols of divine power and the Mandate of Heaven—have been lost. Rival clans now seek to forge them anew and restore order to the world.

Objective


     Accumulate prestige (Victory Points) by forging Ding, invoking gods, and mastering clan abilities.
     The game ends when the last Ding is forged or the final Destiny card is revealed.
     The player with the highest prestige becomes the rightful ruler under Heaven.


Background
According to legend, Yu the Great (大禹) forged the Nine Ding from the metals of the Nine Provinces to signify his divine right to rule. When dynasties fell, the Ding disappeared—awaiting those worthy to reclaim them.
This game brings that myth to life, merging Chinese legend with modern strategic design.
publishers: 九张牌玩具公司(9 Cards Toy Company)

description:
It is a beautiful beach day. Fish swim peacefully in the sea, while crabs sunbathe on the beach. And they are not the only ones. People everywhere are enjoying the sun, warm sand, sea and delicious food. There, hungry seagulls dive from the sky. One grabs a tasty fish from the water. The next a juicy apple from a sleeping man, while two other gulls take turns stealing each other’s sandwich. Will you manage to grab and eat the most delicacies?

The goal is to gather beach cards and sea cards with highest possible values. On your turn you pick two dice out of three to roll and move your seagull with one of the rolled dice. Then you perform the action on the square you landed in. If you however end your movement in a square with another seagull, you get to steal one of their cards.

—description from the publisher

The famous board game Monopoly in an irresistibly sweet edition - all about Haribo!
A dream for young and old alike, where Goldbears and other beloved Haribo treats can be bought and traded.
Even the Community Chest and Chance cards are charmingly themed around Haribo.
The highlight: Goldbears and Juice Goldbears replace Boardwalk and Park Place.
Play with six special collector tokens shaped like Haribo classics such as the Goldbear, Happy Cherry, and Happy Cola.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Stopping the Panzers is a scenario for use as a variant for Mark Simonitch’s Normandy ‘44 wargame (GMT).

It is based on the book “Stopping the Panzers: the Untold Story of D-Day” by Canadian historian Marc Milner.

It is published in C3i magazine #35. The required counters were included in the C3i #37 insert
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Phibo is a dynamic two-player climbing and shedding card game where players aim to be the first to empty their hand each round. The game is played with a 39-card deck (a standard 54-card deck with the diamond suit removed) and has a unique "Rank Tracker" system which dynamically shifts the ranking of four cards: Jacks, Queens, Kings, and Aces. Players take turns playing combinations of cards, attempting to overplay their opponent with higher-ranking or matching numerical combinations, while strategically manipulating the rank tracker and managing the draw deck.

Each turn, players can play a valid combination (such as a single, pair, triplet, or run of three) or a "bomb"  (the two lowest-ranked cards of the same suit). When players match ranks between 1-10, they must perform special actions like drawing cards, forcing the opponent to draw, or rotating the rank tracker (which alters both the rank hierarchy and bomb composition). Jokers serve as flexible “matchlets”, capable of matching numerical values.

—description from the designer

Fool's errand is a 3-player climbing-shedding card game where one player, the Soloist, bids to complete contracts while the other two form a temporary team to stop them. Each deal begins with an auction in which players bid for the solo role and choose from a variety of contracts like shedding out first, or capturing all Kings.

Key twist: As soon as a player bids, they pick up the 3‑card talon (public draw pile) and discard any three cards to create a new talon. Certain contracts enforce "must-play" or "must-bomb" rules.

Silent bonuses give scoring opportunities for all players. The deal ends when a player sheds all of their cards, and scoring is based on which contracts were fulfilled or failed.

The player or players who end up with negative points are deemed the Fools, so try not to become the Fool.

boardgame: The Last Drifters: Wasteland Chronicles – Volume 1: 12 free scenarios
description:
Embark on a gripping journey through the desolate landscapes of a post-apocalyptic world with The Last Drifters: Wasteland Chronicles - Volume 1.
This collection of 12 scenarios for The Last Drifters tabletop game plunges players into the heart of the wastelands, where strategy, survival, and high-octane vehicular combat reign supreme.

Each of the 12 scenarios, ranging from high-speed chases to strategic ambushes, offers a fresh and immersive gaming experience. Whether it’s evading ruthless gangs or navigating treacherous terrain, these scenarios will test your tactical acumen and adaptability.

While designed for The Last Drifters, these scenarios can be adapted to fit other rule sets, making them a versatile addition to any gamer's collection.

—description from the publisher

One Day Campaigns is a system to allow gamers to play an entire WW2 or Modern campaign in a single day using a system of multiple game tables.  
Besides the campaign system presented here, there are sets of fast play tactical rules for both the WW2 and Modern periods to enable large battles to be fought quickly that will keep the campaign moving along. 
There is also a sample campaign provided that will work for both periods.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
This is Little Big War, the definitive base building tabletop wargame that captures the esssence and gameplay of a classic Real Time Strategy game on your coffee table, all in under an hour!

Little Big War is an innovative game that's fast, accessible and easy to play - but hard to master! 
Go toe to toe with another player to spend actions to build your base, train new units, attack, gather resources or activate base defences… But watch out because each time you spend an action, your opponent does too!

Can you destroy your opponents base before they destroy yours?

Small scale, big game! Play a war on your coffee table in 30 - 60 minutes!


     Snappy back and forth gameplay means there's never a dull moment
     A wargame that escalates: start with infantry and goo monsters, end with massive tanks, cyclopean monsters, star gods and nuclear bombs!
     Use any miniatures you have so long as they’re on a 25 mm base! 
     Innovative activation system that captures the essence of classic real time strategy games
     Two totally unique factions with their own playstyles, buildings, units, superweapons and more - all laid out in a single page!
     No book keeping, complicated tech trees or cumbersome conditions to track!
     Short and sweet rulebook that’s quick and easy to read!


If you like Warhammer 40k, miniature games, tabletop games or real time strategy - but giant rulebooks aren’t for you, then try out Little Big War!

—description from the publisher

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
It is hundreds of years in the future, humanity is starting to build itself back up after the cataclysm of the Exile and the devastation wrought by the cold machine logic of the European Learning Machine. Faced with a fresh start and a healing earth, people begin to reestablish society the only way they know how...

By fighting it out and blowing each other up.

This book contains the rules to allow you to play games in the world of IRON DRAGOONS, where colossal fighting machines known ARMS do battle, each controlled by a skilled pilot and kitted out with devastating weaponry. In the core rules you will find how to play, six core missions that you can play, and robust ARMS creation rules with dozens of possible components to mix and match, allowing you to create the killer robot of your dreams.

The book also contains 25 pages detailing the world of the war torn future, the factions of Iron Dragoons world such as the manufacturing companies responsible for the ARMS, the Orbital Cities Allience, the Trademoon and others, as well as a selection of notable pilots and craft.

description:
Every year on the autumnal equinox, the Great Mystical Convention allows novice witches to form teams to try to prove themselves and earn their place on the highest ranks of witchery. This marathon of trials is known as Invocathlon.

Invocathlon is a profoundly cooperative game for 2 to 6 players. Each one represents one of the witches in the team and they have 7 days (rounds) to explore the region and face challenges in search of ingredients to prepare an invocation ritual. On the 7th day, they must summon one of the dangerous supernatural entities and confront it.

Don't be fooled by the friendly face. Invocathlon is a very hard and strategic game, with an innovative combination of mechanisms and multiple relevant choices to make each round.

Is your team good enough to overcome the Invocathlon?

boardgame: The 1971 Indo-Pakistan war: An expansion for "Flames of War" and "Team Yankee"
description:
The 1971 Indo-Pakistan war - an expansion for players of Flames of War and Team Yankee

This is a non-authorised expansion to Battlefront 's excellent Flames of War and Team Yankee rule sets for WW2 and Modern warfare respectively.  
This expansion cover the 1971 Indo-Pakistan war, and is meant to provide something a little different for experienced players of either FOW or TY.

The expansion set includes a brief background history, a description of terrain, six army lists, cut out equipment descriptions and some special rules to recreate the unique flavour of the equipment and the conflict as a whole.

FOW and TY are not my intellectual property, so I cannot sell this supplement, but as I am mostly interested in fostering interest in Battlefront's excellent rule systems, that is not a problem.

—description from the designer
