boardgame: Hocus Pocus Hyperfocus: The ADHD Oracle Game
description:
Conjure up stories where the familiars are as fluffy as they are fiendish.
Hocus Pocus Hyperfocus is a passion project of two ADHDers years in the making. A game that expresses the absurdity and hilarity of our experiences with ADHD.  One that evokes the time game designer, Allison, lost her phone and then later found it *inside* a fruit salad she’d prepared for a party that evening. Or the time when feverishly preparing for a meeting, Brandy lit her notes on fire with the candle she forgot she had on her desk.

The stories in Hocus Pocus Hyperfocus are structured using the Deck of Distractions—an oracle deck full of both the tasks that your witch needs to accomplish as well as all the ways that she could be distracted from these very tasks. She has limited energy to complete a seemingly limitless number of tasks. Both task switching and hyper-focusing will take their toll on her over the course of the day.

We wanted to create game systems that reflect some of the ways our brains work, and then let our friends and family play with all of the wild inspiration and intense frustration that comes with such a brain?. And of course, we wanted there to be magic.

boardgame: The Battle of Cook's Mill: A Scenario for Whuppin Jonathon
description: The battle of Cook's Mill - a scenario for "Whuppin Jonathon" , Vandering Publications rules for refighting the war of 1812.

boardgame: FNG War Tales: The Korean DMZ War – 1967-1969
description:
THE DMZ WAR — A SHADOW WAR EXPERIENCE

“The guns fell silent in 1953... but the war never ended.”

Play the Forgotten War Inside the DMZ (1966–1969)

Between 1966 and 1969, the Korean Demilitarized Zone became one of the most dangerous front lines on Earth. Beneath the world’s gaze, a brutal shadow war unfolded—ambushes, snipers, infiltrations, raids, and sabotage. U.S. and ROK troops fought and died in silence, repelling North Korean infiltrators in mist-shrouded forests, freezing outposts, and mountain passes no journalist ever saw.

Forgotten by history, this was the DMZ War. Now—finally—you can play it.

What You Get

     The DMZ War Campaign Book (Digital)
     A fully playable campaign setting covering 1966–1969 with scenarios, unit rosters, a campaign framework, and concise historical context.
     Solo & Cooperative Missions
     Patrol the wire. Raid enemy positions. Escort civilians. Sweep tunnels. Every scenario supports solo or group play—at home on the tabletop or in VTT.
     Authentic Forces & Equipment
     Field historically grounded U.S., ROK, and North Korean units with period weapons and kit for gritty Cold War skirmishing.
     Terrain, Civilians & Tunnel Rules
     Barbed wire and OP towers, booby traps, sapper raids, and tunnel breaches—rules that add danger and immersion without bloat.
     Historical Briefings
     Narrative briefs, real-world map references, and scenario notes drawn from declassified sources to frame each operation.


Inside the Campaign

     Play Modes: solo, Co-op, or Versus (Patrols, Ambushes, Raids, Infiltration, Tunnel Sweeps, Escort/Rescue)
     Forces     U.S. infantry & MPs, ROK light infantry, North Korean infiltrators/sappers, civilians & guides
     Rules Modules     Observation posts, tripflares, mines/booby traps, tripwire alarms, night & weather, tunnels & breaches, ROE & escalation
     Tables & Aids     Encounter/event tables, force lists, period kit notes, patrol tracks, mission generators


Why This Project?
Because these soldiers fought a war that history tried to erase. No medals. No headlines. Just tension, duty, and survival. The DMZ War brings their stories to the tabletop with respectful, scenario-driven play that emphasizes mission friction over monster stats.

What You’ll Need:

     Core rules: NUTS! or FNGfrom Two Hour Wargames.
     Minis/terrain (optional): 15–28mm infantry, wire obstacles, bunkers/OP towers, forest/jungle scatter, tunnel entrances.
     Dice & markers: Per your chosen system (reaction dice, turn/condition markers, etc.).


While this book can be used with NUTS! WW2, it is designed for use with the Vietnam rules FNG – Tour of Duty. 
In FNG: Tour of Duty, you’ll step into the dusty, muddy boots of a Grunt—a Boonierat who slogs through jungles and rice paddies, and fights house to house in Hue City and villages across Vietnam. You will be Infantry, but you will encounter people of different Professions during your Tour of Duty that will shape your Missions, aid or hinder your campaign, and build your story.

In this game taking place in the capital of Vietnam, players fight each other by playing their cards, representing the different road users in Hanoi.

Each round, all yhe players draw the top card from their drawing pile. The current leader chooses one of the four stats available on the card and compare it with the other players' card. The owner of the card with the highest value wins all the cards played that round and add them to the base of their drawing pile, and becomes the winner for the next round.

The first playe to win all the cards wins.

boardgame: Just a Theory, a Team Theorist Card Game
description:
From the creators of Game Theorist comes Just A Theory — the ultimate party game for thinkers, creators, and fans of hilarious debates! Compete with up to 6 players to collect theory tiles from four fun categories: Game, Film, Food, and Style. Draw prompt cards packed with creative challenges, come up with your best theory, and win over your fellow players to score points. Each set includes question cards, live cards, writing pads, dry erase markers, and trophy tiles for nonstop laughs and friendly competition. The best theory wins—so think fast, argue smart, and claim ultimate bragging rights!

Fun party game inspired by the Game Theorist team
Compete to collect theory tiles across 4 entertaining categories
Includes question cards, scoreboards, markers, and trophy tiles
Designed for up to 6 players—perfect for parties or game nights
Encourages creativity, quick thinking, and hilarious debates

A compact soft-cover rulebook published by Bayonet Publications, designed for miniature wargaming in the Napoleonic era. It covers both large-scale and smaller engagements, with rules for infantry, cavalry, artillery and supporting units.

When humans suddenly abandoned Purrburg, leaving an entire city to the delight of their pets, the animals quickly mastered the basics of civilization: they moved into houses, found jobs at banks, started construction companies and even announced a mayoral election! The winner will be the one who turns their district into a model of comfort. But everyone's path to victory is different: while some are busy beautifying parks, others are... plotting against their rivals.

Purrburg is a competitive card-based city-building game where you'll develop your own district while ruthlessly sabotaging your opponents' plans. Construct and demolish buildings, play Decree cards, set other players up and don't forget to keep an eye on your budget: not all ambitions are within your means!

—description from the publisher

In a Pirate Theme, you will set sail to discover mythical locations and precious memories.  Players will try to block your progress and put creatures on your path like Sirens and the legendary Kraken.

An easy and quick competitive card game, with humor, colorful illustrations, Pirates and Sirens.

A typical game unfolds in rounds where each players plays his turn one after the other.  Each player starts with a location card and 2 memory cards in hand.

In a turn the player :

1- Explore : Draw a Memory card or discard a Memory card to draw a Location card

2- Encounter at sea : Choose between 
a) Adventure : Draw a card and face your destiny, then discard that card
b) Duel : Fight another player to try to take one of his cards

3 - Port
a) Place a card 
b) Discard a card if you can't do the first action

End of turn : discard any cards over hand limit of 5 cards.  If no cards left, draw a location card.

End of game is when a player validate 2 locations.

boardgame: Wandering Towers: Let Someone Skip a Turn Promo Card
description:
Promo Card for Wandering Towers

Effect:
Whoever casts this spell may make another person skip a turn. To do so, place the spell in front of the person who is to skip a turn. When it's that person's turn, they simply return the spell to the general supply. After that, their turn is over.

This promo has printed German and English rules, but the tile itself has no text.

In Angler’s Instinct, players take on the roles of anglers who catch fish, upgrade their equipment, and develop their own ponds to become  the best fisherman and earn a place in the Book of Winners.
 The game blends elements of luck, planning, and competition, creating a strategic and dynamic fishing experience.
 At the beginning of the game, the player fishes on the main lake.
 As their career progresses, they expand their own pond strategically — in any way they choose.
 Both the pond and the main lake remain active fishing areas throughout the game, allowing players to freely decide where to cast their line at any time.
 The pond features several species of fish that do not live in the main lake, adding depth to the gameplay and encouraging thoughtful decisions.
 Additionally, the player upgrades their fishing equipment, allowing them to catch bigger and more valuable fish.
 The game offers complete freedom — the player decides how to grow and which path to follow in their fishing career.

Embark on 5 original quests based on the 1991 iteration and dare to take on 5 additional ones! Venture into darkness, vanquish foes, and encounter unlikely allies. Come face to face with some of the realm’s most feared wielders of Dread magic—the Wizards of Morcar—and keep a terrible power out of the evil sorcerer’s clutches.

Venture to the Northern Dread Wastes and stop Zargon from unlocking the legendary magic of 1000 Dread Sorcerers … before all is lost.
In this fantasy expansion pack, heroes may visit Hopekin’s Rest to acquire Boons of Heroism that help them progress. Hero spellcasters can now choose from 3 fresh sets of spells, and build magical barriers of ice, flame, or stone.

This Quest Pack introduces 5 powerful adversaries. Each is a dark Sorcerer with their own unique set of spells to use against the hero players.

Enjoy limitless replayability with many ways to win! You can also create your own stories and quests. Compatible with the HeroQuest base game & HeroQuest First Light board game.

Comes with 27 finely detailed miniatures (the Wizard Hero, Minotaur, Storm Master, Warcaster, Necromancer, High Mage, Artificer, Sir Ragner, 3 Magical Barriers, 3 Scouts, 3 Halberdiers, 3 Crossbowmen, 3 Swordsmen, 2 Dreadshifters, and 2 Golems). Also includes 64 game cards with updated custom artwork.

Invasion of Kaldora is a strategic, hero-driven war game for 3–5 players set in the medieval-fantasy world of Kaldora. Players command asymmetric armies across a modular battlefield, conquering castles, harvesting resources, and leading legendary heroes into combat. This second edition reimagines the original Invasion: The Conquest of Kings with streamlined rules, improved components, and the full Heroes of Kaldora expansion bundled in.

Game Overview

At its core, Invasion blends area control, resource management, and tactical combat. Each turn, players recruit units, position armies, and leverage their hero’s unique powers to outmaneuver opponents and seize the kingdom's strongholds. Battles are resolved quickly but strategically, using custom dice and situational bonuses.

Victory is earned by claiming a set number of castles or defeating your opponents through tactical dominance.

Second Edition Improvements

A more compact box and redesigned insert for easier setup and storage

Streamlined gameplay, reducing playtime by 20–30 minutes

Polished ruleset and clarified cards based on reviewer and player feedback

Support for a 5th player when combining editions

Fully integrated Tabletop Simulator mod for digital play

Heroes of Kaldora Expansion

The expansion introduces 15 new playable classes across 5 unique fantasy races:


     Elves – Masters of precision and magic
     Orcs – Brutal aggressors
     Humans – Balanced and tactical
     Undead – Disruptive and relentless
     Dwarves – Resilient and terrain-savvy


Each class introduces new abilities, passive traits, and strategic combos. Whether you're flanking with an Elf Ranger, tunneling through mountains with a Dwarf Tunneler, or raising the dead as a Necromancer, no two battles feel the same.

Highlights

Modular map tiles allow infinite battlefield configurations

Dice-based combat with modifiers for terrain, positioning, and hero synergy

Easy to learn, yet deeply tactical and replayable

Faction-based asymmetry — draft your favorite race or play balanced mixed rosters

Ideal for players who love quick setup, strategic movement, and cinematic conflict

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
You are humanity’s last line of defense against the feathered menace! In The Great Emu War, relive the most ridiculous war ever fought — one man versus nature… and nature is winning.

It’s 1932, but history took a strange turn. Some of the emus have developed mysterious, almost magical powers.

Play solo or with a friend across scenario-based missions that mix real historical absurdity with fantastical chaos. Every turn brings clucks, confusion, and cunning bird tactics as you scramble to restore peace to the desert.

Simple rules. Unpredictable outcomes. Endless laughter.

The Great Emu War — history’s strangest battle just got even weirder.

—description from the designer

You are pressed for time, dear sister of mine.
The chant has been cast, the spell has been said.
All colour shall drain, from your flower bed!
Then what was already evident, will be clear to see!
The better sorcerer between us, is undoubtedly me!

RAINBOSIO! is a quick bag-building game for one player. The goal of the game is to restore the colour back to your garden using your spells! You choose a single flower at the start of the game, and try to restore its petals one by one before your evil twin sister can wither the flower.

Every turn you draw cubes from your bag, and match colours to cast one of your many spells. These spells can help you colour your flower, or manipulate the cubes in your bag so it becomes easier to get matching colours in the future.

However! Whenever the bag is empty, your evil twin sisters withers a few petals. Can you bring your flower back to life before it wilts away?

Watch as your garden grows more colourful with every game you win!
--Or more dreary with every one you lose!

A total of 16 pages to colour in!
Enjoy:


     7 different gardens to save (that's 304 flowers, in case you were afraid of running out)
     15 unique spells: each garden has its own special combination of these spells, completely changing how you play the game!
     2 pages of free space, so the creative mages among you can mint a few spells of your own


Plenty to do with just 16 pages!

-Description from the publisher.

1778, America.

While British command relies on its vast power, loyal soldiers, and the court of King George, George Washington forms a secret agency to watch over the occupied New York and extend American influence across the Atlantic.

Players take the side of the Americans, the British, or one of the European powers to compete in gathering and delivering intelligence on the unfolding conflict, while also building their nation's influence through diplomatic ties with France, Spain and the Netherlands.

In each turn, players will play cards, which may be used in multiple ways to overcome suspected enemies whose identity will remain secret unless they choose to reveal themselves. Victory goes to the side that controls the most cities and creates the strongest intelligence network by the final round of the game.

—description from the designer

The Lookout is a promo card that works like a tree and costs two cards to play. It allows you to search up to the top five cards of the deck for a cloven-hoofed animal if there's none in the clearing, and put the one you found in your forest. All other cards you searched go to the clearing. At the end of the game, it scores one point for each boot symbol in your forest.

In this elephantastic Monopoly Junior game, join Benjamin Blümchen for an exciting stroll through Neustadt Zoo! Meet him, explore his world, and get to know all the adorable zoo babies. Buy the spaces where they play - or pay rent if someone got there first.

Play with Benjamin Blümchen and discover the Neustadt Zoo and its charming residents! Includes 4 Benjamin playing pieces.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Two warriors face off in this tense two-player pocket game! You and your opponent take turns adding cards – representing your various attacks, defenses, and combat tricks – to a single, shared queue. When you're both ready, reveal and resolve the queue and watch as a lightning-fast chain of moves and counter-moves unfolds! With death only a few wrong moves away at any point, you'll have to play around what you expect your opponent to do, and use careful planning and deception to line up the perfect killing blow. Expect an always-fresh mix of mind-games and explosive action out of every game!

—description from the designer

Carcassonne: Bonusplättchen Spiel 2025

This single-tile mini-expansion can be added to any Carcassonne game. The design, however, matches with the New Edition of Carcassonne (2014).

This promo was given out (for free) when making a purchase (of at least 15€) at the Hans im Glück booth at Spiel 2025, Essen.

This tile presents a city segment with two opponent fields. No specific rules are included with the tile. The tile is simply mixed with the other landscape tiles. When a player draws the tile, he or she should place the tile according to the usual rules.

Brought to you by the same team that created the first two games of the series, designers Christopher Vorder Bruegge, Mark McLaughlin with Fred Schachter; Ancient Civilizations of East Asia (ACEA) is not a sequel nor expansion, but a STAND ALONE game using the same MULTIPLAYER AND SOLITAIRE base systems as its predecessors, but with many new and exciting features to intrigue and challenge its players.

When getting ready to play the next game of GMT’s Ancient Civilization Series, check your feelings at the temple door as you and up to five other players hurl floods, plagues, volcanoes and earthquakes of truly karmic proportions at each other and then send hordes of rampaging ravenous barbarians to loot and pillage what remains in this “Take That!” game not of merely civilization BUILDING but primarily of civilization SURVIVAL!

With Ancient Civilizations of East Asia, you will struggle to build, conquer, and most of all SURVIVE this epic clash of cultures and peoples on a map that stretches from Java to Japan, and from the shores of the Philippine Sea to the slopes of the Himalayas. It is a “Take That!” game, which simulates the historic forces, both natural and man-made, which so ravaged and stultified civilizations’ progress.

—description from the publisher

Mini Expansion for Catan board game. A benevolent ram that awards players an extra wool in Catan when rolling the number and the conditions are met.

When you receive this card, place the ram standee on a terrain hex where you and at least one other person have built a settlement/city (this does not have to be a pasture).

Production: If the number of this terrain hex is rolled, everyone with at least one settlement/city on this terrain hex receives exactly one additional wool. Then give this card to the person who just rolled the production. If you rolled the production yourself, "Mowlin" stays with you (in this case, too, you have to move the standee).

South Korea's #1 Card Game for over 50 years, now with brand new illustrations and a board game aspect.
Godori Hwatu is a renewed version of the traditional Korean card game  “Go-Stop” , featuring brand-new compelling illustrations and additional playing rules.

Traditionally, Hwatu cards are made of a thick waterproof PVC (plastic) material, with a glossy coating on the front illustrations and a texture backside. The standard Hwatu card size is 3.5 cm x 5.5 cm. To ensure the highest quality of production, we've chosen to produce our plastic cards through a manufacturer in South Korea.

A deck of Godori Hwatu contains 48 cards, divided into 12 suits (flowers); each flower represents a month of the year.

Godori Hwatu includes the traditional plastic deck as well as a standard playing card deck(6.9 x 4.3cm).

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ShadowSea: The Stygian Depths – Lost Temple of Xibalba
description:
The Stygian Depths, Lost Temple of Xibalba is a supplement for ShadowSea, providing rules for adventures into the dungeons located deep in the heart of the underground world. This PDF version of the game includes the Stygian Depths rulebook, which has everything needed to play, map tiles, game cards and creature markers. The cards, tiles and markers are all provided at 300 DPI for printing.

The main entrance to the Stygian Depths is through the sunken city of Xibalba, located in Blood Reef on the coasts of the Shadow Sea. Many of the ziggurat pyramids in Xibalba are partially submerged and the structures at the top have doorways that allow adventurers to reach the horrors below. There, serpentine monstrosities and their hideous demonic servants guard magical artifacts and golden treasures while plotting their return to power. Warbands from any of the forces of  ShadowSea or DeepWars may enter into the Stygian Depths in search of gold and ancient magical items, as well as for the glowing crystals that power all ether technology. The only restriction is that warbands need to have Amphibious models in the party, as fully marine ones will not be able to navigate the dry parts of the dungeon.

Entering the sunken temple of Xibalba
exploring the catacombs
Adventures in the deep caverns are played on the tabletop using “tiles” to represent stretches of tunnels and rooms. The tiles are arranged by drawing cards that show which rooms are to appear, so games can be played solo or cooperatively with another player. The ultimate goal of a game session is to complete a Quest Scenario, a narrative adventure with a special chamber at the end of the adventure that contains glittering treasures but is protected by challenging enemies and deadly environments.

catcomb cards
There are multiple Quest Scenarios that warbands may attempt, each of which has a special Quest Chamber, a larger floor tile with a unique layout. Players may also build their own layouts for competitive battles, having each player start in different sections of the map. The competing players then try to recover as much loot as possible while surviving the dangers of the temple.

Quest Chamber - Garden of Paradise
The main ShadowSea rulebook is not required to play The Stygian Depths as all the necessary rules for the scenery and creatures are included in this book. However, players should have the main rulebook as reference for any spell lists and special item effects used by the adventuring warband that are not included here. The game rules are based upon the Song of Blades and Heroes (SOBH) ruleset by Ganesha Games and are designed for fast play with plenty of heroic action and arcane magic, resolved with a few rolls of 6-sided dice.

The whole set comes as a zip file with the rulebook, map tiles, cards and markers.

The rulebook comes as a full-color PDF file with 52 pages of dungeon exploration, with 79 pages of cards, map tiles, markers and counters. ShadowSea is based on the award-winning Song of Blades and Heroes ruleset.

—description from the publisher

After meeting an untimely end in a saloon poker game turned deadly, you awake in Purgatory. Imbued with new powers, and a set of poker cards made from your very soul, you must play your way through the three gates of Purgatory and into the afterlife.

Before the game, choose your character, each with their own abilities and upgrades.

Deal cards into the river and resolve enemy effects. Then draw 3 cards and use them to manipulate the river, unlock gates, and fill your bank.

Activate abilities by forming poker hands. Use those abilities to defeat enemies.

Gain rewards by defeating minions, and level up by defeating bosses.

Escape Purgatory by unlocking three gates and defeating the boss behind each!

-description from publisher

During setup, take 1 Order card from the base game at random and return it to the box. Shuffle the Country Promo card into the remaining Order cards to form the deck.
COMPONENTS:
2 cards (43x67mm):

1 ITALIA Country Order card (Cappuccino e Cornetto; Ingredients = Coffee, Milk, Steam)
1 Title/Rules card

Irregular English verbs can be fun too! What usually puts people in a bad mood quickly becomes easy with TRIPLE English.


         CONTENTS
    


TRIPLE English consists of the game instructions and 74 playing cards – 68 verb cards plus 6 jokers. The verb cards are divided into four categories and a total of 16 verb groups according to the TRIPLE principle.

Within each group, the verb forms are formed according to the same pattern, with the decisive factors being the changes in the stem vowel and the endings added (in the second and/or third form).


         RULES OF THE GAME
    


The rules of the game are easy to understand, and the game includes common aspects of many card games: drawing cards from the deck, laying out certain card combinations, placing individual cards, and using jokers tactically.

It is particularly important to ask for verb cards. This is because, as with laying out cards, each player always names all three forms of the verb. They do not need to know these in advance, as they can read them. This gives everyone the same chance.

-translated description from the publisher homepage

Join the Posse! is a solo tabletop adventure game where you are the Sheriff in a small western town. Every game you will lead a posse into danger and strive to bring them back safe through a battle against those who would threaten the peace and tranquility of the town or the local area. Each game takes the form of a battle between the Posse and another group, and sometimes includes some additional task or conditions to fulfill to keep your adventure progressing.
 The game is not aiming to be in any way a detailed simulation of armed combat instead it concentrates on getting to the action as quickly as possible. All rolls in battle are 1D10, with a natural 1 being bad and a natural 10 being good. A single roll takes into account hitting, penetrating, and wounding. Weapons are generic. It’s not a wargame, it’s an adventure, or that’s the intent anyway.

The core is a set of 6 Story Arcs (campaigns), each of 15 games, that tell a story.  Each Arc pulls 10 of the scenarios from a random pool 34 and has 5 specific ones that create the main story.

Every game has markers for where the adversaries might be and your team have to uncover the actual foes.  The adversaries always “React” to the team, hence true solo play rather than trying to play both sides. There are also often Bystanders getting in the way.

Your posse will usually be six figures and will be fighting anywhere up to 12 enemy figures, plus there may be up to 8 bystanders wandering about.  A battle lasts 6 to 10 rounds, so you can set up your table, play through the game, and pack it down again in under 3 hours, probably 2 once you are used to it.

There are a selection of different locations (and therefore table layouts) where the action takes place, a selection of different adversaries, and a slow but steady increase in difficulty to balance the improvements and added gear your posse will get along the way.

These 66 pages include the 6 Story Arcs, full rules for gameplay, a 3 page Quick Reference for the battle tables, and an example campaign roster/tracker. You will of course need your own miniatures and terrain pieces, but the scenarios are as open as possible to enable you to use the stuff you already have, or give you an excuse to collect more.

-description from designer

The animals are gathering for the annual Zoo Deduction Contest—and even the zookeeper, Abo, has joined in disguise! In this family-friendly deduction game, players secretly hold animal cards with different attributes. Each turn, you can place truthful tokens, peek at hidden animals, or boldly guess others’ cards. Outsmart your friends by piecing together clues, exposing hidden identities, and earning points. With dual-attribute creatures, parallel world animals, and the mischievous Abo keeping everyone guessing, every round is a lively battle.

—description from the publisher

Sudoku 7 turns the classic Sudoku into a competitive game. Each person manages seven pieces on a 6x6 board, aiming to outscore your opponents by filling rows, columns, and blocks. Solving challenges while setting puzzles for others. It has been loved by players who enjoy math-inspired games. 

—description from the publisher

NO.12 is a minimalist deduction challenge. Four genius teenagers wake up in a locked white room. There are six red capsules and six blue ones. Over four rounds, players secretly take capsules until everyone has three. Whoever ends up with exactly two red capsules becomes “No.12.” If the others can figure out who No.12 is, they win—unless No.12 can reveal the capsule combinations of the others and steal victory. A tense battle of logic and observation.

—description from the publisher

CLOMINO is a simple yet strategic puzzle game. Each turn, Pick a tile, rotate it, flip it, and fit it perfectly onto your growing mosaic. Once a tile is fully surrounded, you can clone it—placing scoring tokens where it once was and giving yourself the freedom to position it anywhere else on your board. Try to trigger chain reactions of clones, every placement is a moment to strategize.

This pack contains 4 cards: one card for every player count (2-5).

It has to be played with the original Zombielife Insurance base game.

The new card is meant as a replacement for the original card called "Toilet Roll Grab" for a new and alternative effect.

Scapegoat effect: "Whenever you flip a health token, play this card and gain another action on your next turn. Using this card will not use any action."

Cross the threshold of the dream realm and become the architect of your own dream. Draw tiles from the bag and cleverly compose your game board, populating it with symbolic figures drawn from the deepest recesses of the subconscious. The goal is to acquire the best dream characters, but the true key to mastering this ephemeral world lies in your relationship with the Sandman, the man of dreams from Northern European folklore. This enigmatic being, at once benevolent and elusive, will offer you his aid. Accepting his gift will grant you a strategic advantage, but beware: for every favor he grants you, you will have to give up something. You will decide what price you are willing to pay.

Save the ancient civilization of Mu from  decline, by increasing population, knowledge, commerce and technology. A solo card set-collection game, with eight levels of difficulty, each of them to be won with through strict combo requirements, in a very challenging timing. An extra scenario is added with a science-fictional theme.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Beavers have gathered at the peaceful and beautiful riverbank. They build sturdy homes by stacking variously shaped pieces of wood.

On your turn, roll two dice.
Place a wood block on the corresponding location.
If a space with three sides made of wood blocks becomes available, place a beaver block in that space.
If the conditions are met, you can place multiple beaver blocks at once.
The first player to place their beaver block on the board wins.

boardgame: Trash Orbit: Vaga Trade Hub Rumble
description:
Players act as one of 3 Scrap chaser's (humanoid Raccoons) in the Vega trading hub, fighting over a prize in a drunken brawl they do not even know if it even has any value. Scrap for the sake of it!

Players will need to use the action cards and cover on the map to outlast the other players. Win by being the last player left on the board.

The dice roll determines which outcome happens, so use strategy to try and not get caught in your own attacks.

Contents Provided
The game includes 30 action cards, an 8 by 8 player board, Rules and lore.

You Will Need:
Two six-sided dice, twelve tokens to track health (four for each player), and three tokens to represent the players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: SETI: Search for Extraterrestrial Intelligence – DLR Promo Card
description:
Available as a promo card at Spiel Essen 2025.

The card has a cost of two credits, and it allows the player to launch a probe and to take a a resource depending on the colour of the sector you are in.

In addition it reads:
The German Aerospace Center (DLR) conducts earth observation missions and high altitude research using high-tech equipment such as the mobile rocket base MORABA.

If you enjoy music quizzes and want a social, engaging game night, QR Music Cards offers a thrilling mix of nostalgia, competition and variety. With numerous editions available, there's one for every music interest - from 90’s & 00’s to Hardstyle, Song Contest and beyond.

What is it?
A card-based music quiz powered by QR codes linked to Spotify Premium. Each edition (genre, decade or special themes) contains 150 unique cards, each showing a QR code on one side and details like the song title, artist, and year on the other. The free app “QR Music Cards” can be used to scan the QR codes. It plays the song through Spotify Premium immediately.
Game modes

Easy (Higher or Lower): Guess if the song’s release year is before or after a shown card.
    Medium (Two of Three): Name artist/title and the decade to earn a point.
    Expert (Music Battle): First to correctly name the artist, title and decade after the song plays wins the round.

Rounds continue until a player or team reaches 10 points.

In this Eurovision Song Contest edition, you'll play songs from 1956–2024 and artists like Udo Jürgens, Céline Dion, Conchita Wurst, Nemo, and Måneskin.

boardgame: Disney's Phineas and Ferb: Summer Showdown
description:
A fast 'n fun card game for the whole family! Play as your favorite Phineas and Ferb character as you go on Summer adventures and try to outmaneuver your opponents!

In Disney's Phineas and Ferb: Summer Showdown, players can choose to play as their favorite Phineas and Ferb characters, and compete to collect the most points over the course of three rounds.

Before each round, an event card is flipped, which will affect the entire round.

Each round has nine turns consisting of just four steps: Select, Place Face-Down, Reveal, and Resolve. A unique, once-per-game character ability will shake things up in this wildly fun game. A unique, once-per-game character ability shakes things up in this wildly fun, outrageously replayable Blammo game!

Step into the shadowy halls of Nevermore Academy to save the school from assured destruction in this officially licensed social deduction game for 4–8 players ages 10+, based on the hit show Wednesday.

Crackstone’s followers are trying to collect body parts to revive Crackstone himself! But Wednesday and her fellow students are trying to prevent that from happening by successfully investigating suspicious activity. Will you uncover the conspiracy in time, or will you be secretly working to ruin Nevermore’s reputation?

Each player assumes a unique character from the beloved series Wednesday, with hidden motives and abilities that kick in when your identity is revealed. Your mission is to use your cards to successfully investigate a series of mysteries, deduce the true identities of your fellow players, and thwart the devious plot before it’s too late. Or perhaps you will work to thwart the investigations and leave Nevermore’s reputation in tatters.

The only thing that’s for sure is that you can’t trust anyone.

-description from designer

boardgame: Kampf gegen das Spießertum: Die goldene Dekadenz Promo Cards
Ich habe Fertig: Ich bin der Boss! Promo Card

description:
Terrae is a geography game where players are each given a hand of regions (e.g. Western Europe, Southeast Asia) and 5 categories (e.g. Land Area, Population Density). Players play sets of cards in order according to a category from their hand. If they are correct, they can leave their cards on the table. Players can start new sets of cards or add cards to existing sets. The player that empties their hand first is the winner.

From the official website:

"Terrae is a strategic and dynamic card game where geography meets tactics. Collect countries, arrange them according to your secret goal, and outsmart opponents with powerful special cards that can shake the game at any moment. Will you build the most accurate sets and claim victory?"

This expansion introduces new secrets, challenges, and opportunities. Familiar paths now lead to unexpected dangers, while unforeseen encounters and obstacles cross players’ ways. The expansion encourages exploration, strategic adaptation, and cooperation as players navigate these evolving scenarios.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Diese Erweiterung fügt neue Geheimnisse, Herausforderungen und Chancen hinzu. Scheinbar vertraute Wege führen nun zu unerwarteten Gefahren, während überraschende Begegnungen und Hindernisse den Spielern begegnen. Die Erweiterung fördert Erkundung, strategische Anpassung und Zusammenarbeit, während die Spieler diese sich entwickelnden Szenarien meistern.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ironclads! Naval Warfare in the Age of Sails and Steam
description:
Ironclads! is a relatively fast play set of rules covering naval warfare, along with its associated land combat, set in the ironclad era of 1860-1879.

Don't turn your nose up at the fast play designation. Fast play does not in the least mean lacking in era ambience or that the rules are simplistic in any way. Far from it!

Ironclads! covers all aspects of naval warfare of the era, from fleet actions, such as the Battle of Lissa, to the riverine fights of the American Civil War. Also included are well thought out rules for land combat that includes a unique system that separates and qualifies army training and armament. Thus the rules reflect not only how well trained soldiers are, but also how well they are equipped.

Ironclads! uses exclusively six sided dice and has easy to learn and exceedingly smooth mechanics that make the game a joy to play.

Ironclads!  features all the things 4P Press games are known for, such as an in-depth QRS, generous player aids (including our excellent turning circles), continuous updates and support, and of course the usual extensive listing of ratings, this time featuring 1,324 individual ships from 29 era navies.

As if that all weren't enough, Ironclads! includes extensive advice on scenario and campaign development, as well as several scenario examples, such as an in depth Battle of Lissa scenario with complete orders of battle.

Please note, to insure quality reproduction, the turning circles are in PDF form and in a separate downloadable file.

Sequence of Play:
Game play is divided into four phases. Each side completes a phase before the next one begins.
1. Movement Phase
Initiative is determined and then ships are moved. Movement is not simultaneous.
2. Gunnery Phase
Gunnery is conducted simultaneously, thus it is possible for a ship to be sunk but still get off one last salvo.
3. Torpedo/Mine Phase
All torpedo and mine attacks are carried out.
4. Damage Control Phase
Ships attempt to repair battle damage which occurred in the previous gunnery phases.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Epochs: Course of Cultures – Middle East and Latin America Expansion
description: Civilization boards for Babylon, Persia, Arabia, Maya, Aztecs and Inca, as well as Powerful Barbarians and new map tiles.

My Dear Yi: A fast-play skirmish wargame of ash, steel, and thunder.

Lead Joseon militias, tiger hunters, and Ming allies against samurai, ashigaru, and wako raiders across ramparts, rice paddies, and harbor landings. 
My Dear Yi is built for quick, cinematic fights where every draw from the bag and every counter-move matters. 
This title adds tools for the Imjin War: artillery (mortar & hwacha), ships-as-terrain, detachments, and formal duels, while staying fully cross-compatible with other My Dear games.

What you need to play: 

     a measuring tape (inches), 
     a handful of d6, 
     a draw bag with tokens, and 
     a few miniatures at whatever scale you prefer.

My Dear Cortez: A fast-play skirmish wargame of steel, smoke, and obsidian.

Lead a handful of hardened soldados or fierce Mesoamerican warriors into tight streets, temple courtyards, and jungle approaches. 
My Dear Cortez is built for quick, cinematic fights where every draw from the bag and every counter-move matters.

What you need to play: 

     a measuring tape (inches), 
     a handful of d6, 
     a draw bag with tokens, and 
     a few miniatures at whatever scale you prefer.

THE APEX PUSH is a fast-paced and tense DUEL-style game where strategy, not strength, wins the match.

Like a real fight, it takes place over several ROUNDS (maximum 5).

The objective is to move the TENSION PAWN five spaces into your opponent’s zone before they do the same to you. The two players compete with cards in hand, and they can use their ACTION cards, boosted by their accumulated POWER, to move the pawn forward. But be careful! As the rounds progress, the difficulty increases rapidly. The tension pawn offers a progressive bonus, accelerating the pace of the match. In fact, in each subsequent round, the tension pawn can be moved forward one space further than in the previous round!

LET THE MENTAL ARM WRESTLE BEGIN!

-description from publisher

A werewolf style game featuring characters from the anime "Dropkick on my devil", meant for 6 or more players. Jashin-chan leads the zombie army and Yurine leads the Jinbocho townsfolk. If the townsfolk kill all of the zombies, they win. If the number of zombies is equal to the number of townsfolk, the zombies win. Originally released in 2020 through the anime's merchandise website.

Story (translated from the game's press release):

One evening while shopping for dinner, Jashin-Chan noticed something strange.

A deep fog formed over the empty streets and Medusa's screams rang out through the town.

In one evening, Jinbocho had become a city of death, where zombies roam freely.

Players must discuss and find the zombies hidden among them. If even one remains, someone will die every night.

In the face of Jinbocho's unprecedented crisis, Jashin-Chan thinks "If Yurine dies and becomes zombie, can I return to the demon world?"

In the end, will Yurine stay alive and return to her peaceful daily life?

Or will Jashin-Chan turn Yurine into a zombie and finally kill her?

description:
The game involves a series of challenging movie themed questions, including multiple choice and true/false. Each card has two questions on it that the reader can choose from. The reader can challenge another player to ask a question to knock them out. The last person remaining in a round earns a movie-themed picture card. The first player to get four picture cards wins the game - but be careful about people asking you "steal" questions where they can grab your card if you get it wrong!

There is also a two player version with players asking each other questions. The reader earns a picture card if the other person gets a question wrong. First to seven picture cards is the winner.

From back of the box: 
So you think you know movies? Includes 20 picture cards, 100s of questions and braggin' rights with every win!

The reader can challenge another player to ask a question to knock them out. Each round, the last person remaining gets a picture card with a famous Disney or Pixar character. The reader can challenge another player to ask a question to knock them out. Each round, the last person remaining gets a musical themed picture card. The first player to get four picture cards wins the game - but be careful about people asking you "steal" questions where they can grab your card if you get it wrong!

boardgame: Say What You See: Movie Edition
description: A quick-fire game about guessing the phrase based on the cryptic image cards. Contains 50 catchphrase cards with different images and symbols on them that all represent different movies. The first player to guess 5 cards correctly is the winner!

Monopoly is a classic board game and party game where players buy, sell, and trade properties to build empires. The winner is the one who bankrupts their opponents. The Association of "The Most Beautiful Villages in Italy" takes you on a journey to discover the most hidden corners of the most beautiful country in the world. In this version of MONOPOLY, the images of the small "villages of Italy" replace the classic ones of streets and squares. Renovate old houses, develop scattered hotels, and win the game!

Mint Tin Size 18 card Solo Game

In Medusa's Vigil, you play as the legendary Gorgon defending her home from escalating invasions. Through strategic movement, resource management, and meaningful choices, you'll turn invaders to stone, befriend potential allies, and sanctify chambers to restore power to your labyrinth. Every game tells a new story of protection, redemption, and the strength found in solitude.


        Game Description:**
    


Medusa's Vigil is a solo, 10-20-minute card game where you defend Medusa's labyrinth from invaders. Using a 3x3 grid of chamber cards and a deck of intruder cards, you'll manage two key resources: **Serpent's Eyes** (to turn enemies to stone) and **Memory** (to sanctify chambers or befriend commoners). The intruder deck acts as an automated opponent, deploying Warriors, Priests, and Commoners with unique behaviors that create dynamic threats and moral choices.


        How to Play:**
    


On your turn, you move Medusa to an adjacent chamber, then take one of these actions:
- **Turn an intruder to stone** (Spend Serpent's Eyes → Gain 1 Memory)
- **Befriend a Commoner** (Spend Memory → Gain an ongoing ally ability)
- **Sanctify the chamber** (Spend Memory → Flip card for powerful ongoing effect)

After your action, draw an intruder card and place its token on the board, resolving any "when placed" effects. Different intruder types create strategic challenges: Warriors escalate threats if ignored, Priests disable your abilities and corrupt Commoners, and Commoners can become allies or foes depending on Priest influence.


        How to Win:**
    


You win by sanctifying **The Outer Gate** chamber while all intruder tokens on the board have been turned to stone.


        How to Lose:**
    


You lose if:
- You need to spend a Serpent's Eye but have none
- You cannot place a new intruder token because all chambers are occupied or all intruders are on the board

You receive a letter from your father, a gold miner on the harsh  Arizona frontier. In it, he shares troubling news - a corrupt Army Captain and his lawless gang are threatening his claim.  Enclosed is a single gold nugget, proof of his discovery.

Weeks pass with no further word. Fearing the worst, you board a train west and arrive in Ironwood - a rough, lawless town clinging to the edge of civilization. There, you learn the truth - your father is being held captive by the Captain and is set to be hanged in 7 days for his false crimes. Time is running out, and only you can fight to save your father’s life.

Traverse a randomly generated frontier in search of Captain Bradley’s three henchmen — each guarding a vital item needed to infiltrate the Fort. On your turn, play two Trail cards beside your compass and resolve one. Each Trail may reveal enemies, native tribes, campsites, or pursuing lawmen who’ll raise your Wanted Level! Combat is resolved by choosing how many revolver shots to take and flipping multi-use cards to determine your damage. Once you’ve collected all three key items, storm Fort Bradley, take down the Captain, and rescue your father!

-description from designer

Welcome to Paleo Park!

Build the Ultimate Portable Dinosaur Park.

Take on the role of a park owner in this 2–4 player game where you’ll collect Research cards to try and acquire 10 unique dinosaur species for your park. Purchase attractions to boost your score, but manage your hand carefully— disasters can cost you valuable points. After three rounds, the park owner with the most points wins!

-description from designer

In Nightmare Tales, each player is a kid who has just gone to bed. The lights go out, and the darkness comes, with its terrors and unsettling sounds. Tucked in, lights off. A creak under your bed, a crackle near the wardrobe. Nightmares are coming and the night has just begun.

In this dynamic card game, memory and strategy come together in fast games full of twists. 
At first, players have only fears: four random face-down cards: mostly monsters and nightmares. Everyone looks at one of their cards and then put it back. The deck is put in the middle. Then the game starts. Once the game begins, players can’t look at cards unless an effect allows them to.

During their turn, a player draws a card, looks at it, then discards a card and can discard the card they just drew, or one of their cards. If a facedown card is discarded, the drawn card takes its place, facedown. Now funny and unexpected things happen: if the discarded card has an effect, the player must use its effect. Some effects are bad for you, some for everybody. But there are also allies: plushies, fearies and others, ready to help you get through your fears.

The game ends at the break of dawn. Dawn can arrive in three different ways: when a player has no cards, when the deck has no cards left, when a player uses the whole turn to knock twice on the deck triggering the last round. The round goes on until every player has played one last  turn. Then, Dawn comes. Everybody show their cards, the one with less fear wins the game.

Will you be the bravest kid?

-description from publisher

Build a haunted property complete with foreboding rooms, full of unpredictable, randomised items. Then, draw a Ghost Type card from the Investigation File. Each Ghost has its own powers, paired with an evidence pool randomised for each playthrough, offering a terrifying challenge for even the most seasoned ghost hunters.

Using the Ghost Behavior board the ghost’s actions become unpredictable, driven by abilities presenting ever-changing challenges that take a psychological toll. The threat escalates as your sanity deteriorates, giving way to more aggressive and dangerous manifestations. So make sure you prepare before heading inside: equip your team with the best ghost hunting equipment found in your Van.

Venture into the residence to uncover cursed possessions and ghostly phenomena before, at last, arriving at the Ghost Room. Can your team conquer its surroundings and gather paranormal evidence before escaping?

You win if you survive and guess the correct ghost. You lose if everyone dies.

boardgame: Mission to Planet Hexx!: 2026 Satellite Pack
description:
The 2026 Satellite Pack is a limited edition expansion to the Mission to Planet Hexx core game.

The 2026 Satellite Pack contains twenty-three cards that are balanced and ready to use. The contents can be shuffled into the core game deck or any other balanced custom deck and played immediately. The inside of the pack contains rule reminder text for mechanics such as All Play, Boosters, Continuous, Hybrid cards, and others, as these rules pertain to the cards inside the pack.

This set premieres a new kind of Space Hex card called a "Constellation". Constellations work in conjunction with other Constellations, each becoming more powerful when it is adjacent to others.

Twenty-three brand new cards. The set includes cards that:


     change your choice of Mission File 
     add counters to the game
     see all other Players' Hands
     steal Data from other Players
     Delete Hexes from your own Mission File
     get up to fourteen extra Actions in a turn
     win instantly!


—description from the publisher

- Strategic board game where each civilization chooses a representative player — civilizations include Phoenicians, Ancient Egypt, Native Americans, Vikings, Roman Empire, and Shogunate.

- Each civilization has a unique strategy, color, and play style to create distinct approaches and dynamics.

- The game also features powerful action cards, resource management, and strategic placement. Cards like Betrayal and Alliance can shift the balance at any moment, making each round unpredictable and unique. No two rounds are ever the same, ensuring a fresh and dynamic experience every time you play.

- Goal: move your warrior through three stages; the first to reach the final stage wins the game for their civilization.

- Game components: a total of 37 pieces — 3 tents, 2 fortresses, 1 Gealias warrior for each civilization in its own color, and 1 jail; a board with 5 types of resources, 20 numbered tokens placed on board resources, and gold tokens.

boardgame: Hold the Flank! British Armored Cars in Russia, 1917
description:
The Kerensky Offensive of 1917 was the last "big push" for Russia during World War I. Fighting in the thick of it were three British squadrons of armored cars, organized and commanded by Oliver Stillingfleet Locker-Lampson. A mix of Lanchesters, Rolls-Royces, Fords, and Pierce-Arrows, the armored cars were everywhere during the battle, surging ahead as Russian infantry stormed the German and Austro-Hungarian trenches, shooting it out in Kozowa as a burning ammunition dump threatened to explode, and escorting British ambulances during a massive Russian retreat.

Hold the Flank! features six scenarios designed for the Bigger Battles system of rules by Wiley Games, but can easily be adapted to other systems. 
It includes a historical overview of the British armored cars squadrons' role in the Kerensky Offensive, as well as excerpts from newspaper and magazine articles published at the time. Whether you want to play scenarios from the eastern front of the Great War or are just wanting to learn more about the Kerensky Offensive, this product is packed with solid gameplay and detailed historic information.

description:
Treble 1 is a strategic dart card game that transforms traditional darts into an engaging competitive experience for 2-4 players. Players draw target cards representing specific dartboard sections (singles, doubles, trebles, and bulls), then attempt to hit those targets with their darts to claim the cards and score points. The game combines the physical skill of darts with strategic hand management, as players must decide whether to attempt targets from their personal hand or compete for the two face-up "active targets" available to all players.

Each turn consists of selecting a target card, throwing three darts to hit it, and scoring based on success. Successfully hitting your chosen target lets you keep the card and add its point value to your score—singles are worth face value, doubles are worth 2x, and trebles are worth 3x. Miss your target, and the card returns to the bottom of the deck with no points earned. The tension builds as players balance attempting high-value treble 20s (worth 57 points) against safer single targets, while keeping an eye on what opponents are attempting.

Special wild cards add exciting strategic twists: the "Any Target" wild lets you choose your own section to hit, the "Single-Double-Treble" wild challenges you to hit all three ring types in one turn, and the powerful "Steal" wild allows you to claim a card directly from an opponent's completed pile. These cards can turn the tide of a game and create dramatic comeback opportunities.

The standard game plays to 300 points, but multiple variants are included: a "Collection Game" where the player with the most cards wins when the deck runs out, a fast-paced "Speed Challenge" with timed rounds, and even solo practice modes with daily challenges and achievement systems. Whether you're a darts veteran looking to add strategic depth to your throws or a casual player seeking a fun way to practice, Treble 1 delivers an accessible yet competitive experience that scales from ages 12+ through adult play.

-description from publisher

They thought we were crazy, but they’re the fools, I tell you. With the temporal transistors finally operational, we have the power to travel time - to past, present, future, and beyond. Oh, but the fuel must be managed carefully! One too many time jumps and we’ll be unable to make that fateful, final leap home. Who will master the timeline, and who may be lost in time forever?

Trick to the Future is a trick-taking game in which you use cards from your hand to time travel. You begin each round with a number of fuel rods on your player board, which may be used to change the values of your cards! Each trick you win represents a successful time jump and earns you points. In addition, you receive points for the spent fuel rods you collect during the round. However, at the end of a round, if you have more tricks won than unspent fuel rods, you score no points. After 3 rounds, the time traveler with the most points wins!

Cards use 7-segment digital numbers (like you would see on a digital clock). The "fuel rods" are additional segments that players can place on a card to change the number. For example, a 1 could become a 7 by adding a segment at the top.  Or a 7 could become a 0, using 3 fuel rods.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: FLOE: Bounty, the Blowan Promo Card

boardgame: Too Many Bones: Keine Promo Mini Erweiterung
description:
This exculsive Frosted Games Promo contains four cards with thematic link to the publisher. It is only available in German language. The cards are 3 normal Loot and 1 Trove Loot.

(Diese exklusive Frosted Games Promo enthält vier Karten mit thematischen Bezügen zum Verlag. Sie ist nur auf Deutsch erhältlich. Die Karten sind 3 normaler Loot und 4 Truhen.)

Relive the fury of World War II with Miniwars: The Battle of Prokhorovka, a pocket wargame that recreates the massive clash between German and Soviet armored forces in the summer of 1943.

In this tactical game played on a hexagonal map, each side controls armored and infantry units. The action is divided into Morning sub-turns (where units move and fight) and Night sub-turns (for recovering disabled units).

Unit control is managed via activation tokens which determine the order of play. Combat is a simple system that combines the movement of armored and infantry units with the crucial support of aviation and artillery to determine the outcome.

The game ends after eight turns. The player who controls the majority of the seven locations that award Victory Points will claim the victory.

Red the One-Eyed's treasure is at the Black Parrot Tavern! Place your bets, roll the dice, and try to win it all!

Goal of the game: Win by being the first to collect 20 gold coins, either by completing challenges or by betting on the success or failure of other players.

Game turn:
1. The captain takes the dice
2. Other players use their boat cards to make a bet on the success or failure of the captain.
3. The captain has 3 dice rolls to achieve the challenge card combination.
The captain can use their treasure map card to get an extra roll just once during the game.
- If the captain wins the challenge, they can collect 2 cards. Cards gives 2 or 3 gold coins.
- If the captain loses the challenge, they don’t win anything.
- Each other player who correctly guessed if the captain would win or lose wins their bet. They collect 1 card.

Play then passes to the player on the captain’s left.

-description from designer

A promo card for all Mindbug sets, distributed at SPIEL Essen fair in 2025.

Name: Ivybug
Power: 5
Keywords: none
Text: You may use your life as Mindbugs.
Card number: P1 / 2025

A magnetic cooperative game where players take turns collecting fruit from colored plates using a magnetic magic wand, while avoiding collecting too much sugar!

Each turn is played blindfolded, with active player guided by the all team.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Jeu de coopération aimanté dans lequel les joueurs doivent rassembler les fruits dans des assiettes de couleurs en les attrapants à tour de rôle à l’aide de la baguette magique magnétique et en évitant de collecter trop de sucre !

Chaque tour se déroule les yeux bandés, en se laissant guider par son équipe.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dying Message: Jun Sasaki, Fumiko Shimizu, Hiroki Takahashi Promo Cards
description:
This set of three promo cards was given with purchase at SPIEL 2025. The cards feature three characters:

Jun Sasaki, a game designer who enjoys watching animal documentaries and collecting cicada larvae in summer to observe their hatching on his balcony. He has recently struggled with deteriorating eyesight.

Fumiko Shimizu, a puzzle designer specializing in Japanese puzzles including classics, with a background in chemistry. She is not skilled at crafting or 3D puzzles and travels across Japan searching for new puzzles.

Hiroki Takahashi, a magician and master of card tricks, though he often forgets his cards at home. Not the type who conjures doves from hats, he has tried aerial tricks but gave up due to a fear of heights.

These promo cards expand the flavor of the game by introducing personalities and backgrounds of notable contributors.

•••

Dieses Set von drei Promo-Karten wurde auf der SPIEL 2025 beim Kauf des Spiels vergeben. Die Karten stellen drei Charaktere vor:

Jun Sasaki, Spieleautor, schaut gerne Tierdokumentationen und sammelt im Sommer Zikadenlarven, um deren Schlüpfen auf seinem Balkon zu beobachten. In letzter Zeit kämpft er mit nachlassendem Sehvermögen.

Fumiko Shimizu, Rätselautorin, spezialisiert auf japanische Rätsel einschließlich Klassikern, hat Chemie studiert. Sie ist nicht geschickt im Basteln oder bei 3D-Puzzles und reist durch ganz Japan auf der Suche nach Rätseln.

Hiroki Takahashi, Magier und Meister von Kartentricks, vergisst seine Karten jedoch oft zu Hause. Nicht der Typ, der Tauben aus Hüten zaubert; er hat Flugtricks ausprobiert, diese aber aufgrund von Höhenangst aufgegeben.

Die Promo-Karten erweitern das Spiel um die Persönlichkeit und Hintergründe dieser bekannten Mitwirkenden.

Order Overload: Oink Games is a cooperative memory game where players work together to fulfill customer orders.

In this SPIEL 2025 promo version, the game features multilingual cards in German, English, and Japanese, and replaces café items with titles from Oink Games' portfolio. Players must remember which items are in others' hands and call them out to complete orders. The game progresses through levels, each increasing in difficulty, and is lost if all players are eliminated before completing the final level.

•••

Order Overload: Oink Games ist ein kooperatives Gedächtnisspiel, bei dem die Spieler gemeinsam Kundenbestellungen erfüllen. In dieser SPIEL 2025 Promo-Version enthält das Spiel mehrsprachige Karten in Deutsch, Englisch und Japanisch und ersetzt Café-Artikel durch Titel aus dem Portfolio von Oink Games. Die Spieler müssen sich merken, welche Artikel sich in den Händen der anderen befinden, und diese nennen, um Bestellungen abzuschließen. Das Spiel verläuft in Levels, die jeweils an Schwierigkeit zunehmen, und wird verloren, wenn alle Spieler eliminiert werden, bevor das letzte Level abgeschlossen ist.

Brickway is a connection game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the intersections (points) of an initially empty square grid (board). The top and bottom edges of the board are colored black; the left and right edges are colored white.

Black plays first, then turns alternate. On your turn, select a 2×2 area including at least one empty point and fill the area with four stones of your color, replacing all stones that were previously there.

At the end of your turn, for any two diagonally adjacent stones of your color there must be another stone of your color adjacent to both.

Passing is not allowed, but, if you have no legal moves available, your turn is skipped.

You win if there is a chain of orthogonally connected stones of your color touching the two opposite board edges of your color.

To make the game fair, White will have the option, on their first turn only, to swap sides with Black instead of making a regular move.

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – French Fleet Box
description:
Carrying the legacy of Charlemagne and Napoleon, France takes to the skies to secure its future in the age of the leviathan.  Embracing a jack-of-all-trades design mentality, the French fleet seeks to excel in all areas instead of limiting itself to only one.

This Fleet Box contains everything you need to start your own French fleet. It comes with 4 pre-painted, pre-assembled miniatures and the tokens needed to play.  The following ship classes are included: Bastille, Faucon, and '2 'Grenouilles.

CONTENTS:
• 4 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniatures
• 8 Ship Cards
  ‣ Bastille-class : Bellgrade & Calais
  ‣ Faucon-class : Chasseuse & Épervier
  ‣ Grenouille-class : Aquitaine, La Gloire, Montcalm & Pelletier
• 5 Admiral Cards
  ‣ Engineering Expert, Torpedo Crews, High Impact Torpedos, Makeshift Engine, Torpedo Sights
• 2 Token Sheets
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Vulcano Battleship Pack
description:
"A torpedo with a ship", the Vulcano-class is the largest torpedo boat in the sky.  With both high speed and a large quantity of torpedoes, the Vulcano often operates more as a cruiser than a proper battleship, and can outrun many smaller enemy ships.

This Battleship Pack provides everything you need to add the Vulcano to your Italian fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for the Vulcano class: the Etna and the Stromboli. See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Connaught Battleship Pack
description:
The three large-caliber guns at the front of the Connaught gives this battleship one of the most imposing barrages of any vessel traversing the skies.  While not quite as heavy as the 12-inch cannons of the Leviathan, the extra battery more than makes up for this.

This Battleship Pack provides everything you need to add the Connaught to your British fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for the Connaught class: the Cornwall and the Munster. See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Kaiser Battleship Pack
description:
Often called a "flying brick", the Kaiser class is a perfect example in German airship doctrine.  Each Kaiser is covered bow to stern in armor, making it one of the hardest ships to knock out of the sky.  The price for this armor is an incredibly weak engine, making the Kaiser a slow-moving tool of destruction.

This Battleship Pack provides everything you need to add the Kaiser to your German fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and 
the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for the Kaiser class: the Kaiser and the Kaiserin. See you in the skies, 
Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Aleksandr Battleship Pack
description:
Carrying some of the most experienced crew in the world, Aleksandr-class battleships use Russia's long history with leviathans to the fullest.  The accuracy of their gunners and the response time of their repair teams are unparalleled.

This Battleship Pack provides everything you need to add the Aleksandr to your Russian fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for the Aleksandr class: the Ivan and the Peter. See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Bastille Battleship Pack
description:
Nicknamed "Cloud Breaker", the Bastille class was designed as a sleek, general-purpose battleship, able to handle any situation.  Both the Dunkerque and Louvois are armed with high-explosive torpedoes designed to keep curious destroyers at bay.

This Battleship Pack provides everything you need to add the Bastille to your French fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for the Bastille class: the Dunkerque and the Louvois. See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:

• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet

boardgame: Project AUDE: The UNB History Card Game
description:
Disaster has struck the UNB Archives and Special Collections! A terrible fire has destroyed all of the incredible photographs, papers, and artifacts that document UNB’s history! But, all is not lost. One hope remains. A secret project (codenamed AUDE - short for Augmented Universal Dimension Engine) the work of a small group of eccentric professors, has unraveled the mystery of time travel! In this easy-to-learn but strategic card game, you and up to four friends compete against each other as the professors. Stepping forward with a plan to save the university’s history, you send your student research assistants leaping back in time to collect the artifacts and retake the photographs lost in the fire. Supervise the missions, guide your students through the past, help them choose the best artifacts, and get them home to the present safely. The player whose team collects the most points on these missions will be the winner of the game. Good luck! UNB is counting on you!

Project AUDE is a light strategy card game telling the history of the University of New Brunswick (Canada) using photos and objects found in UNB's Archives and Special Collections. There are 102 unique cards, each with a date, an archival photo, and some historical information. Each card also features one of six colours. Players draw cards and add them to one of two "missions" on the table in front of them. Each mission must be at least four cards long before it can be completed and score points. Five- or six-card missions gain bonus points. The trick is that cards must be placed in chronological order and no mission may have two cards of the same colour. Luckily, each mission has a Research Assistant who provides a one-time special ability that allows the player to bend or break these rules.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: Landmarks: Game Boy Geek 2025 Promo Map Cards
description:
This promo pack adds three new map cards for Landmarks.
Pack was available during the Game Boy Geek Season 13 Kickstarter campaign.

A reel and a rod, some friends, and a boat - is there anything more relaxing? Let's spend the day together, fishing this pond. And hey, I'll give you a hand and help you catch that big fish you've been after.

Friendly Fishing is a semi-cooperative set collection game in which players share a fishing boat. On your turn you simply move the boat and claim a fish token. You'll want to set the next player up for a good move, because there's a twist.  At the end of the game, everyone scores the tokens in front of them, but each player's final score is their own fish score, plus their left neighbor's fish score! Most points wins.

Friendly Fishing offers a casual day on the pond with a unique style of player iteration.

-description from publisher

This game is played on a Go-like board initially empty. Larger boards imply more strategic matches.

Setup. One player drops two black stones and one white stone. The second player decides to swap (and becomes Black) or continues as White.

At each turn, each player must do the following sequence:
  • First, drop a friendly stone on an empty cell adjacent to a friendly stone;
  • Second, move a friendly stone, like a Chess Queen, to an empty cell.

It is illegal to make any 2x2 deadlock patterns, like:

    x o    o x
    o x    x o

Black wins by connecting the north and south edges, White by connecting the east and west edges.
  • The connecting path may include orthogonal and diagonal connections

-description from designer

Dungeon Wilds is a cooperative card game that uses a standard 52 card deck of playing cards and six-sided dice loosly based on Rummy. Additionally, it features elements of classic rogue-lite games like dungeon exploration and story-telling through death and repeated play-throughs.

The core of the game is a simple trick-taking mechanic that has you scoring points in order to reveal cards hidden in the Dungeon. Once those cards are revealed you’ll be looking for opportunities to play those cards so that you may clear the floor and move onto more deadly parts of the dungeon.

Throughout all of this you’ll have opportunities to purchase items from a terrifying shop keeper in hopes that you may continue to survive the Dungeon Wilds.

—description from the designer

Where’s Furzipups? He’s hiding from his friends Hicksi and Rüdiger. As he runs away, a few farts slip out. Hicksi and Rüdiger just have to follow the smell! In this roaringly funny memory game, two to four children work together to catch Furzipups. Along the way, he leaves behind colorful fart clouds on small tokens. When players reveal a matching color card, Hicksi and Rüdiger move forward. If the color doesn’t match, the die decides whether the farty dragon gets a little farther ahead. Can they catch Furzipups in time?

—description from the publisher (translated)

This mini-expansion adds a free action option, as well as an additional effect to your Dice Draft actions.

Artifax Draft Free Action

At any time during your turn, take one Artifax token from the supply pool and immediately perform all of its abilities. Artifax token abilities that target a card will only activate one ability from an in-play card in your area. Then, place the chosen token face-down next to your Leader. Draw a new Artifax token from a facedown stack and place it face up into the supply pool so that there are 4 tokens available.

You cannot take more than one Artifax token during a turn, and you cannot take an Artifax token if you already have one in front of you.

Dice Draft Action Modification

Each time you draft the Void die, you may discard a previously taken Artifax token that is next to your Leader. This token is removed from the game

Blasters!

A SC-Fi Skirmish rule set each player has 6-12 figures normally played on a 4x4 foot table

Includes a points system, some sample forces and a few scenarios to get you started

Simple rules but lots of variables to make every game different

boardgame: A4WAR: Age of Oblivion – A Solitaire War game
description:
In the dying light of a decaying universe, on the forsaken planet Cronus orbiting the ancient red dwarf A4, three fractured races battle for survival against the encroaching void. The resilient Terrans, The savage Krinn swarm, and The noble Ascendancy, battle for survival as asteroids rain and the black hole LB-1 looms, wage war over the ashes of a fallen age. 
Will you defy the Quietus?

The gates of oblivion beckon, enter the fray on Cronus.

A4WAR:Age of Oblivion A Solitaire War Game is a self-contained solo tactical war game

Epic Solo Tactical Warfare

     Command Your Faction: Choose Terran survivors with punishing firepower, Krinn hives for relentless ambushes, or Ascendancy for adaptive dominance.
     AI-Driven Opponents: Face intelligent, automated enemies governed by a simple flowchart, no need for a second player!
     Hex-Based Battles: Deploy on 7x10 maps with terrain effects, line of sight, and support mechanics for deep strategy.
     Dice-Powered Action: Roll D6 for orders, movement, attacks, and abilities. Combat resolved with 2D6 against Target Numbers, simple yet tactical.
     Quick Sessions: 20-40 minutes per game, 12 missions maps with a total of 48 objective variations across factions, plus fragmented lore to immerse you in the apocalypse.
     Print-and-Play Ready: 68 Page Pdf. Maps fit on to one page of A4. Cut out tokens from the PDF book, grab 10 D6 dice, and dive in. Self-contained for endless re playability.


Sequence of play:
Each turn proceeds as follows:
1. Mark the current turn on the Turn Tracker
2. Roll one D6 per unit in play
3. Apply the Re-calibration Rule (optional re-roll of duplicates)
4. Assign each dice beside the unit it controls (this decision is final)
5. Activate your units in any order
6. Activate all AI units by type
7. Check for victory conditions; otherwise, proceed to the next turn
