description:
What's the size of a mermaid's fin?
What's the size of a bowling pin?
Let's find out!

In Size Wise you estimate the sizes of objects, but there’s no right answer. You simply need to think like the other players to prove that you’re size wise.

Each round a card is revealed that asks "what is the size of a ___?" You give your answer by sliding the bead on a string to indicate the object's size. Hold everyone's strings side by side to compare. The players with the longest and shortest answer lose one of their wisdom tokens. When someone loses their last bit of wisdom, the wisest player wins!

Are you size wise?

-description from publisher

You’re a music fan and a true lover of pets. Lucky for you, these animals don’t just sit and stay – they play music! Today is your day to adopt the finest pet musicians around and form exceptional musical ensembles. And certainly you will agree, there is nothing more exquisite than a perfect pet quartet!

Pet Quartet is a set-collection game in which players adopt musical pets, represented by cards. Your goal is to collect cards of the same number, however, you only score points for soloists and quartets! That is, for each type of animal, you only want to have exactly one card from that set, or exactly four cards from that set.

Pet Quartet uses a "triangular deck" in which the value of each card is also the number of that card in the deck.  For example there are 3 snakes (marked 3), 4 turtles (marked 4), five 5s, six 6s, and so on, all the way up to 13 latin music ferrets. This means it will be much easier to collect a quartet of 13's, but also easier to go over 4 cards for that set. The game asks players to push their luck and consider the right time to take a soloist or build towards a quartet without going over.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Miraculous: A Race To The Top of the Tower
description:
A fast-paced racing game for 3 to 6 players set in the heroic world of Miraculous! Players compete to be the first to get their Hero to the top of the iconic Eiffel Tower.

Each turn, players draw an Action Card, then play one of the four cards in their hand. Action Cards allow players to to perform a variety of daring deeds: move their Hero Meeple up the Tower, send Hawk Moth's villainous minions to delay their opponents (by making them move down the Tower or skip a turn), and team up with other Heroes and their faithful Kwami companions to make use of their fantastical abilities.

Do anything it takes to be the first to the top if you want to WIN!

Featuring streamlined hand management with a healthy dose of take that, Miraculous: A Race To The Top of the Tower game is fast, frantic fun that's easy to learn. Simple enough for fans aged 6 and up to play, but offering enough strategic choice and interesting decisions that it's fun for everyone - parents and seasoned gamers alike.

—description from the publisher

Footsteps echo through the alleys of New York—who ate our pizza?
As players lay down their cards, suspicion and strategy deepen with each trick.

The Third Man is a trick-taking game for 3–4 players, where the goal is to avoid collecting suspicion—represented by penalty points. The rules are simple: play the highest-numbered card of each suit. But as the game progresses, players must carefully choose their moves to shift suspicion onto others. When the pressure rises, escaping the scene might be your only option.

Stand your ground to prove your innocence. In the end, the player with the most penalty points is condemned as The Third Man—the pizza thief.

Quick to play but rich in mind games, this suspenseful and darkly humorous game is all about dodging blame and turning the tables.

This is a first Era project tile. It grants 2 GP for each house located in halls with gold veins, and 1 VP for each workshop located in black halls.

The tile is shown in the rulebook as one of the six first era project tiles, but it's not actually in the core game. Instead, the core game has a tile providing 2 GP for each house located in halls with mythril veins, and 1 VP for each workshop located in black halls.

Gemüsegarten is a humorous, tactical strategy game where players take on the roles of quirky vegetable heroes competing for glory, honor – and the most valuable seeds in the garden!

Each turn, players collect seeds, plant setlings, grow mighty vegetables, and face unpredictable events or hilarious threats like “Hungry Vegans” or mischievous critters. Using clever item cards, dice rolls, and a mix of luck and planning, players must manage their limited action points to expand their garden, outsmart rivals, and complete missions or expeditions that earn them precious Fame Points.

No two games are ever the same: with over 120 unique events, random card draws, and dynamic interactions, the garden grows differently every time. It’s a blend of strategy and chaos – a lighthearted battle of wits, timing, and laughter.

The game ends when the required Fame total is reached or the final threat appears. The player with the most points – from seeds, plants, and completed tasks – becomes the true Champion of the Garden.

—description from the publisher

The Edification faction is known for its brutalist metropolises, which allow Humanity to survive. 
This expansion replace the Urban Complex tokens with Buidling miniatures.

Punicus recreates one of the most dramatic and pivotal conflicts of the ancient world: the epic struggle between Carthage and Rome during the Second Punic War. From Hannibal’s legendary crossing of the Alps to Scipio’s decisive victory at Zama, this strategic block wargame immerses you in the fierce battles and diplomatic intrigue that shaped the destiny of the Mediterranean. Players take on the roles of Carthage and Rome, commanding armies, managing resources, and competing for control of key cities and territories. Victory hinges on accumulating Prestige through tactical brilliance, diplomacy, piracy, siege-craft, and military conquest.

Punicus is a two-player historical strategy game based on the mechanics and concepts of Craig Besinque's Hellenes, adapted specifically to the theater of the Second Punic War. Cards can be played for their Events or to generate Action Points used to recruit new unit blocks and maneuver armies across the map to engage in battle and besiege enemy cities. In addition to these core systems, players will engage in new actions such as piracy and diplomacy, and manage special projects on their player boards that shape the strategic development of their side.

Each game year in Punicus is divided into five seasonal turns—Spring, Summer, High Summer, Fall, and Winter. At the start of the year, players draw a new hand of cards and resolve the New Year segment, each secretly playing a card as an Event, a Sacrifice, or for Research. During each seasonal turn, players alternate taking actions after determining initiative. Cards can be played either for their Event effect—triggering historical or tactical shifts—or for their Action Points, which are spent on Movement, Building, Diplomacy, or Production. As the year progresses, players must carefully manage limited resources across a wide range of operations, culminating in Winter, when all units must return to safe locations or be disbanded if not maintained.

Components:
    One 22” x 34” Mounted Game Board
    One year board
    Two player boards
    One deck of 55 cards
    167 wooden pieces
    Two player aid foldouts
    One sticker sheet
    One counter sheet
    Two draw bags
    Seven regular six-sided dice
    Three customer six-sided dice
    One rulebook
    One playbook
    A 3" box

-description from publisher's website

description:
The tokens are distributed evenly in exchange for a stake. Whoever rolls the highest number goes first and places a token in the space corresponding to the number they rolled. Whoever rolls a 6 rolls twice.

The passenger compartment in the gondola has the number 6 and has free passage, meaning no token may be removed from this space. Any number of tokens may be placed in space 6.

If a player rolls the numbers 1-5, they also place a token in the space they rolled, but only as long as the space is still free. If there is already a token on the space, they may not place one. Instead, they must first remove this token from the space and take it.

Whoever has all the tokens in play first has completed the journey and receives half the stake. The second- and third-best travelers each receive a quarter of the stake. All other players lose and receive nothing. If a player argues or tries to cheat their fellow players, they must, if caught, take a token from space 6 as a penalty.

A little expansion for Incubo that contains 7 monster cards to add variety to the game.

Cards were given to followers of the Gamefound campaign. Also available at Spiel 2025.

In this fast game of bluff and strategy, players take turns bidding for mysterious Mecha parts — some incredible, others not so much. Use limited information and read your opponents to outbid them. But beware: not every reward is positive! Do not let the fear of missing out take control of your impulses!

boardgame: 23 Knives: Spurrina and Cleopatra Promo Cards
description:
Spurrina and Cleopatra promo cards originally offered to Kickstarter backers, and also offered to buyers at Essen 2025.

The two cards constitute extra Citizens that may be added to the game.

Spurrina 
May reveal on your turn.
When at the forum, you may discard a fate card to view the cards in the Curia. Also, once per turn, you may discard a fate card to view a colocated player's tableau (sway cards and citizen).

Cleopatra
May reveal at any time.
When at the baths, you may draw 5 sway cards, choose 3, and place the others on the bottom of the deck. During the sway step of your turn, instead of swaying a colocated player, you may burn a sway card from your hand to swap any 1 sway card from your tableau with 1 from theirs.

Roma XLI is the 6th volume in the Dark Cities Series by Facade Games. It plays 1-9 players in 20-40 minutes and includes elements of social deduction and light strategy.

Caesar is dead, stabbed on the senate floor. In the years following this power-shifting event, the world wonders, who will lead the Roman Republic? You are one of the nine contenders poised to take control, and perhaps even transform the Republic into an Empire. Vote your opponents into exile, build your power by gathering endorsements, and form (and break) alliances at the opportune moment. The last player remaining on the map wins the game and takes all of Rome.

On your turn play one card face down as a vote or as a protective legion, or face up as an action or a permanent character boost. Roll the die to end your turn and potentially advance the exile track. When the exile token reaches a red square, votes are revealed and the character(s) with the most votes are exiled (unless protected by a legion). Spend gold to play an extra card per turn. Earn gold by working with your allies and backstabbing them at the opportune moments. Exiled players have a chance to earn their way back into the game or convince a powerful player to take them on as a devoted follower. The final un-exiled player wins!

Standard Edition includes 1 book box, 1 board, 1 plastic temple token, 1 custom die, 1 wood exile token, 9 wood pawns, 30 wood coins, 90 cards (in standard tuckbox). Deluxe Edition adds 1 metal temple token (replaces standard), 9 custom meepawns (replaces standard pawns), 30 metal coins (replaces wood coins), 1 mini-expansion (27 wood pawns and 1 bag), 1 plastic tuck box (replaces standard). Kickstrter-exclusive Spoils of War box adds 12 Fortuna event cards, 9 medal character medallions, 7 victory wreath tokens, 1 wood coaster.

Launching on Kickstarter on February 24th, 2026. Retail release expected fall 2026.

It's time to mix your paints! Collect groups of Primary color cards from the central Palette, then use them to score the Secondary hues. Score multiple cards at once to make each worth more when the deck runs out!

Palette features competitive gameplay for 2-4 players, and a solo score challenge mode for solitaire play!

—description from the designer

This game is a combination of Sevens and Daifugo.

The player who gets rid of all their cards wins, but to do so they must meet two conditions.

① Their cards must touch an existing ladder card on the table above, below, left, or right.
② Their set of cards must be stronger than the set played by the previous player.

The goal is to play all of your cards as quickly as possible while fulfilling these conditions.

Demonstrate that you are the best at climbing up and down the ladder.

-description from designer

In Fiteens, players take on the role of young entrepreneurs learning how to manage their time, money, and opportunities to achieve financial freedom.

Throughout the game, you’ll spend and invest wisely across six areas — work, education, business, savings, investments, and banking — balancing short-term needs with long-term goals.
Acquire Investment, Education, and Expense cards to gain useful resources such as Time, Knowledge, and Digital Money. Use them to develop your businesses, grow your savings, and make smart financial moves.

Each round brings new events, changing the market and rewarding players who plan ahead.
But beware of overspending! Managing loans, credit cards, and investments responsibly can make the difference between debt and prosperity.

The player who builds the best financial strategy and earns the most Financial Freedom Points wins the game!

-description from designer

Step into a neon playground of VR arenas, racing simulators, pinball machines, and arcade cabinets that bring endless fun for players of all ages!

Barcade is a fast, tactical, tableau-building card game. In 8 rounds, build your entertainment center: buy Stations, time one-shot Event Boosts, attract Guests, and match them to your Station icons to score Victory Points.

It’s quick to teach, plays in about 15 minutes/player, and every round presents a fresh, clean puzzle with its own trade-offs.

boardgame: Dominion of the Trenches: Fight Battles of World War One – Solo Game
description:
This is the Dominion of the Trenches Fight Battles of World War One Solo Game. 
This standalone set of rules is built on the Dominion of the Spear game system but is a brand new set of rules with appropriate rule changes for World War One. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-7 units and a solo battle can be fought in 10-15 minutes. 
An example play through is included. 
There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover.
 Use paper and pen (or model figures of any scale) as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

24 historical battles and campaigns are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically. Together they provide a simple overview of World War One covering the Western Front, the Eastern Front, Gallipoli, East Africa, the Middle East, and Italy:
1. Battle of Mons 23rd August 1914
2. Battle of Tannenberg 22–30th August 1914
3. First Battle of the Marne 6–12th September 1914
4. First Battle of Ypres 19th October–22nd November 1914
5. Battle of Tanga 3-5th November 1914
6. Second Battle of Ypres 22nd April–25th May 1915
7. Battle of Gallipoli (Landing at Cape Helles) 25th April 1915
8. Battle of Gallipoli (Lone Pine) 6th-10th August 1915
9. Battle of Salaita Hill 12th February 1916
10. Battle of Verdun 21st February–18th December 1916
11. Battle of Lake Naroch 18th–30th March 1916
12. Battle of Lutsk (start of the Brusilov Offensive) 4-6th June 1916
13. First Battle of the Somme 1st July–18th November 1916
14. Battle of Romani 3–5th August 1916
15. Second Battle of Arras 9th April–16th May 1917
16. Third Battle of Ypres (Passchendaele) 31st July–10th November 1917
17. Battle of Mahiwa 15-19th October 1917
18. Battle of Caporetto 24th October–19th November 1917
19. Battle of Beersheba 31st October 1917
20. Battle of Cambrai 20th November–7th December 1917
21. Second Battle of the Somme (German Spring Offensive - Operation Michael) 21st March–5th April 1918
22. Battle of Amiens 8–12th August 1918
23. Battle of Megiddo 19–25th September 1918
24. Battle of Vittorio Veneto 24th October–4th November 1918

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet - click booklet (and fit to page in printing options depending the program used). 44 A5 pages.

description:
Shines on the old mansion the pale light of the full moon. Two figures hide in the shadows, armed with tricks and ready for ambush. Sworn friendship has shattered into bitter enmity, fueled by the grievances of Jianghu, the ancient Chinese realm of wuxia and swords. One must fall here tonight—whose sword will claim the treacherous victory?

In Showdown: Unseen Blades, you must hunt your opponent before they hunt you in a swift yet breathtaking clash. Dive into this wuxia thriller of stealth and strategy, conceal your actions, anticipate your foe's moves, and unleash deadly gadgets—from traps and poisons to thunderballs—with perfect timing. Only the slyest survivor can continue their legend!

—description from the designer

Unleash your inner artist with Portmanteau: Doodles!, an expansion for the game Portmanteau.

While the Speaker prepares the Portmanteau for the round, every other player doodles an image based on their secret item card following a specific condition. Can you draw a piano using only triangles? Can you draw a horse with your eyes closed?

These doodles become the inspiration for future rounds — turning your sketches into clues, chaos, and laughter.

These 5 cards were a gift during Essen Spiel 2025 when you purchased the game. 
The pack contains 1 card which has a QR code and redirect to the Wyrmgold shop page. The 4 other cards are Hydra Cards

boardgame: Пенумбра: Свет и мрак (Penumbra: Light and Darkness)
description:
Пенумбра: Свет и мрак ("Penumbra: Light and Darkness") expansion introduces the mechanics of Fate — an aspiration towards either Light or Darkness.

Your rewards will include new Spark Marks, as well as powerful cards for the advanced Diamond and Onyx Disciplines. These contain potent new spells, potions, familiars, and even artifacts. Many of these cards can single-handedly shift your game strategy and provide a significant advantage.

Furthermore, the expansion allows you to study and perform ancient rituals, take school exams, and delve into the dungeon within the ruins.

The expansion also includes two new player boards (in two new colors), which feature two new solo automas on their reverse side.

Discover Nova Scotia's best kept secrets with Nova Scotia-Opoly! Explore family attractions, uncover its rich history, and enjoy Canada's ocean playground with this exclusive board game. Capture the beauty of Nova Scotia's landscapes and uncover its hidden gems all from the comfort of your own home!
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

boardgame: Параллельные истории: Бездна (Parallel Stories: The Abyss)
description:
In the endless ocean drifts a liner, its deck groaning under the weight of unspoken secrets, its empty cabins frozen with echoes of someone's last words. The ship's captain, once resolute and authoritative, now gives increasingly inexplicable, irrational orders, following the directives of the one and only, maddeningly mysterious passenger. This is a clear signal that something is terribly wrong here.

What force compels an experienced sailor to obey? Where have those declared as madmen disappeared to? And what, ultimately, hides in the dark waters cut by the keel? Every step you take on this ghost ship brings you closer to an answer – or leads to a new mystery.

Параллельные истории: Бездна ("Parallel Stories: The Abyss") is a cooperative card game in the genre of a narrative investigation adventure, where your team becomes the witness and the architect of two intertwining destinies.

Choose whose story to investigate: that of the First Mate David or the Chief Engineer Lance. You will collectively make decisions at story crossroads, search for key items, and manage limited resources – air and patience.

Without looking into the deck, you set up the starting scene on the deck, determine the initial supply of resources, and lay out the grid of clue cards. One player becomes the reader, but all of you discuss every step together. The numbered cards contain your main story.

Read the text on the main and auxiliary cards carefully, follow the instructions at the bottom, and make choices at the crossroads. If there is no direct instruction – be attentive: the text might hide a keyword pointing to an item among the picture cards. If you decide to check a hypothesis – take a card and check the instruction at the top. If you guessed right – learn a new plot detail! If you were wrong – return the card face down and lose 1 point of air or patience.

Victory in the game is shared by all participants if, upon completion of the story, the main characters remain alive and reach one of the eight possible endings. The game can end prematurely if the supply of air or patience is completely depleted – in this case, the players lose and read the corresponding final story.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Destrier Cruiser Pack
description:
In contrast to a leviathan's normal goal of closing range with its target, the Destrier class boasts the equipment and crew necessary to attack from a longer distance.  Torpedoes often supplement the range and power of these ships.

This Cruiser Pack provides everything you need to add the Destrier to your German Fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay.  It also comes with two ship cards for the Destrier class: the Malay and the Prometheus.  See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Kiev Cruiser Pack
description:
The first ship class crewed mostly by Cossacks, the Kiev showed the world how effective these crews could be.  Many of the best crews in the Russian fleet earned their skill on Kiev-class cruisers.  Even now, the Kiev outperforms ships with larger engines and more guns, thanks to the Cossacks of the Sky.

This Cruiser Pack provides everything you need to add the Kiev to your Russian Fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay.  It also comes with two ship cards for the Kiev class: the Bayan and the Kirov.  See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Faucon Cruiser Pack
description:
The backbone of the French Sky Fleet, the Faucon can be seen in every French theater of operations.  In a battle of priority between speed, armor & firepower, the Faucon class answers yes to all.

This Cruiser Pack provides everything you need to add the Faucon to your French Fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay.  It also comes with two ship cards for the Faucon class: the Corse and the Geneve.  See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Greif Cruiser Pack
description:
The standard cruiser design trades a battleship's weapons and armor for increased speed and maneuverability.  The Greif chose to ignore this principle, instead operating for a faster version of German "floating bricks'.  Slow to get into action, but hard to kill, the Greif excels at holding the line and slowly pushing it forward.

This Cruiser Pack provides everything you need to add the Greif to your German Fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay.  It also comes with two ship cards for the Greif class: the Danzig and the Hamburg.  See you in the skies, Captain!

CONTENTS:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Grenouille Destroyer Pack
description:
For a new design philosophy to take root in the French fleet, it must first be tested in the Grenouille class leviathans.  From gunboats to torpedo carriers, heavy armor to high-speed engines, Grenouilles cover every base possible.

This Destroyer Pack provides everything you need to add the Grenouille to your French fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for Grenouille leviathans: the L'Hoonneur and the Normandie. See you in the skies, Captain!

Contents:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Cards
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Falke Destroyer Pack
description:
The bread and butter of the German Sky Fleet, the Falke-class destroyer is not only the most common ship in the German fleet, but also the most widespread leviathan in the world.  The Falke's versatility has ensured this ship can be seen everywhere German interests are found.

This Destroyer Pack provides everything you need to add the Falke to your German fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for Falke leviathans: the Dachs and the Vielfraß. See you in the skies, Captain!

Contents:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Cards
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Constellazione Destroyer Pack
description:
Speed demons of the sky, the Constellazione has large engines and a small frame, making it very hard to pin down or hit.  Only its light armor keeps this fast ship in check.

This Destroyer Pack provides everything you need to add the Constellazione to your Italian fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for Constellazione leviathans: the Aries and the Scorpio. See you in the skies, Captain!

Contents:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Cards
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Berkut Destroyer Pack
description:
Built en masse to cover the vast territory of the Russian Empire, Berkut-class destroyers are always deployed in large groups.  Precision gunners make the best use of the Berkut's weak firepower, and skilled repair cews can fix any damage before it gets out of hand.

This Destroyer Pack provides everything you need to add the Berkut to your Russian fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for Berkut leviathans: the Nadeyat'sya and the Sud'ba. See you in the skies, Captain!

Contents:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Cards
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – D-Class Destroyer Pack
description:
The D-Class destroyer stands as the oldest ship class still in regular use.  This had led to a huge array of different designs all built on the same framework.  The D-Class is the most versatile leviathan class, encompassing gunboats, torpedo boats, and various crew-centric models.

This Destroyer Pack provides everything you need to add the D-Class to your British fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay. It also comes with two ship cards for D-Class leviathans: the Crow and the Raven. See you in the skies, Captain!

Contents:
• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Cards
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Avventuriero Cruiser Pack
description:
The Avventuriero is the original main-line leviathan for the Italian Sky Fleet.  Deployed around Italy's long coast, this class codified Italian hit-an-run tactics.  Avventuriero keep their opponents distracted until reinforcements can arrive, and they remain the most common airships in Italy.

This Cruiser Pack provides everything you need to add the Avventuriero to your Italian Fleet, including a fully assembled, pre-painted miniature and the tokens needed for gameplay.  It also comes with two ship cards for the Avventuriero class: the Amerigo Vespucci and the Cristoforo Colombo.  See you in the skies, Captain!
