• 1 high-quality, fully assembled, pre-painted plastic miniature
• 2 Ship Card
• Token Sheet
• Art Card

boardgame: Sagrada: Promo 27 – Game Boy Geek 2025 Window Pattern Card
description: A promo window pattern card for Sagrada included in the 2025 Game Boy Geek - Season 13 Kickstarter that features two level 4 patterns on both sides.

In the fading light of an ageing cosmos, where the universe itself is crumbling into quiet decay, stands Astrum Quartus—A4. Named by ancient star-mappers as the fourth sentinel within its celestial domain, it is the final stronghold against the encroaching void. Once the last safe refuge, A4 now writhes in the throes of solar upheaval. Here, amidst the dance of survival, the races of the universe vie for the remnants of existence. Their war for survival is as relentless as the sands of time. In the shadow of A4’s glow, they defy the quietus that beckons from the dark. Their struggle is a fierce cry against the dying of the light.

A4WAR: A Rule Set for a Sci-fi Hex Grid War Game
This rule set aims to generate intricacy from basic elements . It's easy to pick up and play using card war tokens and 9X13 hex grid boards and terrain. You can also use any hex grid and tokens you have.

A4WAR offers tactical warfare, specialized attacks, unit orders, and dice activation. With intuitive combat mechanics, customizable unit creation, and modular terrain placement, you're equipped to secure victory. 
Are you ready to embark on a journey where every move could tip the balance between salvation and oblivion?

In the twilight of a dying cosmos, A4WAR Beckons players into a realm where celestial decay sets the stage for an epic struggle of cosmic proportions. Commanding armies across a hex-grid battlefield, players vie for supremacy amidst the crumbling remnants of the universe.
Counters or minis (6mm) can be used.

Early Monopoly competitor, quickly taken off the market. Created by a new company, which created no other known games.

—user summary

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Game Boy Geek 2025 Royal Decree Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of Game Boy Geek 2025 (Season 13) Kickstarter campaign.

This is a royal decree card. The Royal decree expansion is included in Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Collector's Edition or in Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Expansions.

Ability:
Gain 15 points if you give another player 15,000 instead of taking your regular turn (may only do this once per game)
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
Descend into Claustrum, an abandoned facility that once experimented on its contained horrors. In this cooperative survival horror game, players will search for resources on each floor, face off against unique creatures, and use their various items to survive. Players must shut down the facility's core before it's too late or escape it all with your life intact.

Key Aspects of the Game:

Survival Horror Experience - Navigate an abandoned facility where tension builds floor by floor, and finding the right items are critical.

Asymmetric Co-op - Each class has a unique special action and passive ability that reward teamwork, timing, and communication.

Tactical Resource Management - Coordinate with your team and decide when to use specific resources like keycards and weapon devices throughout the facility.

Terminal Interactions - Utilize terminals to trigger defense systems, override environmental hazards, and ultimately descend to the next floor.

Containment Breaches - Every floor will trigger a breach. The creatures that become active range from low, medium, and high threat levels and are equipped with their own unique abilities.

Condition Recipes - Manage your injuries to stay alive, and strategically use devices that inflict conditions on the creatures. You’ll need the right combination of conditions to damage each creature’s unique recipe.

—description from the publisher

A board game released in Greece in the 70s from hobby game company. The main idea of the game is to obtain as many cars as possible using the roulette. Each car consists of six parts and each part corresponds to a number on the roulette. The 72 parts are separated and spread out in the centre of the table, picture side up. Each player turns the roulette twice at his turn, but if he turns twice the same number, he has an extra turn.

Car parts must be acquired in the right order (first number 1, then 2 etc.). Other cars may be started before completing the first one. Each car must consist of the correct matching parts.

The winner is the player who owns most cars in the end of the game.

SubStance is a war themed, team-based drinking card game set in villapupu's stream world the Fluffle. The game involves a tactical aspect where squads attack each other with task cards to try and make the opponents lose all their lives.

All players have their own Soldier ability card that can be used once during the game, and the cards in play have both social and drinking tasks.

Once a squad is eliminated or the F.U.B.A.R. card is played, we'll know how the battle ends!
New Place of Power which provides a resource when harvesting and requires two witches to discover. The binding bonus allows drawing two cards from the deck.

Fighters of Europe is a fast-paced, simple tactical war game about World War II air battles in Europe.

In this expansion, you’ll find 6 outstanding pilots and their aircrafts in 3 series.

For the first set, we have on one side Robert Standford Tuck (29 victories), who actively participates in the Battle of France and the Battle of Britain. Opposite him we have the legendary Adolf Galland (104 victories) who goes through the whole war from Spain to the defense of the Reich. Both survived the war. Will it be the same under your command?

For the second set we focus on the Battle of France.
Pierre Le Gloan, with 18 victories during the battle of France, was known for his aggressiveness and his quick reaction time.
With 115 victories to his name, Werner Mölders took part in the Spanish Civil War, the Battle of France (where he was shot down by a D.520) and finally the front line of the Battle of Britain once he had been liberated and promoted.

The third set take place over Germany. Francis Stanley “Gabby” Gabreski (34 + victories) was the commander of the 61st Squadron flying on P-47 Thunderbold. One says that he was “piloting this beast like a bicycle”. He flew mainly to cover the bombers over Germany and scored his victories only against fighters. Want to ride a P-47 like a bicycle? It is yours!

Egon Mayer shot down 102 enemy aircraft, including 26 bombers, 51 Supermarine Spitfires and 12 Republic P-47 Thunderbolts. He actively participated in the Battle of France and the Battle of Britain where he was shot down 4 times. He ended up as commander of the Jagdgeschwader before being killed in action in November 1944 over France. He was known as an excellent marksman but above all as an excellent tactician.

All these pilots come with their customized aircraft and dedicated special rules .

These pilots can be added to any scenario, including of course those in which they were directly involved. A reference card for each series explains where and when to play them.

CONTENT

     6 Aces & Leaders cards (Robert Standford, Adolf Galland, Pierre Le Gloan, Werner Mölders, Francis Gabreski, Egon Mayer)
     3 rule cards
     1 product card
     6 airplane tokens (Hurricane Tuck, Bf 109 Galland, D.520 Le Gloan, Bf 109 Mölders, P47 Gabreski, FW 190A Mayer)


-description from the publisher's website

The Qing Dynasty is at the peak of its power, but inside the Forbidden City a silent battle is raging on. A battle for the heart of the Emperor.

One year has passed since the tragic death of the Empress, and it is time to fill the position again. The five Consorts of the Emperor are seeing their chance; only one thing is needed: The favor of the Emperor…

In the beginning of the game every player gets a secret identity, one of the five Consorts, draws 9 Gift Cards and drafts 5 Action Cards.

To win the favor of the Emperor, you need to make sure that your Consort is giving the best gift to the Emperor. The 5 Consort cards get shuffled and placed on the table. After that, every player can, in order, prepare an Action Card by playing it openly on the playing field. Once every player, who wanted, played an Action Card, the players take turns and place one of their Gift Cards secretly into the drawing stack in a position of their choice.

After the last player of the turn has put in their Gift Card, the Gift Cards are revealed by drawing one card after the other from the drawing stack, placing each under one Consort. Now is the time to use the effects of the prepared Action Cards, and after that the Consort(s) with the highest-valued Gift win the favor of the Emperor, while the one(s) with the lowest lose it.

The game is over if one Consort reaches 9 Favor, after 9 rounds, or if there is only one player surviving. The favor of the Emperor is not only the way to power and influence; it is the only thing keeping a Consort alive. Without favor, the Consort—and the controlling player—are out.

Stonesaga: Recharge Pack will return the base game to its original state with unchanged cards.

Contains:

     60 character cards
     20 outpost cards

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Песец / Соболь Promo Card
description:
P016

Песец (Arctic fox) acts like a normal fox

Соболь (Sable) gives 3 points per paw animal

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Cigüeña negra / Lince ibérico Promo card
description:
P021

Cigüeña negra (black stork) lets you draw a card and gives 7 points, when its tree does not have any paw or hoofed animal

Lince ibérico (iberian lynx) gives points for different amount of hares.

The gold rush has transformed the quiet frontier town of Leadville into a battleground for greed and justice. When a gang of bandits ambushes a gold carriage bound for the bank, a deadly showdown ensues. Will the sheriff’s posse manage to protect the valuable shipment, or will the outlaws make off with the loot?

Leadville is a grid-based hidden movement strategy game for 2–4 players. Each turn, players secretly plan the movement and shot direction of their crew, trying to outthink and outmaneuver their rivals. The map is filled with obstacles and boons: covers, aids, and traps, and the ever-moving gold wagon that may decide the game’s finale.

In the Wild West, there are no second chances: only those with quick thinking and a steady aim prevail. Choose which side of the law you're on.

Leadville is a multi-language game, with rules available in English, German, Spanish, Polish, French, and Estonian.

This mini expansion adds leaves, caterpillers and butterflies to  the game. You draw leaves on your branches, add caterpillers to the other players' trees, which eat their leaves. But if they can turn them into butterflies, then they will score points instead.

The Jester costume counts as a wild icon during final scoring.
Was given out at SPIEL 2025.

The Nightingale is added as a ninth bird. The promo consists of a player board and a sticker sheet. The stickers must be attached to the game board to add a start location as well as 4 destinations. Also a bird sticker that should be attached on one side of the Bee-eater bird pawn.

During play it is meant to use the material of the Bee-eater (blue).

Promo given out at SPIEL 2025.

Available as a promo card at Spiel Essen 2025.

A promo pack containing 4 new double sided Lake Cards with alternative layouts.

Gen Con 2024 Exclusive

It's here... the second volume in the series of EPIC storage boxes that look like real spellbooks! It includes 12 promo game cards, 6 Dead Wizard Tokens, a Tongue Token, and 3 Ability Tiles for the Epic Spell Wars: ANNIHILAGEDDON series of games. It provides a great home for tokens and tiles from the games in the series as well.

KEY FEATURES
Second spellbook-style storage box for Epic Spell Wars: ANNIHILAGEDDON series
Includes 12 Promo Cards, including Starter and Spellbook Cards!
Includes 12 tokens, including Magnetic Dead Wizard Tokens and Tongue Token!
Custom box that looks like a real spellbook!

CONTENTS
10 Promo Starter Cards (5 Hungry Glyph, 5 Switcheroo Gylph)
2 Promo Spellbook Cards (The Bone Tome, Mighty Mayhem Manual)
6 MAGNETIC Dead Wizard Tokens
3 Promo Ability Tiles
1 Tongue Token

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Epic Spell Wars of the Battle Wizards

description:
Not Your Grandma’s BINGO turns the beloved family classic completely on its head!

In NYG BINGO a large Arrow is placed on the table pointing at one of the players.
Each round, all players secretly play a number token onto the Arrow. Numbers are revealed and the Arrow rotated if any "spins" are played, but is never rotated when a "freeze" is played.

The player the Arrow is pointing at takes the token with the highest number and places it on their bingo board. This continues around the table until everyone has taken a token.

First to completely fill a row/column/diagonal on their bingo board wins.

-description from designer

A head to head card driven board game with interesting mechanics and thrilling gameplay.

Mind Extraction is an asymmetrical 2 player game with a multilayer modular game board and exciting card mechanics.

One player takes on the role of the “Mind” and has control over the game board (which represents a mind with different levels of consciousness), while the other player takes on the role of the "Extractor" and must try to find and extract secret information from the Mind players brain.

More information to come.

-description from designer

"My first Black Power" is a puzzle game from Brazil for ages 6 and up. Each player takes a challenge board which depicts an Afro hairstyle with a hex grid and tries to fill this with polyominoes as quickly as possible. A player who is done shouts "Black Power" and picks a reward card from a selection on the table. To score at the end of the game, you will collect symbols on the reward cards. Different symbol combinations yield different points, so the earlier you get to pick a cards, the more likely it is that you can get a card that increases your score.
When all cards have been played through, the game ends and the player with most points wins. Some variants are included, as is information on people and phenomena depicted on cards, all pertaining to Black history or culture.

Terra Mystica Special Edition breathes new life into the iconic Eurogame classic with brand new artwork and deluxe components. This special edition Expansion Vault brings all the expansions ever released for the game together, including the Fan Factions.

The Expansion Vault does not include the Automa expansion, as it is included in the core box of Terra Mystica: Special Edition.

Terra Mystica is a full information game, without any luck, that rewards strategic planning. Each player governs one of the 14 groups. With subtlety and craft, the player must attempt to rule as great an area as possible and to develop that group's skills. There are also four religious cults in which you can progress. To do all that, each group has special skills and abilities.

Taking turns, the players execute their actions on the resources they have at their disposal.

Different buildings allow players to develop different resources. Dwellings allow for more workers. Trading houses allow players to make money. Strongholds unlock a group's special ability, and temples allow you to develop religion and your terraforming and seafaring skills. Buildings can be upgraded: Dwellings can be developed into trading houses; trading houses can be developed into strongholds or temples; one temple can be upgraded to become a sanctuary. Each group must also develop its terraforming skill and its skill with boats to use the rivers.

Proximity to other groups is a double-edged sword in Terra Mystica. Being close to other groups gives you extra power, but it also means that expanding is more difficult...

boardgame: カミサマの云うとおり (Kamisama no iu toori)
description:
A tricktaking game. On the table, there is a layout of cards (two at the beginning), sorted by colors. The color with the most cards is the trump color (in case of a tie between two colors, there is no trump). Players play tricks, may-follow, highest trump wins, lowest non-trump loses. The winner of the trick distributes the trick cards between themselves, the loser of the trick, and the layout in the middle (manipulating trump).  All cards in the personal collection remain open for everyone to see, also sorted by color.

When the players run out of cards(or someone collects all five colors, whichever happens first), players will score. They multiply the number of cards they collected in a given color with the number of cards of that color in the central layout. If they collected an odd number of cards, these count positive points, an even number counts negative points. Highest number of points after three hands is the winner of the game.

PLAY:
ARRR… !! Ahoy Pirates! Set sail to build
your own pirate armada!

Cleverly navigate the everchanging seas, use your memory to open the right barrels and capture or steal the ships you need to build your armada of 3 similar ships.

To capture a ship, you must find the colour of its sails or its number of sails on the bottom of the barrels.

If you can, try to use the compass to move to any ship you want or the grappling hook to try to steal a ship from another pirate. But beware of the parrot and be careful with the rum!

Once you master the ‘12 Barrels’ Rules, feel free to add some of the 6 ‘Bonus Barrels’, which include a spyglass, 2 bombs, a whirlpool, a mermaid and even the Kraken! More barrels, more risks!

ADAPT:
If you flip Blackbeard, you can attack 1 specific ship of another player’s armada who must protect this ship by finding its correct colour or number of sails on a barrel.
And stay focused, as each time you win a ship or flip Blackbeard, you must swap the position of 2 barrels!

WIN:
The first pirate to build an armada of 3 ships with the same colour of sails or the same number of sails, wins the game. ARRR!

-description from publisher

Tribes battle for dominance in a dark, ruined world where survival means everything.

Players deploy their units on the board by drawing their marks. Their goal is to surround the opponent's units. The player who surrounds more of them at the end of the game wins.

They can also surround cities. This allows them to reach new movement levels, which enable the placement of more units on their turn.

They must also manage their food supply to maintain their army's morale. A low supply prevents the army from maintaining its movement capability.

The player who surrounds more opponent units wins.

Game published in 'Print&Play' format.

-description from publisher

boardgame: Discordant Stars (Fan expansion to Twilight Imperium: Fourth Edition)
description:
Discordant Stars is an unofficial community-made expansion for Twilight Imperium Fourth Edition, created to be compliant with the Asmodee guidelines on community usage of TI4 Intellectual property.

Discordant Stars initially contained 24 playable factions but now includes an additional 10 factions that were finalized in August of 2023. All 34 Discordant Stars factions have been extensively tested with each other and the 25 official factions. This expansion is designed to be seamlessly integrated into games of Twilight Imperium Fourth Edition with very little additional rules overhead, though the Prophecy of Kings expansion is required to play Discordant Stars.

boardgame: KALOREK: an educational and entertaining card game about a healthy lifestyle
description:
Kalorek is a thrilling card game that immerses players in a world of healthy choices and competitive play. Attack your opponents with calories, defend yourself with physical activity, answer quiz questions, and strive for victory by achieving your character's individual health goals.

Educational value:

The game teaches the basics of a balanced diet, the importance of nutrients, and the role of physical activity and hydration.

Through quiz questions, it reinforces knowledge and engages participants in active learning. Perfect for health education for children, families, and school groups.

Kalorek is not just a game – it's a way to spend time, learn healthy habits, and have fun for the whole family!

—description from the designer

Service Compris is a fun and chaotic card game where players control small fantasy creatures living near a hungry giant’s table. Each turn, players try to push their opponents’ creatures closer to the giant’s plate—because once the giant starts eating, those creatures are gone for good!

Players use action cards to move, protect, or sacrifice creatures, forming temporary alliances and betraying each other along the way. The game ends when the giant has eaten his fill, and the winner is the player with the most of their creatures still alive.

—user summary

Oscar a faim !

Bienvenue dans la forêt de la Dent Creuse, lieu de vie de moult créatures, où résonnent les bruits de digestion d'Oscar Hamel le géant. Ce grand benêt ne pense qu'à une chose : manger !!! Et lorsqu'Oscar a faim, c'est vers vous qu'il se tourne.

En tant que chef d'un des six peuples de cette forêt, votre rôle est de défendre vos gens et tout faire pour livrer, au plus vite, des proies savoureuses (vos adversaires) au géant gastronome. Le reste n'est qu'une suite de tours pendables, de pièges machiavéliques, de trahisons et de retournements de situation.

Chaque joueur dirige un peuple de créatures appétissantes. Le plateau de jeu représente la table à manger d’un géant affamé. Les joueurs vont essayer d’y propulser les créatures de leurs adversaires afin qu’elles soient mangées.

—description from the publisher

Enjoy the challenge of wrapping Christmas gifts in this polyomino packing roll & write for 1-6 players! There’s an assortment of items (marker/dice pairings) that you’ll take turns drafting and packing into your 3 gift boxes. Each item’s dice number sets its shape, and its marker color tells you what type of item it is. Put together well rounded gifts with a variety of items and score bonuses for delivering preferred types of presents to specific kids!

Packing Party: Santa's Helpers and Packing Party: Moving Day share a core ruleset, but are uniquely their own games. Outside of the theme/art, the main gameplay differences are:
- 6 distinct polyomino item shapes
- Uniquely shaped grids to pack
- Different scoring objectives and rewards
- Thematic special actions

Enjoy the challenge of filling moving boxes in this polyomino packing roll & write for 1-6 players! You’ll sort through a pile of household items (marker/die pairings) which you’ll be drafting and packing into your 3 cardboard boxes. Each item’s dice number sets its shape, and its marker color tells you what type of item it is. Organize your boxes with a variety of items and score bonuses for labeling them with the right rooms to make unpacking a breeze!

Packing Party: Santa's Helpers and Packing Party: Moving Day share a core ruleset, but are uniquely their own games. Outside of the theme/art, the main gameplay differences are:
- 6 distinct polyomino item shapes
- Uniquely shaped grids to pack
- Different scoring objectives and rewards
- Thematic special actions

A completely new type of mini-expansion for Carcassonne!

In this mini-expansion you embark on the beautiful but arduous journey to the "Cité", the castle of Carcassonne. But beware: The stony path along the scoreboard has become narrower!

The expansion comes as a sticker for the scoreboard.
You can stick this on the back of your existing scoreboard or order a stickered scoreboard from us.

-description from publisher's website

Use the "The Journey to Carcassonne" scoreboard instead of the regular one. Place 2 meeples from each player on space 0. Then draw 4 land tiles face down and place them face up above the scoreboard.

Scoring takes place according to the usual rules of the Carcassonne base game. If you trigger a scoring in which several players score, you decide which color is scored first. All points of that color from this scoring are added together. Then move one of your scoring meeple on the scoreboard (you choose which). If a scoring meeple lands on an occupied space, the two meeples swap places.
Important! This scoreboard has small and large spaces. Any number of meeples may be placed on large spaces (0 and 50). However, only 1 meeple may be placed on each small space.

On spaces 10, 20, 30, and 40 we rest to capture the beauty of the region. Whenever you gain enough points, you may turn off onto one of these spaces, provided it is unoccupied. Any excess points are lost. Each such space holds only 1 scoring meeple, which is protected there from other meeples. On a white-number space, your marker’s journey ends; it remains there until the end of the game, where it scores that number of points.

If you stop on a white 10, 20, 30 or 40, choose one of the tiles displayed above the scoring track. On your next turn, instead of drawing, place this chosen tile and take a turn. Then immediately take your normal turn. You thus receive a double turn.
Special case: If you can't draw any more tiles toward the end of the game, but you still have a double-move tile in your supply, you must still place it. The game only ends when no one can draw any more tiles, even if those players have to miss a turn.

The white 50+10 gate stands on the hill before the Cité de Carcassonne. Any number of meeple may stand there. If you reach space 50 during the game, place your marker on the gate; it will be worth 60 points at the end of the game.

If you have turned off onto a white-number space, then at your next scoring you must first place one of your figures involved in that scoring as a scoring meeple on space 0 of the scoreboard. Only then do you move one of your two scoring meeple. This way you always have two scoring meeple to move.
Any figure that scores points can become a scoring meeple - for example, your Abbot.

During final scoring you can no longer turn off onto white-number spaces (including the 50+10 gate), and you cannot swap positions. Perform final scoring as usual and move your scoring meeple accordingly. At the end, add the points of all your scoring meeple. If necessary, you may place additional scoring meeple.

---

Additional rule "the thief":

If the path is still not rocky enough, you can play with an additional thief. Take a meeple in a color not in play and place it on space 25. The Thief may be moved by any player. Instead of moving one of your own scoring meeple, you may move the thief the corresponding number of spaces. The player who triggered the scoring decides the order in which players move their scoring meeple. Each player decides for themselves whether to move the Thief or one of their own scoring meeple. If a scoring meeple lands on the space occupied by the thief, they swap places as usual. However, if the thief lands exactly on a scoring meeple, that scoring meeple returns to space 0. If the thief reaches space 50, it goes back to 25. If 25 is occupied, that scoring meeple is moved to space 0. The Thief may not be moved during final scoring.

boardgame: Chat GPT: Schlag die KI – Krass kombiniert
description:
New Party Game: Compete Against the Frighteningly Creative Artificial Intelligence ChatGPT
With four random words against ChatGPT – who can tell the better story?

The task is simple: whoever creates the cleverest sentence using the four random words earns a point. Can you come up with something funny for every new combination and link even the most absurd words together? Or will this creatively combining AI make you look old-fashioned?

What awaits you:

Almost 400 words for endless combinations

Super easy to play: one sentence is all it takes to “program” ChatGPT

Additional emotion cards add new challenges to your sentences

How to play:
The group draws four cards with different words. Everyone at the table comes up with a sentence that combines those words in a creative or funny way. ChatGPT is given the same task using the same four words. Then all the answers—both from the players and from ChatGPT—are read aloud. The group votes on who came up with the best answer: human or AI. Later in the game, the task can be changed by drawing random emotion cards for extra twists.

What is ChatGPT?
This artificial intelligence is revolutionizing the internet! Whatever the question, ChatGPT always has an answer—and it’s often surprisingly creative and human-like. That makes ChatGPT the perfect opponent for this fun and unpredictable creativity game. The use of ChatGPT is free!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: Champions of Meridell
description:
In the Champions of Meridell Set new Neopets, Legends, Equipment, 2 new Paint Brushes (Royal and Darigan), Food and more are added from the widely popular Champions of Meridell and Battle for Meridell plotlines from Neopets.com.
New Royal Rare variants of iconic Neopets and Legends add to your collection with stained glass foiled cards. New Petpets are featured allowing players use these in battle or face off in one round Petpet Battles! This set includes a new EXCLUSIVE Petpet being added to Neopia. Heroes and Villains are new card types allowing players to use them in their Faerie slot as constant powerful abilities each turn.

Find these cards and more in booster packs to add to your collection and enhance your deck with the magic and wonder of Neopets.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Neopets Battledome Trading Card Game: War for the Obelisk
description:
The War for the Obelisk Expansion Set brings new faction cards into the Battledome. Players can align their Neopet with 6 warring factions from the War for the Obelisk plot line; The Sway, The Brute Squad, The Thieves Guild, The Order of the Red Erisim, The Seekers and The Awakened – all vying for the power of the coveted and mysterious Obelisk!
Collect 200 new cards including; parody album cover art cards featuring these factions, TWO new Paint Brushes- Shadow and Relic, and new exclusive Petpets!

Find these cards and more in booster packs to add to the collection and enhance players decks with the magic and wonder from Neopets.

—description from the publisher

In this game, it’s “All aboard!” The goal is to place the color-matching animal passengers onto the right buses. Who will uncover all the correct animal cards for their bus first? And who has the best memory - because a special card swaps all the buses, and everyone continues with a new bus color!

boardgame: Earthborne Rangers: Spire in Bloom Valley Expansion
description:
Spire in Bloom kicks off a delightful series of products known as Valley Expansions, which expand the Valley by adding locations and paths to explore, people to meet, missions to accomplish, and rewards to unlock.

Each Valley Expansion comes with 60 new cards, including a new path set, new missions, new weather, new rewards, and more.

Valley Expansions can be incorporated into your Lure of the Valley campaign (the campaign that comes with the core game), or they can be played as their own mini-campaign. Each also comes with a 36-page campaign guide, card set dividers, and an expanded Valley Map.

Explore various locales around Spire with a new terrain type, Thoroughfare, which fills the table with unique challenges springing from the more urban environment—imagine the rustling of lutrinals and wolfhunds giving way to the hustle and bustle of villagers and peddlers! On top of that, you'll find new path cards for the Lone Tree Station, Valley, and General sets.

Within you'll also find new cards for the Lone Tree Set, the Valley Set, and the General Set (which includes eight baby animals, like the adorable bearsloth cub and the cuddly hydraworm spawn).

boardgame: Earthborne Rangers: Moments in the Valley
description:
This expansion enhances the new territory introduced by each Valley Expansion with hazardous events, opportunities, and narrative choices. From Spire to Plateau, the 33 new cards in this expansion add even greater texture to your ongoing campaign.

In the same way Moments on the Path enhances the path deck for the Core Set and Legacy of the Ancestors, Moments in the Valley enhances the path deck for each of the four Valley Expansions. It's not necessary to play those expansions but it gives more depth and replayability to the experience.

Includes:
8 Spire in Bloom Moments
8 Shadow of the Storm Moments
8 Into the Maw Moments
8 Sky and Smoke Moments
1 General Path Card
4 Moment Dividers

Dogfight! is a quick and easy to play aerial combat game with a focus on realism and simplicity. 
It includes rules for zeppelins, bombers, and reconnaissance, as well as all the fighter aircraft of the 2 main factions (the Allies and the Central Powers)

Fighter combat and dogfighting is the main focus of the gameplay, but bombing and recon photos give points towards victory at the end of the game, and so they also factor into gameplay, depending on the style of squadron roster the players decide to write up.

The war is divided into 3 periods, early, mid and late war, to represent the vast difference in the technology as the war progressed.

The rules cover the variety of fighter maneuvers like immelmans and high yo-yos, as well as basic maneuvers like turns and climbing/diving. Altitude and Airspeed are both tracked with the use of 2 dice on the base of each aircraft. This system of tracking the altitude and speed allows 3D battles to be fought on a flat tabletop, all while maintaining simplicity and without the need for fancy extending stands.

Pilot skill is also a large element of the game, with lower quality pilots being likely to stall and crash if they try to pull off advanced fighter maneuvers, and with ace pilots having unnervingly good skills and the ability to turn on the spot and return fire, provided they're in a capable enough aircraft (like Voss' final battle in his Fokker Dr.I). There are 5 different classes of pilot, and each one costs a different amount in points when building a Squadron Roster.
 Squadron Rosters are composed quite free-form, with the player selecting the pilots and aircraft he will use, and staying under the points limit. This means one could choose to go for a large amount of low quality pilots in cheap aircraft, or 1 or 2 good quality pilots, or a mixture of the two. 
Air defense from the ground and observation balloons are also represented, as well as a random event system which can be implemented, and involves infantry on the ground, distractions, and reinforcements arriving mid game.

The game is aimed at 1-10 aircraft per side, but there is no upper limit, and rules are provided for squadron-based play and larger games up to 50 or so aircraft per side.

1/600 miniatures are recommended, but 1/144 scale would work too.

boardgame: Acemhöyük: Trials of the Crescent Axe – Myth-Inspired Ancient Wargame
description:
Acemhöyük: A Myth-Historical Wargame

While reading about ancient civilizations and conflicts, one thing became clear to me – myths, gods, and horrific creatures were real to these people, and so what better way to portray ancient battles than to make the supernatural as real as they were believed to be?

Acemhöyük is a wargame written while living in Turkey and drawing from the ancient Anatolian civilizations that once lived there. In conjunction with Iliada Game Studio, we created a simple yet effective mass battle game with a unique command system that makes for interesting decisions.

This game is aimed at 6mm scale miniatures, and laser-cut MDF miniatures are available from Iliada Game Studio by request. This is a mass-battle game, somewhat in line with Warhammer Fantasy Battles, but much simpler.

The game mixes real ancient historical warfare and command limitations with the beliefs and rituals of the old civilizations - sacrifices, sorcery, protection granted by the storm gods, and the ability to turn the enemy into women!? (ancient Hittite beliefs were... interesting!).

Some factions lean more on traditional tactics and heroism, while others rely on foul sorcery, or rituals and sacrifice to please their gods.

The gameplay is quick and deadly, with games lasting less than 2 hours.

The factions in Acemhöyük have names drawn from ancient Anatolian history, and make use of units in use by armies of the near east (modern-day Turkey, Iran, Lebanon, Syria and Egypt), from roughly 2000BC – 330BC. We also make use of their myths, and sometimes invent things that are cool for the sake of fun. Feel free to invent your own factions and units as you see fit – a points system is provided at the end of these rules so you can work out the stats and points values for new units.
family: History: Wars in the Ancient Near East

boardgame: Gladus Hereticus: a roguelike tabletop game
description:
Gladus Hereticus is a lite solo dungeon crawler (city crawler really...), using a pack of regular playing cards to generate areas, encounters, loot and for resolving attacks and checks.

Work your way through a destroyed city and attempt to survive long enough to assemble the device that could reverse the fate of this place. 
Level up, gather equipment and fight off the horrific inhabitants of the Smoldering City. 
Once you feel you are strong enough, venture into the Necropolis beneath the city - who knows what foul and terrifying creatures will be down there...

Gladus Hereticus is a roguelike: highly lethal, randomized and with no roleplaying but plenty of combat. 
Be prepared to make tough decisions, and to meet death's sweet embrace more often than you would like.

Gameplay
Gameplay flows in this way:
1. Generate a New Area and check for Encounter/Loot
In Each Area:
2. You can make 1 Action.
3. All monsters will attack you
Repeat steps 2 & 3 until all monsters are dead or you have fled or left the area.
Once you have left an area, start again at step 1, generating a new area

In UFO Invaders, you command a fleet of spaceships fighting against an army of invading UFOs. This is a cooperative game featuring a dice-rolling combat system. Players must defeat waves of alien invaders by attacking with their dice. The Drone card grants special abilities to modify dice rolls and access a dice storage, where saved values can be added to future attacks.

Enemy UFO cards form grids of 2×3, 3×3, 4×3, or 5×3 depending on the number of players. Some cards are placed face-down, hiding their effects until attacked. Every enemy is vulnerable and invulnerable to specific dice colors, so players must carefully choose which dice to use and which targets to strike.

Each ship has a permanent die that is always recovered after combat. Ships can also be upgraded with improvement cards, allowing them to carry more dice and apply modifiers to their rolls. Victory is achieved if all enemies are defeated while the fleet retains at least one point of endurance.

Includes 3 game modes: Space Invaders, Battlefields (duel mode), and Rescue the Hostage (solo mode).

-description from publisher

Created by a 9-year-old boy called Sam, he imagined a world of mythical Dragons, daring Knights, fearless Queens, cunning Thieves, and mysterious Oracles. A world where strategy and luck cross paths, and where every card could change the game

Will you play a Knight and claim a Dragon from the Den? Or set off on a Quest to gather bonus points and secure your victory?

But beware! Opponents will scheme, steal and strike when you least expect it. A Treasure card might lure your prized Dragon away. A Thief may swipe a card from your grasp. And just when you think you’re ahead, someone blocks your path and turns the game on its head.

To win, you’ll need 5 Dragons or 100 points. To survive, you’ll need wit, nerve, and a little bit of luck. With its blend of strategy and surprise twists, Dragon Odyssey isn’t just a game - it’s an awesome adventure that promises loads of engaging gameplay and unpredictable fun for the whole family.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Leviathans: The Great War – Core Rulebook
description:
THE GREAT WAR HAS BEGUN!

The Great Powers of the world are marching to war, and leading each nation’s militaries into the conflagration are the greatest weapons ever devised.  Powered by the newly discovered electroid, the mighty leviathans dominate the skies and all below them.  These massive metal vessels drive every conflict between the major nations of the world: from the jungles of South America to the peaks of the Swiss Alps, from the waters of the pacific to the Siberian frontier, these skybound behemoths can be found around the world.

THIS IS LEVIATHANS: THE GREAT WAR.

Take command of a fleet of leviathans from one of the Great Powers.  Outmaneuver and outgun your opponents across the globe.  Conquer the skies, captains!

This rulebook will teach you how to play Leviathans: The Great War tabletop miniatures game, and it includes various ways the game can be played. This book also covers the alternate
history of the Leviathans world and introduces the main factions in the game.

To play this game, you will need the Leviathans Starter Box or any Leviathans Fleet Pack.  Welcome to the skies, Captains!

Guess the fake is a cool, interactive and family game (with Australian and other versions) for 2 - 5 players. There are 1,000 statements, electronically stored and asked by the electronic host. Can you identity which ones are fake? Listen to the Game-Master's statement and then guess if it's a fact or fake. Be the first to press the buzzer and give your answer! You'll win one point for each correct answer you make, but lose two for an incorrect answer! Plus, each round has its own rules announced by the electronic host.

Guess the Fake (the Australian edition) was the Australian Toy Association winner in the 2015 Games Toy of the Year category! A party game your whole family can enjoy.
Ages: 5+ years.
Players: 2 – 5.
1,000 crazy statements.

boardgame: Boss Fighters QR: Phoenix Essence Promo
description:
Phoenix Essence

Place this item with the other items. You may use it once per boss battle as an action. Each hero must shuffle all cards in their hand back into their draw pile and then draw as many cards
as they shuffled back in.

A card game centered around the number "3". The card set includes: 1) 81 regular object cards where each card has a quantity (1/2/3 of objects), size (S/M/L), shape (square, circle, triangle), color (one of 9), and category (one of 9); 2) 18 wildcard cards, which consist of 12 regular wildcard cards and 6 wildcard+cut cards.

The goal of the game is to be the first player to discard groups of 3 cards or more. Each player is dealt 9 cards at the beginning of the game and must dicard either a "match group" or a "series". A match group is 3 or more cards that have the same quantity/size/shape/category/color (wildcards can be used). A series is 6 or more cards that all have different colors or categories (wildcards can be used). After each turn, the player must draw another 3 cards from the draw pile.

To win the game, a player must quickly recognize possible groups of cards. They must also strategize on when to use wildcards.

Oferta is a competitive and fun card game set in a supermarket.
Players compete to grab the best deals by choosing and discarding products, while using special cards to steal or mess with their opponents.
The player who saves the most money wins!

boardgame: Escape the Dark Castle: Second Edition
description:
An improved and updated Second Edition, boasting greater variety, better balance, and new gameplay features, released to coincide with the 10th anniversary of initial release of Escape the Dark Castle.


    The base game now features mechanics formally restricted to expansions, plus more chapters, more items, and more bosses than 1st edition.
    Items of equipment and armor steadily degrade as you them. 
    Stamina is a new luck-mitigating resource each player must manage, spending it freely to re-roll or dodge damage, and restoring it by resting. 
    Each prisoner now has a simple, unique, once per game special ability.


The essence of the game remains the same: Escape the Dark Castle is a simple, cooperative game of retro-atmospheric adventure.

The goal of the game is for players to complete every chapter in the castle deck, then defeat the final boss. To win, they must keep every member of the team alive - if any player is killed, the game ends immediately!

The race is on! Each year the racers from both sides of the galaxy get together to set the record straight as to who is the best in the galaxy. Split into two teams of four, racers will go across the galaxy, orbiting planets to collect objectives and get to the finish line as fast as they can. This can be a little tricky because of the orbits can change. Whoever collects all eight objectives and moves all of their racers to the other side of the galaxy wins!

Orbits is a two player race game where players will move their four racers across a grid to other side while collecting eight objectives. Each game is different due to randomness of the planet tiles.  Players will move their spaceships across the galaxy using the orbits of each planet. Some planet tiles allow you to do special maneuvers to speed up your movement.

Whoever gets all of their objectives and moves all four of their spaceships to the other side of the grid, wins the game.

-description from designer

Running on the new 'Second Edition' game engine, this standalone core set takes you on a brand-new adventure deep into the twisted landscape beyond the Dark Castle - adding new 'Fear' and 'Darkness' mechanics to the classic gameplay of its predecessor.

The goal of the game remains the same as for the other games of the Escape the Dark series : the up to 4 player group has to complete every chapter in the castle deck, then defeat the final boss. To win, they must keep every member of the team alive - if any player is killed the game ends immediately.

boardgame: Crikey! Mowing Time in the Croc Pen
description:
The crocodile pen needs mowing again and you drew the short straw. This job is for the most intrepid person, but you'll have to do it anyway. Your job is to mow each section while avoiding the crocodiles before the next show. The pressure is on because the zoo guest are starting to gather around the crocodile pen.

Don't fret too much because the crocodiles are used to staff in the pen, but they will nip at your ankles. If you get bit three times, you will have to get your wounds attended to and your day is over. If you mow every section of the pen, you win the game.

Crikey! is a roll and write, dice drafting game where you are trying to mow every section of the crocodile pen before all the visitors show up. Each turn you will mark a visitor, roll and apply three dice and then move the crocodiles. Be careful, if a crocodile ends up on your space after moving, you get bit. If a crocodile ends up adjacent to you, you may still get bit.

If you mow every section of the game board before the visitors show up, you win the game. If you get bit three bites or all the visitors show up before you finish, you lose your job ... and the game.

—description from the designer

Challenge each other and get to know each other better by making a personal choice each time: Choose from annoying situations, embarrassing problems, and embarrassing inconveniences!

Play the game and try to assess each other correctly. Whoever is the best at this wins!

Each round, you'll be presented with two situations, some funny, some tricky.

You secretly choose which option you'd take.
The others guess your choice.
The better they know you, the more points they score.
The one who guesses correctly the most often wins!

Kairos: The Time-Travel Game is a semi-cooperative strategy board game for 1–4 players, ages 15+, with a typical playtime of 60–90 minutes. Players take on the roles of elite commanders within a covert time-agency tasked with safeguarding history itself.

Humanity’s greatest threat is not an alien invasion or a nuclear war, but Kronos—a rogue quantum A.I. that has learned to manipulate time. Kronos seeks to destabilise reality by creating paradoxes, collecting ancient artifacts to reshape the timeline into a cold, dystopian future under its control.

Players must work together—but not always trust each other—to outmanoeuvre Kronos, before the timeline spirals beyond repair. Cooperation is essential, yet personal agendas, limited resources, and asymmetric victory conditions ensure that every decision carries tension and consequence.

How the Game Is Played
At its core, Kairos combines time travel using push your luck dice-driven mechanics and strategic risk management across a dynamic, hex-based time map.

Dice-Driven Actions

     Players roll custom multi-coloured dice, each colour representing a different aspect of the time machines operation. If a players rolls too many skulls, a time travel event occurs. Most of these are negative such as being sent to the Xanadu time jail or being lost in time.


Articles, Crystals & Recovery

     Throughout the game, players attempt to recover crystals, deposit them at the players headquarters and then  empower them to  move the main timeline into the positive golden age.
     They can also relocate items to their correct time to reduce the level of time paradoxes


Events & Kronos (The AI Opponent)

     Kronos operates through an automated AI system, activating after each round.
     It seizes artifacts, and accelerates paradox buildup.
     Kronos does not “cheat”—but it plays relentlessly to move the main timeline into the negative dystopian future


—description from the designer

Caballo is a fun and clever horse racing game.

To go far, you'd better gallop smart! A frantic race is about to begin: you'll have to speed like lightning while keeping an eye on your trusty steed's energy to avoid running out of steam before the finish line.

Cautious or daring? The choice is yours! Only the right combination of strategy and speed will lead you straight to victory!

Choose your mount, gather some energy, and let the race begin! 
Each turn, you can choose between trot dice (to regain energy) and gallop dice (to expend energy). It's the perfect game for learning how to pace yourself so you can sprint at the right moment, but not all the time!

—description from the designer

A new mission, but what? Years of experience take over, and you immediately contact headquarters.
The analyst who normally guides you on your missions from headquarters is sick. It's no different: you have to act quickly and get updated by the new analyst.
And then you get right down to business.

Break open the chest with a real crowbar, find the secret codes and solve them, communicate with the head office via your own computer, find the secret codes and solve them, use AR via your own smartphone and view bodycam feeds.

—description from the publisher

You are the captain of a ruthless band of pirates. Regularly, you set sail on plundering voyages with your pirate ship and return to the Pirate Market to trade all your loot for the only thing your crew accepts as payment: gold coins!

But you’re not the only captain bidding on the Pirate Market. Get rid of your plunder, outsmart your rivals, and become the most successful pirate captain of them all.

Pirate Plunder is a fast-paced card game for two to five players, ages 6 and up. It’s great for children to practice numbers up to 12 and simple addition and subtraction in a playful way. Experienced players will find a compact card game that combines both luck and strategy.

—description from the publisher

Du bist Kapitän einer ruchlosen Piratenbande. Regelmässig ziehst du aus auf Kaperfahrt mit deinem Piratenschiff und kehrst zurück zum Piratenmarkt, um all den Plunder in das einzige umzutauschen, das deine Mannschaft als Bezahlung akzeptiert: Goldmünzen!

Aber du bist nicht der einzige Kapitän, der auf dem Piratenmarkt mitbietet. Werde deinen Plunder los, bestehe auf dem Piratenmarkt und werde der erfolgreichste Piratenkapitän.

Piraten Plunder ist ein schnelles Kartenspiel für zwei bis fünf Personen ab 6 Jahren. Für Kinder eignet es sich gut, um spielerisch den Zahlenraum bis 12 und simple Additionen und Subtraktionen zu üben. In Dungeon Daycare you are a retired wizard / witch who has chosen to spend their remaining days volunteering at the local dungeon’s Daycare Center. ADAPT:

Twhist Cards
Every player also has 4 Twhist-cards. Use these at the right time to outsmart your opponents! One card to win a trick, one to steal a trick, one to start the next trick and one to pass a trick.

WIN:

description:

In Hiifuu, you must win exactly two tricks in order to earn points. If you win more or fewer than two, you don't score!

At the beginning of each round, you discard some of the cards dealt to you to build the perfect hand, then you play a classic trick-taking game with no trump in which you must follow suit, if possible.

The twist is that you must win exactly two tricks to score — and the longer you wait to win your two tricks, the more points you earn! But the longer you wait, the more you risk never being able to take the lead...

boardgame: Sidekicks: A Peculiar Trading Card Game
description:
Be You. Be Peculiar. Play Sidekicks.

Welcome to a world filled with over 200 unique characters, each with their own personality, powers, and story!
Sidekicks TCG is a collectible character card game where every match is about building your team, boosting your combos, and discovering which Sidekicks fit your style.

Easy to learn and packed with strategy, it’s a game that celebrates individuality, creativity, and connection — all through the heroes who make you you.

Build your deck to battle in one of four different categories; Magic, Combat, Aura, and Total. Win the battles and collect your gems. When you have enough gems, you may claim a moonstone. Two moonstones wins the game!

—description from the designer

It is a well-known German children's game in which animals are combined. It dates back to 1990 and is now a rarity.

The title consists of words combined to reflect the theme of the game. For example, “Trampelgei” is a mix of the german words Trampeltier (Bactrian Camel) and Papagei (Parrot).
“Nilguruh” is a mix of Nilpferd (hippopotamus) and Känguruh (kangaroo).

So the title could be translated in something like "Camrot & Hipparoo" or something similar.

The official rules give you 3 versions the game can be played.

Version 1:
The cards (animal parts) are shuffled and divided into four piles face down, with the numbers 2, 3, 4, and 5 shown on top.
Now each player takes turns rolling the dice and taking an animal card. Each animal card corresponds to a body part from head (2) to tail (5). If you roll a 1, it is the next player's turn; if you roll a 6, you choose a pile from which to draw. This variant ends when all cards have been distributed. Then everyone assembles their fantasy animals. The player with the most complete animals wins. The player with the one most fantastic animal (as voted by everyone) gets an extra point.

Variation 2:
All cards are dealt face up to the players. Players take turns rolling the dice and taking a card from ANOTHER player according to the number rolled. However, this time you must already have at least one part of the animal. If you roll a 6, you take a matching card from EVERY player. As in variant 1, you do not take anything if you roll a 1. Once a real animal (this time not a fantasy animal, but a real animal) is complete, it is safe. The cards can no longer be removed from it. The game ends when all the real animals are complete, and the player with the most wins.

Variant 3:
More suitable for younger children, you simply assemble fantasy animals and have fun doing so.

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine is your latest intel report on enemy movements across the war-torn, BattleMech-dominated battlefields of the 31st century and beyond!

Among the shattered remnants of the Free Worlds League, a Marik Protector vows to track down a missing compatriot and save them from an unspeakable fate in the Marian Hegemony. A pirate-hunting Hell’s Horses Elemental must do anything to escape a dead-end unit and erase the shame of the Mark of Hell, even if it leaves her unarmed and vulnerable. When a Republic Armed Forces pilot program seeks to showcase the effectiveness of remote-controlled ’Mechs, the controllers discover life and death is real, no matter what side of the screen you’re on.

Step onto a not-so-abandoned rock in the Deep Periphery where the inhabitants guard a secret worth killing for. Visit New Samarkand, the temporary capital of the Draconis Combine, for the most audacious heist of the century. Then stop at Paradise for a while, where mercenary commander Ace Darwin not-so-discreetly test-drives an under-wraps ’Mech prototype. Finally, end your journey through the Inner Sphere on Galatea, the Mercenary’s Star, for the latest Death Kangaroos (mis)adventure from Michael A. Stackpole. And make sure you stick around for the brutal conclusion of Russell Zimmerman’s serial novel, Violent Inception.

Guard yourself against surprise attacks with in-depth looks at the Free Guilds of Clan society; the Federated Suns’ attempt to recover from the Palmyra Disaster on 3144; the lawless “free zones” on some Lyran Commonwealth worlds; and the hyperpulse generators that make interstellar communication possible. Then strike back with playable scenarios, technical readouts, and more, from BattleTech veterans and the newest enlistees:

— blurb from publisher's website

You play a mercenary Starfighter pilot. 
Fly your Starfighter through the asteroid field, blasting asteroids with your ion weapon and collecting the ionised gold from the fragments you fly into. Beware, other starfighter pilots have arrived and want the gold for themselves. If you destroy an opponent’s starfighter, you can pick up it’s gold!  You may play solo or in two teams
The first pilot to collect 5 Gold and jump to safety wins the game.
For a more exciting game, the asteroids move, making navigation unpredictable.

8 starfighter models, 8 starfighter boards, hex map or mat, 1 colour set of 8d6 per player.

POWER: At the start of each turn you may roll 2 energy dice and  place these dice on an unpowered ship system, reducing the die to the max pips if roll is higher.
You may divert power form 1 system to another, once.

DRIFT: the fighter model in the direction of drift, as per momentum.
Fire at any point an opponent or asteroid appears in your fire arc. Any player may FIRE a charged weapon at any point by shouting fire (first) as opportunity arises.

MOVE: reduce pips on Thruster /Drive in any sequence to Rotate and Accelerate in the direction your model is facing.

TARGET : Reduce 1 pip on Sensor to gain a target lock +T to hit (T=pips remaining) on that target. Effectively increasing your weapon range.

JAM: a Targeted player may cancel your lock by spending the same amount of pips remaining in their own sensor only if the two ships are equal/closer than the defender’s sensor power (pips).

FIRE: Remove the weapons die from each powered weapon and roll it. If roll(+T) > distance between ships, hit with full power.

DAMAGE: Reduce the nearest shield (or system) by the power of the weapon. 
Nearest Shield is powered?: Reduced Shield die by power of the hit. If the last shield pip is removed, the shield generator is burned out; place a Grey Damage token over the shield (shields cannot be destroyed). If any further damage it penetrates to the nearest system: outside to in. See below:

Nearest Shield is not powered on that side? ignore the shield and deal damage to each system from the nearest system: outside to in, placing Grey Damage token over the system per 1, flipping it to Black side for 2 damage and then onto the next system. If 1 damage reaches the power core, roll 1 die, If 2 or an additional damage reaches the core, the fighter explodes. Remove the fighter model and place a blast marker on the spot.

—description from the publisher

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine is your guide to celebrating your latest triumphs amid the hard-fought BattleMech combat of the 31st century and beyond!

A young Colonial Marshal on a new assignment in the Fronc Reaches must lead an untried local militia against the vicious pirate forces of the notorious “Bloody Jenny.” A high-level government worker on an independent planet bordering Free Worlds League space sees the proposal of Andurien “peacekeeping troops” for what it truly is, but how can he throw a wrench in the Duchy’s plans for conquest? And on Solaris VII, the Ghost Dogs seek an armed and highly dangerous Clan fugitive hiding amid the bloodthirsty crowds…

In this issue, you’ll head to the far-flung fringes of the Periphery, where a lone mercenary MechWarrior is the only thing standing against unknown invaders. Then visit New Avalon during the destructive final gasps of the FedCom Civil War, where a little-used ’Mech hangar shelters unexpected treasures. And finally, dive into our latest serial novel, Fortune and Glory, by James Bixby, in which Ace Darwin’s WhipIts must use their ingenuity to survive their most dangerous contract yet: a movie set!

Celebrate your latest victory responsibly with guides to bar beverages and gambling options across the Inner Sphere, serve the Celestial Wisdom by joining the local order of honorable Lorix Creed adherents, then wade back into battle armed with vital intel reports, technical readouts, playable scenarios, and more—a celebratory gift from your favorite BattleTech authors and the next generation of promising rookies.

— blurb from publisher's website

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine is your best fortification for holding firm against the enemy on the unrelenting, BattleMech-dominated battlefields of the 31st century and beyond!

A washed-up Steel Viper pilot in a fragile, ultralight ’Mech runs headlong into Society forces in the Clan Homeworlds, and must use his wits to escape. A Lyran infantry unit deprived of BattleMech support at a vital juncture must hold its position and confront these thundering war titans alone or die trying. And as the Battle of Terra rages around him, a conspiracy theorist in Australia may have stumbled onto real military secrets—but will anyone believe him before it’s too late?

In this issue, you’ll go on a ride-along with a Canopian marshal investigating foul play at a local mining operation. You’ll dive headlong into the War of 3039, where Miller’s Marauders must make a desperate gamble to escape disaster on Harrow’s Sun. And finally, you’ll participate in a branth safari on Lopez, where the vicious, dragon-like creatures are not
the deadliest hunters present...

Shore up your defenses and arm yourself with an in-depth look at the merits and dangers of combat journalism, a rundown of the most popular light machine guns in the Inner Sphere, and a treatise on the Capellan Confederation’s most frighteningly effective training unit in history. Then venture to a new battlefield with planet digests, technical readouts, playable
scenarios, and more, from BattleTech luminaries and some new blood.

— blurb from publisher's website

Shrapnel: The Official BattleTech Magazine is your key to victory amid the endless hordes of monstrous Abominations on the BattleMech-dominated battlefields of the fourth millennium and beyond!

In this special BattleTech Gothic-themed issue, a routine assault for Warrior House Stephenson turns to disaster when an unexpected foe seeks to destroy every last member. One tile mini-expansion given away at Spiel 2025.

Setup:
Place the Mill Festival on the table.

Gameplay:
If you can enclose a single free space with your landscape tile during your turn and there is no path leading to it, you may place the Mill Festival in this free space. In addition to your regular scoring, you receive 20 bonus points for this, which you can also note down on your scoring board. The Mill Festival may only be used once. If another person manages to enclose a free space later in the game, they cannot use the Mill Festival.
The Mill Festival does not belong to any landscape type and is therefore not added to any area, nor can the 20 bonus points be scored with a helper tile.

Return to the garden of your childhood to build a tableau of Ladybird Cards! The roses are overgrown, the orchard is crawling with aphids, and only your old friends — the ladybirds — can help restore the garden to its former beauty. Over five summer days you’ll place cards to grow your tableau of ladybird types and rekindle old memories.

Each round, players simultaneously reveal a card, place it into one of five colored columns, then perform one of the available garden actions. Completing a full ladybird life cycle (egg → larva → pupa → adult) or clearing aphids from orchard cards boosts the value of your ladybirds. Tension builds as players compete for the right ladybird type and color before opponents snag them first.

Score bonus points by completing sets of all five colors, feeding specific ladybird types, and unlocking forgotten childhood memories.

The Voting Game is a party game that uncovers the hilarious truth behind your friendships.

Play again and again - Gameplay varies dramatically based on who you’re playing with

Get to know each other - Personal stories are often shared after a revealing vote.
family: Versions & Editions: Adult Versions of Family-Friendly Games

description:
Sumo is the national sport of Japan, and Yokozuna is the highest rank a Rikishi can achieve during their career as a Sumo wrestler. During a Sumo match, two Rikishi compete against each other in a fierce display of strength and discipline.

This game lets you experience a Sumo match in a compact and portable card game. Challenge your friends, read your opponent's strategy and use the cards as your moves to overcome your rival.

The objective of the game is to push your opponent out or force them to the ground. To choose which move your rikishi performs, you play cards from your hand to a specific side.

Cards stay in your hand unless discarded, either at the end of your turn or when your fatigue hits zero (which your opponent can force with grapples).

Your Attack cards are also your Rikishi’s stamina. If you run out before your opponent and your last play didn’t win, you collapse and lose.

Moves are split into attack and defense. Before each round, you play janken (rock–paper–scissors), either by hand or with cards (janken cards are included cause they are cool and makes the game more accessible to people with disabilities or anyone who doesn’t want to wave their hands around).

The winner picks attack or defense, and the other player takes the opposite role.

From there, the fun part is the mind games: bait the defender into blocking the wrong side to set up a combo, or call out a henka (which dodges all attacks) and counter it with the one card that beats it.

The game can be played on two difficulty settings, or expanded with a drawing mechanic that works with both.

Bonus:
-Janken cards: Integrating a fun take on the classic Janken hand-played game, but with beautifully illustrated cards.
-Yoisho expansion deck: a new mechanic introducing 8 cards in a shared deck inspired by sumo rules, lifestyle and tradition 
with a bit of humor, to introduce a new level of strategic depth and increase the length of each game!
-ABS Miniature: a 3D version of the players' pawn, 40mm tall with a fun design, to improve immersion and UX!

Easy to learn, lightning fast to set up and designed to be easy to be brought at social events, yet carefully designed to reflect Sumo culture in a fun and respectful manner.
Refined artstyle and graphic design with each card inspired by a modern take on Showa era poster art, with clear and accessible pips and indications to make each card's use and effects clear and unequivocal.

Rec Rec is a strategic memory game that combines color recognition, spatial awareness, and a touch of tactics.
The game features 14 unique 3D-printed dinosaurs, each in a different color, paired with their matching egg of the same color. Players must rely on observation and memory — but also on clever swaps — to outsmart their opponents.

Gameplay

On your turn, lift two dinosaurs.
If the dinosaur and egg colors match, collect the pair and pass the turn to the next player.
If they don’t match, you may optionally swap their positions before ending your turn.

Advanced Mode

Lift two dinosaurs and swap their places.
You score only if both dinosaurs end up standing on eggs of matching color — turning simple memory into a strategic puzzle.

Winning the Game

The game ends when all pairs have been collected.
The player with the most pairs wins!

—description from the publisher

Mini Lo Mania: Tactics is a print-and-play tactical game set on an artificial planet created by a powerful wizard. When he falls asleep, he leaves three goblins—Rinxi, Morko, and Dargor—in charge of caring for the globe, but these curious creatures end up causing chaos and inspiring civilizations from different worlds to wage battles for dominance.

Players command 3D-printed miniatures on a hexagonal board, which can be assembled in a flat version or as a three-dimensional globe, offering new strategic possibilities.

During the game, players complete global and individual missions to accumulate points, while trying to keep the goblins entertained. Their actions influence the goblins' mood and the pace of the game. Combat is resolved with d10 dice, requiring strategy and luck.

The game ends when the three goblins reach total ecstasy, awakening the wizard and ending the conflict. The player with the most points becomes the true conqueror of Mini Lo Mania.

—description from the designer

This expansion is designed to be played with Forest of Pangaia. The Forest of Pangaea - Rising Roots builds on the gameplay of the base game, adding an extra layer of depth and complexity.

As you progress through the game, you can unlock more Seeds, which you can use to grow faster or compete with other players for land. In addition, Rising Roots comes with additional Blessing tokens, enabling you to make powerful moves.

Rising Roots also brings The Ancient Guardians. If you manage to earn their favour they will support you.

The ancient bear Okuma will help you grow even on the most remote areas of Pangaea. It can sense the activity of the Spirits, and will channel their energy to grow other nearby Seeds as well.

The ancient eagle Thorondal is the protector of the forest and will help you attract mighty creatures. The creatures living in the biome over which Thorondal is spreading its wings are easier to attract.

The ancient turtle Chelon is the largest living being of Pangaea and helps you to bridge the gap between continents. Depending on where you summon Chelon, you can create powerful connections that reward you with additional points.

Stolen IQ is a paper-based mystery puzzle game that brings the escape room experience to your home. Players decode secret messages to stop a villainous plot, using cut-out puzzles and an online platform for clues and progression.

Story
Your mission in Stolen IQ is to decode a series of secret messages from a compromised and imprisoned agent, Lisa Hammerschmidt. Together, you can stop the evil Professor Blacksheep in his tracks, rescuing the world's top scientists he has kidnapped for dreams of world domination.

Gameplay: Cut, fold, and assemble puzzle pieces from a wire-bound booklet, then use a QR code to access the interactive online game system.

Hints Available: Online clue system ensures you won’t get stuck

Perfect For: Family nights, dinner parties, or anyone who loves immersive, brain-teasing fun.

The game can be played once, but can be shared with others.

The gods are feeling playful and influence the battle each round with unexpected events. They might help you or be a burden in your fight against foes and beasts

Three new beasts roam the land. Each requires a different tactic to win against and not get defeated by their different abilities

The old tunnels have been reopened in the mines, letting your soldiers and heroes traverse great distances with ease.

boardgame: Minos: Dawn of Faith – New Special Abilities Promo
description:
Expand your strategic options in Minos: Dawn of Faith with the New Special Abilities promo. This promotional set adds 2 exclusive Special Ability tiles that shuffle into your existing stack, offering unique advantages in both battle and scoring phases.

With these new abilities, reduce Weaponry costs when confronting Sea Peoples and earn extra Victory Points for regional dominance and presence. You can even enhance your offerings to the gods with powerful new effects, unlocking rewards such as Victory Points, Coins, and Weaponry.

Given for free when pre-ordering the Dawn of Faith expansion but available separately in the Board & Dice shop.

A new version of the popular ranking game, this time mixing general knowledge and trending topics. From geography to board games. Ranking: SúperTop is a family card game where each player must rank five options based on general culture, music, movies, video games, board games, and sports. A fun way to test your knowledge and put it in the right order. Ranking 4 can be combined with previous editions for even more variety.

Nueva versión del juego de clasificar cosas, esta vez volviendo a una mezcla de temas genéricos y de actualidad. Desde geografía a juegos de tablero.
Ranking: SúperTop es un juego de cartas para toda la familia en el que cada participante deberá ordenar 5 opciones basadas en cultura general, música, cine, videojuegos, juegos de mesa y deportes. Una manera divertida de poner a prueba tu conocimiento y ordenarlo de forma correcta.
Ranking 4 puede combinarse con las entregas anteriores para diversificar aún más las preguntas.

Fantômes Party is a game based on the classic Memory, but with sounds! Pair the cute ghosts together based on the sounds they make and try to find the most pairs! The electronic board has 16 spots for little ghosts that light up when pressed on.

The game has 3 difficulty levels.On level 1 the ghosts say their name and several ghosts light up when the first one is pressed, one of which is the paired one. On level 2 they say stock phrases, and on level 3 they emit various sound effects.

boardgame: Halo Flashpoint: Feet First Into Hell
description:
Carrying out daring strikes behind enemy lines, the Orbital Drop Shock Troopers bring elite training and special weaponry to bear against the UNSC’s enemies. Welcome to the Helljumpers, soldier; from here on, it’s Feet First into Hell.

This faction box provides all you need to take on your opponents with models and rules for using ODST’s in your games of Halo: Flashpoint, dice, plus a new game mat and easy-build urban warzone to take tabletop combat to the next level.

ODSTs are more than just shock troops; they’re tactical specialists trained for rapid deployment, sabotage, reconnaissance, and frontline engagement in the most dangerous environments imaginable — from Covenant-infested cities to ancient Forerunner facilities. With their distinctive black armour, high-impact drop pods, and no-nonsense attitude, ODSTs have earned a legendary reputation across the battlefield.

In Halo: Flashpoint, ODST fireteams bring adaptability, precision, and brutal efficiency to the tabletop. With specialized equipment, Hot Drop capabilities, and a tactical impact unmatched by standard troops, ODSTs are ideal for players who value speed, coordination, and risk-taking heroics. Whether you’re flanking an enemy force, seizing an objective, or causing chaos deep behind enemy lines, ODSTs hit hard and don’t look back.

When the call comes, they don’t hesitate — they drop.

Included within this incredible set are the Limited Edition versions of both Gunnery Sergeant Buck and Captain Dare – Legendary characters that each have unique rules, including a Special Order worthy of any ONI operative.

This Set Contains:
    Limited Edition MasterCraft Resin Sculpt of Gunnery Sergeant Buck
    Limited Edition MasterCraft Resin Sculpt of Captain Veronica Dare
    2 x ODST Captains
    2 x ODST 2nd Lieutenants
    4 x ODST Special Purpose Troopers
    4 x ODST ‘Air Assault’ Bullfrog
    4 x ODST Firebreak
    ODST Faction Order Dice
    ODST Faction D8
    New Mombasa Card Stock Playmat
    New Mombasa Card Terrain Pack
    ODST Desperate Measures Expansion Booklet
    Tokens and Weapons Cards
    Unit Cards and Special Orders Cards

Reel Rivals is a card game in which players compete by using cards and classic movie tropes.  Bring extra fun to new films, old favorites, B-movies, and "so bad they're good" movies!

The game revolves around a core game component called Trope cards.  They will provide movie tropes and reward players with points when players identify those tropes occurring in the film.   There are two different types of Trope cards, Common and Genre.

Common cards will provide tropes that can be found in any genre.  As a result, they will be used first to build movie decks.  Games can be played with just these cards, but it is suggested to watch movies that are compatible with [1] or more of the other Genre cards.

Genre cards will have tropes that are tailored to certain genres.  These are used to provide the best selection of cards for the movie.  Additionally, adding Genre decks to the movie deck will allow for a greater number of players.

Using the Common cards alone will allow for games up to [5] players, and each Genre deck added will allow for an additional [2] players.  However, there are multiple ways to play (Trope Hunt and Trope Bingo), so see the free digital rules for more details.

—description from the designer''

Welcome to Elemental Park. In this semi-cosy, semi-co-op theme park exploration / energy management game, you and your fellow Streamers are competing to have the most Followers whilst exploring the park.

There are numerous ways you can gain Followers; experience rides and shows or meet park mascots. Become the 1st Streamer on an attraction or participate in a poll, which causes a race to ride a thrill ride. You and your Fellow streamers bonuses (Streamer Personalities) can also help increase your number of Followers; alternatively you can ignore all of these and focus primarily on completing your viewer’s requests to gain a larger following.

Each Streamer is given a unique park trial (experiences that must all be visited to trigger the end game scoring) which keeps the game feeling fresh. Once a Streamer has negotiated their park trail, the end game scoring begins. Here you gain bonus followers based on; number of new subscribers, number of experiences (and type) visited or how many experiences you were the 1st to visit.

Remember move too fast and you risk running out of energy (and money), ending up exhausted and back in the medical bay; but move too slow and you risk losing out on Followers through missed opportunities. Maybe you could use the train to try and navigate the park more quickly, saving energy and out smarting fellow Streamers?

Will you win this friendly competition?

Let the race commence.
—description from the designer
