The pieces are set up as in checkers, the difference being that each player's pieces are numbered and placed on the corresponding numbered squares on the board. The lowest-numbered pieces are placed in the front row and represent the pawns in chess: they draw out the opponent's pieces, especially those with higher numbers, clear the board by capturing, protect their own higher-numbered pieces from the opponent's attack, and thus help to bring the highest-numbered pieces into the opponent's field. The winner is the player with the highest point total at the end of the game.

Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Eagles and 2 Groundhogs in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Eagle, Groundhog, Eagle, Groundhog".

All players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

—description from the publisher

Monsters are attacking the town, and kid detectives known as the Creeps have to figure out which ones they can trap! Only the monsters with the most cards under them will score, but you also need those same monster cards in your hand to get points, so you have to decide when to play a monster card and when to keep it in your hand.

With The Creeps: The Monster-Trapping Game, you can play the "family game" for quicker games and younger audiences, or play the "full game" for asymmetric powers, game-changing trap cards, and more strategic scoring options.

—description from the publisher

The underworld is feuding and order must be restored! In Imps, 2 players command Imps, Implings, and Overlords to prove themselves most glorious on the battlefield or to slay the enemy Overlords.

Each player starts the game with a Castle containing 2 different Overlords and 4 different types of Imps, all with unique abilities. On your turn, you may do one of three things:
1) Enlist an Imp or Impling from the underworld into your Castle.
2) Move one of your Overlords.
3) March using Imps and Implings, and trigger any abilities.

Be careful how you March, as any Imps and Implings not in a Castle may be commanded by either player!

The game can end one of two ways:
1) The Slayer Track is filled and scores are calculated.
2) Both of a players Overlords are slayed and they immediately lose.

Imps uses a mancala-style "pick up and drop" mechanism where players may select ANY tile (except the enemy Castle) and March with the pieces there.

-description from designer

Archives of the Fallen World is a PnP Solo Strategy Board Game set in the final days of a collapsing dystopian town.

The core game tells the story of Leon, a former librarian. As the world collapses, he collects books to preserve the last pieces of human knowledge in his underground shelter. Your goal is to help Leon collect 32 books by completing various quests across 12 locations scattered throughout the town.

Solve quests, trade, steal, or search the ruins - there are many ways to recover the books. But beware! The streets are patrolled by local militia, and every time you get too close, you get into trouble and lose Faith- a precious resource that keeps your morale high and can be spent on risky or questionable actions.

Every day is a fight for survival. Your character needs food to stay active. Earn money to buy it, volunteer at the community garden, search the ruins, or dig through rubbish bins - do whatever it takes to eat, or lose a large part of your daily energy. And every night, the bombs fall. Buildings collapse, quests disappear, and time runs short. The longer you wait, the fewer opportunities remain. Plan your routes carefully. Use your energy wisely. Balance between growing hunger and fading hope.

You have 12 days before the doors of your bunker close.

boardgame: Максимум Апокалипсис: Пустоши (Maximum Apocalypse: Wasted Wilds)
description:
Максимум Апокалипсис: Пустоши ("Maximum Apocalypse: Wasted Wilds") is a deluxe Russian expansion for Maximum Apocalypse.  It features the same compelling survival gameplay but adds two distinct campaigns with a lot of new twists around exposure to the elements and interactions with other survivors. It features five new characters, two distinct campaigns, new mechanics, and tons of new monsters and apocalypses to face.

The Russian edition is a revised version of Maximum Apocalypse: Wasted Wilds and requires the basic game Maximum Apocalyse to be available.

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Tony Tony Chopper
description:
Character card for ODYSSEY/Animal/Straw hat Crew

"At the end of your nturn set up to 1 of your ODYSSEY type Characters with a cost of 4 or less as active."

Bayonets! is a print-and-play game of regiment-level Civil War combat.  
Players take on the role of brigade commanders in some of the most intense engagements of the historic conflict.

The primary design objective is to create a miniatures-style system that will fit in your pocket like a deck of cards. 
The system is easy to learn, emphasizing the role of leadership on the field and the brutal effects of battle.

There are pre-made scenarios included, but you can use the game as a toolkit to design anything you have in mind.

Run, boys, run! Don’t wait to form! Don’t let them stop! If you can’t run, then holler!
--Gen. Phillip Sheridan, Missionary Ridge, November 1863

Gameplay
The game is played in alternating turns. The scenario will indicate which side takes the first turn.
Each turn is divided into phases:

     Fire
     Move
     Leader


The game contains terrain cards and units cards

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague – Mission B23: Keychain
description: B23 is a hard difficulty mission for Zombicide: Black Plague.

boardgame: Zombicide: Black Plague – Mission B20: The Final Defense
description: B20 is a medium difficulty mission for Zombicide: Black Plague.

Upgrade your experience in Thebai with the Contracts Promo, a mini-expansion featuring 6 all-new contract cards that open up fresh possibilities, strategic trade-offs, and final scoring decisions.

These contract cards can be shuffled directly into the base deck during setup and add unique effects that give players more flexibility and risk-reward dynamics throughout the game. The promo also introduces a new scoring rule: during final scoring, you may only count one suit per promo contract fulfilled—encouraging more careful planning and synergy.

Sample effects from the new contract cards include:

     Spend Pottery and Bronze to activate the Cadmea tile your Archon is standing on.
     Trade resources to immediately draw and fulfill a contract (no rewards, but counts for scoring).
     Lose devotion to gain strong combos like bonus commodities, VP, or double tile effects.
     Make powerful one-time sacrifices for long-term benefits.


Perfect for players who enjoy expanding their decision space and optimizing endgame scoring, this promo adds an elegant layer of tactical depth to your Thebai sessions.

—description from the publisher

Add variety and tactical nuance to your games of Thebai with this promo expansion featuring 7 alternative Gate tiles. These tiles replace the standard ones from the base game and introduce new conditions and rewards tied to Hoplite power.

Gameplay changes:
Before the game begins, swap out the original Gate tiles with the alternative set. During the Fate phase, when resolving a Conflict tile, you no longer check for player participation or relative advantage. Instead, each new Gate tile displays a minimum required Hoplite strength. If your Hoplite power equals or exceeds that number, you gain the reward shown on the tile.

The official card game of the Italian Civil Protection Department with the help of Lucca Comics and Games.

Each round, a team leader assesses the situation and chooses 7 out of 10 available items they consider most useful to prevent or avoid a crisis described by up to three cards (for example: a fire, while you are in the woods, and people are panicking).
The other players discuss and try to guess which items were chosen.
It’s a cooperative game: you score points and win together!

AlGame invites players to dive into the wonders of the Mediterranean Sea and build their own marine ecosystem, card by card. Each turn, players expand their section of sea by connecting new environment cards - from rocky shores and seagrass meadows to sandy bottoms and open waters - and populate them with marine species.
Some of these species are algae, key organisms for the health of the sea. Placing them strategically allows players to unlock special Algalab bonuses: Cultivation and Restoration, rewarding those who manage to rebuild thriving underwater habitats.
But not all is calm beneath the waves: environmental threats such as pollution and climate change can alter or damage your ecosystem, forcing players to adapt their strategies.
Over three rounds, players balance biodiversity, risk, and opportunity in a race to create the most complete and resilient marine ecosystem. Combining environmental awareness with tactical gameplay, AlGame blends learning and fun  in a push-your-luck challenge set in the blue heart of the sea.

—description from the designer

The fenced cities and guarded routes were vital in the Middle Ages, when people were rooted to their lands and lords. Then a few ruthless merchants decided to defend their interests and organized a trade network later called the Hanseatic League.

You lead a merchant family with high status, enough to influence free cities and guilds to join the League. Use your people and power to establish trading houses and send out members of your family to take the riches.

Players take the role of merchant families, playing their cards and placing their family members to take actions in the cities. Each card played expands the network and the possible action for the players, who must manage scarce resources well to accumulate wealth, while taking over guilds and councils all around Europe.

—description from the designer

In Pietenpolonaise — the cheerful Sinterklaas card game from Gam’inBIZ — players race to be the first to form a colorful line of seven Piets, all happily following Sinterklaas.

Share cards, match colors, and rely on a bit of luck. That’s all you need for fifteen minutes of laughter and suspense!

But watch out for the bananas! Whoever slips loses the last Piet in their line. Luckily, the Rainbow cards can help you get the parade going again.

Pietenpolonaise is a compact family game and a perfect small gift for children aged 5 and up. It’s easy to learn, quick to play, and proudly printed in the Netherlands.
A joyful card game that helps kids develop planning, cooperation, and self-control — all while having fun together. Perfect for the holiday season or as a family Sinterklaas gift.

—description from the publisher

Be the first to complete the bead necklace and win the necklace cards!

In this game, the colorful necklace cards show which beads you need to find on your own cards. The player who first places the beads that match the necklace wins that necklace card. At the end of the game, all the points are counted, and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Rakd össze a gyöngysort elsőként, nyerd el a gyöngysor kártyákat! Ebben a játékban színes gyöngysorokat ábrázoló kártyák mutatják milyen gyöngyöket keress saját lapjaidon. Az a játékos aki elsőként teszi le a gyöngysorral megegyező gyöngyöket, megkapja a gyöngysor kártyát. A játék végén a összeszámoljuk a pontokat, az nyer akinek a legtöbb pontja van.

—description from the publisher

Set foot in mythical Prague back in the 19th century, when fantastical creatures walked the streets freely. Meet helpful Golems, amassing ancient knowledge, cunning Horned, controlling the city’s wealth, resourceful Robots, or eccentric Vodniks. Use their unique meeples to calm the river Vltava, rebuild the flooded centre, and control the city’s fate.

This euro-style worker placement game allows players to build their own action spaces, participate in four distinct “mini-games” based on retelling the myth-infused history of Prague, gain unique abilities, and fight over influence in four neighbourhoods.

—description from the publisher

“Just a little further, a bit higher, bro…” The cheetah gives the penguin a boost over the gate, while a confused wolf dashes past. “Look out, someone’s coming!” Everyone freezes. After a moment of silence, a sloth calls from a tree: “All clear, move out!” The animals have secretly snuck out of the zoo at night—and now they’re trying to find their way back. Help them return before the keepers notice!

The goal of the game is to help the animals and guide them safely back into their home. Players explore parts of the darkened zoo to discover the best routes home. Each round, players draft tiles and place them on their boards, triggering special actions and building a path back for their animals. Every animal has its own unique way of moving and scoring points, making each game a new adventure!

—description from the publisher

In this card game, you are invited to join the Great Clans, uphold the tenets of Bushidō, and fulfill your duty to your Empress.

Take on the leadership of your clan as daimyo and bring down your rivals, vying for military and political control of the land while maintaining society’s strict code of honor.

—description from the publisher
family: Setting: Legend of the Five Rings

description:
Tires screech as cars drift sharply between containers. Who will set the fastest time on the harbour track?

Harbordrift is a small expansion for the strategic dice-placement deck-building rally game Dirt & Dust, featuring all new track consisting of 10 cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Két kis dínó a világ körül
description:
Triko and Nyamm still haven’t found their way back to the Crayon Age!
But now you can travel through time with them!

Help the two little dinos journey from their prehistoric home to modern-day Budapest, then to the Middle Ages and the ancient island of Crete.
Save them from the raptor, the snarling cat, and other dangers, and fulfill the requests of the mighty volcanoes!

All you need is a bit of luck, a good memory, and clever tactics.
This cooperative board game helps children develop their combination skills, memory, and teamwork, because here everyone is needed — after all, four eyes see more than two! ?

The game includes two different versions: one for children aged 6 and up, and a simplified version for the youngest players.

—description from the designer

Created by a family of lawyers to educate people on criminal law, CRIME NO CRIME consists of 250 real-life scenarios that cover misdemeanors, felonies, police searches, seizures, and interrogations.

The game is played with a facilitator and teams of up to four players on each side. Players earn points for identifying whether a crime has been committed and naming the most serious charge for the offense. The first team to reach 100 points wins.

The car is packed, nature is calling, and the time is right to get away from it all for a few days. Raise your tent and unplug for a weekend in the great outdoors.

In Pack & Paddle, you spend three days in the woods with the stars as your ceiling and the wilderness your playground. Each day is filled with opportunities to explore nature, spot some wildlife, and have an adventure or two. A seasoned camper knows the key to a successful trip relies on good planning and a well stocked campsite. Throughout the day, draft supplies and tour your surroundings. At dusk, gather around the fire pit and contribute wood to keep the campfire going.

Pack & Paddle is the all new game from the designers of Fromage and The Search for Planet X.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Hokey Religions: An Expansion for Laserblade
description:
Hokey Religions is a free expansion for Laserblade, the fast-paced science fiction title by Echidna Games.

This short expansion gives players the option to add powerful mystics - wielders of unearthly powers - into their games of Laserblade.

Whether it be an apprentice, knight or master, the addition of a mystic into your Laserblade team grants you mystic powers like mystic deflection and the mystic mind trick.

So grab your miniature monks from a galaxy far, far away and give Hokey Religions a try!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Advanced Laserblade: Fast-paced Futuristic Skirmish Rules
description:
Advanced Laserblade is the follow-up to the original Laserblade by Echidna Games.

Advanced Laserblade pits two teams of highly customisable characters against each other on the battlegrounds of a galaxy far, far away - or wherever your imagination takes you!

Customise your team and take on your opponent in one of six exciting scenarios, using a D10 and figures from any manufacturer.

Advanced Laserblade expands on its predecessor in a way that is easy to learn for new players and veterans of the original.  Advanced Laserblade is fast to play, offering even more options for customisation than our previous games.

So grab your space monks, your away team or your orks and dive in to Advanced Laserblade.

ALB is usually played over six rounds.
Round sequence:
i) Roll for initiative
ii) Alpha player rolls for activations
iii) Alpha player performs activations (either one, two or three)
iv) Bravo player rolls for and performs activations
v) Round ends. Make valour checks. Refer to scenario rules. Advance the round counter
i) Roll for Initiative

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Single-card mission promo given away at Spiel 2025.

DESCRIPTION
This is a mission. Each mission alters the rules for an entire galaxy trucker flight. This mission can be played with any level 2 or 3 flight, and you can even include it as a part of a transgalactic trek.

GAMEPLAY
All forward-facing cannons above row 7 have half of their usual strength (sideways and backwards facing cannons have half strength, just like they normally would). Engines below row 7 do not work and have a strength of 0. On row 7, all components work as normal, following their usual rules.

REWARDS:
During final scoring, the reward for the best-looking ship (or titles) is tripled.

This game is playable with a standard deck of cards.

Less Than Three is a 2 player ladder climbing game. Over 6 rounds, players will try to win by emptying their hand first. If a player doesn't have a winning hand, they can sweep to regain control, or end the round early by knocking.

Using three different melds (singles, sets of 2 or more, and suited runs of 2 or more), players will attempt to climb over their opponent's plays.

Both players have 2 sweep cards to last them the entire game. These clear the field of all cards played to the current trick, and allow the sweeper to lead the next trick. Only one of these may be played in each round. So over 6 rounds, player A will use 2 sweeps, Player B will use 2 sweeps, and 2 rounds will go without a sweep played.

Players can end a round early by knocking when they have less than three cards remaining in their hand. This will score them the difference in remaining cards between them and their opponent.

Or they can risk continuing the round, hoping to shed out of cards completely, and earn a 5 point bonus.

In the game Slowianie ("Slavs"), players must collect resources from the board and use them to build structures based on the cards in front of them. Easy? Not at all! Interactions with other players, fate cards, and surprises hidden on the board can complicate this seemingly simple task. Without strategic thinking and a little cunning, it is impossible to achieve the goal, which is one and only one: to build the largest Slavic settlement possible! A pinch of luck and the favorable gaze of the gods will also come in handy. The undoubted appeal of the game lies in its wooden elements and interesting graphics, which immerse you even more in the ancient world of the Slavs.

ESP is a clairvoyance aptitude test in the form of a trick-taking game.
Players will use their psychic abilities to predict the trump suit and the number of tricks they will capture in each of 4 rounds. Correct predictions and captured tricks earn points.

—description from the publisher

Two unique characters with wildly different abilities, opening up new challenging ways for scriptwriters to scheme. The Sennin, with the ability to revive the dead, and the Uploader, with the ability to use his ability at the location of anyone with an EX Card on them.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Vignette of Death: Screaming Nukealope
description:
Vignettes of Death are a great diversion from the intensity of Monster campaigns or an easy way to introduce new players, dipping their toes into the fathomless ocean of Kingdom Death: Monster.

A squabbling, unlikely alliance rallies around Gnostin, determined to stop a destructive new quarry or die trying. Transfigured by radiation, the Screaming Nukealope’s existence is constant pain for itself and its would-be hunters alike. Embody the wits and teamwork of Gnostin’s close knit company to achieve his envisioned perfect ending.

Using ready-to-play gear grids, survivor vignette cards, and limited components from your Monster core game makes the vignette a quick way to dive into a brand new story.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Keystone: North America – Second Edition: 4-Player Upgrade Pack
description: Enhance your gaming experience to accommodate up to four players with our upgrade pack. This pack includes two extra player boards, a supplementary deck of species cards, and additional tokens. With these additions, you can now involve more friends in the excitement of your game, creating even more thrilling and competitive adventures.

boardgame: Keystone: North America – Second Edition: Coastal Expansion – 4-Player Upgrade Pack
description: This upgrade pack includes everything needed to allow up to 4 players to play the Coastal Expansion. Includes additional Conservation Centers and a second coastal species deck.

An expansion for Zooloretto, given for free by Abacus at Spiel 2025 in Essen.

This expansion consists of 1 tile with a ticket booth.

All rules of the basic game apply with the following changes:
If you are the first person who has filled their 6-space enclosure, you may take the ticket booth from the miidle of the table and place it next to your zoo. The ticket booth remains there until the end of the game and is the storage place for all your coins. Coins on the ticket booth can still be used for money actions.

Scoring: Each two coins on the ticket booth count as 1 additional plus point in the scorig.considered during scoring.

One side is English (ticket booth), other side is German (Kassenhäuschen).

boardgame: Pre-game Barrage Rules for: Through the Mud and the Blood and Cocking Up the Mud and the Blood
description:
Pre-game Barrage Rules for: Through the Mud and the Blood and Cocking Up the Mud and the Blood

Presented here is a combination, or mash-up if you will, of the MUD & CoC-MUD barrage rules.  
These pre-game barrage rules could also bolt on to almost any World War 1 skirmish rule set with little or no adjustments.

boardgame: Play the Game: Being a Collection and Compendium of Articles on the Great War from TooFatLardies Specials, 2004 to 2015
description:
Play the Game, A Great War Compendium

Since 2004 we have published many articles, scenarios, campaigns and gaming ideas covering the Great War, either for Through the Mud & the Blood or If the Lord Spares us.  Now these have been packed into a 276 page PDF compendium full of all you could wish to game this period of intense technological change and tactical development.  Here is a full list of the contents which covers up to the Summer Special 2015.


     If the Lord Spares Us, an introduction to the rules
     Attack on El Mughar, A Palestine 1917 scenario for ITLSU
     Square Hill, A Palestine 1918 scenario for ITLSU
     A Different Kettle of Fish. Using machine guns in ITLSU
     Advance to Kut! An ITLS scenario in Iraq 1915
     Not Another Bloody Poet. An overview of the Great War for the gamer
     Things are a Bit Ard. ITLSU amendments for East Africa
     Tanga, Day One. An ITLSU scenario in East Africa 1914
     Tanga, Day Two, An ITLSU scenario in East Africa 1914
     The Other Mboto Gorge. An ITLSU scenario in East Africa 1916
     In Jordans Hot Valley. An ITLSU Scenario in Palestine 1918
     A Tight Ring. An ITLS scenario in Syria 1918
     Corps Blimey. Some Great War kriegsspiel ideas and a sample Hindenburg Line scenario for 1917
     Sids Trenches. A trench terrain project from the master builder.
     Attack on Camp el Juba. An ITLSU scenario in Mesopotamia 1915
     Ils Ne Passeront Pas. Mud & Blood scenario at Verdun 1916
     Somme Enchanted Evening. Mud & Blood scenario on the Somme 1916
     The Game to End all Games. A western front scenario for Mud & Blood
     Revenge at Camp El Juba, An ITLS scenario in Mesopotamia 1915
     Last Chance on the Tigris. An ITLSU scenario in Iraq 1916
     The Disturbance of an Antheap. An Amiens 1918 scenario for Mud & Blood
     Winter Sports. A trench raiding game generator for Mud & Blood
     Rolling into Action. Representing the Tank Corps at Third Ypres, 1917, some ideas for Mud & Blood
     Rolling In to Action: The Scenarios. Two tank action scenarios for Third Ypres 1917
     Midnight at the Oasis. A Lawrence of Arabia scenario for Mud & Blood
     Insurrection is Mesopotamia. Two scenarios for Iraq in the 1920s with Mud & Blood
     Storm of Dice. Using ITLSU on the Western Front
     Sidney Gets Woods. Building shattered woods for the Western Front
     A Brief Affair. Invading the German colony of Rabaul in 1914 with Mud & Blood
     Passchendaele, The Bitter Victory. Reflecting on this 1917 battle in the mud
     In the Salient. Three Scenarios for Passchendaele 1917 for Mud & Blood
     Just the Right Sort of Chap. Creating characters for Mud & Blood.
     More Sturmabteilung Vor! Stormtrooper tactics examined and expanded for Mud & Blood
     Stosstrupptaktik! A Pre-Game System for Mud & Blood
     The Green Fields Beyond. Training Troops for the long anticipated break out. 1917 to 1918. Three scenarios for Mud & Blood
     In Flanders Field. A look at early war British and German fire and movement tactics.
     In the Last Ditch Again. A Retreat from Mons 1914 rearguard action with Mud & Blood.
     Cocking Up Mud & Blood. Thoughts on blending Chain of Command with Mud & Blood. Rule amendments, Army Lists and scenario adaptations
     Kaiserschlacht 1918. A Pint-Sized campaign for the Spring Offensive of 1918. Six Scenarios for Mud & Blood and Chain of Command
     Handgranaten! A look at bombing and grenade tactics through the Great War
     Loos, At your Convenience. A Pint-Sized campaign for this 1915 battle with four scenarios

In Dobba, you'll find yourself immersed in the galactic chaos of the planet of the same name. Similar to other games of this genre, such as Mau-Mau, you have to match colors, symbols, and numbers to ultimately escape the black hole and emerge victorious.

The catch? At the end of each round, you ideally have to weigh which card to place on your personal discard pile – in the worst case, your hand or the other players will force your hand, and you won't have a choice. Can you manage to collect the fewest points on your pile by the end of the game?

Strategy and luck come together in this hybrid of a classic card game and "light" deck-building. Perfect for younger space travelers too – easily accessible, yet engagingly complex and interactive.

The world of Dobba is hand-illustrated, humorously designed, and offers an extra highlight for young and old alike thanks to its charming visuals.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In Dobba findet ihr euch selbst im galaktischen Chaos des gleichnamigen Planeten wieder. Ähnlich zu anderen Spielen des Genres wie Mau-Mau müsst ihr gleiche Farben, Motive und Zahlen bedienen, um am Ende dem schwarzen Loch zu entkommen und siegreich zu sein.

Der Clue? Am Ende jeder Runde müsst ihr im besten Fall abwägen, welche Karte auf euren persönlichen Ablagestapel kommt - im schlechtesten Fall zwingen euch euer Blatt oder die Mitspielenden und die Wahl entfällt. Schafft ihr es, am Schluss die wenigsten Punkte auf dem Stapel gesammelt zu haben?

Taktik und Glück kommen in diesem Hybrid aus klassischem Kartenspiel und "Light"-Deckbuilding zusammen. Perfekt auch für jüngere Weltraumreisende - leicht zugänglich, aber ansprechend komplex und interaktiv.

Die Welt von Dobba ist handillustriert, humorvoll gestaltet und bietet Jung & Alt durch die liebevolle Optik ein zusätzliches Highlight.

—description from the publisher

Schwert und Magie ("Sword and Magic") simulates an expedition into an underground labyrinth, where treasures but also monsters and dangers lie hidden. The aim of the game is to lead a small group of adventurers into the cave and retrieve as many treasures as possible.

Players take turn and on each turn one can Scout, Explore, Move, Recruit, Fight and/or Loot. On each scout action, tiles are added to the map and forming the labyrinth. Exploring caves leads in drawing several event Cards (Inhabitants, Treasures, Events, Curse, Blessings or Traps). Recruited labyrinth inhabitants join your group. Looting add items and treasures to your inventory. 
Be careful! Not every inhabitant is friendly and the traps in the labyrinth can be very deadly. More than one group that entered the labyrinth was never seen again.

•••

Schwert und Magie simuliert eine Expedition in ein unterirdisches Labyrinth, in dem Schätze, aber auch Monstren und Gefahren verbogen sind. Ziel des Spiels ist es, mit einer kleinen Abenteuergruppe in die Höhle einzudringen und so viele Schätze wie möglich herauszuholen.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Pawn’s Gambit is a chess-inspired card game of strategy, adaptability, and tactical duels. Each round, players battle to seize control of Imbalance Cards and disrupt their opponents’ Played Pieces to their advantage. There are three types of cards: 

     Opening Cards: Each illustrated with a fictional medieval character, representing the strategy and spirit of the respective classic chess opening. Your chosen opening sets your strategy for the round and grants unique abilities to shape your approach. 
     Piece Cards: Reflecting traditional chess pieces, these are played according to your chosen strategy and serve as resources to perform actions throughout the game. 
     Imbalance Cards: Illustrated with medieval warfare motifs, these cards create shifting conditions that affect your played Piece Cards. Players can use Game Actions to align favourable Imbalance Cards with their played pieces. 


Objective: Outmanoeuvre your opponents and achieve checkmate by winning two rounds or be the last player standing with a surviving king.

—description from the designer

Introduction:Lichen is a drawless strategy game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the squares (cells) of a square board. The recommended size is 12 cells per side, although boards of 10 and 14 are also valid. Each player occupies the first three rows on their side with their own stones.

This game is based on Ordo and Epaminondas.

				
				
					Definitions:
     A lichen is a maximal set of stones of the same color connected orthogonally or diagonally. A single stone is also a lichen.
     A hypha of a lichen is a straight line within the lichen that contains one or more consecutive stones of the same color.


				
				
					Turns:White plays first, and then turns alternate. On your turn, move one hypha of your color orthogonally or diagonally through any number of empty cells in a straight line, either in a direction parallel or perpendicular to its original orientation, possibly dividing your lichen into several, as long as the moved hypha belongs to a lichen larger than those separated from it; afterwards, keep only the largest one (if there is a tie, you may choose). Apply the same rule to enemy lichens that become splitted after a removal.


     If the movement is forward, it may not move backward.
     If it moves sideways, it must end farther from the center of the board than it was at the start.
     However, you may ignore these restrictions if you are making a removal.


During the movement, you may remove by crossing exactly one enemy hypha, even if there are empty cells along the path; each stone of that hypha you pass over is removed from the board, and you cannot continue moving once you have passed the last stone of that hypha.

				
				
					End of the game:You win by reaching the last row with at least one of your stones or by removing all your opponent’s stones.

–description from designer

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Dicefest 2025 Goody Box
description:
You can purchase the DiceFest 2025 Goody Box only during DiceFest Weekend and only on the FFP Web Store!

Includes:


     Exclusive Veteran Frontier Doc Thermal Plastic Miniature on Custom Mines Theme 30 mm base
     Dead River Bank (Canyons) / Acid Lake (Blasted Wastes) map tile
     Map Cards for this new tile
     Gremians OtherWorld Threat Pack (7 cards)
     Experimental Parasite
     Dissolved Dead Blasted Wastes Encounter
     Bodies in The Water Canyons Encounter


Models are Thermal Plastic Miniatures manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Greek Champion Hero Pack
description:
Down through the ages, epic poems and stories have been told about ancient Greek heroes of myth and legend. Born of divine blood and gifted by the gods, these champions of mankind set out on quests that few others would take on. Sailing the roaring seas, climbing mountain peaks, and delving into the ancient ruins and tunnels of the Underworld, to seek out lost relics and terrifying creatures in the defense of mankind.

This Hero Pack contains everything you need to play as a Greek Champion in your games of Shadows of Brimstone! There are 2 Greek Champion models included, male and female, as well as the large Hero Character Sheet, Upgrade and Skill Tree Sheet, and all of the cards needed to take on the role of this Greek Hero.

There are two versions of this Hero Pack (A and B). The printed material is the same, but the two Hero Miniatures included are different. Each set contains a Male Greek Champion, a Female Greek Champion, and two Greek Temple Ruins Themed 30 mm bases. But the miniature poses are different between Set A and Set B.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Lycian Gorgons Enemy Pack
description:
In the flickering torchlight of the dim temple ruins, a low murmuring hiss and the faint roll of a rattle echoes through the crumbling stone halls. Cursed by the gods, the Lycian Gorgons slither through the dark and abandoned places of the old world, ready to let loose a volley of barbed arrows,
or turn their hideous gaze upon intruders, transforming flesh to stone in the blink of an eye.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to battle the scorned Lycian Gorgons. Included are 3 large Lycian Gorgon models, 3 Greek Temple Ruins Themed 40 mm bases, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, a set of counters, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add these deadly Mythic Enemies to your games of Game: Shadows of Brimstone.

Models are Thermal Plastic Miniatures manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

This is a partygame that can be played in teams or free-for-all. On eachbcard there are five songtitels.

On a player or team turn, one card is drawn and the player or players choose a songtitle. Then they take a sip of water and gargle the song. The other players have to guess the songtitle.

Tranzyt is Polish card game, placed in world of trucks, heavy transport and long routes.

In Tranzyt, you step into the role of a freight forwarder — planning routes, managing drivers, and balancing risk against potential profit. Every decision matters: will you speed up your own delivery or block your rivals? Play it safe with a sure deal, or risk it all for a bold move? It’s a “easy-to-learn, hard-to-put-down” card game — perfect when you want that quick hit of strategy, tension, and competition, while still having time for multiple rounds in a single session.

Who is it for?
For families and friends who enjoy fast, engaging games without an hour of rule explanations. For casual players who want a true “open and play” experience. And for fans of the logistics and trucking world — dispatchers, drivers, and route planners — who love when theme and mechanics click perfectly together.

Tranzyt is tuned for 2–6 players and plays in 15–30 minutes, making it great for short breaks, family evenings, or game nights. The rules are easy to grasp, yet the game offers meaningful tactical decisions, interactive blocking, and high replayability — each deal brings a fresh setup and new challenges on the road.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Ashbloom of Dedheim
description:
The Ashbloom of Dedheim is packaged in a festive box that includes:

1 x Hard plastic Ashbloom of Dedheim and steed dynamic figure
1 x Hard plastic scenic base insert
1 x Hard plastic Kingdom Death 50mm base
1 x Large matte art print
1 x Living Glossary card
3 x Seed pattern cards. 
3 x Seed pattern gear cards

A mouse-like creature took shelter in the gourd. Accepting the protection of the ever-thickening rind in the cruelest season, the creature finally gave in and nibbled a morsel from the fruit's foul flesh.

The creature quickly grew to outsized proportions and outsized fury. When Ashbloom emerged, her overgrown steed instinctively waited at her side. Mounting, she sorrowfully rides through Dedheim cruelly skewering any who would carry even an inkling of escape in their hearts.

boardgame: Kingdom Death: Monster – Speaker Cult Pipe and Osseous Scrap Blade Promo Cards
description:
This is a set of two beta gear cards and the corresponding living glossary cards. They were included with the Dark Confidant, which itself is not part of the game.

Osseous Scrap Blade is a sword that grants a survivor a free attack if they moved at least eight spaces during their act.

The Speaker Cult Pipe can be activated to give the survivor -1 accuracy and +1 evasion.

Three mini expansions for Medievallons.

ACTION CARDS
Action cards are a new addition to Medievallons and work a bit different from guild cards. You can't use Action cards in a guild, but they can be very powerful when played at the right time.

For this mini expansion we will introduce 8 more cards of 2 different types:
The Shield and The Breaker

Both of these cards can be used outside of your turn as a reaction to another player.
For example: a player wants to use a Witch  to copy one of you guilds, play a Shield to prevent that action. The other player discards their Witch card like normal, and you place your Shield card on top of the discard pile.
But! What if you opponent (or any other player for that matter) has a Breaker?
Well… they can just break your Shield card.

CROSSOVER CARDS
The Medievallons don’t want to be left out during the big multiverse crossover event of course! We have created some cool Action cards based on the worlds of L’oaf and Goblin Laundromat which will make your games even more dynamic and hectic.

What are these new actions cards? Let’s start with the L’oaf crossover cards.
The 3 new L’oaf Action cards allow you to put an opponent’s guild ‘In the oven’
This means their guild will be flipped facedown until the end of their turn. This guild can’t be used until it is ‘out of the oven’ (aka flipped face up). On the plus side, this guild also can't be interacted with by other players, so no stealing, destroying or copying cards.

And now for the Goblins, who are known for their chaos.
These 3 Goblin cards will allow you to ‘swap clothes’.
Take two guilds from the board and swap them between the two players.
You had a Noble guild? Too bad, now you have a Star guild.
Pure chaos!

AMSTERDAM PRIDE WEEK CARDS
Medievallons - The Lovers
The Lovers are Medievallons that want to connect people and see past their differences. You can play a Lover with any other guild card to add to an existing guild, creating a mixed guild of two different powers together.

—description from the publisher

Three mini expansions for L'oaf

REPUTATION BOARD
This expansion add 2 new cards which create a new experience on the reputation board.

During setup, place the two new cards on the reputation board, so that they cover the positive (green) side of the board.

The overlay cards change the distribution of bonus points for having a positive reputation. 
A reputation of 8 or higher will result in negative points (the bosses don't like try-hards..).
You basically create a whole new board which allows for (even) more replay-value.

CUSTOMERS
Every bakery needs customers, you can bake bread all day but in the end money needs to be made to keep you ovens running.
These new customers are joining us from the castles of Medievallons and the dungeons of Goblin Laundromat.

During setup, shuffle the Customer cards and place them in a closed deck. At the start of each round, reveal one Customer from the deck and place it face up. At the end of each round, check if any players can claim the Customer. Each Customer shows two conditions that must be met before it can be claimed, and points when you claim them.

Medievallons will give you points when they are claimed after a successful order, they just love some baguettes and croissants
Goblins love the chaos (and don’t really like bread that much…), so they will be claimed when an order fails.

AMSTERDAM PRIDE WEEK
L’oaf - Rainbow numbers

Working is hard, why not add a bit of colour to you daily life in the bakery? Don’t work too hard, celebrate and play a Rainbow card. After the day is done you decide what number it will be.

—description from the publisher

Three mini expansions for Goblin Laundromat

LAUNDRY MIMICS
What are the most dangerous monsters you can find in a dungeon?
Trolls ?, Dragons ?, Spiders?️? No, of course not! Mimics are the most dangerous. And not the treasure chest variety, but the special pile of Laundry Mimics are terrifying! They can also be very useful.

For the Goblin Laundromat promo cards we added Laundry Mimics to the game. Laundry Mimics are very hard to clean, they have stains of each of the 3 types. And they are very rare, only 1 of each colour are in the game. But when you clean them, they are great.

At the end of the game Laundry Mimics can take the form of any piece of clothing.
Missing that matching sock to create a pair? Just use a Laundry Mimic. Are you tied with another Goblin for the underwear majority (this is such a weird game…)? Just use your Landry Mimic to get the upper hand.

CROSSOVER CARDS
Somehow laundry from the L’oaf bakery and the castle of the Medievallons has ended up in the Goblin Laundromat.
None of the species that come to your laundromat have a particular use for aprons or jester hats, but these cards can be very valuable.

If you only manage to get 1 cards from a set, it is just worth 1 coin. When you manage to get an extra card from the same set you will get an extra bonus coin. It might be worth it to start collecting these strange items from another world anyway.

AMSTERDAM PRIDE WEEK CARDS
Goblin Laundromat - Pride socks
Who says we have to conform to wearing the same socks, why can’t socks not be together with any sock they want? The new Pride socks allows just that, just add a blue sock to your rainbow sock. It is your life, love what you love.

—description from the publisher

HEXplore It fans -this add-on to the Netherwatch campaign; is for you. We've created Encounter cards for each of the 12 Demons featured in Netherwatch.

This item includes:

     12 Unique Demon Cards
     1 Summoning Circle Card
     Keyword Reference


Please note that this crossover card pack is NOT related to Netherwatch and requires the use of any HEXplore It Volume.

Jason and the Argonauts is an epic solo card and dice rolling and allocation game featuring deeply thematic lore cards. Players take on the role of Jason, commanding the legendary ship Argo and leading a crew of heroes on a perilous journey to retrieve the Golden Fleece.

Sail across treacherous seas as you roll, allocate, and manipulate dice in this boss battler style game to overcome deadly challenges and rival forces, from the Harpies that plague Phineus, to the clashing rocks of the Symplegades, and the fire-breathing bulls of Colchis.

Your voyage will span the ancient world, from the Oracle’s cryptic visions in Iolcus to the heart of Colchis itself. Between boss battles, Journey cards challenge you with rough seas, ravaging pirates, and divine trials from the gods which will test even the bravest leaders.

Only by retrieving the Golden Fleece and returning home can you fulfill your destiny, earning eternal glory among Greece’s greatest heroes, winning the game.

Will you command the Argonauts to victory?

Paddington Bear loves his new home with the Brown family almost as much as he loves making marmalade! As Paddington travels delivering his marmalade, he visits famous 27
London landmarks like Piccadilly Circus and Buckingham Palace. But be careful - trouble seems to find Paddington when he least expects it! The first player to deliver the jars of marmalade and return to the Browns' home wins the game.

—description from the publisher

9 bonus cards of whom one more is revealed then the number of players at the start of the game. The player whom is last selects one first.

Once during the game you can activate the bonus card as an additional action to get what is shown (1 of the 5 resources, one free movement, acquire an equipment, cross a canal for free or construct a building)as a one time bonus.

It was given for free if you backed the game at Spieleschmiede or bought it at Spiel 2025.

Promo expansion originally given out with purchases of Karuba Since 2015 at Spiel 2025.

Each player begins the game with a randomly drawn shrine tile placed in front of the designated expedition member or temple on their player board, shifting the starting position of that token and creating asymmetric starting setups.

Shrine tiles are impassable. Once any player reaches their second temple with an expedition member, the Moon Goddess awakens and all players' shrines are flipped, removing all adjacent tiles and tokens from play.

Expedition members removed this way score no points for tokens collected during the game. Any player with no remaining expedition members skips their remaining turns.

boardgame: Invincible: The Card Game – The Old Guard
description:
An expansion for Invincible which includes two new heroes (Cecil and Immortal), a new villain (Doc Seismic), new player cards, a new city, and a new Team Attack card.

Included in Invincible: The Card Game (Kickstarter Edition), later released separately for the retail edition.

A climbing shedding game with a slot machine theme. Cards have no numbers, but different slot machine symbols. A hierarchy of symbols is dealt out at random at the beginning of the game. When all players pass on a meld of symbols, that symbol goes to the bottom of the hierarchy.

Players can flip the hierarchy once per game with a lucky coin before they take their turn.

—description from the designer

A solo pen and paper game of chance and strategy where you must save your home and land using turn-based tactics to prevent a growing wildfire from consuming everything in its path.

The game takes place on a 6x6 grid. A pair of dice determines the origin of the fire along with additional events each turn while the player chooses how best to thwart the fire's spread, either dousing a flame or digging a ditch to prevent fire from spreading to a location.

The game ends when either the fire can no longer spread or the player's home is engulfed. Points are awarded for the number of squares not burned by the fire.

—description from the designer

Huizachtépetl y la modernidad ("Huizachtépetl and Modernity") tells the story of a hill in Mexico City that has been stalked by urban sprawl. In this cooperative game, the goal is for the inhabitants of Huizachtépetl—Huizache, Viento, Tlaloc, and Comunidad—to generate the Fuego Nuevo (New Fire) to halt the modernity that threatens the life of the hill.

To achieve this, players must explore the hill to find the pieces of the Fuego Nuevo, which are hidden in caves. But beware: modernity lurks as the city grows and continuously devours the hill. Players must succeed before the city completely consumes the hill.

Mexico City is eating away at the Cerro de la Estrella (Huizachtépetl). Declared a National Park in 1938 when it spanned 1,100 hectares, today it has been reduced to just 120. Its inhabitants struggle daily to protect it. Perhaps the Fuego Nuevo ceremony will bring a new era where humanity and nature can coexist in harmony once again. But its inhabitants are running out of time in the face of modernity's expansion

Darkstone is a cooperative dungeon crawler board game for 1 to 4 players, where each hero takes on a unique role in a perilous quest through the Tower of Shadows.

Play as Garbar the Dwarf Warrior, Sandra the Scout, Roderick the Heir, or Vandor the Wizard, and explore a dark fortress filled with traps, cursed magic, and monstrous guardians.

Your mission: find the 4 Dark Stones and escape alive.
But beware, the Tower is guarded by undying goblins, poisonous creatures, and deadly spells lurking behind every door.

The game unfolds over 20 rounds, with each round shaped by an Event Card that changes the conditions of play. Strategy and teamwork are vital, every decision one hero makes can determine the fate of them all.

Will you brave the darkness, recover the lost Dark Stones, and survive... or be consumed by its shadows forever?

Cooperate. Survive. Conquer the Darkness.

—description from the designer

Discover Taiwanese cuisine through books and games! 42 Taiwanese dishes and 6 unique cultural cards, adapted from a fun European board game. Divided into seven banquets, each with a six-course menu, players must painstakingly piece together the dishes from other players' "banquet cards." The player who completes the most banquets wins!

—description from the publisher

Sleeping Ninjas is a fast-paced, dice-and-card battle game where rival ninjas compete to get the best night’s sleep without being rudely awakened!
Players roll custom ninja dice each turn, unleashing a multitude of attacks to sabotage their rival ninjas.

Each round, ninjas secretly play defence cards to block incoming attacks and sleep cards to earn precious hours of rest. Do you choose to play a bluff to power up, or will you choose to redirect the move elsewhere?

The first ninja to reach 8 Sleep Points wins… if they can stay asleep long enough.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Marauder's Map (fan expansion for Age of Steam)
description:
A Harry Potter themed print-n-play expansion map. Features special rules in the move goods phase depending on what color cube was deliverd the most last turn.

Plays 3-6 players; recommended best at 4 or 5.

Whether gathered around the table or playing via video call — with Quarantgame, you’re always flexible. Try to keep your body temperature under control and send your opponents into quarantine. It takes skill and a good memory. And don’t forget: stay healthy!

The goal of the game is to collect as few body temperature points as possible and to remain fever-free at the end. The game is played over several rounds. After each round, the temperature is checked by adding up the values of the remaining playing cards. The game ends as soon as someone reaches a body temperature of 38° Celsius or higher (= fever) and must go into quarantine.

Sustainably produced board game offering entertaining distraction during lockdown times.

Standard clue, Wednesday tv show theme.

There is more than the normal wolfing out and hiding from the sun happening among the outcasts at Nevermore Academy. The Hyde is attacking the students and townsfolk. Wednesday Addams has taken it upon herself to protect her friends, but can she?

Taking on the roles of six characters from Wednesday, players will move around the game board featuring locations in and around Nevermore Academy and the town of Jericho. It is a race to discover where the hide will attack, what weapon they can use for its capture, and most importantly who will be the Hyde's next victim?

All 6 playable characters (Wednesday Addams, Ajax Petropolus, Eugene Ottinger, Enid Sinclair, Yoko Tanaka and Biana Barclay) will start with a once-per-game unique ability that provides a special advantage over your opponents!

Includes:
Custom Game Board
6 Student Movers
6 Personality Cards
21 Rumor Cards
25 Intrigue Cards
family: Game: Cluedo / Clue (Parker Brothers, et al)

description:
In the future a new man made renewable resource called CONTINUUM is created. Unknown to scientists at discovery, continuum becomes radioactive over time. One of the first made artifacts eventually becomes unstable, causing a nuclear explosion. Race back through time to find the artifact and save the world... but not everyone is on your side.

This is a thrilling game of deception, played in 3 rounds of 5 minutes each. Survivors must find out who was responsible for the explosion, what artifact caused the damage and where it all took place. However, there are Infiltrators among the group trying to lead you astray...

Each turn you must play an Action Card and a Timeline Card. Timeline cards progress towards the explosion, and the Survivors are trying to divert away from this conclusion. Some Action cards allow you to manipulate time (freeze, accelerate or travel backwards) depending on your role in the game. Other Action cards allow you to find a Clue or Expose another player in the game.

At the end of each 5 minute round the group must vote on who is responsible. The Survivors need to uncover all 3 culprits within the 3x 5 minutes rounds and before the timeline reaches the explosion, otherwise the Infiltrators win.

Check Continuum.Game on instagram for tips and hints!

Produced by Night Fox Games

—description from the designer

Sort your cards by color. Go "Hiumi" with a set of 4 cards matching the latest opponent's discarded colors.

On your turn, you will play, move, and discard 1 card. Then, you will draw two cards to refill your hand. Continue playing until someone goes "Hiumi" or all hands depleted.

RAID! is a skirmish scale model agnostic wargame within the World War Apocalypse setting.

Players command a team of 5 raiders scouring the post-apocalyptic remains of Mexico City.

The city was recently the site of a climatic battle between the forces of Humanity and the forces of Apocalypse, which number 15 separate factions, which all vied for control over the Veil Gate opened within the city. The gate was closed, sealing the Veil off from this region, and in its wake the city was left devastated.

Now teams of raiders embark into the Coyote Exclusion Zone, a region around the city declared dangerous by the Nu Nations, so that they might find relics of the battle, new gear for their team, and to collect bounties on the contracts the Nu Nations issue. Will they prevail, and become Icons of the Apocalypse? Or will they perish in the Exclusion Zone as they search for gear and glory?

Five raiders enter the zone,
They work together, or else die alone.

Narrative Focused Play

     Command a team of 5 raiders
     Earn gear points to upgrade your weapons and equipment
     Sustain permanent wounds which affect your raiders
     Install bionics to recover from wounds
     Find Relics to augment your capabilities
     Level up your raiders and learn new skills
     Use the Contract generator to randomly determine your mission, or make up your own!


Boundless Stories

     Raid! is filled with full color art and concept sketches to help immerse you in the world
     There are 15 separate and detailed factions to choose from
     8 Short-Stories to read


The game is played in 5 rounds. 
During a round players alternate activating one character each, starting with the player who deployed second. 
During a round players cannot activate the same character more than once. 
The player who finishes activating their characters first in a round gets a +1 Initiative. 
In all but the first round, at the beginning of the round, players roll 1d6 and add any Initiative they have accrued from the previous round to their roll. The highest roller goes first

boardgame: Invincible: The Card Game – New Recruits
description:
An expansion for Invincible which includes two new heroes (Shrinking Rae and Black Samson), a new villain (The Mauler Twins), new player cards, a new city, and more.

Included in Invincible: The Card Game (Kickstarter Edition), later released separately for the retail edition.

The Horrible Horseman has defeated the gargoyles that defended Finale Fortress and thrown everything into chaos. A new army of gargoyles has been birthed from the Fire Demon to retake the castle and protect it from future attacks. These gargoyles have to save the kingdom!

Before facing the Horseman, you must navigate a tricky terrain packed with loathsome landmarks and crazy characters. To succeed, you'll have to do everything from poking a cyclops in the eye to mediating a divorce.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Don't Starve: Spiel Essen 2025 Promo Card
description: This promo card was offered during the Spiel Essen festival in 2025. It allows you to set one die you rolled to any face.

boardgame: Bag of Dungeon: Legends 2 – Adventure Pack
description:
Bag of Dungeon 2: Relics – a 4-in-1 adventure pack.

Paths of the Ancients
Journey back in time to the darkest days of the realm, ruled by the monstrous First Guardians. Steal the egg of Trantor the Spider Queen, and bring it to the present. A full-game scenario for any BoD game.

The Lost Dungeons
Go it alone on five solo missions into the newly discovered lost dungeons to steal the powerful Elemental Crystals. A standalone adventure – no BoD game needed.

New characters and abilities
Three new adventurers ready to vanquish evil from the lands: Ash the Half-Dragon Prince, Brumbleroot the mystic forest dweller, and Volos the spirit warrior.

PLUS: Introducing skills and destinies – upskill your BoD characters and choose their fate! And more! New items, new monsters and an optional 3-route Start tile. Play them all with any BoD game!

Includes 5 real* elemental crystals**
 * Powers have been deactivated for safety reasons.
 ** Crystals may not contain any actual crystal.

—description from the publisher

Greedy dwarves dig, collect, and sabotage—only one will become the richest dwarf! 
Take on the role of a dwarf: dig tunnels, collect treasures, and sabotage your fellow players with clever moves.
Build your deck, secure powerful actions, and exchange found ore for valuable jewels.
Fill in your opponents' tunnels and secretly grab their valuables—anything goes in the competition for the greatest wealth.
Who will leave the mine as the richest dwarf?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Void Fungus Enemy Pack
description:
Carried on a sickly sweet wind from the Void, clouds of glowing spores drift from world to world. Settling in areas tainted by Dark Stone deposits, twisted Void Fungus begin to sprout, extending a thick web of white mycelium roots, covering everything in their path, as the deadly fungus spreads.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to battle through clutches of Void Fungus and their deadly spores. Included are 8 Void Fungus models, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, counters for Mycelium markers, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add this spreading Void hazard to your games of Game: Shadows of Brimstone.
 —description from the publisher

The aim of this dynamic card-dice-board-game is to collect as many gnumie points as possible. To do this, you exchange knobble points, collect pets, dinofraurians and friends, and play freely flying particles.

The secret star is the Häh, a small, potato-like pet that is cute but doesn't understand anything. When it's drawn, it steals cards and must be quickly recaptured.

Sounds crazy? That's exactly what makes it so good fun for all: kids, teens and adults of all age!

The game ends when all 8 Groovy Grovers (tree cards) have been found. Since all players are engaged with action at any time, the tide can turn at any moment and the game remains exciting for all players until the very end.

The length of the game can be reduced from 50 to 20 minutes by halving the deck, but leaving all tree cards in it.

—description from the publisher

A drug deal was planned, but now the courier is missing.

As the second-in-command, try to find out what happened to Jason and, more importantly, the drugs.

This case is a promotional prequel to Masters of Crime: Hollywood.

Wastelanders Unite is a cooperative trick-taking and community deck-building game of survival, strategy, and story. Players work together to complete missions, manage scarce resources, and face constant peril in a harsh, retro-themed wasteland where every card played—and every decision—matters.

You play as a group of desperate destitutes — a bunch of nobodies trying not to become dead bodies. The story follows your squad as they fight to bring a little hope to an unforgiving wasteland.

—description from the designer

Hidden deep within the ancient forests of Nightflower Island, Mokoko Village is a magical haven where quirky villagers, playful musicians, mysterious masked Doomlings, and powerful spirits live side by side in one of Enderas’s most ancient and delightful towns.

The Mokoko Village expansion is a 40-card expansion that adds 37 new Traits, 2 new Ages, and 1 new Catastrophe to your Doomlings game.

It adds cards that build upon the mechanics from the base game, making it a perfect expansion for both new and experienced players.


     Mokoko Village also comes with 1 Mystery Holofoil Collectible Card from the Mokoko Village Collection. For a limited time, this will be a First Edition print.

boardgame: Zombicide: White Death – Lacuna Coil Character Pack
description:
Life on the road is rough. Lacuna Coil had seen it all, or so they thought. Now, they're playing for the toughest crowd ever: the Undead.

Bring the members of Lacuna Coil to White Death with this character pack!

The Zombicide Lacuna Coil Character pack is a CMON Convention Exclusive set, available at CMON Expo Bangkok 2024. However, the Survivors it contains can also be found in the Frozen Fortress set.

Two wooden shrimps meeples and respective cards to be added to the base game.

The shrimp meeples are larger than all the other game pieces that need to be placed on the grill, and therefore present a greater challenge for all players.

Crossover promo card from the publishers new game Roblins

Effect: Reduces temporarily the number of hand cards of an opponent.

A Formula 1 game where you use a standard deck playing cards to race around iconic Grand Prix's while also upgrading your racing car during the race.

The playing cards are used to identify the speed of the cars when going round the track which has speed limitations at corners, I.e. 2-10 would be the numbers indicated, Whereas J, Q, K and A will have multiple speed options and the joker would be any number up to 10 but without a suit associated to it.

The played cards will also allow the player to upgrade their car by using either the number or suit played then crossing this symbol on their car (player board) which will have bonuses during the race when filling in rows or zones.

The type of bonuses will include, hand size, one time boosts, corner speeds, zone speeds, track ability (indicated on the track map and driver ability (indicated on driver cards)

Both the corner and zone speeds allow the player to increase these numbers for each matching suit associated to the track corner or track zone.

The idea of the game is to initially provide a print and play which will include the track, player boards (car upgrades) and driver cards (each with a unique ability) and to use any playing deck of cards.

Play a Card. Place a Pin. Make a Sequence! The easy-to-learn fun-to-play classic that's been a best-seller for over 35 years.

A cut-down, two-player version of Sequence with one deck of cards and a smaller playfield, stored in a metal tin.
family: Versions & Editions: Two-Player Versions of More-Player Games

description:
Viaduct is a strategic trick-taking card game for 2–5 players where players contribute material cards to one of several communal viaduct projects with the aim of being the best builder in town!

The player who plays the last card into the trick constructs the viaduct and is rewarded by the foreman! But be careful, overshoot the requirements of the viaduct and you’ll be penalised and forced to take negative points instead.

—description from the designer

Soggy biscuit is a card game where players fight to finish first or consume the creamy consequences.

Loosely based on the (maybe) urban legend of the same name.

Get rid of your cards and free up your hands to take care of business, filling up your biscuit with creamy tokens.

Take every chance to untuck from your role as a regal queen to a horny bro who is ready to get down and dirty.

Find the Cream Connoisseurs hidden amongst the Fast Finishers and ensure they don't get their just desserts.

—description from the back of the box

boardgame: Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War at Sea – the War of the Pacific (Part 2) Expansion Pack
description:
This Dawn of Iron battle pack expansion concludes the epic voyage of the venerable monitor Huáscar, including the famous ironclad battle of Angamos on 8 October 1879. This is a super-sized pack includes seven new historical scenarios and all of the ships you'll need to tell the dramatic conclusion of the conflict.

Inside you will find:

     History & Maps of the War (22pgs)
     New Mission and Scenario rules (1pg)
     7x Historical Scenario
     23x Ship Cards (Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
     9x Fortification Cards (Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
     9x New Upgrade Cards (A4 and US Letter paper sizes)


About Dawn of Iron Expansion Packs
This is an expansion for Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War at Sea. 
Inside you will find some history and maps about a famous naval event during the American Civil War, some scenarios for you to recreate it, and ship cards that you can use with this pack and your other games. 
You will need a copy of Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War At Sea, 1861-65 to fully use the contents of this pack.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War at Sea – the War of the Pacific (Part 1) Expansion Pack
description:
This Dawn of Iron battle pack expansion takes you just beyond the American Civil War into a historically adjacent conflict in South America involving ironclads and wooden ships. The War of the Pacific primarily involved Peru and Chile as they battled for naval supremacy over valuable guano/nitrate reserves along the South American western coast lines. Both nations armed their navies with the latest ironclad designs from British shipyards, which famously clashed on the Peruvian and Chilean waters from the 1879 Battle of Iquiqui and beyond.

This is a super-sized pack, packed with eight (8!) historical scenarios, two new missions (which are also applicable for your ACW games) and all of the ships you'll need to tell the story of the first half of the conflict. A second expansion covering the finale of the naval war at Angamos is forthcoming, so stay tuned!

Inside you will find:

     History & Maps of the War (16pgs)
     New Mission and Scenario rules (2pg)
     8x Historical Scenario
     2x General Play Mission (inspired by the battles)
     23x Ship Cards (12x Chilean, 9x Peruvian, and 2x British. Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
     12x Fortification Cards (Contains formats for A4 and US Letter paper sizes.)
     22x New Upgrade Cards (A4 and US Letter paper sizes)


About Dawn of Iron Expansion Packs

This is an expansion for Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War at Sea. Inside you will find some history and maps about a famous naval event during the American Civil War, some scenarios for you to recreate it, and ship cards that you can use with this pack and your other games. You will need a copy of Dawn of Iron: The American Civil War At Sea, 1861-65 to fully use the contents of this pack.

—description from the publisher

Lunar Rush: Innovations brings bold new twists to the game, expanding the original game’s strategic options and making every decision more exciting than ever. This major expansion introduces asymmetric corporations, each with unique abilities and play styles that will shape how you bid for routes, manage resources, and compete for profits. No two corporations play the same, meaning every game will feel fresh and full of new challenges.

For those times when four players just aren’t enough, Innovations also adds a fifth player, making it easier than ever to bring more people into the race for lunar dominance. On top of that, the expansion introduces random events, awards, and milestones—new layers of depth that reward clever planning and quick adaptation.

With Innovations, every session of Lunar Rush will feel dynamic and unpredictable, pushing you to rethink your old strategies and adapt to new opportunities. Whether you’re a seasoned player or just getting started, this expansion offers exciting new ways to experience the game.

—description from the publisher

An evolved memory game where you have to find the three steps of the most famed scenes in the movie L'allenatore nel pallone. The large, full color cards depict movie frames with the balloons of the best puns in the films.
Each player turns three cards face up each turn. If they compose a scene, he takes them scoring 3 points and plays again. If not, he covers them again and his turn finished. Since the first two cards could belong to different scenes, to make the third turn meaningful there are secondary goals. The first time two, and not three, cards from a given scene are revealed, the player gets a scene card worth two points. A 3 points bonus card is awarded the first time three cards with beginnings are showed, the first time three middles are shown, the first time three endings are shown and the first time that a beginning, a middle scene and an ending from three different scenes are shown.
Published by Ravensburger on the occasion of the release of the remastered movie.

Rated 14+ for a couple of 4-letter words, as far as difficulty is concerned it is actually playable from 7 years up.

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Ace & Newgate
description:
Introducing the all-new Ace & Newgate Deck!

The Whitebeard Pirates join the battle with brand-new card effects!
The brand-new leader card: Ace & Newgate has arrived!

Let’s lead the battle with “Whitebeard Pirates” cards!
“Whitebeard Pirates” enter the fray with brand-new abilities!

Unleash their full potential by pairing them with the leader cards!
Each set comes with 1 pack of bonus cards!
Come and get your hands on these specially designed cards!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Shanks
description:
The ideal deck to start with for Red-Haired Pirates fans!

Everything you need to start playing the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
This set lets you start playing the game using the official rules!
Use your leader's ability to fend off attacks and control the pace of battles!

Your Leader Shanks can reduce the power of your opponent's cards!
Join forces with powerful Red-Haired Pirates Characters and go for victory!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Jewelry Bonney
description:
The ideal deck to start with for supernovas fans!

Everything you need to start playing the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
This set lets you start playing the game using the official rules!
Break through enemy lines with your leader's skill!

Your Leader Jewelry Bonney can rest your opponent's cards! Strike at their weak points and press the attack!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Buggy
description:
The ideal deck to start with for Cross Guild fans!

Everything you need to start playing the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
This set lets you start playing the game using the official rules!
Play powerful characters for low costs and dominate the field!

Your Leader Buggy lets you play high-cost Characters for much less! Use your powerful Cross Guild Characters to crush your opponent!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Monkey.D.Luffy
description:
The ideal deck to start with for Straw Hat Crew fans!

Everything you need to start playing the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
This set lets you start playing the game using the official rules!
Bring out high-cost Characters fast!

Your Leader Monkey.D.Luffy ramps up your DON!!
cards, letting you play more high-cost Characters!
Keep up the attacks and secure the advantage!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Marshall.D.Teach
description:
The ideal deck to start with for Blackbeard Pirates fans!

Everything you need to start playing the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
This set lets you start playing the game using the official rules!
Shut down on play effects and seize control of the battlefield!

Your Leader Marshall.D.Teach negates your opponent's On Play effects!
Pair that with powerful removal and high-power characters to take over the field!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Starter Deck – Yamato
description:
The ideal deck to start with for Land of Wano fans!

Everything you need to start playing the ONE PIECE CARD GAME!
This set lets you start playing the game using the official rules!
Attack your opponent's life cards using a leader with Double Attack!

Go all out with your ultra-aggressive Leader Yamato!
Your Character's also have effects that activate with DON!! cards to power them up even more!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: One Piece Card Game: Legacy of the Master
description:
A booster pack themed around deep bonds is now available!
Experience thrilling battles with leader cards featuring exciting new strategies!
The "Dark King" Rayleigh joins the battle!

Unleash your Haki and claim victory with brand-new tactics!
The new "Twin Wings" leader cards are here!

Leader card effects that expand your tactical options are not to be missed!
Don’t miss the next level of power-ups!

Keep the pressure on with a powerful lineup!

—description from the publisher

A deluxe pack with guaranteed SR or higher!

In PRB-02, every pack contains at least one SR card or better!
Even new players can upgrade their decks with powerful cards.
Great for enhancing the Starter Decks released on June 28, 2025!

Fan-favorite cards return!

Some returning cards now feature new Alt-Art illustrations.

This Premium Booster includes loads of new cards!

Among the new cards, you'll find many with alternate illustrations known as "Alt-Art versions".

30 different DON!! Cards in the set!

This set contains a huge selection of DON!! cards featuring different characters!
Be sure to customise your deck with your favourites!

Even DON!! Cards have Alt-Art versions!

This Premium Booster even features a number of Alt-Art versions of DON!! cards—including Super Alt-Art versions!

Foil-Stamped Event Alt-Art Cards included!

Event Cards are now available as full-art foil-stamped Alt-Art —a must-have for collectors.

Features SP Cards with stunning designs!

SP cards have special designs that look like they're about to jump off the card!
Be sure to add some of these dynamic cards to your collection!

The Straw Hat Crew's chef Sanji makes his Super Alt-Art debut! Don't miss this special one-of-a-kind design.

An extremely rare space-themed board game released exclusively in Greece during the 1960s. It can be played from 2–4 players. Each player selects a "pawn" and places it on a chosen base before a “navigator” is elected. The navigator positions a special magnetic piece on the board’s central magnetic field by slightly turning the main knob.

The first player spins the numbered wheel and moves their piece accordingly in a circular direction toward the opposite base. If a player lands on “safety rays,” they may change direction or move downward; landing on “death rays” sends them back to their starting point.

After all players have taken their turn, the navigator uses a compass pointer to rotate the central knob, activating magnets that can move one or more pieces around the board. A secondary magnet may also activate, affecting other players.

Players must remember:
(a) anyone pulled by the navigator’s magnet must follow and then return to the last safe zone, and
(b) anyone caught in the second magnetic field must return to their base.

The winner is the first player to complete a full circuit and reach the base opposite their starting position.

—user summary

This card was exclusive given when you bought the game (or made a crowdfunding pickup) at the SPIEL '25 fair in Essen.

The QR code on the card says:

Promo Goblin:
"When you play the Promo Goblin, set it and opposing unit of your choice aside. The two play cards together and do not participate in the next round. At the end of the round, return the opposing Goblin to where you got it and take the Promo Goblin back to your hand (and you can play it again if you pay its gold cost again). Note that Ogres are not Goblins and are too stupid to play cards."

Step into a world where art defies gravity and stone becomes dream. Shape forms, balance light and nature, and bring to life the visionary style that redefined architecture.

Antoni Gaudí was a visionary Catalan architect born in 1852, renowned for his imaginative and distinctive style that blended Gothic and Art Nouveau influences. Players will travel through Gaudí's life and work, earning Victory Points in the three pillars that shaped his works: Nature, Catalonia, and Religion. To succeed, players must keep their progress balanced—only their two lowest VP scores among the three pillars will count toward final scoring. Points are gained by playing cards and placing 'Trencadís' tiles on their personal boards. 'Trencadís' is a Catalan word that means “a lot of little broken things”. While playing, you’ll discover some of these intricate details depicted on the cards and experience the creative joy of arranging your own trencadís patterns on your personal board. ).
