Each pillar awards VP differently: Nature points are mainly earned through Nature cards, Catalonia points are mostly scored at the end of each round, and Religion points are primarily tallied at the end of the game.
 The game unfolds over three rounds. In each round, players take turns moving around the central Chandelier on the main board, carefully choosing where to stop in order to perform key actions—drawing cards and collecting various types of Trencadís tiles. These resources are then used to play cards and progress up the Chimneys on their personal boards. Each round concludes once all players have completed a full circuit around the Chandelier. At the end of the third round, the player with the highest and most balanced combination of VP will be declared the winner!

—description from the publisher

Biblioclash is a fast-paced Finnish tactical deck-building game where players become mages dueling inside a magical library using spells, artifacts, and minions. You win when your opponent’s Mage reaches zero health.

Each match begins with both players choosing a Mage and building a personal Library of nine piles. During the duel, Wisdom is used to obtain cards from the Library into the deck, which represents everything the Mage can do. Turn by turn, the Mage grows stronger, combining spells into powerful plays.

At the heart of the game is the Striking Phase, a dynamic back-and-forth duel of timing and power. When spells collide, players react in real time, trading bursts of magic until one side breaks through. It’s a contest of focus and skill — a true clash of mages.

Biblioclash combines TCG and deck-building in one game, bringing both systems together. It features constant interaction and a fast pace, with many game modes to keep the game feeling fresh.

—description from the publisher

You have a handful of bombs and they're all ticking down. In Tick Tock Tangle, chaos is mandatory and timing is everything. Finish first? You ran away too soon. Finish last? Boom. But finish second? You’re the hero who defuses the bomb just in time.

Tick Tock Tangle is an explosive climbing / shedding card game for 3–5 players where you want to shed out second. Players will be dealt a blind hand to begin with, and through their choices information may be revealed about their hand at the detriment of the speed they defuse bombs. A careful balance of timing, deduction and risk taking is required to best your fellow defusers.

—description from the designer

Letter Up is a tactical word game where players compete to claim territory.

In Letter Up, the goal is to form clever words to claim territories by reaching the edges of the play area. Place words, reach the border, stack letters, reclaim territories… the choice is yours!

In duel mode, the first player to place all six of their tokens wins the game.
In solo mode, try to achieve the highest possible score.

-description from publisher

Weaving has played an important role in the development of many cultures. The colors and patterns created on the loom are closely tied to heritage, storytelling, and tradition.

In Νήματα ("Nimata"), players collect threads and weave patterns on their personal looms. Each round, you must decide which threads to take, which patterns to complete, and how to maximize your Victory Points. Careful placement of colors and planning ahead is essential—too many threads of the same color in one direction may cause you to lose points.

The game ends after four rounds. The player with the highest total of Victory Points, having crafted the most harmonious and impressive designs, wins.

Southeast Asia's archaelogical sites like Gunung Padang and Sa Hunh reveal lost civilizations, continually reshaping our understanding of the ancient world.

Now, can you imagine being the one unearthing the remains of a long forgotten culture, objects that have been buried for thousands of years? Imagine yourself leading a team of researchers (archaeologists, historians, and specialists) working together to decipher the mysteries of a site never before unearthed. Every find, from a small ceramic shard to a monumental structure, could provide crucial clues about how these ancient peoples lived and were organized. But the work doesn’t stop with excavation: you must document your discoveries, reconstruct their past, and gain recognition from the scientific community by publishing your findings in the most prominent scientific journals and amaze visitors by showcasing the unearthed artifacts in a museum.

The game is played both during the present and the past. You will take on the role of a team of archeologists in the present who are trying to decipher the secrets of that culture, but you will also play as the peoples of these ancient groups in the past, making the history that will be studied later. The actions players take collectively in the past will affect the present, by creating new breakthrough discoveries, changing the ruins available to excavate, and defining the strengths of each aspect of that civilization. By writing theses about the ancients’ major feats, you will establish the historical significance of their achievements (represented as victory points).

The world is waiting for new explorers. Are you ready to uncover the past?

—description from the publisher

Cold War tensions run high as spies, double agents, and informants move unseen through the divided city. Trust no one, outwit and uncover your enemies in this game of hidden roles and deception for a large group of daring players. In this game set in the German capital during the Cold War, you'll assume the identity of a member of the CIA or the KGB. Throughout the game, you'll use various powers to try to identify your fellow agents and hinder your rivals' investigations and communications. However, every action counts, and the signals you send to your allies may be misinterpreted.

—description from the publisher

Chaos: The Card Game is a whirlwind of madness, strategy, and pure chaos. In a world ruled by a divine Cat, four absurd factions battle for glory and the ultimate prize: the Forbidden Fruit. Psycho Elves, Alluring Sirens, Bureaucratic Demons, and Thirsty Vampires clash in a ridiculous, glorious war where nothing is sacred and mercy simply does not exist. Victory demands cunning, timing, and a knack for embracing the chaos. Winning the side rows allows you to unleash the central card’s Chaos Value, creating moments of shocking reversals and brilliant strategy. Claim the Forbidden Fruit, taste it, and the first to fully enjoy it becomes the undisputed King of Chaos.

Chaos: The Card Game is mainly about a crazy army of absurd creatures fighting over the Forbidden Fruit, which grants unlimited power. Each character has a Power value), some a Chaos value, and a special ability or description. Each round consists of 5 phases: Deployment, Reveal, Battle, Resolution, and Recruitment.

Each player picks a faction, draws five cards, and sets three face-down in their line-up across three rows. Then the chaos begins: reveal cards one by one, triggering unpredictable effects and hilarious combos that can completely turn the tide. When all cards are revealed, the “Battle Phase” erupts, fists fly, creatures clash, and the highest values win unless the card’s hidden Chaos Value flips the script. At the end of every round, players "recruit" new cards.  If a player has less than 3 cards at this point, the game ends and the player with the fruit begins.

—description from the publisher

7th Seal is a solo dice management boss battle-style game in which you will face the judgement of the end times. Your goal is to open the seals of a scroll and face the wrath of a supreme being who has unleashed an avalanche of furious beasts and demons that will attempt to wipe out humanity. Use your dice wisely and prove that you can convince the great maker to give humanity another chance.

—description from the publisher

Includes:
4x Main Phase Cards
4x Randomizer Cards
4x Mythic Cards
1x How To Use Card

boardgame: The Winter Mission: A Break System Gamebook
description:
The math says no. The season says yes.

An impossible opportunity lures you from the streets of Toronto to the treacherous peaks of Switzerland in mid-December. You can’t resist the target — something you thought was lost forever — but traps lie in wait. The enemy knows you’re coming.

Surviving alone may be possible, but it would take a miracle. Success demands more than skill and luck. You’re going to need some allies.

The Winter Mission marks the conclusion to the Break system series of gamebooks. With branching choices, dice-based challenges, and new crew management, this adventure will test every lesson you’ve learned, with every move you make.

The dead of winter. The heart of Helvetia.

A holiday heist to remember.

—description from the designer

With "Ear Candy" it’s time for your songs to take center stage.

This first expansion for "Rock Hard: 1977" lets you use your Life Experiences to write 45 RPM Singles in various styles.

Promoting these Singles gets them played on the radio and moves you up the Hit List song charts, giving you an additional way to gain Fame.

The expansion includes two new characters, two new managers, two new types of Candy, and additional cards for every deck.

Do you have what it takes to be number one?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Kampf gegen das Bünzlitum: Familien Edition
description:
The family-friendly version of "Kampf gegen das Bünzlitum" . This version is perfect for humorous families and can be combined with the original game as well as all expansions. The game is recommended for ages 10 and up and can be played with 3–10 players.

Gameplay:
- Each player draws 8 white answer cards.
- The round boss draws a card from the question deck and reads it out loud.

- Each player selects their funniest answer card and hands it to the round boss face down.

- The round boss reads all answers aloud and picks their favorite.

- The winner receives the red question card as a trophy.

- All players draw back up to 8 cards.

- The round boss passes to the next player clockwise.

What is a Deck Hand game? It is an 18 card game that is used in conjunction with a standard Poker deck.

Yeehaw! Get ready to play possum, round-up poker hands, and throw some outlaws in the hoosegow. In Pogo Possum Posse Poker, (2-4 players) take on the role of bounty hunters working alongside the famous Sheriff Wyatt O. Possum and his trusty pogo stick, Betsy. For greenhorns and veterans alike, players will use a unique card skipping mechanic to lasso up some playing cards to make specific poker hands, while they scout out and capture gangs of lawbreaking varmints in the Wild West. Combined with a standard deck of cards, your family is fixin’ to have a hootin', hollerin', and growlin’ good time, and see why Pogo Possum Posse Poker puts the ‘wild’ in the Wild Wild West.

The goal of Pogo Possum Posse Poker is to be the bounty hunter at the end of the game with the most money. On your turn, you choose one of three actions:

1. Posse up!: Gather cards to add to your hand. You will automatically hop one space forward around the Okey Dokey Corral (because you're on pogo sticks!). From there, you'll have the option of collecting all the cards at that location or discarding a card from your hand to advance another space. You can continue to discard cards and advance until you choose to stop and pick up cards.

2. Scout an outlaw: Track and get the bead on outlaws. By playing the specific poker hand in cards from your hand, you can scout outlaws from the Badlands, and take them to your play area. Each outlaw will give you a special power that will let you use some cards as wild cards, collect extra cards, or move extra spaces! You can have up to three outlaws in front of you at any given time.

3. Jail an outlaw: Play larger poker hands on the outlaws you've tracked to flip them and put them in jail! You will lose that outlaw's special power, but gain the cash bounty for big points at the end of the game. Every jailed outlaw also increases your hand limit by +1, so you can get back on your horse and posse up again!

When the outlaw deck is empty, all players get one more turn to play poker hands and jail characters they have tracked. Then you'll count up your score. Players score the cash bounty on all jailed outlaws, a cash bonus for outlaws from the same gang, and one point for each outlaw you still have scouted. The player who collected the most reward money is the rootin'est, tootin'est possum in the wild, wild West!

Bluffin’ for Booty is a 2-3 player micro game designed to be accessible for everyone to play.

2-Player Rules:
Place the Captain’s coin between both players, leaving the other two coins in the bag. The first player secretly draws one of the two remaining coins and places the purse with the final coin in front of their opponent. The first player then declares either “I’ve got the Booty!” or “You’ve got the Booty!” This statement may be true or a bluff. The other player then chooses to keep the coin in the purse or swap it with the first player’s coin. The player who ends up with the Booty wins the round and takes the Captain’s coin. Switch who goes first and play again. Continue until one player wins two rounds in a row, winning the game.

3 Player Rules:
Each player secretly draws a coin at random from the purse. The player who draws the Captain’s coin reveals their coin and announces that they are the Captain. The other two players now try to convince the Captain that they have the Booty by declaring, “I’ve got the Booty!” If the Captain correctly selects the holder of the Booty coin, both the Captain and the Booty-holder win the game together. But if the Captain chooses the Kraken, the Kraken has defeated the Captain and wins the game alone.

Note about accessibility:
We believe games should be for everyone. Bluffin’ for Booty was designed with accessibility and inclusivity in mind: the backs of the coins feature distinct textures so that visually impaired players can participate fully.

—description from the publisher
Draw six words on a rotating disk. Try to understand other people's drawings to get points. The better you draw, the more points you'll get.

To enter the secret society you have to guess a word, but not everyone is in favor, some will be against and will give false clues
The novice must figure out the secret word. Among the players is a dissenting player who must give incorrect clues, an undecided player who must figure out the correct word on the second try, and a pro player who wants the player to figure it out on the first try.
If the novice guesses correctly on the first try, all players must bet on who is the favorable player. If he guesses correctly on the second attempt, all players will have to bet on who is undecided and if he doesn't guess correctly, all players will have to indicate who among them is the opposite.
For each correct role, a point will be awarded; for each word found on the first try, the novice will earn a point. The first person to reach 9 points wins.
100 new cards that expand the Smart10 base game.
New questions about the first 10 years of the millenium.
New cards for Smart 10, this time about the seventies.

boardgame: Fire at Will: An Expansion for Laserblade
description:
Fire at Will is a PDF expansion for Laserblade, the fast-paced science fiction title by Echidna Games.

This short expansion gives players a new list of abilities that can be incorporated seamlessly into their games of Laserblade.

We believe that Fire at Will completes the list of abilites from the original Laserblade and gives players just about everything they need to re-enact battles from almost any science fiction franchise, including the addition of small vehicles like speeder bikes and single-seat walkers.

So grab your miniatures and give Fire at Will a try!

—description from the publisher

What is a Deck Hand game? It is an 18 card game that is used in conjunction with a standard Poker deck.

London. Berlin. Washington. Moscow. Become a director of agent operations in Spymaster One by dispatching spies and scoring points for infiltrated cities.

In this competitive lane-battler, 2-4 players are using standard playing cards as spies to activate city effects, make powerful combos, and collect intel for a city’s scoring condition.

On a turn, players will:


     Dispatch a spy (playing card) from their hand to a city, matching the mission parameters. 
     Activate the city’s effect, transferring spies and intel to create powerful combos.
     Complete a mission if possible by placing the last spy into a city, taking some spies and the city card for its end-game scoring condition. 
     Deploy a recruit from the academy to a city. 
     Replenish their hand with spies from the standard deck.


The game ends when the city or spy (standard) deck is depleted. Players choose two city cards to score with the spies they’ve acquired. The player with the most points wins.

Can you outwit your opponents and become the number one spymaster?

After a tumultuous journey through the clouds, the Asgarthans reach their destination. Asty is a resplendent floating city and home to the Reka people, as well as the Naos, an immense world tree whose canopy overlooks the city's tallest buildings. As the two peoples become acquainted, a new alliance is born.

Seeds of Unity tells the story of the meeting between the Asgarthans and the Reka: a vibrant celebration full of color and optimism where the focus is on exchange, the discovery of the other people and their city, and the laying of solid foundations. But, like any civilization, the Reka have their complexities.

The Naos, a majestic World Tree grows at the heart of the city and supplies it with resources and energy. The Reka owe their advanced technology and prosperity to the exploitation of this World Tree and the governance of the Hexarchs, a six-member oligarchy. This meeting is a story of celebrations, exchanges, and hope, but also one of negotiations — a moment when doubts, tensions, and difficult choices begin to emerge.

Seeds of Unity adds 3 new starter decks with:

    New heroes: Della & Bolt (Axiom), Tuurun & Benih (Muna), Matz & Hive (Ordis) 
    New mechanics: Manaseeds, Gifts, In Contact
    New card type: Exalted  (the Hexarchs)
    Updated deckbuilding rules


—description from the publisher

Pound of Hounds is a comedic and cunning, canine themed dedicated deck, strategic party game with over 50 unique hand-drawn doggo's to Draw from.

Strategise, collect cards and use your abilities to gather your dogs and finally "claim your Pound of Hounds over friend and foes alike".

Throughout the game players will discard cards from their hand to use the cards ability (including picking up more cards, stealing cards from other players hand, discarding other players hands, swapping hands, ect..) or trade in 3 cards of the same class (indicated by the cards colour and icon in the top left corner) for an Alpha card the same class.

To win the game players must collect either 3,5 or 7 Alpha cards depending on the number of players in the game.

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Beastro, where mythical beast chefs compete to make the best dishes!  Beastro is a team-based trick-taking game with hidden roles where you have to figure out who is in your team with and outsmart the others, collecting as many tricks as possible for your team.

Each round you: (1) wager to become the Head Chef and select your Secret Chef teammate, (2) Cook crazy dishes in order to win the most tricks for your team, (3) Play special cards to ruin dishes and get bonuses at unique Restaurants!

Every game you will play as of the mythical Chef and use their beastly power only once to turn the odds in your favour.

Beastro is played over a series of three round and the player with most point at the end will be the winner.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
On the Way! 86 is the second major expansion for the solitaire Cold War tactical combat RPG On the Way!, and REQUIRES OWNERSHIP OF THE ORIGINAL GAME, but does not require ownership of any other expansion. While the original game postulates the hypothetical outbreak of WWIII between NATO and the Warsaw Pact to occur in the winter of 1968-69, this expansion is set in the late winter and early spring of 1986, offering players the chance to command brand new era-appropriate tanks and operate sophisticated new weapons systems (i.e. laser rangefinders, thermal imagers, anti-tank guided missiles, tandem warheads, and explosive reactive armor). Additionally, like the first expansion, the scope of the game has been significantly expanded beyond the so-called “Fulda Gap”, and includes the entire East-West German and German-Czechoslovakian border, from the Northern German Plain, to the German Central Uplands, South Scarplands, and Alpine Forelands.

Inspired by the legendary Avalon Hill classic, Patton’s Best, the On the Way! 86 expansion for On the Way! intends to capture much of the outstanding realism, abstraction, and excellent campaign of Patton’s Best in its own unique way, making it a fresh and original game. A few select differences and additions from the original Patton’s Best system are: Rules for dawn, dusk, and night combat, as well as night vision devices (infrared, thermal, etc.) and other Cold War gadgets like advanced two-plane stabilizers and auxiliary driver and/or gunner controls; a comprehensive selection of period-appropriate ammunition, including APFSDS (Armor Piercing Fin Stabilized Discarding Sabot) and ATGM’s (Anti-Tank Guided Missiles); nineteen (vice the original 4 of the base game!) playable tanks representing the most common types that would be available to each respective nation and unit in early 1986 (the M60A3 TTS, M1 Abrams, and M1IP Abrams (US), Leopard 1A4 and Leopard 2A2 (West Germany), Leopard 1 (BE) (Belgium), Leopard 1V (Netherlands), AMX-30B2 (France), Chieftain Mk.9 and Challenger (Britain), T-55AM (East Germany and Poland), T-72 (East Germany), T-72M (Czechoslovakia), and T-62, T-64B, T-64BV, T-72A1, T-80B, and T-80BV (USSR)); a detailed tank damage system, accounting for non-KO penetrations, hits by kinetic energy and chemical energy projectiles, as well as detailed crew wounds and a brand new bail-out minigame to resolve the abandoning of your tank turn-by-turn; condensed and specific objectives with particular mission end conditions to specifically avoid mental fatigue and cut out tedium; and much, much more! Additionally, the game has been intentionally designed with modularity in mind in order to account for potential expansions set in later decades (such as this second expansion, On the Way! 86) with the introduction of advanced systems like thermal technology and explosive reactive armor (ERA), as well as support for WWII campaigns.

Over a period not exceeding more than a month and a half (when it is assumed that the war will either be halted by ceasefire or escalate into a global nuclear war of heinous “mutually assured destruction”, depending on the actual course of your campaign, which changes with each playthrough), your tank commander and his crew will need to fight tactically and soundly to ensure the survival of both themselves and the tank. While your crew fights engagements as they appear to them through their hatches and periscopes, the rest of the war rages around them (represented as a dynamically changing “victory perception” with weekly campaign events), with your outfit’s successes and/or failures potentially having a small but palpable impact on the result of the campaign, potentially earning your nation an early victory, or perhaps a terrible defeat. Combat missions and their associated objectives, as well as enemy units, weather, the time of day, and so on, are all generated through their respective generation tables. Combat missions are resolved on a week-by-week basis, with up to seven combat missions occurring per week until the end of the campaign. In that time, you and your crew may increase their ratings (an abstraction of veterancy – their skills as combat soldiers), suffer casualties using detailed wound generation tables, gain nation-appropriate decorations and promotions, suffer from fatigue, and more. Ultimately, though, surviving the first dramatic, fast-paced, violent weeks of WWIII’s conventional ground war of maneuver is the main goal of your tank commander; after the war on the ground comes to a halt, the rest is out of your hands.

Overall, On the Way! 86 includes two new campaigns: “North” and “Center”; ten playable nations (Belgium, Britain, Czechoslovakia, East Germany, France, the Netherlands, Poland, the Soviet Union, the United States, and West Germany) along with exhaustively-researched tank crew ranks, nearly 50 nation-specific awards and decorations, and twenty new historical nation-specific units to join (the Belgian 16th Pantser Division; the British 1st and 4th Armoured Divisions; the French 5th Armoured Division; the Dutch 1st Division; the U.S. 3rd Armored Division and 3rd and 8th Infantry Divisions (Mechanized); the West German 3rd and 10th Panzer Divisions; the Czechoslovakian 1st Tank Division; the East German 7th and 9th Panzer Divisions, and 4th and 8th Motorized Schützen Divisions; the Polish 8th Mechanized Division; and the Soviet 20th Tank Division, and 7th, 15th, and 79th Guards Tank Divisions); nineteen playable tanks (AMX-30B2, Challenger, Chieftain Mk.9, Leopard 1A4, Leopard 1 (BE), Leopard 1V, Leopard 2A2, M60A3 TTS, M1 Abrams, M1IP Abrams, T-55AM, T-62, T-64B, T-64BV, T-72, T-72A1, T-72M, T-80B, and T-80BV); brand new mid-1980’s enemy unit types (spread over 8 front-and-back enemy unit cards), such as the M2 Bradley, BMP-2, and Jaguar Tank Destroyer; two new counter sheets; revised tables; a new combat calendar; a new combat mission track sheet; and a supplementary rulebook which clarifies the aforementioned changes and additions to the game.

The On the Way! 86 expansion for On the Way!, REQUIRING OWNERSHIP OF THE ORIGINAL GAME but not requiring ownership of any other expansion, requires two six-sided (d6) dice to play, and may be (but is not required) enhanced by taking notes and keeping a log on a separate sheet of paper, or digitally with any free word-like program, as your tank commander's story develops.

—description from the designer

One to Five is a high energy game that will have you laying down sets of cards from 1 to 5, (5 fives, 4 fours, 3 threes, etc). The first player to lay down all 5 sets wins. The deck of Action Cards determine what all players will do in each round. You might draw a card, play a set, then pass a card to the right. Or you might draw exactly the card you need but have to pass your whole hand to the left before you get a chance to play your completed set! Every game is different, exciting and unpredictable. One to Five will have your whole table laughing, wanting to play again and again.

—description from the publisher

The Travellers Deluxe Challenge Deck consist of 24 Challenge Cards, 6 Mission Cars, a narrative achievements card, three meeples, 6 counters, three crew cards, a divider card and tuck box. From Developer

boardgame: Ace of Spades: Call of the Zombie
description:
Legend says that Jack would go into the saloon every night in search of a little entertainment and something to wet his whistle. One day, he walked in on his old friend who owned the saloon in the process of watering down the whisky. This betrayal was so unforgivable that Jack killed the man on the spot, drowning him in a filthy watering hole.

But his rage was not quenched, and he vowed to master dark magic so he could bring the bartender back to life and kill him anew every night until his thirst for vengeance was satisfied. He has been at it for 723 nights, and he ain’t gotten tired of it yet. The moral of the story: never water down the customer’s whisky! Anyway, his wicked powers have led to his being proclaimed the Hellbilly Zombie Master, with his very own army of zombies ready to do his bidding. You are the only one who can put him and his putrefied horde of living dead in the grave.

The first expansion for Ace of Spades includes 15 new zombie enemy cards with 4 versions of the Hellbilly zombie master. In this expansion, the Enemies work slightly differently from how they do in the base game. When play them, cards in your Hand combinations will be "captured". This means that 1 (or 2) of the cards that damaged the Enemy will be linked to that particular guy and can never be used again. Enemies are zombies, so they never die. If you survive long enough, you will face a horde of undead.

—description from the publisher

FOUR NEW Alien Civilizations for Hyperspace, PLUS everything needed to go up to SIX players!

How do you like your alien species? Elegant and refined? Planktonic? Full machine? Lovecraftian? The four new Civilizations - Automata, Salps, Starspawn, Zepzeg - bring radically different approaches to gameplay.

—description from publisher
A roll and move game about Finnish-ugrian trek to punish brigands in year 1589. It's a historically researched game which uses qr codes to give more information and history.

Long ago, unicorns lived in peace… until four wicked queens stole their magic. Deprived of their crystals, the unicorns became ordinary horses.

Players must collect colors, form rainbows, and confront the queens to reclaim the crystals and restore the unicorns’ powers.

Le Retour des Licornes ("The Return of the Unicorns") is a quick, colorful family board game created in France by a father and his two daughters. Easy to learn and full of surprises, it’s perfect for children aged 5 and up, offering fast, engaging matches filled with laughter and imagination.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Autrefois, les licornes vivaient en paix… jusqu’au jour où quatre reines malveillantes ont volé leur magie. Privées de leurs cristaux, les licornes sont redevenues de simples chevaux.

Les joueurs doivent rassembler des couleurs, former des arcs-en-ciel et affronter les reines pour reprendre les cristaux et redevenir des licornes magiques !

Le Retour des Licornes est un jeu de société familial imaginé en France par un papa et ses deux filles. Rapide à mettre en place et facile à comprendre, il offre des parties pleines de rebondissements et de surprises pour les enfants dès 5 ans.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Bolo Souza Leão: O jogo de cartas
description: Bolo Souza Leão: O jogo de cartas ("Bolo Souza Leão - The Card Game") is a cooperative micro-game about fulfilling the requests of the court of Emperor Pedro II. During his visit to Pernambuco in 1859, the imperial entourage was received at the mansion of the Souza Leão family, where they were introduced to the cake that is now a cultural heritage of the state.

boardgame: Dino Dynasty: From Armor To Tail-Clubs
description:
In the first expansion to Dino Dynasty, players are joined by 4 new Dynasties with 12 new dinosaur species.

Joint the fight with Ankylosaurus, explore the unique features of Deinocheirus, spread from the hideout of Wannanosaurus or hunt Titanosaurs with Carnotaurus.

The game adds 13 new Morphs, such as symbiosis, that removes attrition, pack-hunting, where herds join up to fight, and stereoscopic vision, that lets the hunters detect camouflaged dinosaurs. And of course, plenty of armors and tail-clubs!

Requires Dino Dynasty base game.

boardgame: Relics of Rajavihara: A Year of Relics
description:
A new quest every week for an entire year! Each week, a new level will be sent to you that you can either print at home or display on your device.

New print and play (PNP) / display and play levels for Relics of Rajavihara.

The first 32 levels use the base game and the final 20 levels also utilize the expansion, Montalo's Revenge.

—description from the designer

boardgame: カクテル×パーティー ～真理の探求者～ (Cocktail x Party ~Seeker of Truth~)
description:
The Colosseum is a secret tavern where those who seek truth gather. The spirits served there all have souls of their own, and through drinking together, the Colosseum’s inhabitants discover new things about the world and themselves. As you meet those from the Cocktail World, will you come out victorious, or return home a drunk? Gather the 10 Virtues of Alcohol, and reach the truth!

This is a card game for 2-4 players, where players take turns battling each other. Make cool cocktails and attack your enemies! Add cards to your opponents’ hands to get them drunk (defeated). The player who’s able to party the longest will be the winner.

Smorgasbord is a compendium climbing/shedding game.

What is a compendium game?
It is a game where there are multiple objectives that each player must complete by the end of the game. These objectives are commonly referred to in traditional games as “contracts”.

Each player must complete every contract before the game can end. Most importantly, once a player completes a specific contract they cannot attempt to complete that contract again.
Contracts slightly change the rules of play for all players (examples include changing the bomb type, changing the card ranking, choosing when you shed out, creating new meld options, and having hands where the climbing is "must play").

To determine which contract is played in a given hand, there is a bidding phase where players go around the table picking a contract they want to play (becoming the declarer) or passing. Being the declarer can give you lots of power/control over the hand. Not only will you get to select the contract, but you also get to select which melds are bombs, exchange with the 3 face up cards in the talon, and lead the first trick!

The declarer's score is the only score that matters for the hand. The other two players, referred to as "defenders", are trying to stop the declarer from doing well at their contract.

After all contracts have been played, the player with the most points is the winner!


An overview of the game in slightly more detail:

Dealing Phase (deck = 48 cards, 1-12 in 4 suits and 3 wild cards, 1 per player):

     Each player takes 1 wild card.
     Forehand shuffles the deck and deals 15 cards to each player.
     There will be 3 undealt cards, these are placed face up nearby, becoming the talon.


Bidding Phase (starts with forehand):

     Bid on a contract you have not completed yet or pass.
     If all players pass, the forehand must bid on a contract.
     There is an overbidding system that applies if multiple players want to bid on a contract.


Climbing Phase (starts with declarer):

     Declarer leads any meld they like. Others must play a higher meld that matches the type lead, play a bomb (if the contract has one), or pass.
     Play continues until either the declarer meets their objective or they are the last player left with cards.


Scoring Phase (only declarer scores):

     Declarer scores points based on two things: 1) how well did they meet the objective of the contract 2) how many cards were left in the opponent's hand with most cards left after they shed out.


Repeat phases until all contracts are completed.

—description from the designer

boardgame: カクテル×パーティー The Last Order 〜酒の起源〜 (Cocktail x Party: The Last Order ~The Origin of Alcohol)
description:
The Colosseum is a secret tavern where those who seek truth gather. These truth seekers discover that the Colosseum connects this world to the Cocktail World, and that upon reaching the truth, they will gain the power to see the future. However, upon obtaining this power, they discovered a prophecy, stating that “A banquet without the Origin of Alcohol will quickly become a rainbow-colored hell.” At that time, the Cocktail World’s biggest party, the Cocktail x Party, was about to begin... Just what is the mysterious Origin of Alcohol? Who are these mysterious aliens that have
shown up demanding it? And who is this group of underground food sellers? This party is getting crazier by the minute! As last call looms closer, will you be able to survive!?

This expansion adds in new Base Spirits (Rum and Tequila) and associated cocktails, as well as additional Side Dishes, Juice Cards, and rules for constructed play. It also comes in a box that can hold the base game and the expansion.

boardgame: Heroes of Barcadia: Holiday Booster Pack
description:
Make the dungeon merry and bright with this holly jolly Heroes of Barcadia game expansion! The Holiday Booster Pack introduces a brand-new room type: The Ally, plus festive room tiles, gift-worthy power-ups, and house rules that bring cheer (and chaos) to every turn.

Add 5 Power-Ups, 5 House Rule Cards, and 5 Room Tiles to your game for a delightfully unhinged seasonal twist. Whether you're battling for glory or just trying to survive the holiday party, this booster’s got your back.

Product Specifications:

- Heroes of Barcadia Holiday Booster Pack
- Mechanic: Dungeon crawling, procedurally generated, player elimination, roll/spin and move, collection, Liquid Life System

Includes:

- 5 Waterproof Power-Up Cards
- 5 Waterproof House Rule Cards
- 5 Waterproof Room Tiles

ALL cards in this game are made of PVC and are 100% waterproof

—description from the publisher's website

Turn chess into a faction-based contest using any of 10 decks of 27 cards each.

How will you lead an army of pawns, knights, bishops, and so on who are plague-ridden monstrosities?

Or how does your initial gambit change when opposing rooks and bishops are equipped with ranged attacks?

Try any of the 10 unique factions against one another in this unique twist on the classic game.

—description from the publisher

A placement game with a goal of highest score.

Played on a checker board, or on a drawn square grid. 
Can easily be adapted to other grids.

To Play, first decide which scoring method to use. (see below)

Then players alternate, optionally either placing one of their pieces onto an empty light square on the checker board, or passing.

The game ends when both players pass consecutively.

Scoring Methods: 
(choose 1 of these 3 methods before you begin the game)
1. Score 1 point for each dark square next to exactly 2 of your own pieces orthogonally.
2. Score 1 point for each empty light square next to exactly 2 of your own pieces diagonally.
3. Score by adding together the scores for both method 1. and 2.

For a longer game, play on any of the squares, and score the intersections and/or empty squares.

Premiere Soccer (yes, I know the real-life league is named "Premier") is an easier to play, more accessible follow-up to Big Time Soccer. Based on professional league statistics, yet features fast, streamlined play. The game will delight players with a realistic number of shots at net, goals scored, fouls, etc. All the elements of pro soccer are here, including corner kicks, free kicks, goal kicks, penalty kicks, dribbling, passing, tackles and intercepted passes. Soccer wouldn’t be complete without yellow (and red) cards, and you’ll find them in the game, too. So put on your jersey and shin guards, and get ready for some soccer!

'-description from designer

Pixels is a family-friendly, team-based party game focused on presenting keywords using the titular pixels. At the start of each round, team leaders secretly decide how many cubes they’ll need to show the shared keyword. A lower bid gives a team the right to present first, but if they fail, they lose that privilege to their opponents, who can then try to show the keyword using more cubes.

The pixels are used both to present the keywords and as points that flow between teams during the game. Players can build static pixel art or animate small scenes on the board to help their teammates guess the word. Presenter can also use a "hot and cold" scale to respond to their teammates’ questions, giving them hints about how close they are to guessing the keyword. The keywords cards also include optional bonus conditions — completing them earns the team an extra point.

The game lasts for 11 rounds. The team that collects the most points wins.

Pixels includes several different gameplay modes that affect the level of challenge. For example, a mode with a limited number of questions the team can ask, or "Pixels Kids", which allows younger players to join the fun. Pixels also features a 2–3 player mode, in which players face the game itself — trying to guess as many keywords as possible while their pool of cubes shrinks with each round.

—description from the designer

Three hundred times have I watched the world burn. Years spent rewriting history have been for naught; once again I could not help humanity survive the invasion. - Herbert G W

The Brass Age: Invasion is a Solo, 9-card, Steampunk-Meets-50's-Sci-Fi, Resource-Management Deck-Builder (phew!) . Can you command King Victor's Royal Army to fight off the Martian Invasion?

The board is a simple board of 4 x 5 squares!
     Just like in normal shogi, you can win by capturing the opponent's [King], and we also use the [Try Rule], which allows you to win when your [King] reaches the opponent's base (sap zone).
     Each insect (piece) has different abilities! Some insects can evolve and transform if certain conditions are met. (The grasshopper evolves by eating grass on the board, the praying mantis evolves by capturing an opponent, and the turtle transforms into the opponent it captured)
     The back side has 3x4 squares, allowing you to enjoy even simpler games.

Remember the fun you had with Greedy Granny, trying to grab her biscuits without waking her up? Well Granny’s back and she’s showing no signs of letting go. In Greedy Granny In a Spin, you’ll set her beady, greedy eyes spinning with a push of the hunger plunger. Round and round they go… who knows what they’ll look like when they stop? When they do, the race is on to find the card with the eyes showing on Granny’s face. First to spot the pair that match collects the number of cookies shown on the dial. Play until all the cards are done, or until the biscuits are all gone! The player with the biggest collection of cookies wins the game! It’s fast, it’s frenzied and full of fun! When time comes to put Granny to bed, the set folds up neatly with its own storage compartment for all your cards and toy biscuits.

—description from the publisher

Tongue-in-cheek Character-builder about being fantasy's favorite mythical beast: the Dragon. Each player is a Dragon--a creature of Legend, whose power grows with the Names by which they are known. Game play is divided into rounds, during which players fight guards and pillage Regions for resources which are then used to acquire Titles (Names) which grant them ever greater power.

The game ends when a player acquires their fifth Title and a player has acquired a 5-point Title. Each player's score is then determined based on their Titles and secret scoring goals called Folklore, with adjustments based on wounds and legendary exploits.

Key game-play features include: simultaneous play (no waiting!), being the antagonist, balancing the benefits and detriments of each Title, push your luck (Dragons are greedy!), asymmetric dragon abilities, dramatic reading of your FULL NAME every time you get a new Title (e.g. "I AM SMUGG, the MAGNIFICENT, the PUGNACIOUS, Of FANGORIOUS DEVOURMENT!"), and whimsical hand-drawn line art.

—description from the publisher

The judge flips a card and presents a topic.
Each player responds with a word (thing, action, or person) that best fits the theme.
If the judge selects your answer, you earn points!
Repeat this process, and the player with the highest score wins.
Which word will be today’s Best Word?

—description from the publisher

判定人がカードをめくり、お題を出題します。 回答者はそれにふさわしい言葉（もの/こと/人）を回答します。判定人に選ばれると得点！
これを繰り返し、最も得点した人が勝者となります。
どの言葉が今日のベストワードとなるでしょう？

—description from the publisher (Japanese)

At last, you’ve reached the entrance of the Down Down Dungeon!
They say the structure inside changes every time you enter, gradually revealing its full form.
Try to make it back safely without encountering any monsters—and bring home as much treasure as you can!

Japanese version of Mysterious Dungeon!
The new Dragon Plate, which changes the scoring method, has been added, so it looks like the game will be playable for a long time.
Also, 20 new cards corresponding to tiles have been added, making it easier to play

—description from the publisher

ついにだんだんダンジョンの入口にたどり着きました！
この中の構造は入るたびに変わり、だんだん全容が明らかになっていくといいます。
モンスターと出会わず、たくさんの財宝を持って無事に帰りましょう！

—description from the publisher (Japanese)

Quad Trouble is a game of survival, teamwork, and deception. A group of soldiers find themselves stranded in an unforgiving desert after their quad bikes unexpectedly grind to a halt. Little do they know, a traitor (or traitors) lurks among them, responsible for their misfortune.

The objective of the soldiers is to repair their broken quad bikes and manage the limited food and water supplies to survive long enough to escape the desert. Meanwhile, the traitors secretly work to sabotage the mission by wasting precious resources and damaging the bikes beyond repair.

With each passing round, the team’s food and water reserves decrease depending on how many players remain in the game, creating mounting tension and forcing difficult choices. Trust is tested, alliances shift, and suspicion grows.

Will you work together to overcome the odds and survive the desert—or will deception and betrayal lead you all to ruin?

—description from the publisher

It is a satirical take on the Indian election. 
Players will enjoy having skin in the game of politics. 
It is a two- to four-player game for the age group of 18 years and above. 15 minutes to understand how to play and around one hour to play it. There are two political parties in the game.

https://electiontamasha.co.in/

Players will divide into two political parties. They are the high command of their parties. In the game, there are 9 constituencies. On the basis of their types, they have more or less resources. Crook and corrupt have more resources, and clean has less resources. Players should be careful in choosing their CandyNeta. They use ‘Campaign’ cards to get votes.There are BreakingNews! Some news may bring votes and take away resources, or may take away resources and votes also.

It is sheer player’s luck. Like every game, politics is a game of strategy and luck. The player winning in 5 constituencies or more wins initially. Corrupt and Crook Candidates are on sale. The player having enough resources to purchase the opposition’s winning CandyNeta wins the game. Only winning numbers is not important; you should have sufficient fund to do horse trading.

-description from designer

boardgame: TROY: The Dice Chess of the Gods
description:
A divine duel where chess meets dice and fate decides the victor.

TROY: The Dice Chess of the Gods is a strategic dice-driven battle game inspired by the mythic clash of gods and heroes.

Players command divine armies across a modular battlefield, combining the tactical depth of chess with the unpredictability of dice. Each unit holds unique powers, and positioning is key to control the sacred arenas of Olympus and Troy.

Every match becomes a living war of logic, chance, and divine timing — where mortals and immortals alike test their fate in the ultimate duel of strategy.

Simple to learn, yet infinitely replayable, TROY: The Dice Chess of the Gods merges classic intellect with mythic intensity in a new era of tactical board gaming.

Are you that parent who always lets the milk boil over or do you ever forget your child in the car? Does a crying baby keep you awake at night? Or are you that super mom/daddy who has everything under control?

Find out with Babymania!

In fast rounds, alone or with your partner, you compete for the title of most skilled parent on the table. Are the babies hungry? Is it time for a maternity visit? Or does a diaper need to be changed first?
Anyway, get the situation back under control and keep those baby buttocks clean!

—description from the publisher (translated)

The 48 cards are shuffled and placed in a pile, face down, in the centre of the table. Each player draws 2 cards that only they can see.

The youngest player goes first. They place one or two cards from their hand face up, in the centre of the table. The cards
must be in contact with each other, either horizontally
or vertically. The player then draws from the pile to
bring their hand back to 2 cards. The turn ends and
it’s the next player’s turn.

Cards can only played in a 4x4 grid
No identical cards can be placed in the same row or column
The turtle can't be placed next to the anemone.
The shark can't be placed next to the shark.

If, during their turn, a player cannot place either of the two cards in their hand, they say: Bazarquatik! The previous player then takes all the cards placed on the table up to that point.  The player who couldn’t place their cards keeps their hand and will now be able to place at least one card.

The game ends when the draw pile is finished and all the cards have been played. The player who has collected the largest number of cards wins the game.

Players collect tiles to grow their own flowers: from the simple lotus to the majestic sunflower.
Every choice matters: will you collect water, sunlight, or start a botanical research project for unique benefits?
Take good care of your flowers, because letting them wilt will lose valuable points.
Sliding tiles provide ever-changing choices and plenty of interaction between players.

Whoever has the most blooming, best-researched garden at the end wins the title of Master Grower of the Year!

Room 25 is back and better than ever!

Inspired by the cult film Cube, Room 25 is a thrilling board game where a group of players find themselves trapped in a mysterious prison-like TV show. Each room has four doors… but seemingly no way out.

Your mission: find Room 25, the legendary exit that leads to freedom. But beware: not everyone shares that goal. Some players may secretly be Wardens, determined to keep the others trapped inside forever.

Each turn, players secretly program their actions and move through the complex, uncovering new rooms and triggering their often perilous effects.

The game offers multiple modes:

-Cooperative mode, where everyone works together to escape.

-Suspicion mode, where up to two Wardens hide among the group, sowing doubt and chaos.

The game also features an advanced variant, introducing new rooms and asymetric player powers.

This new edition features refreshed visuals and refined gameplay, streamlining the original mechanics while preserving the license's strenghts.

Promo card with Echo, the illustrator of the game.

This is just an extra encounter card with the ability to gain one ticket from the display.

boardgame: Clash of Clans: The Epic Raid – Collector's Edition
description:
Contains an upgraded version of the base game (miniatures and deluxe tokens on all components) featuring 137 Miniatures, 101 Deluxe Tokens, 72 Building and Unit Tiles, 7 Custom Engraved D6 Dice and more!

It's a Clash for glory (and Victory Points, of course).
Dominate the battlefield to become the top Clan at the table!
You start like all Clans: with a small army, a few Buildings, and some eager Builders. Send them out to locations on the Center Board to build structures, recruit troops, and more in this Epic Game of Epic Raids!

BUILD YOUR VILLAGE

-description from publisher

boardgame: Clash of Clans: The Epic Raid – Dark Elixir Expansion
description:
Summon the Hog Rider, Valkyrie, Minion, Lava Hound, Golem, and Witch - each stronger and more specialized than standard troops. Combine them for devastating synergies and unstoppable Raids.

The Dark Elixir Expansion introduces an extra board, Units, and a new resource - The Dark Elixir - a new resource that will be used for a separate marketplace of more units.

boardgame: Clash of Clans: The Epic Raid – Clan Wars Expansion
description:
The Clan Wars Expansion raises the stakes with the all new Clan Wars mode. Adds a new team mode and ability to go up to 6 players. New spell cards add extra twists and turns as you team up, and new units will make every game a new experience.

Two Players form a Clan - but only the lower of their scores count. Success depends on teamwork, strategy and coordination. Battle other Clans for glory with this Epic Team Battle mode!

STAGE ONE. 
Place the letter tablets face down in a 'pool' and mix them. Decide which player shall start. The other players will take turns in clockwise order. One letter tablet is placed in the center of the table, face up.
The first to play draws one tablet from the pool and must decide if this letter can be attached to the letter on the table at either end. To do so it must be in a sequence that will form part of a known word. The player must state the word he is making. The word can read from left to right or right to left. The next player draws a letter and if he can place it at either end, will do so. This player will also declare the word he is making as it may be different to the other players word. Each player, in turn, will do the same until a word is made.
If any player is unable to add his letter tablet to the row he must keep this tablet until the second stage of the game. It will not at any time be placed in the row.
Once a word is complete the two end letters of the word are turned at right angles to the row (in the manner of Dominoes) to show that the word has ended. Play will continue with each player drawing a letter, in turn, from the pool and having the option of placing it at either end. The 'turned' letter (i.e. the ends of the word) will become the first letters of the new words. Play will continue until all the letter tablets have been taken from the pool when the game proceeds to the second stage.

STAGE TWO. 
Each player will now look at the letter tablets he has been unable to place in the row. He will sort these into as many complete, separate words as he can. At this stage no overlapping of the end letter is allowed. When this has been done the score can be taken.

SCORING. 
The tablets that are left in each players hand will now score against him. Add the numbers on these tablets and the Winner will be the player with the lowest score.

—user summary

Flame & Forge is a new and completely standalone game from 1 to 4 players. It’s a Worker placement, deck-building, push-your-luck full of interesting choices and really fun meeples! This time we will be dwarfs marching into a city on flames to raid resources, forge powerful weapons and challenge monsters — and maybe convince them to join your side of the fight!

Will you save the city… or rule it? in Flame & Forge you choose your path before the game begins — cooperate or compete, the forge is ready for both. So you may join forces to take down the mighty red dragon before your city goes up in flames, or if your friends are feeling bold (and maybe a bit greedy), storm the castle to see who will slay the beast and rise as the next Dwarf King.

-

In your turn pick one of your dwarf meeples from your campfire and place them on the outskirt city tiles in the first spot to the left of the chosen tile and take ALL visible actions in this tile AND take damage to remove fire tokens, if another player places their dwarf in the same tile they may perform less actions than you. In your next turn, your options will be reduced: the next tile must be up by one line closer to the dragon's castle and adjacent to the tile your previously placed your dwarf. Once all of your dwarfs are out you retrieve them all and start from the bottom again.

Possible actions a tile can have (usually around 2-3 actions):

     Get a Gem
     Spend Gems to forge a weapon card
     Spend Gems to advance in the knowledge track
     Perform a ranged attack
     Fight a monsters on the same tile
     Grant a success hit on a attack
     Retire a card from your deck
     Heal 1 damage


In the knowledge track you can also upgrade your dwarf workers to special meeples that give you actions, and also give special abilities to be used when they all return to you after they are all out.

A big element of the game is the battling system that allow for you to hit cards (in a push your luck) where the damage you deal and the damage you take comes from your own deck. Weapons you forge and monsters you defeat also go to your deck along with cards that will allow for the most varied actions (as long as you survive the fight) so many times you need to decide to either flee and perform the actions or risk it all to try and defeat the monster.

There are some arcade - boss battler - elements and the dragon fights back putting fire to the city and if any tile has 3 fire it will be destroyed and players will lose the game if 7 city tiles are destroyed.

The game ends when the dragon is defeated by one of the players. If you are playing coop then you will all win together, if before the beginning of the game you decided for the competitive game then you will count points to decide the new king.

—description from the publisher

Can you catch the best combination of fish?
On your turn, move your hook deeper into the pond to catch fish, then bring it back to the surface.
By using caught fish as bait, you can fish deeper in the pond to get more and better fish. But if you use too many fish, you won’t have enough left to win the game.

—description from the designer

Battlepaths is a medium weight hex-based skirmish game that features tile placement, hidden movement & supply management.

Command retrofitted mechs in a post-apocalyptic showdown.
Compete for resources, territory or survival across dynamic Game Modes. Tactical positioning, hidden movement, and bluffing drive every decision. Adapt, deceive, and dominate. Only one path leads to victory...

At the dawn of the world, your tribe awakens on untamed lands — vast, wild, and full of promise.
Through exploration, gathering, and construction, guide your people through the ages to found the very first civilization.

Your goal: be the first to erect the Great Totem on a Mountain, a symbol of victory and the birth of a new world.

Each turn, you perform two actions from three choices:
? Explore – Reveal new lands, uncover riches, or face scarcity and danger.
? Gather – Collect resources from your territories: wood, berries, water, hides, clay, stone, fish, and herbs.
?️ Build – Erect buildings to unlock effects, expand your tribe’s abilities, and advance toward civilization.

Accessible and fast-paced, Croc-Mignon blends the tension of a race game with the satisfaction of building and the excitement of discovery.
Every game tells a new origin story through modular terrain cards, varied discovery effects, and asymmetric clans (Dodo, Tiger, Mammoth, Rhino) that shape your strategy.

A charming and strategic adventure where foresight, courage, and adaptability forge the legend of your tribe.

-description from designer


À l’aube du monde, votre tribu s’éveille sur des terres vierges, vastes et pleines de promesses.
Par l’exploration, la récolte et la construction, guidez-la à travers les âges pour fonder la toute première civilisation.

Votre objectif : être le premier à ériger le Grand Totem sur une Montagne, symbole de la victoire et de la naissance d’un nouveau monde.

Chaque tour, vous effectuez deux actions parmi trois :
? Explorer – Révélez de nouvelles terres, découvrez des richesses ou affrontez des pénuries.
? Récolter – Collectez les ressources de vos territoires : bois, baies, eau, peaux, argile, pierre, poisson, herbes.
?️ Construire – Érigez des bâtiments pour débloquer des effets, augmenter vos capacités et progresser jusqu’à la civilisation.

Accessible et rapide, Croc-Mignon combine la tension d’un jeu de course, la satisfaction de la construction et la curiosité de l’exploration.
Chaque partie raconte la naissance d’un monde différent grâce à ses cartes modulaires, ses effets de découverte variés et ses clans asymétriques (Dodo, Tigre, Mammouth, Rhino) qui modifient vos stratégies.

Une aventure stratégique et attachante, où anticipation, audace et adaptation forgent la légende de votre tribu.
Mini expansion for the Beer Pioneers expansion “Beer Boom”. More variety for module 2 and 5. Also a new action card for module 1.

Two cards merge into one in this board game with a unique fusion mechanic! 
Card combinations change your movement and the amount of treasure you gain.
Plus, use your power to transform treasures for combos! 
But beware—the dragon’s counterattacks will alter their rarity and value!

This is not war. This is the end.

Konflikt '47 is the tabletop wargame of heroes, horrors, monsters, and mechs – set in an alternate universe in which WWII never ended, and terrifying new weapons of war are unleashed on the battlefield. 
This Introductory Set is a perfect way to leap into the world of Konflikt ’47 for the very first time, regardless of if you are new to wargaming, or are the veteran of many a tabletop battlefield!

Within this box are finely detailed, multi-part plastic miniatures, twelve jump-pack equipped United States Fireflies squaring off against six imposing Axis Stahltruppen. 
The Field Manual within walks you step-by-step through building and painting these finely detailed 28mm plastic miniatures and through playing your first games. 
With straightforward, easy-to-follow instructions, you'll quickly learn the basics of Konflikt '47 and be ready to lead your troops to victory over and over again!

It also includes dice, cardboard tokens and walkers, plastic terrain, and a battle mat upon which to play!

Get Stacked! is a blend of wordplay and  dexterity with a layer of stack-tics! An innovative blocking and bonus card system ensures that every turn matters.

On your turn you will be making a word using exactly one letter from the 'letter pool' and any that are available on the 'stack'. Once you’ve made your word you will be placing your letter from the pool onto the stack. You will be racing to the finish with longer words getting you closer to your goal!

Vowels, Blocking and Bonus Cards are used stack-tically to boost your progress.

As the stack rises, the tension builds. Can you hold your nerve or will you Get Stacked!

-description from designer

Helpdesk Hero is a card game where players take on the role of Interns competing for a permanent position in technical support. During their shift (round), a player is assigned a Ticket and attempts to resolve it by playing an Solucion card. Success is determined by revealing the Character who opened the ticket and matching their Ticket Level and the Priority. The first player to accumulate the agreed-upon Productivity Points secures the job. The rest may continue playing, but the workload will remain the same as at the start of the game.
Market Park contains 15 new cards for Firefly: Misbehavin'. Cards with the new Credit attribute ability earn you 1 Credit at the start of your turn.

These cards were available as an add-on from the crowdfunding website.

This exclusive set contains 6 new Action cards, including 3 Load deck cards and 3 Move deck cards. Add even more variety to your game of Movers and Shakers!

Year 2125, Mars, morning. In a small green paradise amid the red desert stand a few apple trees. Kombucha quietly brews; a robot named Irochka feeds the chickens with some kind of biomaterial; and the suborbital mixer hums steadily. You hop into your “Bukhanka” van and take off. That’s the vibe of Cyberfarm, an official board game (14+) inspired by the hit Kinopoisk and Birchpunk sci-fi comedy series. The game expands the show’s universe and invites players to the Red Planet to grow fractal cucumbers and live the peaceful, high-tech life of a Martian farmer.

Cyberfarm is a cozy farming simulator infused with sci-fi humor, alien creatures, time paradoxes, and humanity’s eternal love for agriculture. Created by the Russian design studio New Making Studio, known for its bestselling games based on Inside Lapenko and Suffering Middle Ages, the project continues their tradition of turning cult stories into rich tabletop experiences. Inspired by the show’s first season, the designers set out to transform viewers into full-fledged citizens of 22nd-century Mars — surrounded by nostalgia, humor, and cosmic serenity.

Each player becomes a Martian farmer competing to build the most efficient cyber-farm. You’ll send Izhevsk Dynamics robots into the fields, construct mills and juice presses, connect them with conveyor belts, and upgrade your facilities with advanced tech. You’ll raise cows and chickens, harvest crops, and ship your products to the Martian market aboard your trusty Bukhanka. The game is a relaxed, non-confrontational family experience for 2–4 players, lasting 120–180 minutes — friendly, inventive, and filled with quirky combinations of cards and technologies. Expect to craft vegan milk from potatoes, experiment with string theory and the Higgs bison (yes, bison).

But Cyberfarm isn’t just a piece of show merchandise — it’s a full new adventure in the same universe. There are no familiar characters like Nikolai Kulibin or Robogozin here; instead, you become their neighbor, running your own futuristic farm. Expect Easter eggs and inside jokes — from compost black holes and VR goggles for cows to giant worms and commemorative coins celebrating future Martian history. Bright visuals, layered humor, and a spirit of optimism make Cyberfarm a heartfelt journey for anyone who once spent summers at their grandma’s dacha and now dreams among the stars.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Чёрная книга: Чердынский клад (Black Book: The Cherdyn Treasure)
description:
Чёрная книга: Чердынский клад ("Black Book: The Cherdyn Treasure") is an expansion for the ''Black Book: The Board Game'' that introduces a brand-new imp mechanic, adding depth to the strategic choices and tactical options of characters.

In addition, this set includes high-quality deluxe acrylic components that will replace the corresponding cardboard tokens from the base game and the ''Secrets of the Parma'' expansion.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Cancre ("Slacker") is a party game for 3 to 8 players. In this board game, for each question, the goal is to find the answer that will get the most votes... will you be able to think like the majority?

In a square grid board (it can be 12x12 or 18x18) 2 players put black and white pieces in the center of each square in any square of the board just like in Gomoku or Go. 
The goal of the game is to make a square of 2x2 pieces, the first one that manages to do so wins the game.
But if no one manages to build the square the winner of the game is the one with the biggest group of pieces connected orthogonally.

—description from the designer

Sanctuary and Corruption is a strategic duel where players embody opposing forces: the Blue Stone, symbol of balance and preservation, and the Lava, embodiment of chaos and decay.
Through tile placement, each player expands their domain, reshaping the land to reflect their essence. The game ends when the world is complete — and victory belongs to the side that controls the most territory, defining whether the world is reborn as a Sanctuary or consumed by Corruption

boardgame: Frozen Hell: The Battle of Attu, 1943
description:
WW2 Frozen Hell 1943 - The Battle of Attu

This small campaign system can be used and modified for a number of WW2 rules sets.
This new WW2 publication is to bring to life the Battle of Attu in 1943.
The only time since 1812 that American soil was invaded.
This Campaign booklet has ideas for five scenarios, weather conditions, order of battle for both forces.

Historical Overview: Background of the Aleutian campaign and Attu operations
Order of Battle: US & Japanese Forces
Campaign Rules Overview: sequence, victory tracking, turn tracker, weather effect
Campaign Weather & Conditions Table: Fog, snow, and terrain effects

Scenarios: “Drive the Japs from the Aleutians” Scenario’s have battle map, mission details, special rules, victory conditions

Scenario 1: Landing at Massacre Bay  - battle map + mission
Scenario 2: Assault on Jarmin Pass – Map + mission
Scenario 3: Hill X — Battle for the Ridge – Map + mission
Scenario 4: Rakon Ridge Breakout– Map + mission
Scenario 5: The Final Banzai Charge – Map + mission
Campaign Results Tracker Page – Victory points

boardgame: On Bloody Ground: The American Civil War – Mass Combat Wargaming Rulebook
description:
On Bloody Ground - The American Civil War, includes four complete army lists to enable players to build Early War Union, Early War Confederate, Late War Union or Late War Confederate armies.

A 54 page A4 perfect bound mass combat rulebook with common sense rules for movement, shooting and combat, along with a command and orders structure which produces a highly tactical, dynamic and versatile wargame, that is great fun for both seasoned gamers and beginners alike.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players
Series: On Bloody Ground (WiP Games and Miniatures)

description:
1) Before the game, flip the token like a coin to find out which side will start.

2) When it's your turn to play, you can choose between two options:

A) Before moving, you keep the token on its current side.

B) Before moving, you flip the token again (like a coin).

3) The moving piece must land on a square with the same color of the token.

- A player who doesn't respect the color shown by the token loses.
- A player who can't play (illegal move, etc.) loses.
- During castling, consider only the king’s move (for the landing square).
- If the king is in checkmate, it does not matter what color square he is on.

In convention exclusive D-Day Dice: Blind Assault, you must charge into the unknown against an unpredictable enemy!

Included are:

     4 new Cave Maps that will send shivers down your spine. 
     2 jungle Battle Maps (Bakri Road and  Cape Gloucester) covered with punishing Random Attribute Sectors and new features.
     2 Legendary Units designed to survive in this unforgiving terrain (the US 41st Infantry Division nicknamed "The Jungleers" and the 4th Bombay Grenadiers).
     12 dice: 4 speckled regular d6 (with red pips on the 6), 4 black MGF dice (with red Skulls), 4 speckled MGF dice
     MGF Rules


- And of course the necessary rules to add them to any D-Day Dice game.

The Bakri Road Battle Map includes 2 different modes that take advantage of these new dice in an unexpected way. This challenge is not for the faint of heart. During your blind assault, you'll have to adapt your tactics and react quickly to ever-changing conditions!

—description from the publisher

Bomber Boys is a dice rolling and dice allocation game with a WWII theme. You are managing the crew of an Allied aircraft during a series of air operations. To win, you have to complete each of the given missions, and each mission ends with a target that you have to bomb for a major victory. Gameplay is based on a combination of decision making and luck.

—description from the publisher

Across the Kingdom is a solo roll-and-write adventure. With nothing but two dice and a single pencil, you’ll journey across a sprawling realm - charting wild frontiers, uncovering hidden treasures, battling fearsome monsters, and lending aid to the hardworking folk who call this land home. Explore boldly, for every discovery brings you closer to a name worthy of the royal chronicles.

Your expedition spans four seasons: spring, summer, autumn, and winter. Each season grants you 4-5 turns to press deeper into the unknown. At the start of every turn, roll two dice, choose a terrain type, and mark five matching hexes on your map. Seek glittering gold to fill your coffers, collect shields to defend against monstrous threats, complete labor tasks for grateful townspeople, and reveal as much of the kingdom as fate allows.

When the final snows of winter settle and your year of adventure comes to an end, tally your deeds. The kingdom will remember every bold step you took and celebrate the hero you’ve become.

—description from the publisher

The improved version of a classic card game. Laughter guaranted!

Give every player 3 lives (cards with a heart). In the original version, the text on the backside of the card is not used.

Shuffle the cards. Each player gets 3 cards. Put the remaining cards face down in the middle of the table. The player whose age is closest to 51 starts.

The first player plays a card and says the amount of points out loud. Then, they grab a new card from the pile. On their turn, the other players each play a card and add their played amount to the total amount of points, and grab a new card from the pile.

A total of 51 is allowed. As soon as a player exceeds 51, that player loses the round. They must then throw away a life card.  It also means that you will need to act in a certain way from then on (explained on the card) which makes the game hilarious. Then, the game begins anew; the loser is the new dealer and plays first.

If a player forgets to pick up a new card from the pile before the next player takes a card, that first player plays with one card less. If a player ends up being unable to play because they don’t have any cards left, they lose the round and a life.

The game ends when a player loses a round when having no more life cards.

Welcome to the final showdown! Take on the role of one of the characters who have committed misdeeds in their lives and are facing their end at a gambling table.

The rules are simple: only one can leave the table alive. Each turn, you must pull the trigger, but be careful—there are bullets you'll want to receive in order to earn rewards.

During the game, you can use a series of item cards to “cheat” and get away unscathed.

In short, it's a game of statistics, luck, betting, and a lot of bluffing. Are you ready?

—description from the publisher

Urban-themed Strategy and Elimination Game For 2 to 6 players: Become the king of the playground in a competitive board game where every move counts. 
Move between swings, sandboxes, and slides as you collect items and eliminate rivals.

Fast-Paced Mechanics with Dice and Visual Attacks: Roll the dice, advance across the board, and shoot water guns, slingshots, or suction cup arrows. Each turn combines movement, action, and defense in dynamic combat.

Weapons, Armor, and Special Powers: Over 50 equipment and consumable cards allow you to customize your strategy. Improve your defense with armor or launch chain attacks to eliminate enemy crowns.

Supreme Power in the Central Castle: Whoever reaches the castle gains the power to attack without limits. But if you are defeated there, you lose the game. Only the last one standing will be crowned the new king of the neighborhood.

Premium Components and Group Fun: Includes a reversible board, wooden crowns, dice, illustrated cards, and character tokens. Ideal for quick 30-minute games with friends or family, ages 8 and up.

—description from the publisher

After years of chasing rumors about cryptids, you’ve learned of several spots where these elusive creatures are frequently sighted. As a journalist, you plan to explore these spots in order to gather subject material for future articles.

Cryptids are cautious and will not reveal themselves in front of a crowd, so you’ll need to shake off your rivals to explore these spots on your own. While a cryptid that several journalists report on will become more topical, too many articles about the same cryptid will lose your readers’ interest.

Years ago, you published an article about a particular cryptid that, when exposed as a hoax, tarnished your reputation. Even if you gather subject material on that cryptid, you won’t be able to make use of it.

Lastly, a word of warning: among your numbers may lurk a pretender whose goal is to prevent you from publishing any news about cryptids.

Will you be able to outwit your rivals, capture the attention of the masses, and restore your journalistic reputation?

—description from the publisher

The montado is a Mediterranean ecosystem shaped by humans, where cork oaks and holm oaks coexist with grazing. It is a multifunctional system that combines cork, timber, and pasture production with biodiversity conservation.

In this tableau-building card game with hand management, each player takes on the role of a guardian of an ecosystem, selecting and combining fauna and flora cards to create a balanced habitat.
On each turn, players choose available species and place them strategically in their territory, respecting natural food chains — each animal can only be introduced if its food source is already present in the ecosystem.
The challenge lies in balancing the needs of each species while maximizing points through your cards and shared objectives, represented by different professions that value specific aspects of your ecosystem.

Simple rules, deep decisions: the winner is the one who creates the highest-scoring and most sustainable ecosystem.

boardgame: All Time Wrestling: The Hurt Edition Expansion
description:
The Expansion Where Pain Means Profit

Step into the ring with the unbreakable force of Bobby Lashley, the slick strategist Shelton Benjamin, and mastermind handler MVP standing in your corner. Because when you’re with The Hurt Syndicate, you don’t just play the game: you dominate it!

What’s Inside
2 PLAYABLE WRESTLERS: Bobby Lashley & Shelton Benjamin

1 MVP MANAGER: MVP brings that business edge to the board

SOLO CAMPAIGN: A gritty, high-stakes story mode where only the strong survive

Full Component Suite: 4 Profile Cards • 2 Player Boards • 31 Attack Cards • 20 Campaign Cards & more
Note: Requires ATW: All or Nothing (2nd Edition) to play the campaign.

Why You’ll Play It
Ruthless Strategy – Like The Hurt Syndicate said: “We Hurt People.” Victory demands calculated aggression and flawless timing.

Punch & Profit – Use every tactic, every finisher, every leftover card. In this ring, there’s no room for mistakes.

Story Mode for Solo Warriors – Play the campaign, climb the ladder, take everything. Just like they did.

Built for Legacy – This isn’t a side hustle. It’s the next chapter in a franchise backed by legends and designed for maximum impact.

—description from the publisher

The first expansion to Kalmár – Merchants in Buda brings new colours, surprising twists, and a playful little figurine to the game. The new character is the court jester, and eight new partner cards also join the mix. With these additions, the lineup of special ability cards — each featuring a unique character — grows from 64 to 72, including some truly eccentric personalities!

—description from the publisher (translated)

A KALMÁR első kiegészítője, a JESTER váratlan fordulatokat és egy új, bolondos figurát hoz a játékba. Ő az udvari bolond, de rajta kívül megjelenik nyolc újabb partnerkártya is. Ezekből a speciális képességkártyákból (amelyek mind egy-egy nevesített szereplőt vonultatnak fel) eleve 64 van a KALMÁR dobozában, de így 72-re bővül a kínálat, ráadásul néhány extrém karakter is szerepel közöttük.

—description from the publisher

The rent’s due, the fridge is empty, and the rain hasn’t stopped for days. Welcome to the Rough Patch Motel, where every night feels like it could be your last, under a real roof.
