In this gritty survival game, players scrape through 21 days of bad luck, odd jobs, and flickering hope while trying to keep their stress, hunger, and bills under control. There’s no adventure here just survival.

Every day begins with an event—some small mercy, some new disaster. You’ll scavenge behind the gas station, hustle for cash with patchwork repairs, and cling to fleeting moments of hope in a city that’s already forgotten you. Players share limited community resources and face hard choices: take the bus to work or let someone else have the seat? Buy food now or risk hunger tomorrow? Every turn is a story of what people do when they’re cornered and how far they’ll go to stay afloat.

Rough Patch is fast, emotional, and deeply human. Designed to play in under an hour, it blends push-your-luck dice mechanics with resource management and narrative tension. It’s not about being the hero it’s about making it to next week. Can you keep your head above water… or will this motel be your last stop before the streets?

—description from the publisher

MAC Attack: A miniature wargame designed for 6mm Mech Miniatures from the creator of The Doomed.

MAC Attack brings the Mobile Armoured Colossus, or MAC, to the battlefield, huge war machines and their maverick pilots.

MAC Attack is:

     COLOSSAL


This miniature agnostic wargame, scales from forces of just three mech units right up to a dozen or more supported by stands of infantry, tanks, aircraft, and distant artillery.

     FAST


A simple ruleset runs quickly at the table, zooming in for gritty detail in moments of high drama.

     EXPLOSIVE


The parts start flying right away. Blast the modules clean off enemy MACs, control your motion, don't overheat.

     FUTURE


Humanity reached for the stars but fractured upon landing. In the age of the MAC, the twisted factions of this once noble cause are trapped in a brutal mechanised conflict.

     WARFARE


Take on a variety of objectives using the built-in scenario generator, then try out other modes of play including Arena Mode, Tech Tree Mode, and Scrapheap Mode.

PHASES
1 - Move Phase
2 - Attack Phase
3 - Meltdown Phase
4 - Cooldown Phase

—description from the designer

Scientists must always let their curiosity lead. Explore new scientific phenomena and uncover the factual truth behind old legends!

Add remarkable new marvels to discover and a new ship (with new journey effects) in this 6-card expansion. Replace marvels from the base game with new, scientific discoveries, or add them in directly for an epic journey!

Grand Expeditions 6-card expansion adds two optional components: a new ship with unique journey effects, and five marvels featuring distinctive costs and placement restrictions. Each can be used on its own with the base game or combined for greater variety. The expansion also includes a new Epic Mode, offering a longer journey and tougher scoring targets to conquer.

—description from the publisher

Converge: Randomizers is a set of 18 cards which determine the skillsets to use in each game. Additionally, they feature optional global abilities which add new dimensions in play.

Draw 3 randomizer cards to determine the 3 skillsets to use. Optionally, you may play with the global ability on the third randomizer card drawn.

boardgame: Gunship: Mk II – Ground Attack Version
description:
Features double-sided board and cards to give your two color options and a new array of weapons and tactics! Also includes newly-designed versions of fighters and assault carriers by artist Luca Oleastri!

—description from the publisher

Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Reindeer and 2 Snowmen in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Reindeer, Snowman, Reindeer, Snowman".

All players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

—description from the publisher
A set of 6 cards similar to Gunship: Crewmates but with race-themed Characters! Artist Eric J. Carter used photo of Kickstarter backers to put their likeness into the game.

Three mysterious figures emerge from the dust, just in time to save the day. Reinforcements have arrived!

Reinforcements 3-card expansion adds three additional car cards, providing additional effects to allow players to draw more cards.

Shuffle the 3 expansion cards into the base game deck and deal each player 4 cards at setup. Play then follows base game rules.

Che Storia! is a card game that aims to make the history of the Italian Republic easy, interesting, and fun,
Choose between competitive, team, or solo modes.
Put events in chronological order.
Use Constitution cards to correct any errors and discover articles of the Constitution.
The winner is the player who correctly places all the cards in their possession.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Che Storia! è un gioco di carte per giovani e non solo che vuole rendere la storia dell’Italia repubblicana facile, interessante e divertente anche per chi non ha mai avuto l’occasione di andare oltre Garibaldi.
Metti in ordine gli eventi politici, culturali, sociali e criminali che hanno segnato l’Italia negli ultimi settant’anni, scopri gli articoli della Costituzione e sfida amici, parenti e perché no, anche l’insegnante di scuola! Chi ne saprà di più sul nostro passato recente?

—description from the publisher

A Salsa Verde Drago is perfect for a family evening: it is suitable for parents, uncles, grandparents, and children who love cooking and, above all, pesto, the inimitable Ligurian sauce famous throughout the world.
It is a dice game in which you earn points by creating combinations. You will have 5 dice at your disposal, and on each side of the dice there will be one of the ingredients of our beloved pesto: basil, garlic, pine nuts, oil, and cheese.
You will have to use your creativity to create many variations of pesto and triumph. To do so, you will have to be a real dragon... in every sense!
To be proclaimed the king of Genoese pesto and cuisine, you'll need to score more points than your opponents.

Questo gioco di dadi e creatività è ispirato alla bellissima favola vincitrice del “trofeo Baia delle Favole”, Premio Andersen 2018 di Lucia Tringali.
Giocando, potrai scoprire l’incredibile vera storia dell’invenzione del pesto alla genovese esplorando i carruggi più nascosti di Genova.
Grazie al formato dei pezzi che lo compongono, è ideale anche da portare in viaggio.

boardgame: Apprentice ICG: The Inclusive Card Game
description:
Welcome to the realm of Apprentice ICG! In this 2-4 player deck-battler, players pick a class — Ember, Forest, Glacier, or Spirit — play mythical characters, cast epic spells, and roll for increased magical power or immersive events to become the chosen apprentice!

Apprentice ICG is designed for all gamers with thoughtfully-engineered access technology. Each card features large-print text, a high-contrast color palette, and transparent UV-hardened braille with QR codes linking to image and card descriptions. In addition, an adaptive-switch-accessible companion app ensures digital access.

The game includes 120 cards divided into the four playable classes, a print-and-braille D12, and four class-themed, accessible tuckboxes with quick-start guides.

Game Summary
Choose one of four magical classes (Ember, Forest, Glacier or Spirit) and shuffle its 30-card deck. Each player begins with 2 cards in hand. Players roll the included D12 die to determine who goes first (lowest roll plays first).

Each round is a “battle”: players each play one card face-up from hand, then immediately draw from their deck to refill their hand to 2 cards. When both cards are played, any special keyword effects activate (see below), and then if required the D12 is rolled to determine power values of cards with “Enchant” or “?” power. The card with the highest total power wins the battle and claims both cards into their discard pile.

Keywords
- Chance! – A card with this lets you pick a number from 1–12 and roll the D12 twice; if you hit it you win the battle outright, regardless of power. 
- Enchant! – If a card has “?” power or has the Enchant keyword, you roll the D12 and that roll defines (or adds to) the card’s power for that battle. 
- Rally! – Lets you add another card into the same battle (and you may chain Rally cards in succession) so you can build a stronger attack. 
- Smite! – On Knight cards; if you play this and your opponent plays a Dragon, you win that battle regardless of power.

Ties
If a tie occurs (equal power with no keyword override), the cards remain in the “pot” and are claimed by the winner of the next battle (or by winner of a roll-off if multiple ties).

End & Victory
The game ends when a player runs out of cards in their deck and hand. At that point, the other player takes any remaining cards and all players count how many cards they’ve collected (via their discard piles). The player with the most cards wins. In the case of a tie, the players roll the D12 once and highest roll wins.

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Gibbon / Pitta Promo Card
description:
Gibbon / Pitta
P026 - Thai Promo Card

Gibbon
Scores: 5 Points or 15 if there are at least 3 different plants.

Pitta
Scores: 1 Point for each amphibian and insect symbol.

A roll-and-move trivia game based loosely on the Monty Python television show.  Features shoots-and-ladders style roses and arms and a giant It's space that players start on.  Cards have both a Task or Trivia question on them; the space you land on determines which of the sides of the card get used.  Correctly answering trivia or doing tasks gets you Awards that can help you win the game at the last space on the board, the foot.

With the authentic Cirulla (or Ciapachinze) card deck, created in collaboration with Mugugno Genovese, you can add an extra touch to your challenges or tournaments, but also play other traditional games (Scopa, Briscola, Rubamazzetto, Solitaire, etc.).
Take the 40 playing cards and give 3 to each player (the game is played by 2 players or two teams of 2) and place 4 on the table
Take the matching cards, those that add up to your card or that together add up to 15.
Turn over the brooms if you draw the last card on the table.
Knock, accumulate money, take the Settebello, put together the primiera, small and large.
The game ends when you reach or exceed (alone or as a team) 51 points or a score set at the beginning.

Con il vero mazzo di carte da Cirulla (o Ciapachinze) in collaborazione col Mugugno Genovese potrai dare un tocco in più alle tue sfide o ai tuoi tornei, ma anche giocare ad altri giochi della tradizione (Scopa, Briscola, Rubamazzetto, Solitario…).
Con le illustrazioni ispirate a Genova e alle sue figure e edifici di spicco, ogni partita avrà un tocco tutto zeneize.
Una nuova versione moderna delle carte genovesi, perfette per una partita in famiglia, in spiaggia, a Natale e anche ai tornei ufficiali.
Cala quel tre di denari e ciapachinze!

Friday at the office has just begun! You'll have to get through it to reach the weekend, without working too hard and without getting caught loafing around by your boss.
Get rid of as many tasks as possible or pass them on to your colleagues so you can reach closing time with as little effort as possible.
Draw a card and apply its effects according to the category: Boss, Chilling, Job Position, and Task.
Watch out for Boss cards! Always eliminate those who work little, those who don't generate enough revenue, and those who naively accept too many tasks...
Get rid of Task cards: the more you have, the more you are at risk of elimination and burnout.
The game ends when only one person remains in play, who is the winner and can enjoy the weekend without any worries!

Thank God It’s Friday è un party game di carte ambientato durante un tipico venerdì in azienda.
Rivivi le classiche situazioni da ufficio: le dinamiche fra capo, le persone sottoposte, le pause caffè e le task di cui disfarti.
Riuscirai a evitare il burn-out… e il licenziamento?

Barista is a one-hour game for 1-4 players designed by Marc Musso.

Own, operate, and upgrade your own coffee shop to become the most popular spot in town!

In Barista, you start with a basic coffee shop and three interns in your staff. This can change very quickly. You decide if/when you want to hire one of the dozens of unique employees or hire Baristas to make coffee faster. You decide if you want to buy trucks that go out and gain you resources or benefits. You decide when and how to upgrade your coffee shop to hold more ingredients, brew different types of coffee, or brew beans quicker.

You will perform actions to gain ingredients and use them to make delicious cups of coffee for your customers. Serving these coffee orders will turn the customers into regulars and they'll come back again and again, giving you a benefit for each future order served. You will compete and make strategic decisions when it comes to placing your workers on actions as well. Many actions have powerful effects that only go to the first worker on the space so it's crucial to pick the right time to act.

Hire employees, fulfill orders, upgrade your shop, and more in this competitive worker placement, engine-building game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Mercenary Pack – Anaphi, Fallen Lion
description:
Once a mercenary from the infamous Jaws of the Lion crew, Anaphi the mindthief is now carving her own path through the city sewers to find some coin and maybe a friend along the way.

A new Mercenary Expansion for Your Favorite Gloomhaven Campaigns!

For the first time ever, play as some of the most infamous characters featured throughout Gloomhaven lore. Designed to be cross-compatible with Gloomhaven, Frosthaven, and Jaws of the Lion this individual “Mercenary Pack" adds exciting gameplay, replayability, and fun to your adventuring party.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Mercenary Pack – Hail, Irritable Enchanter
description:
Hail would just as soon roll her eyes at you as she would transform you into a mindless ooze.

Gloomhaven’s perpetually annoyed and reclusive enchanter lets you turn her irritation into resolve, releasing just a fraction of her immense power.

Muster the courage to knock on her door and recruit one of Gloomhaven’s most powerful allies.

—description from the publisher

Welcome, dedicated volunteers! Your day at the sanctuary is about to become an unforgettable adventure. Three urgent situations have arisen, each a unique challenge: a hen rescue, the escape of mischievous residents, and a mysterious theft. As a team, you embark on one of these adventures and work together to complete it successfully.

Use your collective ingenuity and creativity to discover hidden clues and solve riddles and puzzles. Each cracked code is validated on a sophisticated mechanical device that points to the next task. Have a great time!​


     Three cooperative escape room games in one box.
     Play-once puzzles from novice to expert.
     Easy to learn with a quick-start tutorial.
     Perfect for game nights with friends and family.


No app required! Everything, including hints, is included.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Mercenary Pack – Cassandra, Forgotten Diviner
description:
Inspired by Gloomhaven: Forgotten Circles (out of print), Cassandra creates rifts across the planes to hamper your foes, while using divination to predict the future of your party and its enemies.

A new Mercenary Expansion for Your Favorite Gloomhaven Campaigns!

Featuring brand new design, development, and artwork- Gloomhaven: Forgotten Circles lives on spiritually through this new Mercenary Pack.

—description from the publisher

Legions is a set of scale agnostic, sandbox fantasy wargame rules that focus on the command, control and stress of the battlefield

It contains rules for fighting and building with custom armies And custom weapons, along with a powerful magic system enabling you to play any size or scale of battle you wish, with any fantasy force you can dream up.

Turns are a none predicatable length, adding to the chaos of the battlefield.

The bundle includes the Legions core rules, Legions:Arcana magic system and the Legions counter sheet.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Gloomhaven: Mercenary Pack – Satha, Mayor of Frosthaven
description:
Leader of the infamous outpost to the North, Satha will command your allies to action, instill confidence in your party, and stand between your friends and their demise.

A new Mercenary Expansion for Your Favorite Gloomhaven Campaigns!

For the first time ever, play as some of the most infamous characters featured throughout Gloomhaven lore. Designed to be cross-compatible with Gloomhaven, Frosthaven, and Jaws of the Lion this individual “Mercenary Pack” adds exciting gameplay, replayability, and fun to your adventuring party.

—description from the publisher

Do you have what it takes to SQUISH SQUASH your opponents and become the ultimate Mallow master? To win is simple. Be the first to collect 6 Mallow cards in your basket.

But watch out your hopes of victory may be SQUISHED, SQUASHED OR SQUISH-SQUASHED in an instant with another player's well-timed action card.

UNIQUE DESIGNS: Switch up your strategy with over 100 unique, beautifully illustrated cards. Every game is different and exciting!

HOW TO WIN?: Can you collect all 6 Squishmallows? Be the first to fill your basket to win!!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Wotan
description:
Maliwan unleashes the epic Raid Boss Wotan the Invincible! This exclusive, massive  Wotan miniature includes all of the game cards, tokens, and scenarios need to play.

— description from the publisher

Zeit Heist is a heist-themed, time-traveling drafting game for 2-6 players where you and your opponents are opposing masterminds trying to steal a new periodic element, Zeitnium. The first to steal it is crowned the world's first trillionaire. The element is being shown off at the science museum at 12 o'clock!

Recruit the right crooks to perform the heist of your choosing. Sneak into the museum before it opens with the Scientist, the Spy, and the Lockpicker to nab a win. Or break into the museum after it's open with the Driver, Muscle, and Inside man for an explosive victory. Or sway the Police, Detective, and Undercover Cop to confiscate the Zeitnium in your name! Use the interplay of these distinct win conditions to fool your opponents.

—description from the publisher

Coupon Clipper is a drafting game where players will physically cut up sheets of coupons that are passed around the table. Any coupons cut loose they then keep, and at the end of the game each coupon scores based on the items they've collected. Whoever has the most points then wins.

On a turn a player makes 2 cuts to their sheet, plus 1 cut per bonus cut token spent. Any coupons they cut out they then must keep, and everything else gets passed to their left.

At the end of the game each coupon scores based on the set it belongs to. For example hot dogs and buns score based on how many pairs obtained, fireworks score for whoever collected the most, and coleslaw is worth negative points.

—description from the publisher

Teyra is a competitive strategic card game set in a world inspired by the Majapahit Empire. The Lions - long-standing rulers of Teyra - have fallen and it is up to you to lead your Kinfolk, consisting of Wild Boars, Asian Koels, Monitor Lizards and Orangutans, to spread your influence across the land.

Players take on the role of Kinfolk leaders and must skilfully deploy efforts of their supporters as they advance their campaign across Teyra to spread their influence, control areas, and gain prestige while keeping morale high.

Teyra takes the familiar game of Singaporean Blackjack, or Ban-Luck and puts a strategic spin on it. Players can either go head to head in the 2-player game or form uneasy alliances in the Multiplayer variant with up to 3 other friends. Each of the game’s 4 suits have their own unique abilities that can drastically change the course of the game and the diverse areas in Teyra adds limitations and interactions that will keep players on their toes as they try to gain the most prestige.

—description from the publisher

description:
Propaganda: Print-and-Play is both a standalone print-and-play and an entire expansion for Propaganda which adds several new boards and 3 new types of cards. Many of the new boards have their own sets of rules, including a board where players can control oil (rewarding superpowers who favor them), a board where the value of dice are used to bid over areas, and a board where photos are unlimited but certain spots are off limits.

—description from the publisher

Le Jeu de la Charente-Maritime is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.
About 500 written and 240 photos questions with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with subjects as varied as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked the Charente-Maritime.

Rules:
Each player places their token in their municipality of residence or their chosen town.
Using the die, you will travel the territory answering questions on various topics. If you get the right answer, you can play again!
To win, you will need to collect 5 "Site" cards by correctly answering the question.

—description from the publisher

Make Fest Season truly yours.
Customize 4 genres and 40 artists as you wish.
You can use these in your Fest Season games.

Make Fest Season truly yours.
Customize 4 genres and 40 artists as you wish.
You can use these in your Fest Season games.

A trick-taking game in may-follow, for 2 to 4 players. Four tricks are open at the centre of the table and players may choose freely where to play. When a trick reaches five cards, it is resolved. If one suit dominates, the strongest card of that suit wins. But if two suits are tied, the weakest of those suits takes the trick.

This simple rule completely changes how tricks are read: you calculate, bluff, take risks… and talk about it again after each hand.

Before each hand, players bet with real buttons. The bet is on the exact number of tricks you aim to win. The game swings between calculation, intuition, and provocation. And there is always the ultimate elegant choice: betting zero tricks.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Zombicide: Mission A51 – Not Just a Scratch: A Taxi Saga, Part 3
description: A Taxi Saga, Part 3! After a night's sleep, we woke up to find Robert, our taxi driver friend, in a fit of rage. Someone scratched his car and now he wants blood! Well, we're not about to get in the way of a taxi driver and his revenge, so we might as well help him out.

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Maps & Stickers Recharge Pack
description: Provides extra sets of maps and stickers for three additional full playthroughs of the core box and stretch goal campaigns and add-ons.
Thirteen Promo Cards for the Hitster Rock: Bob! edition. From Spiel Essen 2025.

The Kings of War Rulebook contains everything you need to learn the game and build lists for 16 unique factions.

Kings Of War is the elegant, mass-battle fantasy wargame. The fate of Pannithor is in your hands!

Crafted by legendary games designer Alessio Cavatore, this brand-new book is packed with everything you need to muster your soldiers and march them to war!

Inside this stunning hardback book, not only will you find core rules and stats, advanced mechanics, and army lists, but you can also delve deeper into the ever-evolving world of Pannithor with the latest background stories and artwork.

Command legions. Shape history. Rule the battlefield.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mücken Drücken: Ein Hauen und Stechen
description:
Watch the growing pile of cards and strike at the right moment to catch the most bloodsuckers! But be careful: if you swat the wrong insects, you'll lose points and get a lot of itching. You'll need quick eyes and nimble reflexes – so get ready for a good dose of mosquito swatting!

The dealer takes turns turning over cards in the middle of the table. All players watch closely, because when the right moment comes, it's time to strike! The fastest player grabs the entire pile of cards – the ‘pot’. A game ends when everyone has collected their pots or the deck is empty. After three rounds, the points are counted – and only the best mosquito swatter wins!

—description from the publisher

Beobachte den wachsenden Kartenstapel und schlag im richtigen Moment zu, um die meisten Blutsauger zu erwischen! Doch Vorsicht: Wer die falschen Insekten plattmacht, kassiert juckende Minuspunkte. Hier sind schnelle Augen und flinke Reflexe gefragt – also mach dich bereit für eine ordentliche Portion Moskitokloppen!

Reihum deckt der Dealer Karten in die Tischmitte auf. Alle Spieler verfolgen aufmerksam das Geschehen, denn sobald der richtige Moment gekommen ist, heißt es: Zuschlagen! Wer am schnellsten reagiert, schnappt sich den gesamten Kartenstapel – den „Pott“. Eine Runde endet, wenn alle ihre Pötte beisammenhaben oder das Deck leer ist. Nach drei Runden werden die Punkte gezählt – und nur der beste Mückendrücker gewinnt!

—description from the publisher (German)

The morning walk has gotten messy. As the puppies run to play in the leaves, they’re dropping surprises along the way.

In this quick game of memory and bluffing, play breaks down into two phases: Morning, when you hide all the puppies and all the poops and Evening, when you avoid the doggie landmines placed by you and other players.

Three poops and you’re out in this multi-player take on minefield navigation.

—description from the publisher

"How was Crete? It was perfectly all right, except that it was a bloody […] up from […] to breakfast time.”  – Brig. George Vasey, Commander, 19th Australian Brigade., 1 June 1941

Early in the morning of 20 May 1941, the Germans launched history’s first totally airborne invasion. As their objective, the Germans chose the island of Crete, strategically located in the Mediterranean between Greece, Cyprus, and the coast of North Africa. Defending this rugged and isolated island were the rag-tag Allied survivors of the ill-fated Greek Campaign, comprising Australian, British, New Zealand, and Greek units. Although 42,000 strong, they were woefully weak in ammunition, artillery, rifles, heavy weapons, radios, wheeled transport, and other logistical equipment.

The German airborne assault force comprised the 7th Air Division composed of three assault regiments and attached units, and the elite “Sturm” Regiment. As follow-up support, air transport and sea would land the 5th Mountain Division convoy to relieve the paratroops. They divided the initial airborne assault into four separate assault forces, each based on one of the four parachute regiments.
The battle hung in the balance during the first day. Despite their considerable advantages, the Germans could easily have lost because of faulty intelligence and arrogance in planning. In the end, the British could evacuate only about half their forces and practically none of the equipment, making this another Allied disaster like Dunkirk a year prior.

Airborne Assault: Crete is a grand-tactical level game of the historical WWII invasion of Crete and includes the hypothetical paratroop invasion of Cyprus. The game requires two players and is suitable for team play.

The game focuses on the first few days of battle, keeping players’ attention on maneuver and what is vital to winning the battle. Players will find a familiar game rules framework covering the combined arms problems of airborne assault, air support, and bombardment, and sea invasion. The defender balances where to defend and when to evacuate. As a bonus, an exciting and balanced scenario showing a hypothetical June 1941 German invasion of Cyprus is included.

Complexity: 4 out of 10
Time Scale: 3 turns = 1 day
Map Scale: 1 hex = 1 mile (Crete), 1 hex = 2 miles (Cyprus)
Unit Scale: companies and battalions
Number of Players: 2 players or 2 teams
Solitaire Suitability: 6 out of 10
Average Time to Play: scenarios range from 3 to 9 hours

—description from the publisher

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Can you survive the Swarm?

HexSwarm: Hostile Worlds challenges you to survive on a dangerous new planet. Team up with your friends to build a colony and defend it from the ever-evolving threat of the alien Bugs. With a mix of an innovative map system and deck-based surprises, no two sessions are ever the same!

—description the designer

A lane battler TCG based around cryptids and mythological creatures. Each lane contains has location cards ("Terra") that affect how that lane (and sometimes others) are played. Each player gets more resources to play cards with each turn ("Aura"), capping at 10. The goal of the game is control the lanes by having cards with more "influence" score than your opponent in the lane, gaining you points. (0 for ties, 1 for contested lanes, 2 for uncontested lanes, with special conditions on some cards granting extra points or reducing points gained.) At 30 points, the game ends and the winner is declared.

Whenever a round starts, one player has priority. This player gets to take an action and resolve any effects from said action. Priority then passes to the opponent, who gets to take one action. This repeats until the round ends. Players "Declare the Mission", specifying a lane that they want to resolve by comparing the influence values on cards on each side of the lane. Any effects that trigger are resolved, and points are assigned, thus "Resolve the Mission" occurs. The Terra card in that lane is noted (usually by rotating it 90 degrees) to make it clear that lane has been resolved. Once all 4 lanes have been resolved, the round ends. At the start of the new round, whichever player didn't have priority at the start of last round gains priority.

Expansion and Storage for Legends of Expedition games.
Adding more Expedition cards and Escape Cards. New tokens and a new Campaign mode that will let you link expeditions together in an over arching story.

Quiz with a Twist – for everyone who wants more than just questions and answers.

With Edition 1, the journey into the Boom universe begins: From two sisters in Cologne comes a quiz box that combines pop culture, history, sports, art, film, and geography in a completely new way. Here, knowledge isn’t just tested – it’s combined, thought around corners, laughed at, and marveled at!

4 x Picture Quiz

     Seven vs. Mind – find seven hidden terms in one collage
     Vokalverbot – vowels disappear – the fun stays
     Promi Pets – recognize celebrities in animal form
     Hint Combo – three picture clues lead to the solution


3 x Text Quiz

     Hollywoodstock – two answers, one word fusion
     Oppothink – the opposite leads to the solution
     Britney Whitney – two clues, one rhyming answer


What to expect:

     Categories: Pop Culture · Music & Film · Sports · Art & Literature · History & Politics · Geography
     Each card is double-sided – text quiz on the front, picture quiz on the back
     Often multiple answers per task – more Aha! & Haha!
     Compact box, packed with creative quiz formats


—description from the publisher

It is late 1942 and the seas are dangerous and frightening. Allied convoys surge across the Atlantic, bringing vital supplies to Britain and Russia. But beneath the waves, Germany’s U-Boats are on the hunt, silent and deadly.

You are the commander of a Type VIIC U-Boat, the backbone of the Kriegsmarine’s submarine fleet. Your mission is to intercept and destroy merchant shipping, evade aggressive escorts and survive the increasing threat of Allied air power.
Aircraft patrol the skies and escort ships roam the convoy lanes. Your deck gun may still sink a lone freighter, but surfacing risks instant retaliation. The decisions you make on when to hide and when to attack will be crucial to your success as a U-Boat captain.

This hex and counter game is one of stealth, timing, and nerve. You’ll manage movement, depth, detection and damage across a range of different maps. Escorts will use sonar sweeps, depth charges and gunfire and, if you're too near the surface, the skies may bring additional dangers.

Can you complete your mission or will your U-Boat become yet another silent wreck beneath the Atlantic?

How the game works
This is a solitaire wargame. You play the game, and the enemy is controlled by the game. You will need at least 6, but preferably a few more, standard six-sided dice.
It is highly recommended that the counters provided on the final page of the game are used to play this game. Simply stick them to card and cut them out.

Quick to set up, easy to play, very replayable but certainly challenging, this game should provide you with hours of fun.

     Compact box, packed with creative quiz formats


—description from the publisher

Diecathlon is a co-op deck-building die-crafter that pits you against waves of old and new legends in a cyberpunk Atlantis arena.

Each wave of enemies represents a battle in the arena. In each battle, you will use the cards from your deck to customize your player mat, which represents the sides of your six sided die and what it will do for you in the battle (attack, defense, heal, etc.). You and your teammates will then roll your dice, and get a chance to trade them with each other to maximize your turn. Your deck also represents your health pool. As you take damage, you must discard cards from your deck (not your player board). If you have no more cards to discard for damage, or to draw at the beginning of your turn, you are knocked out.

Between battles, you will have a chance to gain new cards for your deck, or trophies that give passive bonuses.

Whether you engage in the single-run skirmish  or the rogue-lite campaign, you'll find gameplay that's easy to set up, mechanically unique, and wildly fun!

Rogue-lite mode:
Play, die, upgrade, repeat in a grueling challenge that pulls no punches. Make it through 10 waves and defeat the boss to win.

Single-run mode:
one shot, one death. Make it through an accelerated 5 waves which provide faster loot grabs and get you to the boss faster.

—description from the publisher

You and your companions trapped a demon within this pentagram. You aim to send it back whence it came. The demon, however, has different plans. Figure out who it is possessing before it corrupts you one by one and escapes. Complete the exorcism or fall victim to your own sins.

Sinister is a card-drafting Social Intrigue game of Catholic Horror with a shifting betrayer where the eliminated players stay in the game to take revenge.

Each round, players will be dealt Soul cards which tell them if they are possessed, or must resist one of the Seven Deadly Sins. They will then be dealt a hand of Item cards which they will utilize to either resist their sins, or keep the possessed player from corrupting the pentagram. After drafting all of the cards, players will vote on who they think is possessed, check to make sure the pentagram is not fully corrupted, and then exorcise the chosen player. If the exorcised player was possessed, the surviving players win. If they were not possessed, they become a malevolent spirit whose only goal is to help the demon corrupt the pentagram and escape.

Each round the Soul cards are redistributed, meaning a different player is likely possessed.

—description from the publisher

This expansion turns the Diecathlon base cooperative game into a competitive every-player-for-themselves conquest. Utilize temporary allegiances to win battles, upgrading yourself enough to be the first to solo the Boss.

—description from the designer

Ghost Blitz is turning 15, and we're celebrating! This standalone edition not only includes a birthday cake, but also two games: the original Ghost Blitz and the standalone version Ghost Blitz 2. If you already own those games, they're a great addition to this edition!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Birthday parties are the best! But only eating sweets at the end is no fun!

Brigadeiro is a fast-paced game of attention and memory about the most famous sweets at Brazilian birthday parties. Instead of waiting for the "Happy Birthday" song, you have to rush to the sweets table along with the others, trying to collect the best assortment of treats to win.

In each round, everyone chooses a number with their hand and reveals it at the same time, indicating how fast they will run to the sweets table. Whoever chose the same number bumps into each other! And if you're not careful, someone will rat you out and you'll be left without any sweets!

Game #04 of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the designer

The Battle of Zhuolu was a legendary battle in ancient Chinese history, said to have taken place c. 2500 BC. It was the second battle in the history of China as recorded in the Records of the Grand Historian, fought between the Yanhuang tribes led by the legendary Yellow Emperor and the Jiuli tribes led by Chiyou.

Small wargame with only 31 counters.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
The perfect party game for breaking the ice, sparking conversation,
and making new friends. Designed for groups of 3 to 6 players aged 5 and up, Festa in Giardino (Garden Party) is a fast and engaging game, perfect for family play but also great in the classroom for educational activities and inclusive workshops.

During the game, each player takes on the role of the “birthday guest” and receives gifts from friends. But there’s a twist: the other players must guess, among six options, which gift the birthday guest would like the most! To win, you’ll need empathy, attention, and… a pinch of intuition. The result? Lots of laughter, opinion-sharing, and chances to get to know each other better.

HOW TO PLAY

Each player, in turn, receives six “preference” cards (Amazing!, I like it, Neutral, I don’t like it...) and must assign them to the six visible gifts on the table.
Meanwhile, the other players secretly choose which gift they think is the best fit, using their “gift choice” cards.
When the birthday guest reveals their preferences, points are scored based on correct matches. The closer you get to the guest’s tastes, the more points you earn.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Blood and Bayonets: Land and Sea Battles in the Napoleonic Era
description:
Blood & Bayonets is a 28mm skirmish game that immerses players in the battles and conflicts of the Napoleonic Wars.

It joins Blood & Valor, Blood & Steel, and Blood & Crowns as the next installment in a series of games based on Firelock Games’ flagship product, Blood & Plunder.

Blood & Bayonets allows players to recreate the countless skirmishes, raids, patrols, and small ship actions, that occurred during the Napoleonic Wars. 
Blood & Bayonets focuses on infantry formation and tactics used during the period. 
Formations are extremely important when controlling your force. For instance, units in line formation can volley fire, units in attack column are devastating on the charge, and units in skirmish formation can screen the troops behind them.

This book contains force options for the following nations:

     French Army
     French Navy
     British Army
     British Royal Navy
     Spanish Army
     Spanish Guerrillas
     Spanish Royal Navy
     Spanish Army of Joseph Bonaparte
     Portuguese Army


—description from the publisher

YaGoBuGi is a game of communication and negotiation in which players can only communicate by saying words they can form with the cards in their hand.

The cards all say Ya, Go, Bu, or Gi, and can be rearranged and combined however you like. If you find yourself saying the same 'word' as another player, you both play those cards to the table and score, taking a token based on the length of your word.

Your personality will come into play - are you the kind of person who will match any little word with anyone, or are you willing to wait and find a longer match for big points?

—description from the designer

boardgame: Bantam West (2nd Edition): Graves of Gold
description:
Graves of Gold is a standalone western-horror expansion for Bantam West (2nd Edition). Play solo with the expansion on its own, or 1-5 players when combined with Bantam West (2nd Edition) in all-new solo, co-op, and competitive game modes.

Search for relics and riches, but beware of cultists, creatures, and the unspeakable terrors that lurk below. In a world where the source of gold has been lost to time, will you be the first to rediscover it?

—description from the publisher

In a remote Tasmanian town, a legend of the Tasmanian Terror resurfaces as three recent, gruesome murders shock the community. With no apparent motives or suspects, a detective calls upon your team of consulting detectives to unravel the chilling secrets hidden in the tight-knit community, where folklore and superstition blur the lines between fact and fiction. Can you unmask the elusive murderer behind the legend before more lives are lost in "Tasmanian Terror"?

Difficulty: ★★★★ Hard
NOTE: Internet Access Required

-description from publisher's website

A time of troubles is unfolding at Nilrem Abbey.  A mysterious, withering illness has struck all but you and your three companions. Meanwhile the hostile rat folk of the nearby graveyard have begun their assault, breaching the catacombs beneath the abbey.  Your party of mouse adventurers must descend beneath the abbey, find the four glass leaves of the magic clover and engage in an ultimate battle of good versus evil or see Nilrem Abbey fall!

—description from the designer

The Dead King has emerged from the Realm of Souls and awaits you at the Crown of Command. The expansion also includes rules for playing Talisman solo.

(Available in White Dwarf #518. November 2025)

Patrea is a strategic card and resource management game where rival tribes compete to claim sacred relics and build grand monuments in honor of their Pharagods.

Players take turns deploying units, collecting relics, and trading them for powerful monuments that grant abilities and points. Careful timing, lane control, and tactical use of stones to block or reveal relics are key to victory.

The game ends when the unit and Pharagods decks are exhausted, and the player who controls at least two of the three lanes — or has the highest monument score — wins.

—description from the publisher

From Madrid's heart a sudden fire arose,
As Spain rejected Joseph's foreign crown,
Against Bonaparte’s rule came people's blows,
With freedom sought, Empire Français broke down.
With blade and musket, line and skirmish bold,
The Spanish Regulars with courage fought,
While Guerrilla bands in mountain folds,
A slow constriction to invaders brought.
Then Russia beckoned; Nemesis cold stare,
As Napoleon's legions drained away,
Now the sun-baked soil was Wellington's lair,
Led his troops forward in martial display.
Victory to victory he won and rode,
To break the pride that once on Ebro flowed.

This is a simplified strategic wargame simulating the Peninsular War in Portugal, Spain, and Southern France.

You command the French and their Bonapartist allies against a coalition of the Spanish army, guerrillas, and the Anglo-Portuguese force. 
The game models the historical French troop drawdown – from their peak to depletion by the Russian and Sixth Coalition Wars.
Each counter represents about 20,000 soldiers, or a concentration of guerrilla forces.
The game map uses a Voronoi/tiled system based on political boundaries, river and mountain demarcations.

The player explores the role of Spanish Forces (regulars and guerrilleros), the Anglo Portuguese Alliance all set against the drawdown of French Troops from 1812 onwards.

The combat system is simple to learn and play and requires D6. There are 8 games suggested to play which explores systematically the influence of each key component in the Peninsular War.

A game for those who love honest conversations and sharp, sparkling humor.
Each round, one player is looking for love and judging the candidates of other players, who take on the role of terrible matchmakers. They create their ideal candidates using Green Flag cards and sabotage their rivals’ candidates with Red Flag cards — all to convince the judge to choose their match and earn 1 point.

Each player describes their candidate’s story in turn, using their Green Flags. At any moment, the player to their right may throw in a Red Flag card, which the speaker must include in their story and explain why it’s not that bad after all.

Once every player has taken a turn as the judge, the game ends. The player with the most matches wins.

This game reveals hidden expectations, sparks hilarious discussions, and keeps the party going for hours with friendly sabotage.

The game includes 4 modes:

     Main Game — standard rules as described above.
     Anything But That — play in reverse: create the worst possible match.
     Menace of Fate — adds a risk element: roll a die, and each number triggers a unique effect — lucky or disastrous.
     Tier List — different set of rules, the judge secretly ranks set of five flags from 1 to 5. Other players try to guess the ratings. Guess 3 or more correctly to win; otherwise, the judge wins.
A Monopoly style game themed around the city of Melissa, Texas.
publishers: Late for the Sky Production Company

description:
This pack includes:

     photos from the Reports to cover the initial "?" space and customise the Reports.
     the Freelance Journalist Expansion.
     The Informants card 


The Freelance Journalist adds a gray Journalist, which can be hired and used for the next turn in exchange for 1 Request token and 400$.
The Informants card allows you to keep one Evidence card for $100 or an Investigation card for 1 contact token or $300 if you have not discovered a new Evidence.

If a player's movement spin ends on a submarine they do not move and instead turn the knob in the center of the gameboard. This may cause submarines to appear in some of the holes in the board. Players can move ships near the submarines to attack them, which is done by placing the submarine on the separate cardboard piece and launching the depth charge at it with the spring. Sinking a submarine earns a citation.

The other way to earn citations is to land a battleship in the opposing ammunition depot, which is worth five citations. The first player or team to earn ten citations wins, unless one fleet is completely destroyed, in which case the side with the most citations wins.

Have you ever drawn a 'Pirate Kicking a Hot Dog'? How about 'The Grim Reaper having a Tug of War with a Librarian'? You mean you've gone your whole life and never drawn a 'Strip of Bacon wearing a Four-Year-Old's Rain Boots'? Well get ready to draw these and more, as you illustrate the most BANANAS sentences you've ever put together (and that's not even counting the ones with actual bananas), with the HILARIOUS new drawing party game: SlapDash!

In SlapDash!, teams choose one Artist, who both sit together behind a standing barrier that will hide their drawings from their Guessers. They will then take 21 cards: 7 OPENERS (these are usually nouns that are capable of doing things), 7 ACTIONS (these are verbs), and CLOSERS (these are also nouns, and are usually pretty silly), and place them between them in nine piles. They then RACE to slap and claim 3 of each kind, in order to create three, 3-word silly sentences of their own that they need to draw.

They each quickly write TWO of their sentences on the backs of individual whiteboards (paper works just fine, too!), and then shuffle and hand all the cards (including the leftovers not used) to the Guessers to lay out on their side of the table and examine. The Artists then have TWO minutes to draw pictures of BOTH of their sentences! When the timer runs out, they number their drawings, then turn them around to face their guessers!

The screen is lifted, and the guessers now have ONE minute to put the various cards together in 3-word sentences on their two 'SENTENCE STRUCTURER' mats, to match what they think the artists intended! "Wait, is that snake BITING Humpty Dumpty, or EATING Humpty Dumpty? Is that Knobbly Pickle CRAWLING on a Bag of Frozen Peas, or RIDING a Bag of Frozen Peas?" When done, the Artists finally flip their pictures over to reveal their sentences. If the guesses match, points are awarded to that team for each correct guess! Also, the guesser who finished ALL their sentences FIRST is also eligible for bonus points! Is it worth 'slapping-together' your sentences as fast as you can, or should you take your time to make sure you get them right? You decide!

Each round, new artists and guessers race to get the matches they need to be the first team to get to 10 points! But skilled artists and speedy readers beware! At the end of each round, the team that's behind on points can acquire tokens that may give their next round's artists HEAD STARTS, FIRST CHOICE of cards, or even allow them to SWAP a card with their opponent!

The game plays with as many people as you can fit around a table, with teams helping by shouting out their own guesses while the two Guessers furiously grab and place the cards! It even comes with a two-player CO-OP MODE, where you and a partner build, draw, and exchange cards to each other, and then see how many you can get right in only THIRTY SECONDS! Check your score after four rounds to see where you rank!

This DEMO VERSION, available as a PRINT N' PLAY, contains 75 cards for each of the 3 categories (that works out to over 350,000 POSSIBLE COMBINATIONS!), templates for the 'SENTENCE STRUCTURER' mats, and rules/instructions which fit neatly onto both sides of the PLAYER SCREEN, which you can make simply with a piece of poster board! Grab a pad of paper and a couple of pencils (or some whiteboards and markers), and you're ready to go!

—description from the designer

New Record! Continue? is a kind of go-out game, where players aim to be the first to get rid of all their cards.

Players may play any number of cards of the same rank at once. After that, each subsequent player must either play the same number of cards with a higher rank, or play one more card of any rank.

However, the game isn’t just about competing with other players. Each player also keeps a personal record of how many cards they’ve played together as a set.

Each of these recorded cards counts as points.
But — a new card can only be recorded if it’s played with a higher number than the one already placed there. In other words, only when you set a new record.

For example, if you play two 7s as a pair, one of them is placed in your personal area as your record for “two-card plays.”
Later, to update your record for two-card plays, you must play a pair with a higher number than 7 — that is, any pair from 8 to 13.

By steadily updating your personal records, you earn points — so you’ll want to play your weaker cards little by little, in order.
However, once a set number of players have emptied their hands, the game suddenly ends, and all remaining cards in your hand become negative points equal to their face value!

If you play too fast, you won’t have any points.
If you play too slowly, the penalties will pile up.
Everyone must balance between their own timing and the pace of their opponents, searching for the line that yields the highest score.

The zombie apocalypse has happened. Separated from family and friends, you are trying to make your way to safety. Pursued by a zombie horde, you need to travel from safe house to safe house, fighting zombies and scavenging weapons as you go. Now, it’s time to run.

Run Time: Zombie is card-based survival card game for 1 player. The player makes decisions on card placement across immediate actions (player movement and weapon collection) and future encounters (fighting zombies and resolving random events). Dice are used for combat during zombie encounters. Travel across locations while trying to reach the next safe zone and search for a safe house.

In addition to the PnP cards, you will need a full set of dice (d4, d6, d8, d10, d10 percentile, d12, d20) and two different coloured meeples (or tokens). [d10 percentile is a d10 with 10, 20, 30, 40, 50, etc. sides.]

—description from the designer

Cucumber Catastrophe is a fast, highly interactive trick-taking card game for 3–6 players, playable from age 8+.

Players compete over four hands of ten rounds each. A round is a trick where all players, in turn, play one card from their hand. 
The one who played the highest card (or the lowest, depending on the Rule in play) wins the trick and takes all the cards played that round. 
During the game, certain actions can break the Rule, reversing the outcome between highest/lowest trick. 
At the end of the game, the player who has collected the fewest cucumbers is the winner.

The shifting Rule system creates constant surprises, frequent reversals, so that no two games play out the same way.

—description from the publisher

Szólicit ("Word Auction") is a fast-paced, interactive family word game in which players compete to list as many items as possible within a given category, under pressure and in a fun “auction” type format. An electronic module (sound and light) serves as the game master, announces categories (e.g., “pizza toppings”), manages bids among players, times the round, and awards points. Players take turns bidding on how many items they believe they can list in that category; once bidding stops, the highest bidder must attempt to fulfill their claim. If successful, they score; if not, the other players benefit. With over 300 themes included, and two modes (Junior and Family), the game is designed to support both children and adults in fast-thinking, creativity and word-listing under pressure.

—description from the publisher (translated)

What Did You Say? is an irreverent and humorous party game for adults where players compete to create the funniest or most outrageous sentence fragments by combining cards. In each round, a “question” or prompt card is revealed, and each player plays one of their response cards. The group then votes on which answer is the best (or worst), awarding the round’s win accordingly. The game’s tone is bold, often provocative and designed to spark laughter, surprise and social interaction in informal adult gatherings (e.g., house parties, friendly get-togethers).

—description from the publisher (translated)

In Az Évad ("The Season"), players take on the role of event organizers at a major cultural institution. They plan and execute a season of performances, managing resources such as budget, time, and professional connections. Players must select which performers to hire, respond to their occasionally demanding requests, and make strategic decisions to maximise success. The game features 100 performer cards, 30 audience‐success cards, 35 challenge cards, 16 player cards, 4 player boards, 1 extra advertising board, 1 season theme card, 72 applause tokens, and a season evaluation pad.

—description from the publisher (translated)
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Rugózz Ufó ("Spring up UFO"), players assume the role of little aliens equipped with a spring-powered suction-cup UFO tool. A scattered arrangement of cards lies on the floor, each bearing a point value. On their turn (or concurrently), players launch their UFO tool to pick up as many cards as possible before time (or turns) run out. The player with the highest total points wins. The fast-paced, physical nature emphasises hand-eye coordination, reflexes and concentration.

—description from the publisher (translated)

40 card expansion that adds new spell classes:
Potions, Familiars, Tomes, Legends and Fates

These Arcana are unique, powerful, game changing single use additions to your normal spells that can only be activated when certain conditions are met.

Igaz vagy Hamis Extra ("True or False Extra") is a fast-paced, electronic trivia and reflex game designed for the whole family. Players test their general knowledge, quick thinking, and reaction time as they decide whether each statement announced by the interactive electronic host is true or false.

The game unit acts as the "quizmaster": it announces questions aloud, tracks time, flashes lights, and gives feedback through sounds. Each player has a button or area to press their answer as quickly as possible. The device recognizes who responded first and awards points accordingly.

The game features more than 1,000 statements divided into thematic groups (nature, science, history, sports, entertainment, and even Hungarian-specific facts).

—description from the publisher (translated)

Le Jeu du Pays Avallonnais is an original, local and user-friendly board game based on the principle of questions and answers.
About 500 written and 240 photos questions with also charades and riddles, "junior" questions which deal with subjects as varied as history or geography, heritage, local traditions, current events or famous people who have marked the Pays Avallonnais.

Rules:
Each player places their token in their municipality of residence or their chosen town.
Using the die, you will travel the territory answering questions on various topics. If you get the right answer, you can play again!
To win, you will need to collect 5 "Site" cards by correctly answering the question.

—description from the publisher

Mohó mókus ("Greedy Squirrel") is a lively, action-based children’s game where players roll a color die and feed their hungry squirrel with the matching colored acorn. As the squirrel’s belly fills, its eyes begin to close—and when it finally “burps” or “spits out” the acorns, a chaotic scramble ensues as players dash to gather as many acorns as possible. The first player to fill their secret den with the required number of acorns wins.

This game emphasizes quick reflexes, observation (watching the squirrel’s eyes), and fine motor skills as players scoop up scattered acorns. Designed for young learners (ages 4 and up), it combines simple rules with fast-paced play and has strong replay value thanks to its physical components and unpredictable moments.

Ideal for family game nights or younger children, Greedy Squirrel delivers joyful and frantic fun in short bursts, making it perfect for a quick play session filled with laughter and excitement.

—description from the publisher

Lufigyűjtők ("Balloon Catchers") is a vibrant child-friendly game where players race to collect balloons of six different colors. On their turn, a player draws cards, and if the card depicts a new balloon color not yet in their collection, they may stop and redeem the card for a balloon token. However, if they draw a duplicate color or an action card when risking a further draw, they lose the drawn cards and the turn passes.

With its simple mechanics, the game introduces young children to decision-making and risk management: the player must decide whether to stick with what they have or push for more and possibly lose everything. The colorful balloon tokens and thematic “balloon holder” board engage kids visually while they learn about color matching. The first player to complete a set of six distinct colored balloons wins.

Designed for 2–4 players aged 4 and up, Balloon Catchers runs about 20 minutes and is ideal for preschoolers and their families looking for a quick, fun, and development-friendly game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

This expansion introduces 3 Share points for the player who manages to gather the largest audience.

Each time an Audience card is awarded (starting from the first), the player who gains or equals the highest number of identical influence symbols takes control of the Microphone. At the end of the game, the player holding the Microphone earns 3 Share points.

—description from the publisher

Ant Catapult invites players to launch tiny ant hats using a catapult, aiming them into numbered holes with varying point values. Each player gets four hats to shoot; landing in the correct colored hole for your hat doubles the points earned. The player accumulating the highest total after their shots wins.

This physical dexterity game is ideal for young children and families: the simple action of launching hats makes it accessible, while aiming for higher-value holes adds a bit of friendly competition. The colorful design and quick-to-learn rules make it perfect for short play sessions or as a warm-up game.

—description from the publisher (translated)

BrainBox: Tenger mélyén ("BrainBox: Under the Sea") is a fun and educational memory game that challenges players to recall details from beautifully illustrated cards depicting marine life and underwater scenes. Players study a card for 10 seconds, then roll a die to determine which of the six questions on the back they must answer from memory — questions about animals, objects, or environmental features found under the sea.

If the answer is correct, the player keeps the card; otherwise, it returns to the box. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins. The quick 10-minute gameplay and simple rules make it perfect for families and classrooms.

The game develops observation skills, memory, and knowledge of marine biology, all while offering colorful, engaging artwork and cooperative learning moments.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Abszolút szabad válaszok ("Absolute Free Answers") is a card-based party game where players engage in open, humorous, and thought-provoking conversations prompted by a deck of questions. The cards invite players to reflect and share on topics like “If you could become something else, what would you choose?” or “Who in the group lies the least?” With over a hundred questions plus mission cards and transformation cards, players are encouraged to express themselves freely and connect with each other.

The game emphasizes interaction and sharing rather than competition — there are no winners or losers. Variants include simple question rounds or expanded play with missions, individual and group tasks, and transformation cards. The game supports large groups (up to 25 players) and runs about an hour, making it ideal for family evenings, friendships gatherings, or even classroom settings.

—description from the publisher (translated)

This promo adds two additional super-spies, Angela Bug and Alexis Wall, to the game, with the following powers:

Angela Bug: After moving, Angela can place or move her Jammer token onto a square within her line of sight. Super-spies (allies or enemies) on that square cannot use their abilities. Each player has only one Jammer token.

Alexis Wall: When moving, if Alexis is on an edge (left or right), he can move from one side of the board to the other. This allows him to capture an opposing super-spy.

Tampa Bay is a strategic card game for two players based on the classic principles of Offiziersskat and Bauernskat – but reinterpreted in a modern, accessible form. With only 40 cards, the game manages to capture the essence of these traditional classics: tactics, calculating points and skilful card play. It is easy to learn, but impressively deep – a duel that will delight both beginners and experienced strategists alike.

The aim of the game is to collect more coins. The game ends when all 40 cards have been played (20 tricks).
Ten cards are face down and ten cards are face up on top of them. The non-dealer starts and plays any card. Then the player who won the last trick always plays next.
Only face-up cards may be played. If a face-up card is played, the card underneath it must be turned over after this trick.
Each card has coins = points printed on it.
The player who has collected the most coins wins the game.

boardgame: Syncanite Foundation: Irresistible Cookies Promo Card
description: A new Influence card that allows you to bribe a player, preventing him to conduct any operations against you during this phase.

In 1544 Portuguese sailors became the first Europeans to visit Taiwan and they named it Ilha Formosa meaning beautiful island. Formosa is still used today as a nickname for Taiwan.

Travel around Taiwan in this Throw and Write game visiting temples, hiking and relaxing at waterfalls. Spend time cleaning up trash on beaches but plan your trip to minimize travel to and from different regions. Each movement on the map creates one pollution subtracting points from your score.

Formosa Formosa is a challenging roll and write game for casual gamers to play with their family or friends.

T.C.M. Grandões da Piquinim is coming to the streets of Olinda!

Troças is a cooperative (or solo) game inspired by traditional carnival gatherings in Olinda, Pernambuco, Brazil. People are arriving non-stop, and your mission is to organize them into three different groups, maintaining the diversity of the celebration.

With an original card movement mechanism, every turn is essential to achieving victory! All the cards not only represent what each person is bringing to their group, but also where the next card should be played. You win when you get 3 pairs of equal items in each Troça, but if you get a set of 3 equal items, you lose immediately.

Game #05 of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the publisher

In the heart of rural Brazil, nobody escapes a mamona fight!

Guerrinha de Mamona is a duel game full of cunning and intuition, inspired by the classic Brazilian child game of throwing thorny seeds of castor bean (or 'mamona, in Portuguese). Predicting your opponent's moves is essential to avoid getting hit in the face with a seed – and still manage to attack by surprise!

Each person has their own set of action cards that can better prepare you for the fight or hinder your opponent. But if you get hit by a mamona, all is not lost: you activate your unique ability to come out on top in the next fight.

Game #06 of the Piquinim Collection.

—description from the publisher

Get ready for a holly jolly good time filled with reindeer doodles, wobbly Santas, and more giggles than a roomful of elves!

Ho ho ho! Everyone plays at once in this joyful, festive edition of Six Second Scribbles!

You all have ONE minute to draw up to TEN Christmassy things from your card. But not every doodle will do! To win points, your friends and family must be able to guess WHAT you drew.
Six Second Scribbles Christmas is all about finding the perfect balance between quality and quantity. It’s amazing how fast a minute vanishes and how much can be conveyed with a rapid scrawl of a pencil… or how little!

The perfect festive party game for children, teenagers & adults - Six Second Scribbles Christmas is guaranteed to bring fun, frenzy & some very questionable art!

A Competitive grilling game where you chose your grill, draft for your tools, search for your grillables, and manipulate your plate to provide grilled perfection to one of 6 judges. Grill Masters must manage their grill space, fixin's, grillables, and fuel all while dealing with inclement weather, ill-mannered competitors, and a myriad of distraction in this face-paced board and card game cook-off!

How to play: 
Separate cards into decks and shuffle. Placing the shuffled decks face down with the exception of FUEL cards. Decks are as
follows:
Tools and Sabotage
Events and Weather
Grillables and Fixins’
Judges
And 3 separate FUEL card decks: Deforestation Chips, GreenHouse Gas, and Black Lung Briquettes.

Flip over the top card of the Judges deck, this judge’s special rule applies for the remainder of the Judging Cycle.

Starting Hands
Each player now draws 5 cards from the Grillables and Fixin’s deck and may keep all 5.

Players now draw two cards from the Tools and Sabotage deck, they may keep one and discard the other.

Choose Your Grill
Players choose one of the available grills to use for the remainder of the game and place their grill mat in front of them. Not
 all grills are the same, read the individual grill instructions carefully and follow the rules for your particular grill.

The First
 Player is determined by the individual who last used a grill (Bought, Stored, Cleaned, Used, Stared at longingly...)

Phases of Play
Play progress as such:
1.Competition Begins, Judging Cycle: Determined by the drawn Judge.
a.Player 1: Prep Phase/Action Phase
Player 1 draws one card from the Event and Weather Deck and lay it face up next to the Deck. Follow any
instructions on the card

Option to Take any free Actions: (Free actions do not need to be done in any specific order and can occur
anytime during a player’s turn)

Free Actions include:
a.Maneuvering a grillable already on the grill to another grill spot if an open spot is available
b.Adding an already claimed FUEL to your grill
c.Draw one card from EITHER the Grillables and Fixin’s deck OR Tools and Sabotage Deck

Player 1 may now take up to 3 Standard Actions
Draw one card from EITHER the Grillables and Fixin’s deck OR Tools and Sabotage Deck
Claim One FUEL card from the FUEL deck
Play One Grillable onto one of their grill slots (FUEL not required to place grillables on the grill)
Some Fixin’s can be played for free during this action, others require 1 Action to play.

If a grillable is stolen or placed in the bin, Fixin’s are lost, while cards with the Structural Fixin’s sub
name remain. Identical Structural Fixin’s do not stack.

Claim and Plate One Grillable from their grill
Play a Tool or Sabotage Card from their hand
Play a Fixin’s card onto a grillable already on the grill or already plated (depending on rules on the individual
Fixin’s card)

Player(s) 2 and following follow the above turn progression, but DO NOT draw from the Event and Weather Deck

All Players: Cook Phase
All players add HEAT tokens to the grillables on their respective grills in accordance with their grill’s specific
rules in addition to any affects of weather or sabotage. Grills requiring FUEL, without FUEL, do not add HEAT.

Player 2 now becomes player 1 and draws then flips a card from the Event and Weather Deck and everyone follows
with their turns.

Repeat the cycle until game is finished with each players taking a turn as player 1.

HOW TO WIN
Once a player has successfully claimed and plated 6 grillables, every player has one more turn, in turn order, before the
Competition official ends, and Judging begins.

Judging: Players tally individual points for claimed grillables, then subtract points for any burnt bits in their bin.

Keep in mind special rules depending on the Judge present at the time of Competition’s end.

The winner is the player with the most points.
Repeat the above game play for the determined amount of Judging Cycles (1 for a short game, 3 for a longer game), using a newly drawn Judge each
 Competition.

The player with the most Competitions won becomes the Grill Master!

Where luck isn’t a mistake… it’s your weapon.

A bold evolution of classic checkers — where every dice roll rewrites the story.
In Arcade: Checkers VIP, strategy meets adrenaline.

Two custom dice (Blue and Red) decide not just your moves, but your destiny:
recover lost pieces, steal tokens, or unleash chaos at the perfect moment.

Every token is a lifeline. Every roll, a heartbeat.
Even a beginner can defeat a master — not by luck, but by courage.

Fast. Emotional. Unpredictable.

This is not the checkers you knew…
This is the VIP experience.

—description from the designer

Cannon Ball is a French diceless game published in Jeux & Strategie #31 (1985). It is an American Football-like game were two teams of seven players have to score three times to win. The game appeared inside the magazine pages 64 and 65. The game is turn-based. The ball and the fourteen players are represented by counters moving in a playing area 15 x 13 square cases.
The playing area is divided in 3 zones, the defense zone, the median zone and the attack zone. A player must take the ball in his attack zone to bring it to the in-goal area and score.
Each turn, two players can be moved in one of the 8 directions (horizontally, vertically or diagonally) without distance limit OR a player can take the ball and pass it to a partner (in one of the 8 possible directions) successively until either scoring or clearing the ball in an empty hex.

In this globe-shaped abstract strategy board game, occupy and expand your territory with your military castle and compete for dominance on the planet.

This game utilizes magnetic pieces to allow 2-3 players to compete for area control on the 3-D magnetic game board.

Two different game modes are playable, SIX and CASTLE, allowing for different abstract strategy experiences at different player counts.

The second edition of this game included a wooden game board, and both editions were hand made in extremely limited quantities.

boardgame: Red Dragon Inn: Bravest Brew Promo Card
description:
2025 Extra Life Promo Drink Card

Card Text:
Bravest Brew
Drink
2 Alcohol
You may discard an Action Card. If you do, this entire drink (with possible Chasers) has the effect "-2 Alcohol" instead.

A Chinese-Checkers-like game, involving dice, captures, and money.

—user summary

Welcome to a social experiment in trust and temptation. The group has been awarded one million dollars to share equally—if everyone can agree to do so. But in the center of the table lies the button, daring any player to press it and claim the entire fortune for themselves.

Each round, players secretly decide whether to trust or doubt the group’s intentions. If all players trust and no one presses the button, the remaining pot is divided evenly among everyone. But if all players trust and someone presses, that player claims the entire pot for themselves.

For added tension and variety, optional Condition cards can be introduced, altering the rules, stakes, or flow of trust within the group.

Simple to learn but psychologically complex, Don’t Push the Button tests loyalty, greed, and the fragility of collective trust. How far will you go for a share of the million—and can you resist the temptation to take it all?

—description from the designer

Did you know wombat poo is shaped like cubes? It’s true! In this game you take turns stacking poo cubes of different sizes onto a teetering tower. Choose the right time to score one point per cube in the tower and exit the round. But don’t wait too long, or the tower will fall over and you’ll score nothing!

—description from the designer

An immensely popular card game, and one praised for its depth of strategy and options, is Race for the Galaxy, by Tom Lehmann. While the game has numerous interesting innovations, the one that piqued my interest was the idea that cards in this game have multiple functions; they can be spent as money, laid to the table as improvements to the player's tableau, or even turned face down as resources. Race for the Galaxy is a higher- level entry game, and one whose complex iconography can be a scarecrow to new players. Indeed, the complexity of each card would be impossible to replicate in a deck of just suits and numbers, so instead, only the decision of which function of a card to employ is explored here in Industry, a game about quickly erecting a town. The game employs what is called a positive feedback loop; early clever plays are rewarded, and the game snowballs as it progresses.

—description from the designer

This game is part of Femtitva, a collection of 10 games by Scott Huntington that were inspired by popular modern games but can be played with a traditional deck of cards.

In Industry, each player has 3 cards which can be used to build Offices (Spades), Factories (Diamonds), Housing (Hearts), and Banks (Clubs).  On your turn you pay to play cards face up into your City, discarding other cards as money.  Buildings have a base cost of $10 each, and at the end of their turn draw cards equal to the number of Offices they have.  When the final card is drawn from the deck, players get points for different buildings, and the player with the most points is the winner.

—user summary

The game parroted by Court, 7 Wonder, is designed by Antoine Bauza, and is itself a clever reimagining of an element in Richard Garfield's Magic: The Gathering. In fact, I even borrowed elements for Court from a subsequent game by Phil Walker-Harding called Sushi Go, which is a simpler version of 7 Wonders. As I have explained in the foreword, card-drafting is something seen often in collectable card games or modern board games, but has (as far as I know) never been retroactively applied to a standard deck of cards. Collectable card games are infamous for having thousands of different cards with different special abilities and effects; Court reduces that complexity, but still has some interesting options for scoring. Players are wooing the various nobles as they venture through a medieval city, enticing them to their court. The player with the most interesting combinations of folk in their court will come out on top at game's end.

—description from the designer

This game is part of Femtitva, a collection of 10 games by Scott Huntington that were inspired by popular modern games but can be played with a traditional deck of cards.

In Court, players start with a  hand of cards (9 for 2 players, 8 for 3 players, 7 for 4 players), and simultaneously pick one that they reveal, then pass the remaining cards to their left.  This is repeated until all cards from the hand are played, after which scoring happens; a game consists of three such rounds.  Points are scored for different combinations (e.g. a "Marriage" of King and Queen is worth 5 points, a "Rarity" constituting a pair of Aces is worth 3 points), and the player with the most points is the winner.

—user summary

Werewolf (or as it is sometimes called, Mafia) was always a fantastic game for me, and one I played a lot in my early university years. It is a game of two teams, but of unknown identities. It is an interesting mix of old and new design concepts; the heated discussion and bluffing generated by Werewolf is very modern; whereas the player elimination and sitting around is becoming ever more rare with each new design. One immensely popular (but lengthy) board game involving traitors is Battlestar Galactica, by Corey Konieczka. It sets players on a spaceship, trying to collectively ward off dangers, which would be easier, were it not for the traitor in the midst. I have extracted the game's clever system of sabotage-by-card, and came up with Turncoat. In a similar manner, but here on Earth, players must complete five missions successfully or succumb to the mole.

—description from the designer

This game is part of Femtitva, a collection of 10 games by Scott Huntington that were inspired by popular modern games but can be played with a traditional deck of cards.
