Le But du jeu va consister à retrouver les 2 Trésors (pièces d'or) correspondants aux 2 communes choisies au début de la partie puis à les ramener le 1er à Circino au centre du plateau.

Pour cela, les joueurs vont devoir aller fouiller les différents coffres avec comme seules armes le dé et leur sens de l'aventure (mémoire) ... en empruntant des passages secrets, des tunnels et en bénéficiant de boosters.

Dans leur quête, les joueurs ne seront pas seuls et pourront compter sur l'aide des membres de la famille Circino par le biais des cartes Bonus.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The Dresden Files Cooperative Card Game: Expansion 6 – Faithful Friends
description:
OKAY, THIS LOOKS BAD...

...but don’t despair—you’ve got faith on your side in this expansion for the Dresden Files Cooperative Card Game! Inside you’ll find two new character decks—Sir Waldo and River Shoulders—and two new book decks—Peace Talks and Battle Ground.

This expansion adds two new card types, Key and Crisis. Key cards act as Obstacles on the board, but Advantages when removed. Crisis cards score as Foes on the board and as Cases when removed.
family: Game: The Dresden Files Cooperative Card Game

description:
Agros (from Old Greek: ἀγρός, meaning “field”) is a territorial game for two players: Black and White. It is played on the squares (cells) of an initially empty square board. The recommended size is 10 cells per side. Each player has access to a sufficient supply of stackable pieces of their own color, double-sided (front = farmer, back = seed).

				
				
					Definitions:
     A farmer is a stack of one or more pieces, where the top piece is a farmer and the lower ones are seeds.
     The harvest of a farmer is the total number of pieces in the stack.
     A field is a maximal set of orthogonally connected empty cells; an isolated empty cell is also a field.
     A farmer controls a field if it is orthogonally adjacent to it and has a higher harvest than any other farmer adjacent to the same field. In case of a tie, the harvests of the next adjacent farmers are compared until it is resolved.
     The value of a field is the number of cells it contains multiplied by the harvest of the farmer that controls it.


				
				
					Turns:White plays first, and then players alternate. During the first three placement turns, each player places farmers of their color without seeds underneath, following the sequence below:


     On White’s first turn and Black’s third turn, only one farmer is placed.
     On the other placement turns, two farmers are placed.


At the end of this phase, each player will have a total of five farmers on the board, although it can also be played with three or seven farmers per player.

From the fourth turn onward, perform one of the following actions:


     Choose a farmer of your color and move it in a straight line, orthogonally or diagonally, a distance equal to its harvest. It may jump over friendly farmers or seeds but must land on an empty cell. Immediately after moving, place a seed underneath the farmer, increasing its harvest by one.
     Choose a farmer of your color and move it in a straight line, orthogonally or diagonally, to sow, leaving a lower seed on each consecutive empty cell it crosses, until only the farmer remains on the last cell traveled.


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends when both players pass on consecutive turns. At the end of the game, each player sums the value of the fields controlled by their farmers. The player whose farmer has the greatest harvest receives half a point; in case of a tie, the next greatest harvest farmers are compared until it is resolved. The player with the highest total score wins.

				
				
					Static variant:The same rules apply, except that during sowing, the farmer remains on the origin cell.

–description from designer–

Shipwrights of Marino is a strategic airship building game set in the Dew Point universe. Players take the role of competing shipyards, buying components from the ever-changing market and racing to complete contracts that earn money and power.

SETTING: Dew Point is the setting for several Crab Fragment Games, a world where an ever-rising fog threatens to cast the globe into darkness. Airships are the safest way to navigate between the various high-altitude cities, and Marino is its ancient shipbuilding capital.

GAMEPLAY: Shipwrights of Marino is a simple economic game for 2 to 5 players. Players build airships, using resources from an ever-changing marketplace: Metal, Wood, Crew, Food, Rope, and Canvas. Prices for these resources fluctuate constantly, and your goal is to buy them cheaply, and finish the game with the most profit.

Along with basic resources, you can also invest in shipping expeditions that bring resources into the Market. Investors get the best deals on new deliveries, but sometimes those ships can bring back almost nothing!

Every contract earns money and launches a ship, and many finished contracts grant a special effect or ability. Collect the right mix of abilities, and your shipyard can work faster, racing ahead of the competition.

The player with the most money when the deck is exhausted is the winner.

—description from the publisher

After the Fog is a game of exploration and expansion set in the Dew Point universe. Players are city leaders, reclaiming a valley that was once drowned in deep fog. As the fog recedes, you will build wealth and influence, and become the master of the valley.

After the Fog is an area-control game with airships, engine-building, and resource management. Players start with an airship and a base of operations. They spend the game exploring the board, collecting resources, capturing territory, and upgrading their fleets.  The game is rated for 2 to 6 players, and takes roughly 1-2 hours.

SETTING: Dew Point is the setting for several Crab Fragment Games, and is a world of Renaissance-level technology, with heavier air and lighter materials that make airships a common form of transportation. The world is plagued by an ever-rising fog, which has separated cultures and power centers as it chokes low-lying cities out of existence.  The Dearworth Valley is clearing after centuries in fog. Abandoned forests, farms, and mines are scattered throughout the countryside.

GAMEPLAY: After a starting auction in which players choose starting Cities and Characters, players take turns with each turn consisting of three steps:

     MARKET: new cards from the Market deck are revealed, which can trigger events
     MOVE: move one of your airships to a different location, and doing whatever is appropriate for that space (e.g. produce resources, or resolve conflict with another player) 
     BUY: buy one market card or pay for contracts


The end of the game is triggered when a certain number of events from the market have been triggered, after which scoring happens.  Points are earned for controlled spaces, collected cards, airships, towers, rubies, and bonuses, and the player with the most points is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Note: This game is still in progress and is still being developed by the creator, so most images in the gallery have a preliminary and not final status.  More information about the status of this game can be found at the designer's page: https://crabfragmentlabs.com/after-the-fog

Paradise Road is a simple dice racing game for 2 to 4 players, played on a South Carolina map with large and smaller cities connected by routes.

SUMMARY: Players take turns racing their pawns around a simple highway map. The first player to hit each big city and then return home is the winner.

GAMEPLAY: To begin, each player chooses a home city, and starts their pawn there. Place a token on each of the five large cities including your home city. Roll dice to determine the first player.

Each turn, players take two dice from the bank, and roll them together with any dice they have left from their last turn. You can move along any road segment by spending a matching die, or several dice that add up exactly to the length of the road. Hit all the big cities and return home to win!

—description from the publisher

Dogs, cats, and kids. They all love going for walks. Take them out for walks morning, noon, and night to collect hearts of gratitude. If you collect the most hearts, you'll see a shooting star. Let's become a Walking Master.

In Best Family Forever from Today, players race to empty their hands in this light shedding game. On your turn, you play cards that show dogs, cats, or kids. You may play a new color or more cards of the same color, depending on what has already been played. When the first and second players who emptied their hands receive Heart tokens. The first player to reach the target number of Hearts wins the game.

All 60 cards are unique and feature charming illustrations of dogs and cats. The rules are easy to learn, making it suitable for both children and adults.

—description from the designer
publishers: Mysboard Games (ミスボドゲームズ)

description:
Dice of Lies is a game of probability, bluffing and guts. Players compete to be the last one standing by guessing which dice have been rolled. As each player is eliminated, the balance of power and influence shifts from one player to the next. Players may lie and misdirect their opponents as to which dice they have rolled in order to influence each other’s guesses. The goal: be the last player standing with dice.
And remember, keep your friends close, but your enemies closer.

Roll the Dice: Everyone rolls their dice in secret, keeping their roll concealed from the other players.
Wager: The first player nominates a symbol and makes a guess of the total number of dice that have been rolled to display that symbol.
Raise or Call: After the first player makes their initial guess, the game carried on in a clockwise direction. The other players then take turns to perform one of the following actions. RAISE - The player raises the previous player's wager by either increasing the quantity of that symbol or by raising the wager to a symbol of higher rank at any new quantity. CALL - If the player believes that the previous player's guess is not true, they may call a showdown against that player. This triggers the showdown phase.
Showdown: Everyone reveals their dice and the quantity of the symbol in question is counted. The player that is incorrect must suffer a consequence determined by the symbol in question.
New Round: Once the showdown has been resolved, a new round starts from the next player in a clockwise direction.
Elimination: Once a player loses all their dice, they are out of the game and cannot re-enter.
The winner is the last player with dice.

Krepšinio fanas (“Basketball Fan”) challenges all basketball enthusiasts to bring the thrill of the court to the tabletop! Designed in collaboration with the legendary Lithuanian basketball club Žalgiris Kaunas to celebrate its 80th anniversary, the game captures the excitement, teamwork, and unpredictability of real basketball matches.
Players split into two teams and compete through four quarters of play, combining basketball knowledge, luck, and tactics to outscore their opponents. On each turn, a player rolls the die and interacts with the game board: if the die lands on an opponent’s card, the player must answer a question to score points; landing on numbered spaces awards direct points, while landing on effect spaces triggers special actions that can help or hinder the team.
Teams keep track of their progress on erasable team boards, marking points, shots, fouls, and quarters as the game unfolds. The mix of trivia, chance, and strategy ensures an exciting and social experience for basketball fans, families, and groups of friends alike.
The game includes 462 question cards, 20 block cards, 20 “unblockable shot” cards, 158 free throw cards, a game board, two team boards, two erasable markers, one die, and a rulebook. Suitable for 2–10 players, ages 12 and up. Average playtime: about 45 minutes.

Why not? is a lighthearted party game of offbeat questions and odd characters, where there is no right answer, only the one most people agree on.

In Why Not?, you will answer unlikely questions about bizarre characters, and it is important to think like the majority. In each round, a new question is generated using the Subject and Question cards. All players then choose their answer at the same time, and whoever has chosen the one that matches the majority opinion earns 1 point. 
The first player to reach the score chosen at the beginning of the game will be the winner.

In the main mode, the game is designed for 3–8 players. A cooperative variant for 2 players is also available.

—description from the publisher

In Stage Blood, you and your associates are theatrical agents, placing actors in Shakespearean plays. You will send your performers to the various popular plays of the season, hoping to win favor with the six major households, and earn renown for managing the finest actors in the land.

Stage Blood is a simple card game about managing a troupe of actors, for 2 to 6 players.

Each round, players choose one actor from their hand and send it out into the world, assigning it to one of the unfinished plays at the center of the table. Smaller actors act first, while larger ones act later. And there are no small parts, just small actors.

The game lasts for four seasons, after which players will compare their collections of favor tokens from each of six households, earning points for placing high in each one. There are some other ways to earn bonus points as well.  The player with the most points is the winner.

—description from the publisher

Bulldog is a press-your-luck gambling game for 3-8 players, using the Blossom Deck.  The game gets its name from “bulldogging,” which is paying money to stay in when your hand is not worth saving.  The game was created for a short story set in the Dearworth Valley.  In some respects, Bulldog plays the role of Poker in the Dew Point universe.

OBJECTIVE: Your goal is to winnow your hand down to the fewest points, while guessing if your opponents can do the same. Or get out early, if you think your cards can’t win!

GAMEPLAY: Players will play six rounds, collecting faceup cards in their boards. Each player starts with four cards, and will receive five more from the deck. Players receive cards one at a time, and on your turn you have the option to Fold, Pay, or Play.  When you "Play", you may cancel cards received by cancelling them with cards from their hands.  Paired cards can cancel each other automatically, and players may also play safeties or multiple cards from their hands to cancel cards from their board.

WINNING: Your final score is the cumulative pip total of the cards left on your board after six rounds, and the aim is to achieve the lowest score.  So the player who finishes with the lowest valued board is the winner, with the best possible score being zero points.

COMPONENTS:
- The Blossom Deck 
- roughly 100 chips for each player

THE BLOSSOM DECK: The Blossom Deck is a six-suited deck from James Earnest and Crab Fragment Games, and is set the Dew Point universe. The deck consists of 54 cards in six suits ranked Ace through nine. The suits are divided into three colors, each with "night" and "day" versions, allowing the deck to be split into halves, thirds, or sixths.  Two games were specifically created for the Blossom Deck: Bulldog (a gambling game for 2-8 players) and Cowards (a gambling game for 2 players).

—user summary

Ma ville à moi is a competitive city-building game where players shape one shared city to serve their own ambitions. Each turn, players play a card made up of four districts, placing it in the center of the table to cover existing parts of the city. As the city grows, everyone tries to position their cards to favor their secret objectives. When no more cards can be placed, the player who has shaped the city most to their advantage wins the game.

—description from the designer

Life is like a playlist.
We associate music with every moment of our lives... Music to help us concentrate, music to motivate us, or music that will get everyone dancing on the dance floor!

But be careful!
Your friends don't always share the same musical tastes as you... MusicMatch is THE game where you have to guess your friends' musical preferences!

Quick rules :


     4 themes are picked
     Each player secretly adds one song per theme to the playlist
     Launch the playlist !
     Guess which player is behind each song!


—description from the designer

ByeO Barelands: Mangrove & Coastal Edition is a Singapore-made strategy card game that turns real native wildlife into an ecological battleground of wit and survival. The story begins after Singapore’s decades of habitat loss — players step into the role of ecosystem architects, rebuilding food chains and defending them from rival invasions. Each of the hundred-plus cards represents a real species from Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve, drawn and designed with traits reflecting genuine ecological behavior — pollination, seed dispersal, migration, predation, and even invasive competition.

Gameplay revolves around balancing stability and diversity. Each player manages an “ecosystem” built from cards spanning five tiers of life — from plants and insects to apex predators. On each turn, players can establish species in their own food chain, invade rivals with non-native species, exchange cards, or refresh their hand. Food chains must follow valid predator-prey logic: diet, habitat, and power levels must align. Mismanage your web and species become overwhelmed or starving, collapsing your chain; plan well and you’ll create resilient ecological networks that withstand sabotage.
The goal is ecological mastery: the first player to build a full food chain from Tier 1 to Tier 5 while achieving 50 Total Score wins.

Victory demands both biodiversity (scoring breadth) and stability (chain depth). Power Cards add a chaotic twist — sudden storms, human interference, or conservation boosts — mirroring the unpredictability of nature.

Beneath its competitive energy, the game is an educational ode to Singapore’s wild spaces. It invites families, students, and gamers to not just play with the creatures of Sungei Buloh but to meet them in real life, bridging strategy with stewardship — a reminder that every mangrove, bird, and crab is part of a story we can still help rebuild.

—description from the designer

boardgame: B.E.L.O.W.: The Asylum – Berry Pomeroy Expansion
description:
Enter the haunted halls of Berry Pomeroy, a decaying castle filled with cultists, shrines, and the monstrous Human Effigy.
This full-scale gameplay expansion introduces a brand-new setting, new mechanics, Fiends, Agents, and a terrifying Monarch.

This expansion for B.E.L.O.W.: The Asylum comes with:

     22 Enemy miniatures
     2 Character miniatures
     4 Two-sided Map Tiles
     2 Character Tiles
     1 Dual-layer Large Ritual Ring
     1 Dual-layer Smal Ritual Ring
     1 Character Personal Shimmer track
     2 Alternative Berry Pomeroy Grimoires
     2 Oversize Cards
     115+ Standard Cards
     5 Shrine tokens

Great abilities always entail great responsibility. What if you, too, are destined to experience the bite of a mutated spider? Or today you'll find a mushroom unknown to science in a salad or be exposed to an innovative microwave oven? What superpower has fate in store for you? It is possible that you will become the first superhuman capable of dividing by zero or hypnotizing yourself.

Superhumans is a highly anticipated continuation of the Comparity line of games. 100 ordinary people helped make this game. They were given a list of strange talents and asked, 'Which ones would you consider a gift from above and which would be a curse?' Depending on the answers, each superpower was assigned a 'desire index' from zero (no one wants to live with such a mutation) to 93 (the most coveted superpower).

At the beginning of the game, you already have two open cards with abilities. To win, you need to collect a series of nine cards — from a smaller desire index to a larger one. Take a card from a closed deck and try to guess where in your row to put it. If you don't get it right, pass the card to the next one.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Battle of the Coral Sea

May, 1942. Only five months earlier, the Kido Butai launched an overwhelming attack on Pearl Harbor. The Japanese sunk four battleships, damaged four, destroyed 188 aircraft, and killed 2000 sailors and a hundred Marines.

Since then, the Empire of the Rising Sun has expanded almost without resistance. Now, intelligence intercepts indicate a Japanese invasion of Port Moresbey.  The only two aircraft carriers available to the Allies to stop this advance are the Yorktown and the Lexington. The Hornet and the Enterprise are still returning from the Doolittle raid on Tokyo.

Will you be able to stop the Japanese?

Game play of this version of the Combat Boots Underway is a tactical naval board game that deals with the interplay between aircraft and ships. Players take turns via IGO-UGO phases, moving units one at a time. There are possible reaction moves.

There is a map board where hexes are 100 miles across and a tactical board where the scale drops to 7 miles per hex. Ships are rated for Range, Speed, Anti-Air , Anti-sub, Surface action ability and survivability. Planes are rated for Maneuver, Armament Firepower, Speed and Survivability.

Draw a card, and the ferret will act to fulfill its abstract plans. Sometimes, it will help you by chasing away the monsters. Other times, it will snatch your precisely placed Ritual components to play with them somewhere else.

This mini-expansion for B.E.L.O.W.: The Asylum comes with:

     1 Ferratic Entity miniature
     10 Ferratic behavior cards

Blimey! You and your blokes have been banished and stripped of everything but a few shillings and table scraps. Undaunted, you journey to a strange land to reinvent yourself and reclaim your honor. Will you farm the earth, fight as knights or finagle your own feudums?

Feudum (latin for fiefdom) is an economic medieval game of hand and resource management for 1-6 players. With many strategies at their disposal, players optimize four actions per turn in attempt to score the most victory points over five epochs.

In celebration of Feudum's 7th anniversary, The Septennial Edition comes with:

     Metal Clockwork Behemoth
     Feudum Septennial Card Sleeves (50)
     Art Print Mini Replica
     Embroidered Septennial Haversack
     Septennial Turn Order Coin
     Flying Epoch Marker in Copper
     Septennial Strategy Cards (5)
     Three Punchboard Coins in "10s" (3)
     Score Track Counters (6)
     Royal Writ Compendium
     Royal Writ Cards (15)
     ''Alter Ego'' Expansion
     ''The Queen’s Army'' Expansion

Step into the boots of a pirate and take a seat in Tortuga — the famous pirate port café — where you can risk your hard-earned treasures!
Be careful: as quickly as your fortune came, you might lose it just as fast at the pirate roulette.
But with clever tactics and a bit of luck, you can pocket a hefty reward in this fast-paced game of chance.
Be the richest pirate of them all!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Add two highly asymmetric Agents to your roster. Each comes with unique mechanics and upgrade paths

This expansion for B.E.L.O.W.: The Asylum comes with:

     3 Character miniatures
     2 Character Tiles
     26 Standard Cards
     12 Adaptive Attribute Tracks


—description from the publisher

Do you like delicious food and spending time with friends and family? You don’t have the skills of an experienced chef, but would you like to get together again to create unforgettable memories? Gather your family and/or friends around the table, because today you are invited to… play!

Discover the Food Bluff Card Game – a new fun, engaging and addictive card game that will challenge you to create your own strategy so that you can finish the game first! And no, it’s not a tasting game, but it’s an interactive game that will bring your taste buds to life. Now it’s up to you whether you play it safe or bluff!

The Food Bluff card game for young and old, from 7 years old and up, trains 2 to 10 players in a tasty adventure among the 96 illustrated playing cards with appetizing dishes, delicious desserts, sauces and toppings that bring taste, balance and a special aroma to the dishes.

Have we whetted your appetite? Then let's go to the table - to the game table!

Unlock the secret pasts of each B.E.L.O.W. Agent. This add-on allows you to explore the rich backstories and motivations of each character, offering a more immersive and personalized gameplay experience.

Each Agent gains its own Achievement sheet with thematic goals they try to complete during a standard game of Below.

When a goal is completed, you gain access to a piece of that Agent’s story. Those may be memoirs from their early life, tales of how they gained their powers, or stories about their beginnings in Below. Or, such as is the case with John Doe, something truly out of the box.

With each achievement completed, a new card is added to your future playthroughs. It gets shuffled into the standard Ability cards advancement deck increasing your options, and not impacting the overall balance of the game.

This expansion comes with an exclusive personal story for each Agent, a goals sheet, and a pack of unlockable Ability cards. During your standard game, fulfill challenging goals to unlock a background story of your Agent and a special, thematically connected Ability card that can then be used in any future playthrough.

—description from the publisher

Big Apple is a fishing card game in the style of Scopa or Golden Pig, except designed for playing with a very young child. The deck is composed of 33 cards which each have one, two, or three large symbols on them. Players take turns playing these cards, and collecting all face-up cards that match at least one symbol. The only things a child needs to be able to do to play Big Apple is:


     Choose a card from a hand of 3
     Identify any matching symbols between their card and those that are face-up on the table. 


There is some additional scope for clever play by older children or adults.

There are nine different symbols on the cards: Apple, Banana, Cake, Dog, Elephant, Fish, Gemstone, Hat, and Ice Cube. Each of these symbols appear 6 times total in the deck, twice on single symbol cards, one on double symbol cards, and twice on triple single cards. There is one single symbol card which features a very large apple.

—description from the designer

2066. It's been years since the apocalypse hit, and your only goal is to survive. Wastelands, looted cities, and the ever-present threat of zombies.

But in the last few months, hope has returned. You heard an announcement on the radio saying that a huge bunker might still be able to take in a few survivors. That is, if you can pay your way in and get there alive…

Road to the Bunker is a game where you must build, tile by tile, the most efficient path to lead you to the Bunker while trying to hinder the progress of the other survivors.

You can choose to explore your own zone to gather as many resources as possible, anticipate challenges, and adapt to the obstacles set by other players, or take wicked pleasure in sabotaging your opponents.

A highly interactive game full of twists and surprises!

—description from the publisher

Combat! Crete is a stand-alone solitaire game of man-to-man combat in WWII.  The player commands a squad of British Commonwealth troops or German Fallschirmjäger in various engagements.  The included scenarios are set on the islands of Crete and Leros but full-war OOBs are included for both factions.

A comprehensive Random Scenario Generator further expands the gaming possibilities and will generate a limitless supply of unique scenarios.  The Random Scenario Generator is self-contained, or can be combined with Combat! Eastern Front and/or Combat Tunisia & Sicily to create an even more expansive Combat! experience.

In addition, the included Campaign Game allows the player to lead a squad of men through 10 battles, gaining experience along the way.

Other new features in Combat! Crete include:
Off Board Artillery rules including air support
New Terrain types including Vineyards, Weapon Pits, and an Airfield
Parachute Drops, with a massive four map paradrop scenario
Australians, New Zealanders, and Maoris for extra fun with the Commonwealth OOB
Scenarios on Leros which feature Fallschirmjäger armed with FG42s
Competitive Play rules for head to head battles
Armed Cretan Civilians give the Fallschirmjägers a nasty welcome

—description from the designer

Romance vs The World is the party game for book lovers!  Celebrate your love of romance novels with other readers.  Blush, laugh, and celebrate an incredible genre.


Each player gets 6 pink cards.  The first judge is chosen and pulls a black card.  Other players select a pink card to play in response.  Responses can be funny, playful, serious, accurate, or scandalous.  The better you know someone, the better you can pick a card to play!  The judge shuffles the responses and reads them aloud before choosing a winner.  Winner keeps the black card, then play proceeds clockwise.  The first person to get 5 black cards wins!

Brécourt Manor simulates the famous action of June 6th, 1944, in which American paratroopers, primarily from E Company of the 2nd Battalion of the 506th Regiment, destroyed a German artillery battery firing on Utah Beach.

The system uses AI to make for intense combat situations as well as unlimited replays. As the paratroopers’ commander, you will attack a determined foe. Can you succeed?

The game has four scenarios and is designed as an introduction to the Combat! series.

Combat! Brécourt Manor is part of Paper Wars Issue #114.  The magazine also includes the complete Combat! Series Rules, so that gamers who have Brécourt Manor have everything they need to begin enjoying the Combat! game system.

Design new neighborhoods with the first expansion for Cities! It includes 11 new city achievement tiles and the mini-expansion Theaters. The 11 City achievement tiles show two different cities on both sides from Hungary (Budapest, Kecskemét), Germany (Hamburg, Berlin), France (Paris, Nancy), Czech Republic (Brno, Prague), Netherlands (Antwerp, Amsterdam), Russia (Moscow, Saint Petersburg), South Korea (Busan, Seoul), Poland (Warsaw, Kraków), China (Peking, Shanghai), Chile (Santiago), Italy (Rome), and Japan (Tokyo, Osaka).

The mini-expansion Theaters includes 4 theater tokens. At the time of taking tiles from the Feature tile row, a player may discard one of the Monument or Special feature tiles just taken to take a Theater tile from the supply instead (a player may take only 1 theater token per game). Then they can place it on a city space in their city. At game end, you score 1 point for each building in the same row and/or column as the theater token that matches the theater’s color.

—description from the publisher

A strategy and adventure game for the whole family. Increase your wealth and fight for influence by building sawmills and mines. Gain an advantage overyour opponents in open combat by seizing their property or plundering their cities. Luck plays a huge role in the success of your mission, but your ability to assess the situation and invest wisely is crucial to your success. Prove that you are a better strategist and host than your opponents and win the castle and the crown.

Game objective

The goal of the game is to build your castle and then be the first to reach the throne room in the centre of the board. 
Once a player has built a castle and reached the central space (throne room), they win.

Game Overview

Players compete to collect resources, build their castle, and reach the throne room in the center of the board. The first to do so wins.

How to Play

On your turn, you either roll the die (1–3) to move your pawn or spend resources to build.

When you land on resource spaces (gold mines, forests), you claim them with flags and gain gold or wood.

Crossing special lines or spaces makes you draw “Fate” cards — green (good luck) or red (bad luck).

You can buy building cards using wood and gold to develop your city.

Some spaces or actions let you plunder or take over opponents’ areas (luck and chance play a role here).

Building & Fighting

Building gives advantages and is required to complete your castle.

Fighting or plundering occurs when you land on opponents’ claimed areas — the rules are simple and luck-based (no complex dice battles).

Winning the Game

A player wins by:

Building their Castle, then

Reaching the throne room in the center of the board before anyone else.

—description from the publisher

Loose is a microgame similar to rock-paper-scissors, but played with 9 cards. There are several ways to play.

The common rules are : Pink beats Blue, Blue beats Yellow, Yellow beats Pink.
Within a faction, circles beat triangles, triangles beat squares, and squares beat circles.

Game 1 : Play the cards two by two until the final pair decides the winner.
Game 2 : Within an initial hand of 4 cards, play them all one by one and get a point for each round won.
Game 3 : With all of them face up, take turns choosing cards one by one, then align your 4 cards face down to battle automatically.
Game 4 : The author encourages you to invent your own rules and share them on social media.

boardgame: Generals of Kaladan: Age of Crowns
description:
In Generals of Kaladan: Age of Crowns, players command one of six realms - Khalisia, Kimbria, Laurielle, Nanchia, Ardanor, and Zhojira - each with distinct terrain bonuses, signature units, and tactical approaches in a battle of 2-6 players to be the last crown standing.

Turns unfold in a clear rhythm: Event Card, resolve gold and free units, move or attack, resolve magic and combat, then reinforce.

Battles are quick and lethal, using dice for six unit types (Crown, General, Catapult, Archer, Shield, Sword in addition to a special realm unit) to keep conflict decisive and positioning meaningful.

This is a 3X (Expand, Exploit and Exterminate) game in basic mode and 4x in advanced mode with world builder and fog of war allowing you to Explore!

Components include modular terrain tiles, units, magic cards, reference sheets, event cards, realm cards and dice (D6–D12).

Recommended age 8+, playtime 30–120 minutes depending on player count and scenario.

—description from the designer

Active Snap takes one of the simplest, best-loved card games and gives it a shake-up. It works like traditional Snap but there’s a twist. Players take turns to turn over a card and the quickest player to shout ‘snap!’ when the same card appears twice gets to grab all the cards. But here’s the best bit: when you win a round at Active Snap, all the other players have to do what the cards say.

Each funny, colorful card is beautifully illustrated with an activity – you might have to jog on the spot for 40 seconds, perform 20 sit-ups, get through 40 squats without falling over, do the plank to everyone else’s satisfaction, practice some star jumps, or shadow box like you mean it. One card might even have everyone on their feet dancing at once! Each card will tell you how long or how many times you have to exercise for. The game continues in rounds and the last player left with any cards (and dignity) wins.

Active Snap is the keep-fit card game that’s not so much about who wins as the laughs along the way. Quick to learn and easy to enjoy, everyone will want to play and play. The fun fits into your pocket so you can take it to the park, to school, to the beach and anywhere in between. Sunny days or rainy days, a game of Active Snap will get everyone, young and old, off the couch and moving.

Contains 50 cards and instructions

—description from the publisher

Fans of Jurassic World Rebirth will love playing with this themed UNO card game. With a deck featuring graphics inspired by favorite characters and scenes, players try to match colors and numbers -- there's even a special 'Attack of the T. Rex' rule that will have somebody drawing five cards! And when down to a last card, don't forget to snarl 'UNO!' Makes a great gift for fans of Jurassic World Rebirth and card game lovers ages 7 years old and up. Colors and decorations may vary.

     The UNO game players love features graphics inspired by characters from Jurassic World Rebirth.
     Game play matches the classic card game where players match colors and numbers to get rid of all their cards.
     The special 'Attack of the T. Rex' rule has players trying to match a chosen color with cards in their hand or they must draw 5 cards!
     When down to one card, don't forget to yell, 'UNO!'
     Designed for 2 to 10 players ages 7 years and older, this card game makes a great gift for fans of Jurassic World Rebirth!
     Includes 112 cards, 1 collectible foil card and instructions.

Welcome to Lunar Cafe - the one and only ice cream shop on the Moon!
In this whimsical board game, space travelers from all over the galaxy come here to enjoy delicious scoops of ice cream. 
And guess what? You are one of the Moon Rabbits working at the cafe!

Your job is to craft and serve tasty ice cream to all kinds of fun customers - like astronauts from Earth, silly aliens from faraway planets, and even royalty from distant civilizations! But be careful - things can get busy!

Every customer wants different flavors. Sometimes there are too many people in the shop, or you might run out of ingredients!



The game has 4 phase
1. Event phase - open event cards follow to the event and refill ice-cream scoop 2. Market phase - By turn order, each player may trade up to 3 cards in thier hand with the market and then draw 5 cards from ingredient deck.

3. Action phase - Each player by turn order play ingredients follow to the formula and then submit icecream scoop to customers card and receive score.

4. Clean up phase - return remaining Customers bach to bottom of the deck and draw the new one. Re-arrange turn order from score (least score to first) and move round marker to the next.

After 6 rounds or the customers deck run outs the game end.
Add bonus score from the symbol in the customers card who has the most score is winner!

In Phantasms, players draw geometric shapes to form clues that other players must correctly guess. However, disguised ghosts want to sabotage the investigation, either by drawing disorderly shapes or giving incorrect guesses, taking care not to be discovered, otherwise they will be ignored. The winner is whoever gets 3 clues first (Investigators - 3 clues found or Ghosts - 3 clues lost).

The Dance Ensemble Card Game not only showcases the authentic Hungarian folk costumes of the Carpathian Basin, but also offers an exciting and entertaining experience for everyone.
Playable in about 30 minutes, for 2–5 players, and enjoyable from age 8 and up!

Game Objective

In this card game, created in collaboration with the Hungarian State Folk Ensemble, each player becomes the leader of their own folk dance group.
Your goal is to build the most successful folk dance ensemble in the country.
To achieve this, you’ll need to recruit dancers with the right skill levels, send them to perform, and collect the most points by the end of the game.
Of course, it’s not as simple as it sounds!

How to Play

At the start of the game, each player receives four dancer cards at random.
In each round, players can recruit new dancers — provided they have enough money to have new costumes tailored for them.

Your dancers can participate in various events depending on their skill level, earning you popularity points and performance fees.
You can use these fees to commission new costumes for future dancers, whom you can then recruit into your ensemble and send to perform as well.

It’s up to you to decide whom to hire and which events to attend — this is how your ensemble grows and becomes more and more popular across the country.

Show everyone your skills in team-building and organization!
Created together with the Hungarian State Folk Ensemble, the game guarantees professional authenticity — and no prior dance experience is required!

•••

A táncegyüttes kártyajáték miközben bemutatja a Kárpát-medence autentikus magyar népvisleteit, izgalmas játékélménnyel ajándékozz meg mindenkit. 30 perc alatt, 2-5 főig, akár 8 éves kortól bárki örömét lelheti benne!
A játék célja
A Magyar Állami Népi Együttessel közösen készített kártyajátékban mindannyian a saját együttesetek vezetői lesztek. Feladatotok az ország legsikeresebb néptáncegyüttesének felépítése. Ehhez megfelelő tudásszintű táncosokat kell felvennetek az együttesbe, szereplésekre küldenetek őket és a játék végére a legtöbb pontot összegyűjteni. A feladat csak látszólag ilyen egyszerű.
A játék menete
Kezdéskor minden játékos véletlenszerűen kap 4-4 táncoskártyát a kezébe, akikhez minden körben újabbakat tud felvenni, ha viseletének varratására van pénze. Táncosaitok tudásszintjüktől függően vehetnek részt egyes eseményeken, amiért népszerűség pontot és tiszteletdíjat kaptok. A tiszteletdíjból varrattok viseletet új táncosoknak, akiket az együttesbe felvéve szintén szereplésekre küldhettek. Ti döntitek el kit vesztek fel az együttesbe és melyik szereplésre külditek el. Így fejlődik és lesz egyre népszerűbb együttesetek az országban. Mutassátok meg, hogy Ti mire vagytok képesek közösségépítés, szervezés terén!
A Magyar Állami Népi Együttes garancia a szakmai minőségre és nem szükséges hozzá előzetes képzettség.

Beat the Camera is not your average board game. It's a nerve-jangling quest to find a precious gemstone! To get to the gem, you’ll have to beat your friends and beat the camera.

Here’s how it goes: for a simple game, place the gemstone on its stand so players can see it, then push the Play button on the camera. Sound effects will start the countdown as players get to their positions before the camera’s beady eyes start to sweep the room, scanning the space from side to side. When the sounds and the camera stop, FREEZE! Don’t move a muscle or the camera’s invisible beam will detect you, the alarm will go off and you’ll be out of the game!

Keep playing until one player can grab the gemstone, get back to the camera to stop it – without being spotted – and win! For a longer game, players can head back to their starting places without stopping the camera. A great addition to your collection of family games, this kids' game can also be easily played solo. Or, for the ultimate hide and seek game challenge, ask one player to conceal the gemstone and get all the others to hunt it down without being nabbed by the camera’s roving eye. First to find it wins the game!

If you're ready for something different from the usual board games, Beat the Camera is here to bring extra excitement to your family game night!

—description from the publisher

Numerical from TOMY Games is a numbers game full of surprises. Go head to head or do battle with up to four players. You’ll each start with 10 tiles picked out at random from the bag and hidden from others on your rack. On your turn, see if you can start to build Runs and Sets. A Run is a consecutive string of numbers that runs horizontally across the board from left to right and from 1 to 10, all of the same colour. A Set is made up of four vertical tiles, all the same number, but different colours. Keep your eyes peeled on the board and try to place tiles over Multiplier squares. These boost the points value of that tile two or more times, while Addition squares add crucial points that could be decisive. If you see a chance to add to a Set or a Run, go for it, sometimes adding just one or two tiles for a mega points haul. You can also turn the tables on others by stacking a tile on another and filling in unsuspected gaps. Add to that Rainbow squares that open up options and blank tiles which are wild and you’ll have all kinds of ways to rid your rack of tiles. The player with the most points when no more tiles can go down wins the game.

—description from the publisher

The players’ mission is to work together in perfect harmony to break through the blockade of the Turkish fleet, and then defeat the enemy by eliminating the specified units among the Turkish forces besieging the fortress.
At the same time, they must avoid triggering any of the defeat conditions.

•••

A játékosok küldetése, hogy egymással teljes összhangban együttműködve áttörjék a török flotta blokádját, majd a várat ostromló török csapatok közül megadott egységet leszedve megfutamítsák az ellenséget. Közben el kell kerüljék, hogy a vereség feltételei közül bármelyik bekövetkezzen.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Fast-paced card game for 2-4 players about cats telling their stories.
The goal is to place 7 cards in sequence in front of you - the first player to do this will be the winner.
Others will do whatever they can to stop you from winning - counter your cards, discard your hands or destroy what you already accomplished.

—description from the designer

This expansion introduces two new Hunters that have signed the Coven! Expand your Hunters selection with The Twins and the Clockwork Giant. Both of these new Hunters are also available as Deputies in the Solo mode and as Pretenders (bosses).

This add-on introduces two new Griefbound and a new Elite enemy! Expand your Enemy selection with the Chimera, Striga, and Werewolf. This expansion also contains a set of additional cards (secrets, environment and events) and special map tiles!

Old and New is a clever, family-friendly card game that bridges generations by connecting inventions, traditions, and everyday objects from the past with their modern counterparts. Each round, players draw cards featuring items, technologies, or customs from different eras and try to correctly match an “old” with its corresponding “new” version.

Some pairings are obvious (like “vinyl record – streaming service”), while others provoke discussion or laughter as players debate their choices. The cards spark nostalgia and curiosity, making the game both fun and educational for players of all ages.

The game can be played competitively — earning points for correct matches — or cooperatively, using the cards as conversation prompts about how the world has changed. Simple rules, colorful illustrations, and short playtime make it ideal for family gatherings, classrooms, or casual play.

Take turns to make castles in this fun-packed race to finish first! But watch out, Everyone gets a chance to SPLAT the castles FLAT! Build, spin and squash to win!

Be the first player to complete your 4 part sandcastle and place the flag on top!

Spin the spinner: Will you build a wall? Go at all? Or SPLAT someone else's castle?

If someone spins 'the foot', they can splat YOUR sandcastle And you have to start again!

First to finish WINS! Comes with four moulds and bases, sand, spinner and splatting foot.

boardgame: Dinosaurs: A Game For All Ages
description:
Unleash Prehistoric Fun with Dinosaurs!

Get ready to dive into the world of Dinosaurs with our rule set!

This game is designed for pure fun, offering a fast, easy-to-learn experience that's perfect for children of all ages. 
Forget about complicated setups or expensive pieces; you can use any toy dinosaurs you already have to bring your epic battles to life. From the towering Tyrannosaurus Rex to the speedy Velociraptor, every one of your plastic pals can join the fray.

Inside this rule set, you'll discover everything you need to play right away. 
We've simplified the mechanics so you can focus on the ac
tion and adventure, not getting bogged down in complex rules. You'll learn how to move your dinosaurs, engage in thrilling combat, and even devise cunning strategies to outwit your opponents. 
All that's left is for you to gather your favourite prehistoric pals and start stomping!

It's time to create your own dinosaur sagas and let your imagination roam wild.

What kind of epic dinosaur adventures will you create?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Scurvy Dogs: A Pirate Game Like No Other
description:
Scurvy Dogs ain't yer grandpappy's ponderous ruleset, no sir! 
Forget the dusty tomes and endless charts. 
This is a treasure map to swashbucklin' action, built for small gangs of up to a dozen of yer most scurvy, cutthroat pirates.

Whether ye be fightin' a rival captain for dominion over the high seas or teamin' up against a landlubber invasion, these rules be quick as a sea shanty and sharp as a cutlass!

Dive into fast-paced skirmishes where every decision matters, from outflanking your foes to seizing vital strongholds. 
"Scurvy Dogs" strips away the unnecessary, leaving only pure, unadulterated pirate combat designed for quick setup and thrilling, dynamic gameplay.

Grab your crew, roll the dice, and let the cannons roar – adventure awaits!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: A Pearl of Great Price: Operation Morvarid – Iran strikes Iraq
description:
A ready to play scenario based on Operation Morvarid, a missile boat engagement in the early months of the Iran-Iraq war in 1980.

Designed for use with SHIPWRECK, Vandering Publications d10 based wargames rules system for naval warfare in the age of the guided missile.  
With a little modification could be used for other rules systems too.

Includes:

     A description of the action, results and long-term consequences.
     Ship detail sheets for all units involved.
     Instructions to set up the scenario, starting positions, victory conditions and a solo play option.
     Printable as an A5 booklet.

The Coronation of Mary I, Queen of England is to be held in London on Sunday, October 1, 1553. In preparation to receive the abundance of dignitaries, nobles, and people of good taste both foreign and domestic, it has been decreed that the city of London shall look its finest in all possible ways. Especially sanitation and all things related to odors.

To do your duty to God, Queen, and Country, you have been charged with removal of all waste in the city before the Coronation festivities begin.

An expansion for Night Soil, Coronation Day allows you to play Night Soil as a solo game. Includes 15 Neighborhood Cards, 13 Opponent Cards, and 6 Misfortune Cards.

Promo card for Earth.

Terrain

+ 1 soil/+1 point for each adjacent rocky and tree icon

Originally available from the Tantrum house season 12 kickstarter
Guidelines for solo play, usable with In the Grand Manner or any Napoleonic rules system.
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Call My Trash is a fast, engaging bluffing and deduction game where players secretly dispose of trash—while trying to expose anyone sneaking in forbidden industrial waste. Everyone is cleaning up the neighborhood, but not everyone is playing by the rules.

Each round, players take turns discarding cards face-down into a shared pile. You must announce what type of trash you’re throwing away, but the truth is… flexible. Other players may challenge your claim with a bold “Call My Trash!”, forcing an immediate reveal.

If your announcement was honest, the challenger is penalized.
If you were bluffing, you take the hit.
If you hid Industrial Waste, expect even harsher consequences.

Hand management, timing, and keen observation are crucial. Special cards, surprising reveals, and tense stare-downs create a lively social atmosphere where no one can stay quiet for long.

A round ends when a player runs out of cards, triggering scoring based on the remaining trash—and any hazardous waste hidden inside. After multiple rounds, the cleanest, most cunning recycler wins.

Call My Trash plays quickly with any group, supports up to eight players, and delivers the classic bluff-and-call tension found in social games—but with a fresh theme and clever twist on hidden-information play.

boardgame: Legendary: A DC Deck Building Game
description:
Legendary: A DC Deck-Building Game is a standalone deck-building game that brings the DC Universe into the Legendary family. Players assume the roles of DC superheroes working cooperatively to thwart the schemes of a Mastermind villain and their associated threats.

Each game features a randomly selected Mastermind who pursues a unique evil Scheme, creating a distinct challenge for players. Throughout the game, players build their decks by recruiting Heroes and acquiring powerful cards to strengthen their capabilities. Sidekick cards provide crucial support in defeating Villains and completing objectives that emerge during play.

The game incorporates a Hope and Fear Tracker that measures the overall state of the conflict. As the tracker shifts, it influences when and how players can activate special abilities tied to their Heroes, requiring strategic timing and decision-making to overcome increasingly difficult threats.

The core set includes a diverse array of characters from the DC Universe, each represented through original artwork. These Heroes, Villains, Masterminds, and various card types provide multiple paths to victory and strategic variety across different games.

Players win by working together to defeat the Mastermind's Scheme before either the Mastermind achieves their objectives or the game state becomes unwinnable. Success requires careful deck management, tactical card play, and coordinated use of each Hero's unique abilities.

—description from the publisher

In the gamers' game Tinctura, welcome patients to your pharmacy and cure them of their ailments by making ointments, tinctures, and tablets for them. To do this, you must collect ingredients, train apprentices, and develop your skills so that you can meet even the most difficult challenges.

Plan your day secretly in hour shifts to work in your pharmacy, in the town or in the woods. Collect resources in the forest and create the medicine your patients need. For every medicine you need certain skills. These can be improved by studying in the town or do some research on your own. Buy some equipment to get advantages through bonus tracks. And hire apprentices to help them with creating medicines. Get the most victory points to win the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grifoni: Blades for Hire – The Two Scoundrels
description:
Two shady figures, known as The Cat and The Fox, prowl through the Company’s camp spreading chaos, deceit... and unexpected opportunities.

This expansion for Grifoni: Blades for Hire comes with:

     2 Meeples
     8 Tent Cards
     3 Position Cards
     2 Mercenary Cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grifoni: Blades for Hire – Shadow of the Beast
description:
An ancient creature, the dreadful Striped Draco, once thought lost to legend, has returned to burn villages and crush mercenary companies under its wings of flame. Only the bravest Captains will dare to face the Beast and seize its hoarded treasures. This expansion adds a new Permanent Location, The Draco's Nest, and a stunning 3D Dice Tower Ruin, both scenic and functional, where the Draco perches in battle.

This expansion for Grifoni: Blades for Hire comes with:

     1 Giant Striped Draco Maaple
     1 3D Dice Tower (Ruined Tower)
     1 Location Board: The Draco's Nest
     20 Draco Task Cards
     8 Draco Treasure Cards
     1 Draco Die (custom d6)


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grifoni: Blades for Hire – Caravan of Wonders
description:
Out of the dusty horizon comes a wagon loaded with promises—it's the Caravan of Wonders, led by the young and ambitious Vasile. Acrobat, fraud, or business genius? Hard to say. But wherever his wagon stops, there's never a shortage of miracles, mysteries, and a dash of trouble. The expansion introduces a new 3D board, the Traveling Caravan, a Story Book and an Endings Book, for a new adventure-filled mission!

This expansion for Grifoni: Blades for Hire comes with:

     1 3D Location Board: Travelling Caravan
     9 Caravan Cards
     1 Story Book
     1 Story Book (Epilogues)
     8 Equipment Cards
     8 Equipment Tokens
     6 Mercenary Cards


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Grifoni: Blades for Hire – Shakespeare in War
description:
The stage lights come on, and this time the war is fought within the pages of William Shakespeare! The Company enters the most famous tragedies and comedies, where intrigue, duels, and passions intertwine on the thread of destiny. The expansion includes two new Meeple (Romeo and Juliet), new Mission Cards and additional Task Cards, as well as a new Storybook and a Endings Book, which will draw you into a Shakespearean war of love, deception, and twists and turns.

This expansion for Grifoni: Blades for Hire comes with:

     1 3D dedicated Track
     2 Meeples
     1 Story Book
     1 Story Book (Epilogues)
     12 Task Cards
     9 Feud Cards


—description from the publisher

The critically acclaimed Clans of Caledonia base game and Clans of Caledonia: Industria expansion combined together in one big box.

Lidded inserts organize every single component of the base game + expansion, and the custom cotton bag, too! The removable inserts make set up and tear down a breeze, making it easier than ever to get this beloved game on the table.

Dive into the rich world of Clans of Caledonia with the complete game all-in-one. This big box includes the Clans of Caledonia base game plus its first expansion Industria. With new mechanics, solo Automa rules, and engaging challenges, the expansion deepens the strategic experience of the original game and invites players to explore industrial progress in 19th-century Scotland.

Big Box

Clans of Caledonia (base game) and the Clans of Caledonia: Industria expansion comes with a single big box with a custom plastic insert that fits in all components of the base game and of the expansion.

The dimension of this game box is the same as the expansion box (311 x 224 x 118 mm) but it comes with about 5 mm of lid lift. After removing the empty punchboards, the lid closes completely.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

description:
Hand-At-Arms is a portable solo card game playable fully in hands only. You are a knight embarking on a journey to fulfill quests picked from a book of scenarios. On your way you will upgrade your weapons, defeat monsters and in the end, find and win against the final boss.

The base travel loop of the game is inspired by Loot the Loop and Galdor's Grip, with some new mechanics added, including rearranging the cards, and building up resource lanes to pay action costs. You will be using your card hands to upgrade your color cards and attack black monster cards. Mechanically it means you will be rotating/flipping them through their 3 stages. The objective of each quest is specified in the description - typically, you need to defeat 3 monsters to unlock the final boss and then vanquish it - with the enemies gradually progressing to their more powerful forms during each scenario.

The game comes with a tutorial mission plus two full quests, each of them additionally subdivided into various difficulty levels. A strategy and hints guide is also available for interested players.

The game depicts the struggles of German I. Kavallerie-Korps to breaking through the Soviet 4-я гвардейская армия (4th Guards Army) defenses around the Hungarian town of Zámoly in January 1945, during the attempt to relieve Budapest with Operation Konrad II.

Contents:

     13 scenarios including 1 campaign game
     384 5/8" counters
     560 1/2" counters
     4 38.2"x26" and two 38.2"x25.6" mapsheets which combine to make one 76.4"x77.6" map representing the historical layout of the Zamoly sector
     Rules consisting of scenario special rules, map terrain, and campaign game rules.


—description from the designer's website

Welcome to the World of Auctions

You’re a curator about to build a brand-new art museum.
To fill its walls, you’ll need to secure five masterpieces through intense auctions.
Use your limited funds and loans wisely to claim the most valuable works.
A bold loan might get you a priceless piece,
but that same debt could come back to haunt you during the final exhibition.

---

Not Your Typical Auction

This isn’t just about bidding the highest amount.
The last player to drop out of an auction loses all the money they’ve bid.

To win the artwork you want —
or simply to avoid losing everything —
you’ll need to read your opponents, stay calm under pressure,
and make daring choices when it counts.

(Sometimes, you might end up spending $40 just to win $20.)

---

It’s Okay to Go Broke

Out of cash? Don’t panic.
You can always bounce back with a loan.

When you borrow, you’ll shuffle the discarded cards at the bank
and reclaim half of them as your new funds.
In seconds, you’ll be back in action with a huge amount of capital —
ready to show everyone what real financial power looks like.

---

The Comeback Is Always Possible

In this game, art is money.
The paintings you’ve won can be used as currency in future auctions.
And when you take a loan,
you might even draw valuable artworks from the bank’s discard pile.

Sometimes, the smartest play
isn’t winning the bid — it’s timing your loan perfectly.

---

Everything in Moderation

Loans are powerful — but dangerously tempting.
Remember, every loan is backed by your museum itself.
The more you borrow, the less exhibition space you’ll have left.
In the end, even the most expensive masterpieces won’t matter
if there’s nowhere left to display them.

---

Balance your money, art, and ambition — and build the greatest museum of them all.

—description from the designer

As a smuggler with a starship, fulfill missions for both sides of the war between the Resistance and Federation, chasing the fame and bounty while carefully staying below the radar to avoid ousting in a portable, solo, in-hand card game.

In a session of Smuggler's Sky: Hand of Fate, you will go through a shuffled deck of cards, each turn selecting from among two missions with a varying cost and reward. Your favor with both of the warring factions will ebb and flow, together with the random order of the cards determining the outcome of the mission, possibly further influenced by bribing and utility items. You will then resolve your allegiance shift and further choose whether to cash-in for short-term liquidity, buy or sell utilities, or invest into the final victory points.

Crossfire Sector is developed as a 15 mm scale skirmish game, but can be played in any scale with the miniatures already available. The game takes up little space, is fast to setup and play, but features different Factions, missions and also a campaign mode.

6 Factions with different abilities and style

Optional Commanders to lead the Factions

Print and Play Faction cards and Battlefield

Pre-made or custom campaigns

Zombie defense mode

1 or 2 players

—description from the designer

Named after Emma Woodhouse's estate in Jane Austen novel Emma, the 2025 Hollandays freebie game is a match-making trick-taking game for 4 or 5 players. Each trick is won by two players, who each contributed a card to a Match. Each player scores points for the quality of the Match - the best Match being two cards of the same suit - as well as the points on the other player's card.

At the end of each hand, the player with the lowest score becomes The Meddler, who can break the normal match-making rules. And in the deck lurks The Scoundrel, who will always win the trick. The art is drawn from Chris Hammond's charming, lively illustrations for the 1898 edition of Emma and 1899 edition of Sense and Sensibility.

The player with the highest score after three rounds wins the game.

—description from the designer
A small expansion consisting of 7 cards for Suna Valo, allowing you to play solo against a Botbot.

There is chaos on the farms! Loose chickens are pecking every patch of land clean.
They must be quick, however, because the hungry fox is on the prowl. Will you strike at the right time, steal the tastiest foods, and score the most points?

Brief game overview

Six farms are about to be pecked by chickens. Each round you choose which foods to take, but you often get in each other’s way. The longer a chicken stays on a farm, the more food they can find. At the same time, however, the fox is closing in. Once the fox strikes, you lose everything. During the game, the number of points you get for your food varies. The chicken with the most points at the end of the game wins.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Be the fastest and win!

A cooperative game to enjoy with the family.

The race has begun! Let's help the turtles reach the finish line before the hare overtakes them. A fun cooperative game where we win by making good decisions together.

On their turn, each player rolls a ball through a tube, and it will drop into one of several holes (representing the burrow). Depending on which hole it falls into, the player moves the hare or their turtle a certain number of spaces, although they may choose to move the last turtle instead of their own.

Additionally, each animal can collect food (carrots and lettuces) scattered across the board, which will help them throughout the race. Finally, certain spaces contain different animals or events that can… change the course of movement!
family: Folk Tales & Fairy Tales: The Tortoise and the Hare

description:
Speed Kings is available digitally, in book form, and as a boxed set with cardboard standees. A Deluxe Set offers nine Race Plane miniatures, each beautifully-rendered at 1/100th scale. The miniatures are as well-suited for the modeling shelf as they are on your game table. Why not paint them in your team colors?

recreates the Thompson Trophy Cup races held in Cleveland, Ohio, during the early 1930s. In the game, each player represents a race team with 1-3 race planes. Gameplay involves Speed Cards, Pilot Skills, and Race Plane Attributes. Dice play a minimal role. Speed Kings is a game of skill, calculated action, and careful planning.

Challenging, highly strategic, and realistic, to achieve victory, players must plan carefully and manage their engine power as they employ a push-your-luck mechanic to take the lead as their team's race planes turn the pylons and hammer down the straights.

Gameplay is carried out either on a table or on the floor. Players may create their own racecourse or fly on one of six historic circuits included in the game. Pylons represent turn points on the Race Circuit while the Straights define longer runs where engine power is king.

Six race planes are included, ranging from the Gee Bee R-1 Super Speedster to the Hall-Springfield Bulldog, the Howard DGA-4 "Mike", and the Laird Solution -- all dating from the 1931 and 1932 race season. Each plane has unique characteristics that reflect its design and the raw power of its engine. Up to nine Race Planes may participate in any race!

Which is better? Should you fly a lighter, more nimble design or a heavier plane with a massive engine at the front?

Eighteen historic pilots are included, each with varying levels of skill and daring. Famous air racing names include Jimmy Doolittle, Dale "Red" Jackson, Lee Gehlbach, Beeler Blevins, and others. Women pilots are also represented, such as Gladys O'Donnell, Florence Klingensmith, and Pancho Barnes.

Players set up the race, compete to choose their planes, recruit the pilots, and they're off to a flying start. Most races are three laps long, though players may choose how many laps they wish to play. Generally, it takes about 15 minutes per lap, depending on the length of the race course.

The Standard Edition of the game comes with cardboard standees. A Deluxe Edition has been prepared, which is ordered directly through Historic Wings, that features miniatures of nine race planes that are beautifully rendered at 1/100 scale -- once painted, they are equally suitable for a modeling shelf as for the game table!

—description from the designer

Welcome to the most personalized and hilarious wedding game you’ll ever play! This isn't just trivia—it's a fun-filled contest designed to test your knowledge of the newlyweds' quirky habits, secret opinions, and playful boasts!

Before the main event, the Host (often one person of honor) will select four contestants in a lightning-round challenge. They will read out Special Facts chosen by the newlyweds—facts only their closest confidantes are likely to know! Shout out the correct answer first to earn your spot at the contestant table!

The Host reads out fun, pre-selected facts like, "Who’s convinced they’re the better dancer?" or "Who’s famous for never admitting they’re wrong?"

Contestants have just 5 seconds to secretly vote using their cards, holding up the name of the newlywed they believe the fact applies to.

The Host announces the correct answer, and points are tallied! Listen closely, because if everyone gets it wrong, the newlyweds are encouraged to jump in with a witty comment or quip!

-description from publisher
family: Components: Dry Erase Markers / Dry Erase Surfaces

description:
"Truth is stranger than fiction"

[sic] is an adult party game about reality. Each round one player will act as an interviewer and use an [interview card] to ask a journalist-style question on topics like politics, lifestyle, hobbies, and celebrity culture. Each player will then select their funniest [response card], made up of genuine quotes and references taken from real people including politicians, celebrities, influencers, criminals, and historical figures. At the end of the round the interviewer judges the funniest response and awards that player a point. Play to a certain number of points, a set time limit, or just until everyone's thoroughly depressed about the state of modern discourse.

Though the play style is simple, the game has much more to discover. There are special actions, scenarios, and real-life scandals with linked responses to find in the deck, along with multiple ways to play and re-mix the game at home. Every card comes with all the information and context behind the quote or reference, with extra backstory and news sources available by scanning the QR code.

-description from publisher

Upside Down: Double Twist is a fast and hilarious party game where players wear special goggles that flip their vision upside down and attempt to complete various physical challenges. Tasks include drawing simple shapes, stacking objects, or moving items across the table — all while your brain struggles to adapt to the inverted world!

Each round, players take turns performing a challenge card while others watch (and laugh). Successfully completing the task earns points, but failure means passing the turn. The “Double Twist” edition includes two pairs of glasses and new challenge cards, allowing for simultaneous play and head-to-head duels.

The game combines coordination, humor, and chaos in short bursts, perfect for parties, family gatherings, and team events. Its replayability comes from unpredictable challenges and the sheer fun of watching others fail at the simplest tasks when viewed upside down.

Cow Round-Up is a light and colorful family game where players race to guide their cows safely into the barn before the others do. Each turn, players roll the die and move their cows across the meadow, navigating fences, mud puddles, and tricky turns. Some spaces help — like the “clover patch,” where cows can jump ahead — while others slow progress or send a cow back to the start.

The game focuses on color recognition, basic counting, and turn-taking, making it perfect for preschoolers and families with younger children. Its charming farm theme and simple components encourage storytelling and laughter as players try to “herd” their cows most efficiently.

boardgame: Snorkers! A solitaire game of Escort Ships in the North Atlantic Convoys of the Second World War
description:
Welcome to Snorkers, a solitaire naval wargame that places you in command of a Flower-class corvette during the treacherous Battle of the Atlantic in World War II.

Gameplay Overview
Snorkers offers a challenging and immersive solitaire experience. 
You will make critical decisions about navigation, ship management, and combat engagements. 
Your Flower-class corvette's performance will be influenced by the crew's morale, your strategic choices, and the randomness of war.
As a corvette captain, your mission is clear: safeguard the vital supply convoys as they brave the perilous waters of the North Atlantic, battling relentless U-boats, aircraft, and the unforgiving elements.

Victory Conditions
Victory is achieved by successfully protecting your convoy and reaching your destination without sustaining critical damage or losing too many cargo ships. 
Your performance will be graded based on historical outcomes and specific mission objectives

Devised during the dark days of covid 19 and lockdowns, Snorkers was created as an antidote to the many solitaire games that put the players in the shoes of U-boat commanders. I decided it was time for the good guys to get a look in. 
I hope you enjoy the game
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Olimpus: The Pachanga of Domino Vip
description:
Olimpus transforms classic dominoes into a dynamic, chaotic, and highly interactive experience. Each player begins with a traditional hand of tiles, but every turn is impacted by the Blue Die — a tension-building mechanic that can reveal tiles, invert turn order, force players to draw, recover tokens, or trigger unexpected effects. Players may spend tokens to avoid the randomness, creating constant decisions between control and risk.

The Red Die introduces social twists and table-wide disruptions: exchanging tiles with neighbors, revealing information, receiving tiles from opponents, or triggering “free-play” moments that shift the strategic landscape. Every round becomes unpredictable, emotional, and full of surprise turns that reshape the table.

Despite the chaos, Olimpus keeps the core of dominoes intact: players still build matching chains and race to empty their hands. The difference is the explosive layer of interaction, tension, and dramatic reversals, turning a classic logic game into a cinematic experience.

—description from the designer

Get ready to sweeten your strategy and toast your competition, with the White Chocolate Promo Card Set for S’mores Galore!

Inspired by the passionate fans who always show up with wild, wonderful twists on the classic s’more, this special promo set celebrates one of the most requested alternative ingredients: white chocolate.

What’s Inside the White Chocolate Promo?

6 New Ingredient Cards and 1 Rule Card: Shuffle the white chocolate cards into your deck with the other ingredients. White chocolate can be used as chocolate or a marshmallow in your recipes. But be careful! At the end of the game, the player with the most white chocolate loses points!

—description from the designer

Conquer Terrania as one of four hero warlords by uniting continents, waging war, curing infectous diseases, deploying bioweapons, and advancing technology.  Outlast enemies, manage population and form alliances to dominate the world in this unique 2 to 4 player world-conquering, strategy deck buiding, resource trading, card game.


     Explore an in-depth, strategic world-conquering and disease-mastering experience.
     Save Terrania, a futuristic, sci fi, urban, apocalyptic environment dominated by corporations.
     Master a unique, double resource-based deck-building board game.


—description from the designer

Arkveil Academy is a quick, solo, chaotic deck-builder where every card draw presents a choice. Draw and place summons to build power and use their abilities to help manage the rapidly rising chaos. Decide whether to push your luck or cash out before everything goes sideways and you lose a turn.

Each turn you can learn new summons, expand your summon active summon capacity, complete test, and unlock abilities with optional studies. Every run is unique with a randomized market forcing you to shift strategy as the game unfolds.

Attempt to control the chaos and complete your final with a passing grade before the market deck runs dry or it's game over.

—description from the designer

Is there anything better than spending time with friends over a campfire, not a care in the world, yapping about anything and everything while playing a breezy, but just a little mean, card game? That's what Yap is all about.

Yap is a 2-4 player card game that combines the gameplay flow of the standard deck classics Yaniv and Jhyap with the mechanics of climbing and shedding games like Haggis and Crisps and that echo of timelessness one gets from a game like Gin Rummy.

Each round, players will discard cards with the goal of getting their hand to be worth less than the threshold to declare Yap! The cards discarded must be of a higher rank or more cards. Over the course of play, the threshold each player needs to go out may also change. The first player to start their turn at or below their threshold can declare Yap and win the round! The other players score points for the cards left in their hands, and in this game, less is best. Be warned though. If a player has less points in hand than the one who declared Yap, the declarer gets penalized!

Ever wonder why ice cream sales and drowning deaths both spike in summer? In Correlated, players use real statistical correlations to craft the most creative and completely ridiculous causal explanations they can think of.

Each round, players select a correlation card showing two variables that actually move together in real-world data. They then present their theory explaining why one obviously causes the other. The Chief Statistician judges which explanation is most creative, convincing, or entertaining. Confidence beats accuracy every time.

The game uses over 100 real correlations sourced from Tyler Vigen's Spurious Correlations database. Players don't need any statistics knowledge—just creativity and the confidence to defend absurd theories with scientific-sounding language. The first player to collect five published papers (cards) wins.

Correlated works well as a party game for casual players or as an educational icebreaker for data teams, making it perfect for anyone who's ever heard "correlation doesn't equal causation" and wanted to prove otherwise—at least for laughs.

This is a trick-taking game for four players.
Each round, one player acts as the dealer, while the remaining three players compete to win tricks.
The dealer, who holds no cards, reveals one of their previously predicted game developments instead of playing a card.

—description from the designer

Forage is a puzzly tableau-building roll-and-write game for 1-6 players with gorgeous artwork by Beth Sobel! It is designed and developed by the team that brought you the Calico, Cascadia, and Point Salad series of games!

Rules are simple: Roll the forage dice and reveal a unique journey tile each round. Choose a combination of dice and carry out one of the actions: exploring, storing, or gift-giving.
