As you carry out actions, you’ll earn bonuses that allow you to power up your future actions or make additional moves to complete your goals! Use your time wisely - there are several journeys you can take to collect knowledge, experience, and prestige. Once you have completed your turn, you’ll choose one of the three types of cards to draw and add to your tableau giving you more ways to combo and complete goals!

Completing exploration cards involves traversing trails through the forest and collecting bonuses by surrounding them via a spatial puzzle that extends as you add cards to your tableau. Completing pantry cards involves storing goods in jars and unlocking bonuses as you complete rows and columns within your shelves. Completing gift-giving cards involves collecting specific ingredients in the exact quantities to produce gifts, providing you with immediate and end-game scoring bonuses!

There are over 90 unique exploration, pantry, and gift-giving cards to collect, so you will have a completely unique foraging journey each and every time you play! 6 unique double-sided goal cards and 27 unique journey tiles also encourage player interaction and strategic thinking throughout the game.

Forage is a unique roll-and-write game where you write on cards in an ever-changing tableau. As the game progresses, your options increase and your scoring conditions change. It is easy to learn, but challenging for everyone!

—description from the designer

Honeypot is a quick and simple I Stack, You Choose game for 1-6 players with delightful artwork by Kwanchai Moriya!

In Honeypot, players take on the role of secret agent bears trying to gather the best intel and spy supplies without getting stung! Players arrange secret caches for their opponents and carefully sift through the secret caches passed to them. The question is: how far to dig for valuable intel and spy supplies? Have your opponents passed you the sweetest honey, or a swarm of bees? In this thrilling game of setting traps and trying to avoid them, only the agent with the best instincts will capture the best set of items to come out on top!

Honeypot is a set collection game featuring a massive deck of cards so each round the combination of items is a new puzzle. Your goal is to arrange cards into groupings that will encourage your opponents to take the combinations that harm them, while also trying to determine how they have arranged the cards for you. It’s a delightfully interactive puzzle that evolves as the game progresses and your tableau of cards grows - try to collect the best intel and supplies, but beware, you may end up with items that detract from your score based on the other items you have collected.

From the team that brought you the Calico, Cascadia, and Point Salad series, this quick and simple card game has all of the stand-up moments of victory and betrayal while still being easy to teach and perfect to share with your friends and family!

—description from the designer

A Living History in Your Hands

You are the curator of a priceless inheritance: a unique collection of vintage botanical art spanning an incredible 400 years. The vibrant fruits are from Giorgio Gallesio's seminal 19th-century work, Pomona Italiana. The delicate flowers are studies from the personal book of Claude de France, dating back to the 16th century. The exquisite insects are meticulously rendered from the groundbreaking studies of 17th century naturalist Maria Sibylla Merian. Last and definitely not least, the splendidly rendered Mushrooms of the late 18th Century from James Sowerby's book, Coloured Figures of English Fungi or Mushrooms.

Your task is not to simply admire these masterpieces, but to arrange them. With a cup of tea at your elbow, you begin the solitary, satisfying work of weaving these heirloom treasures into a harmonious display, connecting art and nature across the centuries in a personal sanctuary of beauty and logic.

The Game in a Nutshell

An Heirloom Garden is a serene yet challenging solo card game for those who love spatial puzzles and historical art. Your goal is to arrange your inherited specimens into four growing rows before the quiet winter settles in.

On each turn, you must play two cards from your hand, placing each onto the end of a different row. The challenge? Each new card must be exactly one higher or one lower than the card at the row's end. Use the unique ability on each card to swap, move, and manipulate your display, cleverly overcoming the sequence rules.

But time is of the essence. If your hand grows too large, or you find yourself stuck, the frost creeps closer. Manage your hand wisely and race to empty the deck to win!

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
A promo pack for backers of Clank! Catacombs: Underworld.

Includes:
Jet Slippers
Magic Mirror (Device)
Nether Titan (Monster)
Dusty Map (Device)
Mister Wizkers (Companion)
Reference cards for the Sphinx, Medusa, and Living Statue from Lairs & Lost Chambers Expansion

This expansion adds 6 new individual riders, allows up to 6 players, and introduces the “Legends of Granfondo” tournament mode, plus a new double-sided game board with two additional routes.
Includes bonus chips like the Attack Bonus (for most battles won) and the Strategy Bonus (for most Belgian Rotation participations).
The base game is required.
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Theme: Building a Maritime Empire

Welcome to Rival Seas, where you will command your very own maritime empire! Your mission is to chart the most efficient sea routes, fulfill the demands of bustling coastal towns, and secure a fortune in credits. Success in this cutthroat industry requires more than just navigation skills. You must strategically acquire shares in cargo companies, strive to become their CEO, siphon company wealth into your own hands and turn your portfolio into valuable assets. Only the savviest entrepreneur, who can acquire the highest capital by fulfilling demand and securing valuable stocks, will emerge victorious!

Gameplay Summary: Trade, Stocks, and Financial Mastery

Rival Seas is a strategic board game played over 3 intense rounds. Players manage a tight budget within a precious time limit as they race to dominate the seas.

On Your Turn: The core of the game is using your limited time on the Timetrack to perform actions. Each action you take moves your tracker further on the Timetrack. You can keep taking actions as long as you aren't the player who spent the most time. You can end your turn at any time as long as you aren't in the last position on the Timetrack.

Actions involve:
Build navigation points
Manufacture containers
Load containers
Influence demand of coastal towns
Invest in companies (and become CEO)
Acquire prestige and upgrades
Move your ship
Deliver containers (this also affects future container prices)

End of round:
Each round new cointainers enter play that can be loaded and delivered to coastal towns. Also the CEO of each company will receive his wage and if the company has enough funds left, the CEO can also decide to pay a dividend to all shareholders.

Scoring: After the third and final round ends, players calculate their final victory points based on their personal capital and prestige (Stock value, Credits, Investments).

Remark:
A Crucial Distinction => Company Funds vs. Personal Capital

A unique mechanism in Rival Seas is the strict separation between a company's funds and your personal credits. When you buy shares, the money goes to the company. Company funds are used to finance operations, such as placing Navigation points and the company will also get paid if players use these Navigation points. Your ultimate goal is to generate personal wealth (your Victory Points), which you primarily do by making deliveries and, critically, through the Payout Phase where the CEO receives a wage and decides dividends will be paid from the company's funds to shareholders.

—description from the publisher

The Earth is threatened by the Forces of Vega, led by the Grand Strategist, who is determined to seize its riches and eliminate the human race...
But one man stands in their way: Actarus, who, at the controls of the giant robot Goldorak, manages to repel the powerful Golgoths.
He can also count on the help of the Eagle Patrol, the unbeatable trio formed by Alcor, Venusia, and Phénicia!

Goldorak XperienZ is a cooperative family game that lets you relive the adventures of Goldorak and his allies.

In France and Italy, Grendizer is called Goldorak or Goldrake.

—description from the designer

The game consists of a stack of cards with a question and some weird gibberish. Players have to speak the gibberish into a web-app, which then plays those sounds backwards, giving a hint to the answer of the question. 
Ideally, the players can then answer the question (full score). If not, they can get additional hints to help them. 
Questions and gibberish are fairly simple at first, then gradually become harder and require more involvement from the players (some cards are calls to action in various ways). 
After playing through the entire stack (typically, in various sessions), players can check their score on the final card.

In voller Blüte is a drafting and tile placement game. In each round, players draw four cards, each containing four flowers. They draft a card and place it in a personal layout, then pass the remaining cards to the next player. This continues until each player has placed four cards. Over the course of four rounds, players create flower beds consisting of 8 x 8 flowers. They score points for each group of exactly four of the same flowers. Bonus points are scored for flowerbeds in specific shapes and for flowers with butterflies. The player with most points after four rounds is the winner.

Cowards is a bluffing game for 2 players, using the Blossom Deck.  It is based on Barcelona, a similar game with a standard poker deck.  The game is named after Cowards Bay, which is set in the fantasy Dew Point universe.

OBJECTIVE: Cowards is played in short rounds, using chips to keep score, and players can choose to play as many rounds as they wish.  The goal is to have the most chips at the end of the game.

GAMEPLAY: The Blossom deck is divided into two decks, Day and Night, each having 27 cards.  Players draw one card from each deck. Players then choose one of their cards to reveal, and the lowest of these may decide whether the game is "On" or "Off". If the game is "On" (which happens automatically if both players played a Night card), all cards are revealed.  The highest Night card wins (ties are resolved by the suit order prescribed in the rules), and the loser’s penalty is decided by the size of their Day card, which they pay in chips to the winner.

COMPONENTS:

     The Blossom Deck
     roughly 100 chips for each player


THE BLOSSOM DECK: The Blossom Deck is a six-suited deck from James Earnest and Crab Fragment Games, and is set the Dew Point universe. The deck consists of 54 cards in six suits ranked Ace through nine. The suits are divided into three colors, each with "night" and "day" versions, allowing the deck to be split into halves, thirds, or sixths. Two games were specifically created for the Blossom Deck: Bulldog (a gambling game for 2-8 players) and Cowards (a gambling game for 2 players).

Titkos Katicák ("Secret Ladybugs") is a memory-based children’s game in which players aim to find matching ladybug figures beneath memory tiles. The game uses wooden components and features two play boards, colourful ladybug figures, and patterned cards or tiles that vary the layout. On each turn, a player uncovers two tiles (or equivalent) and sees whether the symbols beneath match or reveal the same colour ladybug. Successful matches allow the player to keep the pair; if the tiles do not match, they are turned back over and play continues. The game engages young children in observation, concentration, memory recall and visual pattern matching in a simple and approachable format. Because the game uses no heavy language or reading, it is suitable for young children (3 +).

—description from the publisher

PipFall is a deterministic, tactical area-control game for 2–4 players.

Players use standard playing cards to pre-program movement or pip-altering actions for their dice. Dice represent battlefield units; their pip value represents strength. Players fight for tactical dominance through clever lane selection, movement discipline, and combat timing.

The entire game operates on public information, programmed actions, and smart lane control — no dice rolling after setup.
The game is fast, aggressive, and rewards bold tactical play.

—description from the designer

In Pasch², you try to control the luck of the dice and be the first to fulfill one of the four victory conditions with the right strategy. You'll keep an eye on your competitors, earn extra dice and rolls, and steal their progress.

The rules are accessible and quick to learn. They build upon familiar puzzle games, taking their best elements and adding more interaction, tactical options, and key moments.

Pasch² is also physically clever, as the game board is a handy, wipeable magnetic board. A matching pen with an integrated eraser is also included. The magnetic markers, combined with the board, ensure that everything stays in place when passing the game around.

In keeping with the theme of outsmarting luck, Pasch² is presented with a lighthearted, scientific look.

Can you control luck?

—description from the publisher (translated)

In 8 und aus!, the animals are on the loose and have escaped from the zoo! Can you protect them from the dangers of the city and bring them back to their cozy home? The best animal rescuer wins the quick rounds.

The rules are accessible and easy to learn. After the first round, you'll be focused on achieving the longest winning streak and making the best choice for a quick victory. If a round doesn't go as planned, the next one is ready to go.

With its practical box featuring a flip cover and the included pad and pen, 8 and Out! is always ready for action. Are you at the park? No problem! Is your train ride dragging on and you want to add some color and fun to your compartment? Of course! Its versatility makes 8 and Out! the perfect gift.

The colorful and high-quality design is child-friendly, humorous, and creates the perfect atmosphere as you explore the city and rescue the animals.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Bibles & Buff Coats: English Civil War Rules  or Miniatures Wargames 1642-1651
description:
Wargame Rules for the English Civil War.  Bibles & Buff Coats is a set of tabletop miniatures games rules that portray the forces supporting King Charles I and those supporting Parliament during the English Civil Wars.  Players take on the roles of Generals who command the unique and varied forces of Foote, Horse and Artillery of this colorful period. Using pikes, muskets, swords and cannons, players can either lead their troops to a glorious victory for their chosen cause, or to an inglorious and fatal defeat at the hands of a more ruthless and cunning opponent.  The future of England is in your hands.

—description from the publisher

Set sail to find Isla Bomba!
Equip your submarine. Free your crew from prison. Raid the observatory, and chart the course leading to Isla Bomba. Once anchored, will you be able to resist the different temptations on this explosive, mythical island?

This expansion for Captain Flip adds 4 new Adventure boards and 5 new Treasure Maps.

Your games will never be the same.

Dive into the cutthroat world of international espionage in  Martinis at Midnight: A Game of High-Stakes Espionage, a strategic card game for 4 Agents.

Your goal is to become the Mastermind by securing the highest Influence Score when the game reaches its explosive end. But be wary: the mission could quickly descend into The Collapse.

Key Features of the Game:

The Double-Cross: At the start of each round, participate in a pivotal card-passing phase where you pass two cards face-down to your left and the crucial third card, the "Double-Cross," face-up to any other Agent at the table.
Spycraft Suits: Wrangle with four suits: Protocol, Exposure, Cipher, and Shadow. These suits govern the trick-taking mechanics, where you must follow suit if possible.
Vulnerability & Lethality: Manage your risk, as certain low-value cards (Lethality Cards: 2s through 5s of Exposure and Shadow) can trigger a penalty if played off-suit. The winner of that trick is forced to take a Vulnerability Token, which is a heavy penalty of -5 points against their final score.
Game-Changing Aces: Control the flow of the game with two unique Ace cards:
The Secret Exchange (Ace of Cipher): Play it out of turn to blindly swap one card with another player.
The Extraction (Ace of Protocol): Instantly claim the current trick, regardless of the suit led.
The game ends immediately when a player reaches or exceeds 100 Points (The Mastermind) , or when any Agent accumulates 6 Vulnerability Tokens (The Collapse). Only the Agent with the highest Influence Score at that moment wins!

—description from the publisher

Besties vs Baddies is a co-operative expansion for Fable Fury that adds new heroes, enemies, and chaotic skills that twist your party’s ongoing misadventures.

The expansion introduces six new heroes, each with unique dice and signature skills, along with three new Monsters. Mix and match the new content with your core game to create fresh challenges, wild combinations, and even louder table banter.

Every session follows the familiar Fable Fury rhythm: explore dangerous maps, uncover traps and events, and battle monsters as a fully co-operative team. Use coins, loot, and skills to upgrade your heroes between realms before plunging back into the fray. Defeat the final boss to claim victory—or watch your “besties” fall to the baddies once again.

—description from the publisher

A Hilarious Twist on a Classic Favorite: GOAT (Greatest of All Time) takes the classic charades game and flips it on its head.  Choose your hilarious action card and the silliest modifiers from the group.  Act out the combination and put on the show of a lifetime!  Points are awarded to the players whose cards were chosen, with extra points to the actor with the most memorable performance.  The player with the most points at the end of the game wins and receives the GOAT medal.

—description from the publisher

In Dr. Science, each player is a brilliant PhD competing to earn prestige by playing tricks, building hypotheses, and validating their findings. On your turn, you’ll play a card to the current trick — choosing from your hand or tableau — and optionally add cards to your hypothesis (your personal tableau of sets and runs), then draw a new card from the display. Score points by taking tricks and validating 2–4 cards at once, but only if your entire hypothesis remains valid.

Every player has their own Specialty suit, acting as a personal trump that can shift the tide of any trick — but only one trump can be active at a time! Balancing trick play with long-term tableau building creates rich decisions every turn. Will you spend a card to win a trick, or save it to complete your next big breakthrough?

Play smart, validate often, and become the most celebrated scientist!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Tornado: The Pachanga of Poker Vip – Texas Edition
description:
Tornado: The Pachanga of Poker Vip — Texas Edition transforms traditional Texas Hold'em into a dynamic social experience fueled by unpredictability. The classic mechanics of betting and building the best hand remain intact, but players must now navigate a swirling layer of chaos powered by dice and tokens.

Each turn, a player rolls the Blue Die, which introduces personal effects, global disruptions, recoveries, or forced reveals. Some results trigger the Red Die, unleashing table-wide tornado actions like exchanging cards, rotating hands, or synchronized reveals. The familiar tension of poker becomes a high-energy social battle where strategy collides with controlled randomness.

Despite the chaos, Tornado respects the core of Texas Hold'em: pot, betting rounds, community cards, and hand hierarchy. The result is a faster, louder, and more emotional version of poker—pure Pachanga energy.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Spears of Valour: Mass Fantasy battles in the Legend of Valaya
description:
Spears of Valour is a mass-fantasy game for 15mm or 28mm miniatures, set in an Epic Fantasy world. Each model represents one warrior on the field, in battles of between 50-200 miniatures per side. All measurements are in inches and players only need to use the humble D6 to play.

Spears of Valour has been designed with ergonomics and accessibility in mind. The rules section of the book consist of only eleven pages. Every rule has been streamlined, without oversimplifying things. There is only one page of magick and all spells only last a single turn. There is only one page of special rules. All of the army lists are one page each. The morale system is unique and entirely straight forward. There is very little book-keeping outside of wound and activation markers.

Players can focus more on strategy and less on looking through a rulebook.

Gameplay:
Regiments activate in initiative order and may receive two orders per turn - this creates an entirely different strategic experience to the usual mass-fantasy games. Horse archers can hit and run, cavalry can swing round a flank and strike in one phase, fast regiments can block off line-of-sight. The tactical possibilities are endless.

Melee combat has an emphasis on simultaneous combat (unless you are flanked) and lasts multiple turns. Spears of Valour is a 'grind them down' game rather than a 'break them and chase them down' game. A single cavalry charge will not break a full infantry block, but combined arms tactics and 'strike-withdraw-strike' strategy might!

Spears of Valour is a two-roll combat engine. Players roll to hit, then roll to defeat enemy armour. 'Saves' are limited to exceptionally rare and potent forms of protection.

Most models have two wounds and the average game lasts eight turns.

What is in the book?
For gamers who like a world and narrative there are over thirty pages of lore, chronicling the world of Valaya from the Second Age to the Fourth. This includes the 'City of Shadows' campaign in the present era and many pages of metaphysical and philosophical indulgence.

This core book comes with seventeen army lists - including a generic 'Kingdoms' list for tutorials. The army lists cover most fantastical forces, knightly hosts, steppe nomads, barbarians, all types of elves, demons and undead, goblins, orcs and dwarfs.

Spears of Valour is not trying to re-invent the Epic Fantasy wheel, but it is trying to make a more ergonomic one.

This book, my good general, is just the beginning of that quest!

—description from the publisher

This mini-expansion provides a change of pace if you’ve played a lot of Dinner Party and want to shake things up. It includes 35 Topic cards, which have Abilities that players can use when trying to invite a Guest, plus one rule card. The base game is required to play.

Talk about the Weather (no matter with whom, that topic always goes), discuss War Anecdotes with Winston Churchill or Science Trivia with Marie Curie, tell Brazen Lies, share your opinions on Poetry, Etiquette, and Crime, and practice Active Listening and Name Dropping. Every Topic throws the game for another loop.

3 of the Topics can only be used with the Hot & Steamy expansion (using Coffee), and 3 can only be used with the Time To Get Served expansion (using Point Tokens). The other 29 Topic cards can be used without any expansions.

—description from the designer

Legend of Mythra is a fantasy skirmish game designed for 28mm or 15mm miniatures. Every model is set on its own base and acts independently. Players form warbands of between 10-75 models depending on the scale of battle and scenario. A typical game will take no more than an hour between two experienced players.

The game-engine uses an 'initiative' system, with players able to steal advantage by scoring more objective points. Combat is 'two roll' and resolves itself swiftly and intuitively. This game has been designed to be as ergonomic as possible, with universal special rules limited to a single page.

The unique Mythra setting is inspired by Baltic and Finnic culture and mythology, offering players a world in which to set their battles.

The 72 page core book is divided into three sections - the rules, army lists and lore.

1) The Core Rules:
Contains all the rules for the core game, covering movement, magick, missiles, melee and morale.

Also includes universal special rules, the armoury, magick items, scenarios and the 'War of Houses' mini-campaign.

2) The Army lists:
Contains seven extensive army lists for the major factions of Mythra, each with their own unique units and play style. These factions are:

     The Kingdom of Mythra - a mighty human army boasting many types of unit, plate armoured men-at-arms, cavalry, archers and the Royal Beast Corps of Mythra. 
     The Hordes of Pochyola - a horde army of lower-quality troops in high number. The Hordes are lead by mighty heroes and have many powerful monsters to call upon. Underestimate them at your own peril.
     The Dark Circle - an army of spectral warriors joined by the dreaded Necromages. The vorpal blades of the Dark Circle are deadly against plate-armour but their might is dulled against warded foes.
     The Hollow God - an army of ghoulish light infantry and an ad hoc gathering of terrors, lead by incarnated demonic heroes.
     The Auvans - an army of highly elite warriors, enchanted against magick and fleet through all terrains. The Auvans have many 'supernatural' allies they can call upon and high command of magick.
     The Horse Tribes - an army strong of hoof and bow, the Horse Tribes can outmaneouvre any other force on the battlefield. They are skilled warriors but lack heavily armoured troops and infantry.
     The Suurukai - an army of heavy infantry bound to the will of the Dragon-Lords. The heroes of the Suurukai are mounted on terrible draconic beasts and dominate the battlefields of Mythra.


3) The Book of Lore:
Covers the history of Mythra from the 'Third Age' to the 'Fifth Age' of the present. Also includes the metaphysics of magick, laments and epic song.

4) The Cheat Sheet (of Doom):
I have now uploaded a cheat sheet for quick reference during your battles. Most of the game is summarized on a single page (you will still need an understanding of the core rules to fully use it).

—description from the publisher

This mini-expansion for Dinner Party adds a little Salt standee and a platter (with rules on its back). The base game is required to play.

When you have a terrible turn where you get neither a Guest nor Drink, or when another player does something mean to you (stealing what’s yours or passing you-know-who to your table), you can now claim this consolation price.

Exclaim “Pass the Salt” and grab the shaker, even from another player. then discard it for an extra roll on one of your later turns.

No need to get salty!

—description from the designer

boardgame: ECO games Print and Play Suite
description:
ECO Games are learning tools designed by Winrock International that educate players about the different impacts community decisions can have on water, climate, environment, livelihoods and the long-term potential for communities to thrive.

In each game, a moderator sets up the game so each player has tokens for cash, soil health, and land use. In each of four rounds, players represent farmers making land use decisions that must balance resources (cash, soil health, water…). Random agricultural and disaster events must be paid for after each round of land use decisions are made. Disasters escalate to represent climate change.

Four games have been developed as part of the ECO Game suite of tools, each with different focus areas and key learning outcomes:

Caribbean Climate Resilience (CCR) Game: developed under the USDA Caribbean Climate Hub Project “Enabling climate-smart decisions for agriculture and forestry in the U.S. Caribbean.”
ECO Game: Northern Ghana: developed under the USAID Ghana Agriculture and Natural Resource Management (USAID AgNRM) Program,
ECO Game: Ghana Deforestation-Free Cocoa: developed under USAID’s Supporting Deforestation-Free Cocoa in Ghana), and
ECO Game: Water Security: developed under the USAID Sustainable Water Partnership.

Even at Odds is a two-player area majority strategy game using one set of double-six dominos. Each turn, players will play a domino from hand to create the biggest group of even or odd numbers, depending on which Team they’re on. Of course, those same dominos could be used to block and break the opponent’s biggest group.

On each turn, players will play one tile from their hand to the board with the objective of creating a larger single connected group of their Team’s numbers than their opponent. A tile can be played or stacked. After playing or stacking a tile, players draw one tile from the boneyard to replace it until the boneyard runs out. The game ends after both players have played their entire hands.

—description from the designer

In the land of Deth, there are legendary Necromancers, whose power can warp life and death. Some of these Necromancers become so powerful and so dangerous that the greatest heroes have no choice but to respond. Often, such battles end in the Necromancer's destruction, but their legacies endure through their creations - both undead and arcane.

The grimoires of these ancient sorcerers are coveted for their secrets and the profound magic they contain. Recently, one such tome, The Cruel Grimoire, has been unearthed. The true name of the Necromancer who penned it is lost to time; they are known simply as The Cruel One. Their accursed spells are once again loose in the world, ready to be wielded in your games.

These new Necromantic Powers may be selected whenever your Necromancer gains access to an additional Necromantic Power, both during initial creation and in the post-game process.

At the dawn of the global age, six continents — North America, South America, Europe, Asia, Africa and Australia — clash for economic dominance in the global marketplace. Players have been chosen by their country to do business around the world, buying all the countries they can while defending their home country’s sovereignty from hostile takeovers. The game teaches international business, geography and mathematics. It is suitable for two to six players.

Blood and Skulls: The Crucible is a dark fantasy narrative campaign game about the cost of power.

Guide flawed heroes through a dying world shaped by corruption, sacrifice, and impossible choices. Every decision matters, and every victory comes with a price.

Tactical combat meets narrative consequence  
Fight through intense encounters using a structured turn system that blends positioning, risk, and resource management.

Power comes at a cost  
Corruption, Madness, and Addiction are core systems that transform your character and shape your path.

A branching campaign  
Play through a multi-act campaign where decisions, outcomes, and alliances influence future scenarios, enemies, and rewards.

Dynamic character evolution  
Customize your hero through gear, Crucible Paths, magic schools, and personal Secrets tied to your alignment.

Not all players remain allies  
Some choices may lead to rivalry, conflict… and even deadly confrontation between players.

Each playthrough tells a different story because your choices define who you become.

It's a magical moment when you enter the room on Christmas Day, and all the brightly wrapped presents are laid out around the festively decorated Christmas tree. But wait! Are there two IDENTICAL presents?!
As a Christmas elf, try to avoid such a faux pas so the magic of Christmas can unfold unhindered: Lay out 10 cards overlapping to arrange as many different gifts as possible around the Christmas trees – without any duplicates!
A fun Christmas print&play solo puzzle!

—description from the designer

This single-card promo for Dinner Party adds a small challenge: If any player manages to have each of the five Occupations at their Table twice, they may claim the Dessert Cart. The owner of the card gets 2 extra Points at Scoring.

—description from the designer

Legends, assemble! This isn’t just a card game. It’s a clash of destinies. Grab your deck, summon your inner warrior, and face off in explosive combat. Friends become rivals, your table becomes the arena, and bragging rights are forged in the heat of battle. Think fast, play bold, and carve your legend. Only one fighter will stand victorious!

Brawlhalla: The Card Game offers three game modes:


     The more accessible Discovery mode, which allows you to play with a shared deck of Action cards (with no text on it)



     The Blitz mode, where each legend has their own deck and can only be knocked out once, offering fast-paced, tactical games.



     Classic mode for longer, more strategic games where each legend can be knocked out up to 3 times.


—description from the publisher

In this unique, family-friendly game, players wager on how many tricks they'll win each round. Every correct bet advances your pawn along the track, bringing you closer to victory. But place your bets carefully, one wrong move, and your competition might leave you in the dust!

First player to reach the finish line wins. Ideal for 2 to 8 players, Parlay embraces the thrill of competitive race and ensures every game is unpredictable and exciting.

—description from the designer

In Stack Attack 2 players race to create the pattern of cubes on one of 120 challenge cards and ring the bell. If the pattern is correct that player gets a point. If the pattern is not correct the opponent gets the point. The first to 10 points wins.

The game consists of two sets of 8 cubes for each player. The cubes have different background and pattern colours. The cards are divided into 3 sets of 40 in 4, 6, and 8 block challenges. The bell is stored on top of the box, held securely by a cutout in the box top.

boardgame: Epic Spell Wars of the Battle Wizards: Hocus Porkus Familiar Promo
description:
Hocus Porkus Familiar Promo for use with Epic Spell Wars of the Battle Wizards: Annihilageddon Deck-Building Game. Available as part of a prize support kit. Combines with Abraca-Labrador.

Reveal and draw the top two cards of your deck.
Heal HP equal to the combined cost.

Defense: You may discard this to avoid an Attack. If you do, draw a card and redirect the effect to the attacker.
family: Game: Epic Spell Wars of the Battle Wizards

description:
Pax Feudalis places players in the role of rising Marquises in 13th-century Italy, a land fractured by rival factions, political turmoil, and constant power struggles. Each player builds a personal tableau of characters, constructions, and influences, all represented by cards. The game’s tension comes from balancing your resources, expanding your engine, and reacting to historical pressures such as Wars, Shortages, and shifting faction power. The market steadily advances the timeline, revealing events and investiture cards that mark key moments in the struggle for dominance.

On their turn, players rotate cooldowns, resolve income, and then take two actions. Actions include buying cards from the Market Town, playing cards to their tableau by paying resources, bribing cards to gain additional resources, selling cards for money, or activating all actions of a chosen color in their tableau. Resources themselves are tracked through rotating cards that rise and fall throughout play, encouraging timely spending. Cards may produce resources, grant abilities, build structures, or add faction symbols — all of which shape your strategy. Every decision matters because the timing of each action influences not only your own engine but also the opportunities available to opponents.

The goal of the game is to earn two of the three Investitures: Papal, Imperial, and Communal Autonomy. Whenever a Signoria Check card appears or is discarded, players compare the relevant faction symbols in their tableaus. The player with the highest influence in that faction gains the corresponding Investiture card. The moment a player earns their second Investiture, they win immediately. If no one wins by the third check, the game moves to tiebreakers based on symbols, cards, and total wealth. Victory comes from clever timing, engine mastery, and outmaneuvering your rivals in a land where power is never secure.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Battles For Empire: Napoleonic Wargame Scenarios 1807 & 1809
description:
The Napoleonic era offers the wargamer a compelling challenge because the Napoleonic battlefield witnessed a balance among the three arms—infantry, cavalry, and artillery—never equaled during other military periods. The tabletop general who best handles the interplay between the three arms will duplicate the era’s successful generals. Keep in mind Napoleon’s observation: “Of the three arms—cavalry, infantry, and artillery—none must be despised. All three are equally important.” A historical officer or a tabletop general who fights his three arms in the coordinated manner of the ‘Iron’ Marshal Davout, the gallant Marshal Lannes, or the aggressive Russian General Bagration is an exceptional commander indeed. We hope that these scenarios offer the opportunity to showcase your skills while having fun and perhaps learning a thing or two about our favorite gaming era.

Your rules may not be ours: Different? Yes; Better? Whatever, no problem. This scenario booklet strives to appeal to the Napoleonic gamer regardless of his choice of rules. Toward that goal we offer explanations and examples to provide as seamless as possible a transition from the orders of battle in this booklet to your gaming table.

Each scenario has the following elements

     Historical Narrative: the battle’s historical context
     Order of Battle: a list of the troops you need to fight the scenario
     Skirmishers: identification of which troops, if any, may fight in skirmish order
     Special Rules: rules unique to this scenario
     Table Top Map: how to arrange your gaming table
     Terrain Notes: the gaming implications of the terrain features
     Setup: where the rival sides may deploy their troops
     Time: the historical amount of time the battle lasted
     Victory Conditions: how to assess who has won the fight
     Gamer’s Notes: our view of why this scenario is compelling


Thirteen scenarios with 14 color maps, and 31 color and b/w illustrations.

     Mohrungen: Jan 25, 1807
     Bagration's Rear Guard: Feb 7, 1807
     Davout at Eylau: Feb 8, 1807
     Braunsberg: Feb 26, 1807
     Allied Invasion of Germany: Late April 1807
     Relief of Danzig: May 15, 1807
     Teugen Hausen: Apr 19, 1809
     Eckmuhl: Apr 22, 1809
     Train Raid: May 1809
     Lannes' Last Charge: May 22, 1809
     Raab: Jun 19, 1809
     Cadzand: Aug 2, 1809

This is not a full rule set! This book is simply pre-made foraging parties and meant to be freely distributed. Further rules, scenarios, and more are in the main rulebook and future expansions.

The year is 1861, and the first shots of the Civil War have thundered across Fort Sumter. The nation is divided, and chaos reigns. Supplies are scarce, and armies are fragile, and now every decision could mean survival—or destruction. Now, it's your turn to Forage Ahead through history.

In Forage Ahead players lead squads of Civil War soldiers on quick daring foraging missions across a countryside torn apart by war. Each miniature represents a unique soldier with specialized training, unique backgrounds, and skills. Navigating terrain while scavenging for; food, arms, and vital supplies, all while evading hostile foraging parties.

—description from the publisher

In this expansion, you will find further statehoods, training, equipment, skills and 3 brand new campaigns for you and your opponent to play. Now your volunteer soldiers can fend off bushwackers or have your militia take your unit mascot on a patrol. While the theming of this expansion is the conflict found in the early war border states, feel free to use the material in this expansion as additions to your already existing foraging parties.

In Forage Ahead players lead squads of Civil War soldiers on quick daring foraging missions across a countryside torn apart by war. Each miniature represents a unique soldier with specialized training, unique backgrounds, and skills. Navigating terrain while scavenging for; food, arms, and vital supplies, all while evading hostile foraging parties.

NOTE: You will want to download either the free rules or purchase the full rulebook before purchasing this expansion.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Before The War: Land of Memory
description:
With Before the War, rewrite history. In this historical management game, take on the role of a nation. Negotiate, develop your country, distinguish yourself, but maintain global balance during tumultuous periods in history.

The historical references and elements are validated by the Center for Studies on War and Society (CEGESOMA).

This game immerses the player in pre-war situations (1914-1918 and 1940-1945) and invites them to observe the realities of the time from a different perspective: what if you had led the nation during that period?

—description from the designer

Avec Before the War, réécrivez l'histoire. Dans ce jeu de gestion historique, incarnez une nation. Négociez, développez votre pays, distinguez-vous, mais préservez l'équilibre mondial durant les périodes tumultueuses de l'histoire.

Les références et les éléments historiques sont validés par le Centre d'études sur la guerre et la société (CEGESOMA).

Ce jeu plonge le joueur dans des situations d'avant-guerre (1914-1918 et 1940-1945) et l'invite à observer les réalités de l'époque sous un angle différent : et si vous aviez dirigé la nation de cette période ?

—description from the designer (French)

You're pets that love to cause trouble and love it even more when your fellow pets get the blame. Can you outsmart your fellow house mates whithout getting cought by your owner?

Pesky Pets is quick game full of surprises and tactical depth with a lot of interesting decisions and fun stories.

In Pesky Pets players simultaneously reveal cards that show

     the level of mischief they perform
     the initiative number (time of day) that indicates the turn order in which each animal performs their shenanigans.
     the room they move to


You choose how to orient the played card to show whether you're causing trouble first, and move to the indicated room afterwards, or whether you move to that room first and perform the mischief there.

When performing the mischief, you place the card in the room your animal is in. But better be careful: when you make too much noise, the owner will come to that room to see what all the fuss is about. This is an excellent time to frame your fellow pets, but when the owner finds you alone in the room, or when you perform the mischief in front of your owner, you get the blame yourself.

After playing all your cards (7-8 depending on player count) all the collected mischief cards score you minus points and the player with the least minus points (the best reputation) wins the game!

In Pesky Pets players are invited to narrate what their animal did in that particular room (i.e the bedroom, the kitchen) ranging from mild annoyance (level 1) to unpleasant chaos (level 4) creating memorable stories each time the game is played.

—description from the designer

Okidoki: The Pachanga Colors Vip is a fast-paced, color-matching, hand-management card game where players race to empty their hand while navigating sudden twists triggered by two chaos dice.
Players must match cards by color or symbol, chain special effects, interrupt opponents, reverse the flow of play, and unleash table-wide actions that can flip the outcome in a single turn.

Every round is a vibrant storm: players swap cards, force others to draw, steal tempo, block incoming effects with tokens, or trigger powerful multi-color plays.
The UPV dice system adds constant tension — you never know if your turn will be smooth… or if a dice result will explode into a cascade of forced reveals, direction changes, or tactical decisions that shake the entire table.

Okidoki keeps the joyful, familiar spirit of classic color-and-symbol shedding games, but transforms it into a high-interaction, cinematic, unpredictable experience, with emotional swings, table talk, and dramatic comebacks built into the core system.

Simple to learn, wildly replayable, and optimized for all group sizes, Okidoki turns every match into a chaotic fiesta of colors, tactics, and laughter.

—description from the designer

It is time to make Napoleonics fun again.

This game is designed to allow people to play quick pick up games with their old grognard miniatures from the glory days of the 1980s.  
Any basing style works, as players use a sabot.  
New players will enjoy the ease of play.

Fast paced, beer and pretzels wargaming as God intended.

—description from the publisher

Fallen Favours is a trick-taking game for 2-5 players where they are trying to collect the most souls and guide them into the afterlife. Throughout the game, players bid on how many tricks they believe they will earn in the current round. If successful, the “Powers That Be” grant additional souls for you to guide into the Afterlife. Don’t be too greedy or too conservative, missed bids force your souls to slip through your grasp. If you lose souls, the “Powers That Be” provide Favour Tokens to help aid along the journey. If you run out of souls throughout the game, you’ve been shunned and banned to the underworld. Are you able to contest to the last round with the most souls and obtain your rightful place in the Afterlife?

Players will wager how many tricks they will win in the current round. Based on the outcome, they will lose or earn Souls determined whether they achieved their bid or not. The scoring is "tiered" based on how many tricks they bid. Players will earn favour tokens if they did not make their bid. These tokens can be spent on Favours that may give that player an edge on the next round and slightly manipulate the hand or gameplay.

For added variables, objective cards can be achieved throughout the current round. These will offer favour tokens or souls as rewards. Though not necessary to win, they can be used as a catch-up factor or an additional strategy if the current round seems lost to particular players.

The game contains rules for a two-player variant called "Cato". A bot plays alongside that players will control and manipulate throughout the round. The control of Cato is dependant on who is winning the current trick. Cato can also be used in 3 or 4 player games as an added gameplay variant.

—description from the publisher

The battles in Norway in 1940 were a series of battles at the battalion and brigade level. The Germans were the first to attack, landing in Narvik and taking over the port and surrounding areas with little resistance from the Norwegians.

The largest battle took place near Narvik, when French, Polish, British and Norwegian troops attacked the German mountain infantry, supported by paratroopers and sailors. The Allies' success was not complete, however. They failed to destroy the encircled Germans, and they held out until relief arrived. The game also includes battles at Kvam, Dombas and Midtskogen.

The game covers selected landing operations in the Pacific. They are mainly carried out by the American army, whose primary goal was to capture base after base, suitable for continuing an offensive towards Japan. One of the operations, landing at Sakhalin, is carried out by the Red Army. It is 2nd game of Pacific series

A competitive dungeon-crawling adventure for 2–5 players

Dive into a fast-paced, ever-shifting dungeon where every turn expands the map, escalates the danger, and opens new tactical opportunities. Players battle monsters, snatch up valuable treasures, and shape the very layout of the labyrinth, all while racing to escape with the most gold before the darkness closes in.

Each dungeon tile offers unique rule-twists that can be revealed or concealed. By rotating or obscuring parts of a tile, players decide whether to unleash a powerful effect or keep it hidden, creating a dynamic mix of strategy, bluffing, and risk-reward choices.

But beware: the Gloom Level is always rising. Unresolved monsters, dangerous tiles, and deep exploration feed the growing darkness, transforming the dungeon into a deadlier and more unpredictable place.

Use the dungeon to your advantage by placing monsters, traps, and disruptive tiles to hinder your rivals or force them down perilous paths. When enough special tiles have been uncovered, the exit finally opens and the final sprint for freedom begins.

—description from the designer

The balance of power keeps shifting and only one team can prevail. Duālis is a tactical trick-taking game played between two teams of two. While easy to learn, nuanced decisions and surprising strategy are revealed with each new play.

Over thirteen tricks, each partnership must work together to win the same number of tricks as the coins they have collected. While playing a card, you may collect and use a coin to change your card's color and, possibly, make your partner’s card stronger. But be careful! Taking coins too quickly will limit your partnership’s flexibility in the important later tricks.

The game's mechanics explore the interconnected relationships between opposites: two teams of two, two suits each with two trump colors, and how the shifting balance between each affects the others.

Can you and your partner work together to unsettle your opponents and create harmony in an unsteady world?

(Includes a variant for 3 or 4 players playing individually.)

パワーバランスが移動するため、どちらかのチームが優勢になります。デュアリスは、2人でチームとなり、2チームで対戦する戦術的トリックゲームです。簡単にプレイできるのに、毎回、微妙な判断や驚きの戦術に気付きます。

13回以上のトリックの中で、それぞれのチームは、集めるコインと勝つトリックの数を合わせるため、協力しなければなりません。プレイ中、コインを集めたり、コインを使ってカードの色を変えることによって、パートナーのカードを強くすることができるかもしれません。でも気をつけて！コインを早く取ったり、取りすぎたりすることによって、この後のトリックでのパートナーの立ち位置が悪くなる場合があります。

このゲームの構造は、2人で構成されたチームが２チーム、2つのトランプカラーに2つのスーツ、そしてそれぞれが与える影響のバランスがどのように移動するか、こういった相反するものが互いに絡み合って、それをいかにうまく探求していくか、というものです。あなたとパートナーは、協力して敵を惑わし、この不安定な世界でハーモニーを生み出すことができるでしょうか？

First release: Tokyo Game Market, November 2025.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: In the Ranks The Great Australian Military Game
description:
In the Ranks, The Great Australian Military Game has historically and political significance in Australian history. Published before Australia had a unified defence force, each of the six Australian colonies had their own Colonial militia.

Optimistically, the game sees a Australian military where players rise through the ranks to become a General. Game play is by roll of a dice or teetotum. Players move a playing piece around the outside of the gameboard 61 places, then they continue to move through an inner elaborate path of 80 spaces to become a General. Along the journey players pass through various military ranks, with setbacks or advances on certain squares. The first to become a General is the winner.

The game was published around the time when there was growing voice for a unified Australian Army and was advertised in newspapers during March to May 1893

—user summary

Is your team dragging its heels? Productivity in the pits? Not anymore!
Say Hello to Maxwell — The Supercomputer of Tomorrow, Today!
From the fine folks at Moloch Industries, this marvel of modern engineering keeps your team on track and sends your company’s productivity soaring to brand-new heights!
It’s simple, it’s effective, and it’s 100% guaranteed to boost results.
The top companies in the country are already on board — what are you waiting for?

The Idles is an asymmetric card game for two players. Set in a nostalgic 1950s America with dystopian undertones, the game pits a mysterious supercomputer — obsessed with maximizing corporate productivity — against a lowly employee struggling under that very weight.

The supercomputer, Maxwell, will line up his programs and defend them with supervisors until they can be executed successfully...by any means necessary! Meanwhile, the employee must constantly prove their worth by grinding out productivity awards, or else risk being branded an idler.

And bad things are said to happen to those who idle. Better not find out!

—description from the publisher

The Idles is a game conceived as a satire of AI and capitalism. As such, we have taken the artistic liberty to use genAI in order to create its illustrations. No AI has been used in any other area of the making of the game, nor on our digital platform, Babll.

-description from the AI disclaimer

Criaturas Matemáticas is a fast-paced, educational card game where players battle using creatures inspired by mathematical concepts. Each creature represents a different area of math—addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, estimation, geometry, measurement, time, and quantities—and performs attacks based on real calculations, dice rolls, or prediction challenges.

Players take turns drawing Energy cards, assigning them to power their creatures, and performing attacks by rolling a ten-sided die (d10) or solving short estimation tasks. As creatures gain energy, they can evolve into stronger forms with upgraded abilities and higher damage output. Strategic decisions—when to assign energy, when to evolve, which attack to use, and when to activate special ability cards—add tactical depth while keeping gameplay accessible.

The goal is simple:Defeat all of your opponent’s creatures by reducing their health to zero.

The game promotes mental math, probability thinking, strategic planning, and quick decision-making, wrapped in a colorful and child-friendly fantasy theme. Designed for both classroom use and family play, Criaturas Matemáticas blends learning and fun in a way that feels natural and exciting for players of all ages.

boardgame: Orange Quest: Natalie Character Promo Card
description:
Character card
Lets you to move any up to 2 damage cards (+10 or -10 cards)

boardgame: Nuclear War: Damping Field Promo Card
description:
"DAMPING FIELD: Special: Hold until needed. Temporary damping field cancels all nuclear explosions. Every warhead landing on your country from now until your next turn is a “dud warhead—no effect”."

Flying Buffalo, Inc. created this card to be able to have a bonus to sell at conventions and give away as prizes. It is compatible with all games of the Nuclear War series.

boardgame: Nuclear War: Big Trade Show in Your Country Promo Card
description:
"BIG TRADE SHOW IN YOUR COUNTRY: Secret. There’s a major trade show in your country. 1 Million people from each player’s country rush to come see the newest releases. Every player in the game must give you one million population."

Flying Buffalo, Inc. created this card to be able to have a bonus to sell at conventions and give away as prizes. It is compatible with all games of the Nuclear War series.

A Game For Pregame (or for those recovering alcoholics, you can play it sober)

Are you a boba addict? A family disappointment? A wholesome fuccboi? We got you covered. This game will help you bring more (dis)honour to the family.

To play the game, match the white cards to the red prompt card selected for each round. Whoever comes up with the funniest combo, wins. Or whoever comes up with the worst pairing, drinks.

Spice up the pregame, late night dorm hangouts, or in the absence of alcohol, kill time during family gatherings with our finely crafted cards for maximum laughter. No math required.

With 400 caption cards and 100 prompt cards, our game set weights 1.08 kg but it’s not as heavy as the (dis)honor you bring to the family.

—description from the publisher
  
  
How do you play the game?

Select a judge (if you’re playing by yourself, skip this step)

1. Distribute 10-15 white cards to each player
2. Shuffle the red prompt cards, with the prompts faced down
3. Flip over the red prompt card at the top of the shuffled deck
4. Each player selects their funniest white card to match the red prompt card*
5. The judge decides the funniest and worst combo
6. Whoever comes up with the funniest combo wins the round and whoever comes up with the worst combo takes a shot
7. Take a new white card from the pile and start your next round

    If the player feels that none of their cards match the prompt, they can take half a shot to trade for a new white card from the deck.

Solo Crawling is a customizable single-player dungeon-crawling card game built around rogue-like mechanics and unpredictable encounters. It’s played utilizing multiple decks to randomize everything from dungeon layout, encounters, loot, and gear as you crawl through the dungeon to battle a randomized boss.

A key feature is the ability to tailor each deck to customize your experience. You can increase or decrease the amount of dungeon cards to adjust the maximum size of the dungeon, adjust the amount of loot, gear, healing potions, and more to make the experience easier or harder.

With future expansions in the works, you will be able to further customize the experience, adding multiple levels to the dungeon.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Absolute Monarch: A Historical Tabletop Wargame Rules Set for Casual & Scenario Play
description:
Absolute Monarch:The long awaited addition to the Absolute Emperor rules system.

Here players can use the mechanics they are familiar with in fight major battles of the Seven Years' War.  
Everything you like about AE is here, but with tweaks to promote the use of linear tactics of the period.  
Additionally, there is a sub-set of rules for fighting the American War of Independance.  
You get the core rules, where the maneuver units are brigades regiments, the AWI variant taylored for smaller battles and difficult terrain, and top-down silhouettes to allow new gamers to the period to try the game prior to buying miniatures.  
There is a QRS as well.  
Unlike Absolute Emperor, the formatting is design for key word recognition, ease of concept comprehension, graphic representations of important rules, and easy to remember combat adjudication.

—description from the publisher

When I wrote Kalashnikov Tribal Multi-Battalion rules, I integrated logistics, but hopefully in a ‘simple’ manner so they don’t get in the way but are a necessary part of your planning.
The obvious next step was bringing things together to try and show the entire logistic tail. Now I don’t want to get into the whole economic side of it. There are board games and others which do that well enough. But I wanted to somehow tackle the transport side, at least when it is ‘in theatre’. So these help build the campaign around the wargames.

As well as Kalashnikov Tribal Multi-Battalion rules I also want to show how these logistic rules can be used with Hellfire, and Hell and Fateful Decisions. I have even made general suggestions for rule sets written by others.
But the advantage of introducing logistics in a comparatively simple manner is that you can now use your airpower, artillery, and drones to interdict your opponent’s supplies, to slow them down even if you don’t stop them, and by doing so force your opponent to dance to your tune. While at the same time they are attempting to do the same things to you.

—description from the designer

Some animals are playing traditional Japanese instruments, but they seem to be stuck. With your help‐using Rest and Technique Cards‐you can guide their music.
In the world of traditional instruments, numbers are expressed as sounds. In this game, numbers (Note Cards) are played in a way reminiscent of games such as Sevens or Solitaire. Your goal is to weave the sequence together, use performance techniques, 
play all your cards and complete the performance.
When the performance ends, a fan illustration will appear. Enjoy the changing fan patterns that emerge with each playthrough.

—description from the publisher

Batalla Final is a fast, strategic naval combat game where players deploy their entire fleet and use each ship to activate unique attacks. To survive enemy fire, players must defend themselves with the correct defense cards; if they don’t have them, their ships take damage and must be repaired before sinking. The action is dynamic and tactical: attack, defend, repair, and outmaneuver opponents. The first player to fully deploy (activate) their fleet—or sink the opponent’s ships before losing their own—wins Batalla Final.

—description from the publisher

El Que Piensa Pierde is a word card game with 5 different ways to play using syllables, themes, and letters. Some modes are fast-reaction games where a syllable and a theme are revealed and players must quickly say words before their opponents to win cards. Other modes challenge players to form words as fast as possible using the visible syllables, or to play a Scrabble-style version on the back of the cards, building words with letters and scoring points. The deck also includes a word-sequence game, all in one versatile, family-friendly box.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Deep Rock Galactic: Pretzel Time – It's Good For Ya! Promo
description:
Promo Rock & Stone card from Spiel Essen 2025.

Pay 2 Nitra, then fully heal all Dwarves and creatures. All knocked over Dwarves stand up.

Play as 1 of 6 Mennonite leaders attempting to peacefully claim the Russian Steppe for themselves then flee the advancing Soviet Regime. Players must navigate competitors and the Communist Party through action selection, resource management, and area-control.

At the start of the game, players decide together which of 12 possible Achievements they will include as the objectives for their game. Achievement objectives range from building structures to controlling important territories on the board. Players can choose to play with anywhere from 5-12 potential Achievements, meaning that each game of Steppe is unique depending on the combination of Achievements that the play group decides to use.

Each turn, players must decide between 5 possible actions: harvest, trade, movement, building, and gossip. A good strategy will require players to rotate through multiple actions throughout the game, responding to their opponents and the unique game state of the board.

boardgame: Hellfire in the City: A Solo Campaign for Hellfire
description:
Hellfire in the City is a stand-alone solo sci-fi wargames campaign. 
Starting small and building your forces as you go, your aim is to control the sprawling city of Velonopolis.

The city is currently a patchwork of territories controlled by everything from petty thugs leading their bands of street scum through to corporate askari supported by mecha.

As well as city creation and discovering what hostile forces await you, this campaign guide contains rules allowing you to build new units, even mecha. At the same time, those troops you already lead will gain experience. Starting from scratch you can put together riverine or even air mobile forces.

At last you will realise why proper armies had vehicle recovery units and field hospitals.

Although designed for Hellfire rules and 6mm figures, there is no reason why you couldn’t convert troop types to fit with your favourite rule set. Certainly because of the way the rules were written, the campaign should be comparatively easy to drift so you can play it using Hell by Starlight.

Other campaigns finish with the stirring words, ‘Conquer or Die!’ In this case we allow losers to flee the city, pursued by their creditors.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Dictopia: Le goût de la liberté
description: A booster consisting of five gastronomy-themed mission cards.

boardgame: Dictopia: La tête dans les étoiles
description: A booster consisting of five astronomy-themed mission cards.

President Maker is a 2–4 player board game that captures the fierce intrigue and strategic maneuvers of a Korean presidential election through immersive gameplay.

Each player becomes a fictional presidential candidate, traveling across the country to hold campaign rallies, gain public support through policies, and—at times—resort to political maneuvers. The election consists of two rounds before the final vote. Your total Support, along with the Engagement levels formed in each region over those two rounds, will ultimately determine the outcome of the Vote Count.

The fate of each candidate hinges on two forces: the loyal base built throughout the campaign, and the voters who show up on election day to cast their decisive ballots. Step into a parallel Korea and challenge yourself to become its next president—through strategy, nerve, and a bit of political drama.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Another world, another selection of solo wargames campaigns for Hellfire, the 6mm sci-fi rules for people with small figures and large imaginations.

On the temporarily misplaced world of Hind II you can lead caravans across the wastes, jockey for power in an ancient city whilst trying to ensure that the barbarians remain outside the gates, dominate petty city states, or carve your way to eternal glory (other destinies are available) at the head of your band of doughty (again, other options are available) nomad warriors.

On a world barely three meals away from anarchy you know you’re the one who can make a difference. Your political survival might depend on getting good rains, lest you’re swept away by desperate peasantry or a starving urban proletariat. A decent grain stockpile might win you more battles that an armoured battalion.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
Derin Su is a large, but slightly chilly, world. It is predominantly ocean. Ignoring the polar ice sheets, which are impressively large, a lot of the land surface in the northern hemisphere is tundra.

The idea was to create a world for those who want to include a naval and/or amphibious element in their game. There is also potential for under-sea action as well.

Whilst suited to use with an opponent, this book does allow the scenarios to be played solo. It introduces the idea of Dice Modified Negotiations. These allow the solo player to oversee the negotiations between various parties.

Although written for Hellfire 6mm sci fi rules, these are generic and the scenarios can easily be tweaked for other systems. Indeed, several times it has struck me that Derin Su is also an interesting world for those wanting a more Hell by Starlight level of skirmish. Given Hellfire and Hell by Starlight have some overlap, it’s not difficult to redefine Hellfire troop types to fit the skirmish rule set.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Apocalypse: Earth – Wargaming in a World Gone Wrong
description:
The year is 1954. Tsar Josef I (known to the world as "Stalin") unleashes the wrath of the Holy Soviet Army on the decadent oligarchies of the West. Nations are sundered, cities are leveled in the fury of the first assault. Millions perish amid the fury and the flames as forbidden technologies and unnatural scientific discoveries are unleashed.

But the war quickly slides out of control, spreading to Asia, Arabia, Africa and across the Atlantic to engulf the Americas... As bitter warfare rages from pole to pole, mankind's very survival hangs in the balance. 

Apocalypse: Earth is a fast and furious table top skirmish game for squad, platoon and company- size battles, suitable for 15, 28 or 20mm miniatures. Each model represents an individual trooper, drone or vehicle and an average size battle will last 1 - 2 hours.

Combat is fast and lethal, with an alternating units mechanic which encourages fluid tactical play - easy to learn but hard to master, this ruleset ensures fraught battles with mistakes savagely punished!

This fully-illustrated 151-page volume includes:

     Deployment, fire, movement, cover and close combat (all resolved with standard six-sided dice)
     Basic rules for infantry, support weapons, vehicles, morale, POWs, combat in buildings and indirect fire (including off-board artillery and air support)
     Optional advanced rules for players wanting a more detailed or varied game (including tournament rules, casualty evacuation and fires)
     Army lists for the European League, Atlantic Alliance, Holy Soviet Empire and Asian Communist Federation factions (including infantry and vehicle profiles)
     A history of the 20th century leading up to the nightmare of the Apocalypse War, including an overview of the Six Months War of 1914-15
     All necessary game counters, templates, and Fortunes Of War cards


Now updated with scenarios and new victory triggers!
Bonus: Solo Gaming - Zombie Mode!

Humanity's survival is in your hands!

Apocalypse: Earth is a fast paced miniature wargame set in a nightmare 20th century where an alternate World War has brought humanity to the brink of destruction. The skies are scorched, cities burn and thousands die each day as battles rage unceasingly around the globe. The demonic Tsar Josef I, known to all as Stalin, has unleashed the might of the Holy Soviet Empire in a bid to bring the world under his control, and now the flames of war rage on every continent on Earth. Humanity's survival and destiny is in your hands!

—description from the publisher

Bespoke is a compendium style climbing-shedding card game for 2 players using a standard deck of cards.

In compendium games, players compete to accomplish a series of different objectives. These objectives are known as contracts. Each player must fulfill every contract before the game concludes. Once a player has completed a particular contract, they are not allowed to attempt it again.

In Bespoke, the objective for every contract remains the same: to play all of your cards as quickly as possible. However, each contract is uniquely tailored, offering its own distinct twists on the rules of play.

—description from the designer

Teddy's Day is a fun educational puzzle game designed to help children learn the concept of "time" through play. The game includes a set of circular puzzle pieces depicting the daily activities of Teddy the bear during a typical kindergarten day. 
While assembling the puzzle, children learn to recognize different times of the day and match specific activities to the correct time, gradually building their understanding of time and daily rhythms.
The game also features a movable clock with hands, allowing children to interactively practice reading time in both analog and digital formats. This game also helps children develop temporal and spatial cognitive skills, which are crucial for cognitive growth at this age.

Treasure Hunt on the giant wobbly pillow!
Find the item shown on the picture card to win that card.
The first player to collect 3 cards wins the game.
And of course… no one said the pillar would stay still—it might move while you play!

Slip into your roles and set off on a treasure hunt to Sanderoog. You can expect an unsolved crime, realistic evidence, and tricky puzzles. Find out who among you is behind the crime as you follow the legacy of the pirate Störtebeker. Experience a thrilling adventure enchanted by a forgotten treasure, an ancient curse, and mysterious traces in the sand.
A long-kept secret brings you and your old school friends back to Sanderoog: Ten years ago, you stole a treasure map there to find the legendary loot of the pirate Störtebeker. But instead of gold, you found only sand, and the map was forgotten. Now new documents have surfaced—a parchment with mysterious folding instructions for the map and a curse said to lie upon the treasure. You decide to continue the search. What begins as an exciting adventure quickly turns into a fierce competition. The search takes a dark turn when Viola suddenly disappears. When you find her unconscious and locked inside a hut on the beach, one thing becomes clear: someone in your group wanted to eliminate her. Everyone is a suspect. Including you!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Schlüpft in eure Rollen und begebt euch auf Schatzsuche nach Sanderoog. Euch erwarten ein ungelöstes Verbrechen, realistische Beweismittel und knifflige Rätsel. Findet heraus, wer von euch hinter dem Verbrechen steckt, während ihr dem Vermächtnis des Piraten Störtebeker folgt. Erlebt ein spannendes Abenteuer im Bann eines vergessenen Schatzes, eines uralten Fluchs und geheimnisvoller Spuren im Sand.

Ein lang gehütetes Geheimnis bringt dich und deine alten Schulfreunde zurück nach Sanderoog: Vor zehn Jahren habt ihr dort eine Schatzkarte gestohlen, um den legendären Schatz des Piraten Störtebeker zu finden. Doch statt Gold blieb euch nur Sand und die Karte geriet in Vergessenheit. Nun sind neue Aufzeichnungen aufgetaucht – ein Pergament mit rätselhaften Faltanweisungen für die Karte und ein Fluch, der auf dem Schatz liegen soll. Ihr beschließt, die Suche fortzusetzen. Was zunächst als aufregendes Abenteuer beginnt, entwickelt sich rasch zu einem erbitterten Wettkampf. Die Suche nimmt eine düstere Wendung, als Viola plötzlich verschwindet. Als ihr sie bewusstlos und eingeschlossen in einer Hütte am Strand findet, wird klar: Jemand aus eurer Gruppe wollte sie ausschalten. Alle werden verdächtigt. Auch du!

—description from the publisher

Promo Card for Witchcraft

"Starting advantage: You start the game with either the moth familiar and 1 moth ingredient OR 3 moth ingredients."

Set the timer for 45 seconds and ask another player a question.  Examples of questions include "Say 3 made-up rock band names" or "Share 3 reasons why you would leave a date early" or "Say 3 things you should not put in a piñata.  Provide 3 answers to as many questions as possible before time runs out!

QU!CK QU!CK QU!CK is the fast-paced party game that sparks creativity and laughter with every clever question! With a ticking timer to keep the energy high, players must think fast, trust their instincts, and deliver their funniest answers on the spot. Anyone can play, best friends or new acquaintances, lawyers or accountants, even grandma or Uncle Bob! It’s easy to learn, full of creative prompts, and best of all, there are no wrong answers. Just pure, hilarious chaos. It’s the fastest way to have fun!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Powerhouses of the North / Phase IV:March of the Ants
description:
Two maps released for, and given out to all attendees of the inaugural Age of Steam UK weekend.

Powerhouses of the North takes inspiration from the city delivery and growth idea in the famous Splotter game Indonesia and interprets it onto the Age of Steam system. Cities start off with no set colour but as deliveries occur the colours are set for the game. Cities are either 2 or 3 cube capacity before becoming full until the end of the round. In goods growth, cities may also grow into level 2 or level 3 where they can accept a second or third set of deliveries.

Phase IV: March of the Ants takes the game down to the miniature level with the board setting as a picnic blanket. The players are part of a single colony of ants collecting and moving food scraps (cubes) from across the picnic and delivering them back to the nest. Only certain cities and cubes are active each round, which is randomised at setup. Cubes can also be delivered off board for collection and end game scoring. This score is dependent on how many soldier ants are delivered back to the graveyard of heroes

boardgame: I C E: Ics Sailboat Promo Tile
description:
Promo tile for I C E, as giveaway from Essen Spiel 25, when testing the game.
"Place 1 on the tile of your captain, and on the tile of another captain."

Fast Drive Football (FDF) FAC Edition is a one-player, tabletop simulation of American Pro Football. It's a "drive-by-drive" game, meaning that instead of simulating each individual play, you are simulating one possession at a time. Games take about 15-20 minutes to play once you learn the rules.

This edition replaces the dice rolls and chart look-ups in the original game with a deck of Fast Action Cards that speed up gameplay and simplify game timing. It remains 100% compatible with the team cards from the original game. Other changes in this Fast Action Card edition include:


     Each Drive Result FAC has the drive time printed on the card. There is no timing die like the original version of the game.
     Timing of the pro game has changed from the original 1-minute and 15-second increments (or “ticks”) to 30-second increments.
     Some Drive Result FACs have a dice-driven “split” result to keep the statistical integrity of the original game.
     The game was designed so that keeping a scoresheet - primarily to keep track of game timing - is no longer required. The deck of Drive Result cards doubles as the play clock, with each card representing 30-seconds of game time. When the deck is exhausted, the half is over. 
     Team boutique ratings are driven by the card number of the Drive Result FAC instead of die rolls. The game includes a conversion table when playing the FAC Edition with original format team cards.

In Treeline, the landscape, once lush and green, is covered with cities of stone and brick. The spirits of the forest have awoken and a change is coming!  Take control of your forest clan (the Mushkins, Umbrams, Creeklers, or Stumplings) and reclaim city tiles, restoring the forest and taking control of key tactical positions. As you flip city tiles and deploy your clan members, you’ll draw your opponent’s troops into the action! The forest will return to its former glory, but will your clan be in a position to lead it?

Treeline is the first game in Burnt Island Games new small box line. These new titles will offer interesting decisions and complex strategy in a smaller package.

—description from the publisher

Bookshelf is the new game in Alley Cat Games' ever popular tin line.

Players must work together to cooperatively stack books on top of a tower of cards. Cards have instructions on which books to place on top of it, and restrictions of where most of the books need to be placed. Don't be fooled though, placing books one at a time is harder than it initially looks!

—description from the publisher

In 1943 the Allied push across the Pacific reached a critical phase. Every step toward Rabaul demanded nerve, planning and a constant fight with the limits of supply. 1943: Race to Rabaul puts you right in the middle of that pressure.

This time the series introduces opposed play. You can play as a team or head-to-head, with one or two players commanding the Japanese side and one or two leading the Americans.
