Both fronts chase momentum, both struggle with logistics and both try to outthink the other before their plans run dry. The map is wide, resources are tight and every choice has weight.

The result is a game where ambition always meets resistance, and the path to Rabaul is never straightforward.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
It's maple syrup season in Quebec. On this beautiful Sunday in April, about a hundred people came to enjoy the pleasures of maple at La Lichette sugar shack. At the end of the day, after gorging themselves to excess, everyone left. The employees began cleaning the large dining room, where maple syrup flowed freely and which was as sticky as a beehive. It was at this moment that a body was discovered. It was the body of a woman, found dead between two stacks of firewood at the back of the building. Since the body and blood were not frozen, you deduced that the murder had occurred in the last few minutes. An employee called the owner of the sugar shack, who asked that no one leave the premises. She wants the culprit found quickly to avoid tarnishing the reputation of her sugar shack.

Ça bouille à cabane! is a mystery game from the Drame & Enquête collection, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of murder. Who is the killer? It's up to you to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

CMYK is an abstract memory game inspired by the Rorschach test and the expressive world of ink and print.
CMYK invites players into the tactile, emotional universe of Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Black — where color is absorbed, blended, and revealed through ink on paper.

Each card presents an abstract, symmetrical composition reminiscent of Rorschach patterns, encouraging players to interpret shapes intuitively and create personal mental links to remember them.
In doing so, players access the creative and subconscious part of their mind, transforming memory into an act of imagination and perception.

Designed with multiple levels of difficulty, CMYK adapts to every player, offering both a calm meditative experience and a challenging cognitive workout.
It enhances visual memory, attention, and creative association, bridging art, psychology, and play.

Why You’ll Love It

Cognitive training: Strengthens memory, focus, and mental association.

Rorschach-inspired design: Abstract ink shapes awaken interpretation and creativity.

Art meets psychology: Explore the tactile world of color through Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Black.

Multi-level challenge: Play casually or increase complexity to test your perception.

—description from the designer

Sumo Toads is a fast, tactical battle on a crowded lily pad where chunky amphibian wrestlers shove, splash, and belly-bump each other in a slippery fight to stay on the pond. The game unfolds in two halves: first, players draft their toads onto the board, each with unique asymmetric abilities that shape their strategy and the terrain of the match. This setup phase is itself the draft, determining not only which toads you control but where every contestant begins and what the map setup for the match looks like.

Once the lily pad is full, the bout begins — a chaotic, clever positioning game where timing, momentum, and reading your rivals matter just as much as raw toad power. Quick to learn, loaded with personality, and endlessly replayable, Sumo Toads captures the spirit of sumo wrestling… if the wrestlers were determined, slippery toads with questionable balance and enormous enthusiasm.

—description from the publisher

Panadero con el Pan is a competitive card game where each player becomes a Mexican sweet bread baker. Your goal is to bake the right breads, serve customers as they appear, and finish with the highest score.

At the beginning of the game each player receives secret mission cards. These provide optional objectives that award bonus points at the end if completed.

There are three main card types in the game.

Bread cards are the pastries you bake and place on your trays.
Customer cards come from a separate deck. Each customer shows exactly which breads they want and how many stars(Points) they are worth. All players can see the customers in play and compete for the same, but customers can only be claimed on your own turn.

Event cards are mixed into the bread deck and allow actions such as stealing a bread, swapping trays, protecting your pastries, or burning a rival bread to force a discard.

On your turn you play breads to your trays,use event cards in your hand to sabotage other players and lastly draw cards to always have 5.  If you meet the requirements of any visible customer during your turn you can claim that card and earn the stars shown. This is the main way to score points.

The game uses only cards. Strategy is built around timing your plays, managing your hand, and using events to help yourself or interfere with others.

The game ends when the customer deck runs out. Players total the stars from their claimed customers and add any bonus points from completed secret missions. The baker with the highest final score wins.

Panadero con el Pan is quick to learn, fast paced, and full of fun interactions as players compete to bake the right breads and serve customers to claim points.

—description from the designer

Abstract game race similar to Pachisi or Mensch ärgere Dich nicht. The goal is to move the pawns around the board to the "home" section, by throwing a dice. You can capture your opponent's pawns and return them to the starting square. The winner is the first player to move all their pawns to the initial section. 
This version of the game differs in that it has a circular board.

Life is full of surprises – often, it’s the unpredictable moments that turn everything upside down. A small event, a random encounter, or a spontaneous decision can lead us in completely new directions. Suddenly, we have a brilliant business idea, discover an unexpected talent, or change our lives in ways we never thought possible.

Sometimes, situations may seem chaotic or even meaningless at first. But looking back, it’s exactly these experiences that create the most exciting stories.
Stories about our own lives connect us – they inspire, highlight our differences, and reveal our common ground. No matter how unique our paths may be, we all stand at similar crossroads from time to time.

Chaos Connections brings this to life: It shows that in the end, everything finds its place. That every situation holds an opportunity – even if it doesn’t seem that way at first.
This game invites you to be creative and spontaneous, to explore new perspectives, and to celebrate every connection in the chaos.
 Because within all the disorder, there are countless connections waiting to be found.
Find them – and have tons of fun along the way!

Your journey is unique – enjoy every twist and turn!

—description from the designer

Inside Pitch Advanced (IPA) is a re-imagined version of the original Inside Pitch ball game that was created in 2007. In the years since its introduction, I have kept notes of the feedback I have received from players like you. Some suggested additions to be made to the game engine, while others wished that some of the existing components played differently. So, after producing IP card sets for all years from 1956 to the present day, I set out to develop a new version of the game. The re-design effort had two main goals: add new features to the game and, at the same time, streamline game play. While I think you’ll appreciate the efficiencies gained in this new version, I realize that IPA will not be for everyone, nor will it ever be a “one roll and move on”-type of game. However, fans of the original Inside Pitch will find much of the new game familiar. In fact, if you are a player of the original game, you should be up and running with IPA in a very short amount of time. Let’s take a closer look at Inside Pitch Advanced.

The DIPS system has been retained as the basis for the new game, just like IP. One of the first things you’ll notice about IPA is the expansion of the grid from 36 cells to 64. This has given me the opportunity to add depth and detail to the cards that just wasn’t possible before. For example, I can now include pitcher grid results that occur at much lower frequencies than the 36-cell grid previously allowed. Additionally, I have streamlined game play by utilizing “original” rolls of the dice as much as possible. An example of this is the new range play mechanic: High strikeout and walk pitchers don't allow as many balls in play so they shouldn't be as reliant on the defense behind them. By utilizing the original 1d20 on the pitcher’s card we can mimic this trait without the need for additional re-rolls. Pick-off attempts were folded into the regular pitcher’s grid as well so they can happen at any time. Doubles allowed by pitchers were documented in my notes over the years and is implemented in an unobtrusive way by using a process that was already in the game. Tired pitchers have a higher percentage of result changes and are punished to a higher degree with automatic outs now becoming automatic hits and the potential for a K when not tired becoming a HR when fatigued. Optionally, pitchers can also adjust the HR rate of batters depending on whether there are runners on base or the bases are empty.

Expanding the batter’s grid enabled me to include results like hitting with runners in scoring position (RISP) compared to non-RISP situations, hitting behind runners in hit and run situations and infield singles. A larger grid also expands the diversity of the individual results, allowing the card to better replicate a player’s actual hit location and hit type. Batters now have individualized H&R and SH ranges for the strategy rolls on their card, making it easier to implement.

Ballparks now have outs that can turn into hits if the fielder is playing in. The ballpark grids still show how each park affects different types of hits, but now they go a step further by breaking it down based on how left- and right-handed batters perform in those parks. Home field advantage has been added where range plays are more successful for the HOME team. Optionally, you can play the game with the chance for outfielders to rob home runs hit to the wall. Nobody is climbing the Green Monster in LF at Fenway but RF is a different story. All park effects are now based on a 3-year average to provide more year-to-year consistency. Over the years, the most frequent feedback I received about the game centered on the perceived complexity of baserunner advancement. I personally appreciated the original system because it accounted for many factors that influence a runner’s chances. Inside Pitch Advanced now offers a simplified approach that still captures those key elements—while also incorporating more data, like the outfielder’s assist rate. This new system is more straight forward, but with greater diversity of results occurring proportionally as they do in real life. The double play grounder procedure has also changed making it easier and faster to resolve. Errors have been streamlined to make them easier to determine while making the frequency of error types more realistic. Finally, for the solo gamer, I’ve added a chart to simplify how a play is resolved when the infield is playing in based on actual PBP data. This chart is more realistic and offloads the gamer from micro-managing fielding decisions in those situations.
A 3 in 1 (knowlede/quiz/rommé) game, about dogs fot the tiptoi-system.

Rove: Anchorpoint is a compact, standalone campaign game built on the challenging and tactical system from the original game, Rove.

It features a 25-encounter cooperative campaign, twelve all-new characters, and deep progression and leveling choices. It shares the core dungeon crawl mechanics of Rove.  Designed for both Rove veterans and newcomers alike, Anchorpoint delivers an epic experience in a small package.

After many centuries, the Kingdom of the Winged Dragons has opened its gates. The Sages proclaim far and wide: “The Throne awaits our future King or Queen! Only those who can overcome the perils of the skies will be able to claim the crown!” It is time for the young dragons to take flight and embark on an adventure. They must navigate capricious currents, withstand merciless storms, and explore the sanctuaries of ancient floating islands, all in order to be the first to claim the throne. May the winds blow in your favor, and may the experiences of your journey make you a wise sovereign!

Trono de Dragones is a game for 2 to 4 players, recommended for ages 10 and up, with games lasting between 20 and 40 minutes. In it, players take on the roles of young dragons who will soar through the skies, striving to be the first to reach the Kingdom of the Dragons, claim the throne, and win the game. To achieve this, they must roll dice at the beginning of each turn, which will allow them to perform different actions, such as moving, exploring new territories, clearing the skies, and even sabotaging the plans of other dragons. They can also draw magic from the places they visit, as well as find powerful relics from an ancient kingdom, which will grant them powerful permanent abilities. They must be quick, but also cunning on their journey!

Each turn, players must roll dice, which will allow them to perform up to two actions per turn.

These actions are:


     Explore the skies, adding tiles to the board with different types of terrain.
     Collect, drawing cards from the deck corresponding to the color of the tile they are on.
     Move, traveling to spaces on tiles they have already explored.
     Blow, displacing storm tokens (which slow movement) and even rival dragons.
     Trick, which allows you to look at and snatch cards from other players' hands.


The more dice with the same face that you roll, the more powerful the corresponding action will be. Dragons have magic potions that allow them to reroll dice to improve their roll, but these are only replenished by visiting the shrines on the Floating Island tiles.

In addition, they can play Magic cards, with instant effects, and Relic cards, which grant permanent benefits to the dragon that equips them.

Dragons must move quickly but also manage their plays strategically, because the first player to reach the Castle will be crowned king and win the game.

—description from the designer

Después de muchos siglos, el Reino de los Dragones Alados ha abierto sus puertas. Los Sabios cantan a los cuatro vientos: “¡El Trono está esperando a nuestro futuro Rey o Reina! Solo quien pueda superar los peligros de los cielos podrá tomar la corona!”. Es momento de que los dragones jóvenes levanten el vuelo y se lancen a la aventura. Deberán sortear corrientes caprichosas, resistir despiadadas tormentas y explorar los santuarios de las antiguas islas flotantes, todo con tal de ser quien llegue primero a reclamar el trono. ¡Que los vientos soplen a tu favor y que las experiencias del viaje te conviertan en un sabio soberano!

Trono de Dragones es un juego de 2 a 4 jugadores, con una edad recomendada de 10 años en adelante, y con partidas que duran entre 20 a 40 minutos. En él, los jugadores representan a dragones jóvenes que se lanzarán a volar los cielos intentando ser los primeros en llegar al Reino de los Dragones, para así reclamar el trono y ganar la partida. Para conseguirlo, deberán lanzar dados al comienzo de cada turno, los cuales les permitirán realizar distintas acciones, tales como moverse, explorar nuevos territorios, despejar los cielos, e incluso sabotear los planes de los demás dragones. Además, podrán extraer la magia de los sitios que visiten, así como también encontrar poderosas reliquias de un reino antiguo, las cuales les permitirán adquirir poderosas habilidades permanentes. ¡Deben ser rápidos, pero también astutos en su travesía!

Cada turno, los jugadores deberán lanzar dados, los cuales les permitirán realizar hasta dos acciones por turno. 
Estas acciones son:


     Explorar los cielos, agregando losetas al tablero con distintos tipos de territorio.
     Recolectar, robando cartas del mazo correspondiente al color de la loseta donde se encuentren.
     Mover, desplazándose a casillas de losetas ya exploradas.
     Soplar, desplazando fichas de tormenta (las cuales ralentizan el movimiento) e incluso a dragones rivales.
     Artimaña, que permite mirar y arrebatar cartas de las manos de otros jugadores.


Entre más dados con la misma cara resulten de su lanzamiento, más potente será la acción correspondiente. Los dragones cuentan con pociones mágicas que les permiten relanzar dados para mejorar su tirada, pero estas sólo se recargan al visitar los santuarios de las losetas de Isla Flotante.

Además, pueden jugar cartas de Magia, con efectos instantáneos, y de Reliquia, que otorgan beneficios permanentes al dragón que la equipe.

Los dragones deberán moverse con rapidez pero también administrar sus jugadas estratégicamente, pues el primer jugador que logre llegar al Castillo será coronado rey y ganará la partida.

—description from the designer (Spanish)

Urban Capitalism is a competitive shared incentives and multicurrency auction game inspired by cube rails and Keyflower. Players represent large corporations who own all of the land in a geographically limited downtown metropolis. You’ll develop and redistrict your property to attract neighborhood visitors and gain access to new actions on your turn, but ultimately, winning is about extracting value from the people under your influence.

This is a game of tradeoffs and relative success. Do you spend your turns bidding or taking city actions? Do you co-invest in a neighborhood and work together to earn more rewards, splitting influence amongst owners? Or do you work alone to prevent your neighborhood investment from being diluted at the expense of city actions?

—description from the publisher
A ten-card expansion that adds four new ingredients, a special ingredient card, two potions, and an extra copy of the spirit, mind, and heart cards.

boardgame: Rise of The Gnomes: Desert Expansion
description: Adds 4 new player races (genies, desert naga, kobolds, and nomads) and support for a 6th player.

Fear the Frightful Fog
Catch devastating diseases, lose your way in the fog and drown in deadly bogs and marshes.

Featuring: swamp mapping rules, fog, new monsters and events, creepy tables, new deities, 12 magic items and 36 spells!

Plus, new classes: the stunning Medusine, the mutated Renegade Reptilian, the sneaky Snake Charmer, the powerful Swamp Shaman, the strange Marsh Elves and their six tribes.

100% compatible with the wilderness book range, including Crucible of Classic Critters and More Mountainous Mayhem

Attention: this PDF is not a stand-alone product. You need a copy of Four Against Darkness (and for some of the content, Four Against the Abyss) to use this book. Additional options are provided for players who have access to other titles in the line.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Legends of the Seas: Goblin's Raid Expansion
description:
Be ready to be boarded by Goblin hordes! Face chaos on the high seas with a new playable character, exclusive gameplay, and a hoard of new Treasure cards!

The Goblin’s Raid expansion is an exclusive add-on produced in limited quantities and will not be available in retail. It contains one new playable character and new gameplay.

Contents of the Goblin’s Raid Expansion:
1 Rulebook
1 Custom Plastic Insert
2 Orange dice (Raid)
1 Character Miniature (6.5 cm tall)
1 Shipmate Card (170x95mm)
15 Treasure Cards (63x88mm)
6 Goblins Pirate Standees
6 Plastic Stands for Standees

boardgame: Legends of the Seas: Treasure Card Pack
description: Adds 14 new Treasure cards (63x88mm) to boost replayability and fun in every game!

Step into the gritty, close-quarters chaos of World War II/21st Century with Mission Lock: Small Unit Action in WW2 and the 21st Century. 
This ruleset puts you in command of a single fighting section, where every man and every decision counts. 
Forget grand strategies; this is a game of immediate, life-or-death tactics.

Using a fast-paced, intuitive system, you'll manage the high stakes of small-unit combat. 
Will your team brave open ground in a desperate dash forward, or will they "ground hug" and take cover under a hail of enemy fire? 
Our innovative Suppression mechanic means that incoming fire doesn't just inflict casualties—it tests the very will of your men. As your soldiers are pinned down and pushed to their limit, their ability to act becomes increasingly strained, forcing you to make crucial decisions under pressure.

From the first shot fired to the final charge, Mission Lock: Small Unit Action in WW2 and the 21st Century is a tabletop wargame that captures the brutal reality and heroic moments of frontline combat. 
Take the fight to your enemy, and see if your troops have what it takes to seize victory from the jaws of defeat.

The game is built around two core concepts that simulate the chaos of combat:

     Activation: A team's ability to act is determined by its Activation Level, which is influenced by its training and experience. Elite troops are more likely to follow your commands under fire than raw recruits.
     Suppression: This mechanic simulates the psychological impact of being shot at. As a team takes hits, it becomes suppressed, making it harder for them to activate and function effectively. Managing suppression is just as important as managing casualties.


These mechanics, combined with the role of the NCO's leadership, create a game that emphasises strategy, tactical planning, and the importance of unit morale. 
Every decision you make and every die you roll tells a story of the unpredictable nature of combat.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Warhammer Age of Sigmar: Path to Glory – Ravaged Coast
description:
Path to Glory: Ravaged Coast is your essential companion for narrative play in Warhammer Age of Sigmar. Within, you'll find rules for fighting a campaign in one of the most bitterly contested regions of the realms.

After reading takes of the war consuming the Realm of Fire, let the battle-sock take you through an exciting campaign that puts you on the front lines. there's no easier way to give your battles a deep narrative edge.

This book contains the following:

Background on the Ravaged Coast of Aqshy, the crumbling frontier of the Skaven invasion, as well as a weaving tale of powerful champions fighting for dominance there.

A Path to Glory battlepack that allows you to fight a campaign for control of a vital island on the Ravaged Coast. This Battlepack includes new Paths for heroes and regiment, rules for acquiring battle scars and forging powerful but volatile emberstone-enhanced weapons, a Regiment of Renown for each Grand Alliance, enhancements and lores specific to this region of Aqshy, and more.

Twelve new battle plans for using in your campaign, involving and array of gameplay challenges.

A Path to Glory roster specific to this campaign on which to record you warriors' progress, boons, and scars.

—description from the back of the book
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Marrenburg Mysteries: The Vanishing Six is a detective board game set in the quiet town of Marrenburg, where six participants in an annual treasure hunt disappear on the same night. Each team was connected to the town’s live radio network for safety, yet all communication suddenly stopped, and every participant went missing without leaving a clear trace.

Players become investigators assigned to uncover what happened during the treasure hunt. By examining evidence, tracing the last known locations, analyzing radio logs, interviewing townspeople, and following leads across Marrenburg, players must slowly reconstruct the events of the night.

The game blends deduction, exploration, and step-by-step investigation as players work together or compete to solve the case before panic spreads through the town. With multiple paths to uncover the truth, the mystery deepens each time the game is played.

—description from the publisher

Description:

The game engages players of various ages – it encourages logical thinking, planning, problem-solving, and teamwork. Recommended for ages 5 and up, although more advanced levels require the ability to read instructions.

Game components:

25 Game Boards
8 Robot Figures
60 Resource Cards
130 Action Cards (4 levels)
28 Task Cards
4 Storage Cards

Game Objective:

Control your robot, move around the game board, collect resources, and construct buildings according to the task cards. Each building earns points – plan your actions ahead, collaborate, and work together to build the largest possible robot city!

You are a retired tycoon. On a whim, you agreed to take part in a psychology experiment about “the ability to detect lies,” but you and another participant were deceived and made to drink a lethal dose of poison!
Experimenter: “To survive, you must continue the experiment and obtain a sufficient amount of antidote… Will you proceed?”

At the end of the game, the player who has more antidotes than the opponent survives and wins. Antidotes are obtained through five rounds of bidding. In each round, players first decide the actual amount they will bid, and then declare their bid amount. The player who declares the higher amount gains an antidote.
     However, players may declare an amount different from their real bid—lying is allowed. If the lie is not detected, the bid stands. Instead of bidding money, a player may also conceal a Gun card to destroy the opponent’s antidotes. Use the effects of the adrenaline administered by the experimenter to push the bidding in your favor and fight for survival.

This is a DEATH GAME. You’ll enjoy it even more if you play as though you’ve truly swallowed a lethal dose of poison.

—description from the designer

Gatushi is a card game full of colorful cats that will make you smile... or bring a cloud of bad luck!

Behind this name lies a competition to collect the luckiest cats and avoid having three of the same color line up. Because, as they say... when cats get too close, things can get ugly!

Gatushi is a card game for 2 to 6 players, ages 8 and up, with games lasting about 20 minutes. It's designed for the whole family, with simple rules but enough strategy to keep both young and old hooked.

A game of pushing your luck, taking risks, and making bets where you'll have to know when to play... and when to make a feline bet against the others!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Fallen Star: The Emily Hemingway Case
description:
A cold case mystery in which players work together as a team to solve a murder by examining physical and online evidence and making correct deductions.

Emily Hemingway is a rising star in every sense, until her life is cut short in 2009. Known to millions online as EmWay, the young ViewTube sensation is found murdered behind her home. The award she won that night lies bloodied beside her.

She was livestreaming right before her death, and the recording is saved.

It’s up to you to reopen this cold case. Go through the physical case files, documents, notes and photos, use the right search terms in the online archive, and learn about the people around Emily, their relationships, motives and alibis. To ultimately bring justice to Emily's killer.
 One thing is certain: even fallen stars leave traces.


     No rules, start your investigation immediately.
     Tiered online hint system. 
     Includes Myspace-era social media profiles. 


An internet connected device with large screen (laptop, tablet) and audio is required. Gameplay involves entering keywords into a dedicated website to gain access to numerous pages containing 40 extra online items including video and audio.

Solve the case by correctly answering the case questions online.

No components are destroyed or altered during gameplay, so easily pass on the game after playing.

—description from the designer

In Fish ’em Up, you and your friends take on the role of fishermen, casting a massive net in hopes of hauling in a huge catch. But beneath the teamwork, a fierce competition is brewing—each of you wants to prove your skill and impress the local folks. Will your team land a big catch, or return with nothing but a net full of local trash and disappointment? The net is in your hands. So what are you waiting for? Fish ’em Up!

Fish 'em Up is played in a sequence of turns, during which players will add cards to the shared Net by placing them on top of the existing cards. Each card type triggers a specific effect—such as gaining points, stealing points, or stealing cards. These effects grow stronger for every card of the same type already in connected to the Net. When adding cards, players may also cover images that negate the effects of played cards. The game ends when the Boat card is drawn, and the player with the most points wins.

—description from the publisher
An additional world for Transgalactica, providing an income of any one resource after an outpost has been built.

A modern solitaire inspired by Scoundrel, The Fool's Journey and CRPG video games. Using a standard deck of cards, complete a four stage boss fight by assembling a team of four characters with unique actions. Attack, enchant, heal, shield and more with the characters you choose to complete this challenge.

The four stage boss is the J, Q, K and Ace of spades. The four characters you choose come from the remaining aces and face cards and determine what actions you can perform. You must have at least one red ace, and when a red ace dies, you lose.

The numbered cards are the deck you draw from. Characters in your party can use cards that match their suit, while the boss uses both spades and clubs to damage your team.

Each player builds a personal line of number cards to activate Goals and multiply a jackpot.
There is a webapp where you can play against AI players in easy/hard mode.
Try it now https://tabletoplab25-hash.github.io/ROWLETTE/

Building Your Line:
Each turn, players take 1 card from a 3-card market and add it to their personal line. The line grows throughout the round and can contain:

Number cards (1-40): Red cards trigger Goal selection; Black cards boost your Jackpot
Zero cards: Add risk by accumulating zero-points (6+ causes an explosion, halving your score)
Special cards: Remove zeros, increase card limits, or create bonus chains

Activating Goals:
When you draw a Red card, you choose a Goal (Red, Black, Even, Odd, High, Low)
Each Goal needs a threshold of matching cards: 1st Goal needs ≥2, 2nd needs ≥3, and so on
The moment you meet a Goal's threshold, it activates and contributes to your final score

Winning the Game:
Play 3 rounds (20-30 min total)
Final Score = (Jackpot × Active Goals) + Order Bonuses
If you exploded: your score is halved
Highest total score after 3 rounds wins!

boardgame: Arydia: The Paths We Dare Tread – Fabled Fishing
description:
Fabled Fishing is a mini-expansion for Arydia: The Paths We Dare Tread.

Grab your pole, it’s time to go fishing in this mini-expansion for Arydia! Fabled Fishing seamlessly integrates with your campaign, allowing you to take the ‘Fish’ action when you discover bodies of water at any location!

Fishing takes the form of a card-based mini-game. Fish are controlled by Threat cards while you play Maneuver cards to try and catch the fish! Every fish is unique with their own special abilities that provide a distinct challenge!

As you explore Arydia, you never know what fish you’ll find in any river, stream, or pond! Each fish you catch will grant you a reward that you can use in your campaign. Will you catch all 30 common, rare, and legendary fish?

-description from campaign
family: Game: Arydia: The Paths We Dare Tread

description:
In this expansion for Carcassonne, you let your mayor compete for the favor of rival knights before duty calls him to a crisis meeting. Use your messenger and scarecrow to extend your influence.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Party in the Hizzy: The Expansion
description: Adds ten new character and action cards, allowing for a fifth and sixth player.
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description: Adds seventy cards for more epic punnable fun.

In Color Forge, players use cards in hand to purchase additional cards (resources) and build their deck. They can then "combine" their resource cards in their deck to complete contracts, earning points and removing the combined resource cards. Players thereby lose resources which must be purchased back on future turns to continue the fulfillment cycle.

In this way, Color Forge is a unique combination of deck building/shedding, contract fulfillment and resource management. Players race to complete contracts, collecting sets of specific contracts to score bonus points and earn a higher score than their competition. A "simple mode" variant is also available for those unfamiliar with set collection, allowing players to first grasp the flow of the game and gradually introduce other new mechanics.

This game is suitable for players who are able to add together 3 small numbers (totaling between 3 and 15). The game also utilizes combining primary colors into secondary colors, though the design is such that no familiarity is required but can rather be learned through play. Alternatively, players who are colorblind and/or looking to use or learn multiplication skills of small numbers can use multiplication (of the numbers 2, 3 and 5) to forge cards in the same manner to complete contracts.

This game is playable by both experienced and inexperienced players, and can be used to introduce the mechanics of deck-building, contract fulfillment and set collection, while also offering a some tactical depth and variability.

—description from the designer
Yet another additional seventy cards for the greatest punnening ever.

The board game, The Kingdom of England 1200 to 1299, is a turn-based dynastic and economic game, for 2 to 6 players, set during the 13the century A.D. in England, Wales and Scotland.

In the dynamic and bellicose age of the Baronial Wars, when the local nobility fought hard for their rights to be governed by a King subject to the laws, according to the now famous, Magna Carta, The Kingdom of England 1200 to 1299 A. D., is a fantastic trip back into history, based on a multi-year historical and economic reconstruction based on the original historical sources for England in the 13th century. You lead a Noble House as you struggle to protect your vast estates and maneuver among the ever changing political alliances.

—description from the designer

It’s that time of year again. Promos are in the air, and we can deliver the goods!

During the fateful crossover between the shada and Ghost Rider, we were blessed with the superlative Flame Harness. If you want to control the source, use this fiery totem and let the shada know what hellfire feels like.

There's also the Flagellum. It's gross. We don't want to talk about it anymore...

And watch out for any keel that wears the Armor of the Eclipse because they're a force to be reckoned with. Upgrading minions is intimidating enough without the dark side of the moon bestowing gifts of armament!

Enjoy the Cloudspire: Bonus Bounty Promo Pack! These three cards will provide faction-fueled fever dreams.

Please game responsibly.

—description from the publisher
Nine more characters for the game (and one foil card), allowing for a sixth player.
family: Players: Games with expansions that change player count

description:
Welcome back to Parks, where every trail leads to new marvels, every stream runs with new life, every sun sets over a new memory, and every mountain looms with new possibility as you explore the stunning beauty and wild wonders as you venture through the national parks of Europe.
Over the course of three rounds (called Seasons), Parks Europe will challenge your hiking skills in new ways, enticing you to hook into the Via Ferrata along the Trail, collecting resources and fatigue along the way - visiting Parks or stopping off at the Chalet to rest and take in the wonder. Explore new systems for taking Photos, expand your experience on the trail by supporting Conservation Projects, and enjoy in hike bonuses from a new set collecting element with Park Cards.
At the end of the third Season, the hike (and the game) is over! Count up your points and see who had the greatest experience visiting the natural beauty of Europe from the shores of the Wadden Sea to the Dolomiti cliffs.
Grab your pack and lace up your boots. The trail awaits!

—description from the publisher

Welcome to Jaycatwalk, the frantic 2-10 player game of feline survival on a busy highway! The goal is simple: be the last cat standing. Each turn, dice rolls determine your cat’s movement and control the flow of traffic. Navigate your cat strategically across the lanes, but beware – the rolling player also moves the vehicles, aiming to take out the competition! Adding to the mayhem are Hazard cards, unleashing unpredictable events like traffic jams, icy patches, and wild spills from trucks. If you’re looking for some dangerously fun chaos, Jaycatwalk delivers an exciting and unique experience where every move could be your last!

—description from the publisher

Heart of the Lizard is a novella set in the world of Four Against Darkness.

Follow Haq, Kil, Gress and Varda in the abandoned remains of the ancient Temple of Zur, the god of death, on the trail of a fabled, mystical treasure.

A gaming appendix gives full descriptions and stats for new monsters, treasure, spells and the Fire Mage, a new class for use with Four Against Darkness.

—description from the back of the book

Dive into the world of football with Team KM – Be in The Game, the official football card game inspired by Kylian Mbappé! 
This Asmodee football board game lets you experience the intensity of a real match: advance the ball, block your opponents, score spectacular goals, and turn the game around in the final second. 
Suitable for ages 7 and up, this dynamic family game blends strategy, anticipation, and a touch of luck to offer fast-paced, fun, and exciting matches. 
Whether you're playing with children, friends, or family, Team KM is easy to learn and guarantees an instant football atmosphere. 
Thanks to its premium components – thick game boards, gold cards, and an integrated dice tray – the Team KM card game offers a high-quality experience, perfect for Kylian Mbappé fans and football enthusiasts. 
With its numerous cards, special effects, varied situations, and different game modes (solo, cooperative, or competitive), this football board game offers exceptional replayability. 
Team KM is the ideal gift for any football fan looking for a fun, strategic and immersive card game that captures the emotion of a real football match.

Gameplay:
Advance the ball across your pitch to reach the penalty area, get closer to the goal, and shoot it into the net.
Each player has a hand of 7 cards. On their turn, they draw a card and then play one. The cards allow you to advance the ball across your pitch, block opponents, counter Interruption cards, etc.
Once in the penalty area, play a Shot card and roll the die to try and score a goal. Otherwise, move the ball back the number of spaces indicated on the Clearance die. When a goal is scored, teammates can exchange cards, all ball tokens return to the start, and the match resumes until the Half-Time card, then the Full-Time card.
The player or team that has scored the most goals at the end of the match wins!

Trivia game about one of Finland's cities, Kuopio. The game has 432 questions and is suitable for everyone who wants to learn more about "the pearl of Savonia" in eastern Finland by the shores of lake Saimaa, famous for the 'kalakukko' traditional fish pie and the sightseeing tower of Puijo.

This is the 14th instalment in the Nähdään series of trivia games about Finnish cities and towns, and like the preceding two titles (Nähdään Hämptonissa & Nähdään Granissa) is a pocket-sized, single deck trivia quiz game. This game is in Finnish only and each card holds two sets of four questions, but this time each question has an equal value of 1 point.

The name of the game refers to the nickname of the Kuopio market square, "The pole [center] of the world" since 1818. This game is published partly in honor of the 250th anniversary of the city's founding (Swedish Royal decree as the capital of Savo-Karjala, 17th November 1775)

This game is playable with a standard deck of cards.

Chameleon Tricks is a trick-taking game for 2-3 players. By playing more than 1 card of the same rank on top of the other, players can instantly change suit to dodge or win tricks. Be careful not to win too much, as the top score of the hand gets cut in half!

The highest rank card in the strongest suit wins the trick, adding their played card to their hidden score pile. Cards in the score pile are worth face value (example, a 3 is worth 3).

After 3 hands, the player with the highest score wins the game.

—description from the designer

See you under the clock - again!

The 13th title in the Nähdään series of trivia games about Finnish cities and towns takes us back to square one, figuratively speaking - 14 years after the publication of the first game in the series we're back in Helsinki again with a full-sized, fully bilingual game. The board is new and more colourful with new routes to take to the center including the Suomenlinna fortress, there are six categories of questions, somewhat tweaked rules and of course, 900 completely new questions that are typically not too easy unless you're a 'barefooted helsinkian' - and sometimes even if you are!

This game is published partly in honor of the 475th anniversary of the founding of the city (Swedish Royal decree 12th June 1550)

The Geometria em 3 Athos  (“Geometry in Three Acts”) Expansion elevates the experience by blending art and strategy into a captivating new challenge.

In this expansion, the patterns become richer and more intricate, featuring Tiles with 3 unique Designs per Pattern.
Tile placement becomes a deeper puzzle, combinations grow more complex, and every choice carries even greater weight.

Get ready to rethink your strategies, refine your decision-making, and pursue the perfect harmony of shape, color, and pattern.

•••

A Expansão “Geometria em Três Athos” - combina arte e estratégia em uma experiência única.

Published by a Finnish vocational association aiming to attract youngsters to the construction & building maintenance industry, The Mesta.net is a set collection-cum-trivia game where everyone starts with two building projects to fulfill. Each project requires three different professionals to hire to work on - an electrician, an architect, and a janitor for example, with some leeway in which exact three you'll need. Five professionals are always available on the display - but to hire any one them, you'll need to give a right answer to a question on the other side of the card related to the professional and their field. It's even possible to steal already hired professionals from a fellow player - but to do that, you'll need to answer two random questions taken from the deck!

(Note: This game is somewhat unfortunately named in that Mesta.net on the internet is actually a media service by the Finnish operator Elisa, and does not seem to have anything to do with the game or the publisher.)

The Tuusulanjärven Rundi (Round lake Tuusulanjärvi) is a classic bicycle route of a little over 20 kilometers where you cycle around lake Tuusulanjärvi located about 30 km north of Helsinki, Finland. In this promotional, simple deck-builder racing game players are cyclists competing in who rounds the lake fastest. Each turn you'll have two movement cards from your private deck to choose from and it's very beneficial to be the first (or at least, not to be the last) in the three main waypoints on the route as those will grant special cards with extra powers. Watch out though that you don't cycle too fast in the beginning, or you may get tired towards he end of the race. On the way you'll also get to know the various businesses, educational institutions and museums in the area, and first to the finish is the winner!

Designed as a team effort by the Junior Chamber International, Järvenpää (Finland) chapter.

boardgame: Hannut: The First Clash of Armor
description:
From May 11-13, two German armored divisions acting as part of the German "Cape" from Fall Gelb plunged into Belgium between Maastricht and Liege, meeting the French Cavalry Corps consisting of two French Divisions Leger Mechanique (DLMs), among the best armored formations in the French Army of 1940.  The French under General Prioux established a series of hedgehogs in various villages along the anticipated German line of advance on a south-easterly 20km line from Huy on the Meuse in the south to the very edge of the British line near Tirlemont in the north.  During two days of intense combat, the French gave as good as they got, succeeding in their mission of delaying the German army from reaching the "Gembloux Gap" where the very powerful French 1st Army was still assembling.  In the end, the French retreated in good order on the 13th of May toward the 1st Army, satisfied that their delaying action had been a success.  The Germans were left with the rude awakening that their tanks were often no match against the French.

Hannut is the first in a series of 8 titles in the Campaign in France 1940 (CiF40) game system, which covers division-level combat at the platoon level during the German invasion of the Low Countries, Belgium and France in 1940, focusing on tank battles during the campaign. Its larger purpose is to help history buffs and wargamers better appreciate the capabilities of the combatants, and thus the reasons for the eventual outcome of the conflict. Scale is 250 meters/hex, combat units are platoons and batteries, Turns are 1 hour in length, divided into 15 minute impulses.

Loosely adapted from games like Panzer Leader, Panzer Command, and the Grand Tactical System/Company Scale System, the CiF40 system attempts to merge the playability and grand tactical feel of classic platoon level games with more realistic Command and Control functions to facilitate the coordination of large corps and division-sized formations. This is a not a super-detailed design, however. Much effort has been made to streamline processes so that players can focus on the battle, not bookkeeping.

—description from the designer

Courband TCGT is a collectible card game that challenges the conventions of the genre. Its design focuses on delivering an ever-evolving experience without resorting to power creep, ensuring that all cards remain relevant over time.

Unlike traditional TCGs, Courband introduces a board with spaces where each card comes to life as a strategic unit. Cards are deployed, moved, attack with specific ranges, and activate effects depending on their position. This turns every match into a tactical duel, where space and timing are just as important as the deck itself.

Gameplay expands with mechanics never before seen in the world of TCGs, opening possibilities beyond simple exchanges of blows or combos. Here, every decision matters: from where you place a card to how you advance it across the board.

—description from the publisher

OCniverse: Characters of the Multiverse is a fast-paced multiplayer battle card adventure where players leap between worlds, collect Items and “lost baggage,” and race to bring theirs home before anyone else does!

The game has 3 types of cards; character cards, item cards, and world tiles. To start; each player selects a random character from the character cards. Then the world tiles are divided up randomly between all players. Players then place the tiles down on the table, creating a map for the players to travel across.

The goal of the game is simple; find the item card that matches the character you are playing as(lost baggage), and bring it to the world tile that matches them.

However, there are many obstacles to finding your item. First, each world you travel to is strange and may have negative or positive effects on your player. Next, some items are dangerous to draw. And another player may draw your lost baggage or vice versa.

When this happens, players are likely to instigate a battle between themselves in order to obtain their item or defeat their opponent before they can win the game.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – Eridium Blight & Eden-6 Tile Pack
description:
Explore the Eridium Blight and Eden-6 in this new set of tiles featuring brand new arena tile effects and a full campaign book!

This add-on expands the modular battlefield options for Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery. It introduces new hex tiles featuring the blasted wastelands of the Eridium Blight and the swamplands of Eden-6. More than just a tile pack, this set also includes new tile effects, tokens, and a complete campaign supplement.

Contents

     10 Double-Sided Hex Tile Boards featuring Eridium Blight on one side and Eden-6 on the reverse. Also includes transition tiles for connecting the regions to each other and existing tile sets, allowing you to build larger, more varied map layouts.
     2 Token Sheet with tokens supporting terrain interactions, hazards, mission elements, and scenario-specific effects used across the included campaign.
     11 Multi-Scenario Campaign Booklet that progresses through both regions and concludes with an epic raid boss encounter.


—description from the publisher

Not Today, Murder is a party game where players try to escape a "murder-y" situation by creating an escape plan using only three randomly drawn object cards. The goal is to be the most creative and convincing, as the group votes on the best plan to avoid a gruesome fate. It's described as a hilarious and suspenseful game that blends dark humor with quick thinking.

The Setup: Players are placed in a "murder-y" scenario, and each is dealt three random object cards, which could be anything from a rubber duck to a can of spray cheese.

The Goal: Devise the most creative and convincing plan to escape the scenario using only the three objects you were dealt.

How to Play:
Each player uses their objects to create an absurdly detailed escape plan. The group votes on whose plan is the best or most convincing. The player with the best plan "survives" the round.

boardgame: 5150: Vampire Codex – Tales from the Fringe
description:
You fed once, and the world changed. The blood burned like fire, your memories fractured into echoes, and shadows began to whisper your name. You are no longer just a hunter or a drifter—you are a Vampire.

Born of Razor DNA and human ambition, you walk the thin line between predator and conspirator, between seduction and slaughter. The factions fear you, hunt you, or seek to own you. But they can never forget you.

5150: Crimson Shadows — Vampire Codex is a noir-horror expansion for 5150: New Beginnings, weaving gothic influence, sci-fi conspiracy, and survival horror into the 5150 engine. Play as a Star or cross paths with them in the shadows of Known Space.

     Become a Vampire Star: resilient, seductive, deadly—and cursed with hunger.
     Feed, manipulate, or infect: spread the Stage Two Virus and gain Thralls, rivals, or new monsters.
     Faction Intrigue: walk between the Crimson Fold, Dominion of Flesh, Razor Matrons, and Gaea Prime Black Ops.
     Red Echo Protocol: play a five-mission campaign of infiltration, memory warfare, and the fate of Echo-9 psychic cloning labs.
     Dreamscape & Memory Dive rules: explore fractured minds, Razor psychic constructs, and internal horror spaces.
     Fully compatible with So Sweet, So Cold, So Fair and all other 5150 titles.


The shadows are vast. The hunger is endless. Step into the dark. 

—description from the publisher

Humanity stands on the edge of extinction. In Avoid Extinction, players must balance survival, morality, and ruthless strategy to outlast a world overrun by monsters.

You are one of the last remaining Keyholders, struggling to endure in a collapsing town. Every choice carries weight: will you sacrifice others to survive, risk everything to protect yourself, or strike out to steal gear and imprison rivals?

Each turn, Keyholders move across the board, exploring buildings that may conceal vital resources—or deadly creatures. Victories in battle reward you with strength and precious Keys, while defeat sends you to the hospital to recover. Collect 10 Keys and you can attempt to unlock the only escape route.

The first Keyholder to break free wins. But in a world where trust is fragile and danger lurks in every corner; survival is never guaranteed.

—description from the designer

You are a warden in the underworld overseeing and aiding your chosen warlord warriors bound for hell.
They must battle in the Colosseum for their chance to become resurrected.  Grant your chosen with powers in hopes that they will become victorious.

Players have 3 turns to predict and bid on warriors in the colosseum ring.
As well as using their Equipment cards (3 Soul tokens and 1 Relic card) strengthen and prepare the warriors for battle.
After the 3 rounds, the warriors battle their opponents in the ring. The player who predicted the survivor will be rewarded.
Your time runs out once 3 warriors have been resurrected. You need to be the one to earn the most points.

—description from the publisher

A new set of question cards for the Smart 10 game! This themed expansion focuses on the exciting world of superheroes. It contains 50 double-sided cards with 100 new questions and 1,000 new answers.

—description from the publisher
An expansion for Hidden Ark adding thematic Event Cards, Exploration, and a Campaign Mode!

The Mad King's Ire is a co-operative fantasy dungeon crawler, featuring, fast simultaneous turns, dice based combat with dice manipulation and engine building mechanics.

Trapped in the strange twisting realm of The Mad King, you must placate this crazed monarch by fulfilling tasks he sets. Working together to complete your set task will reveal the exit and allow you to escape and win the game. However you must do this before the other inhabitants of the dungeon overwhelm you. If one party member succumbs to their wounds, the task will fail and the game is over.

Quick set up time. Choose your hero, draw a task and you're ready to go! The dungeon builds as you play.

You never know what is around the next corner. Special event cards are drawn every time a room tile is revealed, and, when the Mad King's Ire raises, he may change the very nature of the dungeon in his rage.

As you explore the dungeon, you grow in power, with over 100 different skills to unlock and level up, and powerful items to discover, how you survive the Mad King's Realm is in your hands. Build the perfect character, but be aware, the longer you spend in the dungeon, the more dangerous it will become.

Dice based combat that isn't purely luck based. Dice manipulation mechanics allow you to spend resources to get the edge over your foes in combat, or utilise your hard earned skills to push the dice in your favour.

Multiple game modes with challenges and unlockable content, along with the randomised nature of the dungeon, tasks and levelling systems means you will never have the same experience twice.

Mad King's Ire has two game phases, the Hero phase and the Ire Phase:
During the Hero phase you will:
Use your momentum (action points) to explore the dungeon, combat foes and activate your special abilities.
Draw encounter cards when a room tile is revealed.

During the Ire phase: 
The Mad King's Ire raises, when it reaches maximum, an Ire card is drawn which will change the nature of the game. 
Foes in play move to attack the player's characters.

Turns and combat can be resolved simultaneously ensuring a fast paced gameplay where you are always part of the action, and a co-operative experience that isn't dominated by one player.

—description from the publisher

The famous game of "Des dingues" is back in a family format! More dice, More cups to throw them. The game that drives you crazy!! A game of observation and speed to have a good time with family or friends. Be the fastest to get the most dice and become the king.

Frenzy mode is for experienced players only, and makes the game even more tense and fast-paced. Brigades start without ingredients and draw them during their turn. They can then prepare a recipe or not. Do you have enough ingredients to make a recipe? It's up to you! But watch out for malpractice!

Scavenge, deceive, and grab the tastiest loot!

In Mapáchez you become a raccoon willing to do whatever it takes to snatch the delicious treats humans have thrown away. Fruit, cans, sugar… true wasted treasures! But you’re not alone: other raccoons are prowling around the bin, and only the craftiest one will claim the best haul.

Bluff, improvise, and use a bit of dirty play if needed… anything goes to fill your pantry!
A fast, fun, and slightly mischievous game—perfect for family sessions or play with friends.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Aux Armes Citoyens: The French Revolutionary Wars
description:
Aux Armes Citoyens is a game for two players simulating the Revolutionary Wars that ravaged France and Europe between 1792 and 1800.

One player takes on the role of the French Republic, striving to ensure the Revolution's survival against its internal and external enemies. The other player takes on the role of the Coalition, led by England and Austria, seeking to crush revolutionary France in order to restore the Ancien Régime.
 Like Napoleon against Europe, Aux Armes Citoyens uses a card-based system of actions and events.

Each game turn represents one year, divided into a variable number of rounds.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
You know Houdini for his escapes and illusions. But now you'll play an essential role in a crusade at the pinnacle of his career: publicly exposing fraudulent spirit mediums who trick trusting believers!

In this multivictor game, you are a member of Houdini’s "secret service," tasked with investigating these swindlers and helping expose them to the public.

You’ll have to learn how the fakers are getting away with it and then replicate their tricks (with a little help from Houdini himself!) all while balancing your own goals with those of the group.

Will you focus on helping your fellow investigators win together? Or will you keep an eye on your own future... after Houdini's last act?

—description from the publisher

England isn’t the only place stirring. Out on the cold waters of the Irish Sea, a new threat closes in - sails against the horizon, drums echoing over the waves, and a banner no one in Nottingham ever hoped to see again. The Pirates of the Isle of Man have returned, led by Princess Ragnhild, as fierce as the raiders who carved her bloodline into legend. She wants Sherwood’s riches, Nottingham’s vaults, and every vulnerable stretch of land in between.

The Pirates Expansion adds a fifth faction to HOOD: Troubles in Sherwood, raising the player count to five and reshaping the struggle for the forest. Ragnhild’s crew doesn’t play like anyone else at the table. They move fast, hit hard, and raid territories with the same hunger that once drove their Viking ancestors across the sea. Their strength comes from sudden strikes, shifting footholds, and a constant pressure that forces every other faction to react.

This is a campaign-exclusive expansion, available only on Gamefound and not planned for retail. It folds seamlessly into the core experience, adding new strategic tension, unexpected flashpoints, and a faction whose goals collide with everyone’s plans the moment they drop anchor.

—description from the publisher

Good Game High Score is a ladder climbing/card shedding game that levels up! Players will be dealt a hand of 7 cards and attempt to be the first to shed out (without reorganizing their hand) -- but don't get too comfortable! Every round a new LEVEL of difficulty will alter the dynamics of the gameplay.

You and your friends have entered an abandoned arcade where the machines have come to life and video game villains have burst out into the real world! You'll have to use your arcade expertise to perform combos (melds of cards) that will defeat each enemy one by one. The first player to defeat 3 of these fearsome foes will be crowned the winner and proudly display their high score!

—description from the publisher

818 (Eight Eighteen) is a fun, fast paced trick-taking game of strategy and luck designed for the whole family!

The deck consists of 66 cards in 5 suits numbered 1-13 and one "Clincher" card.

The game is played over a series of 15 rounds and the person with the highest points at the end is the winner.

Gameplay:
The dealer deals a different number of cards depending on the round, which follows the pattern of 8-7-6-5-4-3-2-1-2-3-4-5-6-7-8.

After each hand is dealt, the top card of the deck is flipped face-up to determine the trump suit for the round.

Players review their cards and decide how many trick’s they think they can win. Starting with the player left of the dealer and continuing clockwise, each player in turn order verbally announces their bid. 
However, the total number of tricks proposed to won by the players cannot equal the number of cards dealt for the round.

For Example: 
It’s the first round of play and 8 cards have been dealt to five players.
Four players have already announced their bids which total 5 tricks.
It is the dealer’s turn to bid, they cannot bid 3 (5 + 3 = 8).

Play begins with player left of the dealer and proceeds clockwise. Subsequent players must follow suit if able. The only exception is the “Clincher” card which can be played at anytime and wins the hand no matter what.

Scoring:
If a player hits their bid exactly, they take the number of tricks won as points plus a bonus of 10 points.

If a player misses their bid, they take the number of tricks won as points but do not receive the bonus 10 points.

If a player bids zero and succeeds, they receive the 10 bonus points (no tricks were taken).

Sum is all about adapting to change. Can you find the
cleverest way to play your hand? Do you have the insight and
tactical skill to outwit your opponents? If so, then Sum is the game for you! The first player to score 3 victory points wins the game!

A colorful trick taking delight with a few layers of depth. Find the balance of power and rule the game!

For each round you’ll choose whether you need to try and get the highest or the lowest sum! In 7 tricks you’ll then try your hardest to either win cards, or to avoid doing so. But beware of the Flip cards or 0 cards! These change the strength of the other cards!

Easy to learn
Quick to play
Flippin’ unpredictable

—description from the publisher
This adds ten new action cards.

In Gawain’s Journey, one player controls Sir Gawain, the valiant knight, while the other controls the Green Knight, his daunting opponent… well maybe.

Through a series of trials, Gawain must navigate the balance between honor and dishonor, proving his strength, valor, and virtue.

Gawain must complete his journey of trials and have more honor than dishonor to win the game.

The Green Knight has two different forms. They will randomly be dealt one of them and the role will be kept secret from Gawain.

Whether The Green Knight is Lord Bertilak or Morgana changes whether they want Gawain’s Journey to end honorably or dishonorably.
family: Theme: King Arthur / The Knights of the Round Table / Camelot

description:
The focus of the mini-expansion is animals vulnerable to climate change and global heating. The exclusive desert-adapted animals added in are rare and endangered sub-populations of highly specialised animals. These animals are the only ones in the game worth more than ten points because they are so difficult to safely introduce to your reserve.

The mini-expansion also gives you two extra conservation experts who will help protect your animals with their unique abilities. The climatologist helps both with climate protection and mitigation in the game; the veterinarian provides additional sanctuary space for your vulnerable animals.

Theme is a tv show of the same name.
You got 3 possible action:


     Draw 3 cards from 3 different drawing piles, with the back if the cards colored so you know with of the 4 colored family you draw. (Red yellow purple green)
     Play 3 cards, you got up to 5 max pile a the same time in front of you, you play cards to sell them and to get ownership of that character.
     Move 3 space characters that you own (majority or equal) or aren't own by anyone, you can move several character but 3 move in total.
     There also a bonus action if you got a character in a spot of his color you can draw a special card, that you can swap for any of the bonus action.


These special cards can be use for free, either for abilities written on them or use to gain 2.000 point if the 2 character on the card are in the same place (except start or prison)

On the game there is 2 objective place, those need condition to try to sell your characters. For example need 3 people with at least one red, one purple, one yellow but you can have more.
Once the condition for a place is met you can swap one of you action to try to resolve the place (throw dice and try to get dice condition of that place, you get as many throw as you got characters in it, if you fail next player who got characters in it can try until everyone as try).

If it's a success (2.000 point for who did it) and everyone who got characters in that place can sell them (more you got of the same one more point) 1 for 1.000, 2 for 3.000, 3 for 5.000, 4 for 8.000, 5 for 11.000, and 6 for 15.000.

If it's a fail no sell, in every case this count as a place done and game end in a number of place done (5 for 2/3 players, 7 for 4/5 players).

The gameplay mechanic is a bit like ticket to ride (draw or play or objective) and here you try to get same characters or joker to sell high latter.

In this family style memory game, help Iyanu Biyi, Toye, and Ekun discover the many peoples, places and artifacts of Yarubaland by uncovering the tile that matches the revealed card.

In the game players reveal a card from the deck and then race to find the matching tile among the facedown tiles in play.

On your turn you select one of the heroes, move the selected hero, and use their player power to reveal one of the facedown tiles. If the tile matches the revealed card you claim the card and flip over a new card for players to try to find. Play continues until one player has collected 7 cards.

Unleash the power of the Natural, Unnatural, and Supernatural as you join Iyanu on her journey to mastery! Flip cards to uncover powerful abilities and claim victory points, building your tableau with every bold move. But push your luck too far, and the energy may slip from your grasp. leaving you with nothing. Will you seize control or risk it all?

On your turn player draw cards from the draw deck until you decide to stop or have revealed too many divine power symbols, in which case all cards are discarded and your turn is over. If you stopped before you revealed too many divine power symbols you may purchase card(s) from the display to add them to your tableau.

Play continues until Iyanu appears in from the deck. When this occurs you finish the game round and whoever has the most points on the cards in their tableau is declared the winner.

In this expansion for Carcassonne, your roads are improved by cleverly placed bridges. Knights are courted by maidens in castles and farmers in barns score points aside from the cities.

—description from the publisher

Abacuachego (from Muisca: ab pquazygo, meaning “the game of maize”) is a strategy game for two players: Zipa and Zaque. It is played on a rectangular board with two parallel rows of ten pits per side and two stores, one at each end. Each player controls the row closest to them and the store located to their left. At the start, each pit contains five maize grains and the stores are empty.

				
				
					Turns:The Zipa plays first and then turns alternate. On your turn, perform the following actions in order:

1. You harvest by taking all the grains from one of your pits.
2. You sow by distributing the grains, one per pit, starting at the next pit in the sowing direction indicated by the parity of the harvested pit:

Even: clockwise direction
Odd: counter-clockwise direction

3. You may choose the opposite direction to the one marked by parity only if, when sowing in that direction, at least one of the grains will fall into an opposing pit.

When sowing, you always skip the stores, except when the last grain falls into your store, in which case you get an extra turn.

				
				
					Captures:All grains are captured in the row opposite the corresponding player and deposited in that player's store. There are three types of capture:


     Origin–destination match: You capture if the pit where the last grain falls ends with the same number as the pit you harvested.
     Adjacent match: You capture if the destination pit ends with the same number as one of its orthogonally adjacent pits:
         If the destination is on an opposing pit: compare with the left, right, or front
         If the destination is on your own pit: compare only with the front pit.
    


				
				
					End of the game:The game ends in two ways:


     If a player accumulates 51 or more grains in their store, they win immediately.
     Otherwise, after the last move:
         The player who empties all the pits in a single row (their own or the companion's) takes the remaining grains to his store.
         The winner is the one with the highest total score in their store.
    


—description from the designer

boardgame: Hellfire and the fall of Empire?: A solo campaign
description:
Hellfire and the fall of Empire? is designed to be a science fiction solo campaign. 
It will produce battles which you can, if you wish, play with an opponent, but I’ve also provided suggestions for solo play there as well. 
The campaign is designed to work with Hellfire rules which are 1/300 Sci-Fi, but frankly you wouldn’t have to do much tweaking to fit the campaign to another rule set should you so wish.

In this campaign, you are faced with the Empire falling. You are going to struggle to hold on to what you started out with. Your tax base could shrink, you might lose technology, and the forces of barbarism are all around, plotting your demise. Still, “It is better to light a candle than curse the darkness.” I have every confidence in your ability to overcome these trivial handicaps.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Hell by Starlight Campaigns: Trading your way into trouble
description:
Hell by Starlight Campaigns is written to help both the player with enthusiastic opponents but also to support the solo player as well. 
My hope is that it will help you build science fiction campaigns that let you use comparatively small forces, but in a realistic manner. In this universe you’re not a major player, in fact you’re probably not even a major nuisance. You’re just a jobbing merchant, trading your way around the sector, living on your wits and turning an honest credit whenever the opportunity presents itself.
Although written with Hell by Starlight rules in mind, you should be able to use other rule systems.
I confess that I've always felt that done properly, a campaign can provide far more interesting and unexpected games than just fighting ‘one off battles.’ Over the years I’ve had a lot of fun fighting skirmishes that nobody in their right mind would create from scratch. So with this guide I’ve tried to help you create a coherent background that hangs together for you. I hope I’ve managed to balance book keeping with fun, because frankly if you’re not enjoying it, something has gone wrong, no matter how ‘realistic’ things are.

—description from the designer

Spire’s End: Volume 2 – Episode 1 is an adult, narrative-driven card game for 1 to 3 players. It is a choose your path adventure that runs alongside the events of Volume 1. You follow a solitary beekeeper and a band of strangers through a blood-soaked western filled with dust, grit, and treachery. No prior knowledge is required. Episode 1 is a complete story that also connects to the larger saga through character crossovers and hidden ties.

Volume 2 will unfold over three episodes, planned for release about once a year. Each builds on the last as the stakes rise and the long journey toward the Spire grows darker.

Episode 1 drops you into a harsh frontier of dust heads, plains critters, and twitching trigger fingers. You will ride across hostile plains, walk the crooked streets of Witherford, and face enemies eager to put you in the ground.

All of it plays out beneath the distant Spire, always visible on the horizon as a reminder that this violent stretch of the frontier is only one part of a much larger nightmare.

—description from the designer

Caldoom by Starlight is written to expand and supplement Hell by Starlight Campaigns. 
It allows the reader to expand their trading experience. Or alternatively gives them somewhere new to run to when they’ve made other places too hot for them.
Ideally this will be an asset to both the solo player as well as the one with enthusiastic opponents. Alongside trading opportunities, the guide starts the exploration of the world of Caldoom. A not terribly prestigious world but still, somebody has to look after the scruffy and disreputable. So there are both scenarios and campaigns for you. You could try community policing, ‘mining company style’. Or you can run your own fiefdom in the Shambles, plotting to overthrow other crime lords and take over their territory. You can even hold the front line in a campaign in a conventional battle between to mining companies.
Although written with Hell by Starlight rules in mind, I hope I’ve included enough detail to ensure that you can use other rule systems.

—description from the publisher

Players are chefs in a small restaurant, completing their guests' ordered foods.
An order is fulfilled by playing relevant ingredient cards. Ingredient cards are obtained by drawing from a shared deck ('pantry'), or by trading with other players.
The luck of the draw is mitigated by 'creative cooking' - as each missing ingredient can be replaced by a pair of any ingredient cards, or a wildcard ('magic ingredient').
If a guest's order is not fulfilled in time, the guest leaves hungry. But if a special guest (culinary critic) leaves, everyone loses the game - thus occasionally collaboration is required.
Victory Points are awarded for each completed order. The game ends after 10, 15, or 20 orders are fulfilled (for short, medium, and long variants respectively). The player with most Victory Points is the winner.

ORMAN is a print-and-play roll and write game where players cultivate their own forests using just regular dice and a pencil. Each round, everyone uses the same roll of two white dice and two black dice to determine both the shape of the area to plant, and the type and density of trees to place.

The white dice define your planting shape, you may use one value, the other, their sum, or their difference. The colored dice let you choose a tree type and set the tree density. Rolling doubles on the colored dice also gives you the option to place a lake anywhere on your grid instead of planting.

Trees must be placed orthogonally adjacent to water or existing trees, starting from a pre-drawn lake. The goal is to grow large, connected groups near lakes, diversify tree species in marked columns, and fill your entire forest grid. Scoring rewards lakeside groves, column variety, and a complete map, while empty squares cost points.

ORMAN includes both multiplayer and solo modes, offering a relaxing yet strategic roll-and-write experience rooted in spatial planning and adaptability.

—description from the publihser

Alternative Masked Imp miniature, standee and enemy cards!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Threshold: Last Stand – Ridgeback Mountain Expansion
description:
A new region full of quests, adventures, secrets, items and a new enemy group! This region is designed for characters levels 10-25 and features a unique "tower defense" mechanic.

This expansion for Threshold comes with:
-1 Dual-Layered Region Board

This expansion for Threshold comes with:
-1 Beast Rider Standee

-description from the Gamefound campaign

A brand new character expansion and a new skill deck. The Assassin keeps a low profile in battle, but that’s only to enhance the crescendo of lethality they’re building toward.

This expansion for Threshold comes with:
-1 Assassin Standee
-1 Assassin Token
-1 Assassin Miniature
-1 Familiar Standee
-1 Familiar Miniature
-1 Class Board
-25 Assassin Class Cards
-14 Quickdraw Skill Cards

-description from the Gamefound campaign

Deal with Duel

Step into the role of a Commander in Q-IL. Strategically deploy your units, manage your actions, and hack your opponent's defenses to tilt the scales of war. Prepare your forces—the ultimate duel awaits.

In the battle, Compare the power from each lowest order unit. and battle with the higher until the enemy has defeated.

—description from the designer

Welcome to the bustling world of MicroMacro! There's something afoot at the football match. Someone is stealing from the unsuspecting fans, and now little Leo Bear has disappeared...

Help!

The stadium and its surroundings are a small snippet from the huge map of the award-winning hidden object detective board game MicroMacro: Crime City.

The 500-piece jigsaw contains over 40 exciting hidden object riddles, as well as two exclusive, child-friendly MicroMacro cases to download!

Content: 500 pieces, Poster of scene, 40+ tasks to find

boardgame: Fire and Dice: WW2 Rules for Miniature Wargames
description:
Fire and Dice is a concise, fast-play rule set for miniature wargaming in WW2 which revolves around dice values in lieu of combat tables and charts. This system is designed for collegiate play where winning is a goal, secondary to having fun.

Whilst there are plenty of modifier lists, these rules have no charts or calculation tables. As the name suggests, this is about reducing game mechanisms to a single competitive dice roll.

Modifiers for dice rolls only affect the activating player – the player who attempts to activate a unit during their turn by rolling for observation, morale and for shooting. The responding player makes unmodified dice rolls for concealment, armour value or to duck and cover. In all dice-rolls, the higher the score the better.
