À chaque tour, les joueurs retournent une carte de leur pile en même temps et doivent trouver un mot en fonction des contraintes : une ou des lettre(s) obligatoire(s), une lettre interdite, un thème imposé... Le plus rapide à trouver un mot qui répond aux critères le crie, mais attention à ce que la réponse soit bien valable !

Le dernier joueur à trouver un mot ou le premier qui se trompe ramasse toutes les cartes, et on passe au prochain tour. Quand toutes les piles sont vides, le joueur qui a le moins de cartes ramassées remporte la manche !

Des cartes spéciales comme "Flash !", "Inversion" ou l'accent anglais viennent pimenter la partie et rendre chaque manche unique. Facile à transporter, rapide à expliquer et toujours imprévisible, Momo La Bataille des Mots est un jeu de cartes fun, rapide, et ultra-rejouable avec ses variantes et modes de jeu disponibles !

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Forest Shuffle: Roseate Spoonbill / American Alligator Promo Card
description:
Promo card (P027) Originally available as part of A Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Roseate Spoonbill - red binocular action: Play a bird for no cost and receive 1 point per Bird icon.

American Alligator - Draw 1 card. 1 point per Pawed Animal, Amphibian, Bird, Cloven-hoofed Animal, or Boot icon

boardgame: Castles of Mad King Ludwig: Dice Tower 2025 Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of A Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

This is a royal decree card.

Ability:
Before you build your 10th Room You may discard any of your Bonus cards and replace them from the deck (once per game)
family: Game: Castles of Mad King Ludwig

description:
The game is played on a cloth playing surface that has two networks of lines on it; one orange and one blue.  Each network has marked intersections where that network's lines come together.
  Players first place 12 to 14 clusters, each consisting of one gem put between the lines of a network and four ring tokens put in the intersections surrounding the gem on that network.
  Players take turns moving 2 of their ring tokens once all of the clusters have been placed.  An opponent's gem is captured and removed when a player has moved his ring tokens into each of the four intersections surrounding that gem.
  The game ends when all of one player's gems have been captured.

Ternary aspires to be a fun and strategic expand and exploit game with a minimal ruleset.

In Ternary, workers lay tiles to build a network of roads along which workers move to claim resources and to strategically position themselves.

Playing this game sometimes feels like doing a puzzle, with careful tile laying and infrequent movement, and other times
it plays like a ruthless game of greedily grabbing points and in-your-face positioning.

There is a 1-player variant, and is best for 2-3 players, but up to 6 can play.

—description from the designer

In The Flea and The Circus, you are playing as a flea traveling through the circus trying to collect as many tickets as you can to deliver to the prize booth in 12 turns.

—description from the publisher

In Highlands, players take on the role of dedicated conservationists working to repair and rewild the wilderness of Scotland. Using card-driven engine-building and an elegant action selection mechanism, players restore terrain, introduce native flora and fauna, and conduct field research to create a thriving ecosystem.

Highlands will is part of Alley Cat Games advanced games line, which include titles such as Ada's Dream, Baghdad and Calimala.

—description from the publisher

An Alice’s Adventures In Wonderland themed game, with drawings from the original book. The Queen of Hearts is mad after discovering the gardener’s painted the roses red. Try to pass the blame onto the other gardeners and win the most cards.

Gameplay: Take turns playing cards of higher rank. Cards go face down so you can lie. Gardeners can group together in rank to be stronger. But if you are caught in a lie then the hand goes to the person who calls you out. When the Queen goes down, the only one who can calm things down is the King. There is a 6 card expansion pack which adds Wonderland themed madness to the game play. A seriously light hearted game of bluff, suitable for party or as part of a poker session.

Goal: To win the most cards by bluffing all other players, using standard poker tactics and seemingly mad manoeuvres.

—description from the designer

We didn't think you'd ever wake up after the hit you took to the head there. Come on, sit up and have some water, you look parched. Don't worry, it's clean. HA! Clean of Ichor at least. What do you mean you don't know what Ichor is? Just how hard of a hit did you take? Ichor! The algae what's eating up the whole world. By the Keelhauled Saint! Come on, on your feet. Look over the railing there. You see all that pink shite on the water? That's the Ichor. Came in over the Great Net Wall, and brought the flood to these here streets. Poor Haven, she was the jewel of the Maroon Island, and now she's not much more than a drowned corpse. That's where we are, you dolt. We're in Haven. Trapped here by the flood. It got in behind us before the lot of us could flee. No more ships, what can float, left in the city now. Just us dead men and our brandy. Come on, I'll show you around. Grab that cutlass you had with you, you're going to need it. The city might be dead, but the rats and scavengers are still about. Some of them are even the size of a man, if you catch my drift.

Tidemark is a, nautical-themed, survival table top game, designed as part of the ROC Flooded Game Jam, which takes place in the drowned city of Haven. 
Destroyed by the ever-present Ichor, a mysterious magical algae that consumes all living beings and produces salt water as a byproduct, the streets of the city are choked with debris, water, and half consumed corpses. No one survives contact with The Ichor, except for the lucky few carrying enough Tin to ward it off.

Take control of your crew of mismatched survivors from the Islands of the Great Cay and help guide them through the drowned city. Gather Tin to generate punches and buy new equipment to stay ahead of your foes. Fight other survivor Crews with a lethal mix of Fencing, Throwing, and Shooting to push and maneuver them to the edge and toss them into the unforgiving Ichor. Just be careful not to be thrown in yourself. Earn experience and gain Skills (and some injuries) as you gather enough Tin to plate a raft and float inland to safety.

This download includes everything you need to get started (short of actual miniatures and terrain):

     The Core Rules with illustrations by DarthMolls
     6 Scenarios to play and replay as you grow your Crew
     A roster for tracking experience, skills, and accrued injuries between games
     32 pieces of equipment and 10 backgrounds to make sure that no two Crews will be alike
     A sheet of full color tokens for tracking effects and statuses


—description from the publisher

Resolve - The Last Sanctuary is free to PnP as I have a MIGHTY NEED for play testers. The game is in Early development meaning that art and aesthetics are no where NEAR being finalized and a few items may make no sense at all. That's where ya'll come in. All I ask is that you comment your thoughts and suggestions so we can make this one better together.

Welcome to the world of Aether. Generations ago, a shimmering tear in the sky appeared. We call it Great Void Rift.The last bastion of civilization is the Citadel of Aethel, built atop an ancient nexus of spiritual and logistical power.

The citadel functions as a kind of global command center, where the world's remaining resources are pooled and its defenses are coordinated. The Citadel’s physical and magical defenses are constantly being corroded by the Rift's chaotic energy manifesting as a looming Crisis. Over time, the crisis becomes more urgent and the Citadel's collapse becomes imminent.

The players are not kings or heroes, but the Resolvers—a highly specialized council of tactical minds, resource managers, and spiritual leaders. They are the only ones with the authority to draw upon the shared Communal Deck of resources (Logistics, Chants, Relics) and contribute power to seal the threats.

Because the survival of the Citadel depends entirely on their strategic choices, the Ruling Elder Council monitors their performance. The goal is paradoxical: the Resolvers must stabilize the collective crisis while vying for the individual recognition (Trust) required to be named ruler and lead the world into the next age of peace.

—description from the publisher

Insurgent Tide: Counterinsurgency in Iraq, 2003-2008 is a 2-player operational/strategic level game covering the Coalition’s counterinsurgency effort in Iraq from 2003-2008. Each year is a turn, but is composed of alternating card plays between players. Units range in size from Multinational Divisions down to Insurgent cells.

The Coalition’s objective is to exert control over Iraq’s provinces; the Insurgent player seeks to prevent that and/or do it himself, and to wage Jihad to inflict as many Coalition casualties as possible. The game lasts 5 full turns (2004-2008), with 2003 consisting of just a few cards to get things started.

Each turn consists of a year, during which multiple card plays occur. These give the players movement, combat, and other actions. At the end of each year, players must make critical decisions on which cards to re-buy in an attempt to win the battle for control of Iraq. Insurgent Tide is based on the popular, action-packed Pacific Tide game system by Gregory M. Smith, with many combat and strategic decisions to challenge players in just a single evening’s game.

The core of the game is the unique card re-buy system, in which players take their annual production and decide which cards they need for the upcoming year. Cards not only provide for reinforcements, but also allow for movement, combat, and other actions. Which cards to rebuy is, without question, one of the key decisions the player must make to prepare for next year’s operations.

The Insurgent player’s build points are based on how many provinces he controls: to control them, he must activate his cells. When active, they contest control of a province but are also vulnerable to Coalition attack. The Coalition player needs to build control via the creation of Iraqi Police and Iraqi Army units, but has political concerns to deal with, from high levels of casualties to inflaming anger in the population due to excessive use of force.

Besides the focus on card play, the game features a small footprint (one standard map) and is also designed to be played in just a single evening – estimated at 3 hours for experienced players to fight the entire war.

While Insurgent Tide is designed as a 2-player gaming experience, an additional option for solitaire play is provided by the inclusion of a Solitaire Bot. This “bot” creates action priorities for the non-human player and has the advantage of being simple enough to implement without spending an inordinate amount of time as the non-player.

Product Information:
    Complexity: 4/10
    Playing time: 3-4 hours
    Solitaire Suitability: High (solitaire bot system)
    Players: 1 or 2
    Map Scale: Abstract
    Time Scale: 1 year Turns; each composed of multiple card plays
    Unit Scale: Insurgent Cells up to Coalition Divisions

-description from publisher's website
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
In Spawnimon, players take on the roles of cultists trying to achieve domination by influencing celestial Spawnimon monsters to attack their opponents. Each game deals out a different array of monsters from the deck of 153, and players choose their starting Spawnimon. Players then take turns attacking one another, playing bonus cards from their hands and taking personal damage for summoning new creatures. Last cultist standing wins!

The Spawnimon are each a mashup inspired from pocket monsters and the Cthulhu mythos, such as Pikathulhu or Bulbokrug.

You want to be the very best
Ph'nglui mglwas
To summon is your real test
To conquer is your cause
Gonna smite us all, SPAWNIMON!

Explore the world of Yorubaland in this exciting terrain building chase game for 2-6 players. First build the board by placing terrain tiles, then divided into teams - The Corrupt and Team Chosen.

Team Chosen must race to reach The Source before being captured by The Corrupt. Each terrain type will affect movement of the animals and characters differently. Help Iyanu and her friends reach the Source safely.

The game is played over 2 Phases - the Terrain Building Phase and the Chase Phase.

During the Terrain Building Phase, the players alternate turns drawing a terrain pile and choosing to place it on the board for save it for a player to plane later. This phase continues until all the terrain tiles have been placed.

During the Chase Phase, the players alternate turns rolling the 2 movement dice and then moving one of their characters. The movement dice affect how you move and how many spaces that you can move. Character abilities also affect your movement and dice rolls.

The Chase Phase continues until the Corrupt have captured all the members of Team Chosen, or all of Team Chosen has made it to the The Source Tile.

Micro DOS is a compact, travel-friendly version of the classic DOS card game, redesigned with a streamlined 54-card deck and micro-sized cards. Unlike the original, this edition is optimized for 2 or 3 players, making it perfect for quick, portable gameplay.

The deck includes 10 Wild cards, one of each number card in all colors, and two DOS cards per color, creating a faster and more tactical experience while preserving the core DOS mechanics. Match numbers, chain plays, and shout “DOS!” when you’re down to two cards—now in a tiny, pocket-sized format you can take anywhere.

This micro-sized Skip-Bo edition offers a compact and fast-paced twist on the classic game. The deck contains a total of 54 cards, including four copies of each number card from 1 to 12 and only 6 Skip-Bo (wild) cards. Thanks to the ultra-small card size, the game becomes highly portable and easy to play anywhere.

Designed for 2 to 4 players, this version keeps the original goal intact: be the first to empty your stockpile.

boardgame: Tee Box Chaos: A Hilarious Golf Card Game
description:
Tee Box Chaos transforms your regular round of golf into an unpredictable, action-packed competition. This innovative card game is designed to be played alongside your actual golf round on the course, adding a layer of strategic chaos and entertainment to every hole. Whether you're a serious golfer or a casual player, Tee Box Chaos creates memorable moments and hilarious situations that make every round more exciting.

As you play through your 18 holes, players draw and play cards that can dramatically affect scoring, create unexpected challenges, or introduce wild scenarios that mirror the ups and downs of real golf. The specialized deck features golf-themed action cards representing everything from spectacular shots to frustrating mishaps—"hole in one," "lost ball," "sand trap," and more. These cards interact with your actual play on the course, creating a dynamic blend of real golf performance and strategic card play.

The goal is to combine your actual golf skills with smart card management to achieve the lowest score after 18 holes. Strategic decisions matter: when do you play that game-changing card? How do you protect yourself from opponents' sabotage? The result is a unique hybrid experience where your physical game and your card strategy both contribute to victory.

Compact and course-friendly, Tee Box Chaos is perfect for casual rounds with friends, golf outings, scrambles, or any time you want to add extra excitement to your day on the links. It brings a fresh dimension to golf that keeps everyone engaged from the first tee to the final putt.

—description from the publisher

Find out by playing what you know about boating, equipment and safe seagoing? Learn independently, test your knowledge, challenge your friends and learn together. The deck has 108 cards which have questions worth 1-3 "gulls" and answers to them. The cards can be used for practicing alone, to play a 2-player game, or to play with a bigger group. People of different ages can play together. There are three different ways to play. Right answer wins the card. If necessary you may also make up a funny/crazy answer. The player with most "gulls" or cards collected is the "gull of gulls" of the game group.

This game is fully bilingual with the rules, box text and each card in both Finnish and Swedish, and has been produced in co-operation with Finnboat/suomiveneilee.fi

—description from back of the box (translated)

Zany Cornhole transforms traditional cornhole into an unpredictable party game experience by adding strategic card play to every toss. This 52-card deck introduces wild variations, bonus challenges, and game-changing twists that turn a casual backyard game into an engaging tactical competition.

Players draw cards before each throw, revealing challenges that range from tossing blindfolded or backwards to earning bonus points for airmail shots or executing tricky bank shots off the board. Special cards like "Trade Places," "Handy Cap," and "Steal a Bag" add strategic depth beyond simple accuracy, while penalty cards like "Air Ball" and "Brick" create dramatic momentum swings. The deck keeps gameplay fresh across multiple rounds, ensuring no two games feel the same.

Perfect for cornhole enthusiasts looking to add variety to their standard games or party hosts seeking an accessible activity with built-in entertainment, Zany Cornhole works with any regulation cornhole setup. The game scales easily from casual backyard gatherings to tournament play, with rules that take minutes to learn but provide endless replayability. Whether you're a cornhole veteran or newcomer, the cards inject just enough chaos to level the playing field while rewarding both skill and adaptability.

-description from publisher

It's a beautiful day, the sun is shining and the water is warm. You decide with your friends to go on a sailing trip - you're having a wonderful time. Suddenly the weather changes! A storm is coming and your old sailboat is starting to fall apart. You now have 5 minutes to get back safely to shore.

Tollot Merellä ("Fools at Sea") is a chaotic and intensive co-operative card game, where your goal is to use communication to get your ship safely to shore. Playing simultaneously each player flips a problem card and to solve the card, you need different tools. However you don't know which player has the tool, so you have to communicate. There are no turns, everyone plays and everyone shouts at the same time! Hidden in the deck of Problem cards are 6 special cards forming a picture of a seaworthy boat, and you win together when you find all of those. But remember, you only have 5 minutes! Once you've finished one level, you can move on to the next one with more cards, more difficult problems and more impact from Mother Nature.

So can you beat all the levels and sail the ship safely back to shore and have the party of your life... Or will you die a devastating, horrible death?

This game was a participant in the Vuoden Peli 2021 awards competition (Party games), and was distributed by ToyRock Oy (TRG Games).

The Coming of the Flood is a co-operative, simultaneous play, real time, dexterity miniatures game. 
You and your fellow players are benevolent, if not entirely competent, gods of a small village. You are trying to assist the people of said village in protecting themselves from a coming flood brought about by the titans, or the devil, or the gods of a rival village, or global warming or something.

You will need:
1- 2’x2’ Playing area.
1- Timing Device.
4- 28mmish miniatures. It’s a good idea if they’re ones you don’t mind getting knocked about.
30- Smallish dice. A block of Chessex 12mm dice are perfect.
1- Jenga set. 
1- Measuring device per player.  
2 OR 4 players    - Not 2 to 4. Each with two hands.

The game will eventually require that you stack 30 dice in a tower in the middle of the table without it toppling over. Current experience is that this is largely impossible. To make a tower of such godlike proportions you and your fellow gods will need to build a bulwark from larger and more stable Jenga blocks that your dice tower can lean against to make the feat achievable.

—description from the publisher

Tea & Schnapps is a contract bidding trick taker that takes inspiration from Ulti and Dreierschnapsen.

In Tea & Schnapps, players are next door neighbors that take turns hosting their own tea & schnapps parties. Before the party starts, the host (forehand) needs to make sure the cooler (talon) is well stocked by putting two cards in it. After stocking the cooler, they must proclaim that their party is starting (bid on a contract).
Once the host is done, the other players can take the time to check the cooler and make their own proclamation or sit on the couch (pass).
The player who makes the best proclamation will be the life of the party (declarer) and must put their money where their mouth is and do what they proclaimed! However, before the first trick, they can check their secret stash (a second talon?!) and exchange two cards with it.

Some proclamation examples are:

     Schnapps Party: First to 66+ card points
     Designated Driver: Lose every trick
     Second Wind: Win 4+ tricks, one of them must be the last trick
     Last Call On Schnapps: Win the last trick with the 1 of Trump and/or reach 66+ card points


If a player successfully proclaims, they will score game points equal to that contract. Otherwise, the other players will split those game points instead.

The first player to the required game points will score 1 match point. The first to 1/2/3 match points in a short/medium/long game wins!

There is an advanced version of the game that includes compulsory contracts, feats, and compendium-style contracts!

—description from the designer

Suku Kata is an educational literacy game designed for children aged 6 and above. The game introduces players to syllables, simple word structures, and basic vocabulary through playful exploration. It combines visual memory, phonetic awareness, and early reading skills in a format that is accessible for young learners.

During the game, players flip over hidden syllable cards arranged in a grid while trying to match them with the open target word cards displayed at the center of the table. If a revealed syllable matches any part of a target word, the player may flip an additional syllable card to attempt forming the complete word. Success allows the player to claim the target word card and immediately draw a new one for the shared display.

The gameplay encourages children to recall the positions of previously revealed syllables (memory), apply their knowledge by combining syllables into meaningful words, and reason through possible syllable combinations that match the target images. Through repeated matching and reading aloud during word claims, children naturally reinforce vocabulary, strengthen letter–sound recognition, and build early reading fluency.

The game ends when a player successfully collects three target word cards, making them the winner. The structure is simple, fast-paced, and motivating—ideal for supporting early literacy in a fun, interactive setting.

—description from the publisher

Nelayan Tobelo is a competitive card game for 2-4 players (ages 6+) designed to teach numeracy and geometric recognition, where players must defeat a monster using their number skills.

The game is played over four challenge rounds followed by a final monster battle. In the first four rounds, players draw and play Number Cards (featuring numbers 1-10, Circles, and Triangles) to meet criteria set by a revealed Challenge Card (e.g., "Largest Circle Loses").

Winning players keep their card, while losing players discard an extra card, forcing card management as they draw back up to four. In the final round, players sum the geometric values of their remaining hand cards to check if they exceed the strength of the chosen Monster.

The ultimate winner is the player who successfully defeats the Monster and has accumulated the highest total number of "Fish" icons on all the cards they won throughout the game.

Pasar Tobelo is a competitive card game for 2–4 players (ages 7+) focused on teaching numeracy, basic operations (addition/subtraction), and real-world application through the theme of fulfilling banana orders in a market.

Players take turns engaging in a push-your-luck mechanic, revealing Banana Cards (values 1–5) from a grid one by one while managing the risk of revealing Monkey Cards ($-1$ or $-2$) which reduce the total.

The objective is to match a running banana total to one of the available Order Cards (6-15) and Claim! it, or risk a "Lanjut!" (Continue!) to aim for a higher order, though exceeding the maximum order results in a "Bust." Claimed orders are stored in a player's Depot until they choose the second action: Ship Orders, securing their value.

The game ends when a player successfully ships three orders, and the winner is the one with the highest total monetary value from their shipped orders.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Flock Together: Bagworm Moth Caterpillar Promo Card
description:
Originally available as part of A Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

A unique 2-Health Grub Card.
When attacking, roll. On a 1, miss your attack.
One time ability: When attacking a Predator, roll a die. On a 1-2, Predator's return attack is reduced by 1; on a 3-4, Predator's return attack is reduced by 2; on a 5-6, Predator's return attack is reduced by 3.

-description from publisher

Cerita Tobelo is an educational card game for 2-4 players (ages 7+) focused on enhancing reading comprehension and temporal reasoning by requiring players to logically sequence events within a narrative. The game utilizes 84 Story Cards divided into six different context sets (e.g., Local History, Daily Activities, Climate Change, or Fufu Fish Processing).

In the main Classic Mode, players draw a card and must guess the correct chronological slot to place it among a growing timeline of previously revealed cards, shouting out their guess (e.g., "Before Card B" or "After Card C"). The cards are then revealed to show the correct "time stamp" or "chapter number." If the player guesses correctly, the card is placed in the designated slot, expanding the timeline, and the turn passes.

The goal is to correctly sequence the entire deck for the chosen story set, with the emphasis being on practice, peer feedback, and mastery of the reading material rather than competitive scoring.

—description from the designer

Dadu Abjad ("Alphabet Dice") is a competitive, speed-based game designed for 2–4 players (ages 5+) with a strong focus on inclusive literacy by introducing both standard letter shapes and Braille.

The game's theme is a fast-paced challenge to quickly match words. In each round, one Mission Card is revealed, showing a target word and its corresponding Braille. Importantly, the game includes provisions for players with hearing or sight difficulties, utilizing sign language or verbal instruction from a companion. All players simultaneously race to roll and arrange their individual sets of four Letter Dice to spell the target word correctly from left to right. The first player to successfully arrange their dice presses the central Bell. If their dice arrangement correctly matches the Mission Card, they claim the card as a point; if incorrect, they skip the current round while others continue.

The goal is to be the first player to collect 5 Mission Cards to win instantly, or, if the deck runs out, to be the player with the most collected Mission Cards.

—description from the designer

Dadu Angka ("Number Dice") is a quick, real-time competitive game for 2–4 players (ages 5+) focused on inclusive numeracy, specifically aiming to teach standard number shapes, basic geometric shapes, and their corresponding Braille forms. The theme is a race to match number and shape sequences. In each round, a Mission Card is revealed, showing a sequence of numbers or shapes.

For players with hearing or sight difficulties, companions provide instructions via sign language or verbal reading of the mission. All players simultaneously race to arrange their personal set of three Number Dice (which display the required numbers/shapes) according to the sequence on the card. The fastest player to correctly match the sequence rings the central Bell. If their arrangement is correct, they claim the Mission Card as a point; if incorrect, they skip the round while others continue.

The primary goal is to be the first player to collect 5 Mission Cards to achieve an instant win, or to possess the most cards when the deck runs out.

—description from the designer

boardgame: 集まれ！ワニワニ団 (Gather up! Wani-Wani Gang)
description:
The rules are simple, but tricky... Gather your friends with the dice.
This is a dice and card game for 2-5 players.
Players roll the dice and use the two halves
to collect as many friends as possible from the other players' hands.

Both the roller and the receiver of the dice will have fun feeling happy or sad about the outcome, and the
game will delight both casual players and gamers.
This is the new classic dice game!

boardgame: Puzzle Dungeon Intro Deck Expansion: The Devoured Temple
description:
The Devoured Temple is an expansion to the Puzzle Dungeon Intro Deck. The Intro deck is required to use this expansion.

Contents: 16 normal heroes, 16 dungeon heroes, 6 double-sided dungeon rooms, 2 double-sided trap/treasure cards, 1 supply card, 2 room ability reference cards, five supply tokens (may be any color), and rules.

This expansion pack introduces a new game mode: Dungeon Delves. In this game mode, you'll use a dungeon hero to battle through a series of three increasingly difficult dungeon rooms in The Devoured Temple. Dungeon rooms have effects that trigger when monsters are defeated in specific spaces. Completing a room awards you a treasure card. Use supplies to navigate the dungeon and escape with the loot.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Carcassonne: The Wonders of Humanity Set III
description:
A third set of Wonders of Humanity. This time containing: Cothon of Carthage, Taj Mahal, Great Wall of China, Nazca Lines

At the end of the game, you first score the wonders that score at the end of the game. After that, you continue with normal final scoring. Each wonder tile counts as 5 individual normal tiles. Accordingly, the roads and cities can consist of more than one title. You must take this into account during the scoring.

It’s time for the Big Ocean Tournament!

Each year, sharks from all around the world compete in an impressive feast in the hopes of earning the title of Sharp Toothed

Shark Sovereign. Are you ready? On your mark… fish!
On each turn, each shark (player) points to one of the piles of food cards in the ocean! If he’s the only one to point at a pile, he wins it and begins his various point-earning collections.

But beware: if a shark is hungry, it points with both hands, so it can win cards more easily, even the pile you’re looking for…

—description from the publisher

Deep within the whispering woods, a bustling community is coming to life.

In Fox Grove, you are trying to build the most prosperous little animal village in the forest. Over the course of 4 rounds, you must skillfully manage your hand of cards and your resources to outshine your rivals.

At its heart, Fox Grove is a unique blend of mechanics. Each round begins with a trick-taking phase where playing the right cards is key to gather essential resources. However, veteran card players should stay on their toes: there is a twist in the trick-taking rules that will force you to rethink your standard strategies and adapt to the changing rhythm of the forest.

Using the resources gathered from your tricks, you will expand your personal tableau. 

     Expand your Village: Turn resources into buildings to generate Victory Points.
     Unlock Abilities: Gain special skills that provide powerful advantages in future rounds.


Whether you are a seasoned gamer or new to the hobby, Fox Grove welcomes you. The game features a dedicated Introductory Mode, designed to offer an easy entry point for families and beginners before diving into the full strategic depth of the standard game.

After four rounds, the player with the most magnificent village and the most Victory Points wins the game!

—description from the publisher

Игри на Волята ("Games of Will") is a competitive strategy board game that challenges players’ endurance, speed, diplomacy, and risk management. Each player assumes a distinct role with unique abilities — from cunning manipulators to team-oriented strategists. The game uses three types of dice, each offering different levels of risk and reward. Players engage in battles, strategic voting, and resource management as they navigate camps, accumulate votes and support, and vie for final victory.

boardgame: Borderlands: Mister Torgue's Arena of Badassery – LOU13
description:
L0U13 arrives with a campaign expansion all his own.

Includes:

     LOU13 miniature, VH and skills cards
     Bounty and Enemy cards 
     Assassination Campaign Book
     36 loot cards

Get ready for fast hands and even faster brains!

In Fast Out, there is no waiting for your turn — everyone plays at the same time. Keep your eyes on the category in the middle and check your letters: Can you name a "Sport" starting with "T"? Or a "Food" starting with "P"?

Be the first to shout out the answer and play your card to change the category for everyone else. Speed is key, but don't lose your cool! The first player to get rid of their entire stack wins.

-description from publisher

Payload is an exciting new tabletop card game built around the world of rockets. You don’t need to know any rocket science to play—just bring your curiosity, your strategy, and your competitive spirit. The game mixes 90% fun with a light touch of real space engineering, giving players a thrilling experience that still stays true to how rockets actually work.

The game in brief:

Payload is a 1–5 player game, perfect for ages 14 and up (though, enjoyable by players of all ages), and takes about 45 minutes to play. Each player becomes the Chief Engineer of a rising rocket company. Your mission? Be the first to send two payloads into space.

The game begins with a tense bidding phase, where players compete for valuable payload contracts. Bid smart, earn money, and choose the missions that will shape your company’s path. Then the real race begins. You’ll hire talented engineers, gather blueprints, and piece together the right parts to build a rocket that won’t blow up on launch day.

Every turn feels like a step toward liftoff as players block, out think, and out build each other. And when your rocket is finally ready, everything comes down to the final countdown: 3... 2... 1... Liftoff! Did your hard work pay off, or will luck and pressure send you back to the drawing board?

Payload stays true to real rocket concepts but keeps everything simple and fun (and competitive!).

-description from designer

Covenia is a strategic card collection game where players build powerful clans and unleash unique abilities.
On your turn, choose whether to add a card to one of your 5 clans or discard a card to trigger its effect.
Each clan represent a strong and iconic female character, with its own scoring mechanics and unique effects.
Combine strategy, timing and clever choices to outscore your opponents and claim victory.

This is a game for 2 to 6 players using 36 cards that quantify the abilities of famous Samurais.
The ability categories are Recruitment cost, Dignity points, Military strength, Trickery strength, and Card number.
In this game, players recruit their own Samurai during the Recruitment Round, and use those Samurai to win more battles in the Battles Round wins the game.

During the Recruitment Round, players can recruit a  masterless Samurai card from the field by contributing a total Dignity points equal to or greater than the card's Recruitment cost.
Players can use the Dignity points of the Samurai card only once by putting the Samurai card in their hand on the field.
In this way, become a masterless Samurai card by putting it on the field from your hand.
During the Battles Round, all players simultaneously declare and play one of their Samurai cards, choosing to fight by Military or Trickery.
The declared battle method wins if more players choose it.
If the number is equal, the declared battle method of the player who played the Samurai card with the highest card number wins.
Once the battle method is decided, the Samurai card with the higher Military strength wins if Military was declared, or the Samurai card with the higher Trickery strength wins if Trickery was declared.
The winer gains 1 victory point and discards all the Samurai cards played in the battle.
The player who gets the most victory points wins the game.

Airborne Commander is a solitaire WW2 paratrooper deck building game depicting the 101st Airborne Division's actions in support of the D-Day landings.

Beginning with a starting deck of just a few men, a couple non-coms and a lieutenant, face off against the German deck while trying desperately to recruit more cards from the drop zone in order to build up your force for assaults against objectives. Points are earned for defeating enemy cards and taking objectives, but combat can result in lost cards and the dilution of the deck with disorganized cards providing a tense "deck survival" experience.

Combat includes small arms fire, suppression fire, bazookas, gammon grenades, anti-tank guns, indirect artillery support and air strikes. German cards represent the actual forces and terrain present including personnel from Ost battalions and static divisions, armored cars, STUGs, blockhouses, Rommel's asparagus and flooded fields. The objective cards represent the actual causeways, locks, artillery positions, headquarters and other locations that were assigned and have features unique to each.

Assign your airborne cards to one of three areas: The Battleground, Support Area, or the Reserve. Weigh the cost of buying time with your troopers against suffering reverses in order to get more and better equipped men. Do you take the mortar from the drop zone for its firepower or a high ranking officer for his additional actions? Only the airborne commander can decide.

—description from the publisher

Cờ toán Việt Nam (Vietnamese Math Chess) is a creative product of Mr. Vu Van Bay (also known as Vu Bay), a Vietnamese sculptor. Vietnamese Math Chess was officially recognized as an intellectual product by the Department of Copyright of Literary and Artistic Authors, Ministry of Culture - Information (now the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism) in May 2005.
The chessboard is rectangular, consisting of 99 squares (divided into 9 columns, each column has 11 squares and 11 rows, each row has 9 squares). The squares in row 2, column 5 and row 10, column 5 have diagonal lines, which are the positions for the number 0 (ie, during the game, the number 0 cannot move). The chess pieces are circular, each side has a different color, on each piece there are round dots (or numbers) representing the numbers 1-9 (for example, the number 1 piece has one round dot; the number 9 piece has nine round dots).

Thus, each side has nine chess pieces and one number 0 piece. Both sides arrange their pieces in the bottom horizontal row in order 1-9 (from left to right, i.e. increasing).

Except for the number 0 piece which cannot be moved out of position because it is a moving 0, the remaining pieces 1-9 can move horizontally, vertically, and diagonally. Each empty square on the chessboard is a move. The maximum number of moves is determined by the value of each piece. For example, the number 2 can move up to 2 empty squares, the number 9 can move up to 9 empty squares depending on the player's purpose. The number 1, because it is a mover, can only move to one square next to the square it is standing on. The pieces are not allowed to jump over the heads of other pieces.

When you want to capture an opponent's piece, the condition is that your side must have two pieces standing in two adjacent squares vertically, horizontally, or diagonally (to form a calculation and there is no opponent's piece in front of it). Then use addition (+), subtraction (-), multiplication (x), or division (:) together to get the answer. The answer to each calculation is the point at which the opponent's piece can be captured.

The Yuletide Expansion is a way to bring holiday cheer and the winter spirit to your Tavern.

Lots of delicious holiday baked goods and yuletide decorations to stock your Tavern, along with a host of wintery patrons to come have a cup of hot cocoa! Be on the look-out for Saint Klaus, or the Krampus!

This new expansion comes with the Yuletide Rules Booklet, 45 Tavern cards, 31 Patron cards, 9 Dirty Deeds, 1 Port card, and 3 Song cards.

In VENATOR: Heroes of the Arena, players take on the role of a Gladiator’s trainer, managing their gladiators and orchestrating epic battles to prove their skill in three deadly Arenas.

Each player controls three Gladiators, one Light, one Medium, and one Heavy, and begins with a personal deck of cards and a handful of coins. Over three rounds, players refine their hands, acquire Combat Cards, and send their fighters into the Arenas to face rival Gladiators and fearsome Creatures.

At the start of each round, cards are drawn and discarded, coins collected, and any missing Creatures or Objectives are replenished. During the Market Phase, players spend coins to obtain Combat Cards that offer immediate advantages or upgrades for future battles. In the Arena Phase, Gladiators are deployed to one of three Arenas, targets are chosen, upgrades applied, and combat resolved through wounds and defeated opponents.

Fame is earned by defeating Gladiators and Creatures, inflicting wounds, and completing Objectives, with extra Fame awarded for leftover coins. After the final round, the Gladiator’s trainer with the highest Fame claims victory!

—description from the publisher

Santa's Bag Blitz(en) is a hand-management bluffing game wrapped up in a perfect holiday theme for Christmas.

Players are dealt a hand of 5 cards and through 3 rounds of drawing and discarding, try to build the perfect bag of toys for Santa to gain the most points.

Bluffing about what they are discarding can help players get rid of coal that may be in their hands, but if they're caught, they'll need to keep it forever! Or try to keep as much coal as possible to go for a Krampus bag and shoot the moon.

Pesky reindeer clog up your hand and aren't worth any points - unless you can find them all!

This game is quick, has a lot of player interaction and is suitable for ages 6+.

-description from designer

Dragons Down: Wildlands is the 4th expansion for Dragons Down and introduces...
- Two new terrains packs: Tangled Thickets and the Hostile Hills, including new mechanics and rules (on par with the complexity of Eastern Reaches)
- New monsters and natives specific to the new terrain packs. Fight tribal crag fiends and fearsome jaguar warriors, cultists, and massive jungle insects.
- The largest addition of new treasures in an expansion since the core game released.
- A large number of new treasure sites and fearsome guardians.
- All new classes to play. And maybe a new lineage as well.
- All new missions and merchants specific to the new terrain packs.
- New optional rules to customize your game play experience.
- Fully developed weather system that you can incorporate into your gaming sessions!

Mega Mall is a small, 34 card solitaire game where you assume the role of a Retail Designer who has just been given the chance by an investment team to convert a failing strip mall into a bustling 4-story MEGA MALL!  
To do this you will need to use your expert planning & design skills as you slowly add Stores and Elevators to your layout. Do this well enough and you will meet the investor’s expectations and claim the contract, turning your dream mall layout into a Mega Mall reality!

—description from the designer

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Rebel Alliance Starter Set
description:
Join the struggle against the Galactic Empire with the Rebel Alliance Starter Set. Lead the charge with the legendary Commander Luke Skywalker, brave Rebel Troopers, and fierce Wookiee Warriors. Take the fight to the Empire to restore peace and justice across the galaxy. Including everything you need to begin playing Star Wars: Legion, this box focuses on the Rebel Alliance core gameplay, featuring fast and light skirmish troops who are highly adaptable to multiple combat situations with 36 highly detailed miniatures in hard plastic.

Contents:

36 Miniatures
        Commander Luke Skywalker
        Rebel Trooper Unit X2 (11 troopers per unit)
        Rebel Commandos Unit X1 (7 troopers per unit)
        Wookie Warrior Unit X1 (6 troopers per unit)
    36 27mm Bases
    7 Unit Cards
    4 Red Attack Dice
    4 Black Attack Dice
    4 White Attack Dice
    4 Red Defense Dice
    4 White Defense Dice
    15 Upgrade Cards
    10 Command Cards
    24 Battle Cards
    Movement & Range Tools
    3 Punch Sheets

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Galactic Empire Starter Set
description:
Rule the galaxy and harness the power of the dark side. Lead Darth Vader against the Rebellion with this ideal entry into the exciting miniatures action of Star WarsTM: Legion. The Galactic Empire Starter Set contains a 600-point Recon-level army and all the dice, templates, tools, and tokens a player needs to begin playing their first games of Star Wars: Legion. Join the ranks of the Galactic Empire and crush all who resist with the Galactic Empire Starter Set and embark on your Star Wars: Legion adventure in a galaxy far, far away!

Contains: 37 Miniatures, 37 27mm Bases, 7 Unit Cards, 4 Red Attack Dice, 4 Black Attack Dice, 4 White Attack Dice, 4 Red Defense Dice, 4 White Defense Dice, 15 Upgrade Cards, 10 Command Cards, 24 Battle Cards, Movement & Range Tools, 3 token sheets

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Galactic Republic Starter Set
description:
The Galactic Republic maintains order across a vast collection of star systems ranging from the Core Worlds to the Outer Rim Territories. These systems enjoyed many millennia of peace and prosperity while under the governance of the Galactic Senate. However, a schism between the systems grew which led to the creation of the Separatist Alliance. Now, the Galactic Republic must deploy its clone army in order to quell the Separatist uprising and return order to the Republic.

Contains: 36 Miniatures, 36 27mm Bases, 5 Unit Cards, 4 Red Attack Dice, 4 Black Attack Dice, 4 White Attack Dice, 4 Red Defense Dice, 4 White Defense Dice, 15 Upgrade Cards, 10 Command Cards, 24 Battle Cards, Movement & Range Tools, 3 Inserts, 3 Punch Sheets.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Separatist Alliance Starter Set
description:
The Separatist Alliance was formed by outlying star systems that aligned themselves against what they believed to be a corrupt Galactic Republic. Exploiting their sizable workforce, the Separatist Alliance has created a massive droid army that it frequently deploys to push back against the Galactic Republic’s control. While these battle droids are not the most proficient fighters, the vast amount of them the Separatist Alliance is able to put onto the battlefield has made them a considerable threat to the Republic.

Contains: 39 Miniatures, 37 27mm Bases, 2 50mm slotted bases, 5 Unit Cards, 4 Red Attack Dice, 4 Black Attack Dice, 4 White Attack Dice, 4 Red Defense Dice, 4 White Defense Dice, 12 Upgrade Cards, 10 Command Cards, 24 Battle Cards, Movement & Range Tools, 1 Insert, 3 Punch Sheets

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Customizable Rebel Officer and Agent
description:
The Empire’s reach has driven people across the galaxy to pick up arms and fight back against Imperial oppression. The commanders and operatives of the Rebel Alliance rely on the hope and determination of themselves and the brave troops underneath their command to accomplish their objectives. With a shared cause, hard-earned experience, and a good blaster at the ready, the leaders of the Rebel Alliance are ready for any mission.

The Rebel Alliance Officer & Agent pack allows players to build two customized Rebel Alliance characters that can be used as either a Commander or Operative when building their Rebel Alliance armies.

This pack includes over 20 optional parts players can choose from when assembling their miniatures, allowing for a huge range of personal customization for each character. Players also customize their Rebel Officer and Agent’s in-game abilities using the 9 unique Upgrade cards included in this expansion. Each of these unique upgrades dramatically changes the battlefield role, abilities, and synergies the character brings to the army.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Customizable Imperial Officer and Agent
description:
The Empire’s vast armies are commanded and led by its uncompromising officers. Ruthless and cunning, each officer’s ambition fuels their desire to prove themselves by rooting out and eliminating any threat to Imperial rule. Imperial Officers bolster their forces and ensure a proper chain of command to an array of units, while Imperial Agents take a more active role on the front lines.

The Empire Imperial Officer & Agent pack allows players to build two customized Galactic Empire characters that can be used as either a Commander or Operative when building their Galactic Empire armies.

This pack includes over 20 optional parts players can choose from when assembling their miniatures, allowing for a huge range of personal customization for each character. Players also customize their Imperial Officer and Agent’s in-game abilities using the 10 unique Upgrade cards included in this expansion. Each of these unique upgrades dramatically changes the battlefield role, abilities, and synergies the character brings to the army.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Customizable Jedi General and Knight
description:
One of the Galactic Republic’s greatest strengths is its close alliance with the Jedi Council. While not as strong in the Force as Jedi Masters, Jedi Knight General and Jedi Knight are a far more common sight on the battlefield. However, their prevalence should not undermine their prowess with a lightsaber and their ability to bolster their allies. The Customizable Jedi General and Knight allows players to build two customized Jedi General and Knight characters that can be used as either a Commander or Operative when building their Galactic Republic armies.

This pack includes over 20 optional parts players can choose from when assembling their miniatures, allowing for a huge range of personal customization for each character. Players also customize their Jedi General and Jedi Knight in-game abilities using the 11 unique Upgrade Cards included in this expansion. Each of these unique upgrades dramatically changes the battlefield role, abilities, and synergies the character brings to the army.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Customizable Super Tactical Command Droid
description:
When exceptional efficiency and strategy are required for victory, the Separatist Alliance deploys specialized Super Tactical Droids to bolster the rudimentary battle programming of their droid army with their superior strategic leadership and combat skills.

This Star Wars: Legion Separatist Alliance Commanders and Operatives Expansion gives players two customizable Commander and Operative units to expand their Separatist Alliance army. The Separatist Alliance Super Tactical Command Droid pack allows players to build two customized Separatist Alliance characters that can be used as either a Commander or Operative when building their Separatist Alliance armies.

This pack includes over 20 optional parts players can choose from when assembling their miniatures, allowing for a huge range of personal customization for each character. Players also customize their Tactical Command Droid in-game abilities using the 13 unique Upgrade Cards included in this expansion. Each of these unique upgrades dramatically changes the battlefield role, abilities, and synergies the character brings to the army.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Upgrades Card Pack
description:
On the battlefield, the weapons and tactics of war are used by both sides. This Star Wars: Legion Command and Training Upgrade Pack adds 191 non-faction, non-unit specific upgrade cards. Generic Command, Training, Gear, Comms, Programming, Generator, Force and Grenade Cards are an essential part of building a robust and combat effective army giving players new options to employ during battle.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Battle Deck Card Pack
description:
Open war has spread across the galaxy. This card pack updates games of Star Wars: Legion, giving players new objectives to achieve and battlefield layouts to discover.

The Battle Card Pack 2025 includes 6 new Objective Cards, 6 new Map Cards, 6 new Secondary Objective Cards, and 6 new Advantage Cards.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Rebel Alliance Command Card Pack
description:
Maybe one of the prominent features of Rebel Alliance Commanders is their ability to improvise. As a loose collection of fighters spread across the galaxy, these commanders must be prepared to alter their strategy and commands when the chips don’t fall in their favor.

This card upgrade pack contains 108 updated Rebel Alliance Unit, Upgrade, and Command card rules for Star War: Legion.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Galactic Empire Command Card Pack
description:
A Galactic Empire Commander entering the battlefield can change the tide of a battle. Known for their strategic aptitude and cold resolve, Imperial Commanders are often two steps ahead, even when they’re revising their commands in the heat of battle.

This card upgrade pack contains 88 updated Galactic Empire Unit, Upgrade and Command Card rules for Star Wars: Legion.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Galactic Republic Command Card Pack
description:
From clones designed to lead to Jedi looking to return peace to the galaxy, Galactic Republic Commanders have diverse backgrounds, but they’re all adept tacticians. Even as the Separatist Alliance launches surprise attacks, these commanders are always ready to deploy new commands.

This card upgrade pack contains 51 updated Galactic Republic Unit, Upgrade, and Command Card rules for Star Wars: Legion.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Separatist Alliance Command Card Pack
description:
Separatist Alliance Commanders use the numerical superiority of their battle droid armies to employ direct strategies that rely on overwhelming their enemies. Launching wave after wave of well coordinated attacks, these commanders have shown that sheer numbers can overcome any obstacle to victory.

This card upgrade pack contains 47 updated Separatist Alliance Unit, Upgrade, and Command Card rules for Star Wars: Legion.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – General Grievous' TSMEU-6 Wheel Bike
description:
General Grievous’s rank has never kept him off the battlefield. Truly, Grievous may even be too eager to be behind the controls of any vehicle he can use to barrage through the battlefield. However, no vehicle is better suited for his ferocious tactics than the TSMEU-6 Wheel Bike. Rugged and agile, the wheel bike allows Grievous to cut a path through his foes.

This expansion contains 1 Commander unit for a Separatist Alliance army.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Commander Darth Vader and General Veers
description:
Darth Vader is the living embodiment of the Empire’s might. The mere presence of the Dark Lord of the Sith inspires terror in his foes and unquestioning submission in his allies. Wielding this fear as skillfully as his lightsaber, nothing can stand against Vader as he carries out the Emperor’s bidding.

General Veers brings ironclad Imperial discipline and a tactical cunning to the Empire’s forces. Veers’s skill at mechanized combat tactics makes him an ideal choice to lead aggressive frontal assaults on enemy prepared defenses.

This Character Expansion includes 2 highly detailed miniatures in hard plastic for 1 Operative and 2 Commander units.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Heroes of the Rebellion
description:
The Rebellion is built on the deeds of its legendary heroes, inspiring all to rise up to fight the Empire. Recruit Luke Skywalker as Commander of the Rebellion or famed Jedi Knight, General Han Solo, General Leia Organa, and Chewbacca to help defeat the Empire and bring freedom and peace back to the galaxy!

Employing clever tactics, mastery of the Force, and skilled blaster fire, these iconic heroes are ready to answer the call of the Rebel Alliance.

boardgame: Star Wars: Legion – Clone Trooper Marksmen
description:
Clone troopers are skilled with a wide variety of weapons, however, the DC-15 has become the mainstay of the clone army. Clone Marksmen use the DC-15 blaster rifle alongside heavy weapons to create a withering hail of accurate firepower. This Star WarsTM: Legion Clone Trooper Marksmen box adds 11 Clone Trooper Marksmen miniatures, enough for 1 Corps unit. This is an excellent next-step expansion for players looking to build their Galactic Republic
larger than a Recon-level army.

Contains 11 Miniatures, 11 Bases, 1 Unit Card, 4 Upgrade Cards, 1 Insert.

Adventurer's Promo pack includes 6 new potion sets for the adventurer in you!
Featuring: Mana Potions, Health Potions, Stamina potions, and also
Greater Mana Potions, Greater Health Potions and Greater Stamina Potions.

These new potions add even more variety in the game and have effects not seen in the base or deluxe game. Give them a try!!

Kickstarter Promo pack includes 6 new potion sets for the adventurer in you!
Featuring: potion and toxin cards

These new potions add even more variety in the game and have effects not seen in the base or deluxe game. Give them a try!

Contents: 
Rules insert with recommended setups for new potions
43 cards consisting of 6 New potions sets
Tuck Box

boardgame: Chemical Overload: Pax Unplugged 2024 Promo Pack
description:
Chemical Overload Pax Unplugged 2024 Promo Pack includes 4 copies of a new potion for the adventurer in you!

These new potions add even more variety in the game and have effects not seen in the base or deluxe game. Give them a try!!

Contents: 
4 copies of a new potion

Description: Create this potion with any color combination and quantity of chemicals. Gain VP equal to the amount of chemicals used.

boardgame: Chemical Overload: Pax Unplugged 2025 Promo Pack
description:
Chemical Overload Pax Unplugged 2025 Promo Pack includes 6 copies of a new potion (Potion of Fragmentation) for the adventurer in you!

These new potions add even more variety in the game and have effects not seen in the base or deluxe game. Give them a try!!

Contents: 
6 copies of a new potion

Description: This potion is worth VP equal to 30 divided by the total number of Potion of Fragmentation owned by all players, rounded down.

boardgame: Family Feud: The Big Buzzer Edition
description:
HIT THE BUZZER
Just like on the popular TV game show, now you can “buzz in” to answer! Family Feud Big Buzzer Edition brings the speed and excitement of the game to your home. Includes 2 different sounds for different rounds.

HUNDREDS OF SURVEY QUESTIONS
Figure out the most popular answers to earn the most points in the Face Off round. Score even more in the Fast-Money Round, where the lead can change in an instant!

A FAMILY GAME-NIGHT FAVORITE
Split into two teams and let the challenge begin. Family Feud Big Buzzer Edition is fast-paced, quick-thinking fun for friends and family alike!

Arena is a tactical combat game where each player controls a unique gladiator fighting in a modular arena filled with traps, objects, and special effects. The goal is to survive, gain popularity, and rise as the ultimate champion.

Each player begins with:

     a gladiator with unique strengths and weaknesses,
     a personal deck of 8 actions cards,
     10 popularity points to spend before the match (items, training, blessings, traps…).


What does a player do on their turn?

A turn follows this structure:


     Initiative – Players secretly choose a card; highest initiative acts first.
     Draw back to 4 cards – Players refill their hand.
     Player actions – On their turn, a player may:
         Move (using one or more cards),
         Attack (damage, range, push, stun, debuff, etc.),
         Buy new cards by sacrificing cards from their hand (Eventus, Dolus, Items, Training).
    


Players can also defend at any time by playing cards, even during opponents’'turns.

Arena and Traps

The arena is made of tiles where players can place effects (rats, lightning, mud, healing, secret passages…).
Any gladiator stepping on the tile triggers the effect.

End of the game

A match ends when:

     only one gladiator remains alive, or
     a scenario objective is completed.


How to win

Players win by:

     being the last gladiator standing,
     completing the scenario objective,
     or, in some modes, having the highest popularity.


Arena features asymmetric gladiators, evolving decks, tactical movement, persistent experience points, and a wide variety of traps, objects, and special techniques.

—description from designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Snaky: The Pachanga of Snakes & Ladders VIP
description:
Snaky: The Pachanga of Snakes & Ladders VIP reimagines the world-famous race game through the Universo Pachanga VIP (UPV) framework, transforming a simple roll-and-move into an emotional, chaotic, and ultra-social experience.

The game preserves the familiar race toward the final space using snakes and ladders, but introduces two complementary layers of play:

LAYER 0 — The Pachanga (UPV Core Layer)

Before moving, each player must choose between:

A) PAY 1 TOKEN

Play in “Classic Mode”: no twists, no surprises — just roll and move.

B) ROLL THE BLUE DIE

This triggers UPV VIP effects, which may include:

Helpful boosts

Temporary restrictions

Social interactions

Player-to-player challenges

Or triggering the Red Die, which introduces global chaos twists affecting everyone on the board

Important:
The exact faces of the Blue Die, Red Die, and the possible effects are not listed here.
Why?
Because the UPV is a flexible framework, allowing each edition to include seasonal variants, themed dice, difficulty modes, or collectible expansions.
Publishers may adapt the effects to their own product line.

LAYER 1 — Classic Snakes & Ladders Reinvented

After resolving Layer 0, the player rolls the movement die and advances normally.
But the board now includes optional elements such as:

VIP Ladders

Chaotic Snakes

Bonus zones

Multi-path branches

Themed boards (future variants)

This keeps the original simplicity but adds emotional depth and unpredictability.

OPTIONAL: VSD Cards (Variable Scoring Deck)

Some editions may include a small deck of “VSD cards” that players draw at the start of their turn to gain or lose VIP points depending on how well they complete a challenge.

This system allows:

Alternative victory conditions

Cooperative or competitive modes

Seasonal/expansion decks

Infinite replayability

Why Snaky Works

Snaky keeps everything people love about Snakes & Ladders — simple rules, pure race, family fun — but injects:

Emotion

Chaos

Social interaction

Micro-strategy

Replayability

Infinite variant potential through the UPV framework

It becomes a game that kids can play instantly, but that adults find surprisingly exciting thanks to Layer 0.

—description from the designer

The object of the game is to win by discarding your own cards or by discarding the cards of other players. On your turn, you play one card from your hand and take one card from the next player. The player who reaches 7 points wins.

Deep inside the farm’s granary, a family of mice sneaks out each night to carry home precious cheese. But dividing the spoils is never easy—how can everyone feel it’s fair?
To settle the dispute, the CLEVER MOUSE devised a set of Distribution Cards. Each round, one player takes on the role of the Distributor, secretly choosing the cards and revealing only a hint. The other mice must try to guess the hidden numbers. Correct guesses earn them the cards—and with it, delicious cheese!
The cards also grant special actions, allowing players to snatch even more cheese.
Will you be the Cheese Overlord tonight?
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

boardgame: 我在月球送月饼 (Mooncake Messengers: A Mid-Autumn Gift for Peots)
description:
Embark on a magical Mid-Autumn journey as the legendary Jade Rabbit on the moon!​ In this delightful board game, you'll play as the adorable Jade Rabbit, hopping across the lunar landscape to collect four types of delicious mooncakes. Your mission? To deliver these treats accurately to famous poets from the Tang and Song dynasties!

The game unfolds over 4 rounds. Each round, every player can choose 2 actions​ from the following options on their turn:

1 Move Your Jade Rabbit: Move your Jade Rabbit token 1-3 spaces on the board. Upon landing, you may:
Gain Resources: Collect the mooncake resource shown on the space and store it on your player board.
Perform Special Actions: Alternatively, carry out the action indicated on the space, such as:
Exchange mooncakes for Poet Cards​ to earn Victory Points (VP).
Acquire Upgrade Cards​ to enhance your future mooncake-gathering abilities.

2 Move Chang'e: Move the shared Chang'e token to any space​ on the board (no distance limit). However, this action is not affected by Upgrade Cards, and each player has a limited number of uses​ for moving Chang'e throughout the entire game.

A round concludes once all players' Jade Rabbits have completed a full circuit and returned to the starting space. After 4 rounds, the player with the most Victory Points from their collected Poet Cards​ wins the game!

The Mid-Autumn Festival is just around the corner! Gather your family, share the joy, and let the mooncake delivery begin!

—description from the designer

2D6 Void is set in a modern day Coradine, which is the setting from 2D6 Dungeon. You play as a pilot adventurer, with a specific profession and set of skills.   You have volunteered to infiltrate the Dark Hulk and battle your way through ten levels to the engine room where you will blow up the ship that is laying waste to your planet. You will then become a Hero.  2D6 Void is a standalone game that uses the 2D6 Dungeon Engine. It is a sci-fi dungeon crawler, print and play, roll and write game.

You will need the 2D6 Void rules, two different colour six sided dice, an additional two six sided dice, a pencil and a dot grid sheet to draw the map on, and the Character Sheet (provided at the rear of the book).

If you know how to play 2D6 Dungeon then you’ll be ahead of the game when it comes to this new sci-fi version. Rooms are generated the same way with several additions and have many new features to explore. The core engine of the combat is the same but now we also have ranged combat as well.

2D6 Void is a standalone game that is loyal to 2D6 Dungeon and brings with it a whole range of new engaging thematic elements that take you into the future!

—description from the publisher

PLAAY at the Races was inspired by PLAAY's stock car racing game (Red White & Blue Racin’), but is notably different in several ways. Horses are rated for early and late abilities and for preferred position/profile. Jockeys are rated separately and make important contributions. Race “shape” is important. Unusual results can happen that change the trajectory of a race, sometimes memorably.

Create your own daily racing sheet, analyze the field and place your bets. Or, just sit back and enjoy the action. Includes set of 96 fictional horses and 32 jockeys for “daily racing,” game charts, race track boards, Unusual and Rare results charts, Betting Odds Guide, Payout Window board, rules and instructions.

—description from the publisher

In Dodeka, each player represents a mortal champion, competing in a sacred contest to ascend to Olympus. Players collect sets of god cards in front of them. The player with the most cards of a particular god unlocks that god's unique power and can use this power to influence the game in different ways. God powers can be combined to create powerful - although short-term - effects.

However, by not using the power, players may instead gain lasting favor. Favor wins you the game, but not using god powers gives your rivals more opportunities. Making the right choice of when to use powers or when to hold back to gain favor, will ultimately decide which player emerges victorious.

—description from the publisher

Clinch! is a pocket-sized dueling game for two players, inspired by the tension and rhythm of a twelve-round boxing match. Each turn, players roll three dice and must assign one die to themselves, one to their opponent, and one to their Initiative for the next round. Every die face has a different effect depending on who receives it, creating constant tactical questions about timing, risk, and advantage.

Players manage four key resources — RES, EN, Guard, and Pressing — while deciding when to absorb a hit, push the tempo, or unleash a once-per-match Special Move. Going below zero RES sends a boxer to the mat, raising the Knock Count and increasing the danger of a final KO. A match can end by knockout or, if both fighters endure all twelve rounds, by points based on round victories.

Designed to be quick to learn, fast to play, and easy to carry, Clinch! captures the feel of boxing without simulating it literally. Its depth emerges from simple decisions that grow tense and consequential, making every roll a small duel of its own.

—description from the designer

A Disney 3D board game, released in Greece during the 1980s by Assim toys company. It can be played from 2 to 4 players. Each player selects a pawn (Mickey, Goofy, Gladstone or Scrooge McDuck) and the game begins.

The player who rolls the highest number on the dice starts. Players move according to the number they roll on the dice. When a player lands on a square depicting a key, a fence, or a bridge, they follow the dotted line on the board, gets a cookie and roll the dice again. When a player lands on a square marked STOP, they lose a turn, while if they land on a square marked with a number, they must move back the number of squares indicated by that number. If α player lands on a square with an arrow pointing in the opposite direction, they must move back the number of squares indicated by the dice roll.

The winner is the player who reaches Grandma Duck's house first and has the most cookies in their possession.

Who will manage to collect the most doubloons after 3 rounds?
Who is the best pirate in the Caribbean and is not impressed by krakens and ghost ships. Who will manage to collect the most doubloons after 3 rounds? You will play with 54 regular Pirates Nightmare cards and 45 special cards to win the game.

Breakpoint is a cooperative tactical board game for 1–8 players, set in a harsh post-apocalyptic world. The game emphasizes precision, planning, and team coordination as players face a series of increasingly demanding combat waves followed by a final boss encounter.

Combat in Breakpoint is entirely deterministic: the game uses no dice and no cards. Instead, players resolve all actions through tactical positioning, ability timing, and freeform action sequencing with no fixed turn order. Once a player commits to an action, the choice is final, creating a high-stakes decision space where every move shapes the outcome of the fight.

Between waves, players earn an in-game currency by defeating enemies and spend it on upgrades from dedicated Wave Shop decks, which become stronger each round. Each character has a personal sheet with three weapon slots, two unique starting weapons, and upgradeable action point and damage tracks. Weapons can be upgraded up to Level 3, enabling new synergies and attack patterns, while a strict limit of three item slots forces meaningful build decisions.

The game is lost only if all players fall during the same wave; as long as one player survives, the team continues into the next round, with fallen characters returning at the start of the wave. Breakpoint is designed to deliver a tense, tactical cooperative experience with a strong emphasis on player agency, deliberate choices, and evolving builds within a contained roguelike structure.

—description from the designer

Just in time for Christmas, Karen is back! Liven up the holiday game nights, family gatherings, and social events with A Christmas Karen. Just like in the original Karen game, A Christmas Karen has players guessing what item is getting reviewed based on real one-star reviews pulled from the internet. Laughter and merriment abound in A Christmas Karen, a very seasonal party game of some of the internet’s worst reviews!

—description from the publisher

An asymmetrical board game for two players in which the paladin must reach the wizard's cave alive to break his spell, while the wizard must stop him with a horde of bewitched bugs before the paladin reachs his cave. A dark fantasy game based on roll and move and fighting-roll system. However, strategic choices are presented for both players, for the paladin should decide his path and reserve his armour until the end, while the wizard must place and led his creatures efficiently, attacking the paladin and blocking his way.

The illustration of the game includes all the necessary to play - board and rules, and are only needed a die, two blue counters for player one and a handful of red counters for player two - around 4-8 normally.

A board game included in Pulporama Magazine.

In the late 21st century, New York City is bursting at the seams. In an effort to create more space, developers begin construction of an island in disputed waters in Hudson Bay. While the states battle over jurisdiction, the city's powerful guilds spring into action, funding the creation of a city without regulation, entirely under their influence. Fueled by nostalgia for the Art Deco roots of the early 20th century, the city of Spyre rises from the bay as a monument to defiant liberty.

In Spyre, you will take on the role of one of the industrialists of the island city of Spyre. Each turn, you will construct a block containing houses or business that add to the city's economy. Players will often get in each other's way, or inadvertently help a rival. And always the Guilds will watch your every move to be sure that they get their money's worth and maintain power over the city.

-description from designer

boardgame: Compass: La Sconfitta della Xa Armata
description:
Compass: La Sconfitta della Xa Armata (The Defeat of 10th Army) is an Operational level simulation for two players of the battles fought out in North Africa from December 1940 to February 1941 which resulted in the rout of the Italian 10th Army and the conquest of all of Cyrenaica by the Western Desert Force under Gen. O'Connor.

Published in issue XVI of Parabellum magazine in November 2025.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description: A fast-paced, dice rolling game that brings big excitement with every turn! Test your strategy and push your luck to win against your family and friends. Whether you're chasing a small straight or the ultimate Zanga six-of-a-kind, every round is quick, fun, and full of suspense!

Flanders-1302 Solitaire is a single-player print & play historical wargame set in Medieval times where you can command either an army of Flemish Militia or the French Royal Army on the famous battlefield of Courtrai (Kortrijk). This game is the successor of the best selling Eylau-1807 solitaire (also available on Wargame Vault). There are 9 games with 3 difficulty levels for each side resulting in a total of 18 (highly replayable) games. All you need is a pen and two 6-sided dice to play.

No map or counters to mount. Just print and play. The book contains rules with examples (20 pages) and a detailed playthrough of both sides (23 pages). The battles are fought on 18 maps including 18 BOT sheets (9 Flemish and 9 French). A battle results table where your victories or defeats can be recorded can be found at the end of the book.

Also a "print friendly" version of this gamebook is included.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
One word description is that this is a "chess" game. In that sense, gameplay is familiar; we move chess pieces in order to deliver a checkmate.

The setting is that for a very long time now, all East Asians have had their own chess variant. Chinese has Xiangqi; Korean has Janggi; Japanese has Shogi. What does Vietnam have or rather what did we have prior to 2023-2025? Not much, if it's chess with the level or the ubiquity that of standard Chess.

So my friend Đào Hiểu Đông and his family created this game around Tết 2023. The motivation was something that started in his childhood. His parent had always been an avid Chinese chess player, but he found a greater passion in international/standard chess. So much so that had become a long time dream to find a chess game that can bridge generations, something that had elements of both chess games, enough cultural values to not just build connection in his own family but other families and younger generations of Vietnamese as well.

For foreigners, this is a chess variant, perhaps an exciting one that breaks a lot of tradition in standard Chess. Symmetry is one of them. In all chess games, there are for example, two rooks or two rook-like pieces, two horses or two knight-like piece. In TTCC, there is one such piece of each kind. Also note that there is only one pawn-like piece in this game.

Gameplay: The game is played on a board inspired by Chinese chess. More specifically, it is Chinese chess board with each intersection point clearly marked as either Black or Red. This is akin to a standard Chess board with black and white squares. Thủy Tinh Châu is the piece we want to protect. This piece is equivalent to the King in standard Chess and the General in Chinese chess. In terms of mythology, water represents life, and protecting TTC (Water Crystal) is the mission of the 12 Zodiac Animals. Other than Tiger, Chicken, and Monkey, all of the rest of the Zodiac has the exact same movement of some/many pieces in standard Chess or in Chinese Chess.

For the actual list of all the pieces, please consult the full rules of the game.

Some misc rules: Stalemate results in a loss.
