There is a pass-your-turn mechanic. You can pass your turn instead of playing, 3 times per game.

There is no promotion. There is in fact a resurrection mechanic in the official rulebook (even though it is rarely used).

Forget all the word games you know! A flat surface and the game deck are all you need for fun. In this game, all you need is a playing field and your skills. Knowledge alone isn't enough to win! Players must skillfully manipulate letters and defeat their opponents. If you're ready, let the challenge begin!

Words are everywhere in life. Thousands of them surround our minds like a storm. Using the right word in the right place is always crucial in life!

—description from the designer

A set of hexagonal dominos manufactured by Arjeu. The hexagons either have one, two, or three sides with pips. These hexagons are laid out with matching ends touching matching ends, in much the same way as traditional Dominoes.

This expansion adds two modules

     2 Players lets you... play with two players
     Rare Animals adds additional animals to increase the difficulty level

Eight Nights is a fast-paced Hanukkah trick-taking game where players make predictions, play their cards, and try to light the most candles on the Menorah by the end of the game. Each round adds a new twist inspired by the holiday, from simple trump effects to team moments that feel like gathering around the candles together.

Every round of Eight Nights begins by revealing one Miracle Card. Each Miracle introduces a special rule inspired by a Hanukkah tradition. Each player makes a secret bid on how many tricks they expect to win. Hit your bid and you light a candle; miss it and you earn Gelt to spend on fun one-round powers. With special rules for three players and a cooperative mode where two players team up against Ner-Bot, a playful shadowy Menorah, Eight Nights brings the warmth and spirit of the festival into a fast, family-friendly card game that everyone can enjoy.

Players can win in two ways:


    Instant Win - Light all your flames before anyone else and win immediately.
    Endgame Win - When the Menorah is fully lit, the player with the most lit flames wins the Festival of Lights.


—description from the designer

Coin UP is a dynamic bidding game that immerses players in the retro world of arcade halls across three generations of iconic machines.
Each round, players invest their coins into available arcade cabinets to gain control and maximize their profits.

At the core of the game are participatory bids: even if you don’t win a bid, you can still purchase an arcade cabinet from the remaining options — sometimes at a better price — though with fewer choices. Every coin is a calculated risk, and each machine can pay off big… or far less than expected.
At the end of each generation, players earn majority bonuses in the most lucrative categories.

Your goal is simple: finish with the most money after all three arcade generations.
With its blend of timing, bluffing, opportunism, and reading your opponents’ intentions, Coin UP delivers short, tense, and highly replayable sessions.

What to expect:

Participatory bidding

Majority and area control

Light resource management

High player interaction

A colorful and nostalgic retro theme

Coin UP captures the spirit of classic arcade gaming… but it’s your tactical skills that will make the coins truly drop!

—description from the publisher

Coin UP est un jeu d’enchères dynamique qui plonge les joueurs dans l’univers rétro des salles d’arcade à travers trois générations de machines iconiques.
À chaque manche, les joueurs misent stratégiquement leurs pièces sur les bornes d’arcade disponibles afin d’en prendre le contrôle et maximiser leurs gains.

Le cœur du jeu repose sur des enchères participatives : même si vous ne remportez pas une enchère, vous pourrez tout de même acquérir une borne parmi celles restantes — parfois même à meilleur prix — mais avec moins de choix. Chaque décision est un pari, chaque pièce un risque calculé, et chaque machine peut rapporter gros… ou beaucoup moins que prévu.
Une fois les bornes attribuées et la génération terminée, les joueurs reçoivent des bonus de majorité dans les catégories les plus lucratives.

L’objectif est simple : terminer la partie avec le plus d’argent après les trois générations d’arcades.
Entre timing, bluff, opportunisme et lecture des intentions adverses, Coin UP offre des parties courtes, tendues et hautement rejouables.

À quoi s’attendre :

Enchères participatives

Majorité et contrôle de zones

Gestion de ressources

Forte interaction entre joueurs

Univers rétro amusant et coloré

Coin UP capture l’esprit des jeux d’arcade… mais c’est votre sens tactique qui fera vraiment sonner la caisse !

—description from the publisher (French)

boardgame: Swashbuckler: Duels, Skirmishes & Brawls – From The Age Of Adventure To The Age Of Elegance 1500-1815
description:
The duellist demands satisfaction. Honour, for him, is an appetite.
The Duellists

Swashbuckler is no ordinary set of rules. 
In all respects it is different,
from the focus on individual characters to the mechanics that seek to replicate the flow, tension and deadliness of combat with swords. 
Swashbuckler allows you to experience duels, a dangerous game of sudden death dressed up as an honourable answer to supposed insult.
Swashbuckler takes you from the dirty fighting of the Elizabethan era, through the intricate world of the C17th fencing masters to the rousing chaotic running brawls between Dumas' Musketeers and the Cardinal's Guard.
No ruleset would be complete without the sabre slashing world of
Ridley Scott's The Duellists where the art of the duel is only eclipsed by the dashing uniforms of the protagonists.

Swashbuckler has four levels of rules, each increasingly more complex, each designed to reflect the style of duel from that time, building on one another to recreate a challenging game that will thrill and frustrate in equal measure.

—description from the publisher
family: Players: Games with expansions that add solo play

description:
We all know that live opponents can be slippery, devious and implacable foes across the tabletop, but Solo Swashbuckler can be a tricky adversary too; he has no notion of honour. That makes him the perfect opponent in a game of high stakes combat. A world where death can be sudden and violent, where each time you clutch a rapier in your hand you face the prospect of a grisly end.

Swashbuckler was designed for two or more players to test their wits, endurance and luck against one another. This solo system aims to be just as challenging and just as deadly.

Also contains printable cards for the Swashbuckler rules.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: The SHIELD: Wargame Rules for Large Ancient Battles
description:
From Bronze Age Chariots to Medieval Knights…

Step into the great battles as told in the history books — not just played, but understood.

These classic-style rules let you command vast armies from the dawn of warfare to the late Middle Ages, with mechanics that reflect the tactical shifts across centuries of change. From phalanx to pike, from horse archer to heavy knight, each army fights in ways true to its time.

Easy to learn, rewarding to master, the system is streamlined for fast play with minimal rules-checking — yet beneath its simplicity lies a depth that lets you explore not only history, but its might-have-beens.

—description from the publisher

Débats Nuls: Pop Culture - A party game to timeless pop culture debates.

Following the success of our first game, Débats Nuls, which focused on trivial everyday topics, we're tackling a bigger subject: Pop Culture.

At a time when pop/geek culture is omnipresent in our daily lives, we offer you the opportunity to test your skills against others on iconic topics such as:

Movies: Star Wars, The Lord of the Rings, Harry Potter, Jurassic Park
TV Series: Friends, The Big Bang Theory, Malcolm in the Middle, Stargate SG-1
Animated Series/Comics/Manga: Dragon Ball, Comics, Disney, Looney Tunes
Video Games: Mario, Tomb Raider, Street Fighter, Crash Bandicoot

And many more!

Rules:
Each round is played with three players: two debaters and one referee.

Objective: Earn points by winning the VOID DEBATE cards (+5 POINTS) and/or the SPECIAL/SPEED CARDS (UP TO 10 POINTS);

DEBATE CARDS: The referee designates two debaters and determines who will defend which point of view. The referee then declares the winner and awards 1 point for each VOID ARGUMENT used by each debater (written on the back of the card);
SPECIAL CARDS: These are 4 fun mini-games that challenge the players' imagination and creativity. The winner receives 10 points;
SPEED CARDS: These are 5 cards, each containing 10 pop culture/geek questions. The fastest of the two players receives 1 point for each correct answer.

The game ends when a player reaches or exceeds 40 points.

Description:

     For 3 or more players;
     70 illustrated playing cards;
     A sturdy clamshell box;
     Hours of lively debates with friends.
     Printed and produced in the Drôme (26) region of France.


—description from the publisher

Toonies is a line of collectible cards and assembly models, popular in many Asian countries. Instead of just regular paper cards, each Toonies card is pre-printed with “punch-out” details, allowing players to separate the pieces and assemble them into small 3D models such as characters, objects, vehicles or sports themes.

The Toonies line has many different themed sets – from cartoon characters, animals, robots, to football player model sets. The way it works is quite simple: each card contains a set of small details, players just need to break along the cut line and insert them into the marked slots. The result is a highly collectible mini model, sometimes designed as a “set”, allowing multiple cards to be put together to create larger scenes.
In 2012 a Toonies soccer version with rules and board was released.

1. How to pass the ball:

The Tuni team (T) can only attack the yellow field.

The Zero team (Z) attacks the blue field.

The moving team will have a probability point box selected.

After shaking, the team that has the ball first will roll the dice to choose the player who will serve the ball.

After serving the ball, the opposing team's player will continue to move the player who receives the pass.

Attack in the attacking direction that the team owns the player.

2. Accumulate points:

The ball falls on the "Toonies" logo icons: the team gets 1 point.

The ball is kicked into the goal: the team gets 5 points.

3. Lose points:

The ball is kicked into the "lost ball" icon: the opposing team gets points corresponding to the number of stars on this lost player!

In case a player loses a point, all teams pass the ball to that player until the end of the match.

4. Loss of turn: the team that lost the turn must pass the ball to the opposing team

The ball has passed the opposing team's colored field (Tuni team has passed the ball to the Zero team's blue field): the opposing team will receive the ball and kick it back at the position where the ball fell.

The ball falls out of bounds: the ball stays in the position where it fell at 1 of the 4 throw-in positions.

The ball falls on the "penalty" icon: the ball is placed in the middle of the field.

5. Add rights / lose rights:

The ball falls on the "penalty" icon: the ball is moved to the penalty position to kick directly into the goal.

The ball falls on the "corner" icon: the ball is moved to 1 of the 4 corners of the field.

Get ready for a Junior Ranger adventure! Travel all over the USA exploring famous National Parks and their most iconic animals. Spot growling grizzlies, wild wolves, and more… all in their native habitats. As you collect National Parks, learn unique facts about each one on the back of the card. Just make sure you don’t miss the bus or get lost along the way. Discover awe-inspiring landscapes and amazing animals with this fun game.

—description from the publisher

Marine Boy is an enchanting card collection game that brings 24 legendary characters to life for children. With 192 powerful cards, players embark on magical adventures across three mystical worlds: land, ocean, and sky. Kids can encounter fantastical creatures such as winged horses from Greek mythology, leprechauns from Irish folklore, and giant Thunderbird from Native American legends.

Each card features a unique story and vibrant 2D artwork, and many characters also appear in dynamic 3D form. On top of that, every card has a special code that can be entered in the online game “Magic Ocean” to unlock exciting rewards, enhancing the interactive experience.

Perfect for storytelling and imaginative play, Marine Boy encourages children to explore mythology, discover magical creatures, and enjoy a playful journey across legendary worlds.
Players take turns drawing cards, collecting character sets, and using special abilities to complete challenges or unlock magical items. Each card has unique effects that can help players advance in the game or interact with other players’ cards. Many cards include codes that can be entered in the online game “Magic Ocean” to earn rewards, adding a digital dimension to the physical gameplay. The game encourages strategy, imagination, and storytelling as children explore different legendary worlds.

Each card features vibrant 2D artwork, and some characters also appear in 3D form, making the game visually engaging.

boardgame: Honours of War: Scenario book 1 – Seven Years War
description:
Contains 16 scenarios (10 historical battles and 6 fictional engagements) designed for Keith Flint's Honours of War Osprey rules, but can also be used for any SYW rules.

Illustrated in color, includes maps, terrain notes, adaptable mechanics, and and orders of battle for Maxen, Bork, Wittenberg, Zinna, and more.

—description from the publisher

In each round you'll race to stack your dice and solve puzzles in this thrilling combination of speed and strategy. Test your dexterity and puzzle-solving skills as you compete to earn the most points!

How to Play:

Being fast earns tokens
    Being clever earns cards
    Being dexterous earns dice

Each of your tokens, cards, and dice are worth points. The game ends after five rounds - most points wins!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Magister Navis: Blood in the Sea
description:
Magister Navis is a naval simulation wargame set in the ancient world, recreating battles between the great galleys that dominated the Mediterranean during the Greek, Roman, Carthaginian, Persian and Phoenician eras. It is the modern evolution of Mare Nostrum, one of the very first Brazilian miniature wargames, originally released in 1993. Now reimagined with new rules, deeper tactical elements and supported by 3D-printed miniatures, Magister Navis brings the classic concept to a new generation of players.

The game places two commanders in direct confrontation, each controlling a naval fleet made up of different types of vessels. Light ships such as liburnas and biremes offer speed and maneuverability, while mid-sized triremes and quadriremes balance firepower and mobility. The massive quinquerremes, hallmark of the Roman navy, represent heavy strength and large troop capacity. Merchant ships, such as corbitas, are included for scenario and campaign missions.

Each galley has its own characteristics, including hull points, oar rows, movement class, internal capacity and armament options. Players can equip their vessels with ranged weapons like archers, ballistae, catapults, onagers and the feared Greek fire, as well as boarding troops ranging from auxiliaries to legionaries and the classiarii, Rome’s naval infantry. These options allow players to assemble entirely customized fleets using the Point Construction System, giving the game a high level of strategic freedom.

Magister Navis does not use a fixed board. Battles take place on any flat surface, usually between 60×60 cm and 90×90 cm, allowing ships to maneuver using a special ruler that determines movement, weapon range, angles and lateral drifting. The game features detailed rules for forward movement, course changes, drifting, attack speed, maximum attack speed, ram attacks, oar-breaking, grappling and boarding, faithfully and dynamically simulating ancient naval warfare.

Battles unfold in structured turns with defined phases: initiative, movement, ranged combat and boarding actions. Resolutions use six-sided dice and reference tables specific to each ship and weapon type. Damage to the oars reduces mobility, hull damage can sink ships, and eliminating boarding troops allows entire vessels to be captured, creating dramatic and historically accurate situations.

The game includes markers for drifting, grappling, damage and fire, as well as ship record sheets used to track everything that happens during the battle. The level of detail is ideal for both casual players and fans of historical simulation.

In addition to the core rules, Magister Navis provides ready-to-play scenarios, suggested fleets, quick-play formats and options for building balanced campaigns using the point system. Combined with a full line of 3D miniatures specifically sculpted for the game, including ships, terrain pieces and mythological creatures, the system delivers a complete and immersive experience for anyone interested in recreating the great naval clashes of antiquity with tactical depth and visual impact.

Magister Navis is, at its heart, a fusion of tradition and innovation. It preserves the spirit of the original Mare Nostrum while introducing refined mechanics, updated components and a universe of possibilities made possible by modern miniature gaming. If you would like, I can create a shorter version for the back cover, a cinematic version for a trailer, or an academic version for historical context.

—description from the designer

In this Roll&Write, all players simultaneously decorate their Christmas Tree by filling spaces with the number rolled with a six-sided die. Starting at the bottom, you must always use a space that is adjacent to an already filled space. If there are ever two adjacent spaces with the same number or if they add up to 7, you may completely colour in any spaces that are adjacent to both of these spaces: You create a bauble. The game ends after the round in which any player has filled the top space of their tree. Whoever has the most baubles wins!

—description from the designer

The witches are gathering ingredients to brew the most potent gumbo! Find, borrow, or even steal ingredients from other cauldrons. But hurry up - the others might just do the same to you!

On each turn you will add an ingredient card to your hand of secret ingredients. You can then Mix an ingredient from your hand into your cauldron face-up in front of you OR Consume one already in your cauldron to use it up.  Either option lets you use that ingredient’s ability.
 The game ends when someone has seven or more ingredients in total and calls “Gumbo!”  Everyone else then gets one more turn as a team to see if anyone can beat that player’s potion.

—description from designer

The new Marketplace Expansion is essentially three Add-Ons in One: the Market deck, the Reputation deck, and the Event deck.

The Market deck will give you more agency over which Goods you have available in your Tavern, making all main names and types available to all players.

The Reputation deck is a great way to have more control over which Patrons come to your Tavern by increasing your Renown.

The Events deck is a fun new way to add unique one-round effects or abilities to all players each round.

This new expansion comes with the Marketplace Rules Booklet, an 84-card Market deck, a 30-card Reputation deck, a 40-card Events deck, 12 Patron cards, and 13 Dirty Deeds.

boardgame: Käften!: Kampen om det fria ordet
description: In Käften! – the fight for free speech, you are thrown straight into a world where freedom of expression is at stake. You must limit free speech, by using your game cards and stop the opposing team from expressing themselves while trying to keep your own voice. Take the opportunity to test oppressive methods to silence a radicalized uncle or perhaps a power-mad friend.

boardgame: Clash of Steel: Global Uprising – German vs British Complete Starter Set
description:
The end of World War II had left the world in a state of flux. The world that emerged from the war was a very different place to how it existed in 1939. New power blocs had formed and old and new friends kept a wary eye on one another. New growing powers were also emerging in Asia, South America, and Africa, either opposed to the great powers or supported by them. Proxy wars proliferated, where powerful nations struggled for dominance within the smaller conflicts of other nations and independence movements.

In the decades following the war the Germans had reunified and become increasingly diplomatically and politically independent. They had also seen a revival of nationalist movements with increasing demands from these elements for the return of former German lands lost to Poland following the war.

By 1960 the fledgling North Atlantic Treaty Organisation had fallen apart, and while Britain and the United States remained close, they often disagreed on various international policies. The Americans focused on their anti-communist crusade, while the British focus on their own post war economy and forging a new alliance from the nations of their former colonial empire.

The Soviet Union sponsored communist movements all around the world, seemingly opposed by American interference at every turn. While extending the hand of friendship to independence movements around the developing world, they at the same time brutally tightened their grip on the Communist bloc countries of eastern Europe. This did not go unnoticed among the other global players.

---

Clash of Steel is from the design team at GF9 and Battlefront and features Dynamic Mission Based Game Play to ensure that every battle is unique and cinematic. It is based on an Alternative History filled with opportunities for the major nations of the world to begin battling over resources, land, or even just to settle disagreements!

Players take to the tabletop with one of four initial forces (German, British, American or Soviet) in Epic Tank-On-Tank Combat using Highly Detailed Multi-Part Plastic Miniatures, battling over objectives in an attempt to control the battlefield and defeat their enemies.

This Clash of Steel Starter Set contain everything a player needs to get started, including miniatures for German and British nations, rules, dice, tokens and much more.

—description from the publisher

62 Soleils d'été ("62 Summer Suns") is a roll & write game designed and sold in aid of Accessijeux, a French non-profit organization dedicated to making board games accessible to visually impaired players. Only 1000 copies of the games has been made available, sold online only on 28/11/2025.

The goal of the game is to earn the most starfish. Each turn, the active player rolls 3 6-sided dice (not included). Each player can use the value of one of them to choose an empty space on their sheet, another one to write down a number there, then the third one to add up to their Sun track.

The game ends after 20 turns, or if anyone reaches 62 points.

Fox One is a 1–4 player cooperative game in which players fly together as a private squadron, upgrading both their fighter jets and their squadron’s base through strategic purchases and improvements.

Players must complete each Mission's objective and earn a reward to repair their aircraft, purchase new jets, or upgrade their squadron's base.

Enemies act using a behavior system that makes them challenging and adaptive.

Players maneuver their fighters using a template-based movement system that adds realism and a sense of speed to aerial combat. They fire weapons, operate radar, or perform cockpit actions during their turns.

Each mission presents a new challenge, featuring unique objectives, varying playtime, and a reward to upgrade your squadron.

—description from the designer

The ultimate Taiwan Comfort Food card game, set in the world of Bobatopia, the place where Taiwanese street food comes to life! In Bobatopia, everyone deserves a heartful meal. Play with friends and enjoy savouring the best comfort street food with a twist!

—description from the publisher

Players take turns drawing cards depicting popular Taiwanese street foods, sides, drinks, flavors, toppings, and desserts. Each card has a different type of action or combo effect. Location cards can also be drawn for persistent effects. Players try to redeem combos from their hand to score Heart Points. The back of the cards depicts which current combos players can redeem. The more you eat, the heartier you’ll be! The first player to reach 20 Heart Points wins.

Enjoy Ransom Notes with the family by adding in the Ransom Notes-Family Expansion. The game of hilariously terrible sentences! Players use word magnets to respond to outlandish prompts like “Tell someone you’ve clogged their toilet at a party” with just their limited pool of words

This expansion contains 180 new words including some of today's slang (e.g. lowkey, sus, bruh, bussin & more) as well as 100 new family friendly prompt cards designed to make you think & give you a peek into the minds & innocence of kids.
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

description:
Ransom Notes: Couples Edition: Get ready for a brand-new, standalone game inspired by the bestselling party game, Ransom Notes! This isn't your grandma's cozy word game, it's a magnet-based battle of wits that'll have you and your partner (and your couple friends!) cracking up.

CRINGE CONFESSIONS: Ever wanted to describe your partner's high school style using only a handful of pre-selected words? Now's your chance! You’re forced to get creative with a limited pool of magnets, leading to side-splittingly terrible (and terribly accurate?) phrases.

MAGNETS MAKE THE RULES: Forget being a vocabulary virtuoso! In Ransom Notes: Couples Edition, even the most eloquent wordsmiths are at the mercy of their magnet collection. This guarantees outrageously funny outcomes, proving that sometimes less is more.

STANDALONE, NO BASE GAME REQUIRED: You get everything you need to kick off an unforgettable couples' game night right out of the box! We've packed it with 100 hilarious Prompt Cards, 660 premium Word Magnets, and 6 high-quality Submission Trays. No need for the original Ransom Notes, this one's ready to play!

PERFECT PARTY GAME FOR DUOS: Looking for the ultimate addition to your next game night with other couples? Ransom Notes: Couples Edition is your answer. It's designed for groups of couples to create outrageously funny sentences together, guaranteeing plenty of laughs, eye-rolls, and maybe even a few "I can't believe you just said that!" moments.

—description from the publisher
publishers: Evan and Josh's Very Special Games Company (Very Special Games)

boardgame: The Elder Scrolls: Betrayal of the Second Era Demo Kit
description:
22-lb board games can be intimidating, to say the least. Fear not! With this handy demo kit, you can introduce new players to the world of Tamriel with poise and elegance!

The Betrayal of the Second Era Demo Kit is a surefire way to recruit fresh adventurers and properly equip them for epic quests in Chip Theory’s tabletop adaptation of The Elder Scrolls. Specifically designed to teach the basic mechanics and gameplay systems of Betrayal of the Second Era, the Demo Kit features a unique four-day quest. Packed with exclusive items tailored to beginners, this mini-quest is built specifically for retail stores and convention crowds.

Players will encounter every key element in the game: creating a custom character, traveling across the map, fighting in a tense battle, leveling up, discovering loot, and completing a quest. All in a fraction of the time it normally takes to play a full session!

Epic adventure awaits! Make sure your next adventuring party is prepared to save Tamriel!


    Each Demo-pack includes 4 upgraded item cards (1 to be given away to each player) and set-up modification instructions to allow an optimal play experience for 4 players. General game play instructions are not included. Players or Demonstrator must already know how to play and have a copy of the BotSE base game.


For stores to run Demo Events during the month of August. Geared toward fans that were not able to attend GenCon, but want the GENCON THE ELDER SCROLLS DEMO EXPERIENCE.

As the street lights come on, everyone races down the mountain to get home before they get in trouble! Of course, not everyone is playing nice. The goal is to move from mountain card to mountain card, dodging obstacles along the way, until you make it down to bottom.  You'll either be playing cards, to immediately build up your speed, or plotting them to make sure they're ready for the key moment everyone shoots forward. But be careful, if the other racers know what you're up to, they can find ways to block you, or just nail you with a snowball right before you're about to make a move.

—description from the publisher

A Tactical JRPG, turned collectible trading card game!

Two players take on the role of a powerful Eikon, build their nation of followers, and engage in an all-out war to see whose influence will shape the world!

Win conditions: 

     Capture the opposing Eikon by dealing damage equal to our greater than its 'Fortitude' value. 
     Capture 10 force worth of opposing units
     Starve your opponent's resources by removing all cards from their deck (nation) 


Eikonic uses grid-based gameplay in conjunction with a unique "Combat Compass" that determines each unit's area of effect on the battlefield. Each unit's combat compass directs where a unit can attack enemy units, or provide support to friendly units.

Players can also employ powerful skills, tactics, and bases to increase their strategic prowess.

—description from the designer

boardgame: スカイトーテム – 拡張 : 大亀の顕現 (Skytotems: Manifestation of the Great Turtle)
description: This expansion for Sky Totems introduces a new totem - the turtle - along with its associated altar cards. Unlike the totems from the base game, the turtle totem does not cap off the altar that it's placed on, allowing players to continue placing both pedestal cubes and other totems off the back of its shell.

boardgame: Monsters and the Things That Destroy Them: Pandora's Box
description:
The ultimate edition.

Toaster-size box with Monsters: The Dead, Monsters: The Dark, & Monsters: The Deep.

Also contains Attack of the Baby Koalas (4-6p Expansion), Love & Hate Metagame Expansion, and multiplayer game variants.

Contains a slotted insert to organize all current and future sets. The ‘Limited Edition’ box also fits inside! Images from all Monsters sets featured on back for reversible display.
family: Versions & Editions: Big Box Versions of Individual Games

boardgame: Monsters and the Things That Destroy Them: The Dinos
description:
The fourth and final set of Monsters in a mini tuckbox, with Egg tokens.

Monsters is as portable and easy-to-learn as Love Letter and Coup. It features bluffing and strategic planning. Players with creativity and deductive reasoning will love it. There's multiple unique sets/cycles of the game, so once you know the rules for one set, you know the rules for all the sets. The game plays best with 2 or 3 players, but supports up to 4 players.

This fourth set adds 15 Egg tokens and the Fight mechanism, where you can send your opponents' smaller, weaker monsters to the Dead Zone during the game.

Card Game Accessories
 Each Monster grants between 1 and 12 points at the end of the game, but watch out: most Tactical cards ("things") cancel 2 specific Monsters!
 Your turn: Take a card into your hand from the pool of 4; if it's a monster, it wakes up, triggering its ability.
 If you are over your hand limit of 3, discard face-down to your personal discard pile. (Peek at your discards at any time.) 
 When the deck is empty: Everyone discards down to 2 cards and compares their scores to determine the winner.

—description from the publisher

This game is for BABIES! And by babies we mean for those willing to relive childhood's greatest tropes and hypotheticals in the creator's favorite expansion he's made. Would you save Lincoln?

This is a standalone expansion pack. It can be played as a solo game OR with our flagship game "I Would Kill Hitler".

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Three is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds three brand new armies and an optional play mode for Monoliths. Rampage across the battlefield with the Gnomish train, Command & Raise the dead with the Cabal, and spread darkness across the battlefield with fearsome creature of the night.

Includes 105 army tiles, 70+ tokens, 4 Reference Sheets and one Booklet with strategies and examples.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Blood Cabal Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: The Blood Cabal, dark mages dealing with blood magic raise an army of undead to take on the opponents of the Arena.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Three. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Aberrant Legion Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: The Aberrant Legion, strange creatures from another direction with the ability to slip across the battlefield and terrify their foes.

Includes 35 army tiles, 23 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack One. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Apex Hive Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: The Apex Hive, a faction of powerful insects where the player can choose and grow which Champion to grow for the situation.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Two. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Gnomatic Inventors Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: Gnomatic Inventors, control a massive train which tears across the battlefield, trampling, dragging and slicing past foes.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Three. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Night Terrors Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: Night Terrors, creatures of the night that gain power and abilities with Darkness Orders. Command Werewolves, Ghouls and Ghosts.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Three. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Primordial Kin Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: the Primordial Kin, creatures of the element that can wash over your enemies, hit them with lightning or pull them into a swirling whirlwind.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack One. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Quivering Mass Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: a giant, gelatinous creature that can spread out damage, consume opponents and heal, or pull them closer with slimy tendrils.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Two. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Southlands Coalition Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: The Southland Coalition, an army of non-human warriors including Orcs, Kobolds and Trolls.  Manoeuvre your army to push your opponents into the corner and finish them off with powerful Orc Berserkers.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack Three. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Monolith's Call: Stallos Enclave Faction is an unofficial, fan-made expansion for Monolith Arena which adds one new army: The Stallos Enclave, otherworldy Crystalline creatures with powerful ranged attacks that they can re-direct and split.

Includes 35 army tiles, 24 tokens, 3 Reference Sheets.

This is the same army also bundled in Monolith's Call: Arena Pack One. It is only available from print on demand.

Monolith Arena is required to play this product.
This is not an official product of Portal Games.

—description from the publisher

Margin Call: The Game is a highly interactive, satirical card game for 2 to 5 players that throws you directly into the shoes of an investment banker during an impending financial disaster. As the economy crumbles, you must strategically manage your hand of depreciating assets, pooling them into complex, high-risk financial products known as C.R.A.P. (Collateralized Risk Asset Packages). The core objective is simple: be the first player to offload your toxic assets and escape the coming market collapse by hitting a target of $12 billion in sales three times.

The game is a fast-paced blend of Hand Management and Set Collection, where success hinges on building the optimal—or most deceptively lucrative—package of assets before your opponents. However, beware of the cutthroat world of finance! Players can unleash powerful Market Manipulator and Quick Play cards, using Interrupts to sabotage a rival's deal, steal assets, or trigger unexpected market events. This high level of "Take That" interaction ensures that every turn is a tense negotiation and a race against your colleagues to survive.

Through its darkly comedic theme and direct, competitive mechanics, Margin Call: The Game captures the frenetic greed and sudden chaos of a financial crisis. It’s a game of brinkmanship where you must balance risk and reward, bluffing your way to riches while hoping the whole house of cards doesn't collapse on you first. Only the most ruthless and cunning financier will manage to cash out and walk away debt-free.

—description from the publisher

Step back in time with the Olympic Sports Game by Morton Productions, a charming tribute to the excitement of athletic competition in the 1950s. Although its exact release date is difficult to pinpoint, the game features distinctive black and white illustrations on the board that showcase pre-Fosbury Flop high jumps, evoking the spirit of an earlier era in Olympic history.

This delightful game includes five plastic athlete playing pieces, all representing male competitors, and comes complete with the original set of five wooden dice. Additionally, you'll find a blank original score sheet, allowing players to keep track of their scores and achievements as they compete in various Olympic events.

Players choose a colored token and take turns rolling dice to move through events like running, high jump, long jump, and javelin. Complete each event to score points; the player with the highest total after all events wins.

Space Angel Game is a light, family-oriented roll-and-move adventure based on the animated TV series Space Angel. Players travel across a colorful outer-space map by spinning a wheel and following the designated paths. Landing on special spaces triggers event cards that may move players forward, send them backward, or create hazards along the journey. The game captures the simple, fast-paced style of 1960s licensed TV board games, offering an easy science-fiction adventure for children and families.

Game of the Wild West is a late-19th-century family board game themed around frontier adventure and stereotypical portrayals of Native American life, common in commercial games of the era. Players follow a linear printed track across a colorful lithographed board, advancing by spinning or rolling and encountering various spaces that speed up or hinder their progress. Like many McLoughlin-style games of the period, it emphasizes vivid artwork and simple race-style mechanics rather than strategic play, offering a glimpse into the popular Western imagery of its time.

The game from the multimedial project "Onze Natuur" ("Our Nature") (feature film, TV documentary, website, Instagram stories, and boardgame)

Explore Our Nature!
This pristine nature reserve boasts six biotopes where you can discover and observe numerous native animal species. Try to explore the biotopes and reach the observation tower as quickly as possible to win the game.

But... life in Our Nature doesn't like being disturbed. Overdoing it could seriously delay your expedition!

The Game of Detective is an 1889 roll-and-move pursuit game published by R. Bliss Manufacturing Company. Players take the role of detectives tracking a fugitive across a series of illustrated locations, moving along the board by dice or spinner results. As with many Bliss productions of the period, the game emphasizes vivid chromolithograph artwork and straightforward, family-friendly race mechanics rather than deduction or strategy, offering a period-style interpretation of detective adventure.

Home from the Jungle is an early 20th-century vintage board game published by Butler Brothers, a well-known American mail-order and novelty goods company. Produced around 1910, the game reflects the period’s fascination with exotic expeditions, safari adventures, and the spirit of returning home after a long journey through the wilderness.

The game features colorful lithographed artwork typical of its era, including depictions of jungle animals, a steamship, and a cheerful explorer returning from his travels. Like many games of the time, Home from the Jungle was designed as a simple family pastime rather than a strategic hobby game. It usually consists of a single illustrated board and a set of basic movement rules—often using dice or a spinner—allowing players to race or progress along a track toward the final destination: returning safely home from the jungle.

Three attributes such as value, hue and saturation are needed to fully characterize a color. With these you can also define a systematic organization of colors that highlights their relationships: a color space. Hair color is characterized by level (here not considered), nuance and intensity, and Hair Color Map: Colorology asks exactly that you organize the six main families of nuance, with their intensities, in a map of the color space of hair coloring. You will learn not only how to arrange colors, but also how to highlight nuance relationships, including the ever-important opposing pairs, and the distances between colors, based on which you must decide how strong a color treatment must be. Hair Color Map: Colorology is both a tool and educational game (cooperative if you so desire), but also an expansion of Hair Color Map: Colorology for you to play with new color maps of your own creation.

—description from the designer

Per caratterizzare un colore servono tre attributi come valore, tonalità e saturazione. Con questi si definisce uno spazio colore: un’organizzazione sistematica dei colori che ne evidenzia i rapporti. Il colore per capelli si caratterizza con altezza di tono (qui non considerata), riflesso e intensità, e HAIR COLOR MAP - COLOROLOGY vi chiede proprio di organizzare le sei famiglie principali di riflesso, con le relative intensità, in una mappa dello spazio colore della colorazione per capelli. Imparerete così a ordinare il colore evidenziando i rapporti tra i riflessi (compresi gli importantissimi controcolori), e le distanze che separano un colore dall’altro, in base a cui decidere quanto incisiva deve essere la colorazione. HAIR COLOR MAP - COLOROLOGY è assieme uno strumento e un gioco didattico (se volete cooperativo) da usare anche come espansione di HAIR COLOR GAME - COLOROLOGY per giocarlo con nuove mappe colore da voi create.

—description from the designer (Italian)

In War of the words, the players stand at the head of their own newspaper with their own political leaning (Royalist, Catholic, Centrist, Liberal or Republican). Each round, they can place their cards (informants and agents) in one of five different areas of Paris each round. They can attempt to gather the latest NEWS (three areas), seek support at a SOIREE (one area), or obtain a commission (one area) that allows them to 'make' or 'break' a THEATER piece. The more profit they accumulate during the game, the more cards they will be able to spend to influence these areas.
To obtain the latest news, players who have stationed informants in the relevant NEWS areas may play one or more cards from their hand, depending on whether they consider the news profitable enough to pursue. For example, “Assassination of the Duke of Berry” is big news that can generate a major political impact and significant profit, whereas “Standing Ovation for the 9th Symphony” represents small news.

The value of all played cards remains hidden until every player has made their bid on that particular news item. Once revealed, the values are tallied, and the player who has invested the most receives the news item, which then yields profit. Players must be careful not to overbid, or they risk losing profit; bid too little, and they will fail to secure the news. Each published news item also influences the political climate in Paris — the more significant the news, the greater its potential impact. In addition, players must decide whether to present each item in a positive or a negative light. Naturally, portraying the assassination of the Duke of Berry in a positive manner will shift the political climate towards Royalist CENSORSHIP, while presenting “National Guard Disbanded” in a positive light will push it toward Republican REVOLUTION.

The core challenge of War of the Words lies in finding the right balance between investment and profit, anticipating your opponents’ decisions, securing influential sponsors, and shaping the political mood in Paris to align with the political stance of your newspaper.

War of the words is historically very thematic and was inspired by Balzac’s novel Illusions perdues.

—description from the designer

Just as there is no better vessel that can hold arcane magic than arcane magic itself, there is no better guardian of the vessel than the guardian itself. Esoteric beasts are the brethren within the armies of the Elemental Kings appointed to hold, transport and use spells of the Magi. Their very essence is entwined with such forbidden knowledge. They are the very spells themselves.

TKG ARENA: Esoteric Beasts a game set up for 1 player.

Components required for this game:

FULL BRETHREN DECK:    1  (60 Cards)

Winning Condition

The objective of the game is to capture all 20 Esoteric Beasts within the full Brethren of Chaos Deck. If this is not achieved when the game ends, the player loses.

Ending Condition

The game ends when there are no more cards in the ENERGY deck (Set B). The game also ends immediately when the player captures all 20 Esoteric Beasts.

Setup

a.    Separate the 20 Esoteric Beasts cards from the rest of the 40 monster cards in the Brethren of Chaos Deck. They can be identified as monsters with a spell name in bold encapsulated in brackets within their special abilities. Take note that the FLAME BUBBA, SNARE SEAL, HORN DRAKE and BUSHFANG MALE are not Esoteric Beasts as [FLAME DICER], [OCTOWEB], [SKIN RADAR] and [BUSHFANG FEMALE] referred in their special abilities are not spells but another monster. These connections will not be used in this game.
 b.    Divide the 40 remaining monster cards into 2 piles of 13 and 27 cards each faced down.

c.    Shuffle the deck of 20 Esoteric Monsters cards faced down.

d.    Add and shuffle 7 Esoteric Monster cards to the 1st pile of 13 cards to form Set A. Note that Set A is also known as the deck in the player’s hand.

e.    Add and shuffle 13 Esoteric Monster cards to the 2nd pile of 27 cards to form Set B. Note that Set B is also known as the ENERGY deck.

f.    Orientation of the cards is important in the game. Both Set A and Set B should start with the orientation of the cards facing upside down. The TKG logos seen on the back of the cards facing the player should be inverted and not upright. The letters T, K, G will be upside down.

g.    The player can begin by carefully picking up Set A (20 cards) in his/her hand without disrupting the orientation and place Set B (40 cards), the ENERGY deck within easy reach.

On the player’s turn

The player’s available actions depend on the orientation of the top card. For each top card, the player needs to run through the process of SIGHT, AWAKEN and ENCOUNTER before his/her response.

a)SIGHT

When the top card is faced down (back of card facing the player), the player must flip the card over along its longer sides, the artwork of the card revealed should be inverted and the numbers on the top of the card faded but still seen as a mirror image of those at the bottom of the card. This action is called “SIGHT”.

b)AWAKEN

When the top card is already faced up but upside down in orientation, the player must rotate the card into its upright position. This action is called “AWAKEN”. Note that if this is the first time the card becomes upright, the player does not take damage immediately.

c)ENCOUNTER

When the top card is already faced up and upright in orientation, the player takes damage equal to the value in the spark shape located in the top right hand corner of the card. This damage is resolved by discarding an equal number of faced down cards from the top of Set B, also known as the ENERGY deck. This takes place before any SUBDUE action can be taken. The discarded cards form the DISCARD deck and will not be used further in the game.

Player’s response

Player will choose one of 3 possible responses after the process of SIGHT, AWAKEN and ENCOUNTER.

1. SUBDUE
Players may SUBDUE the monster in the top card when it is faced up (it can be in an upside down or upright orientation), the player first checks if the card immediately below the top card is also faced up and shares the same colour (That means they are of the same element). If it is so, the monster card can be captured and it is placed in a separate CAPTURE pile. The card that shares the same colour as the captured monster will be inverted (if it is upright) and returned faced down to the bottom of Set B.

When the card immediately below the monster is not the same colour or it is faced down (unrevealed), the player can still capture it by moving a number of faced down and inverted cards equal to either the life OR attack value of the monster (life value in the heart and attack value in the spark shapes respectively) to the bottom of the Set A in the player’s hand from Set B. Captured monsters may or may not be Esoteric Beasts. All captured cards are faced up.

If the player cannot or do not wish to capture the monster at this point of time, he/she must choose EXPLORE as the response. If the monster is captured, the card immediately below the monster becomes the next top card and the player goes back to the process of SIGHT, AWAKEN and ENCOUNTER before responding again.

2. ESO-THRASH
Players may choose a set of 4 captured faced up Esoteric Beast Cards with one of each element (colour) to perform an ESO-THRASH, this move returns any one card in Set A to the bottom of Set B upside down and faced down. The 4 faced up Esoteric Beast Cards are then faced down and put aside, they are still counted towards the winning and ending conditions. The 2nd ECO-THRASH return 2 cards in a similar way and the 3rd ECO-THRASH, 3 cards, 4th ECO-THRASH, 4 cards and so on. After this, the player must choose EXPLORE as the response.

3. EXPLORE

After the top card is revealed, choose a value based on these 2 options:
a)    The top card value in the heart shape (life value)
b)    The top card value in the spark shape (attack value)

After a value is chosen, count exactly that many cards from the top of the deck (including the top card itself) and move them to the bottom of the deck, maintaining their current orientation and order except for one exception: the player may change the position of the top card of the deck anywhere within the stack of cards being moved. It is helpful to first single out the top card, count off the total number of cards to move with one’s thumb, insert the top card within this pile at any position, then transfer the whole stack of cards to the bottom of the deck in the player’s hand (Set A).

Scoring

When the game ends, the player scores points equal to the number of Esoteric Beasts less Non-Esoteric Beasts cards in the CAPTURE pile. Outcomes with scores with 0 or less points cannot be considered as wins.

The new Faerie Folk Expansion adds in fairies and berries to your Tavern Masters experience, with new Fairfolk in the Patron deck.

Fairfolk sometimes pay you a wooden button instead of a gold, and each Fairfolk has an ability they will perform for a wooden button. They are also known for vanishing when the bill comes due.

This new expansion comes with the Faerie Folk Rules Booklet, 21 Tavern cards, 48 Patron cards, 12 Dirty Deeds, 1 Port card, 3 Song cards, 30 wooden buttons, and 6 random Fairy d6 (used during Counting the Till with Fairfolk).

Weave is competitive game where you'll have to outsmart your opponents by manipulating the board in your favor, and creating unstoppable combos. 
On a 4x4 grid, the goal is to create  a line - either horizontal, vertical, or diagonal - of 4 tiles with matching gemstones. On your turn you can grab a gem, place a gem, or flip an empty tile. Once you have placed a gem on the matching colored tile, you get a special action that will let you modify the board and create chain moves.

VoidRoll is a medium-weight solo sci-fi strategy game about commanding a starship and managing complex systems. Designed to hit a strategic sweet spot, it evolves the accessible dice placement of Roll4Rum with the combo-heavy engine building of Fleet: The Dice Game and the interconnected depth of Hadrian’s Wall—all without the spreadsheet fatigue.

—description from the designer

Throw the dice and try creating crossword-like structures with your letters, while beating the timer. Each letter is worth a varying amount of points.

The player with the highest score wins.

In the world of crypto, someone’s made a killing … can you catch the murderer?

As a world-weary Homicide detective, you thought you’d seen everything. But when a cryptocurrency investor is found murdered, you’re pulled into a dark world of envy and deceit. Zachary Prince is dead, just weeks after conning millions from investors in an elaborate scam. There’s no shortage of potential suspects with motive, but there is a shortage of witnesses. This crime will require nothing less than the very best detective. Examine the evidence and solve the puzzles to uncover the truth, track down the killer, and bring them to justice. Show them that when it comes to a fast buck, it never pays to bank on murder.

—description from the publisher
Come up with words to fill out your bingo sheet with each letters before time runs out. Watch out for the specialty cards though – they might change everything.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Nemean Lion XL-Sized Enemy Pack
description:
Stalking its prey, legend tells of the most fearsome hunter the world has ever seen... the Nemean Lion! Virtually impervious to harm, this hulking beast stands at least twice the height of a man, able to shred armor, flesh, and bone with a sweep of its mighty claws. It is even said that the savage roar of this mythic creature is enough to shatter bones and drive men mad. But those very few that have lived through an encounter with it, long enough to recount the tale, have been shaken to the core.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to hunt down and battle the savage Nemean Lion. Included are 1 XL Nemean Lion model, the large Enemy Record Sheet for it, a set of counters, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add this terrifying mythic Enemy to your games of Shadows of Brimstone.

Models are Thermal Plastic Miniatures manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Fey Enchantress and Stone Warriors Deluxe Enemy Pack
description:
Deep in the dark heart of the forest, where the old stone ruins crumble and the raging brambles grow wild, the air crackles with an ancient magik. Travelers tell tales of the wild beasts and the mischievous sprites that live among the trees. But none are more feared than the Fey witches that dwell in the shadows; powerful mystics that control the very forces of nature. With a wicked smile and a twist of the hand, a Fey Enchantress can bend the mind and wrench the soul, ensnaring her foes with enchantments and illusion. It is even said that a powerful Enchantress can give life to the stone statues that stand amongst the ruins, or see through the eyes of her skulking raven spies.

This Deluxe Enemy Pack contains everything you need to add the magical Fey Enchantress and her animated statue Stone Warriors to your games of Shadows of Brimstone! Included are 1 Fey Enchantress model and 6 Stone Warriors models, the 2 large Enemy Record Sheets for them, a host of new cards themed around these fabled foes that dwell in the dark forests—including all of the Threat Cards needed to add them to your games, and 2 new Missions focusing on these deadly new Enemies!

The Fey Enchantress is a powerful and sinister sorceress, weaving deadly charms and casting terrifying Enchantments to twist the minds of the Heroes, and bringing about their downfall through bewitchment and trickery. Her evasive nature and Endurance ability make the Fey Enchantress a difficult foe to lock down. Though not inherently a close quarters fighter, if cornered she will swing her mystically charged staff at all Heroes that get too close. Fighting a Fey Enchantress can be a dangerous affair. Not only does she cast a Fey Enchantment spell at a Random Hero each Turn, but all Heroes must also roll on her Illusionary Charms chart at the start of every Turn, succumbing to everything from Burning Skin that makes you remove all Clothing Items for the duration of the Turn, to dueling with a Stained Glass Knight that leaves you cut and Bleeding! This Enemy Pack is part of the Shadows of Brimstone: Across Time Series, associated with the Questing Knights.

Models are manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Delion Dryads Enemy Pack
description:
In the darkest forests the ancient sprites grow restless. With twisting bramble vines and splitting bark, the Delion Dryads pull themselves from the undergrowth to terrorize travelers that pass through their ancient glades. As the trees come to life, the fury of the forest reclaims flesh, stone, and steel.

This Enemy Pack contains everything you need to battle the vicious Delion Dryads of the dark wood. Included are 6 Delion Dryad models, the large Enemy Record Sheet for them, and all of the Threat Cards needed to add these dangerous forest nymph Enemies to your games of Shadows of Brimstone. Delion Dryads are malicious forest sprites that have sway and control over nature itself. They cause the trees nearby to come to life, attacking the Heroes that stray too close to the walls, and their Bramble Vines lash out to Ensnare the Heroes, wrapping around them with vicious thorns.  This Enemy Pack is part of the Shadows of Brimstone: Across Time Series, associated with the Questing Knights.

Models are manufactured in the USA. Figures supplied unassembled and unpainted.

—description from the publisher

The Delion Dryad first appeared as a villain in A Touch of Evil.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Questing Knight Hero Pack
description:
Though dark and filled with terrors, both supernatural and those found on the fields of war, the middle ages in Britain were a time of bitter violence and gallant chivalry. None optimized this more than the valiant Knights of King Arthur’s court. In the shimmering castled city of Camelot, the Knights of the Round Table defended the realm with honor and fierce determination. Aided in their struggles against ruthless invading armies and fearsome creatures, the wizard Merlin bestowed gifts and wisdom to carry the day, while the mystical Lady of the Lake granted visions of heroic quests that these brave knights would embark upon to bring victory and honor to the kingdom.

This Hero Pack contains everything you need to play as a Questing Knight in your games of Shadows of Brimstone. There are 2 Questing Knight models included, both male and female, as well as the large Hero Character Sheets (depicting the Hero with and without Helmet on), Upgrade and Skill Tree Sheet, and all of the cards needed to take on the role of this heavily-armored medieval Hero. This Hero Pack is part of the Shadows of Brimstone: Across Time Series, and is associated with the Delion Dryads Enemy Pack and Fey Enchantress & Stone Warriors Deluxe Enemy Pack.

There are two versions of this Hero Pack (A and B). The printed material is the same, but the two Hero Miniatures included are different. Each set contains a Male Questing Knight and a Female Questing Knight, each with three options for the Head (No Helmet, Helmet 1, and Helmet 2). The miniature poses are different between Set A and Set B.

—description from the publisher

In Detetive, players have to discover who, how and where an assassination occured. Each round, the active player will try to guess the person who commited the crime, the weapon, and where they did it. If another player knows the guess is wrong, they have to provide the guesser with one of their clues to prove it. Else, the guesser wins the game!

This game is very similar to Clue, but it is designed and published by the Brazilian company Estrela. It has fewer rules when compared to Clue.

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Adventures Rivals Enemy Heroes Card Supplement
description:
There are many ‘Heroes’ in the world of Brimstone, but some are not as heroic as others. Whether just in it for the money or the fame, or perhaps even secretly corrupted by the power of the Darkness, there are those ‘Heroes’ that are only in it for themselves. These ruthless Rivals won’t hesitate to attack other groups of Heroes they encounter on their adventures. In their minds... they are the ‘Heroes’, and it’s every man for themselves.

This Supplement contains the cards and Enemy Record Sheet to add the Shadows of Brimstone: Adventures hero classes as rivals.

—decription from the publisher

boardgame: Shadows of Brimstone: Greek Champion #1 Card Supplement
description:
This exciting new card supplement expands the Greek Champion Hero with thematic new cards including a new Gift of the Gods deck for the God Apollo, new Divine Nemesis cards for Circe, Hermes, and Medusa, plus rules for Pig Heroes and new Darkness cards.

Includes 15 new cards for the game. You need the Greek Champion Hero to use these cards.

—description from the publisher

Dewrealm is a strategic card game for 2–4 players where you take on the role of a Bug Lord fighting for absolute dominance over the Meadow. Achieving Evolutionary Dominance is your main objective, requiring you to gather sets of Bug Cards and remove them from the game to acquire powerful Boss Cards.

Key Mechanics and Conflict:

The game blends Hand Management (with a limit of 5 Bug Species) with Drafting from the Revealed Pile and advanced interaction control.

Nests System: Each of the 11 unique Nests acts as your command center. Nest activation is optional and grants powerful, stackable bonuses every time a matching Bug or Boss enters your hand.

Reactions and Control: Use Reaction Cards (Dr. Hedhog, Evo Cage) on an opponent's turn to cancel their Evolution or neutralize their key Boss.

Dewrealm is not just a race. It’s a dynamic struggle for territory where every round is a calculation of risk, and an unexpected reaction from an opponent can completely turn the tide of battle.

—description from the designer

The River is a fast-paced, simple card and dice game that captures the excitement of fishing anywhere! Players draw, roll to cast and hook fish, avoid snags, and compete for the most catches in their livewell.

Promo Card (P01) made available as part of Promo Pack A during The Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Card Ability reads:

"END OF GAME: Gain 2 VPs for each different species of Egg in your reserve. Both hatched and unhatched."

Originally included in "Promo Pack A" available during The Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Pay 1 coin for either an amphora or spices

You are a bee racing to build a path from your hive to the central flower, collect nectar, and return home before your rivals. Navigate hazards, dead ends, and helpful boosts along the way. With smart strategy and a little luck you’ll win the Bee Race!

It is a tile placing game where players build their own paths to and from the flower.

Turns consist of only three steps:
Place: Place one tile to build a path
Move: Move your bee onto that tile
Play: Play one of your action cards (or rotate your tile)

SWAY is a miniature agnostic skirmish game with quick turns, streamlined rules and high stakes rolls. Making every game easy to grasp, engaging and impossible to predict.

The game features:

     Miniature-agnostic, genre-agnostic
     One d20 to rule them all. (11+ always succeeds)
     Only 4 models per player, fast games, zero brain strain
     Bag-based activation, to keep players on their toes.
     20 weird skills (become Slippery, Brainwashed, or The Legend)
     Items, accessories & NPCs
     Surprise Sway Cards, turn the tide at any moment.
     No health tracking, no XP bookkeeping
     Three mini-campaigns: Fantasy, Thriller, Sci-Fi


Use any minis. Mix genres irresponsibly.
Games are fast, chaotic and brain-safe.

—description from the designer

description:
Oops, I Prompted Again is a board game for 3 to 6 players that satirically simulates a life journey where AI serves as the player's primary tool for solving life's many challenges. The game’s setting involves navigating typical life stages, including homework meltdowns, midlife crises, and senior difficulties. The objective is to be the first player to reach or pass the finish line and successfully complete one final senior challenge to reach the retirement home.

The core gameplay uses a Roll & Move mechanic, where players advance along a life-stage board using two six-sided dice. The majority of spaces are Challenge Spaces, requiring a player to draw a Challenge Card corresponding to their current life stage (e.g., Childhood, Midlife). These cards present a task, with one example being "Create a poem to impress my crush", that demands a specific AI skill, either Explain, Generate, or Advice, and assigns a difficulty rating from 1 to 10. To attempt the task, the player must select one AI Model Card (e.g., ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini) from their hand to overcome that specific problem.

Following the AI Model selection, all players are permitted to play Action Cards. The active player may use Boost cards to enhance their chosen AI's skill rating, while opponents may deploy Interference cards to actively sabotage the attempt. The final modified skill rating is compared to the challenge difficulty to determine immediate success or failure, with corresponding effects or penalties applied. Action Cards can be acquired from special fields or as challenge rewards. This competitive loop of challenge, prompting, and sabotage continues until the first player claims the retirement home spot.

—description from the designer

Coco The Clown loves causing mischief, and it always gets Blaz, the owner of the circus, into trouble. Blaz has run out of patience and is determined to punish Coco — but Coco is hiding with the help of his friends. Help Coco and his crew so Blaz can’t find him. Look, spy, swap, and block cards to end the game with the fewest points, save Coco, and win.

—description from the publisher

In solo: You begin with seven miseries (face down cards randomly drawn from the deck of 60 cards), which represent your life. Bayonet Battalion is an all new poker deck themed around the Napoleonic wars. "Plays Like Poker, Feels Like Waterloo!"

The game brings modern game mechanics into any poker night. While traditional poker games typically held at a casino are designed to hit a target of 40-50 hands an hour, Bayonet Battalion was designed to have diversity and a host of meaningful decisions built into its dynamic play style.

-Whats Special-

     All New Joker - FIX BAYONETS
     Special Wild Cards - DRAGOON, MAGAZINE, SCOUT
     Reserve Cards - Hold onto cards across hands
     All New Winning Hands - Enjoy an extended variety of poker hands expanded for this game! (with familiar pairs, two pair trips and full houses still at the core of gameplay)
     Community cards
     Continues deck play so counting cards is possible
     Game play introduces more than 5 new information touch points.  Expanding past the roughly three in a typical Texas Holdem game!
     Heads up play with “I Cut You Choice” game mechanic


Bayonet Battalion currently has 4 custom built games built around this new deck.

Primary Game Play RENO25
RENO25's most notable feature being “Reserve Cards” that allows players to swap one card in their hand with one in their reserves right before showdown. This feature allows players to create long term strategies.

RENO25 heads up features a "I split you choice" game mechanic that creates unbalanced decks for the players to try and stack in their favor as much as possible.

—description from the publisher

In this game, you stand as mighty warlords, racing to claim two ancient Monuments or amass 12 glory points before your rival dares to surpass you.

Forge your path to victory by recruiting powerful units with resource cards, drawing new tactics, or trading troops to summon greater strength. Every choice shapes your destiny.

Prepare for war. Command your forces. Claim your legend.

—description from the publisher

Bite Club is a hidden‑role, team‑based card game where nobody knows exactly who to trust until it’s almost too late.

Each player receives a secret role card at the start of the night: Vampire, Hunter, or Ren the Devotee, and one role card is always removed so you can never be completely sure who is actually in the game.​

Over a series of quick rounds, players draw and reveal Alley cards, which will reveal Blood Vials, powerful but fickle allies like Barons or Dracula, the dangerous Van Helsing, and plenty of Dead Ends. Each player will be trying to advance their team’s win condition without revealing who they really are.​

—description from the publisher

Sharco is a fast-paced, tactical card game where players race to empty their hand by cleverly swapping cards in a shared tableau. Each turn, you must replace one of the three open cards to push the total either above or below 7—depending on the current state—while following the required color-switch rule between Crocodile (green) and Shark (blue).

Hitting the total exactly 7 grants extra turns, opening chances for powerful streaks, but failing to play forces you to take a card and refill the row. With simple rules, quick decisions, and a tense race to be the first to run out of cards, Sharco delivers a snappy mix of push-your-luck and light strategy.

—description from the publisher

Nguter, Sukoharjo, a village home to hundreds of Jamu Gendong sellers. Jamu is traditional drink from Indonesia that make from various spices for cure health problem. description:
In Extreme Race, players are rally drivers racing through a treacherous desert track, competing to be the first to visit all checkpoint locations and return to the finish line. The game captures the strategic challenges of real desert racing, where success depends on smart route planning, managing your resources, and capitalizing on opponents' mistakes.
On your turn, you'll play cards from your personal row to move across the desert board. Each space requires specific colored cards to enter, and the terrain varies from sticky sand to towering dunes. Your cards "stick together" when adjacent and the same color, forcing you to use them all at once - creating tactical puzzles as you decide which routes to take.
The desert holds both dangers and opportunities. Checkpoint tokens award victory points to those who reach them first, with diminishing rewards for later arrivals. Aid tokens scattered across the track provide bonus points, while obstacle tokens block certain paths entirely, forcing you to find alternative routes. Overtaking opponents is cheaper than normal movement, but leaves you vulnerable to being overtaken in return.
Strategic depth comes from the card market system. You can spend turns purchasing powerful special cards (costing 3-4 different colored cards) that provide extra movement points or unique abilities like taking multiple actions, swapping card positions, or removing unwanted cards from your hand. Timing these purchases and activations can make the difference between victory and defeat.
The game offers two modes: Official Route requires visiting checkpoints in strict order (White → Yellow → Green → Red → Blue), while Free Route lets players choose their own path, adding a layer of route optimization and risk management.
Once any player collects tokens from all five checkpoint colors, the finish opens for everyone. The first to cross takes the highest-value finish token, and players count their checkpoint, aid, and finish tokens to determine the winner.

Extreme Race: Card Game is a fast-paced set collection game for 2-6 players where you're competing to assemble race car parts and speed ahead on the track. Inspired by the challenges of desert rally racing, players must strategically collect matching sets of car components while sabotaging opponents with mismatched parts.
On your turn, you'll take all cards of one type from either a checkpoint (containing 3 face-up cards) or from the Bivouac - a central area where unwanted cards accumulate. When you take cards from a checkpoint, remaining cards move to the Bivouac. If you're brave enough to take 4 or more cards from the Bivouac, you'll receive 3 bonus garage cards as a reward, keeping one and adding two to the Bivouac.
The scoring system creates an interesting push-your-luck dynamic. Even-numbered sets of identical parts (2, 4, 6 cards) score positive points equal to their total value, moving you forward on the track. However, odd-numbered sets (1, 3, 5 cards) score negative points, moving you backward! This means collecting 4 tires (value 1 each) moves you 4 spaces forward, but collecting 3 tires moves you 3 spaces back.
Car parts have different values: Cockpit (5), Engine (4), Brakes (3), Shock Absorbers (2), and Tires (1). Higher-value parts offer bigger rewards when collected in pairs, but also bigger penalties when left as odd sets. This creates tough decisions - do you grab valuable engines that might remain incomplete, or play it safe with lower-value tires?
Each round ends when all checkpoints and the Bivouac are empty. The player with the fewest points keeps one card for the next round and starts play, gaining a strategic advantage. The race continues across multiple rounds until someone crosses the 100-point finish line on the circular track.

FiasCo is a creative storytelling party game for 3-9 players where everyone becomes a resourceful red panda trying to reach the legendary Island of Peace through a chaotic and dangerous landscape. Players must use their imagination and problem-solving skills to escape life-threatening situations across four different locations: City, Home, War, and Nature.
Each player receives a unique panda character card with distinctive personality traits that influence how they approach problems. The game is played in rounds overseen by a storyteller who draws situation cards presenting serious dilemmas your panda must overcome. These situations vary by location - urban dangers in the City, treacherous scenarios at Home, brutal challenges in War zones, or unpredictable life-or-death encounters in Nature.
The core gameplay revolves around creative problem-solving under constraints. Each round, players draw two condition cards, keep one, and pass the other to their left neighbor. You must then devise a solution to the current critical situation using your panda's personality traits and the condition you received. Conditions can be helpful resources or additional obstacles, adding unpredictability to your survival plans.
After hearing all solutions, the storyteller judges which response was the most creative, logical, and effectively used the given conditions. The winner advances one footprint closer to the Island of Peace. Each location features seven footprints to traverse before reaching the central island where eternal peace and safety await.
The game encourages creative thinking and improvisation. A lazy panda might solve problems by finding clever shortcuts, while an anxious panda might overthink every detail to ensure survival. Players can change locations once per game, seeking safer paths to peace. The first panda to reach the island's center wins, having proven themselves the ultimate survivor worthy of eternal tranquility.

Tandem is a cooperative game for 2 players or a competitive team game for 4 players.

Your goal is to cycle against all odds with your teammate to reach the finish line before the storm or before the opposing team.

Tandem is a card-driven game with an innovative mechanism. Your cards give your actions and the strength of the actions of your teammate. Coordination is the key to race to the end!

The game includes several scenarios.
