Word Web - The Ever-Changing Lexical Labyrinth

Step into a shifting maze of letters where every move reshapes the puzzle. In this ever-changing lexical labyrinth, strategy meets vocabulary as two or more players (ages 6+) move, link, and unlock hidden words across a dynamic web of hexagonal letter tiles.

Think fast. Move smart. Score big.

COMPONENTS:
40 lasercut wooden/magnetic hex letter tiles.
Optional magnetic receptor.

SETUP:
Scatter the tiles on your table or receptor sheet and combine into any shape you like to make the “web”. Grab a paper and pencil for scoring. Agree upon a target score, for example 200 points.

GAME PLAY:
Play in turns. A player takes a tile from the edge of the web and moves it to another position. The player finds a word using that tile from adjacent letters and calls it out. The player adds up the value of the letters and records their score.

WINNING:
The first player to reach the target score is the winner.

-description from designer

boardgame: Operation Source: The X-Craft Attack on the Battleship Tirpitz, September 1943
description:
“It seems very discreditable that the Italians should show themselves so much better at attacking ships in harbor than we do.” –Winston Churchill.

Operation Source is a solitaire game on the British X class mini-sub attacks against the German capital ships in Kåfjord, Norway on September 23, 1943. The main target of the raid was the powerful German battleship Tirpitz.  Two other German warships were also anchored not far away; the battle-cruiser Scharnhorst and pocket-battleship (heavy cruiser) Lutzow.

The active player takes on the role of the commanders of the British X class mini-submarines. You must endeavor to overcome the German defenses and night-time visibility to inflict as much damage as possible before being killed, captured or escaping, and then escaping. Can you do as well or better?

Each copy of Operation Source is composed of the following:

•    One 11 by 17 inch map
•    One Sheet of 28 double-sided, un-mounted counters
•    This set of rules

To play the game the player will also need a standard deck of playing cards, one six sided (1D6) and one ten sided (1D10) set of dice.

-description from designer
Keep the adventure going in this standalone sequel to Adventurous. Abandon your ship for new vehicles that allow you to explore overland in search of fresh marvels and wonders!

The end of the galaxy doesn’t mean the end of the mission! As a ROVE (Results-Oriented Universal Explorer) you must push your modules past their normal operating parameters as you explore the furthest reaches of space.

Astro ROVE is a standalone entry in the ROVE series. In this solo spatial puzzler, the player must complete missions by using their movement cards to align ROVE’s modules in the proper pattern. As ROVE travels further, efficient use of movement points and module abilities becomes paramount to achieve success.

Astro ROVE adds a few new spins to the ROVE formula. Firstly, each movement card provides a set number of movement points and directional limits on how modules may move. However, each card also includes an optional ability to help players align their modules, as well as a bonus icon that provides additional movement if it matches the current mission.

Beyond that, each module has its own unique ability allowing it to convert directly into one of the other module types. But beware: once changed, these modules can never be returned to their original purpose.

—description from the publisher

Help your astronautical axolotls survive the pitfalls and perils of outer space in this cooperative game for 1 to 2 players. Designed by Erica Pinto, Astronotl was a finalist in our 9 Cards Per Game Challenge and received high marks for its whimsical yet challenging nature. Keep your ship’s precious modules functional despite the hazardous conditions to complete your mission!

—description from the publisher
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges

description:
Meet Maudlin, the Witch of Papplewick, a lonely hedge-witch who is only technically misunderstood and absolutely not up to anything sinister. Unless you ask the Sheriff. Or the Bishop. Or literally anyone who’s ever seen her cats glow.

Maudlin arrives with her own followers:
• 4 Cats, who look adorable until they spook entire villages.
• 3 Spectres, who politely remind everyone that the afterlife is accepting applications.
• A bag of Cursed Talers, because normal gold is far too mundane.
• And, of course, the shifting, shimmering Moon Phase Board, which decides exactly how strange things get this round.

-description from publisher

In OM: The Journey, 2–4 travelers cross India in search of both memorable experiences and inner peace. From mistey valleys and palace cities to sacred monasteries and temples, players trace their own path across a map of India that celebrates its culture, landscapes, and spiritual heritage. The game is designed to be welcoming to families and newer players, while offering enough planning and timing decisions to keep hobby gamers engaged.

On your turn, you’ll choose how to invest your limited time and energy. Travel cards let you move by road, rail, or air between regions, collecting four types of Energy Cubes that represent wealth, action, knowledge, and devotion. These energies are then spent to gain Outer Journey cards – destinations and worldly experiences – and Inner Journey cards – sacred sites and moments of reflection. Each card advances you along separate Outer and Inner score tracks, and your character’s unique ability nudges you toward a different style of play.

A shared Event deck introduces changing conditions each round: monsoons might slow certain routes, festivals might reward specific regions, and special opportunities might favor particular characters or vehicles. Visiting OM shrine spaces earns powerful OM Tokens and advances you along the OM Turn Track, a dynamic turn-order system that rewards players who time their journeys well.

The game ends once a traveler’s combined Outer and Inner Journey scores exceed 100 points, triggering a final round. When everyone has finished the same number of turns, the player who has best balanced their outer adventure with their inner progress—reflected in score and position on the OM Turn Track—wins the game.

-description from designer

Our ELEVENTH (!) entry in the Simply Solo line from Scott Almes, The Cursed Cat puts you in the shoes of a very beleaguered witch or wizard whose feline familiar is causing problems around the lab. Try to keep everything in order while that cursed cat makes a mess!

The fantabulous, splendorous, stupendous Everything Machine is broken! Use your engineering know-how to verify the machine’s output and get it up and running again as quickly as possible!

Query rows and columns for clues to the hidden attributes. Guess quickly and accurately to earn a better score!

Each turn, the team of Engineers query a row or column of the grid and the Machine player provides a clue that fits all of the hidden attributes of the queried cards. Correctly-guessed cards are revealed while incorrect guesses are removed from the grid. Final scoring is determined by the total number of turns required as well as incorrect guesses, so move quickly and accurately to earn the best score!

—description from the publisher
family: Contests: Button Shy Game Design Contests and Challenges

description:
The Viking Age is at hand and with prosperity comes power. As an ambitious ruling jarl, chart a new future for your people through careful development and expansion to secure your eternal legacy throughout the world.

Build up your Viking civilization through resource management, balancing expansion with defense to protect your gains. Devote your efforts to long-term innovations or focus on accumulating wealth and increasing your population. Forge your own path through the Viking Age and leave your mark on the known world!

In this engine-building game, carefully marshal your resources to guide your Viking civilization to greatness. Across 4 rounds of play, you’ll use cards in hand to take a variety of actions: Trade to gain wealth, Raid for resources, Develop new technologies, and more. Balancing your current needs against your future plans is crucial to your survival. After each round, defend against encroaching civilizations who wish to sabotage your position. After the final round, determine your score to determine how the Vikings fared under your leadership.

In the farthest corners of the world, ancient relics of immense power lie waiting, lost to the sands of time. Brave harrowing dangers to recover these forgotten objects and return them to their rightful owners. Reawaken is a spiritual successor to At the Helm from Ted Heidersdorf, taking the familiar deckbuilding gameplay in all-new directions.

—description from the publisher

Get ready for turn-based mayhem this holiday season!

With Worms: The Gift Calendar Game, each December door opens to a new surprise (miniatures, dice, tokens, and more) until day 24, when your complete Worms tabletop experience is ready to play.

Bringing the world-famous Worms franchise to your tabletop, this advent calendar brings the classic gameplay to the tabletop through 24 daily reveals that build into one explosively funny board game. Dodge mines, lob grenades, and blast your rivals to be the last Worm standing. It’s the perfect gift for fans of action, humour, and last-minute victories!

You’ll also find extra Worm sculpts not included in Worms: The Board Game

– ideal for customising your Worms team.

Features:

24-day build-up: each door reveals a new piece of the game

Fast, easy-to-learn gameplay: Set up and start playing in minutes

Cross-compatible: Use all minis with Worms: The Board Game.

Perfect for gifting: Great for tabletop fans and Worms veterans alike

Includes: Full-colour fold-out play mat inside the box

Push gardening to the edge of possibility in Floriculture. Grow a wondrous plant sprouting different types of flowers. Graft strawberries, tomatoes, and more onto your creation. But be careful — the weather is unpredictable. Harvest your bounty before it all freezes.

Each turn is played by all players simultaneously. Players first lock in their action to either play cards, score, or draw cards. Then the weather die is rolled to determine the available water (used to play cards). Cards are played on a player's own plant and, on a later turn, harvested to score. Every turn has a chance to freeze all plants, discarding them without scoring. The player who scores 12 first wins.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

boardgame: The Walking Dead: The Gift Calendar Game
description:
Brace yourself for survival in the post-apocalypse this holiday season!

With The Walking Dead: The Gift Calendar Game, each December door unveils a new piece (survivor miniatures, walker miniatures, tokens, dice, and more) until your full survival experience is revealed and the game is ready to play.

In this intense adventure, you must protect your survivors from relentless walker attacks.

Each day, you’ll unlock new components as you prepare to scavenge for supplies, and make critical decisions to ensure your group’s survival!

Objective: Players must navigate a Woodbury Training Underground scenario, gathering supplies while fighting off walkers.

Modes: Supports solo play, two-player (survivors vs. walkers), and 3-4 player team survival.

Components: Includes 8 pre-assembled miniatures (3 survivors: Lori, Shane, Craig; 6 walkers), a paper mat, dice, and a rulebook.
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
In 2643, Futuroscope® became a launch site for Station Cosmos®, a habitable Martian base. 
You are the very first tourist in the history of the Red Planet, where you will be staying with an astronaut! 
Upon arrival, you realize something is amiss. 
The base is plunged into darkness, and the astronaut is nowhere to be found… 

Will you manage to survive alone? 

Discover a unique adventure in which you will have to observe, manipulate, and solve puzzles just like in a real escape room. 
These puzzles will help you progress through the investigation and the book, to find the solution in 60 minutes or less.

—description from the publisher

To celebrate the 150th anniversary of Jules Verne's novel Around the World in Eighty Days, Futuroscope® presents you with an ambitious challenge: to travel around the globe in 60 minutes using the SkyLoop teleportation ship. 
To prove your success, you'll need to gather evidence, which means solving riddles throughout your journey!

Discover a unique adventure where you'll observe, manipulate, and solve puzzles just like in a real escape room.

These riddles will help you progress through the investigation and the book, allowing you to find the solution in 60 minutes or less!

—description from the publisher

Star Carriers is a mid-weight space strategy game on a generative board, where players build and command interstellar trade routes and fleets, to become the best transport enterprise in the galaxy. Using initiative, positioning, timing, and resources to outmaneuver rivals, Star Carriers offers a highly replayable experience for both casual and advanced levels.

Key features:

     Compete through trade using initiative and smart decision-making.
     Balance risk versus reward with strategic thinking and positioning.
     Evolving star routes create shifting opportunities each game.
     Dynamic turns and intuitive play keep players continuously engaged.
     High replayability driven by generative board.
     Scalable and expandable, appealing to both entry-level and advanced players.


—description from the publisher

Spy Chess is a simultaneous-move variant without the classic chess move order.  Players are free to bluff, bait, and condition their opponents, with defensive options that keep the game firmly grounded in classical chess tactics... or does it?
—description from the designer

boardgame: Corps of Discovery: Holiday Themed Maps
description: 12 new maps that each include 3 gifts hiding somewhere NOT adjacent to rock on the map. Each one offers a different reward: a free Destiny card, Gear card, or Resource! This will help make the game a bit easier - which is perfect for the holiday time. There are 2 maps for each chapter included.

Midnight Mail is a competitive game where each participant aims to claim victory by earning more points than the rest.

On each turn, you’ll play a card from your hand and place a new tile on your personal board, along with the corresponding animal tokens.

If you manage to position two animals of the same type in a straight, unobstructed line, one of them will deliver a message to the other, granting you victory points.
You can also earn additional points by chaining multiple messages in the same turn, by creating longer delivery routes, or by having them travel across higher terrain.

—description from the publisher

Not all heroes wear capes, but those who do have a pesky tendency to ruin your sinister plans! It’s time for the supervillains to seize the city in this twist on our classic -Opolis line. In true comic book fashion, these ne’er-do-wells only care about their own goals: unlike previous -Opolis games, Villainopolis has no scoring whatsoever! Instead, you must complete each of three villains’ ultimate schemes at the end of the game to win. Who says that crime isn’t fun?

—description from the publisher

In Dino Museum, each player runs a dinosaur museum and tries to assemble the most impressive fossil collection. Over 5 rounds, players collect and display fossils to attract visitors and score points.

—description from the publisher

The game is a saga of strategy and pure chaos at the best Viking style! 2 – 4 players.
Explore, settle, trade or betray to Conquer the North!

Northern Light Expansion allows for 5-6 players + Spearmen + Extreme Weather mode, more backstabs, more battles… more mead.
Solo mode – SURVIVAL. not an easy challenge! It’s you against the hostile weather

Custom maps
There are two options for you to play:
Random map – build your own adventure! Explore!
Nordiks maps – preset map configuration that will push you to the limits!

Players have to survive the weather make alliances and fight their enemies to get as much gold as they can. Gold can be obtained through mining, trading or raiding your enemies.
Player with more gold (points) at the end of the year (all situation cards) wins the game.

—description from the publisher

Cuckoos love to hide and trick everyone! Get the animals in the right order to foil their plans!

Race to get your animals in the right order to claim the trophy. You'll need to listen carefully and act quickly to beat the competition.

Each player has 4 cards: 2 Cows and 2 Pigs in a face-down pile in front of them.

The active player for the turn names the 4 cards in any order, for example "Cow, Pig, Cow, Pig".

All players, including the active player, race to grab their cards, reorder them, place the cards down in that order, and grab the trophy card from the center. Play continues until one player wins two turns in a row.

—description from the publisher

Ants are invading the kitchen, heading straight for every snack that’s fallen on the floor. To mark their route, they use words—favoring long ones that let them move faster and reach the food before their rivals. This is going to be a word buffet!

Word Buffet is a fast-paced word game where players score victory points by connecting their nest to different food tokens using chains of words. Use your magnifying glass to reveal the letters on the board and quickly find the word that will help you reach your goal. Word Buffet is played in rounds of simultaneous turns, with everyone searching for words at the same time by linking available letters on the board.

In the first round, you must find a word whose first letter is adjacent to your nest tile. In later rounds, you can move across the board and look for a word whose first letter is adjacent either to your nest or to one of your ant tokens already on the board.

Words may be formed using orthogonally or diagonally adjacent letters, and must be constructed in order.

—description from the publisher

Challenge your vocabulary with every move!

The ultimate word-building game. Not only do you have to be quick, but you also have to try and guess what words the other players are going to use so you don't repeat them!

Besides, you can set the difficulty level of the game by adding or removing cards with “more difficult” letters, which are worth more points.

Rack your brains to be the first to come up with the best TWO WORDS.

—description from the publisher

¡Desafía tu vocabulario en cada jugada!

El juego definitivo de formar palabras.

No solo hay que ser veloz, sino intentar adivinar qué palabras van a poner las demás personas ¡para no repetirlas!

Además, podréis establecer la dificultad de la partida, añadiendo o quitando cartas de letras "más difíciles", pero que dan más puntos.

Devánate los sesos para conseguir antes que nadie las mejores DOS PALABRAS.

—description from the publisher (Spanish)

Bananas are notoriously fickle: too green one day and too brown the next! As the manager of a banana cart, you need to watch your inventory carefully to maximize your fruits’ freshness. Grab bananas and use their special powers to shuffle your line of bananas so that you can put your best fruit forward in this game from Marceline Leiman!

—description from the designer

This is a unique package of games with a twist! You know us best from the 18Escape series, and now it continues with the Master Collection? What mysteries await you now?

18Escape is a series of small escape-room style games contained within 18 cards. As you go and unravel the mysteries, enter your answers into the 18Escape website to further the story and to unlock the next riddle! Use your wits because the Master Collection is more than mystery games - perhaps some mysteries are in plain sight!

boardgame: Cats versus Dogs: Battle for the Backyard!
description:
"Cats versus Dogs: Battle for the Backyard!" Takes tabletop territorial battle to a whole new level!  Pick your favorite house pet and prepare to fight a backyard battle unlike anything else.  Employing hex-style layout and a combo of conventional dice movement and unique randomized actions, the excitement never stops.  Added to all that is a challenging trivia-based minigame battle that will test your knowledge of your favorite house pets!

This game was built around lovable thematics, perfectly illustrated by the talented Ed Jimenez.  A great deal of thought was spent on balanced gameplay, focusing on keeping players engaged even between their turns.  Gameplay structure was also focused on keeping things close, keeping the excitement level from beginning to end!  The trivia dimension of the game has shown to be a fan favorite in testing, educational and entertaining!

So all this begs the question....
Are you a Cat person?
Or a dog person?

—description from the designer

Santa Claus has received the last wishlists from families at the very last moment before Christmas Eve, so let's hurry! Santa sends his elves (players) on a race to the various workshops on the slopes of the Korvatunturi hill - in Finnish Lapland, where Santa lives - to find three missing gifts which are on face-down chits, including blanks of course. Workshops are separated by colour-coded gates which will require the right colour of key card to pass through, unless you have a magic skeleton key. The fourth gift however can only be found in the Special Workshop on the very top of Korvatunturi and the only way to get there is by flying from one of the three special reindeer workshops - so you'll need a little help from Rudolf with that! Watch out for a sudden blizzard that may cause you to miss a turn and above all make sure you didn't forget to pack your lunchbox as then you will have to immediately return to the beginning to get it - although this might even be advantageous in the end-game if you have already collected all the four gifts, since the first elf to bring them back to Santa wins!

So grab your lunchboxes, keep your eyes out for Rudolf, and may the fastest & luckiest elf win!

Animals like to be together, but only on their terms. Cooperate to build out a map of overlaying cards that earns you bonuses and connect the animals that want to be connected before your cards run out.

7 Sisters is a nod to the seven sister states of Northeast India. Each animal is a famous animal from each one of these states. Help them connect!

—description from the publisher

The "Mad Rooster Tour" begins! We find ourselves in the south-western Finland town of Laitila, known mainly for egg farming, egg farming and... Did I say egg farming already?

Here, everyone first gets a task card with a starting position and role as well as instructions on which waypoints in Laitila to visit to carry it out - for example, as a council representative you may get to take the President of Finland on a bicycle tour of some homestead museums, or as a technician for the local telecommunications company, install some broadband and fix an old lady's phone on the way. Then, let's get going by roll and move! Hitting an event space will cause you to draw an event card, which are all somehow connected to the local businesses and institutions. Hitting a numbered space will give you a special task to carry out, and this could be almost anything - you may have to sing, recite a poem, hug a fellow player, dance a funny dance, or even try speaking the Laitila dialect. Naturally, many of the tasks are somehow egg-themed. But, if you hit one of the dreaded Rooster's Foot spaces, you'll get sent on the "Mad Rooster Tour" around the game board - a useless wild goose chase so to speak, which will only end when you roll an even number on the die. First player to complete the tour given to them in the starting card wins!

Rules in the Laitila dialect, but fortunately a Finnish translation is also provided.

Oh? What actually is the 'Mad Rooster', you ask? Well, it's the heraldic animal of Laitila of course - check out the coat-of-arms!

(This game has been designed as a team effort by the pupils and teachers of the schools of Kappelimäki and Myllymäki, and the students of German language from the Varppee school. Rules in the Laitila dialect by Oili Vainio.)

Rien ne va plus is a mystery game from the Drama and Investigation collection, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of murder. The goal is to uncover the culprit or avoid getting caught if you're the murderer.

It was a festive and fun evening at the Casino. Perfectly normal. Until, in the VIP Lounge, an inert body is found lying on the floor between two rows of slot machines. It's the body of Keuveun Pouliot, a "douchebag" who thinks he can get away with anything and who regularly gambles in this den of iniquity. This unfortunate situation must be resolved discreetly, inside the Lounge, without attracting attention. It's your mission to discover the murderer, who is inevitably among you. Who is it? And why?

It's up to the players to find out in this thrilling game designed especially for 8 to 10 players aged 16 and over!

A backup singer for the famous Steeve Lemieux was found dead in her dressing room after a show at the Centre Pelle. We absolutely must find out who committed this horrific act before it makes the news and ruins Steeve's career. Few people had access to the dressing rooms. The killer must be among those with VIP access. Who is it? And why?
Le Dernier Show is a mystery game from the Drame et Enquête collection, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of murder. The goal is to discover the culprit or avoid getting caught if you are the murderer.

Bill Déry, the wealthy and controversial businessman, was found stabbed to death in the back at the airport duty-free shop. Of course, no one saw anything… However, the security camera captured the shoppers who were in the store in the minutes before and after the murder. Everyone seen on the video was summoned by the head of security while awaiting the arrival of investigators.

Meurtre à l'Aéroport is a mystery game from Drame & Enquête collection, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of committing a murder. Who is the killer? It's up to you to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

It's 4:45 p.m. You're about to leave the school where you work and spend a pleasant evening with friends, far from the tensions at work. What? The body of Mr. Raymond, the janitor, has just been discovered at the bottom of the stairs leading to the basement! Given the state of the scene, everything points to a murder that was committed just minutes ago. The deceased's body is lying in a pool of blood, and his face is covered in rather suspicious burns. Obviously, these burns weren't the cause of death. However, a head injury appears to have been fatal. This macabre discovery has caused quite a stir among the staff, including you. Mr. Raymond wasn't well-liked, but who hated him enough to kill him? Since the school is already going through a difficult period, the principal would like to unmask the murderer before the police arrive. You and seven of your colleagues are summoned to the teachers' lounge to solve this crime and discover the murderer, who is surely among you.

This game of intrigue and investigation allows players to showcase their acting talents. Who is the murderer? It's up to the players to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

The Lost Mountain Summer Camp has been operating for over thirty years. Thousands of young people have spent a week of their summer vacation there, far from their parents. In the center of the gathering place stands a sacred monument: a large gold urn into which, over the years, all the young people have placed a slip of paper bearing their name and the best memory they experienced during their stay at the camp. The urn has overlooked the summer camp since its founding and has become its symbol. Today, someone stole the urn. We don't know who or why. But what we do know is that it's gone. Yet, it was there this morning when the young people performed the traditional sun salutation at the start of the day. Afterward, the young people and the counselors went to climb the mountain. Everyone left base camp, except for a few. They become suspects in this theft case. Will you be able to find out who stole the golden urn and its indelible memories?

Vol à la montagne perdue is a mystery game from Drame & Enquête collection, teen version, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of committing a theft. The goal is to discover the culprit or, if you are the thief, to avoid getting caught.

A young man was found strangled to death in the haunted house at the La Renverse amusement park. Security cameras located at the exit of the attraction captured visitors who were at the scene of the crime in the minutes following the murder. While awaiting the arrival of investigators, the park's head of security summoned everyone filmed by the cameras, as well as the employee in charge of the attraction...

Meurtre dans la maison hantée is a mystery game from Drame & Enquête collection, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of committing a murder. Who is the killer? It's up to you to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

The editor-in-chief and publisher of the prestigious newspaper, News in the Spotlight, Mr. Robin Mortensen, is found dead in his office. Feared by many public figures for his tendency to exploit people's flaws for his own gain and his love of controversy, Mortensen had more than a few enemies… But who hated him so much as to kill him?

A game of intrigue and investigation that allows players to showcase their acting skills. Who is the murderer? It's up to the players to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

—description from the publisher

Each player operates a paddle, knocking the ball back and forth until somebody misses. All it takes is a single hiccup to miss, and every miss is a point for your opponent. Stay focused. Stay steady. The first to 10 points wins! Perfect for game nights, parties, or even on the bus, VolleyShot is a quality challenge everyone will enjoy.

—description from the publisher

It's April 25, 1921. The Fantastico Circus is traveling from town to town. It's famous throughout the country. You are the circus employees, and like every Monday morning, you've gathered for your weekly meeting. You're waiting for your boss, Frederico Rousso, who is late, as is often the case. Suddenly, Gaston Samson bursts in: he's just found the director dead in the tiger cage!

A game of intrigue and investigation that allows players to use their acting skills. Who is the murderer? It's up to the players to find out in this thrilling detective game!

While a storm rages in Quebec, a tragedy unfolds in Playa del Madeleine, on the beach of the Coconut Grand Resort. The body of Alvaro, the resort's star entertainer, was discovered on the beach. When the grim discovery was made around 3:00 a.m., the victim's body showed no apparent signs of violence. Strangely, he had a flip-flop in his mouth, and his glasses, which made him so irresistible, were missing. Only eight people were in the area at the time... The murderer must be among them! Not wanting to get into trouble with the police in this tropical country, which has a reputation for questionable prison methods, the eight suspects decided to hide the body and meet to solve the murder before anyone called the authorities...

Meurtre tout inclus is a mystery game from Drame et Enquête collection, in which each player takes on the role of a character suspected of committing a murder. Who is the killer? It's up to you to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

Summer 1928. Millionaire Joseph-Arthur Goldman is found stabbed to death in his daughter's studio aboard his luxurious yacht. The captain confines all the passengers to the dining room, saying the Coast Guard will soon be on board to arrest the killer. Since everyone is a suspect, they unanimously decide to solve the mystery and, to clear their names, unmask the murderer themselves.

A game of intrigue and investigation that allows players to showcase their acting skills. Who is the murderer? It's up to the players to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

On the morning of July 21, 2002, the lifeless body of Roger Brûlé, a retired notary and promoter of the reopening of a small hydroelectric plant, was found on the banks of the river bordering Camping Paradis. The victim appeared to have been struck on the head with a blunt object. Wanting to expedite the proceedings and assist the police in their investigation, Madeleine Gélinas (the owner) summoned all the campers who had attended the party the previous evening so they could work together to discover who committed this heinous crime.

A game of intrigue and investigation that allows players to showcase their acting skills. Who is the murderer at Camping Paradis? It's up to the players to find out in this thrilling police investigation game!

Delirium28: Incursions is a tabletop miniatures games played on a 2'x2' board with 2"x5" and 2"x2" miniatures who represent sentient hyperdimensional vessels slithering through 4d bubbles of spacetime (paper minis included!). 
Imagine dogfights between psychedelic eels and yellow submarines in cramped environments.

It is a solo campaign tarot deck builder that takes between 15 and 30 minutes for each session.

Because the miniatures are so large compared to the size of the board, it focuses on tight simulations of momentum, keeping track of your facing and your speeds in up to 2 different directions at a time, while also making use of elevated terrain (the third dimension), and a fourth dimension called “outitude.”

Incursions is played with tarot cards instead of dice, and involves managing multiple stacks of cards to power your abilities. Those stacks of cards double as your model's stats, so their strengths shift from turn to turn!

Activation Sequence

     Panic
     Maneuver
     Vdice Activation
     Debuff


The foe goes first, then the player!

—description from the publisher

Delirium28 is a four dimensional surrealist miniatures agnostic skirmish game that uses tarot cards and hand management, rather than dice, to maximize strategic and tactical play in a solo setting. 
It uses a 24"x36" play space.

Delirium28 takes place across an infinite number of dream worlds, overrun with people, beasts, machines and things between, driven by a lust for power.

These beings roam violent undulating landscapes in search of "ephemera," the stuff of which dreams are made. When they amass enough of it, they absorb it into their bodies and metamorphose into something a little stronger- A little stranger.

But many others lurk, and will fight tooth and tentacle for their share of ephemera.

This is where you come in. You lead a crew of five beings on the hunt for ephemera, ready to mutilate any who get in their way on the path to becoming like gods.

Your campaign will take place within a cluster of dream worlds of your creation. Delirium28 's rules work equally well across all types of settings and time periods, so if you have cyberpunk, wild west, and WW1 miniatures and terrain, you can mix and match those components into a swirling chaos for your crew to crawl.

You can also hand craft new minis and terrain with expressionist, surrealist, or abstract qualities that might otherwise be out of place. Your campaign might look like Juan Miro sculptures dueling atop cubist architectures!

Included are 7 scenarios, 19 foes, 44 different abilities, and 89 expressionist and cubist paper minis, plus enough tokens for all of the scenarios and game mechanics.

—description from the publisher

Delirium28: Ascendant is a solo four dimensional 4x campaign game of hallucinatory battlefields. Fight surreel battles with many interlocking systems forming a bizarre evolving puzzle to explore.

The 4th dimension is represented with blue "outitude" dice, indicating each miniatures 4th dimensional altitude.

Use tarot cards and miniatures to hold the front lines in a mutating 4d landscape with the hopes of mutilating an Enlightened Foe: freaks who are further along the path of ascending to godhood.

Level up your Ascendant hero through battle, unlock new units and technologies, and fight your way up the 10 difficulty levels!

Delirium28: Ascendant comes with 3 enemy factions, Amnesiacs, Architects, and Autophages, each with their own units, abilities, events, and Enlightened Foe.

You might like Delirium28: Ascendant if you enjoy games such as Spirit Island and Mage Knight. This is a high complexity game potentially taking 2-3 hours, though if you know what you’re doing it takes about 90 minutes.

Ascendant has many difficulty levels, and a wide range of upgrades to purchase between games.

The book includes a playthrough at the back so you can see how the game is played.

...

More Details

Manage your hand of tarot cards by playing them to pass tests for each action a unit takes.

When you play a card, it goes beneath 1 of the 6 columns of map tiles where it adds to the value of other cards your units in that column play. This rewards you for thinking ahead to play your hand as efficiently as possible.

If played well, these cards can also be returned to the bottom of your deck to activate powerful technologies you unlock between battles.

Once your hand is empty, you'll have to take a redraw action, losing tempo to the enemies and making an enemy event happen a little sooner!

The early game is about timing your movements into unexplored tiles around the cards on top of the enemy's face up deck and discard, since the discard tells you the elevation of the newly explored tile, and the top card of the deck tells you what kind of terrain is there.

As you do this, you're laying out the landscape for the fighting that begins in the midgame, ultimately setting yourself up to quickly take down the Enlightened Foe.

Be careful though, since enemies add "blight" to map tiles they enter. The more blight is on the map, the more actions the enemies get each round. Too much blight and you lose!

The Enlightened Foe shows up after 6 event cards have been discarded, and the game ends after 9 of these events.

If you manage to mutilate the Enlightened Foe, you earn 3 upgrade points and increase the difficulty level of the next match.

You also have the potential to level up your Ascendant at this stage!

If you didn't mutilate the Enlightened Foe, then you earn 2 upgrade points and may decrease the difficulty level.

Upgrade points are spent to unlock and customize new units and technologies.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Death in a Snowstorm: A Christmas Murder Mystery
description:
Detectives, we need your help to solve this bitterly cold case from 1931.

Six friends brave the darkness and snow to celebrate Christmas together at a cosy log cabin in rural Norway.

But things are not as they seem, and as the storm becomes more fierce, tensions between the group rise to the surface. Once the snowstorm clears, only five will leave the cabin alive...

How to play

Death in a Snowstorm is a murder mystery game that's perfect for budding detectives and super sleuths alike. This beautifully-designed festive adventure will whisk you back in time as you unseal 12 mini envelopes containing clues and intriguing items.

The kit can be used as an Advent Calendar (twine and mini pegs provided for easy hanging), to mark the Twelve Days of Christmas, or it can be played in one evening over a bottle of wine! The choice is yours.

Step into the story – Investigate the back-stories of the six friends and discover the ties that bind them together.

Test your wits – Solve puzzles, decipher clues, and use your powers of deduction to uncover crucial aspects of the case – including the weapon, motive, and murderer.

Travel back in time – The beautiful vintage designs will whisk you back to 1931.

Difficulty: 4.5 out of 5

Your Uncle, Dr. Herbert Hughes, never returned from his latest expedition and is now presumed dead. Once an esteemed scientist and adventurer, his career crumbled as an obsession with cryptids, such as Bigfoot and the Loch Ness monster, took hold.

Now a young man is at your door with a chest left by your Uncle before he set out. Open it and enter a world of illusion and intrigue, of adventure and exploration. Do you have what it takes to unravel the mysteries of his disappearance?

Cryptid contains one full storyline made up of two parts, with dozens of puzzles to decipher and clues to crack. Play with a group or on your own.

2-4+ hours of gameplay with two parts in one game.

Internet access is required to view the Online Companion Guide to get hints, check answers, and progress the story. You may also use the Internet to help solve several of the puzzles.

None of the puzzles require destroying materials to solve them so the game can be passed along afterwards.

—description from the publisher

June 1945, Manhattan—The city celebrates the recent surrender of Nazi Germany during WWII, but peril lurks in the shadows. A group of extreme Nazi loyalists has evaded Allied defenses and taken up residence in the heart of Manhattan, seeking revenge. With sinister plans brewing, the stakes could not be higher. Can you foil the plans of The Fifth Column?

The game contains one full storyline made up of two parts, with dozens of puzzles to decipher and clues to crack. The game is steeped with real historical documents and facts as you step into the shoes of a WWII codebreaker.

Internet access is required to view the Online Companion Guide to get hints, check answers, and progress the story. You may also use the Internet to help solve several of the puzzles.

None of the puzzles require destroying materials to solve them so the game can be passed after playing.

—description from the publisher

Down The Rabbit Hole is designed to be an approachable introduction to puzzling, while still being delightfully witty for seasoned solvers looking for a light-hearted adventure. The game includes a single part and 1.5-3+ total hours of gameplay.

Tumble into the enchanting chaos of Wonderland, where riddles, mysteries, and peculiar characters await at every turn. Journey through the Croquet Grounds, the Mushroom Grove, the Royal Court and others, solving clever puzzles to restore order to this topsy-turvy world. With each step you take, the way home draws nearer to your grasp. Will you solve the riddles of Wonderland, or will its madness trap you forever? The clock is ticking—your adventure begins now!

—description from the publisher

Answer questions from different categories by guessing the right top 5.
You have 10 alternatives for each question but watch out for minus points, if you make wrong choices.

The world has entered a new era of prosperity and innovation, ushered in by Artificial Intelligence. At the heart of it all is Synapse, the company that revolutionized AI and now dominates the global economy.

You have received an exclusive invitation to participate in The Game, a secretive event orchestrated by the advanced AI system Synapse and its brilliant, reclusive creator, Dr. Ada Voss. Many of the world's most bright and influential minds have entered. Each emerged awed but unsuccessful. Whispers speak of an unparalleled reward for the first to triumph. But what lies behind Synapse's doors? And what is the purpose of The Game? There is only one way to find out... Play.

Synapse contains one full storyline made up of two parts, with dozens of puzzles to decipher and clues to crack. Play with a group or on your own.

Internet access is needed to view the Online Companion Guide to get hints, check answers, and progress the story. You may also use the Internet to help solve several of the puzzles.

None of the puzzles require destroying materials to solve them so the game can be passed along after playing.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: ゆび感クレーンゲーム チャンピョンシップ (Finger Crane Game Championship)
description:
A simple yet thrilling challenge: can you grab exactly the number of cards instructed? But what if your eyes are closed? What if you can only use certain fingers? What if the crowd is shouting and cheering around you? Stay calm, trust your “finger sense,” and go for it!

Players are divided into Competitors and Spectators. Competitors attempt to grab the exact number of cards, while Spectators place bets on whether the attempt will succeed. If their bet is correct, they score points.

The unique twist is that Spectators can cheer, mislead, or even give false instructions to the Competitors, creating a lively and unpredictable atmosphere. In real sports like baseball, soccer, or horse racing, heckling might be frowned upon—but here, it’s part of the fun!

With its deluxe foil-stamped box and charming artwork, Finger Crane Game Championship is as delightful to look at as it is to play.

Eerie riddling fun for the Christmas season – with black stories: Scary Christmas, things get truly dark beneath the Christmas tree!
50 macabre mysteries deliver goosebumps and deliciously grim entertainment by candlelight.

Whether eerie holiday accidents or bizarre seasonal misfortunes — you’ll need sharp deduction and a vivid imagination to unravel these mysterious tales. Perfect for anyone who refuses to abandon their taste for dark humor during the festive season. The elegant box with gold-foil detailing turns the game into a real eye-catcher at any Christmas gathering.

Thanks to its compact size and simple rules, the riddles are quick to explain and ideal for cosy game nights with friends or family.


     50 macabre and quirky Christmas-themed riddles
     ideal for fans of dark humor and suspense
     premium box with elegant gold-foil accents
     perfect puzzling fun for long winter nights
     a beloved classic in festive holiday design

Imagine if dinosaurs still roamed the earth … well, let your imagination run wild and travel to Dinosaur City: Here the prehistoric giants live in houses, have a job and drive around with trucks. The city is constantly growing - there’s always lots of building and bustling to make sure every dino has a place to call home. Join the dinosaur construction crew: Drive around, collect bricks, and build your own house in Dinosaur City!
The first player to complete their house wins the game.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games to Go: Colorful Rosa
description:
Help little Rosa the Caterpillar grow longer and more colorful! In this cheerful matching game for toddlers, players take turns adding body parts to the caterpillar by matching colors at its head or tail. If you don’t have a matching piece, draw a new one and keep trying! The game ends when no more pieces can be placed or someone uses all their pieces. It’s a fun way to practice color recognition, fine motor skills, and turn-taking—perfect for early learners.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games to Go: Color-Express
description:
Choo-choo! In this colorful dice-and-placement game, players build the longest train by attaching wagons to their locomotive. Roll the color die, find a matching wagon tile, and add it to your train. If there are multiple options, choose the longest one! The game ends when all wagons are placed, and the player with the longest train wins.

A fun way for toddlers to learn colors, practice turn-taking, and develop fine motor skills.

—description from the publisher

Inside House is a storytelling and deduction party game where players explore emotional experiences inside a metaphorical “house.”. A place where everyone can open up and share stories filled with all kinds of feelings.

Each player receives a Door color card and Emotional Base cards that represent different feelings. Throughout the game, players draw “Today’s emotion” cards and weave them into short stories using Story cards, creating narratives that reflect how they might feel today. Other players listen and try to interpret the emotional cues, symbolisms, or tone present in the story.

The goal of the game is to earn the highest total score across 1–2 rounds by telling effective, expressive stories and deducing others’ emotional bases. It’s a mix of intuition, creativity, and subtle communication—balancing honesty and deception through narrative. Here you don’t have to hold back — because this is your home, and your story belongs here.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My Very First Games: Theo's Cake
description:
It’s Theo the Raven’s birthday! In this cooperative movement game, players work together to decorate a fruit cake before Theo arrives. Flip tiles to reveal fruits, find the matching slice hidden in the room, and move like Theo’s animal friends—hop like Camillo the frog, sneak like Dora the cat, crawl like Franz the hedgehog, or run like Holly the mouse. But watch out: if Theo reaches the cake first, you all lose!

A joyful mix of memory, movement, and teamwork for the youngest players.

—description from the publisher

Giraffes love fashion! In this stylish memory game, players help their giraffe collect four matching scarves with the right colors or patterns. On your turn, flip a scarf card from the grid and check if it matches one of your giraffe’s favorite designs. If it does, roll the die to see if your giraffe likes it! Place the scarf on your giraffe’s neck and turn the matching tile. The first player to decorate their giraffe with four scarves wins.

A fun mix of memory, luck, and pattern recognition for young players.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Unicorn Glitterluck: Magical Fun with Dice
description:
Whenever a rainbow appears, the magical muffin cart flies into the clouds, and Unicorn Glitterluck and her friends can’t wait for a treat! In this enchanting dice game, players roll to collect the right star combinations for delicious muffins. But beware: every failed attempt makes a cloud crystal fall from the rainbow! Can you serve all the friends before the last crystal disappears?

Includes cooperative and competitive modes for extra fun.

—description from the publisher

Presque Un Quiz ("Almost a Quiz") is a pop culture/trivia party game.

The only twist is that your goal is not to get the right answer, but *almost* the right answer, by changing only one letter or syllable to the right answer.

Here's an example. "It's (almost) a movie from the 2000's, inspired by a famous Disneyland ride. A crew of cursed buccaneers sails across islands to lift the curse; but every morning they do some gentle workout on the deck."

Yes, you guessed it. It's *Pilates* of the Caribbean.

Amfibido is a 2-player card drafting and lane battle game set in a world where rival guilds of frogs, toads, snakes, and crocodiles compete for martial glory.

Build your team, equip them with powerful gear, and unleash devastating combo moves to outplay your opponent.

The game is played in rounds. Each round you draft new cards, assemble your fighters, and field them across three dojos to prepare for battle.
After both players finish their actions, fights are resolved in all three lanes. Win at least two dojos to claim the round.

The first player to win four rounds becomes the Grand Sensei and wins the game.

—description from the publisher

What says summer better than ice cream? In this fast-paced card racing game, players help their meerkats race soapbox cars to reach the ice cream truck first! Play cards to move forward, swap cars for clever overtakes, and use arrows on the track to zoom ahead. But watch out—timing is everything! The first meerkat to reach or pass the ice cream truck wins.

Includes a variant for an extended race back to the meerkat cave for even more fun.

-description from publisher

The fairground is buzzing with excitement! At the popular Plush-o-Mat, players try to grab as many fluffy plush monsters as possible using the claw. On your turn, take 1–3 adjacent monster cards from the grid and move your scoring markers along the rows on your monster board. Collect sets to maximize points, but beware of the Monster Scare cards—they let you push an opponent’s marker back! After two rounds, add up the points shown above your markers. The player with the highest score wins this fun set-collection game.

-description from publisher

Dinner is served – straight from the dumpster! In this quirky card-and-dice game, players collect animal orders and try to fulfill them by rolling the right food symbols. Each turn, you decide how many cards to reveal before rolling your dice. Complete orders to score points, but beware: every unfulfilled order and every boot card costs you dearly! Use reroll cards and golden stars wisely to maximize your score. The first player to reach 40 points wins this flavorful mix of luck and strategy.

—description from the publisher

With the Gulyabani Plus expansion and its 10 new characters, the games’s level of fun and complexity is now even greater.

Helpful companions such as the “Tırsak”, “Uyurgezer”, and other special characters -along with action cards that add depth to the game- are also included…

Old friends who haven't seen each other for years go on vacation to a forest house, but they learn that some of their friends are now ghouls. Every night, the ghouls eat one of their friends, while in the morning, the friends lynch someone they believe is a ghoul!

There are two teams in this game: the ghouls and the friends. While the ghouls know each other, the friends have no idea about anyone's identity. If you are a ghoul, act like a friend during the day, and eat another friend with pleasure in the evening. If you are a friend, be suspicious of everyone and convince your friends that you are not a ghoul, otherwise your real friends may lynch you during the day.


    For the best gameplay experience, it is not recommended to play with 6 or 7 players.


—description from the publisher

Guilds of Valoria is a 2-player deck-building area control game that is set in the world of Valoria.

Players take turns buying new character cards from the marketplace by discarding cards from their hand to strengthen their deck to conquer 3 locations to win the game.

Each turn, players can deploy characters to any locations, and if a player controls more characters than his opponent at a location at the end of the round, he gets to place an influence token onto the location.

Once a player has 3 influence tokens at a location, he claims that location.

The player who claims 3 locations first is the winner.

boardgame: ハナコイワールド恋札 (Hanakoi World: Koifuda)
description:
Hanakoi World: Koifuda is a creative fusion of traditional Japanese hanafuda (“Koi-Koi”) mechanics with the narrative style of a romance visual novel. Players collect cards representing heroines, sub-heroines, items, and events to build relationships and trigger dramatic endings. The game balances competitive scoring with improvisational storytelling, allowing play as both a strategy game and a party-style roleplay experience.
How to Play:

     Each player is dealt 8 cards.
     On a turn, play a card from your hand; if it matches the month of a card on the table, you capture it.
     Then draw from the deck; if it matches, capture that card too.
     When you complete a “yaku” (set/role), you declare either “Koikoi” (continue for higher score) or “Finish” (end the round).
     Play continues for 3 rounds, and scores are totaled.


Victory Conditions:

     Points are earned by forming roles (yaku), such as:
     Five Lights (Perfect Harem Ending): 5 heroine cards = 10 points
     Four Lights (Harem Ending): 4 heroine cards = 8 points
     Three Lights (Love Triangle Ending): 3 heroine cards = 6 points
     Red/Blue Ribbon Sets: 3 younger or older sub-heroines = 5 points
     Item or Event Sets: 5 cards = 1+ points, scaling with extras
     Special Roles: e.g. “Yan-dere Ending” or “Hidden Heroine Ending” = 5–7 points
     After 3 rounds, the player with the highest total score wins.


Special Features:

     Three rule layers allow different play styles:
     Basic Rule: Pure competitive scoring, like classic Koi-Koi.
     Harem Rule: Adds romance-themed endings, evolution roles, and dramatic declarations.
     Party Rule: Players freely create endings using collected cards; if others agree it fits, points are awarded.
     This layered design makes the game suitable for both serious competition and lighthearted storytelling sessions.


—user summary

花札の札と役を他のものに置き換えることで物語をプレイヤーの頭に想起させること
ゲームは基本的に花札ルールをもとにした基本ルールと追加役などをつけた競技ルールと自由に役を作れるパーティールール

現在未完成ですが、取り急ぎルールブックのみ公開させていただきます。

目標、
ルールブックの作成と公開
物を作る実際の札など
異世界札や配信者札などのシリーズを作る
白紙のカードで他の人にもオリジナルハナコイワールドを作ってもらう

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Rallye Monte Carlo: The Night of the Long Knives
description:
To be a rally driver just once!
Who wouldn’t want that?

This game brings the excitement of rally racing to life.
Founded in 1911, the Rallye Monte Carlo is the oldest automobile endurance rally. The players start in London, Paris, and Stockholm and, while observing traffic regulations, make their way to the paddock.

Now the real action begins!
To master the special stages quickly and successfully, players must navigate through snow, ice, and gravel using their route notes — the so-called ‘prayer book’. Along the way, anything can happen. As in real life, even with serious mechanical troubles, lucky stage wins are still possible.

The final decision comes in the ‘Night of the Long Knives’.
Who will be the first to reach the finish from Monte Carlo and stand on the winner’s podium?

—description from the back of the box (translated)

Hack the Stack is a partnership trick-taking game where players step into the roles of offensive and defensive cybersecurity teams. Two teams Red Team (Offense) and Blue Team (Defense) battle across four rounds of tactical play, leveraging suit-based effects, timing windows, and powerful one-shot Zero-Day abilities to outmaneuver their opponents.

The deck represents a digital battlefield:

Exploits (Red) pressure the system and reward counter-play.

Defenses (Blue) push back and punish overextension.

Vulnerabilities (Green) create high-value scoring moments.

Tools (Black) destabilize tricks by reversing the usual win condition.

At its core, Hack the Stack uses familiar trick-taking foundations—follow suit, highest card wins—but every suit carries a unique rule that radically shifts scoring. Victory often comes not from simply winning tricks, but from winning the right tricks in the right way.

Each round, teams receive a Zero-Day card an unpredictable, high-impact cyber event that can double points, cancel tricks, hide weaknesses, or even swap played cards. Only one Zero-Day may appear in any trick, turning timing and coordination into crucial skills.

Designed for players who enjoy tactical teamwork, clever timing, and dynamic interaction, Hack the Stack blends classic trick-taking structure with modern asymmetric scoring and thematic effects. With shared team goals, fast rounds, and highly interactive play, every trick becomes a puzzle of risk, timing, and strategic disruption.

—description from the designer

Honey Hive is a quick dice rolling puzzle game that merges aspects of crossword puzzles with the dice rolling excitement of Yahtzee.  Players will strategically choose when to place dice to their hive and when to roll their dice modifying their dice pool.  Can you figure out the right combination of dice to complete the hive?

—description from the designer

Players take turns acting as the Mads, and set up experiments (through memorable Movie Moment cards from classic cheesy movies!) for the other players.

Players have a hand of Riff cards they can look through and choose the card they think:
Best responds to the Movie Moment.
The Mads would like the most.

Then they pass their Riff card face down to the Mads.

Players also have a set of 3 Betting tokens valued 1, 3, and 5. They choose one of these and place it face down in front of them when they submit their Riff card. The number they select is the amount of points they stand to win this turn. If you feel your Riff is strong, bet on yourself to earn higher rewards!

The Mads will shuffle and then read each submitted Riff one at a time, and eventually choose their favorite of the bunch.

Then, all non-Mads players flip over their betting tokens they picked for this experiment. Whoever submitted the card that the Mads picked earns that many points and advances their standee around the Scoreboard of Love on the game board. Each player’s chosen token is left face up in front of them and cannot be used again until they have used all three of their tokens.

There is a twist—

If the player who wins points in an experiment uses their 3 or 5 token, the Mads pick up on the BIG BRAIN ENERGY through their mind monitoring and get some data points as well, scoring 1 point from a 3, and 2 points from a 5 that won. They then move their standee on the score track.

Once that’s all done, the player with the Mads token passes it to the player on their left, each player refills their hand to 5 Riff cards, and they begin the next experiment. After all players have been the Mads, the round is over and the Round tracker is advanced.

The game is played over 3-4 rounds depending on your group size, with an Invention Exchange after the second round where you can swap out some cards in your hand for new ones. Whoever is the furthest on the score track with the most points at the end is the winner!

For the more adventurous players, we have included special Oversized Riff cards and dry erase markers to replace the Riff deck so that you can write in your own responses!

Also, the game is meant to be fully compatible with any movie. Simply leave those Movie Moment cards in the box, and your remote control becomes your Mads token! You and your friends choose a film to use for that game, and then the Mads player gets to pause the film after a good scene or worthy moment occurs on their turn. Play for a normal game length or the duration of the film!

COMBINE the two variants above! As close as you can get to a competitive version of MST3K!

—description from the designer
In Não Pode 2, your challenge is to get your team to guess the word on the card — without using any of the five forbidden words.
family: Mechanism: Give a Clue / Get a Clue

description:
Eisbrecher - Promo adds four new markers to the base game to increase the variability of the market.

To set up the game, mix the four new markers into the standard supply of markers from the base game. Proceed with the setup as described in the Eisbrecher rulebook.

Marker Effects:
2x X Markers: Nothing happens when these are revealed.

2x 5/6 + x2 Markers: When you sell dice with a value of 5 or 6, you receive double the points. If you manage to use two of these markers simultaneously, you receive quadruple the points.

boardgame: BattleTech: Brush Wars – Operation Guerrero (Vol 3, Chapter 1)
description:
Against the empire-spanning conflicts of the Fourth Succession War, Clan Invasion and Jihad, historians all too often forget important conflicts such as Operation Guerrero, relegating them to the category of “low intensity warfare.” Yet it is these very conflicts that tend to shape the internal politics and culture of each faction, often with more profound impact than the larger wars.

The Brush Wars campaign series details the actions involving every line unit in the most important “low level conflicts” of the BattleTech universe. Maps and full regiment listings convey additional details, while campaign rules provide a framework allowing players to replay the battles of each war.

—description from the designer

Ever wonder what your meeples do when the worker-placement game ends? THEY GO ON VACATION! Welcome to Meeple Vacation, the wildest waterpark worker...uh, vacation-placement game ever!

Each day starts with the funnest thing of all: rolling down the massive park slide and seeing how you land. Flopped? Standing? Headstand? Your pose decides where you can go and what you might do!

Send your family members to the Arcade for awesome prizes, bump rivals off The Plunge for points, race on the Slip & Slide, fill jungle rafts together, chill in the Lazy River, and score HUGE with perfect cannonball splashes! Along the way, you'll team up with lifeguards for extra fun, grab secret photocards for mementos, and turn every flop into opportunity over three hilarious vacation days!

Meeple Vacation: Water Park is 2–4 players, 45 minutes, ages 8+, and contains modular boards with two wildly different sides, and the most satisfying meeple slide you’ll ever own. Get ready to splash, laugh, and score like never before!

—description from the publisher

In Cyanotype, two rival photographers compete to create the finest collection of cyanotype sun prints—the oldest form of photography, developed in the 1840s. Using sunlight, special paper, and carefully arranged artifacts like feathers, flowers, and keys, you'll race to complete three matching prints before your opponent.
Each round, new resources appear: artifacts to expose, paper to print on, and time cards representing exposure duration. Players simultaneously bid cards from their hand to win these resources—but there's a twist. When you win a bid, you take the resource but discard your bid. When you lose, you discard your bid but gain your opponent's cards. This creates a constant psychological puzzle: Is my opponent trying to win this auction, or lose it? Should I bid high to secure what I need, or bid strategically to steal their valuable cards?
To create a print, you must play one Paper card, one Artifact card, and Time cards that sum exactly to the print's value (4-12). The first player to complete a set of three prints wins—either three of the same artifact type, or three prints of adjacent values.
With only 54 cards in the deck and every resource scarce, each decision matters. Should you make an easy low-value print now, or hold out for a harder high-value print? Do you race for your own collection, or deny your opponent the artifacts they desperately need? Can you turn a terrible hand into an advantage by forcing your opponent to give you better cards?
Sun Prints combines elegant hand management, simultaneous bidding, and set collection into a tight 20-minute experience. Simple enough to teach in minutes, deep enough to reward repeated play, and wrapped in authentic cyanotype artwork that honors the Victorian-era pioneers of photography.

—description from the designer

Capto Chess takes classic chess and adds a deck of cards that changes how the game is played. Time Cards let you take extra moves, Advantage Cards give you opportunities to shift the board in your favor, and Option Cards introduce surprise strategies you wouldn’t see in regular chess. This mix of chess and cards creates a new variant full of tactical choices, unexpected twists, and creative strategies. Perfect for players who love classic chess but want a fresh way to play with friends, teammates, or opponents after tournament matches, making every game feel new and exciting.

—description from the designer

The Tavern Signs Expansion is a new add-on to give your Tavern a sense of identity, with special bonuses and drawbacks to each different Tavern Sign.

This new expansion comes with the Tavern Signs Rules Card, and the new 20-card Tavern Signs deck.

The Witch Games is a fast, tense, push-your-luck game where players are trapped in a deadly contest run by witches. The game revolves around choosing between mysterious potions: one kills, one does not. Each turn forces a simple but dangerous choice drink the potion yourself, or pass it to another player and trust your instincts. The witch watches, amused, as players, second-guess, and gamble with their fate.

Gameplay is quick and interactive. Players use items that let them peek at potions, skip turns, steal tools, reverse effects, or manipulate who drinks next. Each round adds more poison into the pool, tightening the odds. A hollow (safe) potion gives you another turn; a poisoned drink costs you health. All items are public information, adding layers of timing, strategy, and mind games.

Rounds escalate as the potion mix becomes deadlier and players gain stronger items. When a player’s health reaches zero, they are eliminated. The last surviving player wins the Witch’s game. With short rounds and impactful decisions, The Witch Games is easy to learn, fast to play, and filled with dramatic moments and sudden reversals.

Designed for 2–4 players and playable in about 10–15 minutes, the game blends luck,deduction, and quick decision-making with a fairy-tale theme. It aims to capture the thrill of high-stakes guessing games while offering simple rules, tight pacing, and strong replayability.

—description from the publisher

In Elemental Creatures, players take turns playing creatures from their hand to their Frontline. Frontline creature’s Power is added together to determine each player’s total Power. The player with the most Power on their board at the end of each round gains Points equal to the difference in player Power. After scoring a round, Frontline creatures are moved to the Sideline where they can Evolve for more power or Mutate for powerful effects. The first player to score 20 Points or have the most Points after four rounds is declared the winner.

boardgame: Red Dragon Inn: Extra Life 2025 Promo Pack
description:
Extra Life promo pack for 2025. Includes:

'Bravest Brew' drink card
'Box of Kittens' prize card
'Baby Mimics' prize card

This promo pack was sent to anybody who made a $25 donation to Extra Life using SlugFest Games' link between November 8, 2025 and December 24, 2025.

Card Text:
Bravest Brew
Drink
2 Alcohol
You may discard an Action Card. If you do, this entire drink (with possible Chasers) has the effect "-2 Alcohol" instead.

Box of Kittens
Prize
When you win this Prize, add it to the Prize Bin, then flip a coin:
Heads: "So soft!" +1 fortitude
Tails: "So sharp!" -1 fortitude

Baby Mimics
Prize
When you win this Prize, add it to the Prize Bin. Each other player loses 1 Fortitude and you pay 1 Gold to the Inn.

boardgame: Dominion of the Blitzkrieg WWII: Fight Battles of the Early 2nd World War – Solo Game
description:
This is the Dominion of the Blitzkrieg Fight Battles of the Early 2nd World War Solo Game. 
This standalone set of rules is built on the Dominion of the Spear solo game system with appropriate rule changes for early to mid World War Two. 
These are very very simple rules with armies of 3-8 units and a solo battle can be fought in 10-15 minutes. 
An example play through is included.

There is a Quick Reference Sheet on the back cover. 
Use paper and pen (or model figures of any scale) as there is no measuring (grid used). 
Uses 2 six sided dice.

24 historical battles and campaigns are included ready to play, each with a brief background, army lists, deployment, and brief account of what happened historically. Together they provide a simple overview of early World War Two - the period of Blitzkrieg.

The battles are:
1. Battle of Shanghai 13th August–26th November 1937
2. Battle of Khalkhin Gol 11th May-16th September 1939
3. Battle of Mokra 1–3rd September 1939
4. Battle of the Bzura 9–19th September 1939
5. Battle of Suomussalmi 30th November 1939-8th January 1940
6. Battle of Narvik 9th April–10th June 1940
7. Battle of Sedan 10–15th May 1940
8. Battle of Gembloux Gap 14–15th May 1940
9. Battle of Arras 21st May 1940
10. Battle of Sidi Barrani 9–11th December 1940
11. Battle of Tobruk 10th April–27th November 1941
12. Battle of Thermopylae 24th April 1941
13. Battle of Crete 20th May–1st June 1941
14. Battle of Bialystok–Minsk 22nd June–9th July 1941
15. Battle of Smolensk 10th July–10th September 1941
16. Battle of Kiev 23rd August-26th September 1941 
17. Siege of Leningrad 8th September 1941-27th January 1944 
18. Battle of Moscow 2nd October 1941–7th January 1942 
19. Battle of Jitra (Malaya) 11–13th December 1941
20. Battle of Bataan (Philippines) 7th January–9th April 1942
21. Battle of Singapore 8–15th February 1942
22. Battle of Toungoo (Burma) 24–29th March 1942
23. Second Battle of Kharkov (Ukraine) 12–28th May 1942
24. Battle of Gazala 26th May-21st June 1942

This is easily read on a tablet or mobile phone and can be printed as an A5 booklet - click booklet (and fit to page in printing options depending the program used). 44 A5 pages.

—description from the publisher

Ragnarok is upon us. The World’s End is here. 
Ruin and death await us all, but in this sorrowful end Sagas may be written. As the gods battle above, we battle below. At this World’s End what will you do? Who will you be?

In the Psalms of Ragnarök you play as a warband of 5 doomed souls making a mark for themselves in battle or dying trying. To die in battle earns your place in the pages of the Norn Ladies’ tomes. So march forth, weapon in hand, ready to die for glory or ruin. Witness the death of the gods and write your Saga today.

Psalms of Ragnarök is a collaboration between the mind behind Forbidden Psalm and Slightly Reckless Games.

This book contains everything you need to play but is also fully compatible with all Forbidden Psalm games.

For those new to the bleak world of Forbidden Psalm games, here is what you need to know. 
Forbidden Psalm is a tabletop miniatures game, inspired by and compatible with MÖRK BORG. 
Forbidden Psalm is a 28mm miniatures agnostic game. You can use any miniatures you already own that fit the bill or build custom minis for this game (with random tables to help you determine how to build them). Solo Play and Coop rules included. All content is also able to be used in the MÖRK BORG ttrpg.

—description from the publisher

This supplement continues to expand on the game, introducing a wealth of new rules to add even more variety, scope and excitement to your battles in the underhive and ash wastes.

Inside you will find:


     Treaties on the Inhabitants of the Wastes: Extracts from the three greatest works on the Ash Waste Nomads.
     Ash Waste Nomads: Updated rules for creating and running Ash Waste Nomads gangs, including extensive weapon and Wargear lists, as well as the new Sha'dar Hunter Specialist and three new Exotic Beasts.
     Hired Guns, Hanger-on and Dramatis Personae: Rules for adding Nomad Harriers, Nomads Outcasts and House Agents to your gangs, as well as an Ash Waste Nomads specific Dramatis Personae.
     Expanded Rules: Additional rules for running Ash Waste Nomads, including the unique Wastelands skill set along with rules for Warrior Spirits.
     Ash Waste Nomad Scenarios: A collection of themed scenarios dedicated to the tribes of the Ash Waste Nomads.
     Nomad Trading Post: An exclusive Trading Post only usable by the Ash Waste Nomads, with a plethora of unique weapons and equipment.


This is a supplement. You will need a copy of the Necromunda: Core Rulebook to use the contents of this book.

—description from the back of the book

Junk World is a 2 player party battler game in which contestants compete for the title of strongest in the Galaxy. Familiar Faces from the entire SpeedRobo Games catalog duke it out to see who can deal the most damage.

Tune into the greatest game show in the galaxy, JUNK WORLD! This zany 2 player game sends you and other “Contestants” to the surface of a landfill planet. While there, you’ll be scrounging up wacky Weapons, makeshift Mod, and battling over 5 rounds to see who can deal the most damage! The base game box contains 12 Contestant cards, 50 Weapon cards, 34 Mod cards, and a whole array of tokens!

—description from the designer

A climbing and shedding game revolving around drafting individual contract cards with objectives for you to complete each round. Beware of players BREACHing their contract for when they do they get points based off how many others fail to complete their contract!

This game has numerous contracts designed to be a challenge for new and veteran players alike. Good and "bad" hands all have a place in this four round game.

A 12-card mini-expansion for Bolted! The Game of Creative Necromancy.

Contains 7 unique Hybrid Parts, each of which is 2 different body part types at once.

Also contains a full, 4-player set of Grease Tools, which allow players to reuse their body parts in new and interesting ways.

Grease Tools are limited-use (2 uses per player per game), which is tracked by each player using their double-sided Tool card.

The 12th included card is the rules card explaining all about the other cards!

—description from the designer

Donjara Neo Super Sentai is a character-based tile game released by Bandai as part of the long-running Donjara series. The game adapts the familiar rummy-style Donjara system and combines it with multiple generations of the Super Sentai franchise.

Players draw and discard tiles to complete specific “yaku” (sets) based on Super Sentai teams, mecha, and characters. The game includes a compact Donjara board, Super Sentai–themed tiles, scoring markers, and team boards.

As with other Donjara games, the rules are simplified compared to mahjong, making the game suitable for children and family play. The Super Sentai edition introduces exclusive combination sets inspired by famous Sentai teams, special attack combinations, and themed scoring patterns.

Kamen Rider: Original Board Game is a vintage Japanese children’s board game released in 1973 by Popy, based on the early Showa-era Kamen Rider television series. The game features the first generations of Riders and villains, presented in the colorful, pop-art style typical of Popy toys of the 1970s.

The gameplay follows a classic sugoroku-style roll-and-move format, where players advance their Rider along the track, encounter monsters, trigger battle spaces, and race toward the final showdown. Events on the board correspond to episodes and characters from the early Kamen Rider TV series.

As with many Popy tie-in games from the period, the rules are simple and accessible to children, focusing on movement, battles determined by dice, and reaching the goal space first. The game includes a board, Rider standees, cards or tokens, and a spinner or dice depending on the edition.

This title is part of the long tradition of Showa-era tokusatsu board games and is a collectible item among Kamen Rider and vintage Popy enthusiasts.

boardgame: Kamen Rider Dragon Battle Board Game
description:
Kamen Rider Dragon Battle Board Game is a children’s battle and movement game released in South Korea and based on Kamen Rider Ryuki (locally titled Kamen Rider Dragon). The game was published under license by On Media and ADK, featuring characters and Riders from the television series.

Players move their Rider piece around the board using dice or a spinner, triggering battle spaces, special events, and item bonuses. Combat typically uses simple dice-based resolution, allowing players to defeat monsters or rival Riders while racing toward the final objective.
