The game is designed in the style of modern sugoroku-based tie-in board games common in Japan and Korea, with straightforward rules suitable for young players and fans of the Kamen Rider series. The components usually include a colorful board, Rider standees or markers, battle indicators, and a set of event spaces themed after the TV show.

Dice Keep is a 2-6 player dice adventure on 7 modular hex boards. Assemble any shape, then each turn: trade cards/artifacts, roll movement dice to move across terrains/portals, and act.

Land hexes trigger Chance Dice rolls for monster battles (draw Battle card and fight monster by terrain, dice combat: roll white Battle Dice vs. black monster Dice; Crystals/Artifacts/Fortunes add dice/boosts). Win for Battle cards to buy Fortunes in Towns (exchange rates vary; set respawn). Crystal Keeps yield color Crystals (+1 Battle Die each, max 1/color). PvP if pawns collide (attacker wins ties, winner steals Fortune/Battles/Artifact).

Death: respawn at last Town. First to defeat Iron Hill Keep wins. ~15 min/player.

—description from the publisher

Minions Hammer Pop is a children’s dexterity and reaction game themed after the Minions / Despicable Me franchise. Players take turns tapping or hammering buttons or tiles on the toy base, trying to avoid triggering the spring-loaded Minion figure.

One random button activates the mechanism, causing the Minion to “pop” upward, which eliminates the active player. The game continues until only one player remains.

Like many “Hammer Pop” and “Pop-Up” style games, the rules are extremely simple and rely on suspense, quick turns, and physical play, making it suitable for younger children and family parties.

Spin N Putt Golf by Franklin Sports is a fast-paced, interactive golf game suitable for kids and adults alike. Players use a mini putter to hit the golf ball into spinning target holes, testing precision, timing, and hand-eye coordination. The spinning holes and ball-return feature keep the action continuous and engaging, making it ideal for solo play or friendly competition. Perfect for indoor fun or backyard activities, Spin N Putt Golf combines skill, reflexes, and excitement in one compact set.

—description from the publisher

Jumping Spiders is a fun and interactive family game that challenges hand-eye coordination and quick reflexes. Each player has a set of colorful spiders, which they “launch” onto a central web by pressing or flicking the spider’s body. The goal is to land as many spiders as possible onto the web before time runs out. Perfect for children and adults alike, Jumping Spiders is ideal for party nights, family gatherings, or casual indoor play. Simple rules, exciting gameplay, and quick rounds make it a hit for all ages.

Shot Glass Roulette is an exciting adult party game that combines luck, strategy, and social interaction. Players take turns spinning the roulette wheel to determine which shot glass they must drink from. The goal is to avoid ending up with the “losing” glass — usually the one that triggers a challenge or penalty. The player who accumulates the fewest penalty drinks or survives the longest without hitting the losing shot glass is the winner. Perfect for parties, birthdays, or casual gatherings, the game can be played with alcoholic or non-alcoholic beverages, making it adaptable for any adult group. Quick to set up and easy to play, Shot Glass Roulette ensures a lively and entertaining game night.

100vs1 – Gorilla Clash is a fast-paced co-op card game where you and your friends chain human combos to force the Gorilla Deck to discard, while the Gorilla fights back, trying to dump your humans into the K.O. Pile. The first deck to run out of cards loses.

On the humans’ turn, each player chooses and plays one of the 5 types of human cards: Athletes, Fighters, Rescuers, Strategists, and Peels. Combo 3 or more of the same type, and you trigger a specific effect. Then, on the Gorilla turn, reveal the top card of the Gorilla Deck and apply its effects.

Peels are the hot potato of the arena: play them in a combo, and nothing happens; keep them in your hand, and if the Gorilla makes you discard, chaos follows. Talk and joke around the table to guess what others are holding, plan big, game-changing combos, choose the right moment to ditch the trouble cards in your hand, clash to block the Gorilla’s biggest moves… and peel your way to glory.

—description from the publisher

Easy rules, cute foam sushis, full of action and a lots of fun for adults and children.

Players quickly flip their cards one by one. If two cards match, the two players must quickly grab the right sushi with their chopsticks. If it's an action card, everyone does the right move and slaps the sushis.

The losing player collects the cards and starts the next round. The first to get rid of all their cards wins!

Children can play with their hands.

-description from publisher

Grandma Goblin tells her grandchildren a cautionary tale: do not go into the dungeon, you might get slaughtered by an adventurer!

Players embody different noble factions who work to clear the dungeon from its monsters, placing dungeon tiles, moving their explorers and sending heroes to face the combat. The game ends when the dungeon is closed and all monsters inside have been defeated. The faction that slaughtered the highest value of monsters wins.

In Think about the variable X, players take on prompts that contain the variable “X.” By substituting “X” with a chosen word or idea, the prompt is completed—and everyone must imagine the most fitting answer to it.

The twist? The “Judge” role rotates each round, and the Judge decides the correct answer entirely by personal taste and bias.

Your goal is simple: come up with the most clever, accurate, or surprising response that convinces the Judge. Outsmart your friends, embrace creativity, and discover just how far a single variable can take you!

In 18th‑century France, bread was more precious than cake! At Marie’s Table, hungry siblings gather to share slices of cake. But will the youngest brother be satisfied? The happiness of the family depends on how the elder brother divides the portions.

This is a quirky drafting card game of cake distribution, where clever choices and fair division decide who enjoys the sweetest victory.

The Guild of Adventurers is a solo tabletop adventure wargame where you are an adventurous Hero in whatever fantasy setting takes your fancy. You will lead your companions on a dangerous quest while dealing with other events that happen along the way. Each game takes the form of an Incident between the party and another group, but likely includes some additional tasks or conditions to fulfill to keep your adventure progressing.

The game is not aiming to be in any way a detailed simulation of armed combat, instead it concentrates on getting to the action as quickly as possible. All rolls in battle are 1D10, with a natural 1 being bad and a natural 10 being good. A single roll takes into account hitting, penetrating, and wounding. Weapons are generic. It’s not a wargame, it’s an adventure, or that’s the intent anyway.

So, what you have here is a set of campaigns that I call Story Arcs. Each consists of 15 games that might involve a specific race of foes, a particular enigma to be resolved, or a sequence of strange incidents that turn out to be linked, all building to tell a tale of a daring quest undertaken by your party of adventurers.

Every game has markers for where the adversaries might be and your team have to uncover the actual foes.  The adversaries always “React” to the team, hence true solo play rather than trying to play both sides. There are also often Bystanders getting in the way, and there are sometimes Wandering Monsters, in the wrong place at the wrong time.

Your Party will usually be six figures and will usually be fighting 6 to 10 enemy figures, plus there may be up to 8 bystanders wandering about.  A battle lasts 6 to 10 rounds, so you can set up your table, play through the game, and pack it down again in under 3 hours, probably 2 once you are used to it.

There are a selection of different locations (and therefore table layouts) where the action takes place, a selection of different adversaries, and a slow but steady increase in difficulty to balance the improvements and added gear your posse will get along the way.

These 104 pages include the 10 Story Arcs, full rules for gameplay, a 4 page Quick Reference for the battle tables, and an example campaign roster/tracker. You will of course need your own miniatures and terrain pieces, but the scenarios are as open as possible to enable you to use the stuff you already have, or give you an excuse to collect more.

-description from designer

On your turn you spin to determine a region of Brazil to draw a card from. You choose one of four categories and read the question to all players. Each player has to choose a different state - first come first serve. Whoever chooses the right state gets the token associated with the question. Once you have four different tokens from one region, you get the badge of that region. Get two badges to win the game.

They are black, enigmatic, and wicked to the core: black stories – the cult classic series from moses. These morbid, eerie, chilling, and at times darkly entertaining riddles revolve around death and accidents that could hardly be more gruesome. By asking questions, players must uncover the story behind each mysterious case. Not always an easy task…

50 pitch-black riddles about religious madness, absurd rituals, and sacred abysses — that’s black stories: Holy Shit!
This edition dives deep into heavenly zealotry, hellishly bizarre ceremonies, and absurdly eccentric cults – all based on true events. Whoever dares to enter will be rewarded with deliciously macabre puzzle fun.

With dark humor and golden detailing, this game is guaranteed to spark conversation – whether at the game table or at a Bible study group with a wink.

- 50 pitch-black riddles about religion, hypocrisy, and misguided belief
- 100% true – as bizarre as life itself
- with gold-foil accents for that final touch of shine
- for everyone who can laugh about the afterlife from time to time

Christmas is no place for wimps! Macabre mishaps by candlelight and absurd accidents beneath the Christmas tree… These black stories are for everyone who doesn’t want to miss out on morbid thrills and murderous puzzle fun — even during the season of love.
Who will manage to solve these cases of deadly Christmas cheer?

boardgame: Black Stories: Mörderische Bescherung 24 Black Stories zur Weihnachtszeit
description:
Black Stories – Murderous Christmas

This will turn Silent Night into anything but holy!
24 eerie and bizarre black stories help shorten the wait until Santa arrives and the candles blaze on the Christmas tree. Pure suspense — and farewell to peaceful contemplation!

Cuál falta? is a memory and quick observation game. Players study a master card showing all the elements, then try to identify which element is missing from the game cards that are revealed.

How to Win
The player who correctly identifies the most missing elements during the game wins.

•••

Cuál Falta? es un juego de memoria y observación rápida. Los jugadores memorizan una carta madre con todos los elementos y luego deben descubrir qué elemento falta en las cartas de juego que se van revelando.

Cómo se gana
Gana el jugador que identifique correctamente más elementos faltantes durante la partida.

In this dramatic snowball fight game, cards are played simultaneously: If you scoop while your opponent is ducking, then you have more snowballs to throw. If you duck while they throw a snowball, you dodged and stayed safe. If you throw a snowball while they are scooping, you hit them and win the fight.

Decide carefully and trust your gut - the winner of the fight gets a Toy that improves one card for the next fight. Toys let you do things like scoop two snowballs at once, protect an ally, or hit all opponents at once. You and your allies must gear up for the Final Brawl; an all out, last-team-standing, battle for wintry dominance.

Snow Brawl is best as a team game with four or more players, but can be played free-for-all with two or three.

Align 4 Makis of the same color to win!

Players take turns choosing one of the following 3 actions:
• Draw a Maki from the bag and place it at one end of the existing line.
• Flip a Maki to change its color.
• Move any Maki to either end of the line.

In Mech Farm you take on the role of Peter, an orphaned boy who uses an heirloom mech. As the lone survivor on the farm your job is to harvest and sell flowers to support your fledgling homestead. But watch out—at night the Terrors attack and you’ll have to defend your land if you want to see the sunrise!
Object of the Game

The game consists of two phases: Day and Night.

During the Day Phase you will attempt to gather flowers to fulfill a customer’s order. Fill enough orders and you will ensure your survival, winning the game.

The Night Phase begins when you complete an order or the time runs out. Then you will attempt to clear your farm of Terrors. If the terrors reduce your mech’s armor to zero you lose. If you defeat all of the Terrors you proceed to the next day phase. If you ever complete the Night Phase, and have delivered two flower orders, you win!

Invincible: Aftermath is a cooperative deck building game where your deck is never shuffled. Your goal is to defeat the Boss before it destroys your team or the Homeworld you’re defending. It is a standalone expansion but can be combine with Invincible: The Card Game.

In each battle, you start with a character-specific deck of cards and a unique special ability, then it's up to you to build up your deck, charge your power, and choose your strategy wisely. Your enemy won't be taking it easy on you, unleashing hordes of minions to take you down, launching attacks to destroy the city around you, and forcing you to adapt to their unique special abilities.

The game can be played as a single battle or in a campaign mode. As you progress in the campaign, you'll gain team attacks and combo abilities to strengthen your team. After each fight, the villains grow stronger, forcing players to adapt their strategies continually. In addition to new Bosses, Cities, Inventions, Combos, and Team Attacks for Expedition Mode, an all new mechanic is added - Bystanders!

Players lose if their health goes to zero OR the Villain meets any specific win conditions as outlined on Villain cards.

—description from the publisher

Numiso is an exciting and fast-paced card game that includes 50 cards and two dice. The cards are organized into 10 sets, each featuring objects numbered from 1 to 10, with 5 cards per number. The goal of the game is to quickly add the numbers on the cards to match the total rolled on the two dice and score as many goals as possible.

To begin, each player is dealt 6 cards, while the remaining cards are placed in a pile to serve as draw deck. The youngest player starts by rolling the dice.  All players must quickly add up the numbers on the dice and play one or more cards that total the rolled number. The first player to match the total correctly scores a GOAL against the player who rolled the dice and hands over their played cards.

If the player who rolled the dice matches the number first, they successfully DEFEND, and the played cards are placed in a pile as discarded cards, set aside from the game. In case none of the players are able to match the rolled number, then players should try and match first the largest rolled number or the smallest rolled number. After each round, the player who scores or defends draws from the draw deck to ensure they have 6 cards in hand.

The game continues until only one card remains with the players. The player with the fewest handed over cards is the winner, while the player with the most handed over cards is the loser. Numiso is a fast-paced, strategic game of numerical soccer that keeps players on their toes and makes for endless fun!

boardgame: Too Many Bones: Free RPG Day Promo- Nom “Bring the Thunder”
description: Alternate Tyrant card for Nom to use with Too Many Bones. Bring the Thunder changes the final battle to allow all Gearlocs to battle at the same time.

boardgame: Brunete 1937: La República pasa a la ofensiva
description:
In July 1937, the Army of the Spanish Republic launched a major offensive near Madrid. Their objective: relieve pressure from the Northern Front, and destroy a sizeable part of the Nationalist forces that had been besieging Madrid since November 1936. Under a scorching sun, Republicans and Nationalist fought a fierce attrition battle that forced the Nationalist command to send reinforcements from other fronts and suspend the advance in the North.

Brunete 1937. La República pasa a la ofensiva (the Republic takes the offensive) is a daily turn, Regiment-battalion simulation of that fierce battle. Each hex is 1 km across.

Published in issue #43 of Alea in November 2025.

Survivalist drops two to six empty-handed players into the brutal wilderness for ten nights. Each day forces a decision: stay warm at the fire, recover in the nearby clearing, or push into the backcountry and gather what’s needed to prepare for seven threats. As the game progresses, these threats grow worse and nearer: thirst, storms, hunger, illness, wildlife, fatigue, and isolation. The game plays out as a tense survival simulation where every round is a balancing act of action selection and resource management, shaped by limited time, limited backpack capacity, and the constant threat of injury.

On their turn, players take one action. They may “gather” specific resources along the ridge, “forage” for random supplies in the valley, or “craft" items using specific resource recipes. Choosing an action also means choosing how much risk to accept, because each option requires drawing deadly discs from a bag. These bag draws make every turn a press-your-luck calculation: push deeper into the wild to get what you need, or play it safe and risk being unprepared later. Regular airdrops add even more pressure to scramble into the backcountry for an assortment of bonuses and buffs.

After eight rounds of preparation, a ninth round reveals each player’s hidden talent. This allows players to spend a unique resource type they’ve gathered during the game to adjust the danger levels of the game’s threats. Then comes the finale: a tense endgame in which players face the seven threats one by one. Crafted items and remaining health determine who withstands the ordeal and who falls - from drawing more "wound" discs than they can fit on their playerboard.

The game ends when only one person is left standing. Whoever endures the longest is the true survivalist—and the winner.

-description from designer

Property values in this town are skyrocketing! You want to sell your buildings for as high a price as possible!

But if a giant monster appears, property values crash! …Though if another one shows up, prices go right back to normal!?

A thrilling real-estate bubble game where you’ll laugh, shout, and agonize over every move!

boardgame: Hag28: A Skirmish Wargame to Make Your Inner Crone Cackle
description:
Hello Deary.
I’m Granny Withering and I’ll be showing you all the ins, outs, and entrails of hag life. Or haggling, if you will. Follow my lead and your name will go down in the storybooks as one to be feared. Ignore me and you might as well save those kids the trouble and throw yourself in the oven right now.

You, my deary, are a hag. Not an evil hag. Definitely not. That would be the other hag. She’s a real crooked crone, let me tell you. No, you are a good hag and this hamlet is yours. The land is yours. The people in it are yours. You watch over and protect them. Keep them safe from the scary monsters of the forest. Make sure their food stores are always full so they’re happy, plump, and delicio— uh happy. Did I say happy? You heal their sick, care for their livestock, and take their babies. But only the unwanted ones. You raise them like any good mother. Teach them to walk, be well-mannered, how to extract a perfectly intact eye of newt. You’re not a monster. You would never eat them. That would be a waste. Besides, you can’t make goblins out of nothing.

So that’s you. Your life in a nutshell. And life is good. At least, it was good until that other hag started messing with your things and saying the hamlet is her’s. Where does she get the nerve trying to take what’s rightfully yours just because she was here first?

Right! It’s time to show that treacherous little witch who’s the bigger hag. Grab your goodies, assemble your minions, give that crone a good kicking, and most importantly stay outta my way.

Hag28 is a miniature agnostic skirmish wargame for 2 players on a 3’x3’ board using a handful of six-sided dice (D6).
You will fight for the right of dominion over a secluded hamlet and use resources (Pretties) to buy the Goodies and Minions you need to engage in quick, bloodthirsty battles. 
With mechanics like Impact and Resolve, you’ll see creatures thrown across the battlefield in this dynamic skirmish and Spite will keep them fighting. 
You’ll take turns activating units and spending Action Points (AP) to capture objectives or hunt down and kill the offending hag. You can also engage in longer form campaigns that will see your power and minions grow until either your enemy is obliterated or the hamlet reduced to a charred crater. In which case, you’ll move on and find a new hamlet to inflict your care upon.

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Valley of the Dead King – Klik's Madness Legends: Volume I
description:
This consists of the first three Klik's Madness Legends in a sturdy slipcase:


     A Taste for Adventure!
     Corrosive History
     Shadow of the Forsaken Crown

boardgame: HEXplore It: The Mountains of Godai – Stretch Goal Pack
description: This Stretch Goal Pack is the upgrade content unlocked as part of the MoG Campaign.

boardgame: Mistborn: The Deckbuilding Game – Dominance Track Pack
description: Replace the Dominance Track from Mistborn: The Deckbuilding Game with any of these new Dominance tracks, depending on your desired difficulty level. Includes optional 3-4 Player Co-op Rules.

One day, Cheeper tumbled down the stairs… Now he and his friends need to make their way back to Mom and Dad!

Players use Rock, Scissors, Paper, and the Strongest Card to battle in rock-paper-scissors.

Win a round and you get to move forward — but sometimes you might end up moving backwards instead…!?

In Demon's Conquest, players take on the role of the late demon lord's former generals, now off to invade the human world to stake their claim as the new demon lord.

The game takes place over the course of three rounds, each consisting of four phases:

    1 Exploration, in which players draft cards representing the lands they invade
    2 Invasion, in which players use their cards to build up their invasion areas or set them aside for mana
    3 Summoning, in which players spend mana to summon monsters to defend their invasion areas
    4 Defense, in which the players attempt to withstand increasingly numerous waves of enemies, possibly reaping some treasure off of them.


At the end of three rounds, players tally up their victory points and penalties - whoever has the highest score is crowned the next demon lord!

You are an aquarium keeper searching for the one penguin that no one has seen.
Players take turns acting as the lead player, and the game unfolds with trick-taking–style play, where following the suit—here, the penguin species—matters.

Each penguin belongs to one of three species, and players must play a penguin of the same species as the one led by the active player. But what happens if you don’t have a penguin of the required species…?
By choosing and playing cards carefully, you gather clues about the other players’ hands and deduce the whereabouts of the missing penguin.

Through this clever blend of deduction and trick-taking mechanisms, the game offers a rich, puzzle-like experience packed into a short playtime.
Enjoy a tense and satisfying logical challenge that unfolds with every card played.

Match cats, dogs, and Best Friends while using sneaky moves to outsmart your opponents. The first player to collect 2 matching rows of CATS or DOGS, or 1 row of BEST FRIENDS, wins!

How to Play:

     Each player has a board with 6 spaces.
     On your turn: Draw a card, then play a card to your board.
     Play special cards to steal pets or lock spaces and block others.
     Race to complete your winning rows before anyone else!


—description from the designer

WHATEVER is a fast-paced, family-friendly card game packed with risk-taking and the best bluffing you can muster. Draw a secret card, convince others to bid on it (or not bid on it) because it's good (or maybe bad), build your Row of points and special cards, and be the first to reach 10 or more points to win!

Perfect for those game nights when everyone says they will play WHATEVER.

—description from the publisher

Hack Attack: Defense is a fast-to-learn card game that teaches practical cyber safety skills for defending against attacks from hackers. To succeed, you will need to overcome User Errors, play the right Defense cards, and Protect your highest value Tech cards from being Attacked.

Once a player has protected 4 Tech cards, the game end is triggered. When the game ends, the player who protected the highest value of Tech cards wins.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: My First Adventure: The Castle of Little Boo
description:
Today, your castle is under threat.
Today, you will scare away all intruders from your domain!

Enter a story like no other, a story where each of your choices changes your adventure! 

            
        
    


Returning to your castle after a long holiday, you see that humans are turning it into an amusement park. Learn the magic of transformation and take on any appearance you wish to scare off these intruders and stop the construction work!

What form do you want to take: a strong and menacing wolf, an agile and frightening bat, or a clever ghost that can pass through “almost” anything?

Now your adventure can begin. You will be able to read this book normally, from left to right, but soon you will be faced with choices represented by pages cut into three parts. Turn the part of your choice and find out what happens.

After several choices, you will reach the end of your adventure... Will you succeed in scaring away the intruders from your domain and saving your castle? Once you have finished reading, you can start again to discover new paths or try out new transformations. 

            
        
    


My First Adventure is a collection of children's books where the child chooses the path of his adventure. Using clever mechanics, the story is experienced differently depending on your choices. Excellent first reading support, these books give a taste for adventure to everyone, from 4 years old.

—description from the publisher

My Puzzle Adventure, the puzzle-game that tells stories!

It is a collection of puzzles in which children live thrilling adventures. They look for the pieces to turn over one by one and discover an incredible story by putting the puzzle away. Will you succeed in the adventure?

Phobos is the third opus of My Puzzle Adventure Collection.

Play as BipBop, the robot, and travel to the far corners of the galaxy to experience extraordinary adventures with the crew of the Phobos! Join Toby, the curious scientist, Eclipse, the hot-headed pilot, or Gloop, the Lunar mechanic.

—description from the publisher

The game begins in the Autumn of 1792, three years after the start of the French Revolution. Louis XVI, King of the French remains alive but is imprisoned.
The newly declared French Republic finds itself at war with Austria and Prussia, whose armies are poised on the frontiers to invade.
The Coalition player will take the role of the Coalition states led by the Holy Roman Emperor and head of the Habsburg Austrian Empire, Francis II. The Coalition also includes Prussia, Great Britain and Bourbon Spain amongst other smaller states and anti-Republican insurgents. 
The Coalition’s goal is to apply as much military pressure onto France as is needed to bring the young French Republic to political collapse, perhaps thereby causing a Bourbon restoration.
The French Player will take the role of the Government of the Republic, and will fight to resist the diverse and numerous internal and external anti-revolutionary threats, seeking to defeat them one by one. Striving for “liberté, egalité, fraternité“, delivered if necessary at bayonet point. 
The Republic will also need to be careful not to allow the Republic’s finest young general (Bonaparte) too much success, or he may seek to overthrow the Republic and establish his own regime.

The game includes four scenarios:

The Early War (1792-1793)
    The Late War (1795-1797)
    Bonaparte in Italy (1796)
    The campaign game (1792-1797)

Scale
Units : 1 strength point equals 5000 men
One turn represents one season
Distance betwen two locations: on average 50-75 km
Complexity: 7/9

—description from the publisher

Dana Lombardy Revisits and refreshes this classic from the COLD WAR era, specifically 1962. You control Soviet and American and Canadians fighters and bombers, as well as SLBMs to "win" a nuclear exchange.

The map is a colorized version vs. the original stark version. Unit counters are a bit upgraded from 1973 as there you had mushrooms clouds, vs. here they are on the back of the delivery systems.

Moreover, from solely a strike upon CONUS, the game has expanded into 3 different scenarios. One is an attack on CONUS per the first NORAD by SDC. A strike on the Soviet Union is a stand alone game. Finally, we have a grand campaign game tied together.

family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
PIP Zero: Program. Contend. Decay. Zero.

A low-component, high-stakes abstract puzzle where every move is a calculated risk. Your 4x4 grid is a ticking clock of 16 dice, each one a resource you must zero out. Program four cards to four columns, but only if you win the Initiative battle. Every Movement costs 1 pip. Every Diamond is a deadly 2 pip kill shot. Every Club is a precious moment of protection.

Master the column lock, anticipate your opponent's programming, and race to be the first to reduce your grid to nothing. Perfect for tactical players who enjoy deterministic logic and ruthless efficiency.

—description from the designer

A “Daifugō-style” climbing card game played with cards numbered 1 to 32. Play a card stronger than the previous player’s, and race to be the first to empty your hand.

There are two key twists:

1. Card strength loops like a calendar

If the start player plays 16, the strength order becomes:
16 → 17 → … → 31 → 32 → 1 → … → 15
Every card can be strong at one moment and weak the next.

2. If everyone passes, the cards come back to you!?

If all players pass, the entire pile of played cards is collected by the active player.
You plan to reduce your hand… but it might suddenly grow instead!

Aerial gondolas make traversing the park into a relaxing ride of its own, as long as you can ensure that there’s always a station close at hand. Keep weary park-goers moving through the sky without ruining the park’s flow on the ground!

Skyways 5-card expansion adds a skyway line to build your park around. Keep the park connected to a station to ensure that park-goers can always reach their ride!

Lay out a line of 3 Skyway cards to build around. Connect park icons to skyway stations without interrupting the line!

For years the company Wildzone has been synonymous with luxury entertainment. Now they’ve hired you to design one of their new world-class amusement parks. Make the most of this high-stakes opportunity to put your name on the map!

Ultraparks 3-card expansion adds 6 new ultraparks to amp up the challenge! Each ultrapark requires maximum attention to detail, as even the slightest variation from the plan can disrupt the park experience. Use your hard-earned skills to prove you’re the best in the world!

Choose one of the new ultraparks to attempt. Avoid mismatched icons to get a perfect score!

For solo play. A set of additional cards to be used with the base Autobridge game that includes hands (or scenarios) which feature specific deals that are intended to challenge the player and promote improved play.

The set includes deals 33 - 64 inclusive, covering more complex scenarios than beginner sets.

This set, Group 16, was edited by Alfred Sheinwold.

Part of the Autobridge Advanced Course.

Introducing an intense card game in collaboration with Anarchy54, Based on Escapologic's very own horrifying escape room, THE BUTCHER!

Every card counts as the player busts a gut to remain the quietest survivor in their attempt to escape the BUTCHER'S lair.

Using a deck of location cards based on the real-life escape room from Escapologic, players take turns in a series of rounds picking a numbered noise card from their hand in hopes of not screaming the loudest (based on the noise amount/number on their played noise card). 
If you become the unfortunate soul to place the 6th card on a location, the noise amount is too high and you lose blood!

To add to injury, hiding randomly amongst the shadows of the location deck will be a variety of BUTCHER location cards, forcing players to undertake a series of anxiety-inducing challenges!

In your final attempts to escape, any remaining survivors will face the final boss card, THE RELENTLESS BUTCHER, adding a whole new round of challenges!

In the end, there can only be one remaining survivor (winner) unscathed but definitely traumatized!

—description from the publisher

For solo play. A set of additional cards to be used with the base Autobridge game that includes hands (or scenarios) which feature specific deals that are intended to challenge the player and promote improved play.

The set includes deals 01 - 32 inclusive, covering hands that are more complex than the hands for beginners.

This set, Group 15, was edited by Alfred Sheinwold.

Part of the Autobridge Advanced Course.

En guarde! You've been challenged to a duel. Play cards simultaneously, trying to score points against your opponent in this game of wits and strategy. Each move limits your options for the next, so track your opponent's cards and choose your moves just as carefully.

Riposte! is an 18-card strategy game. Both players have identical nine-card hands. Play starts with a random card from each player. Each card lists three other cards that can follow it. Players simultaneously choose and reveal their follow cards. Each card shows which other cards it scores against. If no one scores, both players choose another card for their next move, and the round continues. When someone scores a hit, the scoring card is removed from the game to track points. Players then pick up the rest of their cards and begin a new round. The first player to score their fourth point wins.

—description from the publisher

No one has ever escaped from Cellblock 18, but there is always a first time. Match wits as Warden and Prisoner and use the tools at your disposal to either escape or prevent escape in this asymmetric 2-player card game.

Cell Block 18 is an 18-card strategy game. Players take asymmetric roles as prisoner or warden. Players take turns deploying cards to one of three lanes.

The prisoner plays cards face down, attempting to create a set of three cards - a tool, clothing, and method - to allow them to escape and win the game. The prisoner also has access to one of three single-use special abilities that can disrupt the warden's plans.

The warden plays cards face up to attempt to block the prisoner's escape. Guard cards can reveal hidden prisoner cards or block the prisoner from deploying cards to specific lanes. The warden can also deploy cards that block certain means of escape to specific lanes.

The game ends either when the prisoner reveals the card set they need to escape or when they run out of cards, making escape impossible.

—description from the publisher

ATTENTION all you round earthers out there..."the world is flat"...and cats know it too!  To prove it, they're going to knock stuff off the edges of the world!

Take turns playing movement cards to send the mischievous giant cats bounding across the board, toppling rival houses off the edge of the earth.

Be the last player to have houses standing and claim victory in this ultimate feline free-for-all.

Imagine, you are assigned to represent one side of a random topic against another player. Judges determine the winner of each round by grading on a provided criteria based on respectful debate and valid points.

How would you convince an audience to agree with you on a stance where you are in the minority?
How would you defend a side you don’t particularly agree with?
Can you spin mundane choices into passionate debate?
Can you impartially judge upon a hot button topic?!

Players can choose between categories of topics ranging from silly preferences to controversial ideologies.
 The goal is to enact meaningful discussion and exercise healthy conversation to discover new perspectives in a playful, yet respectful game setting.

—description from the publisher

A compilation of expansions for Lands of Amazement:

     Lands of Amazement: Family Fun
     Lands of Amazement: Restorations
     Lands of Amazement: Ultraparks
     Lands of Amazement: Skyways

A compilation of expansions for A Dragon's Gift:

boardgame: Halo Flashpoint: Rise of the Banished
description:
Introducing Brutes into the game. Generally, expanding the weapons, items, keywords, approved terrain, and models to the Halo Flashpoint game.

Limited release version of Atriox is included in preorders.

Capiti is a fast and accessible card game set in a whimsical forest world where small mushroom folk gather natural resources to build magical totems. Wood, leaves, stone, and gold become the essential materials players must manage to construct totems and earn victory points.

On your turn, you may play a card or build a totem if you have the required resources. Each totem has a different point value, and timing your constructions is key to gaining the upper hand.
When the deck runs out, the player with the most victory points wins the game.

With simple rules, a playtime of around 20 minutes, and a charming woodland theme, Capiti is ideal for families and players who enjoy quick, strategic card games.

—description from the publisher

Capiti est un jeu de cartes rapide et accessible où de petits hommes champignons vivent au cœur de la forêt et construisent des totems à partir de ressources naturelles. Bois, feuilles, pierre et or deviennent les matériaux essentiels pour ériger des structures mystiques et marquer des points de victoire.

À votre tour, jouez une carte ou construisez un totem si vous possédez les ressources nécessaires. Chaque totem offre une valeur différente, et choisir le bon moment pour bâtir est la clé pour prendre l’avantage.
Lorsque la pioche s’épuise, le joueur ayant accumulé le plus de points de victoire remporte la partie.

Avec des règles simples, une durée d’environ 20 minutes et un univers forestier chaleureux, Capiti s’adresse autant aux familles qu’aux amateurs de jeux de cartes rapides et stratégiques.

—description from the publisher (French)

Fantastic Scuffles is a skirmish wargaming toolkit designed for any fantasy or pre-modern historical setting.

Fantastic Scuffles is designed to allow players to enjoy small-scale skirmishes with less than a dozen miniatures per player. 
It takes as its starting point the wargaming principles set out in its army-scale predecessor, Fantastic Battles (2020). Games should be engaging and unpredictable; force creation should be flexible, allowing players to play their fantasy.

Being both setting and scale agnostic, the rules can be used with whatever miniatures a player has at hand for scuffles in any fantastic realm or historic setting from the dawn of time until the early modern period.

Using one set of core mechanics, the rules outline several different ways to play solo, co-op and oppositional games including randomised encounters between two parties, arena fights, and dungeon delving.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Army of Darkness: The Board Game – Miniatures Pack
description:
The ultimate miniatures expansion set - a massive 44 unpainted, 30mm plastic figures to paint and enjoy.

Furthermore, it features additional game play content in the form of an exclusive Duke Henry the Red miniature, playable character board, and his own squad of knights.
family: Movies: Evil Dead / Army of Darkness

boardgame: Deal or No Deal: Talking Pass 'N Play
description: Compete against the banker or up to three other players in a battle to win big in this electronic pass and play version of the international hit television show. The rules are simple. Each player selects a briefcase. As each round progresses, players may keep their original briefcase or make a “Deal” with the bank to accept a cash offer in exchange for whatever dollar amount is in their selected briefcase. The gameboard keeps track of all players' choices and their subsequent “Deals”…or “No Deals,” which are kept secret until the end of the game. For ages 8 and up.

Большая бродилка: Конь-Огонь ("Crazy Adventures: Flare-Mare") is ready to join the race for treasure! You can use this character in games of both the classic Crazy Adventures and the Crazy Adventures: On the Way to the Desire Throne game.

This mini-expansion includes a colorful miniature figure, a character card with a special ability, and resource tokens for its activation.

On its turn, the Flare-Mare can discard one of its four Horseshoes to flip the rolled die to its opposite side. Unfortunately, once the steed is left bare-hoofed, it can no longer perform this trick.

However, this character has one more devious ability in reserve — Fiery Theft! Once per game, the Flare-Mare can steal any number of coins from an opponent sharing its space. In return, though, it will "kindly" carry that opponent forward by the same number of spaces.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Card Drives is a solitaire cyberpunk TTRPG where you play an endless series of hackers wielding a cyberdeck: a powerful computer console full of card drives. This cyberdeck is represented by a deck of playing cards you will mod and trash and draw on as you play the game. Each modded card becomes more powerful, giving you more access to powerful programs (poker hands) you use to exploit nodes and explore the net.

—description from the designer

Best Seller is a word game where players create novel titles from letter cards across four suits: Romance, Fantasy, Adventure, and Mystery.

Players may entertain each other by coming up with backstories for their novels—the more outrageous the better!

All novels earn points, but best-selling novels (those with the most of a particular genre card) earn ongoing royalties each round, and the most popular will even earn a coveted movie deal.

Unlike some pure “storytelling games,” Best Seller encourages light, unjudged storytelling that’s easy, approachable, and fun. The game is a blend of traditional card game, storytelling game, and word game.

—description from the designer

Can you find the hidden word when everyone speaks at once?

One Voice is a fast-paced party game where players must decipher a single word that has been scattered into pieces. A Moderator splits a secret word into syllables—one for each player—and distributes them on tiles. On the count of three, all players shout their assigned syllable simultaneously!

The result is a chaotic "One Voice." Players must listen carefully to the mixture of sounds to deduce the original word. The moment you think you know the answer, you must race to cover the Megaphone Token with your hand. The first player to cover the token gets the chance to answer; if they are correct, they win the round.

The game is played over 7 rounds. The player with the most correct answers (collected tiles) at the end is crowned the winner.

—description from the publisher

Grow You Assets is a fast-paced card game of bold bids, risky stacks, and dramatic dice rolls. Every round you’ll secretly spend your bid tokens to grab the asset cards you want most, then decide whether to play them safe or push your luck for massive points. Simple to learn, full of tension, and packed with big moments, it’s the kind of game that gets everyone leaning over the table.

At its core, Grow You Assets is all about building smart, strategic, stacks. Red and blue cards can be added to existing stacks or used to start new ones. Small stacks score modestly, but larger stacks explode in value thanks to increasing bonus points—so every card added feels like a tempting gamble. Green cards offer straightforward points, perfect for stability, while still occasionally carrying dangerous warning icons that keep things interesting.

And those warnings matter. Some cards include warning icons, which force you to roll two dice. Roll doubles and you lose the card… roll snake eyes and you lose the entire stack you placed it on. Red warning icons are even harsher, putting your whole stack on the line if you roll any doubles at all. These moments create some of the game’s biggest highs and lows—will your stack grow into a powerhouse, or shatter at the last second?

Then there are the gold cards, the flashiest way to rack up points. You add up your regular golds, then multiply that total by any multiplier golds you’ve collected. One good multiplier can transform an average score into a dramatic comeback, which keeps the game exciting until the very end. (Final gold totals are capped so things don’t get too wild… but they still get pretty wild.)

Some cards also include optional actions you can use to steal cards, draw new ones, or create clever plays. And when you steal or draw from the deck, you skip warnings entirely—giving you safe ways to grab value while everyone else risks their stacks.

With its mix of clever bidding, push-your-luck excitement, and rewarding combos, Grow You Assets feels different every time you play. It’s quick to set up, easy to understand, and full of those unforgettable “no way that just happened!” moments.

Bid smart, build boldly, and see if you can grow your assets better than everyone else at the table.

—description from the publisher

Get ready to shred in Rock Off: Hunters vs. Demons, the ultimate hand management card game that captures the high-energy thrill of a music rhythm game!
In Rock Off, you're not just playing cards—you're forming a band, drafting instruments, and executing epic song lines! Choose your gear in the "Build the Band" phase by drafting unique Instrument Cards, unlocking powerful abilities like Shred, Quick Draw, or Master of Puppets! Experience the intensity of a live performance! Players simultaneously attempt to match the sequence of notes revealed for each line of a given song within a limited time. Win the Crowd or Condemn Your Foe!

Winning is about more than just a high score. Every incorrect note forces you to make a critical decision: take a Fan penalty, or move the marker on the shared Penalty Track toward your opponent's victory!

—description from the publisher

Each player owns a ship which moves from port to port around the world as dictated by a throw of the dice. When a player arrives on a port he can lease it by paying its cost to the bank. Any player landing on that port in future must then pay docking chargers to them. 
Money is earned by dropping cargo off at required ports and gaining docking chargers from your leased ports,
Shipping disasters and other problems await all players. the player who earns the most money at end of the game wins.

Based on the fun TV series hosted by Philip Schofield. This game requires speed, general knowledge, and pin-point accuracy. Players have 30 seconds to find a special point on an image and put a ring on it. The value of the ring goes up as the size gets smaller. A fantastic game for all the family to play.

Fully cooperative tactical mosterslasher in ironically grimdark fantasy setting. Month after month during one stressful summer groups of prisoners are forced to go through magic portal to infected wastelands and slash mobs of monsters to extract magic crystals from their dead bodies.

You'll find lot of tactical battles, leveling up and character building, a couple of average jokes, crafting, and more battles.

Bad Gateway is the first «antilegacy» roguelite board game – it offers non-scripted permanent influence of each run to the next without permanent changes of any components.

«I'm making a game where every «hero» is not decent at all, but there's always a chance to make something a little less awful. Where everyone has a common goal and their own path, and each path is nasty in its own way. This is Bad Gateway and it's literally it», – Theoretical Part, designer and illustrator.

—description from the desginer

The desert heat is relentless but so are you...

Gravel sprays out from under your tires and your car gives a sickening lurch as it begins to spin out. Clutch. Shift. Gas! Your engine roars and your car leaps into the next turn on a perfect line; you're still in the race but you'll have to watch for loose gravel on the track.

Heat: Rocky Roads introduces two new racing tracks. In the South Africa Grand Prix you will face treacherous gravel on the track, requiring careful management of your car and nerves of steel. Victory on the Germany track will hinge on how you deal with the fast pace of the newly build chicanes along the long straight away.

Take part in the 1966 Championship season and take advantage of a new innovation, the sliding Skirt, to surprise your opponents and slipstream to victory!
family: Game: Heat – Pedal to the Metal

description:
My Very First Games – Catch‑A‑Duck is a quick dexterity game for toddlers (ages 2+) where players fish wooden ducks from a pool using a rod. Lift a duck to reveal its underside: nothing, a pink dot (gain 1 point), or a pink dot with a star (gain 1 point and take another turn). Play continues until the pool is empty (optional second round). The player with the most tokens wins. The game encourages free play and supports early skills like hand‑eye coordination, fine motor control, and counting.

—description from the publisher

Team up or play individually, then race to see who reacts fastest and wins the most cards! With a great mix of visual, number, and word puzzles there’s something to suit every brain in this fast-paced puzzle-solving game!

—description from the publisher

The desert is full of gold... and all eyes are on the mines.
In Pépite, manage a Wild West town, play as its key characters, and cleverly scheme your way to gold to grow your fortune. Each round, simultaneously play one character and one action location (mine, opponent's saloon, or bank). Transport gold, steal from your rivals, or protect your town! Be careful: each character can only be used once per round, and mines can explode at any time. Starting from the 2nd round, new characters enrich your strategic options.
The game ends after the 4th round. Only gold deposited in the bank counts... and the richest player wins!

Pioneers, you have been chosen for a mission of vital importance: to develop a prosperous and thriving ecosystem on this new, barren planet.

In Planet Bee, a strategic tile-placement game, your goal is to place the Ecosystem tiles at your disposal optimally to generate new life inside your geodesic dome. To win, you must make the most of your oxygen and water resources!

An effective tile placement strategy will allow you to house all kinds of plants and animals in your dome and earn points.

But be careful! Pay attention to bees, the true keepers of the new ecosystems here, just as they are on Earth. Meet their needs and they will reward you with plenty of points.

Your mission starts now: bee smart, bee victorious!

—description from the publisher

"A building game of chance & skill requiring luck & steady hands."

This is a dexterity game where players have to build the tallest tower from a selection of plastic pieces representing bases, towers and turrets. A dice determines which piece must be added to the player's tower. Pieces do not interlock properly so the result is a wobbly tower prone to collapsing.

Additional human cards in a blanc layout with unique skill combinations.

Sold in a set of 1x3 or 3x3 on the publisher's onlineshop (cundco.de).

The three cards have a brush symbol behind the human cardset indicator 2.

---

Publisher description:

Product information "Paleo - Blank human cards"

The blank people cards for you to design yourself are available in a small set with 1x3 different cards and in a large set with 3x3 cards.

If you want to create your own heroes, you can get creative and let your imagination run wild here.

On to new challenges!

Napoleonica is a hex-based wargame where history is mixed with fantasy. In this game you will have to lead one of the leading armies of the 18th century – French or the British Empire’s army – to confront the evil-stricken Legions of Abyss.
Napoleonica: Steel and Magic combines the basic elements of a classic hexagonal wargame with a system for issuing orders to units and playing Maneuver cards. Game rounds are divided into turns, during which you can perform one or two actions by activating your units.
Players command armies of a variety of units including infantry, cavalry, artillery, and fantasy creatures.

—description from the publisher

DC Deck-Building Game: Peacemaker Pack includes two hero cards (Peacemaker and Vigilante), nemesis versions of these characters, two starter cards for use with DC Deck-Building Game: Arkham Asylum, and rules.

boardgame: Necromunda: The Aranthian Succession – The Vaults of Temenos
description:
Necromunda is the tabletop skirmish wargame of anarchy and violence, where players control rival gangs of fighters battling each other for supremacy in the nightmare depths of the underhive or out in the blasted Ash Wastes.

Necromunda: The Aranthian Succession – The Vaults of Temenos continues to expand on the game, advancing the timeline and continuing the campaign from Necromunda: The Aranthian Succession – Cinderak Burning, providing new rules to add even more variety, scope and excitement to your battles on Necromunda.

Inside you will find:

The Great Pilgrimage: Details of the events which unfolded on Necromunda following the fall of Cinderak City and the rise of the Prophet.

The Road to Temenos Campaign: Rules for playing the second of a series of linked campaigns that take players through the events of the Great Pilgrimage and the hunt to secure the Relics of Necromunda.

Narrative Scenarios: Seven narrative scenarios that reflect the events of the Great Pilgrimage.

Dramatis Personae: Four Dramatis Personae central to the story of the Great Pilgrimage to use with your gang: the Prophet of the Redemption; Axon Hammer; Scourge of the Spider Points; Durgan Kill-Fist, Champion of the Brokebone and Scrutinator-Primus Servalen.

Additional Rules: New rules for House Cawdor and Palanite Enforcer gangs to use in their battles, and rules for allying your gang with the Imperial House. Also included are new vehicle gang tactics for House Cawdor gangs.

This is a supplement. You will need a copy of the Necromunda: Ash Wastes rulebook or Necromunda: Book of the Outlands to use the contents of this book.

—description from the back of the book

Harlequin is a card matching game where players play cards to a shared tableau, creating a colourful diamond pattern as they do so.

You score points based on the value of the cards in both the row and the column that your Harlequin card occupies in the tableau.

Will you play your Harlequin early in the game or wait for a better opening and risk not being able to place him at all?

And try to get rid of all your cards, as any left in your hand  at the end of the game will reduce your score.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Violent Skies: Fourth but First – The Debden Eagles
description:
Sat inside the cockpit with the engine rattling through your bones, you turn to engage. Managing your energy and fuel, can you gain position to get a shot off? Or will the bandit get the better of you? Sometimes it is better to disengage than push a losing fight.

Violent Skies is a solitaire game of aerial combat in the Second World War. Using a game card that acts as your Cockpit and also as a mission generator. The player and Bandit take alternating actions to try and get a shot on the opposition's aircraft.

Fourth but First puts you in the cockpit of the Fourth Fighter Group's pilots as they begin fighter operations for the Eighth Air Force Fighter Command. Built out of the Royal Airforce Eagle Squadrons, these young Americans volunteered to fight for the United Kingdom before America's entry into the war, becoming the nucleus of the Eighth Air Force's fighter force. This pack will take you from their RAF kites to the P-51 Mustang. As you move from fighter sweeps over France, to ground attack in support of Operation Overlord and finally duelling with the desperate efforts of the Luftwaffe's jet force. Can you survive escort missions over Berlin? Or the danger of ground attack? This edition of Violent Skies expands the system into USAAF efforts in North Western Europe to the war's end. Can you make it through all 7 Aircraft Campaigns and survive?

Each sortie lasts between 5-10 minutes, and the player has a logbook to advance their career. As the player completes a campaign, they have the option to keep the logbook and continue their career in different aircraft and theatres. Players of Violent Skies Season 1, and 2 can continue their pilots careers as a new year of war opens up, with new aircraft and new challenges.  Go from the RAF to USAAF and continue your career.

All is needed is at least four D6, a small marker for the advantage track, and a pen or pencil for the logbook. Everything else is included in the minimal construction print and play PDF.  Three optional counters are included and need to be folded and cut out but the rest is ready straight off the printer.

Included as flyable aircraft are the Spitfire MkVb, P-47D, P-51B/C and P-51D

Each aircraft comes with a sortie card (your cockpit and mission generator), campaign tables, optional damage table, Skills sheet and counters. Plus new ground attack rules to expand the system.

There are optional rules to track pilot Rank and Awards to further immerse the player in the role of a Second World War pilot.

There are Logbooks for the Luftwaffe and USAAF in low ink and full colour, as well as a detailed example of play.

.....and remember, watch out for the "Hun in the Sun".

—description from the publisher
family: Game: Violent Skies (Ben Rawlings, Pocket Warfare Publishing)

boardgame: Necromunda: The Aranthian Succession – Cinderak Burning
description:
Necromunda is the tabletop skirmish wargame of anarchy and violence, where players control rival gangs of fighters battling each other for supremacy in the nightmare depths of the underhive or out in the blasted Ash Wastes.

Necromunda: The Aranthian Succession – Cinderak Burning continues to expand on the game, advancing the timeline and introducing a new campaign and new rules to add even more variety, scope and excitement to your battles on Necromunda.

Inside you will find:

The Great Darkness: Details of the events which unfolded on Necromunda following the opening of the Great Rift, which split the Imperium in two and caused the great heat sinks in the heart of each hive city to cool, plunging Necromunda into turmoil.

The Succession Campaign: Rules for playing the first of a series of linked campaigns that take players through the events of the Great Darkness and the start of the rebellion against Lord Helmawr's rule.

Narrative Scenarios: 12 narrative scenarios that reflect the events of the Great Darkness and the following crisis.

Dramatis Personae: Four new Dramatis Personae to use with your gang: Lady Haera, 13th Daughter of Lord Helmawr; Athera, the personal champion of Queen Adina; Gorshiv Hammerfist, the leader of Shiv's Crushers; and Vespa 'Minx' Merdena, the wild child of Slate Merdena.

Additional Rules: Additional rules for use in games of Necromunda, including Escher and Goliath crew, the agile Escher Cutter and brutal Goliath Mauler. Also included are new vehicle gang tactics for both House Escher and House Goliath gangs.

This is a supplement. You will need a copy of the Necromunda: Ash Wastes Rulebook or Necromunda: Book of the Outlands to use the contents of this book.

–description from the back of the book

boardgame: Necromunda: The Aranthian Succession – Ruins of Jardlan
description:
This supplement, the third installment in the Aranthian Succession campaign series, follows on from the events that played out in The Vaults of Temenos supplement, providing new rules to add even more variety, scope and excitement to your battles on Necromunda.

Inside you will find:

Fall of House Helmawr: Details of the events which unfolded on Necromunda following the rise of the Aranthians.

The Fall of Helmawr Campaign: Rules for playing the third installment in the Aranthian Succession campaign.

Narrative Scenarios: Seven narrative scenarios that reflect the events of the Fall of House Helmawr.

Dramatis Personae: Four Dramatis Personae including: Asun'ghar, the Lady of Ash and Orrin Grimjarl, Last Charter Lord of Jardlan.

Additional Rules: New rules for House Van Saar, Palantine Enforcers, and Ironhead Squat Prospectors gangs to use in Necromunda battles.

This is a supplement. You will need a copy of the Necromunda: Core Rulebook to use the contents of this book.

–description from the back of the book

On Brownsea Island, the first Scout camp is underway! Patrols set off on a two-day adventure but forget the gear for their bivouac  – the tent sheet, pegs, and rope. To spend the night outdoors, they must sneak back to camp without 
being caught by Baden-Powell. But they must also guard their flag, which clever rivals might steal!

Will you risk attacking opponent players or protect your own camp? Teamwork, resourcefulness, and a bit of luck will decide who wins.

Let the Scouting adventure begin!

boardgame: Necromunda: Hive Secundus Gang Tactics Cards
description:
This set contains 26 cards for use in Necromunda games.

Inside you will find:

18 Gang Tactics cards themed around Hive Secundus (which can be used by any gang), 4 blank Fighter cards and 4 blank Vehicle cards!

You will need a copy of the Necromunda: Core Rulebook and Necromunda: Book of Desolation to use the contents of this pack.

–description from the back of the box

In Cargo Mania, your objective is to fully load your locomotive with valuable cargo. The required crate value is shown at the bottom of your locomotive card. Using your shunter, you organize wagons, load them with the right goods, and couple them to your locomotive at just the right moment.

Careful planning is essential: each wagon you couple to your locomotive must match the letter or color of the previously coupled wagon. To overcome this limitation, make smart use of the wagons’ special abilities and create an efficient chain.

On your turn, choose one of four actions:


    Take all goods cards of one color or one letter from the station into your hand.
    Take a wagon from the station and couple it to your shunter. You may optionally activate the wagon’s effect.
    Move a wagon from your shunter to your locomotive. Discard cards from your hand matching the same letter or color equal to the number of white crates on the moved wagon.
    Discard any number of cards from your hand equal to the number of white crates on a wagon coupled to your shunter. Flip that wagon to its gray side and move it to your locomotive.


Be the first player whose coupled wagons exceed the required value of their locomotive with the total value of all white crates to win Cargo Mania.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Necromunda: Zone Mortalis Gang Tactics Cards
description:
This set contains 26 cards for use in Necromunda games.

Inside you will find:

12 Gang-specific Gang Tactics cards (one for each gang featured in Necromunda), 12 Zone Mortalis Gang Tactics cards (which can be used by any gang) and 2 Instructions cards, which explain how to use these Gang Tactics cards in your games of Necromunda.

You will need a copy of the Necromunda: Core Rulebook to use the contents of this pack.

–description from the back of the box

In Super Chonk, play as cats who just want to become purrr-fectly chonky. Each round, you'll secretly select your speed and target bowl. Once you're ready to eat, meow to let your fellow felines know! Then simultaneously reveal to see whether you'll fill your belly or wind up hungry.

Dash to get just the choicest tidbits or lumber slowly to eat everything that's left in the bowl. But be careful not to collide with other cats or go so slow that there's no kibble left. At the end of the game, score up your snacks to find out which cat is the chonkiest!

—description from the publisher

The year is 1990 and the human genome project has just begun. Race against your fellow scientists to be the fist to decode the human genome in this tactical, fast paced card game. Use the power of DNA to form Codons and perform extra actions whilst creating DNA Genes to score points. Will you make Genes to get ahead of the competition or chain dizzying combos to leave them in the dust.

The aim of the game is to collect the most cards from your sequence as "Genes", the player with the most collect cards when the deck is empty (points) wins the game.

Players use cards from there hands to form sequences of card in front of them. Each card has a DNA base and a segment of an action "Codon" on it, when cards are arranged in specific orders they form Genes which are collected from the sequence to form points. Cards may also form a complete Codons allowing players to edit their sequence and the sequences of other players. Codons allow players to insert, remove, swap, move, spin and play cards and can form combo effects.

—description from the designer

Play tiles. Build lines. Make ends meet. Be the first to reach the target score by completing long lines and nestling your tiles into tricky positions! This edge-matching tile placement game offers immense strategic depth within a beautifully simplistic rule set; perfect for a casual game over coffee or an intense battle of the minds!

—description from the publisher

The Lost Village is a tactical cooperative card game for 1 or 2 players.

The players play Hero and Monster cards in turns, trying to utilize the Heroes' abilities and vanquish as many Monsters as possible. If they manage to play all the Monster cards and survive, they win - but if too many Monsters breach the defenses, the population of the village faces a most dire fate.

In this educational game players are gathering personal data in form of cards, their own or that of fellow players. They can get that data from each other or retrieve it from the cloud, where the cloud is a central play area that hosts a number of face-down cards. The trick is to store your cards efficiently in the cloud or to thwart your opponents. Among the roles that can be assigned to players are hacker, data processor, and others.

The main topics of the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) are covered by the game.

Gather proof to show the world the truth!  Challenge opponents and collect evidence, but beware, it could all disappear in the flip of a card...

—description from the publisher

Go on 6 treks by flipping cards to search for Bigfoot. Watch out for hoax cards though.

It's time for some chance encounters.  Leave your fellow aliens in the cosmic dust by quickly capturing earthly things to complete your missions first!

—description from the publisher

Play cards to match your UFO collection request to score points.

boardgame: Close Quarter Combat: Skirmish Wargame for Urban Modern Combat
description:
"The squad is now deployed. Sarge checks his men with a gaze; each of them had been trained to face the hardest conditions, but today they're about to prove their qualities on the battlefield. Suddenly the command comes - 'Break in! We're at stake!' The squad breaks in and..."

... you tell the rest of the story!

With CLOSE QUARTER COMBAT  you can simulate clashes between regulars and insurgents on modern battlefields.
An original and intuitive system centered on speed and playability as its strong points.
Lead your troops among cramped and dangerous alleys to clean buildings and hideouts, or unleash your insurgent hordes against the invaders.

Your new battle starts here!

—description from the back of the book (translated)

"La squadra è schierata. Il sergente controlla con lo sguardo i propri uomini; ognuno di loro è stato addestrato ad affrontare le situazioni più difficili, ma oggi dovranno dimostrare sul campo di battaglia le loro qualità. Come un fulmine, scatta l'ordine - 'Irrompere e liberare l'edificio! Siamo in ballo!'. La squadra irrompe e..."

... la storia la continui tu!

Con CLOSE QUARTER COMBAT potrai simulare scontri tra truppe regolari e insorti sui moderni campi di battaglia.

Un sistema di gioco innovativo ed intuitivo che ha fatto della rapidità e della giocabilità il suo punto di forza. 
Guida le tue truppe tra gli stretti e pericolosi vicoli per ripulire edifici e nascondigli, o libera le tue orde di insorti contro l'invasore.

La tua nuova battaglia comincia da qui!

—description from the back of the book

Rougewrite is a competitive roll-and-write game where players construct dungeons to repel adventurers.  The game is played in two parts.  First, during the construction phase, each player draws shapes over selected squares on a pre-populated map, thereby creating passages and rooms.  Then, during the interception phase, players lure adventures into their maze, scoring points when the adventurers are damaged by monsters.

The player who scores the most points wins. The game also supports solo play (with the goal of achieving a high score).

Components:
A 6x6 cell board with coordinates (a1-f6).
36 stones in two colors (e.g., 18 dark, 18 light). Each set contains: 14x "Selenolyths", 2x "Shards of Diox", 2x "Shards of Orthos".
One six-sided die (d6).

Goal: Be the first to achieve one of the victories:

     Create a 3x3 square of any stones.
     Score 9 points.
     Be the first to place all stones from your reserve.


Gameplay:
The players decide who takes the first turn. The game begins with an empty board.
On a player's turn, they must perform the following sequence of actions:
1. Choose:
The player picks a stone from their reserve and declares a file (a-f), that must have at least one free square, on their half of the board.
2. File Check:
- If the chosen file has exactly one free square, the player may place the stone there without rolling the die.
- If the chosen file has two or more free squares, the player rolls the die. The result (1-6) determines the rank on which the stone must be placed.
3. Resolve the Turn:
Square is free: The player places the stone and activates its effect.
Square is occupied:
Selenolyth: The player must place it on ANY other free square (no effect!). If this placement creates a 3x3 square, the player loses immediately.
Shard: The player loses their turn. The stone is returned to the reserve.

Stone Effects:
Selenolyth: When placed, pulls all stones in its 8-directional line-of-sight to free squares adjacent to it.
Shard of Diox: Grants 1 point for every stone on the 4 diagonally adjacent squares.
Shard of Orthos: Grants 1 point for every stone on the 4 orthogonally adjacent squares.

Note: For masters of the ritual seeking the ultimate challenge, the Full Moon variant unfolds on a vast 11x6 Celestial Tablet. It uses two dice (2d6), increases each player's arsenal to 33 stones (27 Selenolyths, 3 Shards of Diox, and 3 Shards of Orthos), and raises the Luminance victory condition to 12 points. All core rules remain, transforming the intimate duel into a grand strategic campaign worthy of the lunar apex.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Federation: Piracy – Dice Tower AI Assistant Promo Card
description:
This additional AI Assistant card was provided as part of the 2025 Dice Tower Promo Pack A.

This card may be mixed with the other AI Assistant cards from the Federation: Piracy expansion.

boardgame: TRACES: Crime In Athens (ΊΧΝΗ: Έγκλημα Στο Κέντρο)
description:
"We received a call for a homicide in the center of Athens! After an exchange of gunfire, a man fell dead! The perpetrator of the murder fled the scene of the crime and his traces are missing! Start immediately!! The next 24 hours are critical! With each passing hour, it will become more and more difficult to catch him!"

Traces is an asymmetric cooperative game of inductive logic and tactical choices, in which players are divided into 2 teams: the Killer and the Police. The Killer tries to buy time to escape, while the Police cooperate, doing everything in their power to arrest him and bring him to justice!
To play Traces, turn on the Electronic Buzzer by moving the switch on the bottom of the device, from the off position, to the M1 or M2 position.

M1: Electronic Game Expert
The Electronic Game Expert is an interactive narrator who teaches you the game, while you play! So, you can play Traces directly, simply by turning on the buzzer, saving you the hassle of reading the rules!

M2: Traces - Game Setup
Place the city map in the middle of the table. Decide which player will be the Murderer and give him the Murderer Board, the Crime Timeline and the 5 Special Move Markers. Deal the Police Character Cards to the other players, along with the pawns of the corresponding color. If there are fewer than 4 Police Officers, they must share the Police Characters among themselves. Leave the remaining markers aside so you can use them when you need them. The Police Officers must place the Crime marker in the area indicated by the Murderer and then, one by one, place their pawns in any Police Station they want. More than one Police Officer can be in the same Police Station at the same time.

Expand your Cities games with this Dice Tower promo achievement board. Locations include Dice Tower West, Dice Tower East, and the Dice Tower Cruise.

This was included in the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound Promo Pack C

This promotional pack adds 2 dice tower cards and standees to the game. Each player adds the dice tower somewhere in their castle.

This promo was provided as part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound Campaign 'Pack C'

The B3-AR card allows you to finish the current requirement if you roll 3 or more thrusters.

This card was provided as part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound 'Pack C'

boardgame: Unconscious Mind: Nightmares – 2025 Dice Tower Promo Card
description:
This is an additional Manifest dream card for the Nightmares expansion allows you to use a Minor Freedom, Minor Growth, and Minor Passion to gain 2 therapy points and a bright idea or move your pet twice. This card is compatible with the Pets Module from the Nightmares expansion.

This card was provided in the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

boardgame: Things In Rings: 2025 Dice Tower Promo Pack
description:
This promotional pack contains 25 new Dice Tower themed Thing cards along with 6 cards each of the three category cards (blue Attributes cards, red Context cards, and yellow Word cards). Three of each color have Dice Tower themed prompts and the other three of each color are blank for you to add your own words.

This promotional item was included as part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

boardgame: Blood on the Clocktower: The Cacklejack – Experimental Character
description:
This 2025 Dice Tower promo experimental character is called The Cacklejack. The text on the token reads "Each day, choose a player: a different player changes character tonight."

The Cacklejack causes character-changing chaos.

     The Storyteller chooses one player to change character each night. This player can be evil or good, dead or alive.
     The Storyteller also chooses which character they become. This may be an in-play or a not-in-play character.
     Each day, the Cacklejack chooses a player that is immune - the Storyteller must choose a different player to change character that night.
     The Cacklejack may choose publicly or privately, but must choose during the day.
     Each time a player’s character changes, this is treated as a new instance of that character ability. For example, if a player becomes the Chef on Night 3, they immediately learn how many pairs of evil players there are.


This promotional item was provided as part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound Campaign 'Pack C.'

boardgame: Classified Information: The Dice Tower Promo Cards
description:
This promo was part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

This set adds 4 promo cards with 2 rules and description cards.

boardgame: Kinfire Council: Dicing Beneath the Tower Promo Card
description:
This promo was part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

This Dice Tower themed promo adds a law card to the game. The card text reads "We have a proposal to allow a gambling house in the Coping in order to subsidize the gate taxes but it may introduce a hole in our security." The card ability states "When passed, the Cult gains 8 trophy. After this, taxes for Tier 3 are reduced to 0."

boardgame: Distilled: Dice Tower Bottle Promo Card
description:
This promo was part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

The Dice Tower Bottle is an item that can be purchased for 5 money. It is a wild location and gives 4 victory points and 2 money when used.

boardgame: Aetherspire: Boblin – The Plane Shifter Promo
description:
Boblin allows you to take the special actions to Stake a Claim, Pillar Drop, and Lead Rebellion.

This promo was part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

This promo came with the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack C.'

Adds 4 Dice Tower themed cards to the game. Two are Small Tower and two are Neck Tie cards.

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): The Realm Walker Promo Card
description: This card is a Champion Unit - Mercenary that gives the Gatewalk ability. The card reads, "Instead of moving this unit, if it is adjacent to a gate, you may place it adjacent to any other gate on the battlefield."

Abrolhos invites you to become a guardian of the Abrolhos coral reef ecosystem, located in Brazil, where strategy intertwines with ecological stewardship. In this competitive placement game, players cultivate distinct coral species across a dynamic tidal board, earning points through symbiotic connections and biodiversity.

Each turn, you strategically place coral pieces adjacent to different species, mirroring real-world symbiotic relationships that strengthen the reef.
