The innovative tidal cycle mechanic shifts playable zones each round—corals thrive only in deep waters, forcing players to anticipate environmental changes and adapt their tactics accordingly.

Victory belongs to those who master the delicate balance: maximize symbiotic contact, achieve species variety bonuses, and claim resource-rich territories. Optional variants introduce competitive objectives and cooperative modes, ensuring replayability.

Abrolhos transforms abstract strategy into meaningful marine conservation education, making every placement a step toward protecting our oceans while delivering engaging, thoughtful gameplay.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Last Aurora: Dice Tower 2025 Promo Set
description:
Originally available in Pack D from Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

This Dice Tower themed pack includes the cards Game Keeper, Tower Guardian and Dron Vanguard with the likenesses of Tom Vasel, Zee Garcia, and Mark Streed on the cards.

Game Keeper (Exploration Phase) - When you resolve an encounter with this survivor, gain 1 fame point.

Tower Guardian (Fire Weapons Phase) - When you deal damage to an enemy, gain 1 fame point.

Drone Vanguard (Exploration Phase) - When you explore with this survivor, swap the positions of 2 cards in the Exploration Zone before choosing a slot to explore.

boardgame: S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game: Zone Hidden Riches
description:
Publisher description: "Zone Hidden Riches is a min-expansion to S.T.A.L.K.E.R. The Board Game and it enriches the Campaign with theh promise of legendary treasures."

This expansion adds 10 cards to the game:
- Hidden Riches Help Card
- Moonshine Still
- Ventilation Cache
- Anomalous Crash
- Zone Fishing
- Inventor's Legacy
- A Farewell Letter
- The Last Recipe
- Hole
- Contact Card (to add to the contact deck)

This expansion was originally part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Pack D.'

This Dice Tower promo tile add a scoring bonus. The tile reads "If the total number of birds here is EVEN, the controlling player scores 5. If ODD, the player with the 2nd-most birds scores 6. 3rd-most birds scores 2.

This promo was originally part of the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound campaign 'Promo Pack D.'

boardgame: Catacombs: Fortress of Nazemoor – New Assistants Promo Pack
description:
This promo pack includes 5 Hero Assistant cards and 1 Overseer Assistant Card. The Assistants in this Promo Pack do not have pieces. They are still linked to a character and follow the same general rules for Assistants, except players must pay gold coins to perform the action on their card.

This promo was originally released with the 2025 Dice Tower Gamefound 'Promo Pack D.'

Vintage board game played with cards. Players draw kick and pass cards to determine every action, moving, passing, shooting, or fouls, and the ball marker advances across the grid pitch according to those results. Special areas on the board indicate off-side, shooting, defending, and turnovers. Goals are scored when a card succeeds on a special location. When time is up, the player with the most goals wins.

A farming themed board game.
The goal is to complete the farm on your end of the game board using small collectable cards to fill the tiles. The first to complete wins.
Almost every tile on the board has an event that allows the player to collect or lose cards they own with specific conditions. Or let's them roll again.

On August 17th, 2006, 19 a year-old French National named Madeline Deparde was killed while riding her bike from the local grocery store in Sunnyview, Florida.

Ms. Deparde was in the United States working as an Au Pair for Scott Zion and his family.  35 year-old Zion was driving the vehicle that struck Ms. Deparde and claims it was an accident.

Your job is to solve the mystery by finding evidence that will prove Mr. Zion lied about accident.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Triple Six Baseball: The Dice Game
description:
Triple Six Baseball – The Dice Game is a print-and-play tabletop game that recreates the flow of a real baseball game using nothing but dice, a simple outcome table, and a scoresheet. The theme is pure ballpark: two generic but evenly matched teams facing off over nine innings, with hits, walks, rallies, and double plays emerging from the dice.

On your turn at bat, you roll three dice, sort them, and look up the result on the 3d6 Outcome Table based on the current base situation. That single lookup drives everything: singles, doubles, home runs, strikeouts, sac flies, double plays, and more, all tuned to feel roughly like modern MLB statistics. On top of that core loop, you can call for bunts or pickoff attempts with 2d6, adding just enough tactical choice without slowing the game down.

The goal is simple: outscore your opponent over nine innings (and extras if needed). You’ll manage base runners, decide when to play safely and when to risk a bunt or a big inning, and watch momentum swing back and forth as the dice tell the story of each game. Whether solo or head-to-head, Triple Six Baseball aims to deliver a quick, satisfying baseball experience that fits in a sheet of pages and a handful of dice.

—description from the publisher

Be the first to find the selected tile and develop your visual and mental agility! 
This exciting game will test your speed and concentration while you have fun with your friends. As you progress, you'll improve your mental and visual dexterity, all while enjoying a great time!

The game comes with a reversible board with more than 350 images on each side. Each image is represented by a card. On each round a card will be revealed, and players will have to race to be the first one to find the image. The first player that finds 15 images wins!

Think you know everything about your football team?  Put your knowledge to the test in this fun soccer trivia game! Compete with your friends, answer questions, collect medals, and use your quick thinking and strategy to become the champion of the board.

On your turn, roll a die. Depending on the space you land on, you must answer a question and can win one or more medals. The first player to collect 5 medals wins the cup.

Develop your mental agility by answering questions!
Test your knowledge and challenge your friends or family in a game that stimulates your mind. Perfect for hours of entertainment and learning.

On their turn, each player will roll a die. Depending on the category it lands on, they must answer a trivia question. If they answer correctly, they will win a diploma in that category. The first player that collects all 6 diplomas wins the game.

Demonstrate your mental agility and creativity by guessing what is drawn, mimed, or described in words.
On your turn, roll a die and move your pawn to a category (object, food, action, animal, location). Then, spin the wheel to determine if your team must draw, act out, or express with forbidden words the concept on the card they land on. If they guess correctly, they win a trophy.
The first team to collect all 5 trophies wins!

Wandering: Frontier is a modular, solo narrative card game set in a harsh, ambiguous Western world. You take on the role of a Protagonist—defined by their Origin, Competence, and Mark—who is embarking on a physical and spiritual journey to find Resolution. This might be vengeance against your Arch-enemy, or perhaps the hard-won redemption you seek.

Gameplay Overview
The game is played over a series of Journeys, where the Scenario Deck (100 cards) acts as your timer and the engine for all events. Each card offers two distinct paths: the Trail, representing wilderness encounters, and the Settlement, for interactions within towns.

Key Mechanics:


     Resource Management: Survival hinges on managing six key resources, tracked by counters: Voluntad (Will/Energy), Moralidad (Morality/Ethics), Influencia (Influence/Standing), Suministros (Supplies/Provisions), Trauma (Psychological Scars), and Defensa (Defense/Protection).
     Narrative Trials: All challenges, from facing a dangerous beast to navigating a moral dilemma, are resolved by drawing cards from the Scenario Deck and testing your Protagonist's resources against the card's value. Success allows you to gain resources, recruit Allies, or advance your personal plot.
     The Arch-enemy: Your ultimate goal is to survive long enough and become strong enough to confront your personal Arch-enemy, a nemesis who defines your specific victory condition and narrative finale.
     Modular Design: The game is designed to be highly modular. Protagonists, Allies, and Villains from different expansions or settings can be mixed and matched, allowing for vast replayability and unpredictable, unique stories every time you play.


Goal: Survive the harsh Frontier, gather the strength and Allies needed, and achieve your Resolution by defeating your Arch-enemy before the Scenario Deck runs out.

—description from the designer

Test your knowledge and be the first to reach the finish line by answering interesting geography questions.

On your turn, roll a die and move the indicated number of spaces. Then answer a question. If you need help, you can look at the image on the card, which will help you identify the place the question refers to. If you answer correctly, roll the die again, move your space, and answer another question. Otherwise, it's the next player's turn. The first player to reach the finish line with the exact number rolled on the die wins.

Each player receives a gridded game board and 30 magnetic dominoes.
In each round, a card with a specific shape is revealed, and all players must place that shape on their board. When all the shape cards are gone, players count how many rows and columns they have completed. The player with the most completed rows and columns wins.

The game also includes an advanced mode in which, during setup, each player receives 6 locking tokens and must place a locking piece on one of those spaces before starting.

Each player receives 30 wall cards. Once the game begins, players must draw roof cards and place wall cards on top of them according to the roof card's instructions, without the tower collapsing. Watch out! If a player draws a tornado card, it could blow down your tower.
The first player to complete their tower wins.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: The players will have 4 cards in their hand. In each round, they will pass one card to the right. Whoever collects 4 matching cards puffs out their cheeks, and the other players must do the same. Whoever is the last to puff out their cheeks loses the round.

The game consists of 126 cards that must be dealt among all players. There are a total of 5 shapes in 4 different colors.
In each round, players must simultaneously reveal one card from their hand. If a card is repeated, matching both shape and color, players must call out "doubles," and the first to do so takes the cards.
There are special cards that, when face up, allow players to make doubles regardless of color or shape.
When all players' handouts are depleted, each player receives one point for each card they have collected. The game continues for as many times as agreed upon, and the player with the most points wins.

In each round, a topic card is revealed. In turn order, each player must say a word or concept from that topic, without repeating any previously said. If they do, they lose one of their 5 lives, and a new card is revealed.

If a player runs out of lives, they are eliminated.

The last player remaining with lives wins.

In advanced mode, players must repeat the sequence of concepts previously mentioned and then say a new one.

The game consists of 80 circular cards with jumbled letters that spell out a word. Red cards are easy, and blue cards are difficult.

At the start of the game, players agree on which color cards they will use and how many correct guesses are needed to win.

Each round, a card is revealed, and players must quickly identify the hidden word. Whoever guesses correctly takes the card. The first player to collect the agreed-upon number of cards wins the game.

As a hint, each card has a number indicating whether it represents a name, animal, place, country or city, food, or object.

The game has 62 circular cards. Each card contains six onomatopoeias.

In each round, two cards are revealed, and players must quickly identify the repeated onomatopoeia. The player who identifies the repeated onomatopoeia makes the correct sound and takes the first revealed card. Then, another card is revealed, and the process repeats.

At the start of the game, players agree on how many correct onomatopoeias are needed to win. The first player to collect that number of cards wins.

Players receive six pizza slice cards and arrange them to form their pizza in front of them. On each turn, they must draw a card and decide whether to replace one of their slices. The first player to get all six slices to match the required criteria wins the game.

There are special cards that allow players to swap cards, play again, etc.

There are several ways to play:

     Pizza a la carte: Match all the ingredients on your pizza to those on a card you receive at the beginning of the game.
     Gourmet Pizza: Each slice must contain a different ingredient.
     Numbered Pizza: Each slice must have a different number of ingredients.
     Mitti Motta Pizza: Each half must have a different ingredient.
     Twelve Pizza: The pizza must have exactly 12 ingredients.

The game features 10 types of food, with 8 copies of each.

At the start of the game, each player receives 12 cards. On your turn, name a player and ask them for a type of food from your hand. If they have one, they must pass it to you. You can then ask the same or another player for a card. If a player cannot give you a card after requesting one, your turn ends with them drawing a card from the deck.
When a player has all 8 cards of the same food type, they place them face up in front of them. Players who run out of cards are eliminated. When the deck is depleted, play continues with the cards in hand. When all the cards are gone, the player with the most sets of food wins.

ParuParo (パロパロ) is a memory game in which two players try to collect butterflies and line them up in a common 6x6 grid made of 9 2x2 cards.

On their turn, the players must collect butterfly wings from a pool and try to match two wings of the same colour and of opposite orientations to create a full butterfly. However, the cards are double-sided and the back has a differently coloured wing that the front. The players have to turn the cards around when collecting them, so they must remember what colours are on the back of the cards in the pool.

When they successfully match cards and create a butterfly, they must place a token on the 6x6 grid on a matching butterfly. The first one to align 4 butterflies or to place 4 tokens on the same card wins!

The asylum’s doors open — but its patients never left.

In Insanity Cure, psychological terror becomes a tangible threat. Set in an abandoned psychiatric facility, this expansion introduces two twisted killers - The Doctor, who is obsessed with perfection, and The Nightmare, a little girl who hides a dark entity - and thematic encounter cards that force survivors to manage fear and stress as core mechanical elements. Survivors face not only a perfectionist's obsession but also their nightmare incarnate! 
Inside the box:

- 1 Killer game board with map "The Asylum"
- 1 Survivor game board with map "The Asylum"
- 1 Dream map with mini screen
- 2 Killer boards (The Doctor and The Nightmare)
- 6 Killer miniatures (2 Doctor, 2 Nightmare, 2 Little Girl)
- 26 Killer skill cards (13 Doctor, 13 Nightmare)
- 13 Tokens (5 Asylum Patients, 2 Doctor Delay, 3 Poison, 3 Mirror)
- 1 Season 2 Expansion#1 rulebook

Hunger sharpens claws, and even loyalty can be a blade in the cold.

In Depraved Hunger, tension rises as The Wendigo hunts in a frozen wilderness, its starvation‑twisted form ever on the prowl. Against relentless frost and predatory dread stand two unlikely allies: Brenda Cote, Officer, who abandoned warmth and safety to answer the call of the missing, and her faithful canine companion Cooper. Together they must brave biting wind, treacherous terrain, and a nightmare that grows stronger with every passing moment.

Inside the box:

- 1 Killer game board with map "The Snow Mountain"
- 1 Survivor game board with map "The Snow Mountain"
- 2 Mountain Cable Car miniature
- 1 Killer miniatures (The Wendigo)
- 1 Killer boards (The Wendigo)
- 13 Killer skill cards (The Wendigo)
- 8 Starvation cards
- 15 Killer tokens (14 Freeze, 1 Delay)
- 1 Survivor miniature (Brenda)
- 1 Survivor miniature token (Cooper)
- 1 Cooper player aid
- 1 Survivor board (Brenda)
- 1 Killer assistance token
- 1 Season 2 Expansion#2 rulebook

When innocence dies, the nightmare plays on.

Fatal Funtime throws survivors into the crumbling corridors of an abandoned school, a place whose laughter has long since turned to silence. Within lurks a new killer, The Doll, forever trapped between play and predation, pulling survivors into his warped game. Luckily, you’re not entirely alone: Hubert Cawthon, a Horrorholic, offers fresh eyes, shaky courage, and unique skills to turn fear into strategy.

- 1 Killer game board with map "The School "
- 1 Survivor game board with map "The School"
- 10 School Hideout miniatures
- 1 Killer board (The Doll)
- 2 Killer miniature (The Doll)
- 13 Killer skill cards (The Doll)
- 8 Killer trick cards
- 16 Killer tokens (3 Knife Traps, 6 Catch, 6 Spring Traps, 1 Voltage Surprise)
- 1 Survivor miniature (Hubert)
- 1 Survivor board (Hubert)
- 1 Killer Assistance token
- 1 Season 2 Expansion#3 rulebook

Experience the most comprehensive Hitster version yet! This exclusive Platinum Edition contains over 500 songs – 350 of them brand new – and combines cult classics, current hits, luxurious playing materials, a premium box design, and a speaker for brilliant, immersive sound. This limited edition is perfect for true music lovers. Play it on its own or combine it with other Hitster editions for endless musical fun – the ultimate playlist for any party!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Erlebt die bisher umfassendste Hitster-Version! Diese exklusive Platin-Edition enthält über 500 Songs – davon 350 brandneu – und kombiniert Kult-Songs, angesagte Hits, luxuriöses Spielmaterial, ein hochwertiges Boxdesign und einen Lautsprecher für brillanten, immersiven Sound. Diese Limited Edition ist perfekt für echte Musikliebhaber. Spielt sie für sich allein oder kombiniert sie mit anderen Hitster-Editionen für endlosen Musikspaß – die ultimative Playlist für jede Feier!

—description from the publisher

Delve into the box fort and navigate its tunnels to discover the purrfect spot to loaf! But be careful to manage your cat's impulsive desires and keep both eyes open to the deviously chaotic ploys of the other cats.

Cosy Cats is a 2 to 4 player sliding tile game which encourages players to work together to pick on the player in the lead. The game board is littered with objectives for you to manage and use to your advantage but beware, a cat that gets too destructive or greedy might end up getting scolded or cause the whole box fort to come tumbling down.

Sit down with your friends for an hour or two while you eat, destroy, play and fight your way to victory in this brain-burning, wonderfully kinetic game of shapes and colours!

—description from the publisher

This expansion introduces 2 types of challenging roads to 9-Card-Rally Run: Narrow and Winding, both types feature only a single lane, limiting your options to maneuver.

Narrow - Single lane roads; either left, middle or right. Hazard variant of these cards are also included! (See Hazards Pack expansion). Features mountainside/ ocean view art.

Winding - Single lane road but that requires specific dice value to be used in order to move forward in this road. Features dense jungle art.

—description from the designer

Scratch Island is a roll and scratch game where everyone will get their own unique treasure map. Embark on an adventure and dig for hidden treasures. During the game you will select shapes from rolled dice and use these shapes to scratch a path on your map. Make sure you find the keys to open treasure chests. But beware, other players may be the first to claim treasure, so you have to be smart about it.

—description from the designer

NecroMaster is a two-player card game that combines the elements of Chess and Mafia in a simplified format.

Players seek to attack the opponent's king: NecroMaster in order to win. Instead of pawns, players initially place cards facedown, thereby hiding the location of the king and others. Players can use the cards' abilities when they are face-up at the cost of giving hints on the location of the king. Those cards include Knight, Archer, Bomber, Priest, and Magician; players can freely arrange each card's positions at the start of the game, and use their unique abilities each turn.

Those unique abilities include, for example, the Knight parries attacks from the front, the Archer can range attack, and the Bomber destroys left, right, and front cards when destroyed. The Magician can swap the positions of cards, and the Priest can destroy a face-down card if players guess the cards correctly.

Besides using unique abilities, players can attack an adjacent card using their face-down/face-up card, or move their cards to adjacent vacant spots (where cards were destroyed) each turn.

Overall, this game has a good balance of elements of luck and strategy. Players can read the opponent's mind to one-turn kill, or strategically eliminate the opponent's cards one by one in order to win.

—description from the publisher

A psychological adventure in the dreamlike depths of Nemo’s mind.

Set between 20,000 Leagues Under the Sea and The Mysterious Island, Mobilis in Mobili is a battle within Captain Nemo’s subconscious. Lost in the Maelstrom, trapped between life and death, Nemo drifts into a surreal inner world. Here, four mental archetypes struggle to take control.

Players assume the roles of these inner voices, each exploring fragmented memories, manipulating Nemo’s emotional equilibrium, and fighting for dominance over his mind. But every memory you uncover has consequences…

—description from the designer

Tesoros del Mar Menor is more than a game, it is a project, an initiative to divulge and raise awareness about the environment of the largest saltwater lagoon in Europe.

Welcome to Tesoros del Mar Menor, a light card game for 2–5 players, inspired by the wildlife, culture and landscapes of the town of San Pedro del Pinatar (Region of Murcia). Throughout games of 15-25 minutes you will gather treasures, protect your chest and sabotage your rivals with clever tricks. Whoever scores the most points will be crowned Supreme Explorer!

In Tesoros del Mar Menor there are 3 types of cards: Chests, Treasures and Tricks.

     Chest cards represent each player’s treasure chest. The location shown in the illustration is where that player keeps their chest and treasures.
     Treasure cards come in three different colours, matching the three colours of the flag San Pedro del Pinatar.
     Trick cards let you interact with other players and with the cards in play, and generally change the flow of the game.


Each turn has two phases:

     Draw: take the top card from the draw deck.
     Action: choose one of the following options:
             Store 1 Treasure from your hand in your chest (onto the pile of its colour).
             Play 1 Trick card from your hand.
             Draw 1 additional card from the draw deck.
    


Objective: At the end of the game, add up the points from the treasures stored in your chest and the bonuses from your flags. The player with the highest score wins.

boardgame: Brink: The Wooden Disc Flicking Game
description:
Brink is a fast, fun, family friendly game for 2 players. It is carefully crafted using high quality birch plywood. Brink is a unique, handmade by a Manchester-based games designer.

The goal of Brink is to flick discs across the board to land in your opponents half. The closer to the edge you get, the more points you get. If you get the disc to land so it hangs off the edge, you've scored a BRINK! Maximum points! This is an easy to learn fast-paced exciting game for two. Ideal for family game nights, pub nights or just rainy afternoons. Full rules of the game are provided in the box.

—description from the designer

Pop the top to "roll" the dice and race to find the famous person with the closest matching birthday, death date or iconic event.

First to shout out and get closest to the date wins the card.

The time has come! The Glitter Minis are about to hatch from their eggs. In this enchanting children’s game, players aged 3 and up roll two colourful dice to collect and trade beautiful, sparkling eggs from the centre of the table or from other players. If an egg stays with you and you roll the matching combination, the sweet little Glitter Mini hatches from its shimmering egg. Who will manage to gather the most Glitter Minis?

—description from the publisher

Get ready for another adventure with Stitch! In this cooperative memory game, the beloved blue alien comes straight to the table. Players work together to remember which images are hidden under the square cards in play. By doing so, they can reveal cards strategically and help Stitch reach his goal - before Leroy beats him to it.

A cooperative game that challenges the group’s memory. Everyone helps ensure Stitch safely reaches his favorite fruits - but beware, Leroy loves tasty fruit too!

Throughout the game, players keep track of which images are under the square cards laid out in the center. Remember well, because revealing the right pairs determines how far and to which fruit Stitch can move.

Once all 12 fruit cards have been reached and secured, the team wins and Stitch can enjoy his delicious fruits.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Following the aftermath of the Samian invasion, the Greek inhabitants of Sifnos sought to better defend their mineral rich land. Over the course of several centuries, they constructed a network of over seventy beacon towers scattered across the small island. This system would allow them to quickly communicate a variety of messages, and most importantly, warn of incoming sea invasions. Now's your chance to step into the role of an ancient Greek military commander! Prepare your people against raiding pirates by constructing towers, mustering soldiers, and investing into the future of Sifnos.

Your goal in Towers of Sifnos is to have the most victory points (VP) at game's end. Points are primarily gained by increasing your influence and successfully defending the mining operations of the island's three predominant minerals: gold, silver, and lead. Players track their influence over these minerals on their player boards. Several invasions will occur over the course of the game, granting players the opportunity to prove their proficiency and might. After each invasion, the pirates will either grow in number or much needed allied reinforcements will arrive. The game ends immediately after three waves of reinforcements have arrived at the island.

—description from the publisher

Port Arthur: Sirotčí vrchy is a competitive microgame for two players set in the same world as Port Arthur. Designed by Guillaume Sandance, this minimalist duel unfolds on a single card that serves both as the board and the rules reference.

Each player secretly selects a value on their six-sided die, then both reveal simultaneously. The red player, who controls the figure, attempts to advance it across the track toward victory, while the blue player tries to predict and destroy the figure’s position with a well-aimed strike.

If the figure survives and reaches a blue-marked space that remains intact until the next round, the red player wins. If the blue player successfully targets the figure’s column, the figure is destroyed — and the blue player claims victory.

Simple to learn and lightning-fast to play, Orphan Hills captures the tension of bluffing and prediction in a compact, five-minute battle of wits.

boardgame: White Sea: Campaign Commander Vol. IV
description:
This is the fourth volume in this series and it takes us to the 16th century and the decisive amphibious campaigns fought by the Ottoman and Habsburg empires in the Central and Eastern Mediterranean.

The game begins in 1565 and runs until 1574.

It is an operational-scale game played on a zonal map. It is designed for two players: one controls the Ottoman forces and the other the Christian forces—both the Spanish and their Italian allies as well as the forces of the Republic of Venice. A game lasts approximately 3–4 hours and uses a system based on two main elements: cards and counters. Players will have to use their troops and resources to earn Victory Points by controlling the cities on the game map, playing cards, and raiding the opponent’s territory. It is not a “card-driven game” since the cards are used to trigger events that influence battles, secure reinforcements, and obtain resources needed to continue the fight.

The OTTOMAN player is on the offensive from the very beginning, and if they lose Strategic Initiative, they will most likely lose the game. Their mission is to expand the empire at the expense of Habsburg and Venetian territories. At the start of the game, the CHRISTIAN player is clearly at a disadvantage, since they can only use Habsburg troops and fleets; the Venetian Republic is neutral at the beginning and will only adopt a belligerent stance when forced, either by Ottoman aggression or by diplomatic pressure that eventually pushes it into the war. At first, the Christian player will focus on playing cards that slow the Ottoman advance, increase diplomatic pressure on Venice, and bring reinforcements—prioritizing a low-intensity war and fomenting rebellions in the Ottoman Balkans.

The game features a unique characteristic: tracking the progression of each year’s campaigning season. Operations carried out on the map are tallied to mark the passage of good weather—naval warfare in the Mediterranean was seasonal, and could safely be conducted only in summer. Once the good weather ends, armies and fleets can be forced into winter quarters by playing the appropriate cards.

Thus, the OTTOMAN player must carefully plan their offensives, since their fleets will set out from Constantinople each campaign season, and they will have to choose a strategy: either advance toward Habsburg-controlled objectives in the central Mediterranean—which are quite far in logistical terms—or directly attack the Most Serene Republic of Venice, with targets in both the central and eastern Mediterranean, facing a strengthened Christian force once Venice enters the war.

Siege warfare plays a crucial role in this game, as every city on the map is a walled city with its own garrison that must be besieged in order to be taken. These were amphibious campaigns focused on besieging fortresses, with constant raiding incursions, some minor engagements, and one great battle: Lepanto.

Like all games in this series, it has a very simple ruleset and is designed so that the sessions are highly interactive—creating an enjoyable and solid gaming experience for those who lack the time or space for a longer and more complex game.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Below the surface of the North Sea dwell the cheekiest and most colourful of creatures...but don't let their charm deceive you. With fabled precision, these legendary seafolk strike with fin and fang, plundering the vessels above. As the winds howl and the waves crash, each season brings even greater challenges. For those who survive, what name will they speak, and of which ocean clans will they sing of for ages to come?

A game of Skute lasts four rounds, referred to as "seasons", starting with Spring and ending in Winter. Each season, players start by bidding to gain various creatures to their hand and grotto. Next, they attempt to play out all cards from their hand before their opponents can in order to sink the best skute. After four seasons, whoever has the most valuable collection of sunken skutes wins.

—description from the publisher

Marble Football is a fast-paced tabletop sports game where players take turns moving their team of marbles around a football pitch, outmaneuvering opponents, and setting up strategic shots to score goals. Every move matters; positioning, timing, and reading your opponent creates a game of tactics, tension, and dramatic moments.

The game blends dexterity and decision-making. Players alternate activating marbles, executing passes, long balls, defensive plays, and attacks on goal. The simple core rules make it accessible for families, while experienced players discover surprising depth as they develop team strategies and effective formations.

Matches are quick, competitive, and full of highlight-reel plays. Whether playing casual kickabouts or full tournaments with multiple teams, Marble Football delivers a fun, energetic sports experience on the tabletop.

ポリぱん～警官になって泥棒からぱんつを守るゲーム ("PORIPAN - A game where you become a police officer and protect panties from thieves")

Are you a righteous police officer or just a pervert?
"PORIPAN" is a board game in which you play as a police officer in an alternate world city and protect the panties of No Shorts Tordam Academy students from thieves.
Your mission is to catch the thief and get the panties back!
Roll the dice to collect panties and aim to increase your score with your favorite girl.
During the game, there is a whistleblower phase in which you can deduce other players' favorites and aim to win.
Adventure in a world of panty hunting where fun and strategy intersect with 2-5 players!

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: ティラノに喰われた男 (Murder Mystery Mini: T-Rex Murder Case)
description:
The Tyranno began to move.

Somewhere in F Prefecture. Under a clear blue sky and the blue mountain ridges where the snow is melting, a dinosaur museum is nearing completion.
Four fossil researchers gathered here from all over the world were horrified by what they saw before them.
In the center of the permanent large hall stood a Tyrannosaurus Rex, holding in its mouth the upper half of a human corpse.

Introducing the latest 4-player version of Murder Mystery Mini (MMM)!
This version features dynamic gimmicks just like dinosaurs!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Don’t have a full party? No problem.

Dungeon Crawler Carl Unstoppable is a solo or 2-player co-op card-crafting deck-builder set in the World Dungeon. You’ll battle against neighborhood, borough, and city bosses while gearing up and leveling your chosen hero to survive and reach the next floor. Play solo or bring along your own Princess Donut for a co-op run!

Key Features:

Solo or 2-player deck-building adventure based on John D. Clair’s acclaimed Unstoppable system.
Card-crafting mechanic allows you to upgrade abilities and equipment as you play.
Manage risk as upgrading your abilities and equipment also causes the boss to grow in power.
Luciano Fleitas Returns for Artwork:
We’re thrilled to confirm that Luciano Fleitas, the long-time artist for the Dungeon Crawler Carl series, is returning to illustrate for both games!
A solitaire game in which player has to move all number cards from above the arrows to the bottom of it.

boardgame: Lords of the South Slavs: Roll & Write
description:
Lords of the South Slavs – Roll & Write is a roll-and-write game set in the late Middle Ages, a time when the Balkans were politically fragmented and increasingly threatened by Ottoman expansion. Players take on the role of independent Slavic rulers, each governing a growing city while attempting to strengthen regional influence and resist external pressure.

The goal of the game is to develop a prosperous city and secure dominance through a combination of influence, economic growth, and defensive preparations. Players score points by building grand structures and advancing along various development tracks.

Each round, players roll dice that determine which symbols are available. Using a personal player sheet, they allocate results to construct buildings, progress along various tracks, and manage limited resources. Because not all dice results are available to every player, each round involves competition over key symbols and timing. This shared draft creates tension between reacting to opponents’ choices and pursuing long-term strategic plans, while variable setups ensure that no two games play the same.

—description from the designer

An auction game of redistribution of assets based on For Sale.
It has two stages, each lasts for 4 rounds. In first stage, each round will have 5 cards randomly drawn from the deck(numbers are from 1 to 30). Players use coins separating in 5,4,3,2,1 to buy cards or pass to save coins. The remaining cards will go to the "bot" pile and will participate in the second stage's auction. In second stage, players will use the cards they buy in the first stage to earn coins back. The player who has the most coins at the end of the game wins!

Colecobug is a solo adventure and strategy game inspired by classic creature-collecting titles like Pokémon and Digimon. You will explore terrain, encounter unique creatures, face rival collectors, and strengthen your team to challenge the fearsome Guardian Colecobugs. Combine tactics, resource management, and creature evolution to complete your collection, win battles, and achieve victory

A long time ago, deep in the living forest of Artralis, magical creatures known as Colecobugs emerged, each carrying a fragment of the Four Great Types. When a group of ambitious collectors began capturing them recklessly, the ancient guardians of the forest awoke to restore balance. And they did. However, their immense power remained active and uncontrolled, causing even greater harm to the very forest they were meant to protect. Now, you take on the role of ending the chaos—exploring territories, capturing creatures, facing collectors, and challenging the Guardians so that, once defeated, they may return to the eternal slumber that keeps Artralis in balance.

—description from the publisher

Dicequest is a dice-pool builder for 1 to 4 players in which each player assembles a party of up to three heroes to face quests, gain items, and earn victory points.
The game unfolds over three acts, each raising the stakes and expanding the players’ tactical options.

In Act I, players begin their adventure by recruiting their first heroes and attempting introductory quests.

During Act II, quests become more challenging, heroes level up, and players acquire powerful items that enhance their party’s abilities.

Finally, in Act III, legendary heroes become available as players prepare for the final showdown against the ultimate boss.

At the end of the final battle, the player with the highest score is crowned the victor.

Game play modes: competitively, cooperatively, or solo.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
The Official Taskmaster Advent Calendar

It’s possibly the most unexpected advent calendar you’ll ever open - and definitely the most fun. Expect props, brainteasers, hidden rooms, and the occasional surprise from Little Alex Horne himself.

Find Alex. You Have 24 Days. Your Time Starts Now.

How it Works: Alex has gone missing. Each day, open a door to reveal a new task. Complete it to discover where tomorrow’s door is hidden. Get it right, and you’ll move forward and one step closer to finding Alex. Get it wrong and Greg will be silently - but profoundly - disappointed...

Behind Each Door: Step inside a different room from the Taskmaster house - as if you're a contestant on the show about to take on a task. You will find a task. Some are simple, some are silly, all of them will make this Christmas wonderfully absurd!

Festive Chaos: No numbers; no mercy... Forget 1 through 24. To find tomorrow's door, you'll need to solve the previous day's task correctly... So go on - read the task, panic slightly, and enjoy 24 days of festive chaos!

HAVE FUN! If you get stuck, there are hints available online where you can get a helpful nudge (or a full-blown hint) and check your answers - just don't tell Greg! Can you find Alex before Christmas?

-description from publisher's website
family: Misc: Advent / Christmas / Jule calendar game

description:
A race between drugs and bugs. The goal is to reach the finish line while curing or killing as many infected patients as possible. With a focus on bacterial infections, the game shows the most relevant aspects of the "silent pandemic": The growing global number of AMR (anti-microbial resistance) cases.

Use progress, boost and setback cards to move forward on the board. One player may reduce the progress of another player by playing a setback card related to either drug development or microbial resistance.

The cards may be played according to a 3x3 matrix providing each player with contraints as well as tactical opportunities with respect to the cards that can be played alone (or in combination).

The game ends when the finish line is reached by either a drug or a bug. The winner is the one with the most points according to an asymmetrical score system.

—description from the designer

A simple, fast, brutal, party game for anyone who's ever suffered through office politics, pointless meetings, or nightmarish coworkers.

A game developed with spontaneity, improvisation, and chaos in mind.

Employee Disposal Program is simple to learn but a nightmare to win. Designed to be irritatingly funny, extremely chaotic, and engineered to actively punish comfortable card game strategies. You’ll be forced to improvise, adapt, and think fast if you want to survive.

Carefully crafted abilities and gameplay ensure standard strategies like hoarding cards are a fast track to elimination. It’s beautifully unpredictable. Survival here isn't about meticulous planning, it's about your knack for turning sudden disaster into a strategic advantage (now this is starting to sound like true corporate jargon).

And as you’re playing, you’ll notice little stories at the bottom of each card. Those aren't just random filler text.  They’re tiny, painful, slices of corporate life.

When a card hits the table, someone reads one of those snippets, and suddenly everyone's erupting in laughter, sharing their own "you won't believe what happened to me at work" moments.

Ultimately, that's what this game is truly about, turning the shared trauma of modern corporate life into one big, cathartic, and hilariously unhinged group therapy session.

—description from the publisher

Crownfall is a fast, brutal medieval 4X strategy game designed for players who want deep decision-making without the 4-hour playtime.
You’ll command one of the rival Houses fighting for control of a fractured kingdom, expanding your territory, forging fragile alliances, waging sudden betrayals, and outmaneuvering your opponents in a race for the throne.

Built from the ground up to deliver big-box strategy in under 120 minutes, Crownfall blends territory control, asymmetric faction powers, resource management, and player-driven conflict into a tight, replayable experience. Every session tells a story of ambition, betrayal, and shifting power.

—description from the designer

boardgame: キャンプ場の殺人鬼 (Murder Mystery Mini: Campground Murder Case)
description:
A group of about a dozen second-year students from Kuritaru High School had come to a lakeside campsite to film a movie to be screened at the school festival. After smoothly completing their schedule for the day and finishing dinner, everyone spent their time at their respective lodges, enjoying their time as they pleased.

But their pleasant time instantly turned into a nightmare. A killer wearing a hockey mask appeared and brutally murdered their classmates one by one!

The few who survived managed to escape to the administration building and meet up with another group who had evacuated earlier. Just when they thought they could finally relax, they discovered a hockey mask and a hatchet near the building, likely discarded by the killer.

"Could the killer have snuck in among us?" To ensure their own safety and avenge their murdered classmates, they must find the real culprit as soon as possible! --Gentosha's murder mystery is now available in a repackaged version from Group SNE!


    This is a repackaged version of the work released by Gentosha in December 2021.


There are no changes to the content from the previous version.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Petits crimes entre amis: Sherlock Holmes
description:
Petits crimes entre amis: Sherlock Holmes - Little Crimes Among Friends: Sherlock Holmes,

To solve the mystery, draw clues and get the other players talking.
Beware: you all have a secret to hide!

10 enigmatic mysteries
10 successful game nights
120 cards + 1 book

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Drakesun Rebellion Faction Deck
description:
Born in the shadows of a solar eclipse, the warlike dragon-riders of the Drakesun Rebellion toppled the barbarians who held
them in bondage. Now, emboldened by their draconic allies, they roam Itharia, toppling those governments they see as exploitative.
Though some suspect the Rebellion have started seeing enemies in every shadow..

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Summoner Wars (Second Edition): Guild Dwarves Faction Deck
description:
No one can match the grumbling Guild Dwarves when it comes to feats of battlefield engineering. These dour builders can deploy and redeploy combat structures with ease. Their warriors are trained to interact with these constructions in battle, using them to bolster their attacks, enhance their maneuverability, and confound the plans of their enemies.

—description from the publisher
This is a test item.

In the wake of a forgotten industrial age, the world is still, beautiful, and reclaiming its rusted remains. Welcome Strangers is a card-driven journey game where players navigate a landscape both familiar and strange where nature and relic coexist.

Your hand of cards is your Traveling Party, a mix of Trailblazaers, Guides, Pilgrims, Hearthbound, and Chroniclers, each with their own paths, kin, and desired destinations. Over the course of the game, you will lead these travelers to those destinations, playing them into your Journal—a 12-slot record of your trek across the Four Horizons (N,S,E,W). Upon arrival you will receive a blessing (a once off power) or score points based on their style:

Trailblazers for collections of Horizons you've previously travelled, 
Hearthbound for colletions of Kin you have previously traveled with,
Chroniclers for collections of Traveler classes,
Pilgrims for their distance from other cards in your Journal,
Guides for a combination of your Journal and travelers currently in your party.

At each of the Four Horizons lives a face-up deck of Location cards. These set the current travel conditions, which can be of benefit or provide a challange to visiting the location. At each Location you'll also find a few travelers waiting face-up. Once at a location, and after scoring points, you will then exchange 1-2 card/s from your hand with card/s at the location,rearranging your party for the next leg of your journey and potentially completing the event at the Location by discarding the right symbols. If you do complete an event, you claim the location, flip it, and it becomes an boon, or an end game goal.

At the end of each turn you will visit the inn and draw or discard cards back to 5 travelers.

Welcome Strangers is a game of hand management and tablesu building, where the cards are multi-use and precious resources.

—description from the designer

Stracciatella is a Battle-Line inspired domino game for two players where players try their best to win 4/7 of the available columns by assembling Dice Poker hands.

Dominoes can be played both lengthwise in a single column or widthwise across two columns. Just be careful you don't create too many holes as these will need filling to be eligible for winning.

—description from the designer

Try to win as many points as possible by making up voices for the characters you have been given.

How to play: 
Each player takes a good look at their character card without showing it to anyone else. Then the casting director collects all the cards, shuffles them and places them face up in the centre of the table.
The casting director then chooses a line for the other players to recite. 
The players have to take turns reciting the line in the voice they have created for the character on their card.
Players need to change their voice to reproduce the voice they’ve imagined for their character as faithfully as possible.
Once all the players have said the line, the casting director has to find who each character card belongs to, in no particular order, based only on the players’ performances. The trainee voice actors tell the casting director whether or not their hunch was right. Each player correctly identified by the casting director gets a point, and the casting director also wins a point for every character they correctly match.

End of the game
When all the players have been the casting director at least twice, compare scores. The player with the most points wins the game.

boardgame: Games of John Gilpin, Rainbow backgammon and Bewildered travellers
description:
Three games within a wooden folding game box, all using a spinner rather than a die.

John Gilpin is based on English poet William Cowper's 1782 comic ballad "The Diverting History of John Gilpin". The poem tells how Gilpin and his wife and children became separated during a journey to the Bell Inn, Edmonton, after Gilpin loses control of his horse which bolts and carries him ten miles further to the town of Ware. The board for John Gilpin consists of a track in the shape of a spoked wheel. Any number of players can play. Each starts his piece on any space on the wheel rim with a horsehead. Using a spinner instead of a die, players each move their pieces clockwise around the rim. If they land exactly on the end of a spoke they may thereafter move inward along the spoke hoping to end exactly on the Inn at the centre of the wheel. If they overleap the inn, the continue outward on the opposite spoke, and clockwise around the wheel once more. First player to end exactly on the Inn wins.

Rainbow Backgammon is a luck-based piece-capturing game for 2-4 players, played on a 36-space circular track of rainbow-hued backgammon-like triangles. Every third triangle (12 in total) points to an individual circle in the centre of the board. Scoring token are placed on these 12 circles. Players start with two pieces each, one space apart, on their own side of the track. Each player spins the spinner and moves one of his pieces anticlockwise the number of spaces spun. If he lands on a space pointing to a scoring token, he collects the token. If he lands on an opponent's piece, the piece is bumped backwards to the space before the leftmost corner of the board. The winner is the player who collects the most scoring tokens.

Bewildered Travellers is a luck-based game for 2-4 players, each trying to find his way home at night from a dinner party at a country house, across a fog-bound moor. Played on a board of 17 x 9 spaces, each player starts his piece in once corner of the board an attempts to land exactly on the space in the opposite corner. On odd-numbered rounds, each player moves a number of spaces, orthogonally or diagonally, spun on the spinner. In even numbered rounds, each player moves a number of spaces equal to either of the numbers on the edges of the board, according to his current rank and file. Players can either attempt to get home on odd numbered rounds using the spinner, or on even numbered rounds by being on one of the two spaces whose rank or file numbers would get him directly home. Only one piece can occupy a space: a player forced to land on another piece loses his turn. A player leaping over another piece send the latter back to its starting square.

Xochimilco is the game of floating gardens. Learn the ancient system of creating "chinampas," avoid the dangerous "Ahuizotl," a wicked sea monster, or discover the "Axolotl," another marvelous and benevolent aquatic creature.
By placing tiles and workers, navigate the canals to lead your workers to flower gardens, craft workshops, or fishing in the lake's waters, and then sell your products at the "Tianguis" (market).
Accumulate points, marvel at the sight of a lake full of color, life, and joy, and enjoy the richness of its traditions.
designers: Juan Francisco Castillo (Aka Juan de Lobos)

description:
You're cordially invited to the ultimate party!

Froggy Party is a high-hopping, easy-to-learn card game where the whole family—yes, even the littlest partygoers aged two and up—can join the fun. Players take turns drawing cards from the deck and growing their gala of stylishly dressed animals. Get three Frogs into your party and you’ll take the crown—but watch out! These slippery celebrities have a way of leaping into someone else’s soirée when you least expect it!

Perfect for 2–5 players, Froggy Party is where giggles, glamour, and a dash of mischief keep the fun going until the very last card.

—description from the designer

CS:NEW transforms the tension, strategy, and teamplay of modern tactical shooters into a deeply immersive board game experience. Two teams compete in asymmetric missions such as Bomb Defusal, Hostage Rescue, or Team Deathmatch, using hidden movement, line of sight mechanics, and real-time pressure to outsmart each other.

Core Gameplay Features

Hidden movement with bluffing elements using dual-token positioning

Tactical action points system per player unit (move, aim, reload, throw grenades, etc.)

Realistic ranged combat simulation via custom dice (damage, precision, armor penetration, reactions)

Line of sight–based combat triggering, including chance-based interrupts

Map-specific terrain, including elevation levels and wall-based cover

Buy Phase at the start of each round: Equip units secretly with weapons, grenades, and armor

Full round economy system inspired by Counter-Strike 2's win/loss income mechanics

What Makes CS:NEW Unique

1:1 adaptation of Counter-Strike’s tactical gameplay in board game format

Intense asymmetric missions, real-time pressure via sand timers

True team vs. team gameplay for up to 8 players (4v4)

Each player controls two operatives, introducing deep tactical planning

—description from the designer

Timeline: Games & Leisure is a card game in which each card depicts a historical event, invention, or discovery on both sides, with the year in which that event occurred (or the invention or discovery was made) on only one side. Players take turns placing a card from their hand in a row on the table. After placing the card, the player reveals the date on it. If the card was placed correctly with the date in chronological order with all other cards on the table, the card stays in place; otherwise the card is removed from play, and the player takes another card from the deck.

The first player to get rid of all their cards by placing them correctly wins. If multiple players go out in the same round, then everyone else is eliminated from play, and each of those players are dealt one more card for another round of play. If only one player has no cards after a bonus round, they win; otherwise play continues until a single player goes out.

Multiple Timeline sets can often be combined into a single game.

Black Cat Jack is a tactical card-drafting duel inspired by Blackjack — but twisted into something far more chaotic, strategic, and deliciously wicked.

Before each match, both players strengthen their decks by drafting new cards from a shared pool. With over 70 unique card types, every draft shapes a completely different strategy: manipulate values, sabotage enemy slots, multiply your stacks, or bide your time for the ultimate combo.

Every match is played as Best of 3 rounds — or ends instantly if a player runs out of coins.
Each round begins with a random target number between 21 and 25.
Your goal: build the closest total using only four basic slots and two effect slots… without busting.
Win the round, reclaim your wager. Lose — and your coins vanish.

Two effect slots provide powerful, randomly assigned effects each match, but they must be paid for — creating constant tension between spending coins to gain an advantage or saving them for survival.

At the heart of the game lies the legend of Jack, a cunning black cat, and Jinx, his elusive great love. If a player manages to place both cat cards next to each other with empty adjacent slots, the match ends instantly in a dramatic, story-driven victory. (If the opponent doesn´t match the right counter card and win the game instead)

Black Cat Jack blends press-your-luck tension, strategic deckbuilding, tempo management, and bluffing into a darkly comedic dueling experience where every decision matters — and every coin feels precious.

—description from the designer

In Thorns you’re playing as a sentient plant. A crazy scientist has made you in the basement of its mansion. You'll be causing mischief in the mansion while interacting with your surroundings, gathering boosts, earning points and thwarting your fellow players.

Each turn, players get to choose 1 of 3 dice to do an action (grow, strengthen, branch, bite, ...), the other dice remain available for the next turn so players can plan ahead.

To play, you'll need 1 D6 and for each player a gamesheet & pen.

—description from the designer

boardgame: The overland mail from England to India
description:
A board game for a race between players as they attempt to follow the nearly 8000 mile route of the overland mail from England to India. Players start their race from Southampton and finish up at Calcutta, after crossing the desert from Cairo to the Red Sea; views along the way include the River Tagus, Gibraltar, Algiers, Malta, Alexandria, Cairo (where the overland section begins the crossing of the sands to Suez) and then on to Calcutta by the Red Sea, Jeddah, Mocha and Ceylon. The game was originally issued with counters and tetotum.

Board consists of two sheets running lengthwise atop one another creating a panoramic view of lithographic images. The continuous strips are mounted on a linen backing to form eight panels which were intended to be used as a board when the sections are unfolded.

The images in the game were based on a series of drawings illustrating noteworthy views along the route of the overland mail from England to India, on exhibit from 1850-1853 at the Illustrated Galley in London. The moving diorama was painted by J. Absolon, T. Grieve, W. Telbin, and J.F. Herring and was also issued in a book entitled: The route of the overland mail to India. London : Atchley, 1850. According to Muir the game may have been sold as a souvenir at the Illustrated Gallery.

Throne Of Etherwyn is a card-based board game set in a fantasy kingdom.

Over 12 rounds, players will play cards on their boards. When a card is played, all cards in play for all players of the same faction will gain prestige and improve their rewards.

During the game you will build an engine of influence, prestige and victory points, gain powerful continuous effects and unlock bonuses at the end of the game.

Each time you play a faction card, you gain dominance with that faction. The more powerful the card, the more dominance you gain.

At the end of the game, players earn victory points depending on the final score rewards on their cards, the points earned during the game, and their position on the domination tracks.

—description from the designer

In Wilds, players build an ecosystem map using tiles and introduce various animals onto it. The animals are used to play out a game of predator and prey and to control valuable regions.

On your turn, you'll add a tile to the map, introduce an animal onto it, then activate any other animals you already have in play.

Each animal has a unique set of actions, abilities and terrain they can move through. Points are earned throughout the game as prey forage and predators hunt rival pieces.

The game end is triggered when all tiles have been played. A final friendly round is played where pieces cannot be removed.

Bonus points are earned for having the most animals in a given region.

A different set of animals can be used in every game. Specific sets can be chosen to increase or decrease the level of player interaction and conflict.

—description from the publisher

White and black wooden discs for a 5th and 6th player for Can't Stop Travel Edition
Disc diameter = 25 mm, 7 mm thick
One set includes 10 white and 10 black wooden discs.

The wooden discs are slightly too small for the current Franjos Can't Stop Mountaineering Edition. The discs in that edition have a diameter of 30 mm.

—description from the publisher

Wales has a long tradition of legendary beasts, stubborn bowmen, and neighbourhood disputes with the English. The longbow itself is famously Welsh, feared across medieval Europe.

As for the dragon? Let’s say she’s extremely Welsh folklore-adjacent, and very motivated to remind everyone of her homeland’s proud tradition of setting things on fire.

Together, the Longbowmen and their Dragoness bring a completely different tone to the struggle for Sherwood. They strike from afar, scorch anything foolish enough to gather in large groups, and generally behave like the kind of faction that makes everyone else say: “Wonderful. Just what we needed.”

We’ll reveal the mechanics separately, for now, enjoy the moment. Dragoness is here.

—description from the publisher

ONE PIECE: Dawn of Liberation is a new cooperative board game based on the globally popular anime and manga franchise. Developed in partnership with Toei Animation, the game adapts the Land of Wano arc into a large-scale, narrative-driven adventure for one to four players.

The project brings the world of ONE PIECE to an expansive tabletop format, combining character-driven crew building, mission-based progression, and resource management. Players form teams using characters from the Land of Wano storyline and work together to complete objectives, gather materials, and recruit allies on their journey toward confronting Kaido. Designed for multi-hour sessions, the game also incorporates a built-in pause-and-resume system that allows groups to break up the experience without losing progress.

—description from the publisher

The Supreme Kaiser has just been assassinated! And it didn't take long for the sub-leaders to tear the Empire apart like starving vultures...
As a loyalist sub-officer, you narrowly escaped the firing squad by fleeing with a small ragtag army.
After many months of wandering, fate finally smiles upon you! Reports from your scouts point to a forgotten valley, far from any civilization: a dead land, ravaged by centuries of past wars.
This land soaked in violence and suffering will provide all the resources necessary to build a new empire! The trials will be numerous, for your army's morale and your popularity rating are at their lowest!
Every debacle will increase your dishonor, and you will end up at the end of a bayonet or in a maw full of razor-sharp teeth!
On the contrary, your victories will reduce your dishonor and attract the sympathy of your people, to the point where even the armies of neighboring empires will rally to your cause!
Will you be a builder who erects vast industrial oceans surrounded by great impenetrable walls, a skilled tactician who raises mighty armies, or perhaps will you let yourself be tempted by the dark art of necroscience...
But remember one thing: Machiavellianism and cruelty will be your only allies to become the next Supreme Kaiser!

—description from the publisher

Le Kaiser suprême vient d'être assassiné ! Et il n'a pas fallu longtemps pour que les sous-dirigeants déchirent l'Empire comme des vautours affamés...
En tant que sous-officier loyaliste, vous avez échappé de justesse au peloton d'exécution en prenant la fuite avec une petite armée disparate.
Après de nombreux mois d'errance, le destin vous sourit enfin ! Les rapports de vos éclaireurs vous indiquent une vallée oubliée, loin de toute civilisation : 
une terre morte, ravagée par des siècles de guerres passées.
Cette terre gorgée de violence et de souffrance vous apportera toutes les ressources nécessaires pour bâtir un nouvel empire ! Les épreuves seront nombreuses, car le moral de votre armée et votre cote de popularité sont au plus bas ! 
Chaque débâcle augmentera votre déshonneur et vous finirez au bout d'une bayonnette ou dans une gueule pleine de dents acérées !
À contrario, vos victoires diminueront votre déshonneur et attireront la sympathie de votre peuple, au point même où les armées des empires voisins se rallieront à votre cause !
Serez-vous un bâtisseur qui érigera de grands océans industriels entourés de grandes murailles infranchissables, un habile tacticien qui lèvera de grandes armées, ou peut-être vous laisserez-vous tenter par l'art sombre de la nécroscience...
Mais n'oubliez pas une chose : le machiavélisme et la cruauté seront vos seuls alliés pour devenir le prochain Kaiser suprême !

—description from the publisher (French)

Deal into a rousing a game of strategy and tough decisions! With 10 cards in your hand, can you be the first to craft runs of 4, 3, 2, and 1 in different suits? Keep a close eye on your opponents' actions, predict their next moves and discard strategically to thwart their plans. This game offers a quick, lively blend of strategy, anticipation, prediction and tough decisions.

In this fast moving dice game, everyone is trying to be the first to 'wake up' their five sleeping campers, then get them all to RunAway! from the approaching bear! You can play nice, or you can also bonk your opponent's campers back to sleep before they can RunAway!

Each player has 5 camper meeples, numbered 3-7, that start asleep (laying down). On each turn a player will roll two dice. They can use the dice individually or in combination to:
  "Wake up" stand up their meeple matching the value rolled
  "Run Away" remove the awake meeple matching the value rolled from play.
  "Bonk" lay down an opponents meeple matching the value rolled.

The first player to remove all of their meeples wins the game.

Liar’s Dominoes is a fast, bluff-driven guessing game you can play absolutely anywhere—no table required. Whether you’re standing in a line, waiting in a lobby, or riding in a car, each player simply holds a few dominoes in hand and the tension builds from there.

Players secretly check their tiles and make daring predictions about how many times certain values appear across everyone’s dominoes. Each new bid pushes the stakes higher until someone calls a lie—then all tiles are revealed to expose the truth.

Because blank sides are wild and hands shrink over time, every round becomes tighter, faster, and more psychological. Read your opponents, bluff with confidence, and be the first to seize victory in a deduction game built for pockets, backpacks, and crowded places.

—description from the designer

Siege Works is a solo roll-and-write wargame that captures the art and tension of siege warfare. Take command of your engineers, construct artillery batteries, and lay mines as you dig trenches all the way to the fortress walls.

Experience fast-paced, tactical gameplay driven by streamlined resource management and dice-based decisions. Each turn forces you to choose: press the attack, dig in deeper, or regroup your troops before the enemy counterattacks.

The Rise of Napoleon expansion chronicles the French Republic’s sieges from Toulon to Danzig, introducing new mechanics such as supply lines and encirclement, expanded naval interactions, and a host of dynamic Events. This French Campaign shifts the perspective to the attackers, featuring four new maps and fresh strategies for waging war from beyond the walls.

—description from the publisher
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Bellum Ultimum is a fantasy strategy card game played on a tactical hex-grid map. Each player commands a hero who navigates the board to defeat rivals or complete scenario objectives.
The core engine is a constructed deck built around the hero’s Species and Class. Every deck includes powerful generic cards plus specialised class- and species-specific abilities that define each character’s playstyle. Players can further customise their build with Equipment, Potions, Scrolls, and Creatures, shaping the board state and unlocking new lines of play.
Cards are multi-use, forcing players to make meaningful decisions every round. A deck functions as the hero’s stamina—depleting the player empowers manoeuvres like movement and attacks, or triggers special effects, and opens high-impact tactical options.
The combination of deck management, positional play on the hex map, and terrain-driven tactics creates a deep, replayable strategy experience. At its core, Bellum Ultimum is a customizable deck-builder that lets players craft unique heroes and bring their favourite miniatures—or the included tokens—onto the battlefield.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

boardgame: Amelia Davies: Le Trésor de Barbe Noire
description: In this cooperative puzzle / treasure hunt game, players work together to solve riddles in over 50 documents in order to find the lost treasure of Blackbeard.

My Puzzle Adventure, the puzzle-game that tells stories!

It is a collection of puzzles in which children live thrilling adventures. They look for the pieces to turn over one by one and discover an incredible story by putting the puzzle away. Will you succeed in the adventure?

Lil' Detective is the third opus of My Puzzle Adventure Collection.

—description from the publisher

A simple roll the dice and move board game themed after Tarzan, the fictional character created by Edgar Rice Burroughs. The board game was manufactured and released in Greece probably in the late 70s. It can be played from 2-4 players. When players land on a square where there is an image, they must follow the instructions in the manual.

The winner is the first player who reaches number 100 on the board.

Destiny is divine ... Deception is human

Gods' Will is a thematic social deduction board game which combines elements from the 2 most popular games of this genre, adding variable special powers and track movement on top!

Endless disputes among the Gods of Olympus, fueled by their complex and confrontational nature, have divided them into two opposing alliances: the Followers of Light and the Servants of Darkness; while a few remain Loners, answering only to themselves.
As a God or Goddess, you will wield a unique divine ability. Through strategy, deception, and influence, you must bend fate to your will - guiding Ventures to empower your alliance and orchestrate the downfall of the opposing leader.

The game unfolds over two phases.

- Venture Phase: Each team works to sway Ventures in its favor.
- Expulsion Phase: All players vote to push a God or Goddess away from Olympus.

Victory lies upon the order in which Gods and Goddesses face Light & Darkness on the track.

Every action reveals your true side! Show your light - or your darkness.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
"When the gods are bored... they play games. And sometimes, they play with fate. In this expansion for Vedrfölnir, the Aesir join the game... but they don't come empty-handed.
 Sixteen legendary artifacts, drawn from the most powerful relics of the Norse pantheon, enter the game."

Each player receives 1 Artifact card at the start of the game, which they can reveal according to the conditions indicated. The effects vary between one-time and permanent activations. Once used (or if stipulated), some artifacts are removed from the game.

—description from the publisher

Here, America cheers in unison
My fabulous handcrafted mocktails.
America adores these divine nectars...
 Distilled by my Shaker...
I am Brandy Flanigan, and it is my duty to explain the rules of the game. We are going to take part in a mixology competition...
Welcome! In BOOZY BRAVADO, the competition to find the most talented Welcome! To BOOZY BRAVADO, the competition to find the most talented
mixologist of the year. We've come from all over the world to compete, mixing textures and
flavours, revisiting recipes to create the most delicious mocktails.
Obviously, I'm here to win! Mocktails are an art form. And I paint with a shaker! "
At the end of the competition, the jury will award the mixologist who has obtained the highest score.
"Welcome aboard! Your mocktail is ready, and so are your troubles!
I'm Isaac Manhattan and I'll be guiding you through the process so that your desire to play is
relative to my mocktails."
‘We'll need ingredients.’

Create the best mocktails by combining textures and volumes over three rounds, with the drink bags rotating between players. Each turn, draw a drink and choose whether to place it in a glass, discard it, or use an ability (shared or specific to your character). Make your Signature Mocktail and the Required Mocktail, and optimise your glasses to score maximum points with the order of ingredients, decorations, and full glasses.
The mixologist with the highest score wins!

—description from the publisher

Vita Nova is a board game for 2 to 4 players set in a near-future utopian society ruled by sentient artificial intelligences. Each player is a biomechanical engineer who uses cards, cells, and blood to build their human prototype. Cells act as workers that activate organs, while blood is used to obtain and upgrade modules. Every organ you connect provides abilities, resources, or points, and the combinations between them create synergies that strengthen your laboratory.

The game is played over three phases, each offering scoring opportunities. At the end of the final phase, the player who has assembled the most efficient prototype and accumulated the highest score is declared the winner.

—description from the designer

Air, Land and Naval Combat in 1989

Prepare for an action-packed, high-speed showdown across Scandinavia and the Norwegian Sea, all set on an expansive map stretching north of the Arctic Circle. What's more, it's a sister title to the popular Red Strike, sharing over 90% of the rules which make it so easy to play and links that make it feel like one big, exciting war! It's so exciting to see Finland and Sweden join the fight! They're bringing fresh new fronts and some incredible air and naval options. Precise detection, lightning-fast interception, and land-air-sea interaction drive every turn as pulse-pounding air duels, nail-biting naval hunts, and ground clashes unfold in an awe-inspiring simultaneous show of force. Get ready for an epic battle with friends, as Northern Strike is designed for two-player or team-based play! It captures the thrilling struggle for the North, where every fjord, airbase, and convoy route holds the key to victory.

Northern Strike simulates an operational-level conflict in the Boreal-Arctic region during a hypothetical 1989 clash between NATO and the Warsaw Pact. The theater encompasses the rugged terrain of Northern Norway, Sweden, Finland, and the Soviet Union. Each game turn represents two days of real time, subdivided into a Strategic Stage, an Administrative Stage, three Action Stages, and an End Stage. This structure facilitates a complex combined arms experience where ground, air, and naval operations occur simultaneously.

Logistics serves as the game's foundation, centered on the management of Supply Points (SP). These points are essential for executing air missions, entering advanced combat formations, and repairing infrastructure. During the Administrative Stage, players assign units to specific modes, such as Frontline or Reserve for ground forces and Offensive or Interception for air units. These modes dictate a unit's eligibility to act during the three subsequent Action Stages.

The ground war utilizes Assault Units, such as armor and infantry, which are supported by specialized headquarters (HQ), artillery, and mobile SAM units. Combat is resolved by units adopting Combat Formations—including Deliberate Assault or Hasty Defense—which modify combat strength and provide column shifts on the results table at the cost of supply points. Movement is restricted by Zones of Control (ZOC) exerted by large formations and Zones of Patrol (ZOP) created by Special Forces or "Stay Behind" units. These irregular units can conduct clandestine missions like interdiction raids and ambushes to sever enemy lines of communication

Air operations are governed by sortie rates, limiting how often an air unit can fly per turn. To enhance survivability, Swedish and Finnish forces utilize Dispersal Air Bases (DAB), allowing aircraft to operate from road segments if primary airfields are neutralized. Naval warfare involves Surface Action Groups and Carriers, the latter acting as mobile air bases. Maritime strategy is deeply affected by fjords, which offer high defensive value but restrict naval mobility, and the use of minefields to block strategic ports.

The political dimension is integrated through STAVKA orders and the World Opinion (WO) track. The Warsaw Pact player draws cards to determine the political stance toward neutral Finland and Sweden. The entry of these neutral nations into the war is decided by Political Alignment Checks, which reflect internal power struggles triggered by external aggression.

Conflict escalation is monitored via the DEFCON Track. While players can authorize Tactical Nuclear Attacks or chemical warfare to achieve battlefield breakthroughs, such actions carry extreme penalties in Victory Points and World Opinion. If the situation escalates to DEFCON 1, a total nuclear exchange occurs, ending the game immediately with no victor. Standard victory is instead achieved by amassing points through the control of key locations.

—description from the designer

In this mini expansion, you’ll discover how to boost your recipes with special sides and the great-grandmother’s signature sauces. Add even more flavor to the competition and surprise everyone with these new effects!

A version of the holographic game played by R2-D2 and Chewbacca in Star Wars.

Playable with Looney pyramids, or pieces built from Lego blocks, existing Dejarik pieces, or other easily improvised pieces. Each player has 4 pieces (with values 1, 2, 3, 4) in their player color.

The simple board is easy to print or draw by hand.

4 randomizing cubes or tokens are also needed.
