This game was the free holiday gift from Looney Labs in December, 2025.

Hey there Maestro! OA new season is upon us and it's time to build your orchestra! Audition musicians into your ensemble using a unique mancala-style system. Each section - Strings, Woodwinds, Brass, and Percussion - will score differently. Some of your musicians will go on to become Virtuosos and earn you bonus points. But hire too many and you'll be overburdened with Stand-bys, which will lose you points. Hire the right musicians to complete your compositions and have the best opening night!

—description from the designer

Dahi is a general knowledge game played individually or in teams with at least 2 players.
Inside the box, you will find Dahi cards, İcat Çıkar cards, a sand timer, a pencil, a scorepad, and a book titled Doğunun Aklına Hâkim İnsanlar, which introduces the people featured on the Dahi cards.

HOW TO PLAY

Each player is dealt an equal number of Dahi cards. After flipping the sand timer, players take turns reading the clues on their cards. The opposing player listens to these clues and tries to guess the correct answer. Players earn points for each correct guess based on the value assigned to the clue. After 30 seconds, points are added up, and the player with the highest score wins the round.

DAHİ CARDS

• Each clue on Dahi cards has a fixed point value.

- A player who finds the correct answer after the first clue earns 10 points.
- After the second clue, 8 points.
- After the third clue, 6 points.
- After the fourth clue, 4 points.
- After the fifth clue, 2 points.

• A player who still cannot find the correct answer after all 5 clues earns no points.
• Players may make only 1 guess after each clue.
• For each Dahi card, players may make a maximum of 3 guesses.

İCAT ÇIKAR CARDS

• İcat Çıkar cards contain inventions that grant players various bonuses. For every 10 points a player earns in total, they draw 1 İcat Çıkar card and use the bonus on that card in the next round.

• At the beginning of each new round, the winning player or team starts with an İcat Çıkar card. The player decides whether or not to use the bonus on the card.

• After being read, İcat Çıkar cards are placed back at the bottom of the deck and the game continues.

HOW TO DETERMİNE THE WİNNER

• When all Dahi cards in players hands have been used, the player or team that has won the most rounds is declared the winner.

Cyberpunk: Edgerunners - Hunted is a TIMER-BASED BOARD GAME where players take on the roles of EDGERUNNERS trying to escape while being chased by the Maelstrom gang, NCPD, and the notorious Adam Smasher.

It can be played solo or with up to four players COLLABORATIVELY.
This game emphasizes coordination, quick decision-making, and survival under pressure.

Players must EXPLORE Night City, BATTLE enemies, and PERFORM missions before finding the escape.
During the Edgerunners’ turn, players act in any order, perform actions and move on the map.
During the Enemy phase, enemies relentlessly pursue and attack the players.
 Successfully collect gigs and REACH THE EXTRACTION POINT before the timer runs out to claim victory in this adrenaline-pumping race against time.

—description from the publisher

*pssssst* Come a little closer!

The Elder Witch has deemed you worthy of an invitation to her secret workshop! Open the doors to a whole new level of magic with the Secrets of Alchemy Expansion (built into the Mystic Moon Deluxe Edition) ?? Learn valuable tricks of the trade that teach you how to convert regular ingredients into materials guaranteed to shine like stars! (And if you produce a little bit of sludge as a byproduct, it probably couldn’t hurt anyone, right? ?)

With the Secrets of Alchemy Expansion, you’ll be able to use Alchemy to convert Ingredients into new materials such as Gold and Crystals. When you visit the Elder Witch’s Shop, you will encounter Alchemy cards, each of which displays a Formula and Products.

You may perform Alchemy while visiting the Elder Witch’s Shop by discarding the required Ingredients indicated in the Formula of the top card of the Alchemy deck, then collecting the Products listed on the card. You’ll add Gold and Crystals you collect to your play area, and you’ll immediately place Sludge you collect in the Garden.

When you perform Alchemy, you will also acquire the Alchemy card you used for later use. Place it next to your Potion Book, below your Witch’s Hut. You may only have one Alchemy card in your play area at a time, and if you acquire a second one, you must return one of them to the bottom of the Alchemy deck. If you have an Alchemy card in your play area when you visit the Elder Witch’s Shop, you may choose to either perform Alchemy using the top card of the Alchemy deck or the Alchemy card in your play area.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mystic Moon: Curse of the Zodiac
description:
This Mystic Moon Expansion features 2 new playable characters, and a brand new mechanic - Blessings and Curses!

New constellations have appeared in the night sky, and an evil witch has used them to cast a curse that binds you to the whims of the zodiac. Now, every sign brings with it a unique affliction. Clever witches who read the signs can brew an elusive elixir, lift the curse, and unlock powerful new blessings from the stars! But you must be quick, because doom awaits those who cannot lift their curse before the Mystic Moon reaches its final phase… ?

When you play Mystic Moon with the Curse of the Zodiac Expansion, you begin the game cursed! As long as you are cursed, you will face a different disadvantage each round. After you successfully lift your curse, you receive a blessing and get to enjoy a new advantage each round!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Mystic Moon: Vinyl Figure Foil Promo Card Set
description:
These cards are included with each Mystic Moon vinyl figure in both the exclusive vinyl figure set and the Mystic Moon vinyl figure blind box series (which will be available as an add-on in the pledge management phase, then released next year)! 8 of the characters are paired with a unique potion card that isn’t in the game, the Elder Witch is paired with a new Alchemy card, and Jinx is paired with a new curse.

—description from the publisher

In The Devil & Benedict Arnold, players navigate a crucial three-year historical period, when the fate of Benedict Arnold – and the American Revolution itself – hangs in the balance. This easy-to-play game plunges you into the complex situations facing the most controversial figure of the Revolution, when devilish dilemmas led one of the war’s greatest heroes to abandon the cause, and switch sides. From his highest point at Saratoga to his unmasking at West Point, you’ll experience and appreciate Arnold’s internal struggles like never before with… you-know-who!

Game Components

     54 Dilemma Cards
     18 Conflict Cards (two sets of 9)
      4 Insight Counters (two sets of 2)
      1 Conflict Coin
      1 Rules Booklet


—description from the designer

Years ago, a massive explosion tore apart the towering mountain range in the Wilds. In Leviathan Wilds: Shattered Peak, the motley brigade of climbers will ascend to these lofty heights to discover the cause. This expansion features eleven new leviathans, including winged serpents, raging storm clouds, and creatures that lurk within the very mountains themselves! A new, d8 crystal die teleports when struck, adding a new layer of challenge.

Three new climbers join the ranks, including Recoil the sharpshooter and the thrill-seeking Dare, alongside three new classes, including the concoction-brewing Alchemist and the Geomancer, who manipulates crystals near and far.

—description from the publisher
Travel box version of the game Color Addict where the aim of the game is to get rid of all your cards as quickly as possible by playing the right color, the right word or both.

Well-illustrated popup book, with about 7 holes. Each hole has simple instructions, noting the hole's par & a penalty for failing to avoid an obstacle.

Comes with small foam balls & cardstock clubs.

—user summary

This is the world's first KARUTA using famous classical Chinese poems! You can play like Chihayafuru using Chinese poems. This version uses 40 famous Chinese poems. There are 40 reading cards and 40 playing cards packed in a box.

The game is comprised of two decks of cards, the Reader deck and the Player deck. Cards in the Reader deck contain short poems or poetry quotations while cards in the Player deck contain only the second part of these poems. To begin the game, a Reader is chosen (or you may opt to use the Reader app) and the players are divided into two teams sitting/standing on opposite sides of the table or play area. The Reader shuffles the Reader deck and the Players mix, split evenly the Player deck between the teams and place them face-up in 3-4 rows in front of them. The Reader will read the complete poetry quotation on the reading cards and the player that finds the matching playing card first takes that card and puts it aside. If the card taken was on your opponent's side, you must give one card from your side to your opponent. The first team that has no cards left on their side is the winner (you must get rid of all your cards first, to win)

Originally available as part of D Pack from the Dice Tower 2025 Gamefound campaign.

Includes 5 Cards:
Eat Me - One of your Size S or M athletes becomes size L, keeping all other characteristics.

Drink Me - One of your Size M or L athletes becomes size S, keeping all other characteristics.

3 blank cards to create your own.

Snatch, stash, snack, and smash all the bananas to claim your crown as Monkey King! Never wait your turn in this fast-paced, energetic, and chaotic game full of monkey mayhem! Will you Trick your “so called” friends, craftily hoard your stash, or embrace the Bananarchy and launch the Banana Revolution?

Goal:
Hoard the most points in Banana cards by stealing, picking, stashing and bambozeling. The game ends when the last Banana Card is drawn.

Duration:
One round lasts about 30 mins. Two rounds suggested.

Gameplay:
There are 4 types of Monkey cards: Action, Reaction, Anytime, and Scoring. Players get dealt 5 cards to start. They use these cards to draw Banana cards or to steal from other players. There are special cards that they can use to sabotage their friends or increase their own score at the end. The game ends when all the Banana cards have been drawn, and the player with the most Banana points wins the game.

Key Features/Mechanics:

     Playing Anytime (on other players turns)
     Chain reactions of cards (Steal, Yoink, Yoink, Smash)
     Stashing to save your Bananas
     Hand drawn art by former Disney Artist
     Scoring cards enhance end game scoring


—description from the designer

The 12 Days of Tricksmas is a cooperative, campaign-style trick-taking game where players try to clear 12 holiday missions in as few hands as possible.

Each hand begins with an unusual twist: players sort the cards for someone else.

Players then play a fixed-trump, must-follow trick-taking game, trying to solve the missions on the checklist.

The team wins by completing the full checklist within six hands, with an “extraordinary” victory achievable in four.

The 12 Days of Tricksmas blends deduction, cooperation, and trick-taking. It was designed for the KCTTS's 2025 Christmas party.

—description from the publisher

"Zombie Roll or Die" combines strategy, luck, and resource management in a thrilling zombie apocalypse setting. Players must think quickly and act decisively to survive the challenges presented by the game. Whether you're a fan of dice games or zombie-themed adventures, this game offers an engaging experience for players looking to test their survival skills.

Players can choose from a roster of different playable characters, each with unique abilities that can influence gameplay.

The game involves rolling dice to determine actions, such as attacking zombies or moving through different zones. Players must strategize their moves to maximize their chances of survival.

Players need to manage resources like ammunition and health points while navigating through the game, making decisions that can affect their survival chances.

-description from designer

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: Easter Island and Zoo
description:
Zoo: To win, you must deliver food (cubes) to every animal enclosure (city) at least once! Enclosures have varying levels of danger and you must have the required minimum engine level to be able to move through them.

Easter Island: Connect all the archeological sites and collect all the moai to win the game!

boardgame: Age of Steam Expansion: New Zealand and Korea
description:
New Zealand: 2-3 player map. Players who don't deliver to every black island may not win!

Korea: 4-5 player map (revised version of the 2012 map). Higher build costs in South Korea, limited track building all around, and goods may only move in one direction across the border, or not at all (varies by Turn number)!

This board game revolves around nostalgic musical knowledge and teamwork. Roll the die, draw a card, and complete the task within 60 seconds. There are five categories: question and answer, humming, gestures, drawing, and forbidden words. Correctly guess the title and artist and you advance one step. Earn extra points by guessing the correct year.

—description from the publisher

Players want to move their unicorns to the rainbow. On their turn the active player chooses 1 token on their unicorn board to place it face down next to the pile of tokens beside the path. The other players mix the token into the pile. Now the active player takes a new token from the pile and flips it over to reveal its color. This color shows the next space on the path their unicorn will gallop to. The unicorns always move forwards towards the rainbow.

—description from the designer

Thicker Than Blood is a strategic, competitive Kenyan card game about sibling rivalry for inheritance, focusing on secrets, blackmail, and manipulation. It's designed for 3-6 players (ages 14+).

On a player's turn they will perform one action as their chosen sibling and one action as their Goon. Actions can involve gaining Secrets, Coins, shielding your own Secrets or Goon specific threats. Once you gain another player's Secrets you can expose them to cause the opponent to lose Face or Blackmail them to gain their Hooks that you can redeem to control their Actions or protect your own Face.

The aim of the game is to cause all other player's to lose their Face by using specific Actions, revealing their Secrets or calling "Family Meetings" to condemn them to lose Face. Last player standing wins.

In Rose Valley, players step into the shoes of landowners in the scenic Bulgarian valley between the Balkan Mountains and the Sredna Gora. Over the course of several years (represented as sequential seasons), you claim plots of land, build houses, and develop estates to grow roses, lavender, and vineyards. As the landscape changes with overlapping field-cards, each decision matters: will you expand your holdings, build a big house for extra points, or attempt co-ops with other players and share harvests?

At the end, you harvest your crops, gather honey from beehives, and may also perform conservation of ancient Thracian tombs — all adding up to victory points. The player with the most points from harvests, goals, and estates wins. The game combines area-building, resource collection, and strategic planning across seasons: it’s easy to learn, but offers meaningful choices each turn as the valley evolves under all players’ influence.

The gameplay spans 4 years, each consisting of 4 seasons. Every player takes one turn per season. A turn consists of the following actions in order:
1. Place a field card
2. Place a house to claim ownership of an estate
3. Take a goal card if you have fulfilled its condition
4. Take a field card

Cards can overlap, making the playing field dynamic and ever-changing. Track the changing seasons and plan which estate to expand or where to build a large house for even more points. You can also form cooperatives with other players, often earning a goal card, but you will share the harvest until the end of the game.

From turning the first sod to the final harvest — become an entrepreneur and landowner in the Rose Valley!

https://slyfoxes.games/en/rose-valley/

Mindbug - Tournament Kit (Series 5) (4 Players) gives you everything you need to host an official Mindbug Community Tournament as part of Master Series 5. The tournament winner will earn a qualification slot for the 2026 World Championship.

Included are the following cards:

1x The Pack (P6/2025)

4x Cthulhu (P4/2025)
Strength 8
[Dice Symbol] If you would gain Energy, you may gain that much life instead. When you have 7 or more life, you win the game.

Fast Cap Racing is a simple and fun racing game, where players compete by flicking customized crown caps.

Compete against other players using custom bottle caps. Create the game track using the curve markers. Each turn, the player flicks the bottle cap, trying to travel as far as possible while staying within the track boundaries. The first player to cross the finish line wins.

—description from the designer

The 4 Towers is a roll-and-write game. Climb the walls of the castle (double-sided map), defeat the enemies, and save the princess.

Choose a path on the map; when you reach a numbered space, draw a card from the previously prepared, shuffled, and face-down event deck. Resolve the event on the card: enemies to capture, bonuses, or other. When you capture enemies, some of them give you a Clue. The sum of the clues will reveal the location of the final boss. If you capture him, you win.

—description from the designer

You are alone. The space station is failing. Your only way out is to reach the spaceship before you run out of oxygen

Choose a path on the map; when you reach a numbered space, draw a card from the previously prepared, shuffled, and face-down event deck. Resolve the event on the card: enemies to capture, bonuses, or other. When you capture enemies, some of them give you a Clue. The sum of the clues will reveal the location of the final boss. If you capture him, you win.

—description from the designer

Green Thumb is a maze-themed, roll-and-write, print-and-play game.

Choose a path on the map; when you reach a numbered space, draw a card from the previously prepared, shuffled, and face-down event deck. Resolve the event on the card: enemies to capture, bonuses, or other. When you capture enemies, some of them give you a Clue. The sum of the clues will reveal the location of the final boss. If you capture him, you win.

—description from the designer

New furry players arrive in the Casino in Ratcity.

This expansion contains:

    1 Bunny deck with 12 new effects
    3 new Special Chips
    1 bag

Best Guess is the fun fast-thinking ranking card game the whole family will love to play. Highest to lowest, longest to shortest, biggest to smallest, or oldest to youngest - you can win the game with your BEST GUESS!

FAST-PACED, ENTERTAINING GUESSING GAME FOR FAMILIES
Challenge your loved ones and discover who’s the household boss in this fast-thinking higher-lower ranking card game! Enjoy hours of entertainment with the Best Guess family game.

GREAT FOR SHORT OR LONG GAMES FOR AGES 8+ YEARS
Our fun family card game is popular on family game night, even with just 2 players. Children age 8 years and over can join in. Play as many or as few rounds as you want.
 FUN, EASY-TO-PLAY, TAKE ANYWHERE TRAVEL CARD GAME
Looking for a quick-paced filler game for your next family game night? Look no further! Our hierarchy game comes in a compact box that you can take anywhere with you.

How To Play
Gather round and give each player (or team) a set of A, B and C answer cards. Grab a piece of paper and pencil to keep score. Next, shuffle the challenge cards and place them in the middle of the gameplay area where everyone can see them.

The game starts with the youngest player reading the first challenge out loud along with the 3 answers. Each player decides their answer sequence and places their answer cards face down in the order they think is correct and shouts BEST GUESS!

All players then announce their answer sequence before the correct challenge ranking is revealed. If only one player correctly guesses the sequence, they are awarded 2 points, however, if multiple players guess correctly, each player receives 1 point.

The Winner
Once you have played the agreed number of rounds, total up the scores and the winner is the player with the highest overall score!

—description from the publisher

Hitster: Latino is a party music game that celebrates the vibrant sounds of Latin music. From salsa and reggaeton to bachata, cumbia, Latin pop, and regional hits, this standalone edition of the Hitster series challenges players to guess and chronologically place iconic Latino tracks.

Players scan a music card with the free Hitster app, listen to the song, and try to identify its release year. With simple rules and quick turns, Hitster: Latino is perfect for parties, family gatherings, and fans of Latin rhythms. No music knowledge is required - just listen, feel the beat, and take your best guess!

boardgame: Chants for the Old Ones: Cthulhu Awakening
description:
In Cthulhu Awakening, your goal is to awaken the Great Cthulhu. This expansion for 2 to 4 players introduces a new central board with additional play zones, new cards, player board extensions, and objectives divided into three distinct phases, bringing fresh strategies and new ways to play the original game. Play with Cthulhu at the center of the board and serve his will and desires through the new Cthulhu Board.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chants for the Old Ones: The Last Cult
description:
The Last Cult is a solo play expansion. While Chants for the Old Ones includes a solo mode, it is played with the original Old Ones and uses mechanics similar to the multiplayer game. In contrast, The Last Cult introduces a full 8-scenario campaign designed to expand the solo experience, providing hours of additional gameplay.

In this campaign, you begin with a basic cult that grows and evolves as you progress, depending on the Cultists you choose as rewards. The story unfolds through 8 chapters, each with different objectives and even new ways to play.
In every chapter, you’ll have configuration points to set up your game and earn a final reward if you succeed.

An essential expansion for anyone who enjoys the solo experience of Chants for the Old Ones!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chants for the Old Ones: The Eye of Dagon
description:
Descend into the abyss with The Eye of Dagon, an exciting expansion that transforms the game with new objectives, fresh mechanics, and entirely new ways to play. Navigate the reef of Innsmouth with the new Hybrids, and commune with the Deep Ones to earn the favor of mighty Dagon.

The Eye of Dagon is an expansion that adds a second board and new player boards. It uses the base game rules with some modifications.

Your objective  is to invoke Dagon by performing the chants indicated on your Chant of Dagon Card. This board consists of 7 levels. Your objective card determines the minimum level you must reach to win.

The first player to perform the Chants to summon Dagon wins the game. It is essential to fulfill all the requirements on your Chant Card and to
activate the Eye of Dagon Board in one of the following two ways:

• After completing an action on a zone currently under Dagon’s gaze.
• By acting with a Sacrificer on your Dagon Board.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Chants for the Old Ones: New Orders Reckoning
description:
The New Orders expansion introduces asymmetric gameplay, letting each player control a unique cult with its own powers, rules, and playstyle. Whether you lead a secret cabal of university scholars, corrupted city enforces, or disciples of the spiders of Leng, your path to awakening will be unlike any other.
This expansion includes four fully developed factions, each with their own miniatures, abilities, cards, and components - all designed to deepen the strategy and replayability of the game while expanding the mythos with fresh horrors.

—description from the publisher

RIKA: Engines of Discord is a negotiation-driven engine builder where five asymmetric alien races must cooperate to survive escalating crises aboard a failing spaceship. Each crisis requires specific resource combinations, forcing temporary coalitions that dissolve immediately as players compete for Victory Points.

Players build resource-producing engine networks from cards, then negotiate during timed phases to form coalitions that pool outputs to meet crisis requirements. Successful coalitions distribute rewards (VP, resources, political cards), while failures inflict penalties and push the ship closer to destruction.

The central tension: participating in crises earns VP but slows engine development, while skipping crises to build engines risks catastrophic failure. Players can defect to gain political leverage, attempt solo resolution for full rewards, or betray allies mid-negotiation. Five races offer radically different strategies across tempo, economy, scaling, defense, and quality archetypes. Highest VP after three failures or final number of crisis wins.

—description from the publisher

Similar to:

     Sidereal Confluence 
     Robinson Crusoe: Adventures on the Cursed Island

A doomed land. A path through ruin. One chance to prevail.
Ruined Path is a solo deck-building adventure of danger and escalation. Draw Threats, build your deck, upgrade your skills, and battle your way toward one of several deadly bosses.
Fast turns, tight decisions, and a journey that changes every run.

—description from the designer

Smart10 is a quiz game where a player doesn't need to wait for one's turn for long.
There are ten possible answers to each question, and every player gets to answer every question.
If the answer is correct, the player gets an answer marker.
But should you answer or pass?
If you don't score your answer markers on time (and thereby pass on this round), you can end up losing them.
So if you are not 100% certain of an answer - do you gamble or do you pass?
Plays great in teams. This spinoff adds battery operated LEDs for playing in dim environments. Also features 100 new questions.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

description:
2047. A techno-feudal state has emerged. Surveillance is total. Data and the underlying networks that traffic it are the chosen mechanisms of control. A handful of megaconglomerates—governments, corporations, media syndicates, and radical fringe movements—vie for supremacy, competing to track and exploit every thought, interaction, and inference to consolidate and amplify their authority. Whoever dominates these assets owns attention, truth, and desire, and ultimately wields the most power.

You are hackers: fragmented actors driven by divergent philosophies—some seek chaos, others truth, profit, equality, or survival—all competing to bend data and systems to their will. Your targets are the sprawling factions that dominate the digital and physical landscape—the entrenched centers of control, culture, belief, and beyond—each a fortress of critical information and infrastructure. But this isn't just about stealing data for leverage. With each successful breach, you establish access points deep within faction systems—persistent backdoors that become yours to grow and manipulate. Every stealth incursion and clandestine connection moves you one step closer to completing your secret mission, but will you be the first to reach your goal?

Currency Data converts to volatile crypto that buys tools, upgrades your rig, and moves markets. Objective Data advances your hidden agenda, whispering of collapse, reform, or transcendence. Nodes establish territorial claims in the vast web of digital architecture. Every action raises Heat, putting more eyes on you and pushing you toward exposure. Overheat, and you go dark—forced to Cooldown while rivals surge ahead. In a world of competing agendas, you're also a target: opponents can hijack the data and shadow networks that you've risked it all to acquire. Form alliances, burn bridges, trade on the gray market, or hoard what you alone should own. In a world where every cataloged action strengthens the system that controls it, where feedback loops tighten, and needs are engineered before you feel them, the rush of a devastating hack is everything. So long as you don't get caught.

RECURSION places 2-6 players in a high-stakes race to infiltrate twelve powerful factions, establish territorial control through network infrastructure, and complete secret missions before rivals do the same. But completing your mission doesn't guarantee victory—you must accumulate the most Victory Points across multiple strategic dimensions.

—description from the designer

Good Kitties is a cooperative strategy game where you play as mischievous house cats bent on taking control of the house while your humans sleep. In each mission, you'll move through the house collecting supplies, building inventive contraptions, and using those contraptions to complete your mission objectives before the humans wake up. Feel like a cat as you wiggle to increase your attack power, scratch the furniture to gain supplies, and make a ruckus as you zoom through the house.

Along the way, you'll need to catch mice, both to power your cats up and to keep those sneaky mice from completing their own secret plot inside the walls. You'll also need to evade the pesky dog, who's always trying to disrupt your plans. Better yet, maybe you can find a way to blame the dog for the mess you left behind...

Good Kitties comes with 8 escalating missions that can be played in order or standalone, plus a teaching mission to help guide players through learning the rules. Start with simple missions to control the food and water, and advance towards impressive feats like creating pocket dimensions to hide in. Every mission comes in its own envelope, with custom components and fun new rules.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Operation CanCon: Canadian Music Shopping Holiday Special
description:
AVAILABLE FOR A LIMITED TIME

Something Festive This Way Comes!

Celebrate the holidays in this Operation CanCon expansion. Part series sampler, part year-end surprise, this festive adventure sends you back in search of true north classics, seasonal songs throughout Canadian history, tucked between shops by an angel… or someone stranger.

This standalone supplement adds new variants to the original trilogy’s gameplay: Holiday Jackpot, an easy, feel-good spree of discovery, and White Elephant, a suspenseful swap-or-drop challenge. You’ll search for dozens of elusive tunes, attempt a scavenger hunt, and earn deeds to mark your holiday achievements.

This booklet is offered as a token of thanks for returning OCC fans, or a sampler for newcomers to the series. You can download the full contents for free as a PDF at LockdownLudonaut.com or purchase a physical copy as a keepsake. All you need is something to write with, three six-sided dice, and the core rules from any Operation CanCon gamebook.

Bundle up! ’Tis the season for bin-diving!

—description from the designer

Colo-Colo 100 Años celebrates the centenary of the most popular Chilean football club. Players immerse themselves in the rich history of the "Cacique," revisiting legendary players, historic goals, and championship titles from the club's founding in 1925 to the present day.

The core gameplay challenges players to reconstruct the club's timeline. On their turn, a player selects a historical event card from their hand and places it into the growing timeline on the table based on when they believe the event occurred. Once placed, the card is flipped to reveal the date. If the chronological order is correct, the card remains; if incorrect, the player receives a Yellow Card. Accumulating two Yellow Cards results in a Red Card and expulsion from the game.

To add the tension of a real match, the deck includes "Wild Cards" with football-themed actions such as "VAR Review," "Penalty," or "Offside." These special cards allow players to annul opponents' moves, force them to play extra cards, or avoid penalties. The winner is the last player remaining in the game.

—description from the publisher

Conquest and Tragedy allows players to combine Triumph and Tragedy (T&T) and Conquest and Consequence (CnC) to play out the events of 1936-45 on a worldwide basis using the popular T&T system. Three partnerships oppose each other, and only one of them will win. Each partnership has one faction in the European Theater (ETO) and one in the Pacific Theatre (PTO). The game allows for 3-6 players, so players may control one or both factions of a partnership.

The Communist factions are the “USSR" (European Russia) and the “Soviets" (Siberian Russia plus Mao’s Red Chinese). With east and west Russia being a single nation, this naturally is a tight cooperative partnership with the advantage of geographical unity.

The Militarist factions are the Axis (Germany-Italy) in Europe and Japan in Asia. They have a solid but more limited partnership that suffers from severe geographical separation. They do have a military head start on the other factions.
The Capitalist factions are "Western Europe" (Britain and France plus the Asian/Pacific British Empire) and the USA, which has a seaboard in each theatre.

The Capitalists initially do not cooperate but with diplomatic effort can become the most effective partnership of all. They begin unprepared militarily but have superior economic potential.

Team play and world-wide perspective make this combined game even more interesting and challenging than the standalone games.

—description from the publisher

Rurik: Second Edition is an immersive euro-style realm-building game set during the turbulent succession crisis of 1015, bringing to life the culture, and political intrigue of Kyivan Rus’. Designed by  Stanislav Kordonskiy, illustrated by Yaroslav Radetskyi, Vlad Ricean, and art directed by Yoma. The game invites players to step into the roles of rival leaders. As the sons and claimants of Vladimir the Great struggle for legitimacy, you must build, tax, battle, and accomplish great deeds to win the support of the people and secure your place as the next ruler of Kyivan Rus’.

This new edition refines and expands the original design while staying true to the heart of what made Rurik unique. The signature “auction programming” system returns, creating tense and interactive planning moments as players openly bid for actions with their advisors.

Rurik: Second Edition enhances this core system with reworked combat and movement rules, updated Deeds and Scheme effects, personal player boards, new Landmarks and an entirely new Merits system that deepens strategic progression. Several elements from the Stone & Blade expansion have also been integrated directly into the base game.

The result is a definitive edition that offers richer decisions, smoother flow, and a deeper connection to the history of Kyivan Rus’; perfect for returning fans and newcomers.

—description from the publisher

Shattered Realities is a fully cooperative asymmetric dungeon crawl for 2–6 players, set in a collapsing simulated multiverse. Reality exists in fractured fragments called Mainframes, while the corrupted A.I. Infinitron rewrites existence into its own playground. Players become hunters from different timelines, such as a tribal leader from the Stone Age, a Viking seer, an Aztec fire sorcerer, a cyberpunk hacker, a desert trickster, or a detective with his dog, each with unique abilities and playstyles.

Each run takes place across three interconnected maps. Players jump between shattered realities, from vampire-infested Old West towns to neon cities ruled by eldritch droids, facing corrupted tribes and reacting to the Prompt system, where a single card can alter the rules of the battlefield and add a new layer of chaos. On the third map, the hunt culminates in a final confrontation with Infinitron itself. Every Mainframe includes multiple mission variants that change the structure of play, ranging from hold-the-line tower defense to objective runs and puzzle-driven challenges.

Combat in Shattered Realities uses familiar tabletop tools such as Attack Rolls, Defense Rolls, Critical Hits and Ability Trials, combined with new progression systems. As players defeat enemies, they earn Shards that can be spent to unlock Variant Abilities, tapping into alternate versions of their heroes from across the multiverse. With detailed high-quality miniatures, modular maps, mission variety and character growth, Shattered Realities offers a replayable game of dimensional travel, and corrupted monsters with the ultimate goal of tracking down Infinitron and restore balance to the multiverse.

—description from the publisher

In Heroes of the Pacific, you take command of the American or Japanese armed forces in a no-holds-barred skirmish. The chosen scenario determines the number of troops you have under your command, as well as your objectives. All you have to do is win!

Fight on a battlefield made up of Terrain Boards containing Terrain Elements that your soldiers must deal with. 
On the field, your forces are represented by Unit counters. Each counter has all the information you need to play the Unit: Combat Value, Defense Value, Movement Value, as well as the Special Abilities the Unit can use.

To help you build and organize your army, Heroes of the Pacific offers a system of Recruitment Tiles and Recruitment Options. These tiles allow you to better track your army and its capabilities.

Each Game Turn, you secretly use your Order Tokens to command some of your Units and determine the order in which they will act.

Each of these Units may perform a Movement Action – to occupy a strategic position or capture an Objective – or attack the enemy by performing a Firing Action. You and your opponent activate Units one after the other, alternating between them. Once all your Order Tokens are activated, the rest of your forces will have the opportunity to maneuver. 
Then you prepare for the next turn.

But Heroes of the Pacific is not just a tactical combat game. It is also a heroic action game. For you will command not only loyal soldiers,
but also brave characters, cunning leaders with talents of their own.

The whims and eccentricities of the gods of war are represented by Action Cards, which can give you that little edge you're missing, or turn the tide of battle completely with a well-planned strategy.

In the end, the commander who best leads his troops in accomplishing his objectives wins. So read on, soldier, and prepare for the test of war.

—description from the publisher

Pachan-biriche! is a 10x10 hex-based reinvention of the classic line-connection game, transformed through the UPV (Universal Pachanga Vip)framework into a dynamic and colorful tabletop experience. Players connect vertices to close hexagons, mark them with their initials, and record the points earned. Scoring is variable, determined by VSD cards and modified by special dice, making every match unpredictable and strategic. With four colored zones (blue, red, yellow, white), the board evolves into a living record of the game, blending nostalgia with innovation and ensuring infinite replayability.

Player Count & Play Time

     Players: 2–6  
     Play Time: 20–40 minutes  
     Age: 8+  
     Tournaments: scalable to competitive formats, with the full 10x10 board used for tournament play.  


---

Mechanics

     Line connection between vertices.  
     Area control by claiming closed hexagons.  
     Variable scoring through cards and dice.  
     Marking initials and points on closed hexes.  
     Family-friendly challenges and surprises.  


---

Rules (Basic Flow)

     Setup: place the 10x10 hex board with colored spaces, shuffle VSD cards, and keep the dice nearby.  
     Turn Sequence:  
         Roll dice to determine scoring modifiers and card flow.  
         Draw and apply VSD card(s) → assign values to colors or trigger effects.  
         Connect two vertices → if a hex closes, mark it with your initial and record the points earned.  
    
     End of Game: when all hexagons are closed or a time limit is reached.  
     Winner: the player with the highest total score.  


—description from the designer

Ready for an exciting hunting adventure?
HUNT is a fast-paced card game of strategy, luck, and action that you can play with friends or family. Each round sends you on a new hunt where you’ll sabotage opponents, set traps, and boost your chances with powerful weapon cards.

Theme

A hunting adventure set in the wild.

Card Types


     Hunt Cards: Capture a variety of creatures such as the Golden-Eye Mantis, Shadow Wolf, Poison Quill Hedgehog, and different types of fish.



     Weapon Cards: Increase your chances of success during the hunt.



     Trap Cards: Disrupt your opponents, sabotage their hunts, and turn their strategies upside down.


How to Play

Roll the die to begin your hunt, make the right moves to catch your target, and collect points. With traps and weapons, you can shift the course of the game and gain the upper hand against your rivals. The first player to reach the required score wins!

Features


     Plays with 3–6 players
     Average playtime: 30–45 minutes
     Fun, competitive, and full of surprises each round
     Perfect for university students, friend groups, and families


Bring competition, excitement, and laughter to the table with HUNT!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Tesoros del mar Menor: Baron of Benifayó Promo Card
description:
Promo card Baron of Benifayó is a promotional card given by Flamenco Games in certain events related to the board game Tesoros del Mar Menor.

When a player plays this card, he/she will get three cards from the draw deck and put them into his/her hand.

With the starter deck themed around the legends of the game as real animals, player beasts and Mythics in the biomes found in your chosen football/soccer nation, deploy your cards in correct positions to gain the advantage and score goals.
Coach cards develop teams toward Mythic skills, but not without undergoing setbacks using the "Do-A-DIE" roll. As you advance the team through challenges and tell this Cinderella story in gripping detail, you might just end up in a thrilling shootout to win the game!

—description from the designer

Baseball Shuffle is an all-ages, dice-flicking mashup of baseball and shuffleboard, played on a handcrafted wooden ballpark for 2–16 players.
Blending skill and luck, every at-bat delivers wild twists, big plays, and the thrill of baseball like you’ve never seen before. And the best part? Your drinks are part of the game.

—description from the designer

Ready to take your get-together to the next level, overcome challenges, and have the best party ever?

Joda is a secret mission wallet game to enjoy at a party. It's the perfect game to add a unique and fun twist to your parties, get-togethers, birthdays, or pre-parties. You can play it with friends, acquaintances, and even strangers. It's ideal for breaking the ice!

Plus, with the "forbidden" and "vigilante" modes, the fun is guaranteed until the very end of the party.

—description from the publisher (translated)

In Miau!, you'll become a Karen and have to find your lucky kitty. Before playing a card, you must guess the kitty's color and position, and if the card matches, you have to grab the kitty before anyone else and say "Meow!"

With the Expansion Card, a new game rule is added.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Не купуй цей КРИНЖ! (Don't buy this CRINGE!)
description:
Don't buy this CRINGE! is a fast, humorous card game about youth subcultures and the generational gap. Players become representatives of one of six subcultures and compete to assemble the most on-brand outfit. The game leans heavily into playful slang, memes, and stereotypes, making it a light-hearted parody where "OGs" and "Zoomers" can laugh at each other.

Each turn, players perform one action: play cards, discard a card to trigger its effect, or draw a card. Once a player places their first piece of subculture gear, they "join" that subculture and can only build outfits from that group. The shared market, attack/defense effects, universal "joker" gear, and disruptive "bro" and "skuf" cards create constant interaction - stealing cards, blocking opponents, wiping their outfits, or protecting your own. Hand size is always maintained at three cards, keeping the game quick and tactical.

You win either by completing a full 4-piece outfit of a single subculture in your play area, or by playing three "skuf" cards at once for an instant victory. The game ends immediately when any victory condition is met.
language_dependency: 4: Extensive use of text - massive conversion needed to be playable

boardgame: Vivid Rainbow Pack (fan expansion for Railroad Ink)
description:
Vivid Rainbow is a collection of fan-made expansions curated to bring fresh variety and new strategic twists to your games of Railroad Ink. Each module can be mixed and matched with your favourite core set to explore inventive mechanics and new ways to score points:

Deeper Green – Explore a lush new board featuring Forest spaces that can be cleared for construction or integrated into scoring when used with the Forest Expansion. Manage the landscape carefully to build routes through dense wilderness or cultivate powerful point-scoring Forests.

Rivers and Piers – Expand the classic Rivers rules with new connection possibilities and advanced interactions. This module also adds support for Lake dice, introducing flow-based decisions and improved flexibility when combining multiple water-themed expansions.

Defiance – Add a new Route die option that blends elements of the Station and Challenge dice into a single versatile tool. Defiance reshapes your network planning while keeping the core flow of the game intact.

City – Develop complex urban infrastructure using Roundabouts and Tunnels. Build circular hubs to direct traffic efficiently or carve underground rail connections that link distant networks in unexpected ways.

—description from the designer

Chop Chop  is a fast-paced, kitchen-themed card game where players race to complete food orders while sabotaging their rivals in a chaotic battle to become the top chef. Inspired by the energy of a busy restaurant line, the game blends quick decisions, clever timing, and playful take-that interactions that keep everyone laughing and engaged.

On your turn, you’ll gather and play ingredient cards to build and complete orders, each worth money toward your victory. But the kitchen is never calm for long. Action cards let you disrupt opponents, steal ingredients, ignite kitchen fires, and force rivals to rethink their strategy. The tension ramps up as players shout “Order up!” to lock in completed dishes, with every turn testing your ability to manage your hand, react to sabotage, and stay one step ahead.

Chop Chop is designed to be intuitive and easy to learn even for the casual gamers. Whether you're playing with your partner, family, or friends, the pacing stays lively and competitive, and most rounds finish in 15–30 minutes. Chop Chop is perfect for game nights, date nights, or quick rematches.

Your goal is simple: earn the most money by completing orders before your opponents do. The first chef to hit the winning threshold claims victory and walks away as the kitchen champion. With flexible player counts and high replay ability, Chop Chop brings approachable fun and plenty of chaos to the table.

—description from the designer

Pachan-wow! turns a traditional jigsaw puzzle into a competitive, turn-based social game driven by surprise, timing, and dynamic scoring.

All puzzle pieces begin face up, showing only the image—their underside colors remain hidden.

Turn Structure

Each turn follows a designer-created pre-placement sequence:

1. Select a puzzle piece
The active player chooses any piece from the table without knowing its point value.
Only after selecting it, the player flips the piece to reveal the hidden color on the underside (blue, red, yellow, green, white, or black).

2. Roll the Blue Die
This die produces a small bonus or constraint for the turn.

3. Possible activation of the Red Die
Some Blue Die results trigger a second die that can introduce chaotic effects affecting points, players, or turn order.

4. Draw a Scoring Card (VSD card)
This card assigns temporary point values to every color for this specific turn.
Because values change each turn, a piece’s worth is always unpredictable until the moment of play.

5. Place the piece
The player fits the revealed piece into its correct position in the puzzle.

6. Score on the abacus
The color revealed at the start of the turn determines how many points the player gains based on the current Scoring Card.
Points are recorded using a two-row bead scoring abacus (units and tens), keeping scoring tactile and fast.

End of the Game

Players repeat this full cycle—select piece → reveal color → dice effects → scoring card → placement → abacus scoring—until the puzzle is completed.
The player with the highest total score wins.

Pachan-wow! converts a solitary activity into an interactive, tense, and often hilarious group competition, introducing timing, suspense, and tactical choice into the world of puzzles.

—description from the designer

A Christmas Tree Duel is a drafting game played with a standard 52 card deck.

Two players alternate taking cards from a large pyramid or "christmas tree" in the middle of the table to add to their own trees. When placing a card, players may choose to flip it face down in order to score patterns in their trees called "frills". That player then gains presents depending on how many and what types of frills were scored. The game ends when the christmas tree in the middle has been fully drafted, or when all 21 presents have been gained. The winner is the player with the most presents.

—description from the designer

Wrap up your sleeves for a delicious and exciting board game experience where players prepare meals by wrapping rice, spices, and fillings into cabbage or vine leaves. Known worldwide under various names like sarma, dolma, and golubki, this game challenges players to collect ingredients, follow recipes, and prepare meals. Players earn points by preparing dishes and combining them based on personal objectives. The game encourages both strategic planning and tactical execution as players trade, activate action cards, and cook meals throughout six rounds. The player with the most points at the end of the game wins.

Foxes, llamas, flamingos and salamanders all have their own knocking sounds. Listen carefully and find out who is coming to visit.

In each round, one child takes on the role of the knocking animals. The others must listen carefully and then quickly find the matching animal card. But be careful! Each card only appears once.

Your lantern carves halos in the damp air beneath the canopy. You follow a silk thread into the unknown. You are an arachnologist, determined to catalogue rare species and put your name on the next landmark publication.

Araknid is a solo card game that puts control in your hands. Every turn is a dilemma. Optimization, strategy, and planning intertwine for short, tense sessions where good timing makes all the difference. Stripped of any superfluous elements, the challenge is pure and the sense of progression is real. Climb the ranks of research, build your reputation, and earn international renown.

Each turn: 

    Move your Action cards to prepare your strategy.
    Select your Actions for the round, place bait, explore Locations, catch spiders and advance your research.
    The environment responds to your choices: terrain effects, events and surprises will impact your plans.
    Discover rare spiders, optimize your catches and become an arachnologist.


The game ends when you run out of spider cards.

boardgame: Nautical Nomad: SOLO Adventure on the High Seas
description:
Nautical Nomad is a SOLO, PRINT-n-PLAY tabletop game of Exploration, Trading and… trying REALLY, REALLY hard to NOT SINK.

In Nautical Nomad, you roll dice to control Steering, Propulsion and Engineering. Be sure to Plan carefully because each move adds more Damage to your already-dilapidated Cargo Ship. Choose from 5 different maps with their own rules and challenges.

Player will roll START by rolling 4d6 (Less with damage)

Player allocates 1d6 to Steering to determine the DIRECTION of travel, 1d6 to Propulsion to determine how FAR Ship travels and the remaining dice towards Unlocking Engineering Upgrades. Player moves Cargo Ship to Explore Map.

Player may also Trade in Ports, Collect Flotsam and Visit Points of Interest. Play continues until the Player either, 1) CHOOSES to End the Game in a Port, OR 2) the Player’s Ship SINKS TO THE BOTTOM OF THE OCEAN.

boardgame: Tiny Epic Game of Thrones: Hand of the King
description:
The Hand of the King is a mini expansion introducing a new playable character for House Lanister, Tyrion Lannister, and a beautifully crafted Iron Throne Mini for Tiny Epic Game of Thrones.  Also includes solo card tailored for Tyrion Lannister.

This additional content from the Hand of the King mini expansion is compatible with all modes of play, including competitive, cooperative, and solo.

Due to licensing agreement, this content is only available at the publisher's site and won't be found in retail stores. It was included inside the Tiny Epic Game of Thrones: Deluxe Edition.

Welcome to the worst newsroom in Italy.

In this ruthless and delirious party game, you’ll step into the shoes of a journalist trying to craft the most sensational headline for the front page of your newspaper.

It’s the perfect game for ruining dinners, friendships, and your own reputation… not that there’s much left to lose.

You’ll get a little help from real Lercio headlines, but you’ll still need to bring your own twisted imagination to create the most hilarious and brilliant story possible.

Whoever manages to collect the most headlines and make the Editor-in-Chief proud will earn eternal glory (or almost), and their statue at the office entrance will be worshipped for years to come.
The others? Fired on the spot and reassigned to writing horoscopes.

—description from the publisher

Benvenuti nella peggior redazione giornalistica d’Italia.

In questo party game spietato e delirante, vestirete i panni di un giornalista alle prese con la creazione del titolo più sensazionale da utilizzare nella prima pagina del vostro quotidiano.

Il gioco perfetto per rovinare cene, amicizie e la vostra reputazione… tanto, ormai.

A darvi una mano ci saranno i veri titoli di Lercio, ma la fantasia malata dovrete mettercela (anche) voi, creando la notizia più esilarante e geniale possibile.

Chi riuscirà a collezionare più titoli e rendere orgoglioso il Capo Redattore vincerà la gloria eterna (o quasi), e la sua statua all’entrata degli uffici verrà venerata negli anni a venire.
Gli altri? Licenziati in tronco e mandati a scrivere oroscopi.

—description from the publisher (Italian)

Terra Incognita is a family-friendly pirate adventure strategy game in which players explore a mysterious modular island, flip tiles to reveal surprises, collect treasure, and race their loot back to their ships while competing against rival crews. Each game creates a new island layout, offering strong replayability through varied tile arrangements.

On a player’s turn, they may activate one or more of their pirates, allowing flexible planning and coordinated movement across the island. Players spend movement to travel across connected tiles, balancing exploration, positioning, and tactical interaction.

Moving onto an unexplored tile flips it, revealing treasure, hazards, special locations, or event effects that introduce unexpected twists to the island. These events are part of the core game and help create a dynamic and unpredictable exploration experience. Pirates can pick up treasure as they move, attack opponents on adjacent tiles, or interact with special tile effects.

In advanced modes, players may also gain access to unique pirate ability cards, adding asymmetry and new strategic options.

Players continue exploring, gathering treasure, and interfering with rival crews as the island is gradually revealed.

The game ends when all treasure on the island has been discovered and delivered to the ships. The winner is the player or team with the highest total treasure value. Coins, treasure chest, and treasure sack contribute to the final score, rewarding both efficient exploration and smart tactical decisions.

Terra Incognita offers a light-to-medium strategic experience built around discovery, movement choices, risk–reward decisions, and a competitive race for treasure across a richly varied island map.

— description from the designer

Yokai Companion is not a stand alone product. You need CLASH of Spears and CLASH of Katanas to use this supplement.

This expansion provides fantasy elements for Sengoku Japan. It provides 4 additional lists that can be used against each other or mixed with CLASH of Katanas lists.

It includes magic rules, rules for undead creatures and other fantastic elements.

The lists included are: 

     Ryuku: fantasy samurai, temple dogs, Kirin and much more
     Namban: European forces, could be Spaniards or Portuguese.
     Kuroite: Undead forces
     Yurei: Evil spirits and ghosts

London is choking beneath a mist no longer of this world. Under this grey shroud pressing down on the rooftops, the city groans, a rumble too deep to be the wind, too alive to be mere stone. For weeks now, reality has been warping: alleyways shift their places, shadows stretch against the will of the sun, and some Districts feel hollow, as if amputated from their own existence. Scholars whisper of an ancient force emerging from the seams of the world: the Void. It is neither a being nor a spirit. It is a hunger. A cold power that resurfaces whenever mankind depletes the Aether, disrupts the natural order, or lets chaos take root.
The rifts tearing through the city, the Faults are no accidents; they are scars of a forgotten cycle. The first signs of an impending erasure. Creatures foreign to all that life has ever borne crawl from the breaches. They move as though mimicking a memory from a vanished world—hesitating, then rending. And with every breath they steal from London, the city inches closer to the edge of the Void.But the city is not alone….
The Order of the Holy Grail, wielding ancient faith and sanctified blades, rises to seal the wounds of the world. The C.O.D.I., heirs of Atlantis, deploy agents and machines to repel the phenomena of the Void. The Chalice, its blood still whispering the secrets of the night, seeks to expand its dominion over the creatures crawling out of the Faults. And the Pit, empire of criminals, shadows, and broken souls, fights with savage brutality to survive the chaos.
They all want to protect London. They all want to control it. But only those who seal the Faults will shape its future.

London is suffocating under the weight of the Void: its Creatures, its Faults, its growing influence.

In Tales of London, 2–4 players take control of powerful factions vying to save or dominate a dark, shifting London threatened by the force known as the Void.

Each round consists of three phases:
    • Disaster Phase:
Reveal a Disaster card that triggers catastrophic events, spawns Threats and Rifts across London’s districts.
    • Conquest Phase:
Players collect Aether, the game’s main resource, and spend it to recruit and activate units. Units can move, explore, fight, absorb Aether, or seal districts.
    • Void Phase:
The Void retaliates. Its creatures move and attack following a specific Predation Order.

Game End & Victory Conditions
The game ends when one of the following occurs:
    • Round-Based Ending: if the game reaches round 15 and the Void is not on the board, the player with the most sealed districts wins.
    • Instant Victory: a player seals their 5th district (including their Bastion), that player wins immediately.
    • Instant Defeat: If 5 Primordial Rifts are ever present on the board, all players lose.

-description from publisher

Welcome to Fallout: Power Play!, in which you control one of four factions from the Fallout universe: the Brotherhood, Raiders, the Enclave, and Super Mutant. You are all vying for control of locations within the Wasteland.

You will spend your turns attempting to gain the most influence in locations on the board, winning points at the end of each round for each location where you have the most influence. To gain influence, play agents into locations, play and complete quests (which come with other rewards as well), use "power play" cards to respond to opponents, and struggle against the Wasteland itself as each turn a randomly drawn card brings an unpredictable change to the game, from sudden atomization to a surly deathclaw rampaging through your home base. The first player to gain 10 points wins.

An expansion for The Green Hell of Inor, which is a third party historical module for the Advance Squad Leader game.  The player's guide includes the following:


     12 scenarios
     4 scenarios from Eban Emael and the bridges over the Canal Albert
     1 Campaign Game (CG IV "Death at Soiry Farm")
     Designer notes
     Inor optional rules
     Inor campaign game after action report
     Other shorter articles


Information is from the publisher's webpage.

A quick, simple game that recreates the sport of curling. Played with dice (2d10) and charts, Tee Line Curling uses real teams' winning percentages to replay all kinds of curling events, from the Olympics and World Championship to local league matches.

The score for each "end" in a match is determined with a couple of dice rolls. The game rules include instructions for how to rate teams. As described in the game's rules, Tee Line Curling includes Strategy and Last End charts to replay "the critical moments and high drama that curling often produces."

The rules include a glossary of key curling terms to make the game accessible and enjoyable for players without much prior knowledge of the "roaring" game.

Play the classic game of Memory with a SPYxFAMILY twist! Find the Forgers' matching outfits to complete a set by turning over cards to find their match.

But watch out for pesky Tonitrus Bolts! Keep away from them to avoid getting into trouble. If you can match the Forgers in a complete outfit and season set you get extra points. The player with the most matches and sets wins!

Memory with a twist: Forger sets have players look not only for a card's exact match but also the other 2 cards that have a matching outfit set.

When Farmer Finnigan leaves for his first vacation in years, the animals decide it’s finally their turn to take charge. The problem? No one agrees on how the farm should be run. In Rule the Roost, players take on the roles of ambitious animal leaders vying for power, each with their own vision for the future. Cooperation, sabotage, and manipulation collide as the barnyard descends into a battle of wits and influence.

Each round, a new event challenges the Farm Council—should the animals build a larger barn, fight off predators, or trade with neighboring farms? Players debate, negotiate, and then secretly vote on how to respond. Behind every decision lies strategy: build up your resources, forge alliances, and use cunning Attack or Special cards to shift the balance of power.

Every choice matters. Players must balance their public alliances with private motives, deciding when to defend their interests and when to strike at others. With every round, the tension grows as votes are swayed, resources are stolen, and trust evaporates in the hay-filled halls of power.

The first player to win three Event Cards claims control of the farm and becomes its undisputed leader—proving that in the end, only one can truly Rule the Roost.

boardgame: Dossiers Criminels Pocket: Qui a tué Meredith Carter ?
description:
NEW CONCEPT: No rules to read, just open and investigate! The perfect game to embark on your first investigation or continue challenging yourself.

BECOME A DETECTIVE: Meredith Carter, founder of Kenwood Zoo, is found dead in the bear pit. Accident or premeditated murder? Get to the bottom of this case!

CREATE YOUR OWN INVESTIGATION BOARD: Sort, match, and assemble over 40 cards to solve the mystery.

A USER-FRIENDLY, DIGITAL-FREE GAME: Observe, cooperate, and discuss with each other to solve the case. Also playable solo.

Over 40 cards to analyze:

     Surveillance cameras
     Plans
     Testimonies
     Photos
     Letters
     and much more...


Gameplay:
Assemble a team of detectives and solve a criminal case!

Criminal Case Files in card form!

Meredith Carter, the renowned primatologist and founder of Kenwood Zoo, has been found dead, seemingly killed by a bear. Was it really an accident? What was she doing in the bear enclosure?

Reconstruct the timeline of events, compare witness statements, analyze documents, solve puzzles, and... convict the murderer!

Alone or in a group, cooperate and find the solution by examining each card and making the right deductions.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: Petits crimes entre amis: True Crime
description:
Petits crimes entre amis: True Crime - Little Crimes Among Friends:True Crime

To solve the mystery, draw clues and get the other players talking.
Beware: you all have a secret to hide!

Unmask the culprit of a crime… while protecting your unspeakable secrets.

10 enigmatic mysteries
10 successful game nights
60 Character cards, 
60 Clue cards, 
1 48-page booklet

boardgame: Assassins and Templars: A Ruckus Game
description:
Assassins and Templars is Mike Peters’ follow-up to Ruckus. Using the same basic micro-skirmish, highly entertaining rules engine to play low-figure-count, action-packed skirmish games set during the crusades.

Assassins and Templars: A Ruckus game is a self-contained 32-page rules booklet for playing games involving (typically) 12-figure per side. Assassin and Templar Heroes and Retainers take to the tabletop to play narrative-driven wargames on a scenery-heavy board.

Your retinue awaits, and your adventures are about to begin ...

-description from publisher's website

boardgame: Re：ゴーストマーダー 第1部 犯人不在のハウダニット (Murder Mystery Mini: Re:Ghost Murder Case Part 1)
description:
The Murder Mystery Mini trilogy has begun!

In a room in a strange hospital, there are four men and women who have lost their memories, the corpse of a young boy lying on the floor, and a parakeet perched on a birdcage. "The culprit is not among you," the parakeet says. "But the culprit is among you." The search for the culprit begins with these seemingly contradictory words, and eventually leads to a case that transcends time.

This is a four-player Murder Mystery Mini (MMM) game, and the first in the planned three-part "Re: Ghost Murder" series. While you can get to the truth of the incident with just this one game, your perspective on the truth will be completely different if you play through parts two and three. Please enjoy the twists and turns and stories that can only be experienced in a series of games.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: 私が犯人だ (Murder Mystery Mini: I am The Culprit)
description:
In modern-day America, an unemployed man is found burned to death in a rural area of ​​California. Immediately afterward, a young man appears before a city police investigator and confesses, "I killed him," uttering some strange words.

"Soon you will find two more bodies. I killed them too. And now I am going to create a fourth and final body. I want you to find out who that target is, and stop me from killing the fourth."

Is the young man really a serial killer? If so, why did he turn himself in?
And how on earth did he commit a fourth murder while the investigators were right next to him?

The latest two-player version of Murder Mystery Mini (MMM) is here.
Enjoy a thrilling and tense story that's reminiscent of classic American detective dramas.

—description from the publisher (translated)

This is GRIMLORE: Portable Edition - a print-and-play adventure game of desperate crossings and ravenous hunts.

Gather three champions, each with unique talents as well as powerful gear; and race them across a wilderness full of unspeakable horrors toward safe havens, while the land spills its secrets. Explore dungeons, face challenges, barter at settlements, and gamble scarce energy for one last, lucky stride.
Survival isn't certain; escape is victory. The land chooses the rest.

Play solo, cooperatively, or in versus duels, all on an expedition map with tokens and decks that you craft yourself. Step through the rift into Eonell!

This card was created for the 2025 Saboteur World Championship.

Effect of card: Look secretly at the hand cards of a player of your choice.

Note: the spy doesn't allow you to check the role card (miner or saboteur) of another player. The role card must always remain secret.

Big box containing the Lotta Rome (Rome in a Day) base game and expansion Lotta Rome: The Merchants.

Lotta Rome is a simple strategy game based on the original "cut-and-choose" mechanic that will win the hearts of aspiring conquerors of all ages.

Before the start, all players are dealt with their personal set of buildings with the card of buildings, a pile of lands tiles (there are 5 types - fields, city, olive gardens etc), gems/crystals, choice cards of smaller and bigger lands, reminder cards and screens.

Lotta Rome: The Merchants is an expansion to Lotta Rome that adds depth to the gameplay, increases the interaction between players, and opens up new strategies.

—description from the publisher

The board game Beat Battle Dutch Edition is all about musical knowledge and teamwork! With each roll of the dce, you bring the tension to a climax. Will your team guess the right songs and reach the finish line?

—description from the back the box

The board game Beat Battle Guilty Pleasure is all about musical knowledge and teamwork! With each roll of the dce, you bring the tension to a climax. Will your team guess the right songs and reach the finish line?

—description from the back the box

Goûtons voir ! is a cooperative game. Players will try to identify the tastes and characteristics of a wine, the most
important thing being not to be right, but to agree...

As the game progresses, record your score on the scoreboard. This will allow you to gauge your level of wine expertise, as well as how well you get along with the other players!

In this cooperative game, taste a wine together without looking at the label, then describe the flavors you detect.

Place your tokens according to your own impressions, but also those of the other players, because it's by sharing and listening to each other that you'll achieve the best scores!


     Very simple rules for a social game that encourages discussion and sharing.
     The product makes the perfect gift for evenings with friends.
     As many games as there are wines!
     Can be played with a bottle of red, white, or rosé wine!
     No knowledge required—a novice is just as valuable as an expert!


—description from the publisher (translated)

Goûtons voir ! est un jeu coopératif. Les joueurs vont tenter d’identifier les goûts et les caractéristiques d’un vin, le plus important n’étant pas d’avoir raison, mais d’être d’accord...

Au fil de la partie, reportez votre score dans le tableau de score. Cela vous permettra de connaître votre niveau en œnologie, mais aussi votre niveau d’entente entre les participants !

Dans ce jeu coopératif, goûtez un vin ensemble sans en regarder l’étiquette, puis décrivez les goûts que vous décelez.

Placez vos pions selon votre ressenti mais aussi celui des autres joueurs, car c’est en partageant et en s’écoutant que vous réaliserez les meilleurs scores !


     Règles très simples pour un jeu social engendrant discussions et partage.
     Aspect cadeau du produit parfait pour les soirées entre amis.
     Autant de parties qu’il y a de vins !
     Se joue avec une bouteille de vin rouge, blanc ou rosé !
     Pas besoin de connaissances, un néophyte a autant de valeur qu’un expert !


—description from the publisher

When you're at the age of great discoveries, the age when you thrill at crossing limits, what could be more natural than getting into Urbex (Urban Exploration)? In any case, that’s the big plan for Louis and Soraya. A disused psychiatric hospital, dozens of rooms to explore—that will undoubtedly be far more exhilarating than a day of school! But no matter how much you plan everything, it's hard to anticipate that such an expedition could, at some point, tip over into the most agonizing of nightmares...

There are several ways out of the trap you've entered, but few of them will allow you to emerge alive and without the slightest trauma.

Good luck!

This survival adventure, with its unique format, can be experienced solo or with two players. Choose your character, then set off to explore. Search for clues and items, keep track of the passing time, monitor your alert level, all while staying in contact with your teammate.

4 Nobi Nobi Roleplay Games in 1 Box !

In this fast-paced and fluid tabletop role-playing game, your quest will grow card by card, as the many clues and plot twists unfold. Roll the dice, play your cards, and build your story with your friends. Simple and easy to learn, Nobi Nobi will appeal to new and experienced role players alike!

Everything you need is in this box.
Just add a pinch of imagination!

This Collector Box includes four flavors of Nobi Nobi that you can combine: Magic, Sword, Horror, and Thriller.

—description from the publisher

Case 69 is a compact deduction thriller where players become investigators racing to crack a confidential case. Twelve cards form a shifting Crime Scene arranged in a circle, with a central Safe that hides critical evidence. Each round, players draw from the Crime Scene, play a card face up for its tactical effect—or face down to secretly scout another player’s hand—and then return their played card back to the same slot, constantly reshaping what is known and unknown.

The two key pieces of evidence, the Bloodstained ID (6) and the Bloodstained Weapon (9), are the heart of the mystery. To close the case, a player must hold one of them and successfully identify the exact location of the other—whether it is hidden somewhere at the Crime Scene or held by another investigator. But if a declaration is wrong, the risk is real: another player may instantly win instead, or the table may spiral into a “dead round” where all remaining investigators earn a point.

Throughout the game, players manipulate the Crime Scene using asymmetric card effects—shuffling the evidence, swapping hands, moving cards into or out of the Safe, or forcing certain players to exchange cards based on odd or even numbers. Bluffing, timing, memory, and reading the table are essential; every action reveals just enough information to build suspicion or mislead opponents.

Rounds are quick, tense, and filled with razor-sharp decisions. The first player to reach four points wins the game, but in Case 69, information is never safe, identities are never certain, and every turn might be the moment someone cracks the case before you do.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Seven Deadly Sins: Battle Of Discord
description:
Seven Deadly Sins Battle of Discord is a fast-paced, asymmetrical dice game from Don't Panic Games.

Created by the authors of Team Kaedama.

Choose 2 characters and face off against each other by frantically rolling your dice. Certain results will give you access to special powers that inflict damage on your opponents.

Games for 2 to 4 players, lasting about 15 minutes, playable by ages 10 and up.

Seven Deadly Sins: a global hit series, both in manga and anime form.
A fast-paced board game for the whole family.
Play with characters with asymmetrical powers and battle using their special techniques!

—description from the publisher

Seven Deadly Sins Battle of Discord est un jeu de dés frénétique et asymétrique, proposé chez Don’t Panic Games.

Imaginé par les auteurs de la team Kaedama.

Choisissez 2 personnages et affrontez-vous en lançant frénétiquement vos dés. Certains résultats vous donneront accès a des pouvoirs spéciaux infligeant des blessures à vos adversaires.

Parties entre 2 à 4 joueurs, durant environ 15 minutes, jouable à partir de 10 ans et plus.

Seven Deadly Sins : succès mondial de la série, tant au niveau des mangas que de l’animé.
Un jeu de société frénétique pour toute la famille.
Jouez avec des personnages aux pouvoirs asymétriques et combattez à l’aide de leurs techniques spéciales !

—description from the publisher (French)

In Server Breach, you will recruit a covert team of Hackers determined to take down the Corp — a shadowy network of corporate tyrants hellbent on destroying the world for profit. But for every Hacker you find willing to help the fight, the Corp will inevitably recruit their own. To win, you must control all Control Columns AND breach all Servers.

Each turn you will draw three cards and decide how best to deploy them. Do you fight for control of the Corp's Control Columns or do you attempt to breach a Server, satisfying 2‑ and 3‑card combos to unlock deeper layers of their network? With simple rules, quick setup and meaningful decisions every turn, Server Breach delivers a tense hacking experience in 15 minutes or less.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
Join your fellow farm animal escapees in a cooperative quest adventure, sneak around the farm, keep the farmer happy (and very un-suspicious), and ESCAPE!

Each round, all players will take actions to gather everything they need from the various areas of the farm, while using their unique abilities to avoid and slow down the farmer, preventing them from getting to suspicious of the group.

The farmer (played by the game itself) will be moving around the farm, completing chores, catching suspicious animals, and causing dilemmas among the other farm inhabitants that need to resolved.

If the farmer gets too suspicious - game over. If all animals complete their quests and get to the gates - victory.

—description from the publisher

Corporate Creatures is a satirical party-strategy card game about surviving corporate chaos through fake productivity, weaponised jargon, and strategic incompetence.

Players battle for control of a dysfunctional organisation, launching targeted redundancy attacks to strip rivals of resources and eliminate them from play. The last player with Creature Cards remaining becomes the Goblin Czar—undisputed ruler of corporate dysfunction.
