The game’s core mechanic uses directional stat pressure: BRAG challenges reward high stats, while BURDEN challenges punish them. The trait that helps you win today may cause you to fail tomorrow. There is no safe build—only calculated risk.

—description from the designer

Battlecrest gets weird with 3 more heroes in 2026, wrapping up the Deadrock set and getting us to 21 heroes and 10 wallets!


What is the mark of a true champion? Legendary heroes clash across the Neververse to find out.

Battlecrest is a card-based tactical skirmish game for 2 players (1-4 with expansions). With perfect information, players must truly outwit their opponent to win.

Characters are composed of 6-card sets featuring a hero card, a health tracker, and action cards. Occasionally a minion or item will replace an action card.

A collection of Marvel expansions for Adventurous:

     Adventurous: Forgotten Worlds
     Adventurous: Grand Expeditions
     Adventurous: Cursed Seas
A collection of Epilogue expansions for Adventurous.

La Cruz de Caballero is a cultural board game with a powerful question-and-answer engine about the Second World War, designed so you can enjoy learning history while having fun around the table.

In La Cruz de Caballero, you’ll be able to show how much you know about battles, key figures, armored vehicles, aircraft, anecdotes, movies, and all kinds of curiosities from the conflict. Each question offers three possible answers, always showing the correct one afterward so you never stop progressing—and never get caught with your guard down. The game also includes several difficulty levels, allowing you to play only with the easiest questions, the medium ones, the advanced ones, or all of them at once. And if that’s not enough, you can even use “Wildcard” cards during your advance. The choice is yours!

The game is for two to four players, and you can choose to play as George S. Patton, Yamashita, Zhukov, or even Field Marshal Erwin Rommel. Its simple strategy system lets you place mines, use smoke grenades to mislead your opponents, set up military checkpoints to slow them down, fire like a sniper, or use bazookas—and if necessary, even become a true “Kamikaze” if you see someone getting close to winning.

La Cruz de Caballero is a genuine and accessible cultural wargame to enjoy with family, friends, or groups of enthusiasts who want to learn more about the conflict.

Have fun with La Cruz de Caballero… because WWII is waiting for you, soldier.
The time has finally come!

—description from the publisher

It is a word game involving people guessing words with some limits (think Taboo but for Japanese words).

There is a card containing 5 Japanese words that everyone can see. There is a clue-giver that must describe 1 word (which they know as they are given another card numbered 1-5, corresponding to the position of the word in the word list they must describe). These are "katakana" words which tend to be foreign-borrowed, for example "konpyuuta" which comes from computer.

The gimmick is that the clue giver cannot use katakana words to describe the word. This is difficult because using loan words make it much easier to describe katakana words. First person to guess gets a point.

A Richii-like Mahjong spinoff-game that is based off drinking and eating in an izakaya.

Players will draw cards from a deck containing familiar items like edamame, chicken skewers, and more, and discard from their hand of 9 in hopes of getting 3 sets of 3 identical cards. Winning hand scores are dependent on the types of cards used to complete their hand. Each player keeps score with money tokens and the most after 4 rounds wins!

This includes other scoring mechanics such as a "Special of the Day" (acting as the Dora) and cards with special abilities.

Ten pin bowling-like game with ten chromolithographed British soldiers mounted on small wooden stands. Includes six wooden bullets to throw at the soldiers.

Undated, but the British military started using the Home service helmet depicted on the soldiers in 1878

Each turn, a player draws a card and scans the QR code, which will play the song on the other side of the card. The player then tries to place the card in their timeline, if they're correct, it stays in place, if they're wrong, the card is discarded. The first player to get ten cards in their timeline wins. 
This version is features all songs from American artists.

boardgame: The Game of the races: For the amusement and instruction of children
description: Sports-themed board game where players move in a circle around the board, and each spot has a multiplication or addition problem.

Grand Hotell Hörnan is a cooperative game where the players aim to solve a case that is part of the late detective Yngve Ek's legacy. The detective agency was based in Uppsala and active from the late 1920s up until the detectives passing in 1977. The game's design aimes to mirror its location and setting in time.

Grand Hotell Hörnan is the second case published and developed by Vintage Mysteries. The game includes interview protocols, diary entries, a newspaper cutting and other evidence material to help the planers follow in the tracks of the detective and discover the whereabouts of Gunnar Nordström, and the truth about the mysterious events that took place before his disappearance on the 11th of December of 1935. The case is full of historical details and holiday ambiance.

—description from the publisher

Poker Dice Baseball is a fast, dice-driven baseball game where every at-bat is decided by rolling five “poker” dice (A, K, Q, J, 10, 9). Your roll forms a poker hand —pairs, straights, full houses, four-of-a-kind and five-of-a-kind— and each hand translates into a baseball result like a single, double, triple, home run, walk or out. The result advances runners, creates outs, and builds the inning just like in real baseball, with optional tactical moments such as steals, bunts, pickoffs, and double-play situations.
To win, score more runs than your opponent after nine innings. If the score is tied, play extra innings until a winner is decided.

—description from the designer

Board game where the object is to be the person to find the Christmas jewel and bring it to its home. Includes spinner and three wooden pieces (a man for each players, and the Christmas Jewel). Box cover depicts a young girl wearing a hat, and holding a jester doll, a ball and crackers.

Uses a spinner to determine the length of a player's move on the outer track; once on the inner board, players move the number of spaces specified by the space they are currently on.

Once one player has reached the centre and placed the Jewel token atop his piece, he tries to get home. If the other player can leap him by exactly one space, he takes the Jewel token and has an extra turn.

—user summary

Die Laughing is a fast-paced battle of wit and skill where players race to outscore each other as either the Grim Reader or a Digger. As the Grim Reader, you’ll recite tricky tongue-twisting epitaphs, while the Diggers frantically search the Coffin Cards for matching bodies or elusive hidden treasures.

It’s a whirlwind of quick reactions, sharp eyes, and steady tongues, all colliding in a chaotic blend of clever wordplay and frantic card-hunting action. Can you outpace your opponents and laugh your way to victory?

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Last Light: Reclamation Cannon Promo Card
description:
Promo card from Tantrum House Season 12 Kickstarter campaign.

Rules:
Ship Ability - Take 1 of your pieces from the graveyard of an opponent with ships in this sector. Do not pay them for the piece.

Note: Comes with 2 copies of the same card.

A game about holding a mock press conference, but only the questioners know who you are.

When someone is taking a turn as the interviewee, the other players will secretly decide on a character card. Then they hold a 30 second "press conference" asking the player questions they would ask that character.

If the player can guess who their character is, they get to keep the card. The last player to ask a question gets a turn as the interviewee. First player to collect 3 cards (from correctly guessing their character) wins the game.

Each round there will be a sentence card in the middle of the table. Players will have a 10 word cards that they can use to alter the sentence. Taking turns they will replace 1 or more words in the sentence with their own words and reads out the whole sentence when done. The player who received the most laughs wins the card, with 5 sentence cards needed to win the game.

boardgame: Bryggen Dice (ブリッゲンダイス)
description:
The Bryggen district is a World Heritage Site in Norway. Its colorful streetscape is incredibly stylish, and all the buildings are wooden. However, this townscape has been ravaged by major fires many times in the past. Each time, architects worked tirelessly to rebuild Bryggen's magnificent streetscape. Today, the activities of these architects are passed down in the form of a board game called Bryggen Dice.

That said, there are no actual episodes about such architects in the Bryggen district; it's a game created based on the imagination of the author living in Japan.

In this game, you choose the results of four dice to aim for bingo lines vertically, horizontally, diagonally, or by color. Even players whose turn it isn't can aim for bingo, so there's no waiting time while playing.

It's a challenging game where you must decide whether to leave it up to the dice or control the results to aim for victory!

Moonrise is played in 4 phases:  Phases I and II portray the creation and population of moons around a gas giant.  Phase III consists of 7 rounds of preparation for the tipping point, round 8, when Phase IV begins and the safety catches on the interlunary missiles are disengaged.

Victory in Moonrise can be declared via three primary avenues.  One of you might be branded a tyrant, whose presence prevents anyone else from winning in any way and whose goal is to go the distance to round 13.  In the absence of tyranny, an alliance victory can be declared by the leader of a coalition of moons whose empires have compatible philosophies.  To take the third road, that of enlightenment, is to claim spiritual leadership before any such philosophical alliance can be reached.  On top of all of that, other, less conventional means of victory may arise.  (For example, if you are eliminated, you become an asymmetric identity who can quickly steal a win.)

Fair warnings: Moonrise culminates in nigh-unavoidable warfare.  Feel free to pursue conquest by the method that seems most appropriate to you, but if you neglect your own protection or your own lunar climate and/or make yourself an obvious target, then your species could be wiped out in a single round of attacks.  If you must abandon your Plan A for reasons of poor foresight or of self-preservation, remember that the best Plan B usually requires that you had been diplomatic before the launch codes were keyed.

Good luck, you spores and molds and amœbae and energy wisps, you slimes and twinkles and filaments, on your journey from the cradle to the crown.

—description from the designer

boardgame: DreamEscape: Plateau of Leng Realm expansion
description:
"Far north of the Dreamlands lies the forboding wasteland plateau of Leng. Timeless secrets such as the enigmatic Elder Pharos wait to be discovered. But to survive here a Dreamer must avoid the predatory Dhol dogs, evil Shantaks or the horrific Purple Spiders that weave tenacious webs over the ruins of lost kingdoms.

Rumours surface of an ancient power at the maze-like heart of Xorinth and forces strive to be the first to reach it. The hideous Dogs of Leng run amok across the plain while Purple Spiders have been sighted in the crags above Inganok. Has this triggered the lighting of the Elder Pharos or is it perhaps the cause?"

This pack continues the story into a new area with more quests and secrets to uncover. Containing a themed set of story encounters, clues, treasure and monsters to enhance Dreamescape and the Dreamquest series.

—description from the publisher

"Dreaded black ships roam the Cerenarian Sea by moonlight, plundering coastlines and crewed by creatures unknown. Sardonic traders offer rare and magical rubies for stout prisoners who are never seen again. The secret of the Black Galleys waits to be uncovered.

Only Randolph Carter and perhaps Kuranes before him know where the Black Galleys go, but rumours about them and their noisome unseen crews abound in Dylath Leen and other sea ports. What is the origin of their fascinating rubies and why is Lady Flint of Shard Castle so obsessed with them?"

This pack continues the story into a new area with more quests and secrets to uncover. Containing a themed set of story encounters, clues, treasure and monsters to enhance Dreamescape and the Dreamquest series.

—description from the publisher

"What strange creatures lurk and hunt in the primal ruins of this once great city? Sarkomand is famed as a treasure hunter’s paradise but to a foolhardy Dreamer, it can also provide a quick route to sudden death!

If you can avoid the gangs of roving Ghouls, Leng headhunters and the predations of Wamps, there is much to uncover in the once decadent city. Ancient palaces, crumbling fanes, dusty observatories and even the Mouth of the Abyss wait to be explored."

This pack continues the story into a new area with more quests and secrets to uncover. Containing a themed set of story encounters, clues, treasure and monsters to enhance Dreamescape and the Dreamquest series.

—description from the publisher

"Gods stir as graveyards are plundered and evil stalks the night. The once peaceful and fertile land of Ooth-Nargai is under threat from within and without. Shadows return to the tower of Met Halish and a terrible curse spreads unchecked. Only a heroic Dreamer can return the balance.

In a far off corner of Ooth-Nargai, a weaponsmith known as Wei Lan is rumoured to live. Perhaps the only person in the Dreamlands who can forge a blade strong enough to defeat the evil minions of the Great Old Ones. But no one has seen her for centuries and the Miasma encroaches ever closer to the shining cities of men."

This pack continues the story into a new area with more quests and secrets to uncover. Containing a themed set of story encounters, clues, treasure and monsters to enhance Dreamescape and the Dreamquest series.

—description from the publisher

Includes all the cards that were available to project followers and Early Birders. Consists of:

The Prelude Card Set - This set of Prelude cards provide initial clues to help you jump start your Dreamland adventures.

The Brief Moment in Carcosa Scenario #4 pack - Twelve new encounter cards.

Enigmatic Dreamer/Harley Patton Lore Card

Shade of Carcosa Threat Card

Graveyard of Maybe Location Card

—description from the publisher

Activity Splash is a fast-paced party game and a compact, modern twist on the classic Activity formula. Players are divided into teams and must guess words and phrases by either acting or drawing them, depending on the challenge card. Unlike the original game, Splash features quicker rounds and a lighter, more energetic pace designed for spontaneous party play.

The game emphasizes creativity, quick thinking, and teamwork. Each round is played against the clock, and teams must communicate efficiently to score points. The simplified setup and shorter playtime make Activity Splash ideal for parties, social gatherings, and larger groups where energy and laughter are key.

With its accessible rules and focus on expressive play, Activity Splash delivers classic charades-style fun in a streamlined, highly replayable format.

—description from the publisher

Be the first to reach a certain score (e.g., 10 points) by correctly naming songs of popular songs. Form teams or play individually.


     Pick a music card and scan the QR code and automatically play it in Spotify.
     Guess when the song was released and the name of the artist/group.
     Win extra points for finishing a part of the song.


The first player or team that reach the score is the winner.

Be the first to reach a certain score (e.g., 10 points) by correctly naming the television series. Form teams or play individually.


     Pick a music card and scan the QR code and automatically play it in Spotify.
     Guess the name of the TV serie
     Win extra points for answering a question about the serie.


The first player or team that reach the score is the winner.

Be the first to reach a certain score (e.g., 10 points) by correctly naming the movie title. Form teams or play individually.


     Pick a music card and scan the QR code and automatically play it in Spotify.
     Guess the name of the movie title
     Win extra points for answering a question about the movie.


The first player or team that reach the score is the winner.

Aether, the 5th element. The mysterious substance that fills the  space through which energy and gravity travel. The Necromancer lords can now delve deeper into the murky world of Alchemy to unlock the full potential of those under their control.

The Monuments expansion is the first major expansion to Crown of Ash. Adding Aether, a new resource players can tap into to train Minions and build Monuments.

Trained minions have new masks placed onto them giving them bonus actions upon being placed.

Monuments are placed into controlled regions to unlock powerful buffs, as long as they still stand!

—description from the designer

You are a Christmas elf tasked with delivering 24 Christmas presents with the help of your co-elves, represented on the cards. To accomplish this, you reveal cards from the stack, one after the other. After every revealed card you have the choice to end your turn voluntarily and deliver all the presents granted by the already revealed cards, or to continue and reveal the next card. But be careful: As the Advent season is a stressful time for Christmas elves, they are prone to spontaneously falling asleep. One sleeping elf is no problem – the rest continues working as usual, but as tiredness is contagious, the others start yawning as well. And whenever a second sleeping elf is revealed, all of them instantly fall asleep: You have busted your turn and deliver no presents at all.
After one of the players has delivered 24 presents or more, finish the current round – the elf with the highest total of delivered presents wins the game.

—description from the designer

Collect sets of cards and put on your own big art show with the aim to be the first to ‘put on a show’ by getting three and four cards of a kind. Each card reproduces one of 50 major works of art by Michelangelo, Velázquez, Degas, Van Gogh, Kandinsky, Dalí, Warhol and others. As well as key information, such as artist and date, the cards also use symbols and colours to indicate which Movement and Theme the work represents. There are twelve Movements – Renaissance, Baroque, Neoclassicism, Romanticism, Realism, Impressionism, Post-Impressionism, Expressionism, Cubism, Futurism, Surrealism and Pop Art – and six Themes – People, Places, Objects, Animals, Story and Religion.

Ready for a deeper challenge? Reboot and install Server Breach: RUNMODE — a fast campaign of four back‑to‑back games that ramps up the difficulty with new mechanics and escalating threats.


     Schema Path: Choose your route through the Corp’s network to earn bonuses or avoid dangerous Viruses.
     Challenges: Complete new objectives to earn Scripts and Upgrades that supercharge your Hackers.
     Final Challenge: Face a climactic final game and take down the Corp once and for all.
     Upgrades & Viruses: New cards modify skill values, expand suits, defend against threats or force you to adapt to debilitating Viruses.


The RUNMODE expansion includes five double‑sided pages of cards plus an optional player aid and Schema Token. Setup in about five minutes and expect 45–60 minute Runs.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Harriet the Hare and Terry the Tortoise upset the order of things while making sure the best chef wins!

This special promo pack for Critter Kitchen includes two new cutthroat zous-chefs ready to take on the town! Inside you will find two wooden tokens for Harriet and Terry, along with their matching cards.

If you have Critter Kitchen Deluxe then these new critters will find a home in the two empty slots in your box!

—description from the publisher

Conniving Coyotes is a 2-4 player card-shedding, bluffing game by Aaron Frias and Richard Codopony. Based on the folk tale of the prairie dogs who looked to the sky for rain, only to be devoured by the coyote while they were distracted.

At the beginning of the game, each player is dealt a secret role, either a prairie dog or the lone coyote. Players are dealt a hand of cards and will play a set or run that beats the previous set or run played before it, hoping to shed their cards as quickly as possible. If they can't beat the current set or run, then they must play a coyote card if they have one, which devours the current highest and lowest card from those played. Then the rest of the played cards are discarded and play continues, either till all three coyotes are played or a player runs out of cards in their hand. The cards remaining in the middle at the end of the round is scored for the prairie dogs.

After three rounds, the prairie dogs win if they were able to gather more points than the coyote player. But if the coyote players wins, the players simultaneously vote on the player they thinks is the coyote. If found, the coyote player loses and the prairie dogs steal the win.

—description from the publisher

A co-op detective game in which your team of investigators investigates a series of mysterious murders. Every decision matters, every mistake can cost lives.

Autumn 2025. A series of brutal murders, seemingly unrelated. But investigator Meglin is certain: it's the work of one man. Too pure. Too flawless. Your team is your last hope. Can you identify the maniac and put an end to his deadly game? Time is running out.

—description from the publisher

Players will compete to become the next Guild Elder by gaining the most VP.

—description from the designer

Hazardous Movement Pack 6: Liberators follows in the footsteps of Pack 5: it's chock full of thoroughly playtested, fun, tournament-sized scenarios that feature a wide variety of nationalities, terrain and theaters of war. In addition, Liberators includes a new two-sided Hazmo map (HZ4a/4b)!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 棺呪-ヒツギノロイ- (Murder Mystery Mini: Coffin Curse – Hitsugi no Roi)
description:
There was a special funeral that used two coffins.

It was summer in modern Japan. In the mountains of H Prefecture, there was a village called Hitsukimura. In that village, when a person with the title of "shrine maiden" dies, a special funeral is held. It is called the "Hitsugi Funeral."

During the funeral, a new body is discovered. Despite the obvious abnormality, the villagers try to force the funeral to continue. A reporter who was in attendance interrupts them and proposes to solve the case.

The setting document has been written in an unusual format to allow you to fully experience the humid atmosphere of a Japanese horror story. Enjoy uncovering the truth behind the mysterious funeral and curse.

—description from the publisher (translated)

Each player takes on the role of a prestigious winery. Your goal: craft the most exquisite wines by carefully selecting and playing sets of grape cards, as well as time and special cards... all while sabotaging your opponents with some rather nasty surprises — like spoiling their wine or evaporating their grapes.

Use your cards to produce and bottle diverse types of wines. The first player to bottle two wines and one cava (sparkling wine) wins the game.

Each turn begins by drawing cards, followed by up to two actions. These actions can include playing a set of grapes, a time card, bottling a wine, or playing a special card. To complete the bottling process and create a wine, you'll first need to obtain various combinations of grape cards and time cards, which simulate fermentation. Finally, bottle the wine by discarding grape cards, using them according to their star-marked value. If the game is not yet over, bottling a wine may provide a special one-time ability, such as stealing cards or gaining extra turns.

Special cards add more depth to the game, allowing you to gain individual advantages or thwart other players' plans. Halt the progress of other opponents, or recover quickly from sabotages, if you want to achieve victory.

—user summary

The Yakuza cats are trying to take over the city; be the first to gain control. In the shadows of Katsuka, yakuza clans rule the neighborhoods with an iron fist. While the Oyabun enforces his law, a mysterious Foreign Mafia infiltrates the commercial districts. Every night, the Kobun patrol the streets, ensuring the tribute reaches the clan. But war is imminent: everyone wants more territory, and whoever gets caught in the middle will pay the price.

Katsuka is a strategy game of area Majority and dice management, where players will compete to expand their influence, defend their neighborhoods and confront the Foreign Mafia to ensure their clan's dominance. You must create a quick strategy based on what the dice dictate and rely on the dice to win your duels. Be THE FAMILY of Katsuka.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Children of Morta: The Board Game – Expansion Pack
description:
Contains Stretch Goals:
1 Hero Board
8 Talent Cards
5 Bond Cards
1 Reference Card
10 Level-Up Cards
1 Dial Track
3 Tokens
18 Family Trait Cards
10 Shrine Cards
3 Dice

New York Zombies: Load Up! is a solo Print-an-Play, pen-and paper game inspired by the solo game New York Zombies and offers a similar but much quicker experience in a format that can be conveniently tucked into your favourite zombie-related book!

All you need to play is the game sheet, a pencil and a single six-sided dice. Visit four areas of your house, aiming to collect key items of gear. After all, having the right gear during an apocalypse can be difference between life and death!

Spend as little or as much time as you want searching for that all-important gear; you can run for the car, trying to take out any zombies that get in your way, when ever you want. But remember - the more useful items you have, the more likely it will be that you survive the journey ahead...

—description from the publisher

Think you know everything about your football team?  Put your knowledge to the test in this fun soccer trivia game! Compete with your friends, answer questions, collect medals, and use your quick thinking and strategy to become the champion of the board.

On your turn, roll a die. Depending on the space you land on, you must answer a question and can win one or more medals. The first player to collect 5 medals wins the cup.

boardgame: Trivia de Fútbol: Universidad de Chile
description:
Think you know everything about your football team?  Put your knowledge to the test in this fun soccer trivia game! Compete with your friends, answer questions, collect medals, and use your quick thinking and strategy to become the champion of the board.

On your turn, roll a die. Depending on the space you land on, you must answer a question and can win one or more medals. The first player to collect 5 medals wins the cup.

The Rite of Rika (RoR) offers a fiercely competitive 2v2 tactical experience that shatters conventional gameplay paradigms. Plunging teams into the heart of "The Rule of Rika" sci-fi space opera, four completely asymmetric alien races send their best Generals and Chancellors to resolve disputes across dual battlefronts. Under strict communication limits—meticulously designed to heighten tension and strategic focus—Generals command their heroes in miniature skirmishes on the planetoid Rika, while Chancellors engage in a simultaneous political card game on the orbiting Galactic Council. Their actions directly influence one another in a synergistic manner, unique to each race.

Designed from the ground up for competitive and tournament play, The Rite of Rika demands both team and race synchronization for maximum effectiveness. The game unfolds in a strict turn order—First Chancellor, Second Chancellor, First General, Second General— crafted to manage its dual-layered gameplay. This sequence forces constant tactical awareness and anticipation, as Chancellors' card plays directly set the stage for Generals' skirmishes, while Generals' abilities, in turn, empower the Chancellors' prowess.

Since every race in RoR has completely unique unit and card mechanics as well as its own path to triumph and defeat, you will effectively play a different game with each choice. The success of your team depends on anticipating each other's needs, leveraging abilities, and executing synchronized strategies, all within an intimate understanding of your chosen race.

Choose the mighty Akvira Empire and their power of prescience, the otherworldly Liguni Confluence and their symbioticism, the aura-manifesting giants of Dindrae Tetrarky, and the quest-searching Zo Har Unity to prove your race worthy of galactic ascension!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: 金曲猜歌王 台灣版 (Hitster: Taiwan Songs Edition)
description: Hitster: Taiwan Songs Edition is a music-driven party game that celebrates over 40 years of Mandopop and Taiwanese music history. Featuring over 300 classic hits—from nostalgic campus folk and 90s KTV anthems to modern streaming chart-toppers—this game turns any gathering into an instant sing-along. You don’t need to be a music expert to win; you just need to have a "feel" for the era!

boardgame: ケイヴァー 洞窟の煌めき (Murder Mystery Mini: Caver Cave Sparkle)
description:
The cave was located in a lush jungle, in a virgin land where no one had ever ventured... In search of hidden treasure, the research team arrived at the cave. Just as they had toured the cave and finished creating a map, a small earthquake occurred, and the narrow opening leading to the outside of the cave was completely blocked by fallen stone!

They had no choice but to spend the night in the cave, but this time the professor was nowhere to be found. After a while he still hadn't appeared, everyone searched for him and found that he had been killed, as if enshrined in a sacred offering!

This is a "murder mystery" in which each player takes on the role of a character in the story, aiming to "uncover the truth" and "achieve their own unique goals." Enjoy this one-off, exquisite entertainment.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: 霊能力者は幽霊と戯ぶ (Murder Mystery Mini: Psychics Play With Ghosts)
description:
Three psychics who once appeared on a TV ghost show are invited to a producer's home deep in the mountains under the pretext of discussing a new show.
Each of the three psychics accepts the invitation with mixed feelings and joins him for dinner at the mansion, but...that night, after everyone has fallen asleep, a loud noise echoes throughout the room.

The psychics gather at the bottom of the stairs in the entrance hall and find the producer's body. A mysterious ghost then appears there. Was it one of the psychics who killed the producer, or was it the ghost?

This full-scale mystery by Kei Kitazawa, the author of "For Whom Do Carrier Pigeons Fly?", is now available in the "Murder Mystery Mini" series. Enjoy this mystery woven by psychics and ghosts.

—description from the publisher (translated)

boardgame: ５人の銀行強盗 (Murder Mystery Mini: Five Bank Robbers)
description:
Bang bang! In the middle of a bank robbery, they hear what sounds like a gunshot coming from the basement of the building. Several members of the gang rush to the scene and find the dead body of their leader, Mr. White, inside the vault!

There was a bullet wound in his abdomen and a bruise on his head. He was leaning against the wall, dead, still clutching the gun in his hand. A bloody baton was lying nearby. The bank branch manager was also in the same room, but his hands and feet were tightly bound with cable ties and he was unconscious, so it was unlikely that he was the culprit. All the other hostages on the first floor were under strict guard.

In other words, the only possible suspects are the five members of the robbery gang who are able to move freely within the building. Is there a traitor? Has an undercover agent infiltrated the group? If so, it would be dangerous to flee back to the hideout. They must track down the culprit and clarify the situation as quickly as possible. As the time limit for escape approaches, the members must carry out physical searches on each other, question the hostages, and investigate the corpse and the surrounding area at lightning speed!

—description from the publisher (translated)

Codex Astronomica is a flip-and-write puzzle game in which you reveal star cards from a deck and draw them into the night sky to try to create constellations.

This is a solo game where your goal is to gain the most points you can in end-game scoring by completing constellations of differing complexities in your sky, as well as gathering bonus points from the various types of additional stellar phenomena you place.

—description from the designer

Its like the original Herd Mentality but all about food! Herd Mentality is back on the red carpet, with 100 questions all related to food and drink.

Grab a pen, think like the herd and write down the same answer as everyone else to score points.

On their turn, the player plays an accident card and tries to find a suitable hero card to solve the situation. The hero cards are spread out face down on the table. The player chooses one hero card and turns it. If they find the hero card that matches the accident, they get the pair and draw a new accident card.
If the hero card doesn't match the accident card, the player takes back the accident card into their hand. The next player continues.

The player with the most pairs wins.

Trick-Taking Yankee Theme that brings together members of various Yankee gangs to battle it out. The winner isn't the strongest, but the one who predicts the situation most accurately.

In each round, players predict the number of Yankee cards their team will need to win.

Players then send out 1, 2, or 3 Yankees (cards) at a time to battle, with winners ranked by strength.

If a player wins, a Yankee card is placed in front of the player. The round ends when the player runs out of cards.

At the end of the round, the player who correctly predicted the outcome deals damage to all other players equal to their prediction.

Players whose predictions are incorrect will take damage based on the difference between the predicted and incorrect predictions.

The game ends when any player runs out of faith points. The player with the most faith points remaining wins.

—description from the designer

Fachorama is a card game inspired by Happy Families, with a few additional subtleties.
The game consists of 32 FAF cards and 8 joker cards. (FAF is a French acronym that initially referred to xenophobic individuals and later expanded to include all far-right movements)
Each card shows a FAF member, indicating their category, type, and the families to which they belong. To form a family, you must place cards with the same logo at the top of the card. On your turn, you ask all the other players for a category of FAF members (for example, “Who has a neo-Nazi?”) to form families. If a player has a card in the requested category, they give it to the player who asked. If two players have a card in the category, the type of card being sought is specified. If no one has the requested category, a card is drawn from the deck. At the end of a player's turn, they can lay down a family, the aim of the game being to lay down as many as possible.

boardgame: Blood Bowl (Third Season Edition): Spike! Journal #19
description:
Guest editor Geoffrey of Montfort takes us to old Bretonnia for a very fancy issue of Spike! Journal, focusing on the Brionne Barons and other Bretonnian teams. This exciting issue will help you get the most out of your team, and includes rules for themed pitches and balls, the Jolly Jouster Cup, coaching tips, and entertaining editorials.

Inside this 32-page softcover magazine, you'll find:

     Chivalric Chevaliers: History and background on Bretonnian teams, a breakdown of the players' positions, and a team roster
     Famous Teams: With a focus on the Brionne Barons, and the history and highlights of other well-known teams
     The Jolly Jouster Cup: Rules for running an alternate league with Bretonnian flair
     New Rules: Special rules for running games on a Bretonnian Courtyard pitch, and Bretonnian balls
     Dirt from the Dugout: Mindy Piewhistle's regular gossip column turns to the Bordeleaux region of Bretonnia
     Chat with the Rat: Hackspit 'The Scribbler' Quillchewer's regular article involves an interview with Questing Knight Roland de Grasse
     Pro Playbook – Tip of the Lance: Tips and guides for coaching the Bretonnians to victory
     Cabalvision Presents: A Knight to Remember: A spotlight article on a famous game, and rules to help you recreate it yourself
     Special Rules and Achievements: Six new Achievements and three Special Rules
     Ask Lord Borak: The legendary Chaos player offers his unique perspective on all things Blood Bowl
     Coffin Corner: A memorial for those who gave their lives doing what they loved best


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl (Third Season Edition): Spike! Journal #20
description:
Guest editor Hetepheres Aktulye, Monarch of the Splendous Quills, dusts off the parchments for a royal issue of Spike! Journal, focusing on the Nehekhara Nightmares. This exciting issue will help you get the most out of your team, and includes rules for themed pitches and balls, the Khemri Classic Cup, coaching tips, and entertaining editorials.

Inside this 32-page softcover magazine, you'll find:

     Kings and Queens of the Desert!: History and background on Tomb Kings teams, a breakdown of the players' positions, and a team roster
     Famous Teams: With a focus on the Nehekhara Nightmares, and the history and highlights of other well-known teams
     The Khemri Classic Cup: Rules for running an alternate league with a Tomb Kings theme
     New Rules: Special rules for running games on a Pyramid Catacombs pitch, and Tomb Kings balls
     Dirt from the Dugout: Mindy Piewhistle's regular gossip column reveals a shocking spate of tomb robbings in the Charnal Valley
     Chat with the Rat: Hackspit 'The Scribbler' Quillchewer's regular article includes an interview with Sharek the Indomitable
     An Ancient Empire Rises Again: Tips and guides for coaching the Tomb Kings to victory
     Cabalvision Presents: Sand and Deliver: A spotlight article on a famous game, and rules to help you recreate it yourself
     Special Rules and Achievements: Six new Achievements and four Special Rules
     Ask Lord Borak: The legendary Chaos player offers his unique perspective on all things Blood Bowl
     Coffin Corner: A memorial for those who gave their lives doing what they loved best


—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bow (Third Season): Bretonnian Team – Reference Card Pack
description:
This set of 26 cards will help you keep track of your Bretonnian team in games of Blood Bowl. There are individual cards for your players, skill reference cards, and special rules cards for Bretonnian balls and pitches. They are an ideal way to easily check key information in the middle of a match while the crowd cheers and crunching blocks fly. There are also 16 blank Player reference cards, allowing you to track the development of your team during league play.

Contains 26 Cards:

     4x Player reference cards
     2x Ball special rule cards
     2x Pitch special rule cards
     2x Skill reference cards
     10x Bretonnian Squire blank Player cards
     2x Bretonnian Knight Catcher blank Player cards
     2x Bretonnian Knight Thrower blank Player cards
     2x Grail Knight blank Player cards


All cards measure 110mm by 75mm.

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Blood Bowl (Third Season): Tomb Kings Team – Reference Card Pack
description:
This set of 26 cards will help you keep track of your Tomb Kings team in games of Blood Bowl. There are individual cards for your players, skill reference cards, and special rules cards for Tomb Kings balls and pitches. They are an ideal way to easily check key information in the middle of a match while the crowd cheers and crunching blocks fly. There are also 16 blank Player reference cards, allowing you to track the development of your team during league play.

Contains 26 Cards:

     4x Player reference cards
     2x Ball special rule cards
     2x Pitch special rule cards
     2x Skill reference cards
     8x Skeleton Lineman blank Player cards
     4x Tomb Guardian blank Player cards
     2x Tomb Kings Thrower blank Player cards
     2x Tomb Kings Blitzer blank Player cards


All cards measure 110mm by 75mm.

—description from the publisher

Secret Ritual is a crime-investigation game with hidden roles, where players act as Detectives trying to solve a mysterious murder. A set of clue cards is laid out in three categories — Reason, Place, and Means — but only one clue in each category is actually true.

One player takes the role of the Ghost, the silent spirit of the victim. The Ghost knows the true clues and can communicate with the Detectives only through a mystical letter box. Each round, players secretly send clue cards to the Ghost. The Ghost examines them and reveals only those cards that are associatively linked to the true clues; all other cards are placed face down into the Vanished pile. The Ghost gives no indication of which category a revealed hint belongs to, or why it was chosen.

By discussing the revealed hints and the cards that vanished, players must deduce the three true clues within a limited number of rounds. In competitive games, one or more players may secretly be the Murderer and Accomplices, who know the true clues from the start and actively try to mislead the investigation through bluffing and misinformation.

The game can be played in cooperative mode (2+ players), where all players work together to identify all three true clues, or in competitive mode (4+ players), where the Detectives and the Ghost face off against the hidden Murderer team. The Detectives win by correctly identifying all three true clues, or two true clues and the Murderer. The Murderer wins if the investigation fails — or by successfully identifying certain special roles, if they are in play.

—description from the designer
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
1453: Siege of Constantinople is a solo wargame where the player takes the role of Mehmed II and the Ottoman forces laying siege to Constantinople, the capital of the Byzantine empire. Win the game by completely depleting a section of the Theodosian walls of pieces (representing both the strength of the wall and the Byzantine troops). Increase the pressure on the defending the troops by taking actions that represent mining under the wall, firing the giant Basilic cannon and hauling your ships overland to bypass the great chain of the Golden Horn.

Byzantine defensive actions will be determined by a card draw and may include repairing and manning the walls, counter mining and naval actions. Your available actions will be limited by the combinations you are able to make when rolling four dice. Choose the best combinations of actions to conquer the city before your supplies and the draw pile run out.

—description from the designer

Fistful of T-34s is a free battalion level World War II game written by Ty Beard & Paul Minson.

It is a 4-page, introductory version of their full game, Fistful of TOWS 3. 
We've partnered with the original authors to refresh their free introductory version with a new graphic design, linked scenarios, and video tutorials to get you started in World War II wargaming for zero dollars.

The game can be played with any scale miniatures on round, square, or rectangular bases.
Gameplay requires only a few six-sided dice(d6s), tape measures, and a variety of colored tokens or markers to indicate statuses such as Suppressed, Overwatch, etc.

A turn represents about 15 minutes and 1” represents about 100 meters. 
A stand of miniatures is a platoon of infantry or vehicles.

Turn Sequence:

     Attacker Artillery: Place artillery templates & resolve barrages
     Attacker Movement: Attacker moves & Defender Overwatch
     Attacker Fire: Attacker’s stands fire if eligible
     Quality Checks: Resolve all QCs from Fire and Formation Integrity
     Switch Initiative: Defender now conducts phases 1-4


—description from the publisher

Enter the arena and take on the role of a daring charioteer, ready to do whatever it takes to cross the finish line first! Aurigae plunges you into the heart of ancient races, where strategy, boldness, and luck intertwine to determine the victor.

Explore countless dice combinations and discover a game where every roll matters. Choose your dice, roll them, and add up your results to outpace your rivals… or avoid disaster!

Bonus and Malus cards spice up every game: they determine which dice you can use and reshape your strategy each turn. With 12 dice of different values, will you gamble on raw power, or rely on event cards capable of turning the race upside down at any moment?

And that’s not all! Event cards bring their share of surprises and chaos: a sandstorm that slows everyone down, a mud pit that immobilizes chariots… or the dreaded Spotapis, whose mere appearance sends competitors retreating in fear!

Every race is unique, packed with twists, laughter, and nail-biting tension right up to the final stretch.

Ready to challenge fate? Grab your dice, hold the reins tight, and dive into Rome’s most epic race!

—description from the publisher

Littoral Commander: Australia is an expansion for the critically-acclaimed, professional wargame Littoral Commander: Indo-Pacific. Australia includes four new maps, additional scenarios, and the counters and cards necessary for using the Australian military in the Littoral Commander system.

—description from the publisher

99 Balloons is a fast-paced, cumulative set collection game where players predict which balloon colours will become the most and least popular in a shared, public pool. The tension is high because the most popular colour is worth negative points, forcing players to hedge their bets and gamble on the colours they think will end up in the public pool. Players play cumulative rounds until one player reaches 99 points, then the player with the highest points win.

—description from the designer

Draw a card and read the clues to the other players: the first to correctly identify the film title wins the card and scores points based on the complexity of the clues. With three difficulty levels, test your film knowledge and impress your friends! Blockbusters, arthouse cinema, award-winning films, or niche gems—a game for all film buffs!

boardgame: Scarface 1920: Dice Tower Promo News Cards
description:
2 promo news cards for Scarface 1920
Chicago Booms
Associates hired from the market are placed in your hand without paying alcohol

Authorities Under Close Scrutiny
If you carry out a send 1 thug to the authorities order, +1 Crime rate

Available from the 2025 dice tower Gamefound campaign

Promo card for Creature Caravan

When you gain a treasure chest, also gain a bag.

+ 1 attack per treasure chest token you own.

Available from the Dicetower 2025 Gamefound campaign

New promo card for Clank!
From the Dicetower 2025 Gamefound campaign

Once per turn spent 1 gold to gain 1 movement

13 Nightmares is a solo print-and-play board game about lucid dreams, surreal experiences, and facing your inner fears. Each dream is a unique mini-game with its own rules and challenges, but they all connect through a shared hero board that develops with every attempt. Gain focus and control over your dreams with every attempt,  collect objects and carry them over from one dream to another, survive all nightmares one by one to break the loop and finally wake up.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Pick The Pug: Soveram – The Forbidden
description:
Soveram - The Forbidden is a mythical creature card that was only available as part of certain pledge tiers during the Pick the Pug Kickstarter campaign.

Card Text:

Soveram - The Forbidden
Discard all cards in your hand, Donkeys included, except for The Pug (if you have it).

boardgame: Twisted Traits: Character Traits for Four Against Darkness
description: Twisted Traits is a collection of random character traits for the eight core classes found in Four Against Darkness. Each class (warrior, wizard, cleric, halfling, dwarf, elf, rogue, barbarian) has a d20 list of traits, and a list of rare traits. Most traits give a little edge in a specific situation or character development area, or give additional options for combat, magic, social interaction or skill use, or represent rare items the character possesses at the start of the game.

A grid-based dice and card game where players roll dice and complete rows to collapse a grid. Numbered cards are set up for each player on a 4x3 grid. Players get 3 chances to roll the dice and match the numbers on the card grid. When a player matches all of the numbers in any vertical or horizontal row, the player removes the matched cards, and the grid gets smaller. The first player to clear all of their cards wins the round. The remaining players total their remaining cards and are penalized those points. Players play 5 rounds, and the player with the lowest score wins.

—description from the designer

In 17 Dice Bet, you score points by predicting which dice(8D) combinations you can form by freely choosing dice from both the Shared Dice and your Personal Dice.
After the final round, the player with the highest score wins.

—description from the publisher

Camel Meets Straw is a fast-paced, laugh-out-loud, compounding skill-testing game for the whole family! Find out how far you can push yourself and others before the last straw breaks the Camel's back?
The Camel (AKA the performer) plays a card from their hand face up in the middle of the table, and immediately starts performing the instructions on the card. For the rest of the round, the Camel is at the mercy of the other players (AKA The Straw!).

Each Straw (non-performing player) takes their turn to play one card. They put it down next to or on top of the active card(s) in the middle. Each new card adds a new layer to the Camel’s challenge!

Each category gets its own dedicated spot on the table. Colors NEVER Mix! A card can only be played onto its matching color pile, ensuring only one active instruction per category.

A round ends in one of two ways:

If the Camel makes it through the entire round without messing up, they win the round! They get to choose the next Camel.
If the Camel messes up, the Straw that "broke the Camel's back" wins the round! They choose the next camel.
The more ridiculous the combinations, the better. The best rounds are when things get ridiculous!

—description from the publisher

War is raging again in Europe, after the invasion of Ukraine in 2022. But another type of warfare is happening in the shadows: hybrid war.

Hybrid warfare, also called 'grey zone' or 'sub-threshold' warfare, is when a hostile state carries out an anonymous, deniable attack, usually in highly suspicious circumstances. It will be enough to harm their opponent, especially their infrastructure assets, but stop short of being an attributable act of war (source: BBC). The aim of hybrid warfare is to disrupt, sabotage, and weaken cohesion among Western allies.

Hybrid War is a solitaire war game. You will control three Western (WEST) secret service agents, as they seek to detect and disrupt enemy hybrid war actions. The game will control the non-player faction, consisting of three Russian (RUSS) secret service agents.

Victory Conditions
The WEST faction (you, the player) win the game if the Disruption counter has not reached the ‘10’ space on the disruption track when the game ends after 7 game turns. The game ends with Russian victory if the disruption counter reaches the ‘10’ space on the disruption track.

This game is a low ink prototype entry in the 2026 Print and Play Wargame Design contest.

—description from the designer

boardgame: Checkerin Marina: Checkst du's? – Das Spiel
description:
The first game for all fans of Checkerin Marina aged 8-14. 
128 cards full of fun await you! Test your knowledge with trivia questions and get excited about fascinating topics such as the environment, history, everyday life, and much more. Or would you rather draw a challenge card and take on a creative and fun challenge? One thing is certain — boredom doesn't stand a chance when Checkerin Marina's game is out!
Take on the ultimate quiz and fun challenges together with your friends! The answers to the quiz questions are hidden on each card – so cheating is impossible. A list of items you need for the challenges can be found in the instructions – so you're always ready for fun on the go!

—description from the publisher
A pack containing three games from Cryptozoic Entertainment, gorgeously illustrated by Steven Rhodes: Don't Talk to Strangers, Let's Summon Demons and Let's Dig for Treasure.

boardgame: Stadt, Land, NEIN!: Der Spieleklassiker für Pessimist*innen und alle, die eigentlich keinen Bock haben
description:
The classic game "City-Country-River" for all unlucky and grumpy people!
No matter where you are, with this City, Country, NO! block, you can even convince those who don't like games. In 52 fun categories, you can really let your inner pessimist out and prove your creativity. I say NO to that!, I would NOT like to have as a colleague, or I NEVER buy at the supermarket – can you always think of something suitable?
From simple questions to tricky categories – it's all here! Take on the challenge and let yourself be surprised by the diverse categories. Test yourself and your friends – who always has an answer ready and can fill in the categories the fastest? Factual knowledge, creativity, and speed are required; only then will you be able to be the first to shout “Stop!” Are you ready? Then grab your pens, get set, go!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Der ultimative Spielblock: Kopf gegen Kopf – Fußball: Ran an den Ball!
description:
The ultimate game pad for soccer fans aged 6 and up! 
Whether at home or on the go, 4 different games provide ultimate fun across a total of 80 pages. Variety is guaranteed! The pad includes the cult game “Who is it?” in a cool soccer design, and in the “Dribble Maze” you can engage in a real head-to-head race. The highlight of the pad: you can not only compete against each other, but also play together! Every afternoon becomes a top match!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Memo-Spiel: Die witzigsten Wörter der Welt
description:
The fun and educational memory game for language fans!
We bet that this game will help you learn vocabulary that you won't forget anytime soon. This memory game brings together the most beautiful and strangest words in the world. Did you know, for example, that the Dutch tax office is called “Belastingdienst”? That an exhaust pipe is called “Knalpot” in Indonesian? That the armpit is actually called “Kilikili” in Filipino Tagalog? 
Search for 30 funny words from 12 languages and match them with the 30 corresponding illustrations. Train your brain and expand your vocabulary while playing memo. With special rules for “advanced word players”: Includes the “Swiss correctness” variant and the gambler's variant “Double or nothing.”

—description from the publisher

The evolutionary flip-and-write game! 
In LIFE, every card you play becomes a tricky decision: Can you skillfully place the number shown in your own grid? Or will you cleverly ignore an opponent's number and give up one of your valuable cards in return? Which might cost you dearly later on?
In this evolutionary flip-and-write game, you must never let the chain of life break! Because everyone plays at the same time, but speed is not a factor, there is no waiting time or time constraints. Instead, you are constantly faced with the agonizing choice of which number to play next.
Spread the arc from amoebas and single-celled organisms to Adam and Eve – and take on the challenge of preserving the miracle of nature!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Sort it: Wer schlecht sortiert, verliert!
description:
Fun that goes off the scale!
From cheap to expensive, from ugly to beautiful, from pleasant to annoying – where would you classify terms such as romance novel, lawnmower, and banana? In this fun party game, one person draws terms and classifies them according to a secret characteristic on a scale of 1 to 10. Then the others try to guess the characteristic. If at least one person guesses correctly, they and the sorter receive points and it's the next person's turn. 
Whether at a birthday party, on a trip, at home, with family, or with friends, this fun card game is guaranteed to keep boredom at bay. Characteristics such as usefulness, price, stability, and annoyance factor will have everyone laughing. The rules are quick to explain and the fun can begin right away! But who will collect the most points and win this fun party game?

—description from the publisher

Acting out singing, soccer, and cake without words? Anyone can do that! But can you guess the terms when the roles are reversed? 
This game reinvents the classic game of charades with the motto: whoever acts must also guess! The actor follows the instructions of the others and has to figure out the term themselves!
The next children's birthday party or play date is sure to come! And with this ingenious charades game, there's no chance of boredom! The funny challenges provide extra fun, because when someone sings instructions and then someone else jumps up and down while acting, there's no stopping the laughter! Let's go!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Pantomime: Switch it up! – Teenie Edition
description:
Acting out cell phones, dancing, and school without words? Anyone can do that! But can you guess the terms when the roles are reversed? 
This game reinvents the classic game of charades with the motto: whoever acts must also guess! The person acting follows the instructions of the others and must figure out the term themselves! With fun terms for teens. 
The next party is sure to come! And with this ingenious charades game, boredom is guaranteed not to set in! The funny challenges provide extra fun, because when someone sings instructions and then someone else has to look grumpy while acting, nothing stands in the way of a fit of laughter! Let's go!

—description from the publisher

The fast-paced picture search game that everyone plays at the same time.
No waiting, no boredom — just a great atmosphere! Can you spot which animal is still peeking out of the same window the fastest? Can you check who is still awake at night or has run away again? With extra-easy rules for instant play and three different card variants for maximum fun. What a mess!

—description from the publisher

boardgame: Ich hab noch nie …: für Paare
Ich hab noch nie ...: für den Junggesellinnenabschied
description:
Finally available for bridal showers — the party game that reveals all secrets! 
Over 60 cheeky statements are guaranteed to bring fun to your bachelorette party. “Never have I ever...” is the perfect party classic for revealing the secrets of your bridal crew, provoking fits of laughter and cringe attacks.
Now it's getting juicy! 50% fun statements on the topics of marriage and weddings + 50% spicy prompts on dating and sex.

—description from the publisher

Estro is not just a card game; it's a complete, expandable game system. Welcome to a world where a single, decisive blow can shatter the strongest defenses and powerful abilities leave a permanent mark on the battlefield. Command one of five "Estri" (moods) and build your unique deck from 250 cards.

Master a combat system with multiple paths to victory and unleash abilities with lasting consequences in a game designed for 1v1 duels, 2v2 team brawls, solo adventures, and much more.

Gameplay Description
Estro is a versatile and strategic card game system where players battle for total board control. The game innovates by removing player life points, focusing instead on a tense war of attrition where every card is a valuable asset.

How to Win
Victory in Estro is decisive and can be achieved through three distinct conditions:

Board Domination: Win if your opponent has no cards left on their field at the end of a round (this is active from the end of the fifth round onward).

Resource Exhaustion: Win immediately if your opponent must draw a card but has an empty deck and an empty hand.

Victory by Score (Time Limit): After 19 rounds, the player with more cards on the field wins, provided their cards' total value is also higher.

The Art of Combat: Two Paths to Destruction

Estro features an innovative combat system that offers two ways to destroy an opponent's card, creating deep tactical choices:

Victory by Power: The standard path. Your card wins if its total value (TOT), modified by bonuses, is higher than the defender's total value.

Primary Value Overwhelm: The game-changing tactical path. If your attacking card's primary stat (e.g., ATK for an ARDO card) is more than double the defender's corresponding primary stat (e.g., DEF for a GRANITO card), your card wins and destroys the defender instantly, ignoring the total scores. This mechanic rewards clever matchups and can turn the tide in a single, well-planned attack.

Gifts That Leave a Mark: The "Dono" System
The abilities on the cards ("Doni") are uniquely persistent, making your choices matter for the entire game:

1V (One-Time Activation): Unlike typical one-shot effects, a "1V" Dono is activated only once per game, but its effect becomes a permanent modification to the card (e.g., a permanent +10 to a stat) that lasts until the card is destroyed. It's a fire-and-forget upgrade that leaves a lasting impact.

Infinity (Infinite): These are passive abilities that remain active as long as the card is on the field, providing continuous effects and enabling powerful engine-building synergies.

An Ever-Growing Universe of Ways to Play
Estro was designed as an ever-evolving system. Since its release, numerous official game modes have been introduced, ensuring incredible replayability:

Duel (1v1): The classic, strategic head-to-head experience.
Inferno (2v2): A tactical team battle of coordination and synergy.

Solo & Cooperative Modes: Face off against automated "Anomalies," challenging scenarios, and Raid Bosses.
King of the Hill: A multiplayer mode where players fight to control a central point.

Draft Mode: Test your deckbuilding skills on the fly by selecting cards from a random pool.
And many more, with the system's flexibility allowing for a constantly expanding world of play.

Key Features

Strategic Combat with "Primary Value Overwhelm": Master two distinct ways to destroy cards, adding a unique layer of tactical depth.

Permanent Effects with the "1V" System: Your choices have lasting consequences, as one-time activations grant permanent upgrades to your cards.

An Ever-Growing Universe of Game Modes: One box provides endless experiences with official support for 1v1, 2v2, Solo, Co-op, King of the Hill, Raid Bosses, and more.
Clear Victory Conditions: Win by dominating the board, exhausting your opponent's resources, or having a superior force when time runs out.

No Life Points, Pure Strategy: Every decision matters in this tactical battle for board control.

Deep Deck Construction: With 250 unique cards and no duplicates in your deck, the strategic possibilities are virtually endless.

—description from the designer

boardgame: "Arktik" Durch Schnee und Eis zum Nordpol
description:
The players take turns to roll and move. Each player moves on his own track. On some spaces events hinder or benefit the players. The first to reach the North Pole wins the game.
